EDCS-00380791.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudius Eutyches Claudii Catulli Galli clarissimi viri libertus sibi et Rosulae
EDCS-00380791.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudius Eutyches, freedman of Claudius Catullus Gallus, a most distinguished man, [set this up] for himself and for Rosula.
EDCS-00380791.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Claudius Eutyches, a freedman of an equestrian or senatorial master, dedicated a monument for himself and Rosula, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-00380792.text_edition: Apollini Gangarensi Aurelius Capito votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380792.translation: To Apollo Gangarensis, Aurelius Capito willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380792.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Capito to Apollo Gangarensis, fulfilling a vow he had made, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380793.text_edition: Tattaris Veneti filius? et Temus Batonis filia Narensai sic Annaei nnorum XXV hic sita est
EDCS-00380793.translation: Tattaris, son of Venetus(?), and Temus, daughter of Baton, [for] Narensai, also Annaea, aged 25 years. She lies here.
EDCS-00380793.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Narensai, also known as Annaea, who died at age 25, likely erected by Tattaris and Temus.

EDCS-00380795.text_edition: Dis Manibus ? Cornelio  liae Pontinae  Photina et Iu parentibus posuerunt
EDCS-00380795.translation: To the Divine Manes. [---] Cornelius [---] Pontina. Photina and Iu[---] placed [this] for their parents.
EDCS-00380795.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, where Photina and Iu[---] set up a monument for their parents, Cornelius and Pontina.

EDCS-00380796.translation: To the Divine Manes of Varia Valentina.
EDCS-00380796.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Varia Valentina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380797.text_edition: Marcus Conius Marci libertus Quartus negotiator cretarius
EDCS-00380797.translation: Marcus Conius Quartus, freedman of Marcus, a clay merchant.
EDCS-00380797.summary: This inscription records the name and profession of Marcus Conius Quartus, a freedman and a clay merchant, likely from a professional or funerary monument.

EDCS-00380798.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a legatus (legate) and containing parts of personal names, likely from a public or military monument.

EDCS-00380801.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai filius Velina domo Pessinunte veteranus legionis VII nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Caius Mummius posuit
EDCS-00380801.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, of the Velina tribe, from Pessinus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 45 years, with 25 years of service. He lies here. Gaius Mummius placed this.
EDCS-00380801.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius, a veteran of Legio VII from Pessinus, who died at age 45 after 25 years of service, set up by Gaius Mummius.

EDCS-00380802.text_edition: Severus Triochari filius Ubius eques alae Tungrorum nnorum XXVI stipendiorum V hic situs est Urbanus fratri posuit
EDCS-00380802.translation: Severus Ubius, son of Triochari, an equestrian of the Ala Tungrorum, aged 26 years, with 5 years of service. He lies here. Urbanus placed this for his brother.
EDCS-00380802.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Severus Ubius, a cavalryman of the Ala Tungrorum who died at 26 after 5 years of service, erected by his brother Urbanus.

EDCS-00380803.text_edition: Caius Iulius Barcenei filius Theodorus miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum nnum LXV stipendiorum XLII hic situs est posuit Caius Iulius Scapula  frater
EDCS-00380803.translation: Gaius Iulius Theodorus, son of Barceneus, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Cyrrhestae, aged 65 years, with 42 years of service. He lies here. Gaius Iulius Scapula, his brother, placed this.
EDCS-00380803.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Theodorus, a soldier of Cohors II Cyrrhestarum, who died at 65 after 42 years of service, erected by his brother, Gaius Iulius Scapula.

EDCS-00380804.text_edition: Caius Iulius Andromachus miles cohortis II Cyrrestarum nnorum LXV stipendiorum XXXX Caius Iulius Apolla sodalis votum posuit
EDCS-00380804.translation: Gaius Julius Andromachus, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Cyrrhestae, aged 65, with 40 years of service. Gaius Julius Apolla, his comrade, set up this vow.
EDCS-00380804.summary: This is a funerary or votive monument dedicated by Gaius Julius Apolla for his comrade, Gaius Julius Andromachus, a veteran soldier from the 2nd Cohort of Cyrrhestae, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-00380805.text_edition: Dis Manibus Papiriae Metellicae defunctae nnorum XXXIII Lucius Oclatius Hyginus coniugi bene merenti et Arriis Mercurio et Aristioni et Papiriae Isiadi et I Papiriae Thasidi
EDCS-00380805.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Papiria Metellica, who died at the age of 33. Lucius Oclatius Hyginus [set this up] for his well-deserving wife, and for Arrius Mercurius and Arrius Aristio, and for Papiria Isias, and for Papiria Thasis.
EDCS-00380805.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Lucius Oclatius Hyginus for his deceased wife, Papiria Metellica, and several other named individuals, likely family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380806.text_edition: Dis Manibus Prima mater Crispinae Titi libertae defunctae nnorum XVIII posuit
EDCS-00380806.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Prima, mother of Crispina, freedwoman of Titus, who died at the age of 18, set this up.
EDCS-00380806.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Prima, a mother, for her deceased daughter Crispina, a freedwoman of Titus, who died young.

EDCS-00380808.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcellina mater Aureliae Marcellae filiae pientissimae defunctae nnnorum XVIII sibi et posteris suis posuit
EDCS-00380808.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Marcellina, mother of Aurelia Marcella, her most devoted daughter, who died at the age of 18, set this up for herself and her descendants.
EDCS-00380808.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Aurelia Marcellina for her deceased daughter, Aurelia Marcella, who died at 18, also reserving space for herself and her future descendants.

EDCS-00380809.text_edition: Publiae Aeliae Publi filiae Panoni coniugi karissimae Titus Curiatius Seneca maritus IIvir quinquennalis Delmin(iensium)
EDCS-00380809.translation: To Publia Aelia Panon, daughter of Publius, his dearest wife. Titus Curiatius Seneca, her husband, a duovir quinquennalis of the Delminians [set this up].
EDCS-00380809.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Titus Curiatius Seneca, a prominent local magistrate (duovir quinquennalis) of Delminium, for his beloved wife, Publia Aelia Panon.

EDCS-00380810.text_edition: Publius Aelius Quintus scriba publicus Delminiensium votum reddidit libens
EDCS-00380810.translation: Publius Aelius Quintus, a public scribe of the Delminians, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380810.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Publius Aelius Quintus, a public scribe from Delminium, fulfilled a vow to a deity, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380811.translation: To Jupiter, Valeria Rentina [set this up].
EDCS-00380811.summary: This is a simple votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Valeria Rentina.

EDCS-00380812.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Oppia Tertulla Volsi filia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-00380812.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Oppia Tertulla, daughter of Volsus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-00380812.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Oppia Tertulla, daughter of Volsus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-00380817.text_edition: vixit annos  vixit nnos vixit nnos  vixit nnos ius Marci filius Epic et  Hermione
EDCS-00380817.translation: [He/She] lived for years... Marcus, son of Epic, and Hermione.
EDCS-00380817.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely commemorating multiple individuals including Marcus, son of Epic, and Hermione, with age indications that are now largely lost.

EDCS-00380820.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a person named Aper and their lifespan.

EDCS-00380821.text_edition: Titus Carienus? --- Capitonis filius  testamento f(ieri)? i(ussit)? hoc? monimentum heeredem non sequetur FVIS
EDCS-00380821.translation: Titus Carienus, son of Capito, ordered this monument to be made by his will. It shall not pass to an heir. FVIS.
EDCS-00380821.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Titus Carienus, son of Capito, commissioned his own monument by will, specifying it should not be inherited, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03000733.summary: This is a votive inscription by Claudius Niger dedicated to the goddess Diana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000740.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Urgulanio Theophilo Sempronia Quarta marito rarissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-03000740.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Sempronia Quarta placed this for Publius Urgulanius Theophilus, her most unique and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03000740.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Sempronia Quarta in memory of her exceptional husband, Publius Urgulanius Theophilus, probably from the Imperial era.

EDCS-03000741.text_edition: Dis Manibus Semproniae Quartae Publius Urgulanius Ursus matri piissimae  posuit
EDCS-03000741.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Urgulanius Ursus placed this for Sempronia Quarta, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-03000741.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Urgulanius Ursus to his beloved mother, Sempronia Quarta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000742.text_edition: Dis Manibus Praetorinae Licinianus pientissimae conservae posuit annorum XXXXI
EDCS-03000742.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Licinianus placed this for Praetorina, his most devoted fellow slave, who lived for 41 years.
EDCS-03000742.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Licinianus for his cherished fellow slave, Praetorina, who died at the age of 41, indicating a likely Imperial date for this servile memorial.

EDCS-03000743.text_edition: Dis Manibus Modesto defuncto nnorum XXXX Convictus posuit
EDCS-03000743.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Convictus placed this for Modestus, deceased, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-03000743.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Convictus for the deceased Modestus, who lived to be 40 years old, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000744.text_edition: Dis Manibus Laeliae Saturninae matri bene merenti
EDCS-03000744.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Laelia Saturnina, a well-deserving mother.
EDCS-03000744.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Laelia Saturnina, recognized as a deserving mother, likely dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-03000745.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning only the name Euphrosyne.

EDCS-03000747.text_edition: marito opRtimo#optimo#ORTIMO Caetennia Thiodora sic uxor posuit bene merenti
EDCS-03000747.translation: Caetennia Theodora, his wife, thus placed this for her most excellent and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-03000747.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Caetennia Theodora for her most deserving husband, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000748.text_edition: Caius Lollius Cai filius Papiria Valens veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Hostiliae Publi filiae Secundae coniugi sanctissimae
EDCS-03000748.translation: Gaius Lollius Valens, son of Gaius, of the Papirian tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, 'Pia Fidelis', made this in his lifetime for himself and for Hostilia Secunda, daughter of Publius, his most sacred wife.
EDCS-03000748.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Lollius Valens, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, during his lifetime for himself and his revered wife, Hostilia Secunda, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD based on legionary naming conventions and the 'Pia Fidelis' title.

EDCS-03300741.text_edition: Finis novus saltus territorii Tariotarum ex decreto Publi Corneli Dolabellae  pro
EDCS-03300741.translation: New boundary of the Tariotarum territory, by decree of Publius Cornelius Dolabella.
EDCS-03300741.summary: This inscription records a new boundary marker for the Tariotarum territory, established by a decree from Publius Cornelius Dolabella, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03300742.text_edition: Saltus territorii Tariotarum ex decreto Dolabellae legati
EDCS-03300742.translation: The territory of the Tariotarum by decree of Dolabella, the legate.
EDCS-03300742.summary: This inscription describes the territory of the Tariotarum, established or defined by a decree issued by Dolabella, who served as a legate, probably in the early Imperial era.

EDCS-03300743.text_edition: Lucius Attius Luci filius Sergia Augusta annorum XXX stipendiorum VIII miles legionis VII hic situs est
EDCS-03300743.translation: Lucius Attius, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, from Augusta, 30 years old, with 8 years of service, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, lies here.
EDCS-03300743.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, who died at the age of 30 after 8 years of service, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300744.text_edition: Lucius Mummius Luci filius Fabia Ancyra miles legionis VII nnorum XXXVI stipendiorum XVI hic situs est  Arma bis octonis felicia qui tulit annis hic situs est fato Mummius ipse suo si sapiunt obiti data munera percipis ampla et recipis nostrae pignus amicitiae hoc tibi cum titulo posuit Cornelius Chrestus et dixit lachrymans sic sit tibi terra levis  Marcus Cornelius Marci libertus Chrestus posuit
EDCS-03300744.translation: Lucius Mummius, son of Lucius, of the Fabian tribe, from Ancyra, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 36 years old, with 16 years of service, lies here. He who bore successful arms for twice eight years, Mummius himself lies here by his own fate. If the departed are wise, you receive ample gifts bestowed, and accept the pledge of our friendship. Cornelius Chrestus placed this for you with this title, and weeping, said: 'May the earth be light for you.' Marcus Cornelius Chrestus, freedman of Marcus, placed this.
EDCS-03300744.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription for Lucius Mummius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, placed by Marcus Cornelius Chrestus, a freedman, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700656.text_edition: Rufo Titae matri Titua viva fecit
EDCS-03700656.translation: Titua made this for her mother Rufa Titae, while still alive.
EDCS-03700656.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Titua preemptively dedicated a monument to her mother, Rufa Titae, during her own lifetime, a common practice in the Roman period.

EDCS-03700657.summary: This is a dedication to Titus Iulius Ampliatus, likely part of a larger monument, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-03700658.text_edition: Arruntiae Cai filiae Celerinae Caius Arruntius Sedatus filiae carissimae posuit
EDCS-03700658.translation: Gaius Arruntius Sedatus placed this for Arruntia Celerina, daughter of Gaius, his dearest daughter.
EDCS-03700658.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Arruntius Sedatus to his beloved daughter, Arruntia Celerina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700659.text_edition: Veratiae Cai filiae Priscillae Caius Arruntius Sedatus uxori posuit
EDCS-03700659.translation: Gaius Arruntius Sedatus placed this for Veratia Priscilla, daughter of Gaius, his wife.
EDCS-03700659.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Arruntius Sedatus to his wife, Veratia Priscilla, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700661.translation: To Vesia Paullina, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-03700661.summary: This is a dedication to Vesia Paullina, daughter of Titus, likely part of a funerary or commemorative monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700662.text_edition: Vesiae Moderatae Gellius Chrysogonus uxori karissimae
EDCS-03700662.translation: Gellius Chrysogonus placed this for Vesia Moderata, his dearest wife.
EDCS-03700662.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gellius Chrysogonus to his dearest wife, Vesia Moderata, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700663.translation: To Arruntia Secundilla, her daughter Quinta, mother, [set this up].
EDCS-03700663.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother, Quinta, dedicating a monument to her daughter, Arruntia Secundilla.

EDCS-03700664.text_edition: Ephebo actori Elpis sibi et filio posuit
EDCS-03700664.translation: Elpis set this up for herself and for her son Ephebus, the actor.
EDCS-03700664.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Elpis dedicates a monument for herself and her son, Ephebus, who was an actor.

EDCS-03700666.text_edition: Rubriae Titi filiae Maximillae Valeria Marci filia Marcella filiae posuit
EDCS-03700666.translation: Valeria Marcella, daughter of Marcus, set this up for her daughter Rubria Maximilla, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-03700666.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Valeria Marcella dedicating a monument to her daughter, Rubria Maximilla.

EDCS-03700667.translation: To Oppia Paulina, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-03700667.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription for Oppia Paulina, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-03700668.text_edition: Aufidiae Cai filiae Balbinae annorum V Caesia Luci filia Paula avia nepti suae viva fecit
EDCS-03700668.translation: Caesia Paula, daughter of Lucius, her grandmother, made this while living for her granddaughter Aufidia Balbina, daughter of Gaius, who was five years old.
EDCS-03700668.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a five-year-old girl, Aufidia Balbina, set up by her living grandmother, Caesia Paula.

EDCS-03700673.summary: This inscription simply records the name of an individual, Titus Clodius Sabinus, likely part of a larger monument or dedication.

EDCS-03700677.text_edition: Cai Iuli Cis  Cai Iuli Cis
EDCS-03700677.translation: Of Gaius Julius Cis, of Gaius Julius Cis.
EDCS-03700677.summary: This inscription repetitively names Gaius Julius Cis, possibly indicating ownership or a dedication by two individuals with the same name, or a fragmented inscription repeating the name of a single person.

EDCS-04201329.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio V Callisto V Gemellina coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-04201329.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius V. Callistus V. Gemellina [set this up] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-04201329.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Callistus by his wife Gemellina, honoring him as a well-deserving husband.

EDCS-04201550.translation: Appuleia Aretusa, his mother, sets this up for Gaius Trosius Liberalis, a soldier of the Sixth Praetorian Cohort, who lived nineteen years.
EDCS-04201550.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Appuleia Aretusa dedicates a monument to her son, Gaius Trosius Liberalis, a nineteen-year-old soldier of the Sixth Praetorian Cohort.

EDCS-04202098.translation: Trosia Tertia, his daughter, set this up for Lucius Trosius Crescens.
EDCS-04202098.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Trosia Tertia dedicates a monument to her father, Lucius Trosius Crescens.

EDCS-04900821.translation: To Augustus. VA CNSEI LVSAMANHVS MAHS.
EDCS-04900821.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Augustus, likely by a private individual whose name is partially preserved.

EDCS-04900822.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, with tribunician power.
EDCS-04900822.summary: This fragment likely records a dedication to Emperor Tiberius Caesar, mentioning his tribunician power as a dating or honorary formula.

EDCS-04900827.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Aelius Catius libDens#libens#LIDENS posuit
EDCS-04900827.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Aelius Catius willingly placed this.
EDCS-04900827.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus, set up by Aelius Catius.

EDCS-04900830.translation: A most devoted daughter placed this for her well-deserving parent.
EDCS-04900830.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a devoted daughter in memory of her deserving parent, whose name is not preserved.

EDCS-04900831.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Rutilio Agaclyto Rutilia Homonya filia
EDCS-04900831.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Rutilia Homonya, daughter, [placed this] for Lucius Rutilius Agaclytus.
EDCS-04900831.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Lucius Rutilius Agaclytus, set up by his daughter Rutilia Homonya.

EDCS-04900920.text_edition: Nemesi Augustae Caianus votum posuit cum suis
EDCS-04900920.translation: Caianus placed his vow to Nemesis Augusta with his family.
EDCS-04900920.summary: This is a religious inscription recording a vow made to the goddess Nemesis Augusta by Caianus, including his family in the dedication.

EDCS-05000665.summary: This brief inscription appears to be a statement of ownership or attribution relating to Publius Aelius Firminianus.

EDCS-05000667.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Cornelia Edessa miles legionis VII nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XII testamento fieri iussit Lucius Valerius heres posuit et Valeria Quinta liberta hic sita est
EDCS-05000667.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, of the Cornelian tribe, Edessa, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 35 years old, with 12 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Lucius Valerius, his heir, placed it, and Valeria Quinta, a freedwoman, is buried here.
EDCS-05000667.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, erected by his heir Lucius Valerius according to his will, and also commemorating the burial of the freedwoman Valeria Quinta.

EDCS-05000668.text_edition: Quintus Mettius Quinti filius Voltinia Valens miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XIIX testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-05000668.translation: Quintus Mettius, son of Quintus, of the Voltinian tribe, Valens, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, 38 years old, with 18 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-05000668.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Mettius Valens, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, recording that he ordered its erection through his will.

EDCS-05200553.text_edition: us Publi et  libertus? Mus hic situs est VX Publius Servilius  P F vivus fecit
EDCS-05200553.translation: Publius and his freedman Mus is buried here. Publius Servilius, son of Publius, made this in his lifetime.
EDCS-05200553.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Publius Servilius, son of Publius, made this monument for himself and his freedman Mus during his lifetime.

EDCS-05200554.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luci Claudi Ianuari annorum XXX item A
EDCS-05200554.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Claudius Ianuarius, 30 years old. Also A[...]
EDCS-05200554.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Claudius Ianuarius, who died at age 30.

EDCS-06100478.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Marcus Aurelius Attenio miles legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-06100478.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester. Marcus Aurelius Attenio, soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, Antoninian, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06100478.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the deity Silvanus Silvester by Marcus Aurelius Attenio, a soldier of the Legio I Adiutrix.

EDCS-06100479.text_edition: D(eo) Silvano Silvestri Augusto sacrum Quintus Bovius Rufinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-06100479.translation: Sacred to the god Silvanus Silvester Augustus. Quintus Bovius Rufinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06100479.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Silvanus Silvester Augustus by Quintus Bovius Rufinus.

EDCS-06100480.text_edition: Deo Invicto Soli Mithrae Terentius Dalmata MANTIVS votum solvit in honorem amicorum suorum
EDCS-06100480.translation: To the Unconquered God Sol Mithras, Terentius Dalmata, MANTIVS, fulfilled his vow in honor of his friends.
EDCS-06100480.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Sol Invictus Mithras by Terentius Dalmata, made in honor of his friends.

EDCS-06100481.text_edition: Deo Pho(sphoro bono votum posuit AurP(elius)#Aur(elius)#AUP Dar<d=I>anus#Dardanus#DARIA BFN RIB I FIXICI FPI
EDCS-06100481.translation: To the good god Phosphorus, Aurelius Dardanus placed a vow.
EDCS-06100481.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Phosphorus by Aurelius Dardanus.

EDCS-06100482.text_edition: Aurelius Lupus sibbi et coniugi suae Iuliae Maximae piscinam fecit
EDCS-06100482.translation: Aurelius Lupus made a swimming pool for himself and his wife, Iulia Maxima.
EDCS-06100482.summary: This inscription records the construction of a swimming pool by Aurelius Lupus for himself and his wife, Iulia Maxima.

EDCS-06100483.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Aelius Pasiphilus A Iuliae Gemellae coniugi bene merenti se vivo fecit et sibi et Petronio Staphylo filio hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur locus donatus a FAB aera proditio longum pedes octo latum pedes quattuor
EDCS-06100483.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aelius Pasiphilus made this monument for Iulia Gemella, his well-deserving wife, during his lifetime, and for himself and his son Petronius Staphylus. This monument will not pass to an heir. The place was donated by FAB and measures eight feet long by four feet wide.
EDCS-06100483.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Titus Aelius Pasiphilus during his lifetime for his wife Iulia Gemella, himself, and his son Petronius Staphylus, with a specific dimension and condition for inheritance.

EDCS-06100484.text_edition: Varius Sabinus Salonitanus ex collegio fabrorum vivo sibi posuit
EDCS-06100484.translation: Varius Sabinus Salonitanus, from the college of craftsmen, placed this for himself while alive.
EDCS-06100484.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by Varius Sabinus Salonitanus, a member of a college of craftsmen, for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-06100485.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelia Florentina cum suis ex voto posuit
EDCS-06100485.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelia Florentina, with her family, placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-06100485.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelia Florentina and her family, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-06100486.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Billianius Severus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-06100486.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Billianius Severus, a consular beneficiary, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-06100486.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Billianius Severus, who held the position of a consular beneficiary.

EDCS-06100487.text_edition: Silvano  Lucius Titiusnus |(centurio) cohortis I miliariae libens posuit
EDCS-06100487.translation: To Silvanus, Lucius Titiusnus, centurion of the First Millenary Cohort, willingly placed this.
EDCS-06100487.summary: This is a dedicatory altar or monument set up by a centurion to the god Silvanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-07000813.text_edition: sacerdotium C onius Luci filius Sergia Fronto us rei publicae
EDCS-07000813.translation: The priesthood of C. Onius Fronto, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, to the republic.
EDCS-07000813.summary: This inscription records the priesthood of an individual named C. Onius Fronto, possibly commemorating his service to the local community or state.

EDCS-07400592.text_edition: Valeriae Pudentilles sic nnrum XXXV Titus Flavius Maxiarcus coniugi bene merenti posuit item Flavius Bennus et Flavia Sabina filius et filia eius
EDCS-07400592.translation: To Valeria Pudentilla, who lived for 35 years. Titus Flavius Maxiarcus placed this for his well-deserving wife, and likewise Flavius Bennus and Flavia Sabina, his son and daughter, placed this.
EDCS-07400592.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Titus Flavius Maxiarcus for his wife Valeria Pudentilla, with their children Flavius Bennus and Flavia Sabina also participating in the dedication.

EDCS-07400593.text_edition: Dis Manibus Liciniae matri annorum XL Arpagio fratri annorum VI Valerius posuit filius piissimus mitri sic et fratri
EDCS-07400593.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Licinia, his mother, aged 40 years, and to Arpagius, his brother, aged 6 years. Valerius, her most dutiful son, placed this for his mother, and likewise for his brother.
EDCS-07400593.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Valerius, a dutiful son, commemorates his mother Licinia and his brother Arpagius.

EDCS-07400594.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ammatisae vixit annos XXX Canius Dasas matri fecit pro pietate
EDCS-07400594.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Ammatisa, who lived for 30 years. Canius Dasas made this for his mother out of piety.
EDCS-07400594.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Canius Dasas to his mother Ammatisa, expressing his filial piety.

EDCS-07400595.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Ulpia Victorina pro salute sua posuit
EDCS-07400595.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Ulpia Victorina placed this for her own well-being.
EDCS-07400595.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Liber Pater, set up by Ulpia Victorina for her personal health or safety.

EDCS-07400597.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to a person of high status, possibly a senator named Lucius Verus Maximus.

EDCS-07400600.translation: Paio, daughter of Totius Implanti, Cuio.
EDCS-07400600.summary: This is a brief, likely funerary, inscription identifying a woman named Paio, daughter of Totius Implanti, also referred to as Cuio.

EDCS-07400601.text_edition: Daico Samuio Versonis filia annorum III
EDCS-07400601.translation: Daico Samuio, daughter of Verson, aged 3 years.
EDCS-07400601.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Daico Samuio, identifying her parent and age at death.

EDCS-07400602.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lottiae Dracontillae Lotteius Leo et Lottia Cereia filiae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XIIII menses IX parentes posuerunt
EDCS-07400602.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lottia Dracontilla, their most unhappy daughter, who lived for 14 years and 9 months. Her parents, Lotteius Leo and Lottia Cereia, placed this.
EDCS-07400602.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents, Lotteius Leo and Lottia Cereia, for their deceased daughter, Lottia Dracontilla, who died at a young age.

EDCS-07400604.text_edition: Apolleni sic Aurelius Iullianus sic centurio libens possuit sic
EDCS-07400604.translation: To Apollo, Aurelius Iullianus, a centurion, willingly placed this.
EDCS-07400604.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Apollo by Aurelius Iullianus, a centurion.

EDCS-08300491.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Publius Plotius |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08300491.translation: Sacred to Hercules, Publius Plotius, centurion of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08300491.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Hercules by Publius Plotius, a centurion of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-08500496.text_edition: Silvano Augusto Marcus Aurelius Maximus libens posuit
EDCS-08500496.translation: To Silvanus Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Maximus willingly placed this.
EDCS-08500496.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Marcus Aurelius Maximus.

EDCS-08500497.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Publius Volusius Se
EDCS-08500497.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publius Volusius Se[...].
EDCS-08500497.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Volusius Se[...], likely the beginning of a larger text.

EDCS-08500498.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Valerius Severianus veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-08500498.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Valerius Severianus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-08500498.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Valerius Severianus, a veteran, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-08500499.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Aurelius Licinius IIvir posuit
EDCS-08500499.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Aurelius Licinius, a duumvir, placed this.
EDCS-08500499.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Aurelius Licinius, who held the office of duumvir.

EDCS-08500501.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Scenutae quae vixit annos XXXV Aurelius Plares coniugi karissmae et Lavius et Delmanna filiae pientissimae et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-08500501.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Scenuta, who lived 35 years. Aurelius Plares to his dearest wife, and Lavius and Delmanna, her most devoted daughters, placed this for themselves while living.
EDCS-08500501.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Aurelia Scenuta, erected by her husband Aurelius Plares and her daughters Lavius and Delmanna, also serving as a burial monument for themselves.

EDCS-08500502.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali Aurelius Lavius et Aelia Tito libentes posuerunt
EDCS-08500502.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Cohortalis, Aurelius Lavius and Aelia Tito willingly placed this.
EDCS-08500502.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis by Aurelius Lavius and Aelia Tito.

EDCS-08500503.text_edition: Dis Manibus Severinus veteranus vixit annos XIX Temantia mater et fratres posuerunt
EDCS-08500503.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Severinus, a veteran, lived 19 years. Temantia his mother and his brothers placed this.
EDCS-08500503.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Severinus, a veteran who died at age 19, erected by his mother Temantia and his brothers.

EDCS-08500505.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Flaviae Marcellae quae vixit annos XXXIV Nantius Sexti coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-08500505.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Flavia Marcella, who lived 34 years. Nantius, son of Sextus, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08500505.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Flavia Marcella, who lived 34 years, dedicated by her husband Nantius, son of Sextus.

EDCS-08500506.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Cipio Fausto et Frunitae filiae Lucius Paconius Barbario cognatus et Luria Frunita fratri et nepti bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-08500506.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Lucius Cipius Faustus and his daughter Frunita. Lucius Paconius Barbario, his relative, and Luria Frunita erected this for their well-deserving brother and niece.
EDCS-08500506.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by relatives to Lucius Cipius Faustus and his daughter Frunita.

EDCS-08500507.text_edition: Quinto Valerio Quadrato annorum LXI Lucius Valerius Celer
EDCS-08500507.translation: For Quintus Valerius Quadratus, 61 years old. Lucius Valerius Celer [erected this].
EDCS-08500507.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by Lucius Valerius Celer, for Quintus Valerius Quadratus, who died at 61 years old.

EDCS-08500508.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Cornificius Verus libens voto posuit
EDCS-08500508.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Cornificius Verus willingly fulfilled his vow and placed this.
EDCS-08500508.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Cornificius Verus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08600576.text_edition: Lucius Gaviarius Luci filius Titi nepos augur Caius Iulius Caesaris libertus Meges IIviri quinquennales portam et turrim ex decreto decurionum de pecunia publica reficiundas coeraverunt eidemque probaverunt constat HS |||(trium milium)?D
EDCS-08600576.translation: Lucius Gaviarius, son of Lucius, grandson of Titus, augur; Caius Iulius Meges, freedman of Caesar, quinquennial duoviri, supervised the restoration of the gate and tower from public money, by decree of the decurions, and also approved them. The cost amounted to 3500 sesterces.
EDCS-08600576.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a city gate and tower, overseen and approved by Lucius Gaviarius and Caius Iulius Meges, quinquennial duoviri, using public funds as decreed by the decurions, with the cost amounting to 3500 sesterces, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08600577.text_edition: Lucius Gaviarius Luci filius Titi nepos augur Caius Iulius Caesaris libertus Meges IIviri quinquennales murum ex decreto decurionum reficiundum curaverunt isdemque probaverunt constat HS ||(quattuor milium)CC
EDCS-08600577.translation: Lucius Gaviarius, son of Lucius, grandson of Titus, augur; Caius Iulius Meges, freedman of Caesar, quinquennial duoviri, supervised the restoration of the wall by decree of the decurions and also approved it. The cost amounted to 4200 sesterces.
EDCS-08600577.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a city wall, overseen and approved by Lucius Gaviarius and Caius Iulius Meges, quinquennial duoviri, using public funds as decreed by the decurions, with the cost amounting to 4200 sesterces, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08900510.text_edition: Prosalute ddominorum [nnostrorum Imperatoris] Caesaris Gai Iuli Veri Maximini Augusti et Gai Iuli Veri M[aximi] nobilissimi Caesaris res publica Naronitanorum
EDCS-08900510.translation: For the health of our lords, the Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Augustus, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, the public community of Narona [erected this].
EDCS-08900510.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the public community of Narona for the well-being of the emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, likely dating to 236-238 AD.

EDCS-08900511.translation: Pusilla Allia, daughter of Spurius, lies here.
EDCS-08900511.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Pusilla Allia.

EDCS-08900512.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippi sic Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-08900512.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus, the Pious, Fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-08900512.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Iulius Philippus (Philip the Arab), likely dating to 244-249 AD.

EDCS-08900513.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar Augusti
EDCS-08900513.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, acclaimed Imperator for the third time, Consul, Father of the Country, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Augusti [erected this].
EDCS-08900513.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, listing their imperial titles and dating to 236 AD, based on Maximinus's tribunician power and acclamations.

EDCS-08900514.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto et Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari XXIIII
EDCS-08900514.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus, the Pious, Fortunate Augustus, and Marcus Iulius Philippus, most noble Caesar. [Dedicated in the year] 24.
EDCS-08900514.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Iulius Philippus (Philip the Arab) and his son Marcus Iulius Philippus, likely dating to around 247 AD based on the '24' which could refer to an imperial year or age.

EDCS-08900521.text_edition: Cornificius aro nomine suo et Cai Cornifici Cornificiae Gavillae filiorum aedem ae faciendam curavit idemque dedicavit et populo decurio adlectus
EDCS-08900521.translation: Cornificius, on his own behalf and on behalf of his sons Caius Cornificius and Cornificia Gavilla, saw to the construction of the temple and dedicated it; and he was co-opted as a decurion by the people.
EDCS-08900521.summary: This inscription records Cornificius's dedication of a temple, undertaken with his children, and his co-option as a decurion, likely dating from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08900522.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelia Masso  Publio Aelio Victori  Proculo defuncto nnorum L  Publio Aelio Victorino filio defuncto nnorum X mensium VI dulcissimo et marito optimo viva bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-08900522.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Masso, while alive, placed this for Publius Aelius Victor Proculus, who died at 50 years old, and for her most sweet son Publius Aelius Victorinus, who died at 10 years and 6 months old, and for her excellent husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-08900522.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelia Masso for her deceased husband, Publius Aelius Victor Proculus, and their young son, Publius Aelius Victorinus, honoring their memory.

EDCS-08900523.text_edition: Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio divi Iuli nepoti Augusto pontifici maximo consuli IIII imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXV Lucio Volusio Saturnino legato pro praetore
EDCS-08900523.translation: To Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, consul for the fourth time, imperator for the eighth time, holding tribunician power for the twenty-fifth time, [and] to Lucius Volusius Saturninus, legate pro praetore.
EDCS-08900523.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Tiberius, detailing his imperial titles and honors, and also mentions Lucius Volusius Saturninus as his legate, placing its date around 23-24 CE.

EDCS-08900524.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelio Celso coniugi bene merenti con sic quo vixit annos XXXII et Viviae L defunctae annorum LIII Ulpia Successa et sibi vivae posuit
EDCS-08900524.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ulpia Successa, while alive, placed this for Aelius Celsus, her well-deserving husband, with whom she lived for 32 years, and for Vivia L., who died at 53 years old, and for herself.
EDCS-08900524.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Ulpia Successa for her deceased husband, Aelius Celsus, her deceased relative Vivia L., and for herself, indicating a burial plot for multiple individuals.

EDCS-08900525.translation: To Liber Pater Augustus. Cordius Ursinus.
EDCS-08900525.summary: This is a simple dedicatory inscription by Cordius Ursinus to the god Liber Pater, conflated with the imperial cult as 'Augustus'.

EDCS-08900526.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aplis Pantonis sibi et Catoni uxori vivus fecit
EDCS-08900526.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aplis Pantonis, while living, made this for himself and for his wife Cato.
EDCS-08900526.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aplis Pantonis during his lifetime for himself and his wife, Cato, indicating a prepared tomb or memorial.

EDCS-08900527.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Trito viva sibi posuit
EDCS-08900527.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelia Trito, while living, placed this for herself.
EDCS-08900527.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb or memorial prepared by Aurelia Trito for her own future use while she was still alive.

EDCS-08900529.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Macximae sic quae vixit annos XX et Maustae quae vixit annos IIII sorori et filiae defunctis et Celtemioni matri vivae Surilla suis et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-08900529.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Surilla, while alive, placed this for her own people and for herself, for Maxima, who lived for 20 years, and for Mausta, who lived for 4 years, her deceased sister and daughter, and for her mother Celtemio, who is alive.
EDCS-08900529.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Surilla for her deceased sister Maxima and her deceased daughter Mausta, her living mother Celtemio, and for herself, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-08900530.text_edition: Dis Manibus Narens vixit annos XXXVIII Amacae Aeliae Pantonis serva viva sibi aet sic conpari posuit
EDCS-08900530.translation: To the Divine Manes. Narens lived for 38 years. Amaca Aelia Pantonis, a slave, while living, placed this for herself and for her companion.
EDCS-08900530.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a living female slave, Amaca Aelia Pantonis, for Narens, who lived 38 years, and for herself and her companion, indicating a shared burial space.

EDCS-08900531.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Optatus vixit annos LXX Statia Lavo viva sibi et Patae nepti suae quae vixit annos V
EDCS-08900531.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Optatus lived for 70 years. Statia Lavo, while living, placed this for herself and for her granddaughter Pata, who lived for 5 years.
EDCS-08900531.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Statia Lavo during her lifetime for Valerius Optatus, who lived 70 years, for her granddaughter Pata, who lived 5 years, and for herself, signifying a family burial.

EDCS-08900532.text_edition: Iaetto viva matri Dusoni defunctae annorum LXX et Ambae defunctae annorum XII posuit
EDCS-08900532.translation: Iaetto, while alive, placed this for her deceased mother Dusonia, who lived for 70 years, and for Amba, who died at 12 years of age.
EDCS-08900532.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iaetto for her mother and Amba, likely a younger relative, during her own lifetime.

EDCS-08900533.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Secundus Augusti libertus AT et Albia Ingenua cum Secundina filia ex voto pientissimi posuerunt
EDCS-08900533.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Secundus, freedman of Augustus, AT, and Albia Ingenua, with their daughter Secundina, placed this as a most pious vow.
EDCS-08900533.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Secundus (an imperial freedman), Albia Ingenua, and their daughter Secundina, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-08900534.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Severo Celsiano qui vixit annos LXXX Aurelius Plares filius patri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-08900534.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Aurelius Severus Celsianus, who lived for 80 years. Aurelius Plares, his son, placed this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-08900534.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Plares for his father, Titus Aurelius Severus Celsianus, who lived to an advanced age.

EDCS-08901077.text_edition: Lucius Gavius Luci filius Publilia Verona veteranus legionis XV se vivo fecit et Marco Gavio fratri et liberto Hilaro
EDCS-08901077.translation: Lucius Gavius, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, from Verona, a veteran of the Fifteenth Legion, made this in his own lifetime, also for his brother Marcus Gavius and his freedman Hilarus.
EDCS-08901077.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Lucius Gavius, a veteran, during his lifetime for himself, his brother Marcus Gavius, and his freedman Hilarus.

EDCS-08901080.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Granio Luci filio Proclino decurioni coloniae Aequensium flamini beneficiario consularis legionis XIIII geminae istupendiorum sic XIIII defuncto nnorum XXXVIIII Epidia Procula mater filio dulcissimo et sibi libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum in fronte pedes XL in agro pedes XL
EDCS-08901080.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Granius, son of Lucius, Proclinus, decurion of the colony of Aequensium, flamen, beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Gemina Legion, having served 14 stipends, who died at 39 years of age. Epidia Procula, his mother, placed this for her sweetest son, and for herself, her freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants, measuring 40 feet in front and 40 feet in depth.
EDCS-08901080.summary: This is a detailed funerary inscription set up by Epidia Procula for her son, Lucius Granius Proclinus, a civic official and veteran, also providing space for herself and her freedpeople, indicating a substantial burial plot.

EDCS-08901081.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Titus Flavius Pompeius |(centurio) cohortis III Alpinorum Antoninianae curam agens FAB AMP MEN et Vibius Vibianus  protector consularis
EDCS-08901081.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Pompeius, centurion of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum Antoniniana, acting as FAB AMP MEN, and Vibius Vibianus, protector consularis.
EDCS-08901081.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by Titus Flavius Pompeius, a centurion, and Vibius Vibianus, a protector consularis, likely related to military activities or a personal vow.

EDCS-08901082.translation: To Asclepius. Valeria Chrysis fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-08901082.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to Asclepius, made by Valeria Chrysis in fulfillment of a vow, likely for healing.

EDCS-08901084.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Paconiae Montanae Risiniensi quae vixit annos XXXV Aurelia Panto G mater filiae dulcissimae et sibi vivae infelicissimae posuit
EDCS-08901084.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Paconia Montana Risiniensis, who lived for 35 years. Aurelia Panto G, her most unhappy mother, placed this for her sweetest daughter and for herself while alive.
EDCS-08901084.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Panto G for her deceased daughter, Paconia Montana Risiniensis, and also for herself while still living, expressing profound maternal grief.

EDCS-09001555.summary: This is a short dedicatory inscription to the goddess Isis, offered by Raecia Marcella.

EDCS-09001560.text_edition: Romae Augustae sacrum Cornelia Nobilis votum solvit
EDCS-09001560.translation: Sacred to Roma Augusta. Cornelia Nobilis fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09001560.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the deified personification of Rome and the Imperial cult (Roma Augusta), made by Cornelia Nobilis in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09001561.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pius conpar titulum posuit atque vixit con sic illo annos VIII diem I cecidit annorum XXIII Crispinilla
EDCS-09001561.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pius, his comrade, set up this monument and lived with him for 8 years and 1 day. Crispinilla died at 23 years of age.
EDCS-09001561.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Pius for Crispinilla, noting their shared life and her age at death.

EDCS-09200514.text_edition: Lucius Didius Luci libertus Eros Didia Luci liberta Arbuscula Didia Luci liberta Auca havete et tu
EDCS-09200514.translation: Lucius Didius Eros, freedman of Lucius; Didia Arbuscula, freedwoman of Lucius; Didia Auca, freedwoman of Lucius: Hail, and to you too.
EDCS-09200514.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely for a group of freed individuals from the Didia gens, offering a greeting to the departed and the reader.

EDCS-09200515.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Minidia Quintina vixit nnos XXX Lucius Apertio vixit annos XVIII - Apertio Felix vixit annos XVIII quibus memoriam fecit Quintus Apertio Dexter coniugi et filiis suis
EDCS-09200515.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Minidia Quintina lived 30 years. Lucius Apertio lived 18 years. Apertio Felix lived 18 years. For these, Quintus Apertio Dexter made this memorial for his wife and his sons.
EDCS-09200515.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Apertio Dexter to his wife, Minidia Quintina, and his two sons, Lucius Apertio and Apertio Felix.

EDCS-09300007.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Titus Aurelius Severus libens posuit
EDCS-09300007.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Titus Aurelius Severus willingly set this up.
EDCS-09300007.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Titus Aurelius Severus to the god Mithras, likely from a Mithraeum.

EDCS-09300467.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription or dedication to an individual named Raecius Eumolpus.

EDCS-09300468.text_edition: Caio Iulio Celeri Murgiae Risinitano Marcus Prifernius Laetus amico bene merenti
EDCS-09300468.translation: To Gaius Julius Celer of Murgia, a Risinitan. Marcus Prifernius Laetus to his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-09300468.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Prifernius Laetus to his friend, Gaius Julius Celer, who was from Murgia and a Risinitan citizen.

EDCS-09300469.text_edition: Flavius Narcissianus Lupae coniugi carissimae et dulcissimae bene merenti vixit nnos XXII menses V dies XXI
EDCS-09300469.translation: To Flavia Narcissianus, his dearest and sweetest, well-deserving wife, Lupa, who lived 22 years, 5 months, 21 days.
EDCS-09300469.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Narcissianus to his beloved wife, Lupa, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09300470.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Liciniae Inttiae quae vixit annos XXXXV menses VIIII dies XIIII Licinia Dextrilia matri pientissimae posuit
EDCS-09300470.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Licinia Inttia, who lived 45 years, 9 months, 14 days. Licinia Dextrilia set this up for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-09300470.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Licinia Dextrilia to her mother, Licinia Inttia, detailing her mother's age at death.

EDCS-09300471.text_edition: Dis Manibus  CaeselIliae#Caeselliae#CAESEILIAE Victoriae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit mecum nnos XXIIII Caesellii Chrysanthianus maritus et Chrysogonus filius bene merenti
EDCS-09300471.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Caesellia Victoria, his incomparable wife, who lived with me for 24 years. Caesellius Chrysanthianus, her husband, and Chrysogonus, her son, to the well-deserving.
EDCS-09300471.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caesellius Chrysanthianus and his son Chrysogonus to Caesellia Victoria, beloved wife and mother, noting the duration of her marriage.

EDCS-09300472.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Clemens Iunior IIvir et quinquennalis CTI veteranus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-09300472.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Publius Aelius Clemens Junior, duumvir and quinquennalis, a veteran of CTI, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09300472.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Publius Aelius Clemens Junior, holding municipal offices and a veteran, who fulfilled a vow to the god Mithras.

EDCS-09301171.text_edition: Dis Manibus Romula mater filiae pientissimae posuit
EDCS-09301171.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Romula, the mother, placed this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-09301171.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a mother, Romula, dedicates a monument to her deceased daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09301172.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Mascellioni simo sermano Surio rimano  renti O  Germano R M H
EDCS-09301172.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Mascellio, a very honest German Surius Rimanus, Rentius O. Germanus R.M.H.
EDCS-09301172.summary: This fragmented inscription is a funerary dedication to Mascellio, possibly by a German individual, though much of the original context is lost due to illegibility.

EDCS-09400588.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Euphemo bene merenti Crispinius Valentinus adfinis
EDCS-09400588.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Crispinius Valentinus, a relative, [placed this] for Julius Euphemus, who deserved well.
EDCS-09400588.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Crispinius Valentinus, a relative, to Julius Euphemus, commemorating his good deeds.

EDCS-09400589.text_edition: Apollini Augusto sacrum Aurelius Verus decurio equitum cohortis III Alpinorum iterata statione  libens posuit
EDCS-09400589.translation: Sacred to Apollo Augustus. Aurelius Verus, a decurion of the cavalry of the Third Alpine cohort, on repeated guard duty, willingly placed this.
EDCS-09400589.summary: This is a votive inscription to Apollo Augustus, dedicated by Aurelius Verus, a cavalry decurion, likely celebrating a successful period of military service.

EDCS-09400592.text_edition: Verzo Balierius Plati filius decurio vivus fecit sibi et Platori Balierio Plati filio decurioni fratri et Sextoni matri suae defunctis
EDCS-09400592.translation: Verzo Balierius, son of Platus, a decurion, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Plator Balierius, son of Platus, a decurion, his brother, and for Sextona, his mother, who are deceased.
EDCS-09400592.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Verzo Balierius for himself during his lifetime, and in memory of his deceased brother Plator Balierius and his mother Sextona.

EDCS-09401518.text_edition: V Seneca vixit annis XXV Marcus Ulpius Severus F N bene merenti posuit
EDCS-09401518.translation: V. Seneca lived 25 years. Marcus Ulpius Severus F.N. placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-09401518.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Seneca at 25 years old, set up by Marcus Ulpius Severus.

EDCS-09500129.text_edition: et Silvano deo conservatori sacrum pro salute Maximi Or  et Maximi Maximiani a militiis |(centuriae) IIIIC ex voto succepto sic
EDCS-09500129.translation: And sacred to Silvanus, the preserving god, for the health of Maximus Or... and Maximus Maximianus, from the soldiers of the 400th century, from a vow undertaken, thus.
EDCS-09500129.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus for the health of Maximus Or... and Maximus Maximianus, made by soldiers of a specific century in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09500130.text_edition: Titus Flavius manius OCnia Eucarpia Silvestri Silvano ex voto
EDCS-09500130.translation: Titus Flavius Manius Ocnia Eucarpia Silvestri [placed this] for Silvanus, from a vow.
EDCS-09500130.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus, dedicated by Titus Flavius Manius Ocnia Eucarpia Silvestri in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-09500131.text_edition: Bato Peiius Silvano donum dedit libens merito
EDCS-09500131.translation: Bato Peiius willingly and deservedly gave this gift to Silvanus.
EDCS-09500131.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a gift given to the god Silvanus by Bato Peiius, indicating a personal devotion.

EDCS-10000180.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Conservatori sacrum Publius Aelius Pladomenus posuit
EDCS-10000180.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Conservator. Publius Aelius Pladomenus placed this.
EDCS-10000180.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Conservator by Publius Aelius Pladomenus, likely in gratitude for preservation or safety.

EDCS-10000185.text_edition: Serapidi et Isidi Marcus Ulpius Gellianus eques Romanus curator Arbensium Metulensium Splonistarum Malvesatium
EDCS-10000185.translation: To Serapis and Isis, Marcus Ulpius Gellianus, Roman knight, curator of the Arbenses, Metulenses, Splonistae, and Malvesatii.
EDCS-10000185.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Egyptian deities Serapis and Isis by Marcus Ulpius Gellianus, a Roman knight holding curatorial positions for several communities.

EDCS-10000188.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Titus Aelius Lavianus IIvir et Cassius Firmus votum libentes merito solverunt
EDCS-10000188.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Titus Aelius Lavianus, duumvir, and Cassius Firmus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000188.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Hercules Augustus, made by Titus Aelius Lavianus, a duumvir, and Cassius Firmus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000189.text_edition: Libero Patri C Titus Aurelius Nigrinus sacerdos pecunia publica
EDCS-10000189.translation: To Liber Pater, Gaius Titus Aurelius Nigrinus, priest, with public money.
EDCS-10000189.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater by Gaius Titus Aurelius Nigrinus, a priest, funded by public money.

EDCS-10000194.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, specifically in his aspect as 'Cohortalis', likely by a military unit.

EDCS-10000196.text_edition: MalvI et Flavi- Bat parentibus posuit
EDCS-10000196.translation: Malvius and Flavius Bat made this for their parents.
EDCS-10000196.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Malvius and Flavius Bat in honor of their parents.

EDCS-10000197.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Petronius Maximus vivo fecit sibi et suis et Aeliae Rufinae uxori defunctae
EDCS-10000197.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Petronius Maximus made this during his lifetime for himself and his family, and for Aelia Rufina, his deceased wife.
EDCS-10000197.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by Lucius Petronius Maximus during his lifetime for himself, his family, and his deceased wife, Aelia Rufina.

EDCS-10000198.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Nepos filius et Aurelia Ursina coniunx titulum posuerunt Tito Aurelio Carvo veterano annorum LXX
EDCS-10000198.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Nepos, son, and Aurelia Ursina, wife, set up this monument for Titus Aurelius Carvo, a veteran of 70 years.
EDCS-10000198.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument set up by Titus Aurelius Nepos, the son, and Aurelia Ursina, the wife, in memory of Titus Aurelius Carvo, a 70-year-old veteran.

EDCS-10000199.text_edition: dus I  consul fetialis cum Publicia Quarta et Catiis Maximina C Clementino Clemente LI  us Afrodisius flamen
EDCS-10000199.translation: [---]dus I, consul, fetial priest, with Publicia Quarta and Catii Maximina, Gaius Clementinus, Clemens LI, us Afrodisius, flamen.
EDCS-10000199.summary: This inscription appears to list several individuals, including a consul, a fetial priest, and a flamen, potentially associated with a public or religious dedication.

EDCS-10000200.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Paula viva sibi et
EDCS-10000200.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpia Paula, while living, for herself and [---].
EDCS-10000200.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument begun by Ulpia Paula during her lifetime for herself and possibly other family members.

EDCS-10000201.text_edition: Ego iustus olarius et manus meas rugetus sic et fetetus sic
EDCS-10000201.translation: I, Iustus the potter, my hands are wrinkled thus, and thus I made.
EDCS-10000201.summary: This inscription is an epitaph for a potter named Iustus, perhaps emphasizing his craft and age through a description of his hands.

EDCS-10000205.translation: Ingenuus, the son of Iigeus, made this.
EDCS-10000205.summary: This is a brief building or dedication inscription, recording that Ingenuus, son of Iigeus, made or erected something.

EDCS-10000212.text_edition: Zanati Ziraei filio defuncto nnum XXX Annaia Zanatis filia viro et filio et Plarens Ziraei filius patri et fratri
EDCS-10000212.translation: To Zanatis, son of Ziraeus, who died at the age of 30. Annaia, daughter of Zanatis, set this up for her husband and son, and Plarens, son of Ziraeus, for his father and brother.
EDCS-10000212.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Annaia to her deceased husband and son, and by Plarens to his father and brother, for Zanatis, son of Ziraeus, who died at age 30.

EDCS-10000213.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo consuli XI imperatori XI tribunic(ia) [potestate XI &
EDCS-10000213.translation: To Imperator Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine, Pontifex Maximus, Consul for the 11th time, Imperator for the 11th time, holding tribunician power for the 11th time, and...
EDCS-10000213.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Augustus, listing his titles and offices, dating it to around 23-22 BCE based on his eleventh consulate and tribunician power.

EDCS-10000214.text_edition: sacrum ob honorem IIviratus Cai Vibi Restituti Caius Iulius Maximus posuit
EDCS-10000214.translation: Sacred for the honor of the duumvirate of Caius Vibius Restitutus, Caius Iulius Maximus set this up.
EDCS-10000214.summary: This inscription records that Caius Iulius Maximus erected a monument or dedication in honor of Caius Vibius Restitutus's duumvirate, likely a municipal magistracy.

EDCS-10000215.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Septimiae Primae bene merenti posuit Glavius SI
EDCS-10000215.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Glavius set this up for Septimia Prima, who was well-deserving.
EDCS-10000215.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Glavius to Septimia Prima, a woman described as well-deserving.

EDCS-10000216.text_edition: Divo Augusto et Tiberio Caesari Augusti filio Augusto sacrum veterani pagi Scunastici quibus colonia Naronitana agros dedit  Divo Augusto et Tiberio Caesari Augusti filio Augusto sacrum veterani pagi Scunastici quibus colonia Naronitana agros dedit
EDCS-10000216.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus and to Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus. The veterans of the pagus Scunasticus, to whom the colonia Naronitana gave lands, dedicated this.
EDCS-10000216.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by veterans of the pagus Scunasticus, who received land from the colonia Naronitana, to the Divine Augustus and Tiberius Caesar Augustus, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-10000218.text_edition: Betulo Karonis filius domo Eguius miles cohortis III Alpinorum annorum XL stipendiorum XXII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Valerius signifer heres posuit
EDCS-10000218.translation: Betulus, son of Caro, from the home of Aeguia, a soldier of the III Alpine cohort, 40 years old, with 22 years of service, is buried here. Valerius, the standard-bearer, his heir, placed this as he ordered in his will.
EDCS-10000218.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Betulus, a soldier of the III Alpine cohort, erected by his heir, Valerius, according to Betulus's will.

EDCS-10000219.text_edition: eques cohortis III Alpinae domo Vercianus annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XIIII testamento fieri iussit heres posuit hic situs est
EDCS-10000219.translation: An equestrian of the III Alpine cohort, from the home of Vercianus, 38 years old, with 14 years of service, is buried here. His heir placed this as he ordered in his will.
EDCS-10000219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed cavalryman of the III Alpine cohort, erected by his heir according to his will.

EDCS-10000220.text_edition: Tito Flavio Blodi filio Plasso patri pientissimo annorum L et Flaviae Tattae matri annorum XXXX bene meritae et Tito Flavio Epicado fratri annorum XII et Tito Flavio Laedioni  aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo Naronae  Laedio filius vivos sic sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-10000220.translation: Laedio, the son, while still living, made this for himself and his family: for Titus Flavius Plassus, son of Blodus, his most devoted father, 50 years old; and for Flavia Tatta, his well-deserving mother, 40 years old; and for Titus Flavius Epicadus, his brother, 12 years old; and for Titus Flavius Laedio, aedile, quattuorvir with judicial power at Narona.
EDCS-10000220.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Laedio, son of Titus Flavius Plassus, for himself and his family, including his parents, brother, and himself as a municipal magistrate at Narona.

EDCS-10000223.summary: This brief inscription simply records the name Felix and his place of origin, Salona, likely from a personal item or a very short dedication.

EDCS-10000224.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription, likely marking the tomb of a woman named Martia Iulia Valeria Salona Felix.

EDCS-10000226.text_edition: Aurelio Valerino exceptori Impperatorum in officio memoriae qui aput sic civitatem Nicomediensium fati munus complevit vixsit sic annis XXXII et VI mensibus Aurelius Leontius vir docenarius sic et decurio coloniae Salonitanorum ex curatoribus eiusdem civitatis filio dulcissimo adque sic obsequentissimo pater infelix qui quod primo mihi hoc facere debuerat ego feci
EDCS-10000226.translation: To Aurelius Valerinus, excipient of the Emperors, in the office of memory, who fulfilled the duty of fate in this city of Nicomedia, he lived 32 years and 6 months. Aurelius Leontius, a centenarian and decurion of the colony of Salona, one of the curators of the same city, an unhappy father, who did what his son should have done for him first, I have done for him, to his sweetest and most obedient son.
EDCS-10000226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Valerinus, an imperial official in Nicomedia, erected by his father Aurelius Leontius, a prominent citizen of Salona, expressing deep sorrow and a sense of unnatural order of death.

EDCS-10000227.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a Gaius and containing an unclear series of abbreviations.

EDCS-10000228.text_edition: Tito Flavio Titi filio Trofimati Smyrnaeo
EDCS-10000228.translation: To Titus Flavius Trofimas, son of Titus, a Smyrnaean.
EDCS-10000228.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Titus Flavius Trofimas, noting his filiation and origin from Smyrna.

EDCS-10000229.text_edition: Maceria privata cum solo suo Sexti Gelli Primi
EDCS-10000229.translation: Private wall with its land, belonging to Sextus Gellius Primus.
EDCS-10000229.summary: This inscription records the ownership of a private wall and its associated land by Sextus Gellius Primus, possibly a boundary marker or property record.

EDCS-10000230.text_edition: Pontiae Ursulae infanti infelicissimae nnorum duum Aennia Lucida avia
EDCS-10000230.translation: To Pontia Ursula, a most unhappy infant of two years. Aennia Lucida, grandmother.
EDCS-10000230.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription for a two-year-old child, Pontia Ursula, dedicated by her grieving grandmother, Aennia Lucida.

EDCS-10000241.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Fabius Auctus.

EDCS-10000243.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numini Hippi fluminis Cnaeus Tullius Faventinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000243.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Divinity of the river Hippius, Gnaeus Tullius Faventinus, beneficiarius consularis of the First Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000243.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local river god Hippius, dedicated by Gnaeus Tullius Faventinus, a consular beneficiarius of Legion I Adiutrix, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000244.text_edition: Cnaeo Minicio Faustino Iulio Severo tribuno legionis XIIII geminae quaestori provinciae Macedoniae tribuno plebis praetori legato legionis XIIII geminae
EDCS-10000244.translation: To Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Iulius Severus, tribune of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, quaestor of the province of Macedonia, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-10000244.summary: This is an honorary inscription detailing the distinguished senatorial career of Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Iulius Severus, listing his various military and political offices, including multiple roles with the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-10000245.text_edition: Dasantillae Caminariae defunctae annorum IIII A
EDCS-10000245.translation: To Dasantilla Caminaria, deceased, aged four years.
EDCS-10000245.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Dasantilla Caminaria, who died at the young age of four years.

EDCS-10000247.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Arsae sorori enfilci sic Ioses memoria sic posuic sic
EDCS-10000247.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To my sister Arsa, the faithful one. Thus Ioses placed this memorial.
EDCS-10000247.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ioses to his deceased sister, Arsa.

EDCS-10000253.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Varroni filio annorum
EDCS-10000253.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aelius Varro, his son, aged [---].
EDCS-10000253.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Varro, likely by his parent.

EDCS-10000254.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute dd[d](ominorum) nn[n](ostrorum) Imperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici et Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti [et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris et] Iuliae Augustae matris castrorum et Augusti et Caesaris TTiti Fllavii Verecundus procurator Auggustorum nnostrorum et Callimorphus vilicus  XI Kalendas Maias Pompeiaiano et Avito consulibus
EDCS-10000254.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the well-being of our Lords, the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus, and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, [and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar, and] Julia Augusta, mother of the camps and of the Augustus and Caesar. Titus Flavius Verecundus, our procurator Augustorum, and Callimorphus, the vilicus, placed this on the 11th Kalends of May, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-10000254.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth for the health of the imperial family (Septimius Severus, Caracalla, Geta, and Julia Domna), placed by two imperial administrators, Verecundus and Callimorphus, and dated to April 21, 209 CE.

EDCS-10000255.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae matr=RTis#matris#MARTI castrorum sub cura Marci Iuli Macri viri egregii procuratoris Augusti Heliodorus vilicus officinae ferrariae posuit XI Kalendas Maias Modesto II et Probo consulibus
EDCS-10000255.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the well-being of our Lord, the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, and Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the camps. Under the care of Marcus Iulius Macer, a most distinguished man and procurator Augusti, Heliodorus, the superintendent of the ironworks, placed this on the 11th Kalends of May, in the consulship of Modestus II and Probus.
EDCS-10000255.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and his mother Julia Mamaea, set up by Heliodorus, a vilicus, under the direction of the procurator Marcus Iulius Macer, and dated to April 21, 228 CE.

EDCS-10000259.text_edition: Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati  Tatoniae Proculae nnorum XXXIII coniugi pitentissimae Marcus Aurelius Surus veteranus legionis X geminae ex beneficiario et MMarci AureIlii#Aurelii#AURILI Optatus et Firminianus matri infelicissimae et liberti Aurelii Baosus et Sumpa et Zipandus et Quintus et Caroni liberto |(obito) hheredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-10000259.translation: To the Divine Spirits and perpetual security. To Tatonia Procula, aged 33 years, a most devoted wife, Marcus Aurelius Surus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina and former beneficiarius, and Marcus Aurelius Optatus and Firminianus, to their most unhappy mother, and the freedmen Aurelius Baosus, Sumpa, Zipandus, Quintus, and Caron, to their freedman; the heirs ensured this was made.
EDCS-10000259.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tatonia Procula by her husband Marcus Aurelius Surus, her sons Optatus and Firminianus, and her freedmen and a freedman of Caron, ensuring her perpetual peace.

EDCS-10000260.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Matera annorum XXXV Aelius Quartus uxori fecit
EDCS-10000260.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Quartus made this for Ulpia Matera, his wife, aged 35 years.
EDCS-10000260.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aelius Quartus for his wife, Ulpia Matera.

EDCS-10000263.text_edition: Iovi Capitolino Victor decurio Del(minensium civitatis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000263.translation: To Jupiter Capitolinus. Victor, a decurion of the civitas of Delminium, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000263.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Capitolinus, placed by Victor, a decurion from the city of Delminium, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000264.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Gaius Trosius Cripus votum lib(ens) posuit la<e>tus donum merito solvit
EDCS-10000264.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Gaius Trosius Cripus willingly placed his vow, happily and deservedly fulfilling his gift.
EDCS-10000264.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules by Gaius Trosius Cripus in joyful fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000265.text_edition: Apriclus  Silvano Silvestri sacrum Montanus posuit
EDCS-10000265.translation: Apriclus. Montanus placed this sacred dedication to Silvanus Silvester.
EDCS-10000265.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Silvester by Montanus, for Apriclus.

EDCS-10000266.text_edition: Prevo Masio Platoris filia Platori filio feIcit#fecit#FICIT
EDCS-10000266.translation: Prevo Masio, daughter of Plator, made this for Plator, her son.
EDCS-10000266.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Prevo Masio for her son, Plator.

EDCS-10000267.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Pinsus GCermanicus#Germanicus#CERMANICUS Turi filius
EDCS-10000267.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Pinsus Germanicus, son of Turus, [made this offering].
EDCS-10000267.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Pinsus Germanicus, son of Turus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000268.text_edition: MAC Planus Verzulus  et Paio Terminio votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-10000268.translation: Mac Planus, Verzulus, and Paio Terminius willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000268.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording that Mac Planus, Verzulus, and Paio Terminius fulfilled a vow, likely to a deity or deities, as a 'votum solvit' formula suggests.

EDCS-10000269.translation: To Silvanus, Atisus deservedly [fulfilled his] vow.
EDCS-10000269.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to the god Silvanus by Atisus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000270.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Maximus Germanici libertus
EDCS-10000270.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester, Maximus, freedman of Germanicus.
EDCS-10000270.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Silvanus Silvester made by Maximus, a freedman of Germanicus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000271.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Mes(sori) sacrum Aneius Caecilius
EDCS-10000271.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester Messor, Aneius Caecilius [made this].
EDCS-10000271.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Silvanus Silvester Messor by Aneius Caecilius, honoring a specific aspect of the deity.

EDCS-10000272.text_edition: Aplini Bardurio Pladomeni filio annorum LXXX Turus etI#et#EI Sexto filis fecerunt
EDCS-10000272.translation: To Aplinus Bardurius, son of Pladomenus, 80 years old, Turus and Sextus, his sons, made this.
EDCS-10000272.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Turus and Sextus for their father, Aplinus Bardurius, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-10000273.text_edition: Aplis Staticus Aplinis filius nnum XXX hic situs est
