EDCS-10000273.translation: Aplus Staticus, son of Aplus, 30 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aplus Staticus, son of Aplus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10000274.text_edition: Baeio Cursulavia Lavi liberta nnorum C hic sita est
EDCS-10000274.translation: Baeio Cursulavia, freedwoman of Lavius, 100 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baeio Cursulavia, a freedwoman of Lavius, who lived to the advanced age of 100.

EDCS-10000275.text_edition: Bato Samuntius Pantis filius posuit sibi et contubernali suae Platino Avio Pantis filia RE
EDCS-10000275.translation: Bato Samuntius, son of Pantis, set this up for himself and for his partner Platino Avio, daughter of Pantis.
EDCS-10000275.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Bato Samuntius for himself and his female partner, Platino Avio, both being identified by their patronymics.

EDCS-10000276.text_edition: Celso Statica Bubantis XX liberta annorum XXV hic sita est
EDCS-10000276.translation: Celsus Statica Bubantis, freedwoman, 25 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000276.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celsus Statica Bubantis, a freedwoman, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10000277.text_edition: Daeco Scelepeta Pinentis filia annorum L hic sita est
EDCS-10000277.translation: Daeco Scelepeta, daughter of Pinens, 50 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000277.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Daeco Scelepeta, placed by an unspecified individual or simply noting her presence.

EDCS-10000279.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pladomenus Germanisci fecet sic filiae annorum
EDCS-10000279.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Pladomenus, son of Germaniscus, made this for his daughter, aged [---] years.
EDCS-10000279.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes, erected by Pladomenus Germanisci for his deceased daughter.

EDCS-10000280.text_edition: Prevo Samuntio Alesi filia fecit Marco Anio decurioni Setoviae annorum LXX
EDCS-10000280.translation: Prevo Samuntio, daughter of Alesi, made this for Marcus Anius, decurion of Setovia, 70 years old.
EDCS-10000280.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Prevo Samuntio to Marcus Anius, a decurion of Setovia.

EDCS-10000281.text_edition: Statanio et Tritanoni coniugi eius Panto Statania filia parentibus bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-10000281.translation: To Statanio and Tritanona, his wife, Panto Statania, their daughter, placed this for her well-deserving parents.
EDCS-10000281.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Panto Statania in honor of her parents, Statanio and Tritanona.

EDCS-10000283.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Stato Laprica, likely a funerary or dedicatory text.

EDCS-10000284.text_edition: Toto Sarnunia Statti filia annorum XXXV hic sita est
EDCS-10000284.translation: Toto Sarnunia, daughter of Statto, 35 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000284.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Toto Sarnunia, daughter of Statto.

EDCS-10000285.text_edition: Tritano Curbania Triti filia nnorum V
EDCS-10000285.translation: Tritano Curbania, daughter of Tritus, 5 years old.
EDCS-10000285.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Tritano Curbania, daughter of Tritus, who died young.

EDCS-10000286.translation: Turus Staticus, son of Tritus, to Vendo.
EDCS-10000286.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial by Turus Staticus, son of Tritus, to someone named Vendo.

EDCS-10000287.text_edition: Ursa Melandrica Aplinis filia annorum XXVI hic sita est
EDCS-10000287.translation: Ursa Melandrica, daughter of Aplinis, 26 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000287.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Ursa Melandrica, daughter of Aplinis.

EDCS-10000288.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely from a funerary monument, identifying a deceased individual named Quadratius Pladomenus.

EDCS-10000289.text_edition: o candricae Triti coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10000289.translation: To Candrica, wife of Tritus, who deserved well.
EDCS-10000289.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Tritus to his wife Candrica, praising her merits.

EDCS-10000291.text_edition: VIvir Augustalis atria Felicla ria Marci filia Secunda votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-10000291.translation: The Sevirs Augustalis, Atria, Felicla, and Ria, daughters of Marcus Secundus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000291.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by several women, including the Sevirs Augustalis, likely in thanks to a deity or for a public benefaction.

EDCS-10000292.text_edition: Oplus Aetoris filius Caeunus Ampliata Lucilla fecerunt
EDCS-10000292.translation: Oplus, son of Aetor, and Caeunus Ampliata Lucilla made this.
EDCS-10000292.summary: This inscription records an act of construction or dedication undertaken by Oplus and Caeunus Ampliata Lucilla.

EDCS-10000293.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary marker for a young boy named Lucius B, noting his age at death.

EDCS-10000297.text_edition: Vendo Verica Triti filia annorum XXX hic sita est
EDCS-10000297.translation: Vendo Verica, daughter of Tritus, 30 years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000297.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Vendo Verica, identifying her father and age at death.

EDCS-10000298.text_edition: Vadica Apli filia Titua viva fecit sibi et Pasinae Quinti filiae Voltisae matri suae adiutorio Aetoris et Ceuni Vadicis fecerunt
EDCS-10000298.translation: Vadica, daughter of Aplus Titua, made this while alive for herself and for Pasina, daughter of Quintus Voltisa, her mother; Aetor and Ceunus Vadicis made it with help.
EDCS-10000298.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument prepared by Vadica for herself and her mother Pasina while Vadica was still alive, with assistance from Aetor and Ceunus Vadicis.

EDCS-10000299.text_edition: Titus Octavius Cai filius Macer |(centurio) cohortis I Liburnorum Iano Patri votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000299.translation: Titus Octavius Macer, son of Gaius, centurion of the First Cohort of Liburnians, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Father Janus.
EDCS-10000299.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by a military officer, Titus Octavius Macer, to the god Janus.

EDCS-10000305.text_edition: Iulia Ceuni filia Luculla viva fecit sibi et Veturiae Ceuni filiae Paullae matri Sexto Iulio
EDCS-10000305.translation: Julia Luculla, daughter of Ceunus, made this while alive for herself and for Veturia Paulla, daughter of Ceunus, her mother, and for Sextus Julius.
EDCS-10000305.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument prepared by Julia Luculla while she was alive, intended for herself, her mother Veturia Paulla, and Sextus Julius.

EDCS-10000306.text_edition: Caius Iulius Ceuni filius Sergia Curticus Aetor pontem de sua pecunia fecit longum pedes CXXCVII latum pedes X
EDCS-10000306.translation: Gaius Julius Curticus Aetor, son of Ceunus, of the Sergian tribe, built a bridge at his own expense, 197 feet long and 10 feet wide.
EDCS-10000306.summary: This inscription records the public benefaction of a bridge construction, funded and executed by Gaius Julius Curticus Aetor.

EDCS-10000310.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Marci filiae Carae annorum XXV Aelia Iusta mater infelix hic sita est
EDCS-10000310.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julia Cara, daughter of Marcus, 25 years old. Aelia Iusta, her unhappy mother, lies here.
EDCS-10000310.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Julia Cara, set up by her grieving mother, Aelia Iusta, noting Julia's age at death.

EDCS-10000311.text_edition: CESMA Saturninus coniungi sic fecit hic sita est
EDCS-10000311.translation: Cesma Saturninus made this for his spouse; she is laid to rest here.
EDCS-10000311.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Cesma Saturninus created a monument for his deceased wife.

EDCS-10000319.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Valerianus Probi filius annorum X hic situs est
EDCS-10000319.translation: Quintus Valerius Valerianus, son of Probus, aged 10 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10000319.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Valerius Valerianus, a young boy, erected by or on behalf of his family.

EDCS-10000320.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Secundi Turrini Muntani fili obiti annorum IX hic situs est
EDCS-10000320.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundus Turrinus, son of Muntanus, who passed away at 9 years of age, is buried here.
EDCS-10000320.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Secundus Turrinus, a child who died at the age of nine.

EDCS-10000321.text_edition: adio pi annorum rtus us monumentum posuit
EDCS-10000321.translation: [---] Adio Pi, aged [---] years, [---] Ertus Us placed this monument.
EDCS-10000321.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for an individual named Adio Pi, with the dedicator's name partially preserved as Ertus Us.

EDCS-10000322.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription, likely for a child named Probus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-10000323.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, with only a portion of the deceased's name preserved.

EDCS-10000329.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo C T P
EDCS-10000329.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. C. T. P. [initials likely of the dedicator]
EDCS-10000329.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, probably a votive offering, made by an individual whose initials are C.T.P.

EDCS-10000330.text_edition: Ditius Sestenius Frontonis filius annorum CV hic situs est
EDCS-10000330.translation: Ditius Sestenius, son of Fronto, aged 105 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10000330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ditius Sestenius, noting his remarkable age of 105 years at the time of his death.

EDCS-10000334.text_edition: Agape vedua sic pro se et suos sic fecit pedes CCC
EDCS-10000334.translation: Agape, a widow, made this for herself and her family, 300 feet.
EDCS-10000334.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Agape, a widow, prepared a burial plot or monument of 300 feet for herself and her family.

EDCS-10000337.text_edition: Bonae Deae dominae Heiae Augustae triumphali terrae marisque dominatrici conservatrici mentiumque bonarum ac remediorum potenti deae bene iudicanti Calpurnia Luci Pisonis auguris filia Cnaei Pisonis neptis donum dedit
EDCS-10000337.translation: To the Good Goddess, mistress Heia Augusta, triumphant, ruler of land and sea, preserver of good minds and powerful in remedies, the well-judging goddess, Calpurnia, daughter of Lucius Piso the augur, granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, gave this gift.
EDCS-10000337.summary: This is a votive inscription by Calpurnia, daughter of Lucius Piso and granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, dedicating a gift to the goddess Bona Dea, also identified as Heia Augusta, for her various attributes and powers.

EDCS-10000612.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Publio Cornelio Iulio qui vixit annos LV Iulia et Irene filias sic patri pientissimo
EDCS-10000612.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Publius Cornelius Julius, who lived for 55 years. Julia and Irene, his daughters, thus to their most devoted father.
EDCS-10000612.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Julia and Irene to their deceased father, Publius Cornelius Julius, who lived to be 55 years old.

EDCS-10000617.text_edition: Deae xanctae sic Nemesi et Genio municipii Flavius Saturninus bbeneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae libies sic posuit
EDCS-10000617.translation: To the holy goddess Nemesis and the Genius of the municipality, Flavius Saturninus, consular beneficiary of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, freely placed this.
EDCS-10000617.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Flavius Saturninus, a military official, to the goddess Nemesis and the protective spirit of his municipality.

EDCS-10000618.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Aplini veter(ano qui vixit annos LXX et Aureliae Vendoni coniugi Titus Aurelius Turus parentibus piissimis posuit
EDCS-10000618.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Titus Aurelius Aplin, a veteran, who lived for 70 years, and to Aurelia Vendo, his wife. Titus Aurelius Turus placed this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-10000618.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Aurelius Turus to his parents, the veteran Titus Aurelius Aplin and his wife Aurelia Vendo.

EDCS-10000619.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Maximus Argenianus decurio municipii vivus sibi posuit et Aureliae Maximinae filiae quae vixit annos XXX
EDCS-10000619.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelius Maximus Argenianus, decurion of the municipality, placed this while alive for himself and for Aurelia Maximin, his daughter, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-10000619.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelius Maximus Argenianus, a municipal decurion, for himself during his lifetime and for his daughter, Aurelia Maximina, who died at age 30.

EDCS-10000620.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Vendoni quae vixit annos XXX Aurelius Plarens coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000620.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Aurelia Vendo, who lived for 30 years. Aurelius Plarens placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10000620.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Plarens to his wife, Aurelia Vendo, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-10000621.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Proculae quae vixit annis XXVII Lucius Valens Or coniugi pientissimae posuit
EDCS-10000621.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Claudia Procula, who lived for 27 years. Lucius Valens Or placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-10000621.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Valens Or to his wife, Claudia Procula, who lived for 27 years.

EDCS-10000622.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aulo Gablio Liberali qui vixit annos XXIIX Flavius Aper beneficiarius consularis memoriam posuit
EDCS-10000622.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Aulus Gablius Liberalis, who lived for 28 years. Flavius Aper, a consular beneficiary, erected this memorial.
EDCS-10000622.summary: This is a funerary memorial erected by Flavius Aper, a military official, for Aulus Gablius Liberalis, who died at age 28.

EDCS-10000623.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Gav Durus Valeriano qui vixit
EDCS-10000623.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Gavius Durus Valerianus, who lived...
EDCS-10000623.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Gavius Durus Valerianus.

EDCS-10000625.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Paul Gaudene quae vixit annos XL Titus Pr R conpari pientissimae et sibi vivo posuit
EDCS-10000625.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Paul Gaudene, who lived for 40 years. Titus Pr[...] R[...] placed this for his most devoted companion and for himself while alive.
EDCS-10000625.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Pr[...] R[...] for his deceased companion, Paul Gaudene, and for himself while still living.

EDCS-10000626.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Paconio Barbaro decurioni municipii Risinii qui vixit annos XX menses III dies IIII Lucius Paconius Barbarus et Aurelia Panto filio inconparabili sic et sibi vivi infelicissimi posuerunt
EDCS-10000626.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Lucius Paconius Barbarus, decurion of the municipality of Risinium, who lived for 20 years, 3 months, 4 days. Lucius Paconius Barbarus and Aurelia Panto, his most unhappy parents, placed this for their incomparable son, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-10000626.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Paconius Barbarus and Aurelia Panto to their son, also named Lucius Paconius Barbarus, a municipal decurion who died young, and also for themselves while living.

EDCS-10000627.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Ratoniae Autumnae quae vixit annos XL Scardonitanae Asidonius Vitellianus |(centurio) cohortis II milliariae coniugi carissimae posuit
EDCS-10000627.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Ratonia Autumna of Scardona, who lived for 40 years. Asidonius Vitellianus, centurion of the Second Milliary Cohort, placed this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-10000627.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Asidonius Vitellianus, a centurion, to his beloved wife, Ratonia Autumna of Scardona, who died at age 40.

EDCS-10000628.text_edition: Dis Manibus Statiae Aspasiae  filiae Risinii vixit annos XL C Asclepiades coniugi  Statia Sensis matri fecerunt
EDCS-10000628.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Statia Aspasia, daughter of Risinius, who lived for 40 years. C. Asclepiades and Statia Sensis made this for his wife and her mother, respectively.
EDCS-10000628.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Statia Aspasia, daughter of Risinius, who lived 40 years, erected by C. Asclepiades for his wife and Statia Sensis for her mother.

EDCS-10000629.text_edition: mava sibi et Luscio Paridi coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000629.translation: Mava placed this for herself and for Lucius Paris, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10000629.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Mava for herself and for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Paris.

EDCS-10000630.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum monae quae vixit annos LXV C Piliae Despotanus aviae piae et matri vivae et R Piliae posuit
EDCS-10000630.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Mona, who lived for 65 years. C. Pilius Despotanus placed this for his pious grandmother and for his mother, who is still living, and for R. Pilius.
EDCS-10000630.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mona, who lived 65 years, set up by C. Pilius Despotanus for his pious grandmother and for his living mother, and for R. Pilius.

EDCS-10000631.text_edition: aepa et Paulae filiae et Sabinio Certo generi et Maximae neepotae Marcus Fulvinius Natalis bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-10000631.translation: Marcus Fulvinius Natalis placed this for Aepa and Paula, his daughters, and for Sabinius Certus, his son-in-law, and for Maxima, his granddaughter, they being well-deserving.
EDCS-10000631.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Fulvinius Natalis for his daughters Aepa and Paula, his son-in-law Sabinius Certus, and his granddaughter Maxima, acknowledging their good merits.

EDCS-10000635.text_edition: - stes viator qui SSOLISID Aurelius Proculus annorum LX vivus posuit ed sic dicit sit memoria sibi et suis parentibus et coniugi suae Ursaciae viventi quae habet annos XL Aurelius pater Rusticus vixit annos LXXV et Aurelia mater Victorinna vixit annos  L  Ursacia dixit frater Code precor magister adde ed sic meam matrem Proclinam viventem annorum X  in sepulcri sic sumus aeternam sic sedem sic quiescimus  CODDETCPFAVEMUS
EDCS-10000635.translation: May you stand, traveler, who (---) Aurelius Proculus, 60 years old, placed this while living, and thus he says: May there be memory for himself and his parents and his living wife Ursacia, who is 40 years old. His father Aurelius Rusticus lived 75 years, and his mother Aurelia Victorinna lived 50 years. Ursacia said: Brother Code, I beg, master, add thus my mother Proclina, living, 10 years old. In this tomb, thus we are, thus in eternal seat, thus we rest. (---)
EDCS-10000635.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelius Proculus while he was living, for himself, his parents Aurelius Rusticus and Aurelia Victorinna, and his living wife Ursacia, with an addition by Ursacia for her living mother Proclina, celebrating their collective resting place.

EDCS-10000636.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Maximus Magai votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000636.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Maximus Magai willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000636.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maximus Magai, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10000637.text_edition: Dis Manibus  tiae Balbae coniugi pientissimae annorum XVI item Aureliis Silvano et Propinquiano et Baetae et Iuniori filiis Titus Aurelius Silvanus decurio IIvir municipii Malvesiatium posuit
EDCS-10000637.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tiae Balba, his most devoted wife, 16 years old; likewise to Aurelii Silvanus and Propinquianus, and Baeta and Junior, his children. Titus Aurelius Silvanus, decurion, duumvir of the municipality of Malvesiatium, placed this.
EDCS-10000637.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a decurion and duumvir of Malvesiatium, for his most devoted wife Tiae Balba, who died at 16, and for his children Aurelii Silvanus, Propinquianus, Baeta, and Junior.

EDCS-10000638.translation: To Juno Regina. ACT willingly placed this.
EDCS-10000638.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Juno Regina, placed willingly by ACT.

EDCS-10000641.text_edition: Maximinus sculpet sic  Dis Manibus  Leo et Quartilla parentes miseri posuerunt Lupae filiae infelicissimae annorum XXII hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-10000641.translation: Maximinus will carve thus: To the Divine Shades. Leo and Quartilla, sorrowful parents, placed this monument for their most unfortunate daughter Lupa, 22 years old. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-10000641.summary: This is a funerary monument carved by Maximinus, set up by the grieving parents Leo and Quartilla for their most unfortunate daughter Lupa, who died at 22, with a clause that it is not inheritable.

EDCS-10000643.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Tironi mileti sic defuncto per | D E Valentina socero carissimo posuit bene merenti
EDCS-10000643.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelius Tiro, a soldier thus deceased, D. E. Valentina, his dearest father-in-law, placed this for his well-deserving relative.
EDCS-10000643.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by D. E. Valentina to his deceased son-in-law, Aurelius Tiro, a soldier, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000645.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Paentino vixit annos X Tranquillus frater posuit
EDCS-10000645.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Paentinus lived for 10 years. Tranquillus, his brother, placed this.
EDCS-10000645.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Tranquillus for his brother Paentinus, who died at the age of ten, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000646.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tertio annorum VIII parentes bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-10000646.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Tertius, aged 8 years, his parents made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-10000646.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents for their eight-year-old son Tertius, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000647.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quinti Egnati Clementis CCC
EDCS-10000647.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes of Quintus Egnatius Clemens, CCC.
EDCS-10000647.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Egnatius Clemens, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with an unclear numerical abbreviation.

EDCS-10000648.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  L Secundi vixit annos X CVIN VC
EDCS-10000648.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. L. Secundus lived for 10 years. CVIN VC.
EDCS-10000648.summary: This is a funerary inscription for L. Secundus, who lived for ten years, with an enigmatic abbreviation at the end, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000649.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Calpurnius Chresimus vixit annos XXXXV Aurelia Maximina MAIIIII
EDCS-10000649.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Calpurnius Chresimus lived for 45 years. Aurelia Maximina MAIIIII.
EDCS-10000649.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calpurnius Chresimus, who lived 45 years, mentioning Aurelia Maximina, and including an unclear abbreviation, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000650.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Luscio Luriano vixit annos XX Luscii Proculus et Lurianus Sancti filii Statia Sabina socrus immemoriam sic
EDCS-10000650.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Lucius Luscius Lurianus, who lived for 20 years. Luscii Proculus and Lurianus Sancti, his sons, and Statia Sabina, his mother-in-law, in memory thus.
EDCS-10000650.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Luscius Lurianus, who died at age 20, erected by his sons Proculus and Lurianus Sancti, and his mother-in-law Statia Sabina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000656.text_edition: Pomponiae Marci filiae Lepidae Lucius Sextilius Rufus coniugi bene merenti vivus fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-10000656.translation: Lucius Sextilius Rufus made this during his lifetime for Pomponia Lepida, daughter of Marcus, his well-deserving wife, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-10000656.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Sextilius Rufus while alive for his wife Pomponia Lepida, and also for himself and his family, indicating a pre-need burial plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000657.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Eulalae quae et Firma  bene merenti ia posuit
EDCS-10000657.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iulia Eulala, also known as Firma. [---] placed this for her well-deserving [relative/friend].
EDCS-10000657.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Eulala, also known as Firma, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with the dedicator's name partially lost.

EDCS-10000658.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument given the 'posuit' (placed) and mentions of personal names like Fulvia, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000659.text_edition: Lucio Umbrio Valenti Lusia Paula viro suo
EDCS-10000659.translation: To Lucius Umbrius Valens, his husband, Lusia Paula [dedicates this].
EDCS-10000659.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from a tombstone, dedicated by Lusia Paula to her deceased husband, Lucius Umbrius Valens.

EDCS-10000662.text_edition: Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus legatus pro praetore pontem et terminos renovari iussit per Titum Cassium Frontonem optionem legionis IIII Flaviae felicis in fundo Vesiio CN OSC D L V
EDCS-10000662.translation: Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, legate propraetor, ordered the bridge and boundary markers to be renovated through Titus Cassius Fronto, optio of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, on the Vesian estate. CN OSC D L V [unintelligible abbreviation].
EDCS-10000662.summary: This inscription records the renovation of a bridge and boundary markers by order of the provincial governor Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, carried out by an optio of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10000667.text_edition: sacrum ex voto solvit Caius Masurius Eutychus
EDCS-10000667.translation: Gaius Masurius Eutychus fulfilled a vow sacredly.
EDCS-10000667.summary: This is a votive inscription, recording that Gaius Masurius Eutychus fulfilled a sacred vow, likely for a deity.

EDCS-10000668.text_edition: Marcello veterano cohortis III Alpinorum nnorum LXX stipendiorum XLIII  Marcellae nnorum ae Cai libertae Iucundae
EDCS-10000668.translation: To Marcellus, veteran of the Third Cohort of Alpiners, 70 years old, with 43 years of service. To Marcella, years old, and to Caius, her freedwoman, Iucunda.
EDCS-10000668.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcellus, a veteran soldier, and mentions Marcella and Iucunda, likely family members or dependents.

EDCS-10000669.text_edition: Publius Clodius  Publius Mucius Dasius Sextus Tigidius Orestes Publius Terentius Dardanus Sextus Stenius Strato Caius Neratius Nothus IIIIIIviri ob honorem
EDCS-10000669.translation: Publius Clodius, Publius Mucius Dasius, Sextus Tigidius Orestes, Publius Terentius Dardanus, Sextus Stenius Strato, Gaius Neratius Nothus, sextumvirs, in honor of [something].
EDCS-10000669.summary: This inscription lists the names of six men, likely sextumviri (priests or municipal officials), commemorating an act performed in honor of something, possibly a public benefaction or a deity.

EDCS-10000671.text_edition: Publio Cornelio Proculo fratri Publio Cornelio Valenti patri Papiae Provinciae sorori  Lucilia Sperata suis posuit
EDCS-10000671.translation: To Publius Cornelius Proculus, her brother; to Publius Cornelius Valens, her father; to Papia Provincia, her sister. Lucilia Sperata placed this for her family.
EDCS-10000671.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucilia Sperata to her brother Publius Cornelius Proculus, her father Publius Cornelius Valens, and her sister Papia Provincia.

EDCS-10000672.text_edition: Fabia Silvina astrosa mater Quinto Iulio Clementino annorum XXX Quinto Iulio Feroci annorum VIII Quinto Iulio Severo annorum II Quinto Iulio Clementi coniugi viva fecit
EDCS-10000672.translation: Fabia Silvina, an unfortunate mother, while alive, made this for Quintus Iulius Clementinus, 30 years old; Quintus Iulius Ferox, 8 years old; Quintus Iulius Severus, 2 years old; and Quintus Iulius Clemens, her husband.
EDCS-10000672.summary: This is a funerary monument, likely a family tomb, prepared by Fabia Silvina during her lifetime for her three deceased sons and her husband.

EDCS-10000673.translation: To Verecundus and Secunda, Verecunda [dedicates this].
EDCS-10000673.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Verecunda to Verecundus and Secunda, likely family members.

EDCS-10000674.text_edition: nnorum X hic sit- est Lucius Aelius Firmus
EDCS-10000674.translation: Here lies Lucius Aelius Firmus, 10 years old.
EDCS-10000674.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Lucius Aelius Firmus, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-10000680.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Allio Lupiano puero infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XXVII menses VIII dies XV Allius Allianus C suo posuit
EDCS-10000680.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Allius Lupianus, a most unfortunate boy who lived for 27 years, 8 months, and 15 days. Allius Allianus C. placed this for his [son].
EDCS-10000680.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Allius Lupianus, a young man who died prematurely, erected by Allius Allianus for his son.

EDCS-10000681.text_edition: Fabricius et ia filiae ae Maximae? --- infelicissimae quae vixit
EDCS-10000681.translation: Fabricius and his daughter(s) to [his/their] daughter Maxima, most unfortunate, who lived [---]
EDCS-10000681.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young woman named Maxima, set up by her father Fabricius and possibly other family members, lamenting her untimely death.

EDCS-10000682.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Curiatio Eutycho defuncto nnorum plus nnis LV Cania Ursina coniugi incomparabili posuit
EDCS-10000682.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cania Ursina set this up for her incomparable husband, Titus Curiatius Eutychus, deceased at more than 55 years of age.
EDCS-10000682.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cania Ursina to her deceased husband, Titus Curiatius Eutychus, who lived to be over 55 years old.

EDCS-10000684.text_edition: PrA AT fato rapta hic sita est Caius Valerius Cai filius Dento veteranus legionis VII libertae suae posuit
EDCS-10000684.translation: Pra AT, snatched by fate, lies here. Gaius Valerius Dento, son of Gaius, a veteran of the VIIth Legion, set this up for his freedwoman.
EDCS-10000684.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Valerius Dento, a veteran, for his freedwoman, Pra AT, who died prematurely.

EDCS-10000685.text_edition: Marco Vinicio Marci filio Masuriano nnorum II dierum XVIII
EDCS-10000685.translation: To Marcus Vinicius Masurianus, son of Marcus, who lived for 2 years and 18 days.
EDCS-10000685.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Marcus Vinicius Masurianus, who died at the age of two years and eighteen days.

EDCS-10000689.text_edition: Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus cum coniuge et matre Spei sacrum
EDCS-10000689.translation: Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus with his wife and mother dedicated this sacred object to Spes (Hope).
EDCS-10000689.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Spes (Hope) by Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus, his wife, and his mother.

EDCS-10000691.text_edition: Tito Flavio Herennio Iasoni equiti Romano decurioni coloniae Salonitanae pontifici aedili praefecto collegii fabrum et centonariorum filio Herenni Iasonis viri egregii patroni coloniae Salonitanae ob amorem patriae quem ob merita sua consecutus est et erga honorificentiam quam civibus exhibet collegium supra scriptum
EDCS-10000691.translation: To Titus Flavius Herennius Iason, Roman knight, decurion of the colony of Salona, pontifex, aedile, prefect of the college of craftsmen and clothworkers, son of Herennius Iason, an outstanding man and patron of the colony of Salona, on account of his love for his homeland, which he attained through his merits, and for the honor he shows to the citizens, the above-written college [dedicates this].
EDCS-10000691.summary: This is an honorary inscription erected by a college of craftsmen and clothworkers in Salona for Titus Flavius Herennius Iason, a prominent local official and Roman knight, in recognition of his service and patriotism.

EDCS-10000692.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Defensori optioni classis praetoriae Misenatium natione Britto stipendiorum X vixit nnos XXX
EDCS-10000692.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Flavius Defensor, 'optio' of the Praetorian Fleet of Misenum, by nation a Briton, who served 10 years and lived 30 years.
EDCS-10000692.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Defensor, a Briton who served as an 'optio' in the Roman fleet at Misenum and died at the age of 30 after 10 years of service.

EDCS-10000693.text_edition: Dis Manibus Murcuio Iadiae militi cohortis I Belgarum annorum
EDCS-10000693.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Murcuius Iadiae, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Belgians, of [---] years.
EDCS-10000693.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Murcuius Iadiae, a soldier in the 1st Cohort of Belgians, whose age is not fully preserved.

EDCS-10000694.text_edition: Septimius dus Pirami sic miles cohortis III Alpinorum centuria Marcelli IESS hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Plares Annaei eques ex singularibus Augusti
EDCS-10000694.translation: Septimius [---] Pirami, a soldier of the 3rd Cohort of Alpini, in the century of Marcellus, lies here. He ordered this to be made by testament. Plares Annaeus, an eques from the Augustan 'singulares'.
EDCS-10000694.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius, a soldier of the 3rd Cohort of Alpini, buried at his own testamentary request, with Plares Annaeus, an equestrian of the Augustan 'singulares', possibly acting as executor.

EDCS-10000695.text_edition: Marcus Satrius Marci libertus Valens VIvir fecit sibi et Satriae Eufrosyne coniugi pientissimae et Satrio Floro filio Satriae Valentinae filiae libertis libertabusque eorum hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur commune est una cum Statorio Festo posterisque eorum et Calpurnio Callisto amico in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes L
EDCS-10000695.translation: Marcus Satrius Valens, freedman of Marcus, a 'sevir', made this monument for himself and for Satria Euphrosyne, his most devoted wife, and for his son Satrius Florus, and for his daughter Satria Valentina, and for their freedmen and freedwomen. This monument shall not pass to an heir. It is shared with Statorius Festus and their descendants, and with his friend Calpurnius Callistus. In front, 15 feet; in depth, 50 feet.
EDCS-10000695.summary: This is a funerary monument built by Marcus Satrius Valens, a 'sevir' and freedman, for himself, his wife, children, and freedpersons, also sharing rights with Statorius Festus and Calpurnius Callistus, with specified dimensions for the plot.

EDCS-10000696.text_edition: Flavius Crescentianus commea felicem A per propr transire C
EDCS-10000696.translation: Flavius Crescentianus, come happily, A, to pass through C by proper means.
EDCS-10000696.summary: This inscription records a somewhat obscure or cryptic message from Flavius Crescentianus, possibly related to travel or transition.

EDCS-10000697.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Victorinae defunctae nnorum XXV Marcus Aurelius Severus coniugi infelicissimae
EDCS-10000697.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelia Victorina, who died at the age of 25 years. Marcus Aurelius Severus to his most unhappy wife.
EDCS-10000697.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Severus to his deceased wife, Aelia Victorina, who died at a young age.

EDCS-10000698.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Annio Cosmo coniugi defuncto annorum
EDCS-10000698.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Annius Cosmus, his deceased husband, of [---] years.
EDCS-10000698.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Annius Cosmus, erected by his wife.

EDCS-10000700.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Curiatio Eutycho defuncto nnorum plus minus XXXV Curiatia Dioscorilla posuit se viva sibi et marito suo bene merenti
EDCS-10000700.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Curiatius Eutychus, who died at approximately 35 years of age. Curiatia Dioscorilla placed this while alive, for herself and for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10000700.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Curiatia Dioscorilla for her well-deserving husband, Titus Curiatius Eutychus, and also reserving a place for herself.

EDCS-10000701.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Caio Curtio Valenti Cornelia Euhodia coniugi bene merenti fecit
EDCS-10000701.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Euhodia made this for Gaius Curtius Valens, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10000701.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cornelia Euhodia to her well-deserving husband, Gaius Curtius Valens.

EDCS-10000702.text_edition: Flaviae Ursulae defunctae nnorum XXX Lucius Valerius Secundus coniugi
EDCS-10000702.translation: To Flavia Ursula, who died at the age of 30 years. Lucius Valerius Secundus to his wife.
EDCS-10000702.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Lucius Valerius Secundus for his deceased wife, Flavia Ursula.

EDCS-10000704.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pomponiae Hermetillae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit nnos plus minus XXV Pontianus coniugi incomparabili bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000704.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Pomponia Hermetilla, his most devoted wife, who lived for approximately 25 years. Pontianus placed this for his incomparable, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10000704.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Pontianus for his incomparable and well-deserving wife, Pomponia Hermetilla, who died young.

EDCS-10000705.text_edition: Pomponiae Veneriae et Aemilio Eucarpo Pomponia Nice parentibus Marco Aurelio Flavio coniugi incomparabili Aemilia Memoria uxor posuit cum quo vixitS#vixit#VIXIS annis XII
EDCS-10000705.translation: To Pomponia Veneria and Aemilius Eucarpus. Pomponia Nice to her parents. Aemilia Memoria, wife, placed this for her incomparable husband, Marcus Aurelius Flavius, with whom she lived for 12 years.
EDCS-10000705.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription where Pomponia Nice dedicates to her parents, Pomponia Veneria and Aemilius Eucarpus, and Aemilia Memoria dedicates to her incomparable husband, Marcus Aurelius Flavius, with whom she shared 12 years of marriage.

EDCS-10000706.text_edition: Dis Manibus Raetiniae Iadestinae matri piissimae Crescentilla filia bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000706.translation: To the Divine Manes. Crescentilla, daughter, placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving mother, Raetinia Iadestina.
EDCS-10000706.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Crescentilla to her most devoted and well-deserving mother, Raetinia Iadestina.

EDCS-10000707.text_edition: defunct-) annorum XXII Servilia Lea mater infelicissim-
EDCS-10000707.translation: who died at the age of 22 years. Servilia Lea, most unhappy mother.
EDCS-10000707.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by the most unhappy mother, Servilia Lea, for her child who died at age 22.

EDCS-10000709.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luciferiae defunctae nnorum XXII Achillaeus conservae obsequentissimae et Secundae sorori eius ob pietate sic
EDCS-10000709.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Luciferia, who died at 22 years of age. Achillaeus placed this for his most devoted fellow slave, and for Secunda, her sister, because of her piety.
EDCS-10000709.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Luciferia, who died at 22, placed by Achillaeus for his fellow slave and for her sister Secunda.

EDCS-10000710.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tigridi dulcissimae Florus filius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000710.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To the sweetest Tigris. Florus, her son, placed this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10000710.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Florus, a son, to his well-deserving mother, Tigris.

EDCS-10000711.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, recording someone having placed something for an individual named Adeli[s].

EDCS-10000712.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, indicating someone's lifespan and filiation to Vixoria.

EDCS-10000718.text_edition: Quisquis petes Muciani memento sic qum sic videas solum pendere vide ne facias quod tibi fieri non vis Mucianus Clodio Secundo dedi locum
EDCS-10000718.translation: Whoever you are, remember Mucianus. Just as you see this hanging alone, be careful not to do what you would not want done to you. Mucianus gave this place to Clodius Secundus.
EDCS-10000718.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb or monument, containing a moralizing warning to passersby and recording that Mucianus provided the burial plot to Clodius Secundus.

EDCS-10000720.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Ursino defuncto annorum VII mensium V dierum XIII Titus Aurelius Ursinus a et p tiestamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10000720.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Titus Aurelius Ursinus, who died at 7 years, 5 months, and 13 days of age. Titus Aurelius Ursinus ordered this to be made according to his will.
EDCS-10000720.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Ursinus, a young boy, whose monument was ordered to be made according to a will by an individual named Titus Aurelius Ursinus (possibly the father or another relative).

EDCS-10000721.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Victori infelicissimo Siponto defunctus annorum plus menus sic XXX
EDCS-10000721.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aurelius Victor, most unfortunate, who died at Sipontum at about 30 years of age.
EDCS-10000721.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Victor, who died at Sipontum around the age of 30.

EDCS-10000722.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anchariae Quintillae Ancharius Ursinus matri pientissimae
EDCS-10000722.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Ancharia Quintilla. Ancharius Ursinus [placed this] for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-10000722.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ancharius Ursinus to his most devoted mother, Ancharia Quintilla.

EDCS-10000723.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudio Chresto defuncto nnorum XIII mensium IIII dierum VIIII Curia Onesimus pientissimus sodalis bene merenti posuit Claudius Onesimus pater oviti sic Claudia  Chreste pia mater posuerunt
EDCS-10000723.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Claudius Chrestus, who died at 13 years, 4 months, and 9 days of age. Curia Onesimus, his most devoted companion, placed this for the well-deserving. Claudius Onesimus, father, and Claudia Chreste, devoted mother, placed this.
EDCS-10000723.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Chrestus, a young boy, dedicated by his devoted companion Curia Onesimus, and also by his parents, Claudius Onesimus and Claudia Chreste.

EDCS-10000724.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Coelio Eutycheti Margarita bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10000724.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Publius Coelius Eutyches. Margarita placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10000724.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Margarita to the well-deserving Publius Coelius Eutyches.

EDCS-10000725.text_edition: Dis Manibus Seccius Corinthus Valeriae Valentinae defunctae annorum XXXV coniugi carissimae bene merenti
EDCS-10000725.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Seccius Corinthus (set this up) for Valeria Valentina, his dearest wife, who died at 35 years of age, well deserving.
EDCS-10000725.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Seccius Corinthus to his beloved wife, Valeria Valentina, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-10000726.text_edition: Dis Manibus Callistus Aemili et Crescentilla Crescenti filio infelicissimo nnorum II mens(ium) IIII et Fuscus amicus
EDCS-10000726.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Callistus, son of Aemilius, and Crescentilla (set this up) for Crescentius, their most unfortunate son, who lived 2 years and 4 months, and Fuscus, his friend (also set this up).
EDCS-10000726.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Callistus and Crescentilla, along with a friend Fuscus, for their tragically young son, Crescentius, who died at two years and four months old.

EDCS-10000727.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dorimae matri Doretos filius bene merenti
EDCS-10000727.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Doretos, son of Dorima, (set this up) for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10000727.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Doretos for his mother, Dorima, acknowledging her merits.

EDCS-10000733.text_edition: Iovi et Ianae posuit donum Salvius Venerius pro salute collegii
EDCS-10000733.translation: To Jupiter and Jana, Salvius Venerius placed this gift for the health of the college.
EDCS-10000733.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Salvius Venerius to Jupiter and Jana, made on behalf of a college or association for its well-being.

EDCS-10000739.text_edition: Theuda Theodoti filius domo Alexandria annorum LXX hic situs est Galene Theodoti liberta viva fecit sibi et Theodoto et Lupulo filiis suis et Arriae Secundae sorori suae et Primigeniae libertae suae
EDCS-10000739.translation: Theuda, son of Theodotus, from Alexandria, aged 70, is buried here. Galene, freedwoman of Theodotus, made this in her lifetime for herself and for Theodotus and Lupulus, her sons, and for Arria Secunda, her sister, and for Primigenia, her freedwoman.
EDCS-10000739.summary: This is a funerary monument for Theuda, son of Theodotus from Alexandria, who died at 70, also serving as a family tomb prepared by Galene, Theodotus' freedwoman, for herself and several family members and a freedwoman.

EDCS-10000740.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aeli- Le[---]
EDCS-10000740.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, likely for an individual named Aelius or Aelia.

EDCS-10000743.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for an individual named Dirutia.

EDCS-10000744.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Panto Iuliae Statiae nepoti bene merenti annorum XXX
EDCS-10000744.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Panto (set this up) for Iulia Statia, her well-deserving granddaughter, aged 30.
EDCS-10000744.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Panto to her granddaughter, Iulia Statia, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10000745.text_edition: Dis Manibus Firminus urso rapto a parentibus
EDCS-10000745.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Firminus, (who was) snatched away by his parents like a bear.
EDCS-10000745.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Firminus, using a metaphorical expression implying he was taken too soon or fiercely by his parents.

EDCS-10000747.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Fortunae Reduci et Genio Luci Angitiae Septimius Iustianus beneficiarius consularis cum suis posuit Gentiano et Basso cons(ulibus) M I IIII C
EDCS-10000747.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Fortuna Redux, and the Genius of Lucus Angitiae, Septimius Iustianus, beneficiarius consularis, along with his family, placed this in the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus, on the 4th day of March in the year 204.
EDCS-10000747.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Septimius Iustianus, a beneficiarius consularis, and his family to several deities including Jupiter, Juno, Fortuna, and the Genius of Lucus Angitiae, dated to the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus (204 AD).

EDCS-10000749.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Publius Marronius Maximianus votum libens posuit
EDCS-10000749.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Publius Marronius Maximianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000749.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta, set up by Publius Marronius Maximianus to fulfill a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000751.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Naevieno Senecae decurioni aedili IIviro coloniae Aequensium defuncto nnorum XXXXV Seccia Pudentilla marito rarissimo et Naevieni Seneca et Pudentilla filii patri optimo
EDCS-10000751.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Naevienus Seneca, decurion, aedile, duumvir of the colony of Aequensium, who died at 45 years of age. Seccia Pudentilla to her most unique husband, and Naevienus Seneca and Pudentilla, children, to their best father.
EDCS-10000751.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Titus Naevienus Seneca, a prominent local magistrate, erected by his wife Seccia Pudentilla and their children Naevienus Seneca and Pudentilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000752.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Apuleio Apulei filio Proculo  Rufo
EDCS-10000752.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Apuleius Proculus Rufus, son of Apuleius.
EDCS-10000752.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Apuleius Proculus Rufus, identifying him by his filiation, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000753.text_edition: Caecilia Procula testamento fieri iussit sibi et Tiberio Claudio Syrioni coniugi bene merenti Lucius Caecilius Onesimus heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-10000753.translation: Caecilia Procula ordered this to be made by testament for herself and for Tiberius Claudius Syrion, her well-deserving husband. Lucius Caecilius Onesimus, her heir, took care of placing it.
EDCS-10000753.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caecilia Procula, through her will, arranged for a monument for herself and her husband, Tiberius Claudius Syrion, which was subsequently set up by her heir Lucius Caecilius Onesimus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000754.text_edition: Diis Manibus sacrum Luci Tetti Luculli
EDCS-10000754.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes of Lucius Tettius Lucullus.
EDCS-10000754.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Lucius Tettius Lucullus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000755.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sabbatiae defunctae annorum II mensium
EDCS-10000755.translation: To the Divine Manes of Sabbatia, who died at 2 years and [a few] months.
EDCS-10000755.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Sabbatia, who died at the age of two years and several months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000756.text_edition: Dis Manibus riae Titiae  annos XXX tia Spe[rata &
EDCS-10000756.translation: To the Divine Manes of [---]ria Titia [---] 30 years old [---]tia Spe[rata ---].
EDCS-10000756.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely for a woman named Titia who died at 30 years old, set up by a person named Spe[rata], likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000757.text_edition: Dis Manibus io Atroci a Prima
EDCS-10000757.translation: To the Divine Manes. To [---]ius Atrox, by Prima.
EDCS-10000757.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely for an individual named Atrox, set up by Prima, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000759.text_edition: Octavia Fortunata Veneri votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000759.translation: Octavia Fortunata willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Venus.
EDCS-10000759.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Venus, recording that Octavia Fortunata willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000760.text_edition: Marco Caecilio Saturnino veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis et Viriae Publi libertae uxori
EDCS-10000760.translation: To Marcus Caecilius Saturninus, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, and to Viria, freedwoman of Publius, his wife.
EDCS-10000760.summary: This inscription commemorates Marcus Caecilius Saturninus, a veteran of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, and his wife Viria, a freedwoman, likely a funerary monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000761.text_edition: Dis Manibus Naeviae Placidae coniugi inconparabili! imae IIE
EDCS-10000761.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Naevia Placida, my incomparable wife. [Restoration of a number, possibly related to age or years of marriage].
EDCS-10000761.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a husband to his deceased wife, Naevia Placida, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000762.summary: This fragment likely records a public dedication or monument by Fortunatus, a sevirus Augustalis, an official involved in the imperial cult.

EDCS-10000763.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelius Gavius, dedicated for the health of an unstated person or persons.

EDCS-10000765.text_edition: Neptuno Andabatensi Aelius  Maximus Classicianus votum solvit
EDCS-10000765.translation: To Neptune Andabatensis, Aelius Maximus Classicianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000765.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aelius Maximus Classicianus, dedicating a monument to Neptune Andabatensis in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000766.text_edition: BuVC-- defunctae nnorum XXXX Paicus maritus fecit
EDCS-10000766.translation: Paicus, her husband, made this for his deceased [wife], who lived 40 years.
EDCS-10000766.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a husband, Paicus, for his deceased wife who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-10000769.text_edition: L Sabina Nepoti et Frontoniano filiis pientissimis fecit
EDCS-10000769.translation: L. Sabina made this for her most devoted sons, Nepos and Frontonianus.
EDCS-10000769.summary: This is a funerary inscription by L. Sabina for her two deceased sons, Nepos and Frontonianus, expressing maternal devotion.

EDCS-10000772.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri et Dianae Aurelius Platius Varronis libertus posuit
EDCS-10000772.translation: Aurelius Platius, freedman of Varro, placed this to Silvanus Silvester and Diana.
EDCS-10000772.summary: This is a votive dedication by Aurelius Platius, a freedman of Varro, to the deities Silvanus Silvester and Diana.

EDCS-10000776.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Castoribus  Marcus Aurelius Flavus sacerdos Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni votum solvit libens laetus merito
EDCS-10000776.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Castores, Marcus Aurelius Flavus, priest of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, willingly, gladly, deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000776.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aurelius Flavus, a priest of Jupiter Dolichenus, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Castores, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD given the prominence of the Dolichenus cult.

EDCS-10000777.text_edition: Silvanis Augustis sacrum Callimorphus Augusti nostri verna dispensator votum solvit
EDCS-10000777.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Silvani. Callimorphus, a home-born slave and dispenser of our Augustus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000777.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Augustan Silvani by Callimorphus, an imperial slave (verna) and dispenser (dispensator), indicating a dedication related to the imperial household.

EDCS-10000778.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning an imperial procurator (procurator Augusti), suggesting a public work or dedication related to imperial administration.

EDCS-10000780.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a larger text, which is too incomplete to determine its original purpose or context.

EDCS-10000781.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, too incomplete to determine its original purpose or context, possibly containing part of a name or title.

EDCS-10000783.text_edition: Marc- M Cai fili- T XXVI
EDCS-10000783.translation: Marcus, son of Gaius, in the tribe T(urbatina?), aged 26.
EDCS-10000783.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Marcus, son of Gaius, indicating his tribal affiliation and age at death, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-10000787.text_edition: Nemesi Piae in honorem collegii et Ianuari vilici Ianuarius ex corpore possuit sic
EDCS-10000787.translation: To Nemesis the Pious, in honor of the college and of January the steward, January himself placed this, as vowed.
EDCS-10000787.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nemesis the Pious by a man named Ianuarius, who was a steward, made in honor of his collegium and himself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000788.text_edition: Sedato Augusto pro salute Aureli vilici collegius sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000788.translation: To Sedatus Augustus, for the well-being of Aurelius the steward, the college thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled its vow.
EDCS-10000788.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by a collegium to Sedatus Augustus for the health of Aurelius, their steward, as a fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000789.text_edition: Sedato Augusto pro salute Aureli vilici collegae votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-10000789.translation: To Sedatus Augustus, for the well-being of Aurelius the steward, the colleagues willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000789.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by colleagues to Sedatus Augustus for the health of Aurelius, their steward, as a fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000790.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Iulius  procurator per Ianuarium et Bassum VIL
EDCS-10000790.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Julius the procurator, through Ianuarius and Bassus, placed this.
EDCS-10000790.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth for the health of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) made by Julius, a procurator, through two subordinates, likely dating to the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10000791.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Cai Iuli Agathopi conductoris ferrariarum Callimorphus vilicus votum solvit XI Kalendas Maias Muciano et Fabiano consulibus
EDCS-10000791.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the well-being of Gaius Iulius Agathopus, contractor of the ironworks, Callimorphus the steward willingly fulfilled his vow on the 11th Kalends of May, in the consulship of Mucianus and Fabianus.
EDCS-10000791.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth for the health of Gaius Iulius Agathopus, an ironworks contractor, made by Callimorphus, a steward, and dated to April 21, 236 AD, during the reign of Maximinus Thrax.

EDCS-10000792.text_edition: Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Maesiae sic Augustae [] Primus rcus Augusti  Maximo II et Aeliano consulibus XI Kalendas Maias
EDCS-10000792.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the well-being of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and of Iulia Mamaea Augusta, Primus, Augusti's rcus, [in the consulship of] Maximus II and Aelianus, on the 11th Kalends of May.
EDCS-10000792.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth for the health of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea, made by Primus, and dated to April 21, 236 AD, during the reign of Severus Alexander.

EDCS-10000796.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Quintus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000796.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Quintus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000796.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Publius Aelius Quintus, likely as a fulfillment of a vow, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000797.text_edition: Marti  Tossius Sura miles legionis VIII Augustae votum libens posuit
EDCS-10000797.translation: To Mars, Tossius Sura, soldier of the Eighth Augustan Legion, willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-10000797.summary: This inscription records a votive offering dedicated to Mars by a soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta.

EDCS-10000800.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus, likely by an individual named Sicanius.

EDCS-10000801.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri s(acrum) Titus Cai filius
EDCS-10000801.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester, Titus, son of Caius.
EDCS-10000801.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Silvester by Titus, son of Caius.

EDCS-10000803.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Firmus Tiberi Claudi Triti filius Firmus nnorum VIII hic situs est
EDCS-10000803.translation: Tiberius Claudius Firmus, son of Tiberius Claudius Tritus, Firmus, eight years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000803.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Firmus, son of Tiberius Claudius Tritus, who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-10000804.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Rutilio Quinti filio Titiano IIviro quinquennali et Quinto Rutilio Quinti filio Proculo IIviro quinqennali filio eius principi municipi Riditarum
EDCS-10000804.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Quintus Rutilius Titianus, son of Quintus, duumvir quinquennalis, and for Quintus Rutilius Proculus, son of Quintus, his son, also duumvir quinquennalis, leading citizen of the municipality of Riditae.
EDCS-10000804.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Rutilius Titianus and his son Quintus Rutilius Proculus, both prominent magistrates (duumviri quinquennales) in the municipality of Riditae.

EDCS-10000806.text_edition: Aplis Turi Statani filius annorum IIII
EDCS-10000806.translation: Aplus, son of Turi Statanus, four years old.
EDCS-10000806.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Aplus, son of Turi Statanus, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-10000807.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the funerary commemoration of an individual, possibly a son of Boria, who died at the age of forty.

EDCS-10000810.text_edition: Maximus Laedicalius Aplinis filius annorum III hic situs est
EDCS-10000810.translation: Maximus Laedicalius, son of Aplin, three years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000810.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maximus Laedicalius, son of Aplin, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-10000813.text_edition: Pinso Epicadi filius nnorum XXX hic situs est
EDCS-10000813.translation: Pinso, son of Epicadi, thirty years old, lies here.
EDCS-10000813.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pinso, son of Epicadi, who died at the age of thirty.

EDCS-10000814.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunato alumno annorum II mensium III Treboni- posuit
EDCS-10000814.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Fortunatus, foster-child, two years and three months old, Trebonius (?) placed this.
EDCS-10000814.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Fortunatus, a foster-child who died at a young age, and was placed by Trebonius or a person with a similar name.

EDCS-10000815.text_edition: Vendo Acenica Culi filia nnorum XX hic sita est
EDCS-10000815.translation: Vendo Acenica, daughter of Culus, aged 20, is laid here.
EDCS-10000815.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Vendo Acenica, daughter of Culus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10000821.summary: This inscription records the name of an individual, Pladomenus Darmocus, likely from a tombstone or a dedication.

EDCS-10000822.summary: This inscription names Turus Darmocus, identified as the son of Aplinis, likely part of a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-10000823.translation: Sextus made this for Bato Caonatus.
EDCS-10000823.summary: This inscription records that Sextus made a monument or object for Bato Caonatus.

EDCS-10000824.text_edition: Panto Laedietis Aplinis annorum XXX hic sita est
EDCS-10000824.translation: Panto Laedietis, daughter of Aplinis, aged 30, is laid here.
EDCS-10000824.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Panto Laedietis, daughter of Aplinis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10000827.text_edition: T Darmo Iano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000827.translation: T. Darmo willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Ianus.
EDCS-10000827.summary: This is a votive inscription where T. Darmo dedicated an offering to the god Ianus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10000828.text_edition: Lunae votum solvi libens Murine sic
EDCS-10000828.translation: I, Murine, willingly fulfilled a vow to Luna thus.
EDCS-10000828.summary: This is a votive inscription by Murine, who willingly fulfilled a vow to the goddess Luna.

EDCS-10000833.translation: Lucius Iulius Restitutus to Silvanus, sacred.
EDCS-10000833.summary: This is a sacred dedication by Lucius Iulius Restitutus to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-10000834.text_edition: Ieta Aeronia Cai filia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000834.translation: Ieta Aeronia, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10000834.summary: This is a votive inscription by Ieta Aeronia, daughter of Gaius, who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-10000837.text_edition: Claudia Liburno praefecto cohortis IIII praefecto cohortis III Thracum m tribuno militum legionis XXII M AVG PIAEL
EDCS-10000837.translation: To Claudia Liburnus, prefect of the Fourth Cohort, prefect of the Third Cohort of Thracians, military tribune of the Twenty-Second Legion, M. Aug. Piael.
EDCS-10000837.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Claudia Liburnus, detailing his distinguished military career, including commands as prefect of two cohorts and military tribune of Legion XXII.

EDCS-10000839.text_edition: Veratiae Quintinae coniugi carissimae annorum X bene merenti fecit
EDCS-10000839.translation: He made this for Veratia Quintina, his dearest wife, who lived ten years and deserved well.
EDCS-10000839.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a husband for his beloved wife, Veratia Quintina, who died at age ten, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-10000840.summary: This is a brief inscription recording the name of a freedwoman, Darmoca Secunda, who was manumitted by Ceuna, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000842.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit
EDCS-10000842.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Iulius Victorinus, a consular beneficiarius, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000842.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Iulius Victorinus, a beneficiarius serving a consul, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000843.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Marcus Aurelius Dalmata protector consularis pro beneficiario libens posuit
EDCS-10000843.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Marcus Aurelius Dalmata, a consular protector, willingly set this up on behalf of a beneficiarius.
EDCS-10000843.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius Loci by Marcus Aurelius Dalmata, a consular protector, on behalf of a beneficiarius, likely from the later Imperial period.

EDCS-10000844.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Publius Aelius Secundus miles legionis I adiutricis beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-10000844.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, [and] Minerva, Publius Aelius Secundus, soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix, consular beneficiarius.
EDCS-10000844.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, and Minerva by Publius Aelius Secundus, a soldier of Legio I Adiutrix and a consular beneficiarius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000845.translation: [---]rus, a Roman eques, gave this gift.
EDCS-10000845.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording a gift (donum) given by an unnamed Roman eques, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000846.text_edition: Publius Cerisius Publi filius Sabatina I Fa miles  nnorum XL
EDCS-10000846.translation: Publius Cerisius, son of Publius, of the Sabatina tribe, First Legion Adiutrix, soldier, [died] at forty years of age.
EDCS-10000846.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Cerisius, a soldier of Legio I Adiutrix, who died at age 40, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000847.text_edition: Caius Cottius Cai Quirina Priscinus Aug Ne miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI nnm XXXV testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10000847.translation: Gaius Cottius Priscinus, son of Gaius, of the Quirina tribe, Augustus's freedman, soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, with sixteen years of service, aged thirty-five, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10000847.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Cottius Priscinus, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, who died at age 35 with 16 years of service and had the monument erected per his will, likely from the early-to-mid Imperial period.

EDCS-10000848.text_edition: Quintus Iulius Quinti filius Oufentina Mansuetus Como miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII testamento fieri iussit Quintus Iulius Annius miles legionis eiusdem fratri pientissimo faciendum curavit
EDCS-10000848.translation: Quintus Iulius Mansuetus, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, from Como, soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, aged thirty-five, with fourteen years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Quintus Iulius Annius, soldier of the same legion, saw to the erection for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-10000848.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Iulius Mansuetus, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis from Como, who died at age 35 with 14 years of service and had the monument erected per his will by his brother, Quintus Iulius Annius, also a soldier, likely from the early-to-mid Imperial period.

EDCS-10000849.text_edition: Lucius Laberius Luci filius Firmus tribu Claudia domo Foro Claudi miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII h(ic)? s(itus) est Titus Talonius Secundus testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10000849.translation: Lucius Laberius Firmus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, from Forum Claudii, soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, aged thirty-five, with fourteen years of service, is buried here. Titus Talonius Secundus ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10000849.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Laberius Firmus, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis from Forum Claudii, who died at age 35 with 14 years of service, and had the monument erected per his will by Titus Talonius Secundus, likely from the early-to-mid Imperial period.

EDCS-10000850.text_edition: Lucius Magnus Luci Fabia Clemens Tavia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis optio stipendiorum XI annorum XXX posuit Lucius Salvius Clemens frater
EDCS-10000850.translation: Lucius Magnus, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, Clemens Tavia, a soldier of the 11th Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, an optio, served for 11 years and lived for 30 years. Lucius Salvius Clemens, his brother, set this up.
EDCS-10000850.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Magnus, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia, set up by his brother.

EDCS-10000851.text_edition: Quintus Satrius Quinti Publilia Martialis Verona miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XXXIIII stipendiorum XIIII
EDCS-10000851.translation: Quintus Satrius, son of Quintus, of the Publilia tribe, Martialis, from Verona, a soldier of the 11th Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, lived for 34 years and served for 14 years.
EDCS-10000851.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Satrius, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia from Verona.

EDCS-10000852.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tertius Antonius Terti filius Pollia Rufinus Parma miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI
EDCS-10000852.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tertius Antonius Rufinus, son of Tertius, of the Pollia tribe, from Parma, a soldier of the 11th Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, served for 16 years.
EDCS-10000852.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes for Tertius Antonius Rufinus, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia from Parma.

EDCS-10000853.text_edition: Buccus Staumi filius Fabia domo Brixia miles cohortis Montanorum nnorum XXVI stipendiorum IIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10000853.translation: Buccus, son of Staumus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, a soldier of the Cohort of Montani, lived for 26 years and served for 4 years. He lies here. He ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10000853.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Buccus, a soldier of the Cohors Montanorum from Brixia, erected according to his will.

EDCS-10000854.text_edition: Heras Ennomai filius miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum domo Cyrrho nnorum L stipendiorum XXII hic situs est raeus Abemmi filius heres posuit
EDCS-10000854.translation: Heras, son of Ennomai, a soldier of the 2nd Cohort of Cyrrhestae, from Cyrrhus, lived for 50 years and served for 22 years. He lies here. Raeus, son of Abemmus, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-10000854.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heras, a soldier of the Cohors II Cyrrhestarum from Cyrrhus, set up by his heir.

EDCS-10000855.text_edition: Imerix Servofredi filius Batavos sic eques alae Hispanorum I annorum XXVIII stipendiorum VIII hic situs est  azicico  et Apulo co Aetoris filio heres
EDCS-10000855.translation: Imerix, son of Servofredus, a Batavian, specifically a horseman of the 1st Ala Hispanorum, lived for 28 years and served for 8 years. He lies here. [---]zicico and Apulus, son of Aetor, his heir [set this up].
EDCS-10000855.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Imerix, a Batavian cavalryman of the Ala I Hispanorum, set up by his heir(s).

EDCS-10000856.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Allio Saturnino decurioni municipi Varvariae IIIIviro iure dicundo Alli Maximina et Maximus patri bene merenti
EDCS-10000856.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Allius Saturninus, decurion of the municipality of Varvaria, a quattuorvir with judicial power. Allia Maximina and Allius Maximus [made this] for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-10000856.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Titus Allius Saturninus, a municipal magistrate in Varvaria, set up by his children.

EDCS-10000857.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Nonio Clementiano decurioni municipi Burnistarum aedili defuncto annorum XL Nonius Lucius et Valeria Valentilla fratri pientissimo
EDCS-10000857.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Nonius Clementianus, decurion of the municipality of Burnistae, a deceased aedile, who lived for 40 years. Nonius Lucius and Valeria Valentilla [made this] for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-10000857.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Lucius Nonius Clementianus, a deceased municipal magistrate in Burnistae, set up by his siblings.

EDCS-10000858.text_edition: Tito Queresio Aetoris filio patri Volusiae Marci filiae Aetriae matri Queresiae Titi filiae Maximae sorori Queresiae Titi filiae Tertiae sorori Caius Volusius Maximus heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-10000858.translation: To Titus Queresius, son of Aetor, his father; to Volusia, daughter of Marcus, Aetria, his mother; to Queresia Maxima, daughter of Titus, his sister; to Queresia Tertia, daughter of Titus, his sister. Gaius Volusius Maximus, the heir, took care of having this made.
EDCS-10000858.summary: This is a funerary monument for a family, including the father Titus Queresius, his mother, and two sisters, arranged by Gaius Volusius Maximus as heir.

EDCS-10000859.text_edition: Caius Virius Cai libertus Vitalis nnum VI hic situs est Caius Virius et Neuma mater posuere
EDCS-10000859.translation: Gaius Virius Vitalis, freedman of Gaius, lived for 6 years. He lies here. Gaius Virius and Neuma, his mother, set this up.
EDCS-10000859.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Virius Vitalis, a freedman, set up by Gaius Virius (likely his patron) and his mother Neuma.

EDCS-10000860.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Veneriae et Felicissimae filiabus infelicissimis Iulia Secunda mater posuit
EDCS-10000860.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Julia Secunda, the mother, placed this for her most unhappy daughters, Veneria and Felicissima.
EDCS-10000860.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother's dedication of a monument to her two deceased daughters.

EDCS-10000861.summary: This short inscription appears to be a record of two individuals' names, possibly a simple dedication or ownership mark.

EDCS-10000862.text_edition: Lucius Mart filius annorum Lucius OlstIiu[s#Olstius#OLSIIU --- fecit
EDCS-10000862.translation: Lucius Martius, son, aged [---] years. Lucius Olstius made [this].
EDCS-10000862.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for Lucius Martius, which was made by Lucius Olstius, but the age of the deceased is lost.

EDCS-10000867.text_edition: Volltisa Iovi votum solvit libens merito Caminis liberta
EDCS-10000867.translation: Volltisa, a freedwoman of Caminus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-10000867.summary: This inscription records Volltisa, a freedwoman, fulfilling a vow to the god Jupiter.

EDCS-10000868.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Titia Ursula filio infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XXIII
EDCS-10000868.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Aurelius, her most unhappy son, Titia Ursula [placed this], who lived for 23 years.
EDCS-10000868.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titia Ursula to her deceased son, Marcus Aurelius, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-10000869.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Paetilio filio dulcissimo nnorum IIII mensium VIIII dierum VIII Marcus Aurelius Surillio et Apuleia Titia parentes piissimi posuerunt
EDCS-10000869.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius Surillio and Apuleia Titia, most devoted parents, placed this for their sweetest son, Marcus Aurelius Paetilius, who lived for 4 years, 9 months, and 8 days.
EDCS-10000869.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Surillio and Apuleia Titia to their young son, Marcus Aurelius Paetilius, who died in early childhood.

EDCS-10000871.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Eutyches et Cassia Iunia parentes Iulio Rufino filio infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XIII menses VI dies XVIIII
EDCS-10000871.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julius Eutyches and Cassia Junia, parents, [placed this] for their most unhappy son, Julius Rufinus, who lived for 13 years, 6 months, and 19 days.
