EDCS-10000871.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius Eutyches and Cassia Junia to their son, Julius Rufinus, who died as a teenager.

EDCS-10000872.translation: His sister placed this for Lucius Petilius Celer.
EDCS-10000872.summary: This short funerary inscription records a sister dedicating a monument to her brother, Lucius Petilius Celer.

EDCS-10000873.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primio sibi et suis vivos sic fecit
EDCS-10000873.translation: To the Divine Manes. Primius made this for himself and his family while living.
EDCS-10000873.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Primius creating a tomb or monument for himself and his family during his lifetime (a common practice).

EDCS-10000874.summary: This brief inscription is a funerary dedication to Saturninus.

EDCS-10000875.translation: Prisca set this up for her brother Titus Mamaester.
EDCS-10000875.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Prisca for her brother, Titus Mamaester.

EDCS-10000876.text_edition: Dis Manibus Helvio Felici Helvius Felix in memoriam patri sic fecit patri bene merenti
EDCS-10000876.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Helvius Felix made this for Helvius Felix, in memory of his father; for a well-deserving father.
EDCS-10000876.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed and set up by Helvius Felix for his well-deserving father, also named Helvius Felix.

EDCS-10000879.text_edition: Rubria Ceuni filia Pola Saluti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000879.translation: Rubria Pola, daughter of Ceunus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Salus.
EDCS-10000879.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Rubria Pola, daughter of Ceunus, fulfilling her vow to the goddess Salus.

EDCS-10000880.text_edition: Caius Veronius Aetor vivus fecit sibi et Veturiae AetIae#Aetae#AEIAE uxori etI#et#EI Ceuno fE(ilio)#f(ilio)#E etI#et#EI Caio Iulio nepoti
EDCS-10000880.translation: Gaius Veronius Aetor made this in his lifetime for himself and for his wife Veturia Aeta, and for his son Ceuno, and for his grandson Gaius Julius.
EDCS-10000880.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Veronius Aetor during his lifetime for himself, his wife, his son, and his grandson, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-10000881.text_edition: Lucius Sentius Patri Iano v(otum) s(olvit) libens merito
EDCS-10000881.translation: Lucius Sentius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Father Janus.
EDCS-10000881.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Lucius Sentius fulfilling his vow to the god Janus.

EDCS-10000882.text_edition: Triviis sacrum Caius Iulius Celer votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000882.translation: Sacred to the Cross-Roads. Gaius Julius Celer willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000882.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the gods of the cross-roads (Triviae) by Gaius Julius Celer in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000887.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, naming a woman as the daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-10000911.text_edition: Lucio Caleio beneficiario Lucius Valerius Caridus decurio equitum de suo fecit
EDCS-10000911.translation: Lucius Valerius Caridus, decurion of the cavalry, made this from his own funds for Lucius Caleius, a beneficiarius.
EDCS-10000911.summary: This is an honorific or funerary inscription set up by Lucius Valerius Caridus, a cavalry decurion, for Lucius Caleius, a beneficiarius, indicating a connection between military personnel.

EDCS-10000918.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a funerary monument, simply naming Gaius Annius Statianus.

EDCS-10000920.text_edition: Caius Iulius Atticus sibe sic et suis in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XV
EDCS-10000920.translation: Gaius Julius Atticus made this for himself and his family, with a frontage of 10 feet and a depth of 15 feet.
EDCS-10000920.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Julius Atticus for himself and his family, specifying the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-10000921.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Passienae Voltissae Publius Passienus
EDCS-10000921.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Passiena Voltissa. Publius Passienus [set this up].
EDCS-10000921.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Passienus to Passiena Voltissa.

EDCS-10000922.text_edition: Liburniae Luci libertae Callistae Liburnius Luci libertus Smaragdus coniugi
EDCS-10000922.translation: To Liburnia Callista, freedwoman of Lucius. Liburnius Smaragdus, freedman of Lucius, [erected this] for his wife.
EDCS-10000922.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Liburnius Smaragdus for his wife, Liburnia Callista, both freedpersons of Lucius.

EDCS-10000924.text_edition: Lucius I Luci filius Se matri fecit
EDCS-10000924.translation: Lucius I, son of Lucius Se, made this for his mother.
EDCS-10000924.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius I, son of Lucius Se, for his mother.

EDCS-10000926.text_edition: Iovi Optumo Maximo Cornelia Cai filia Tertia votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000926.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Cornelia Tertia, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10000926.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Cornelia Tertia, daughter of Gaius, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000927.text_edition: Iovi Sabasio Iico Lucius Plotius Eperastus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000927.translation: To Jupiter Sabazius Iicus. Lucius Plotius Eperastus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000927.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Sabazius Iicus, dedicated by Lucius Plotius Eperastus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000931.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae Faustus Titi Iuli Saturnini praefecti vehiculorum et conductoris publici portorii servus vilicus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000931.translation: To the Unconquered Sun Mithras. Faustus, slave and overseer of Titus Iulius Saturninus, prefect of the transport service and public tax collector, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his household.
EDCS-10000931.summary: This is a votive inscription to Sol Invictus Mithras, dedicated by Faustus, a slave and overseer, for himself and his household.

EDCS-10000938.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Euhemer(us) servus Augusti votum libens solvit merito
EDCS-10000938.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Euhemerus, slave of the Emperor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000938.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana Augusta, dedicated by Euhemerus, a slave of the Emperor, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10000941.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cervola vixit annis LV fecit Vitalis coniugi bene merenti et sibi
EDCS-10000941.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cervola lived for 55 years. Vitalis made this for his well-deserving wife and for himself.
EDCS-10000941.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cervola, erected by her husband Vitalis for her and for himself.

EDCS-10000942.text_edition: xsa sic Frontonis filia quae veixit sic annos XXVIII  heic sic sita est
EDCS-10000942.translation: [---]sa, daughter of Fronto, who lived for 28 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10000942.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman, daughter of Fronto, who lived 28 years and is buried here.

EDCS-10000943.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vernacula vixit annis XL fecit Eurialis sic coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10000943.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vernacula lived for 40 years. Eurialis made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10000943.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vernacula, erected by her husband Eurialis.

EDCS-10000951.text_edition: Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus  domu Ma
EDCS-10000951.translation: Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus, from the house of Ma[...].
EDCS-10000951.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely commemorating Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus, with an indication of his origin or lineage.

EDCS-10000952.translation: To Quintus Pomponius Rufus, legate of the Emperors.
EDCS-10000952.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Quintus Pomponius Rufus, an imperial legate.

EDCS-10000953.text_edition: cus Cleni filius us Cleni filius pro memoria  faciundum curaverunt
EDCS-10000953.translation: [Mar]cus, son of Clenius, and [Titus], son of Clenius, took care of having this made in memory.
EDCS-10000953.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that two brothers, sons of Clenius, had a monument made in memory of someone, likely a parent or family member.

EDCS-10000954.text_edition: Marti sacrum Turus Longini filius decurio et sacerdotalis pro suis et cognatione Nantania de suo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10000954.translation: Sacred to Mars. Turus, son of Longinus, decurion and priest, for himself and his kin, the Nantanian family, from his own funds, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000954.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mars by Turus, a decurion and priest, for the welfare of himself and his family.

EDCS-10000957.text_edition: Impperatoribus Caessaribus Caio Vibio Gallo et Caio Vibio Volusiano Piis Felicibus Auggustis
EDCS-10000957.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Gaius Vibius Gallus and Gaius Vibius Volusianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augusti.
EDCS-10000957.summary: This is a public dedication to the emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusian, likely dating to their co-reign between 251-253 CE.

EDCS-10000975.text_edition: GDD VISMN  principibus maximis P M ac victoribus semper AAuggustis bono rei publicae natis
EDCS-10000975.translation: [...] great princes, supreme and always victorious Augusti, born for the good of the Republic.
EDCS-10000975.summary: This is a laudatory inscription praising unspecified emperors as great, victorious, and beneficial to the state.

EDCS-10001021.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar A I
EDCS-10001021.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the third time, consul, father of his country, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-10001021.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, detailing their imperial titles and dating to around 236 CE.

EDCS-10001035.text_edition: Imperatori domino nostro Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-10001035.translation: To the Emperor, our lord, Julius Philippus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-10001035.summary: This is a public dedication to the emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to his reign between 244-249 CE.

EDCS-10001042.text_edition: Imppperatoribus dddominis nnnostris Marco Aurelio Caro Augusto et Marco Aurelio Carino et Marco Aurelio Numeriano nobilissimis Caesaribus perpetuis et Numeriano Caesari
EDCS-10001042.translation: To the Emperors, our lords, Marcus Aurelius Carus Augustus, and Marcus Aurelius Carinus, and Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, most noble Caesars, perpetual, and to Numerianus Caesar.
EDCS-10001042.summary: This is a public dedication to the emperors Carus, Carinus, and Numerian, likely dating to their co-reign period around 283 CE, with a slight redundancy in naming Numerian.

EDCS-10100455.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aelius Celsinus decurio libens posuit  COR  ALB
EDCS-10100455.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aelius Celsinus, decurion, willingly placed this. COR ALB.
EDCS-10100455.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aelius Celsinus, a decurion.

EDCS-10100488.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maximo defuncto annorum XL Annia filio pientissimo posuit
EDCS-10100488.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Maximus, who died at 40 years of age. Annia placed this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-10100488.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Annia for her son Maximus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-10100489.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Tessio Marcellino p q(ui) vixit annos XXII Caio Tessio Paterno coiov vixit nnos VIII Ulpia Marci f(ilia) Bidna filio t marito pientissimis
EDCS-10100489.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Tessius Marcellinus, who lived 22 years. To Gaius Tessius Paternus, who lived 8 years. Ulpia, daughter of Marcus, Bidna, placed this for her most devoted son and husband.
EDCS-10100489.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpia Bidna for her most devoted son, Gaius Tessius Paternus, and her husband, Gaius Tessius Marcellinus.

EDCS-10100492.text_edition: Dis Manibus Celeo Cresiano ANISVI VERMABI DANO VARETO pientissimis
EDCS-10100492.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Celeus Cresianus, and to ANISVI VERMABI DANO VARETO, most devoted.
EDCS-10100492.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Celeus Cresianus and others, indicating their devotion.

EDCS-10100493.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Silvanus decurio municipii vixit annos LXX hic situs est Titus Aurelius Proculus patri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-10100493.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Silvanus, decurion of the municipality, lived 70 years. He is laid here. Titus Aurelius Proculus placed this for his most devoted father.
EDCS-10100493.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a municipal decurion who lived 70 years, erected by his son Titus Aurelius Proculus.

EDCS-10100494.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Silviana vixit annos XLV Gaia filia suprascriptae viixit sic annos III Flavius Dalmatius defunctae coUniugi#coniugi#CUNG pientissimae et dulcissimae filiae merentibus posuit
EDCS-10100494.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Silviana lived 45 years. Gaia, the daughter of the aforementioned, lived 3 years. Flavius Dalmatius placed this for his most devoted wife and sweetest deserving daughter.
EDCS-10100494.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavius Dalmatius for his most devoted wife, Aurelia Silviana, and their sweet daughter, Gaia, both deceased.

EDCS-10100495.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo VOIIDA pro valetudine Augusti nostri vovit
EDCS-10100495.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. VOIIDA vowed this for the health of our Augustus.
EDCS-10100495.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by VOIIDA, made in fulfillment of a vow for the emperor's health.

EDCS-10100496.text_edition: AHO Aurelius Valerius arcarius fisci Dalmatiae simul cum GCaudentia#Gaudentia#CAUDETIA ex voto
EDCS-10100496.translation: AHO. Aurelius Valerius, cashier of the Dalmatian treasury, together with Gaudentia, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10100496.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Aurelius Valerius, a treasury official, and Gaudentia, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100501.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpio Nonntioni annorum XL Campanius Marcianus vivus sibi et Iulia Marciana parentes pii IIVET
EDCS-10100501.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ulpius Nonntio, 40 years old. Campanius Marcianus, alive, for himself and Julia Marciana, devoted parents.
EDCS-10100501.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, set up by Campanius Marcianus for himself, Julia Marciana, and Ulpius Nonntio, who died at 40.

EDCS-10100502.text_edition: Dis Inferis Manibus Restituto bene merito Marcella coniux memoriam fecit vixsit! annos LV
EDCS-10100502.translation: To the Infernal Divine Manes. To Restitutus, well-deserving. Marcella his wife made this memorial. He lived 55 years.
EDCS-10100502.summary: This is a funerary inscription, a memorial created by Marcella for her well-deserving husband, Restitutus, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-10100504.translation: Sacred for the health of Maecilia Hilaria.
EDCS-10100504.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated for the health of Maecilia Hilaria.

EDCS-10100505.text_edition: Serapi et Isidi Cnaeus Pompeius Politianus tribunus cohortis II miliariae Dalmatarum
EDCS-10100505.translation: To Serapis and Isis, Gnaeus Pompeius Politianus, tribune of the Second Millenary Cohort of Dalmatians.
EDCS-10100505.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gnaeus Pompeius Politianus, a military tribune, to the deities Serapis and Isis.

EDCS-10100507.text_edition: Invicto Deo Optimo Grudius Aetor Super libentes posuerunt
EDCS-10100507.translation: To the Unconquered Best God, Grudius and Aetor Super willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100507.summary: This inscription is a dedication to an 'Unconquered Best God' by Grudius and Aetor Super.

EDCS-10100509.text_edition: Iovi Fulguratori sacrum Titus Aurelius Marcus veteranus cum suis libens posuit
EDCS-10100509.translation: Sacred to Jupiter the Thunderer, Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, with his family, willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100509.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Fulgurator (the Thunderer) made by a veteran, Titus Aurelius Marcus, along with his family.

EDCS-10100510.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Valerius Satianus libens posuit
EDCS-10100510.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Quintus Valerius Satianus willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100510.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Quintus Valerius Satianus.

EDCS-10100511.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Dussona vixit annos LXXX Maetius compari et sibi vivus et Victorianus filius  sibi vivi titulum ponendum curaverunt
EDCS-10100511.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Dussona lived 80 years. Maetius, for his companion and for himself while living, and Victorianus, his son, for himself while living, saw to the placement of this title.
EDCS-10100511.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Dussona, aged 80, set up by Maetius for himself and his deceased companion, and by their son Victorianus for himself, while still alive.

EDCS-10100512.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius is vivus  CVOAurelio IAL defuncto annorum X posuit
EDCS-10100512.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius, while living, placed this for Aurelio IAL, who died at 10 years old.
EDCS-10100512.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes, set up by Valerius while still alive, for Aurelio IAL, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-10100514.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae  Aelius Aprilis beneficiarius consularis v(otum)? s(olvit) libens merito
EDCS-10100514.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva. Aelius Aprilis, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100514.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aelius Aprilis, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, and Minerva.

EDCS-10100515.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. IICIIANVS.
EDCS-10100515.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a person named IICIIANVS.

EDCS-10100516.text_edition: Imperator Caesar  Parthicus Britannicus G consul IIIII tribunicia potestate  c curante C
EDCS-10100516.translation: Emperor Caesar, Parthicus Britannicus, consul for the fourth time, with tribunician power... under the care of C. [---]
EDCS-10100516.summary: This inscription fragment likely commemorates an imperial act or monument, mentioning an Emperor Caesar with the titles Parthicus and Britannicus, who served as consul four times and held tribunician power, with C. [---] overseeing the work.

EDCS-10100517.text_edition: Aesculapio et Hygiae Auggustis ssacrum Caius Iulius Herculanus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae Moesiae inferioris libens posuit
EDCS-10100517.translation: To Aesculapius and Hygia, the August deities, sacred. Gaius Julius Herculanus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Italica from Moesia Inferior, willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100517.summary: This inscription is a sacred dedication to Aesculapius and Hygia by Gaius Julius Herculanus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Italica from the province of Moesia Inferior.

EDCS-10100518.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Cocceia Maxima votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100518.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Cocceia Maxima willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10100518.summary: This votive inscription records Cocceia Maxima's fulfillment of a vow to Diana Augusta, likely on an altar or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-10100519.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Aemilius Ingenuus miles legionis XI Claudiae beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-10100519.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Aemilius Ingenuus, soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, beneficiarius consularis (aide to the provincial governor).
EDCS-10100519.summary: This dedicatory inscription, likely from an altar or votive monument, records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Aemilius Ingenuus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio XI Claudia.

EDCS-10100520.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. ECM Maximus.
EDCS-10100520.summary: This fragmentary dedicatory inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual named Maximus, whose praenomen and nomen are only partially preserved.

EDCS-10100525.text_edition: Marti Augusto Iulius Longinus beneficiarius consularis ex legione V Macedonica votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100525.translation: To Mars Augustus. Julius Longinus, beneficiarius consularis from the Fifth Macedonian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100525.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mars Augustus by Julius Longinus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio V Macedonica.

EDCS-10100526.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Claudius Maximus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100526.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Claudius Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100526.summary: This votive inscription records Claudius Maximus's fulfillment of a vow to the deity Silvanus, likely on a dedicatory monument.

EDCS-10100527.text_edition: IIvir quinquennalis  GERD IT m libens posuit
EDCS-10100527.translation: Quinquennial duumvir. GERD IT M willingly erected this.
EDCS-10100527.summary: This fragmentary inscription records an erection by a quinquennial duumvir (a senior municipal magistrate holding office for a five-year term), with the dedicant's name only partially preserved.

EDCS-10100529.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Aurelius Baebianus.
EDCS-10100529.summary: This funerary inscription records the name of Titus Aurelius Baebianus, dedicating the monument to the Dis Manibus.

EDCS-10100530.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Licinius defunctus nnorum LXXII Aurelia Thana patrono de se bene merito
EDCS-10100530.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Licinius, deceased at 72 years. Aurelia Thana set this up for her patron, who deserved well of her.
EDCS-10100530.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aurelius Licinius, who died at 72, erected by Aurelia Thana for her deserving patron.

EDCS-10100531.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Catiae Claudia Annia filiae dulcissimae posuit
EDCS-10100531.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Annia set this up for her sweetest daughter, Claudia Catia.
EDCS-10100531.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Claudia Annia for her beloved daughter, Claudia Catia.

EDCS-10100533.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Sergio Iuliano |(centurioni II militavit annos XXV    II Heonia Vera coniugi pientissimo posuit
EDCS-10100533.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Sergius Julianus, centurion, he served 25 years. Heonia Vera set this up for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-10100533.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gaius Sergius Julianus, a centurion who served for 25 years, erected by his devoted wife, Heonia Vera.

EDCS-10100537.text_edition: avii equitis Romani clarissimi viri   matris clarissimae feminae Claudius Ca
EDCS-10100537.translation: Of Avius, a most distinguished Roman knight; of his most distinguished mother. Claudius Ca[...]
EDCS-10100537.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment for a Roman knight and his mother, set up by a Claudius.

EDCS-10100538.translation: To the Divine Manes, of Bae, deceased.
EDCS-10100538.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of a deceased individual named Bae.

EDCS-10100540.text_edition: Dis Manibus IRI municipes municipii IIviratu functo vixit annos LXV Aurelia avi  Aurelius  iv  t pientissimo o s XIII  posuerunt
EDCS-10100540.translation: To the Divine Manes. [...]iri, municipal citizens of the municipality, having served as duumvir, lived 65 years. Aurelia Avi[...], Aurelius [...], thirteen years. They piously placed this.
EDCS-10100540.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia and Aurelius for a duumvir who lived 65 years, likely commemorating their relative or a respected citizen.

EDCS-10100546.translation: To the Divine Manes, Verecundus and [...].
EDCS-10100546.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by Verecundus and another individual for a deceased person or persons.

EDCS-10100548.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Silvanus decurio M(--- municipii Flavi
EDCS-10100548.translation: To the Divine Manes, Titus Flavius Silvanus, decurion of the Flavian municipality of M[...].
EDCS-10100548.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Silvanus, a decurion of a Flavian municipality.

EDCS-10100549.text_edition: Valeriano et Marcellinae Luci filiis vivis posuit
EDCS-10100549.translation: Lucius placed this for his living sons Valerianus and Marcellina.
EDCS-10100549.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription set up by Lucius for his two children, Valerianus and Marcellina, while they were still alive, likely for a tomb or monument intended for their future use.

EDCS-10100551.text_edition: VVIS filio V LIPOSSIB NI IM DASSIO VI AMCRVARISSIM AD VIVINIIV
EDCS-10100551.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument for a son, but much of the text is illegible.

EDCS-10100555.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Clemens IIvir votum libens solvit
EDCS-10100555.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Clemens, duumvir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100555.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Publius Aelius Clemens, a duumvir, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100556.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Apollo
EDCS-10100556.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Ulpius Apollo.
EDCS-10100556.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Apollo, likely in fulfillment of a vow or as an act of devotion.

EDCS-10100563.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto res publica Aquarum S
EDCS-10100563.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated Augustus, the Republic of Aquae S[...].
EDCS-10100563.summary: This is a monumental inscription dedicated to the Emperor Diocletian, erected by the public body of Aquae S[...], likely to commemorate his reign or a specific event.

EDCS-10100565.text_edition: Ulpiae Titi filiae Proculae annorum XX Titus Flavius Valens Varronis filius princeps Desitiatium et Aelia Iusta Scenobarbi filia
EDCS-10100565.translation: To Ulpia Procula, daughter of Titus, twenty years old. Titus Flavius Valens, son of Varro, chief of the Desitiates, and Aelia Iusta, daughter of Scenobarbus, [set this up].
EDCS-10100565.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young woman, Ulpia Procula, set up by Titus Flavius Valens, chief of the Desitiates, and Aelia Iusta, likely her parents or close relatives.

EDCS-10100566.text_edition: Aelia Marcella annorum XX Publius Aeliusmael
EDCS-10100566.translation: Aelia Marcella, twenty years old. Publius Aelius [set this up].
EDCS-10100566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Marcella, twenty years old, likely set up by Publius Aelius.

EDCS-10100567.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a partially preserved name Aelius.

EDCS-10100568.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Acutus APME.
EDCS-10100568.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Acutus, with an unclear abbreviation possibly indicating a profession or other details.

EDCS-10100569.text_edition: Dis Manibus defuncto --- Bonoso puero annorum XV Iustinus pater
EDCS-10100569.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To the deceased Bonosus, a boy fifteen years old. Justinus, his father, [set this up].
EDCS-10100569.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a fifteen-year-old boy named Bonosus, set up by his father Justinus.

EDCS-10100571.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Clario annorum  posuerunt Ursus et Dassius patri hic situs est
EDCS-10100571.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Clarius, [years old]. Ursus and Dassius set this up for their father. He lies here.
EDCS-10100571.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clarius, whose age is missing, set up by his sons Ursus and Dassius.

EDCS-10100574.text_edition: Batoni Liccai filio Teuta Vietis Sceno sic Batonis filius maxime natus Scenocalo Batonis filio Scaevae Batonis filio Calloni Batonis filio Prorado Batonis filio Scenus Batonis filius minime natus ex eis sibi et suis de sua pecunia fieri iussit
EDCS-10100574.translation: For Baton, son of Liccai. Teuta, daughter of Vietis. Sceno, thus the eldest son of Baton. For Scenocalus, son of Baton. For Scaeva, son of Baton. For Callon, son of Baton. For Proradus, son of Baton. Scenus, the youngest son of Baton among them, ordered this to be made for himself and his family from his own money.
EDCS-10100574.summary: This is a family funerary monument, likely for Baton, his wife Teuta, and their sons, ordered by Scenus, the youngest son of Baton, for himself and his kin.

EDCS-10100577.text_edition: Aurelia Nava annorum XLV Aurelius ginianus   ]  Aurelia pa mater eius viva sibi fecit et nepoti suo Marcellino defuncto
EDCS-10100577.translation: Aurelia Nava, forty-five years old. Aurelius Ginianus [---]. Aurelia Pa, his mother, alive, made this for herself and for her deceased grandson Marcellinus.
EDCS-10100577.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Pa, still living, for herself and for her deceased grandson Marcellinus, also mentioning Aurelia Nava and Aurelius Ginianus, likely relatives.

EDCS-10100586.text_edition: Publi filius  et infelic dolore et ius et con quod pater optabat fieri sibi quod pia mater infelix natis fecit uterque parentum
EDCS-10100586.translation: [...] son of Publius [...] and with unhappy grief and right and what the father wished to be made for himself, which the pious, unhappy mother made for her children, both parents.
EDCS-10100586.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription expresses profound parental grief, indicating a monument set up by both parents for their children, possibly for a son of Publius.

EDCS-10100594.text_edition: Lucio puero qui vixit nnos XX Lupus et Cresa filio pientissimo et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-10100594.translation: To Lucius, a boy who lived twenty years. Lupus and Cresa, alive, set this up for their most dutiful son and for themselves.
EDCS-10100594.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a twenty-year-old boy named Lucius, set up by his parents Lupus and Cresa for him and for their own future use.

EDCS-10100597.text_edition: entiano annorum XX Ursinae coniugi karissimae nius tinus ? et sibi
EDCS-10100597.translation: To Entianus, 20 years old. To Ursina, his dearest wife. [...]tinus and for himself.
EDCS-10100597.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Entianus and Ursina, likely erected by their relative [...]tinus, who also reserved a spot for himself.

EDCS-10100598.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, mentioning Marcus Flavius.

EDCS-10100605.text_edition: Minervae sacrum ex voto Lucius Publicius Telesphorus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100605.translation: Sacred to Minerva, in fulfillment of a vow. Lucius Publicius Telesphorus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100605.summary: This inscription is a dedicatory offering to the goddess Minerva by Lucius Publicius Telesphorus, made in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100606.translation: Sextus Iulius Gracilis fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100606.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording that Sextus Iulius Gracilis fulfilled a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-10100608.text_edition: Caio Minicio Luci filio Papiria Fundano VIIviro epulonum tribuno legionis VII fulminatae quaestori tribuno plebis praetori legato legionis XV Apollinaris PIAE UR
EDCS-10100608.translation: To Gaius Minicius, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, Fundanus, septemvir epulonum, tribune of the Legio VII Fulminata, quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of the Legio XV Apollinaris Pia Urbis (dutiful to the city).
EDCS-10100608.summary: This inscription is an honorary dedication listing the cursus honorum of Gaius Minicius Fundanus, detailing his prestigious senatorial career.

EDCS-10100609.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tertullae infelicessimae sic defunctae annorum XII plus minus
EDCS-10100609.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, to Tertulla, who most unhappily died at about 12 years of age.
EDCS-10100609.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tertulla, a young girl who died at approximately 12 years old.

EDCS-10100611.text_edition: Iulius sum cumsuetus sic populo messem arborum
EDCS-10100611.translation: I am Iulius, accustomed to the harvest of trees for the people.
EDCS-10100611.summary: This is an inscription seemingly identifying Iulius as someone involved in arboriculture or forestry, providing wood for the community.

EDCS-10100612.text_edition: Silvano Cor Aelius Maximus Proculini libens posuit
EDCS-10100612.translation: To Silvanus, Heart, Aelius Maximus, son of Proculinus, willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100612.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Silvanus, set up by Aelius Maximus, son of Proculinus.

EDCS-10100613.text_edition: Maximo i ICI ISIAI II IVAI as fratri  infelicissimo
EDCS-10100613.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a brother named Maximus, expressing sorrow for his fate.

EDCS-10100615.text_edition: Invicto aras Sisimbrius decurionum decreto libens posuit
EDCS-10100615.translation: To the Unconquered, Sisimbrius willingly placed altars by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-10100615.summary: This inscription records that Sisimbrius erected altars to an 'Unconquered' deity (likely Sol Invictus or Mithras) in accordance with a decree from the decurions (town councillors).

EDCS-10100616.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Crobia, placed by her husband, whose name is partially illegible.

EDCS-10100619.text_edition: Nymphis et Silvano sacrum Surus Pirami libens posuit
EDCS-10100619.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs and Silvanus. Surus, son of Píramus, willingly set this up.
EDCS-10100619.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs and Silvanus, made by Surus, son of Píramus, as a voluntary offering.

EDCS-10100620.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Dasius Gemelli filius libens merito
EDCS-10100620.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester. Dasius, son of Gemellus, willingly and deservedly [set this up].
EDCS-10100620.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Silvester, set up by Dasius, son of Gemellus, as a voluntary and well-deserved offering.

EDCS-10100622.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  et Genio loci Aelius Anteros beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum libens solvit Imperatore domino nostro Gallieno IIII et Volusiano consulibus Kalendis Aprilibus
EDCS-10100622.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Aelius Anteros, a beneficiarius consularis of the Legio XIIII Gemina, willingly fulfilled his vow on the Kalends of April, in the fourth consulship of our lord Emperor Gallienus and Volusianus.
EDCS-10100622.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Aelius Anteros, a military officer of the Legio XIIII Gemina, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius Loci, dated to April 1st of 262 AD.

EDCS-10100623.translation: [wife] of Scurmianus, while alive, for herself and...
EDCS-10100623.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a woman who was the wife of Scurmianus, and was set up by her for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-10100624.text_edition: et Genio loci Titus Coninius Ursus |(centurio) cohortis VIII voluntariorum gratias agens cum suis votum libens solvit
EDCS-10100624.translation: and to the Genius of the place, Titus Coninius Ursus, centurion of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, giving thanks with his family, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100624.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Titus Coninius Ursus, a centurion, to the Genius Loci, made with his family as an act of gratitude.

EDCS-10100625.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Celsinus votum libens solvit Imperatore domino nostro Gordiano Augusto II et Pompeiano consulibus
EDCS-10100625.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Publius Aelius Celsinus willingly fulfilled his vow in the second consulship of our lord Emperor Gordian Augustus and Pompeianus.
EDCS-10100625.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Aelius Celsinus to Hercules Augustus, dated to 239 AD.

EDCS-10100626.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Celsus PAIOI.
EDCS-10100626.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, for Aelius Celsus, with additional illegible text.

EDCS-10100630.text_edition: e Victorinae  et Secundparentes  pientissimi  bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-10100630.translation: ...and Victorina and Secund..., most devoted parents, placed this for their well-deserving [children/loved ones].
EDCS-10100630.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by devoted parents, Victorina and Secund..., for their well-deserving deceased.

EDCS-10100635.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri sacrum Cassius Aurelius CAEMI COSIRE POPI
EDCS-10100635.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvester. Cassius Aurelius CAEMI COSIRE POPI.
EDCS-10100635.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Silvester, made by Cassius Aurelius, with additional illegible text.

EDCS-10100636.text_edition: Aurelia Cassia mulier cum ais coniugi et filiae   defunct
EDCS-10100636.translation: Aurelia Cassia, a woman, with her husband and daughter, for the deceased.
EDCS-10100636.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Aurelia Cassia for her deceased husband and daughter, possibly marking their burial place.

EDCS-10100638.text_edition: Aelius Maximus Karus Epatinoni coniugi et Aelius Varro filius SINDOC
EDCS-10100638.translation: Aelius Maximus Karus for his wife Epatino, and Aelius Varro, his son.
EDCS-10100638.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aelius Maximus Karus for his wife Epatino, also mentioning his son, Aelius Varro.

EDCS-10100639.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Aurelius Proculus to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-10100640.text_edition: mius Ai usXXI o suis f invaratr suo bene merenti pe apsuit ruti suae socrui pientitissimae
EDCS-10100640.translation: mius Ai usXXI his f invaratr to his well-deserving one pe apsuit to his very devoted mother-in-law.
EDCS-10100640.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary dedication made for a well-deserving individual and a very devoted mother-in-law.

EDCS-10100641.text_edition: opostat IOVINIO  IIOIIS Macximiano sic OOCCASUESIN Masculi Avita MIT IIII FPM  sucvissima uxor OVICIS mieiivi
EDCS-10100641.translation: opostat JOVINIO IIOIIS Maximiano thus OOCCASUESIN Masculi Avita MIT IIII FPM my sweetest wife OVICIS mieiivi.
EDCS-10100641.summary: This heavily fragmented and corrupted inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly by a man to his 'sweetest wife'.

EDCS-10100643.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maximus Diti annorum LXX p v fecit hic situs est
EDCS-10100643.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maximus Ditus, 70 years of age. He made this in his lifetime. He is laid here.
EDCS-10100643.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Maximus Ditus, aged 70, which he had made for himself during his lifetime and marks his burial place.

EDCS-10100645.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavia Procula solvit votum libens
EDCS-10100645.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Flavia Procula willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10100645.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Flavia Procula to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100646.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Metilia Domitia ex voto
EDCS-10100646.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Metilia Domitia, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-10100646.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Metilia Domitia to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, offered as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100647.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary marker for Turus Sarius, indicating he lived for fifty years.

EDCS-10100649.summary: This fragmented inscription indicates that someone named Martinus erected or placed a monument, likely a funerary or votive dedication.

EDCS-10100650.text_edition: Dis Manibus Privato servo hic situs est
EDCS-10100650.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Here lies Privatus, a slave.
EDCS-10100650.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Privatus, a slave, indicating his burial site.

EDCS-10100651.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintianus annorum LXV posuit titulum Amata SIII suo c   V Valen
EDCS-10100651.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintianus, 65 years old, erected this monument. Amata [---] to her [---] Valen[---].
EDCS-10100651.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintianus, aged 65, and notes that Amata erected a monument, likely for him or a related individual, perhaps in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100652.text_edition: ritus Sili servos sic hic situs est  annorum IIII
EDCS-10100652.translation: Ritus, slave of Silius, lies here, 4 years old.
EDCS-10100652.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ritus, a 4-year-old slave belonging to Silius.

EDCS-10100653.text_edition: Dis Manibus Veneto Diti annorum XXV
EDCS-10100653.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Venetus Dit[us], 25 years old.
EDCS-10100653.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Venetus Dit[us], who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10100655.text_edition: Dis Manibus ni annorum XXV coniux posuit
EDCS-10100655.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [---]n[---], 25 years old. His wife erected this.
EDCS-10100655.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an unnamed individual, aged 25, erected by his wife.

EDCS-10100657.text_edition: Liberae Iulia Maximina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100657.translation: To Libera. Iulia Maximina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10100657.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulia Maximina, dedicating an offering to the goddess Libera in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10100659.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Maximus.
EDCS-10100659.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-10100660.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Iulius and Iucundus or Iucunda.

EDCS-10100661.text_edition: Dis Manibus ENDI I Secunannorum LXX VNE  filius OM matri pientissimae posuit hic sita est
EDCS-10100661.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [---]ndius Secun[---], 70 years old. His son [---] erected this for his most devoted mother. She lies here.
EDCS-10100661.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a mother, 70 years old, erected by her devoted son.

EDCS-10100663.text_edition: Silvano  Augusto  Mercurius argenti actor  votum libens posuit
EDCS-10100663.translation: To Silvanus Augustus. Mercurius, a silver merchant, willingly erected this vow.
EDCS-10100663.summary: This is a votive inscription by Mercurius, a silver merchant, dedicating an offering to the god Silvanus Augustus.

EDCS-10100664.translation: To the Divine Shades of Cesonia.
EDCS-10100664.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades of Cesonia.

EDCS-10100672.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Titus Aurelius Codalianus libens posuit
EDCS-10100672.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Titus Aurelius Codalianus willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100672.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Titus Aurelius Codalianus to Hercules Augustus, indicating a religious offering or monument.

EDCS-10100673.text_edition: Iovi Cohortali sacrum Maximus ex voto libens posuit pro suis omnibus
EDCS-10100673.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Cohortalis, Maximus willingly placed this in accordance with a vow for all his family members.
EDCS-10100673.summary: This is a votive inscription by Maximus, dedicated to Jupiter Cohortalis, fulfilling a vow on behalf of his entire family.

EDCS-10100674.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Vurus vilicus HA Secandoni parinti sic qui vixit annos LXXX et Sicundoni coniugi quae vixit annos XX et sibRi#sibi#SIRI vivus memoriam posuit
EDCS-10100674.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vurus, the overseer, to his parent Secundonius, who lived 80 years, and to his wife Secundonia, who lived 20 years, and for himself, alive, placed this memorial.
EDCS-10100674.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Vurus, an overseer, in memory of his deceased parents and wife, and for himself while still living.

EDCS-10100675.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum G Suricino et Septimiae Candidae avis qui vixerunt annos LXXV et illa annos LX et Aurelio Maximo decurioni municipii qui vixit annos LV et Aureliae Maditae quae vixit annos XXXX parentibus et Aureliae Venuconi sorori quae vixit annos XVI Aurelius Lavius decurio municipi et sibi vivus posuit
EDCS-10100675.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Gaius Suricinus and Septimia Candida, his grandparents, who lived 75 years, and she 60 years; and to Aurelius Maximus, decurion of the municipality, who lived 55 years; and to Aurelia Madita, who lived 40 years, his parents; and to Aurelia Venuco, his sister, who lived 16 years. Aurelius Lavius, decurion of the municipality, placed this, and for himself, while living.
EDCS-10100675.summary: This is a comprehensive funerary monument erected by Aurelius Lavius, a municipal decurion, commemorating multiple deceased family members, including grandparents, parents, and a sister, and also for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-10100683.text_edition: Dis Manibus sC(acrum)#s(acrum)#C Aemilii Antonius et Antoninus Aemilio Calvioni defuncto Salonis annorum? LXI
EDCS-10100683.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred. Antonius and Antoninus, for Aemilius Calvio, deceased at Salona, of 61 years.
EDCS-10100683.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Antonius and Antoninus in memory of Aemilius Calvio, who died at Salona at the age of 61.

EDCS-10100684.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Ingenuo qui vixit annos L et Pantoni coniugi eius quae vixit annos XXXV Titus Aurelius Maximus filius parentibus pro pietate
EDCS-10100684.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Titus Aurelius Ingenuus, who lived 50 years, and to Pantonia, his wife, who lived 35 years. Titus Aurelius Maximus, their son, placed this for his parents out of piety.
EDCS-10100684.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Aurelius Maximus for his deceased parents, Titus Aurelius Ingenuus and Pantonia, out of filial piety.

EDCS-10100686.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Titullae Argurianae quae vixit annos LX Aurelia Titulla Cambria amitae pro parte
EDCS-10100686.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Aurelia Titulla Arguriana, who lived 60 years. Aurelia Titulla Cambria, for her aunt, in part.
EDCS-10100686.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelia Titulla Cambria for her deceased aunt, Aurelia Titulla Arguriana.

EDCS-10100688.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesiae Asteridi quae vixit annos XXXV Caius Egnatius Montanus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100688.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Caesia Asteris, who lived 35 years. Gaius Egnatius Montanus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10100688.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Egnatius Montanus for his deceased wife, Caesia Asteris.

EDCS-10100689.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Gavieno Candidiano qui vixit annos XLV et Egnatiae Soteridi coniugi vivae Gavieni Valerianus et Egnatius et Candida filii parentibus posuerunt
EDCS-10100689.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Publius Gavienus Candidianus, who lived 45 years, and to Egnatia Soteris, his wife, who is alive. Gavieni Valerianus and Egnatius and Candida, their children, placed this for their parents.
EDCS-10100689.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by the children, Gavieni Valerianus, Egnatius, and Candida, for their deceased father, Publius Gavienus Candidianus, and for their living mother, Egnatia Soteris.

EDCS-10100690.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Paconio Barbarioni qui vixit annos XXXL etbuni coniugi eius vivae Valerius Faustus parentibus pientissimis posuit
EDCS-10100690.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Lucius Paconius Barbario, who lived for 30 years, and for his wife, Etbuni, while she lives. Valerius Faustus placed this for his most devoted parents.
EDCS-10100690.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Valerius Faustus to his deceased father, Lucius Paconius Barbario, and his living mother, Etbuni, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100691.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marco Plaetorio Ursino qui vixit annos LV Lucius Paconius Barbarioni et Ursina bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10100691.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Marcus Plaetorius Ursinus, who lived for 55 years. Lucius Paconius Barbario and Ursina placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10100691.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Plaetorius Ursinus, dedicated by Lucius Paconius Barbario and Ursina, likely dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-10100692.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iarito Arvi sibi posuit et Fuscae Anae coniugi suae dulcissimae quae vixit annos L
EDCS-10100692.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iaritus Arvus placed this for himself and for Fusca Ana, his sweetest wife, who lived for 50 years.
EDCS-10100692.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Iaritus Arvus for himself and his deceased wife, Fusca Ana, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100695.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aelius Faustus  et
EDCS-10100695.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Aelius Faustus and [---]
EDCS-10100695.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, mentioning Quintus Aelius Faustus as a dedicant or deceased, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-10100697.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Vendo sibi viva posuit et Aurelio Maximo militi filio qui vixit annos XXV
EDCS-10100697.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelia Vendo, while living, placed this for herself and for Aurelius Maximus, her soldier son, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-10100697.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Aurelia Vendo for herself and her deceased soldier son, Aurelius Maximus, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100702.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Feliciano militi legionis XIII geminae qui vixit annos XXV filio dulcissimo Maximus et Firma parentes
EDCS-10100702.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aelius Felicianus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, who lived for 25 years, their sweetest son. Maximus and Firma, his parents, [placed this].
EDCS-10100702.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maximus and Firma to their deceased son, Aelius Felicianus, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100703.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius vivus sibi et Aureliae Fuscae beneene  annorum XX mensium  rarissimae mulieri domum aeternam fecit posuit o v b
EDCS-10100703.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius, while living, made and placed this eternal home for himself and for Aurelia Fusca, a very rare woman of 20 years and [---] months, well-deserving. O. V. B. (meaning obscure).
EDCS-10100703.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius for himself and his deceased wife, Aurelia Fusca, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100704.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  AITVIQ C ICP XII Sextus CP
EDCS-10100704.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. [---] Sextus CP [---]
EDCS-10100704.summary: This is a highly fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades, mentioning a Sextus, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-10100707.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Fusco filio Aurelia Fusca mater qui vixit annos L
EDCS-10100707.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Fuscus, her son. Aurelia Fusca, his mother, [placed this]. He lived for 50 years.
EDCS-10100707.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Fusca to her deceased son, Aurelius Fuscus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100708.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aur Er defunct-- annos LV et Aur Co defunct-- annos XIIX et Aur Amand defunct-- annos IIII Aur Teu ETAVROSI TGASIOVER suis defunctis
EDCS-10100708.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Er, deceased, aged 55 years, and Aurelius Co, deceased, aged 18 years, and Aurelius Amandus, deceased, aged 4 years. Aurelius Teu [---] and Aurelius TGASI OVER [placed this] for his deceased relatives.
EDCS-10100708.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Teu and another individual (Aurelius TGASI OVER) to multiple deceased relatives, Aurelius Er, Aurelius Co, and Aurelius Amandus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100710.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Aplio Plasso patri pientissimo decurioni municipii Dilunti et Anniae filiae infelicissimae defunctae annorum VIIII Publius Aplius Annius vivus sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-10100710.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Aplius Annius, while alive, made this for himself and his family, for Publius Aplius Plassus, his most devoted father, a decurion of the municipality of Diluntum, and for Annaea, his most unfortunate daughter, who died at nine years of age.
EDCS-10100710.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Publius Aplius Annius for himself and his family, including his father, a municipal decurion, and his young deceased daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100712.text_edition: Deae Iunoni sanctae aram posuit Iunia Varena
EDCS-10100712.translation: Iunia Varena placed this altar for the sacred goddess Juno.
EDCS-10100712.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording an altar set up by Iunia Varena to the goddess Juno, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100713.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli-- V coniugi NIISN
EDCS-10100713.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aureli--, for his wife NIISN.
EDCS-10100713.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Shades for a wife by Aureli--.

EDCS-10100716.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelius   o  Valeriae quae defuncta annorum VIIII  parentesNIS SFII filiae infelicissimae
EDCS-10100716.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelius [--] for Valeria, who died at nine years of age, [her] most unfortunate daughter.
EDCS-10100716.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a young deceased daughter named Valeria, dedicated by her parents, Aelius and another individual, to the Divine Shades, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100719.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Victorinae defunctae annorum LXXV Flavia Flaccilla matri
EDCS-10100719.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Flaccilla [made this] for her mother, Aelia Victorina, who died at seventy-five years of age.
EDCS-10100719.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by Flavia Flaccilla to her deceased mother, Aelia Victorina, to the Divine Shades, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100720.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Liciniano decurioni municipii Bistuensium IIviro defuncto annorum
EDCS-10100720.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Titus Flavius Licinianus, a decurion of the municipality of Bistua, a duumvir, who died at [--] years of age.
EDCS-10100720.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Licinianus, a municipal decurion and duumvir, dedicated to the Divine Shades, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100722.text_edition: Dis Manibus Severus posuit sibi et suis
EDCS-10100722.translation: To the Divine Shades. Severus placed this for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100722.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Severus for himself and his family, dedicated to the Divine Shades, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100723.text_edition: Flaviae Iuliae Domitillae uxori me  posuit
EDCS-10100723.translation: [---] placed this for Flavia Julia Domitilla, my wife.
EDCS-10100723.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Flavia Julia Domitilla, dedicated by her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100728.translation: [---] most devoted, and Buvo, his wife.
EDCS-10100728.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that mentions a devoted individual and his wife Buvo, likely part of a larger family monument.

EDCS-10100730.text_edition: sacrum Publius Aelius Pinnes? v(otum) solvit loco dato publice
EDCS-10100730.translation: Sacred. Publius Aelius Pinnes? fulfilled his vow, with the place given publicly.
EDCS-10100730.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Publius Aelius Pinnes fulfilling a vow, with the site being publicly granted, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100731.text_edition: annorum LXXII dierum XXVII Aelia Tertulla marito inconparabili sic et Aeli Saturninus et Rufinus patri piissimo posuerunt et sibi et suis
EDCS-10100731.translation: Aelia Tertulla set this up for her incomparable husband, who lived 72 years and 27 days. Aelius Saturninus and Rufinus likewise set this up for their most devoted father, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-10100731.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Tertulla to her husband, and by Aelius Saturninus and Rufinus to their father, also for themselves and their family.

EDCS-10100732.text_edition: Dis Manibus qui vixit annos LXV posuit Aelius Titus Batonis et sibi
EDCS-10100732.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Titus Batonis set this up for the one who lived 65 years, and for himself.
EDCS-10100732.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Titus Batonis to an unnamed individual who lived 65 years, and also for himself.

EDCS-10100733.text_edition: Dis Manibus coniux et filii sui Aelio Varroni Loisci qui vixit annis XXXX
EDCS-10100733.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. His wife and sons set this up for Aelius Varro Loisci, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-10100733.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his wife and sons to Aelius Varro Loisci, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-10100734.text_edition: ANTV Aurelius Litus Zuni coniugi karissimae posuit et sibi et  suis
EDCS-10100734.translation: Aurelius Litus Zuni set this up for his dearest wife, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100734.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Litus Zuni to his wife, also for himself and his family.

EDCS-10100735.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maximus Beusantis vixit annos LX posuerunt fili pientissimo bene merenti
EDCS-10100735.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Maximus Beusantis lived 60 years. His most devoted sons set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10100735.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his devoted sons to Maximus Beusantis, who lived 60 years and was well-deserving.

EDCS-10100736.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iaetius Varro Pinnetis.
EDCS-10100736.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Iaetius Varro Pinnetis.

EDCS-10100741.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Titus Dasantis se vivo posuit et Aeliae Seioni IMPL coniugi piae defunctae annorum LX posuit et suis
EDCS-10100741.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Titus Dasantis set this up while still alive, and for Aelia Seioni, his pious deceased wife, who lived 60 years, and for his family.
EDCS-10100741.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Titus Dasantis for himself while alive, for his deceased wife Aelia Seioni who lived 60 years, and for his family.

EDCS-10100742.text_edition: Iato Nepotis vixit annos L posuerunt fili et frater
EDCS-10100742.translation: Iato Nepotis lived 50 years. His sons and brother set this up.
EDCS-10100742.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his sons and brother to Iato Nepotis, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-10100743.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Secundi posuit filiae Quiconiugi et suis
EDCS-10100743.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Secundus set this up for his daughter Quiconiugi and his family.
EDCS-10100743.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Secundus to his daughter Quiconiugi and his family.

EDCS-10100744.text_edition: Varronis vixit annos   posuit filiae et suorum
EDCS-10100744.translation: Varronis lived [---] years. He set this up for his daughter and his family.
EDCS-10100744.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Varronis for his daughter and his family, with the age of Varronis being illegible.

EDCS-10100746.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication, mentioning the son of someone named Maxius or similar.

EDCS-10100747.translation: Publius Aelius Proculus of the municipality A[...]
EDCS-10100747.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely identifying Publius Aelius Proculus as a citizen or resident of an unspecified municipality beginning with 'A'.

EDCS-10100748.text_edition: O vi Surionis votum solvit merito libens
EDCS-10100748.translation: O, to Surio, he willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10100748.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to a deity or person named Surio, made willingly and deservedly by an unnamed dedicant.

EDCS-10100750.text_edition: Firmus onis filius d  VC votum solvit libens
EDCS-10100750.translation: Firmus, son of Onis, d. VC, willingly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10100750.summary: This inscription records Firmus, son of Onis, fulfilling a vow willingly, with the meaning of 'd. VC' being unclear without further context.

EDCS-10100753.text_edition: annorum XXXII Aur(elii) unaius et Supera parentes filio carissimo - Aurelius Iucundus fratri pientissimo
EDCS-10100753.translation: To their dearest son, aged 32 years, Aurelius Unaius and Supera, his parents, [set this up]; Aurelius Iucundus [set this up] to his most devoted brother.
EDCS-10100753.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Unaius and Supera to their deceased son, aged 32, and by Aurelius Iucundus to his most devoted brother, likely the same individual.

EDCS-10100756.text_edition: Nepos cum filiis s(olvit) votum libens merito
EDCS-10100756.translation: Nepos, with his sons, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10100756.summary: This inscription records Nepos and his sons willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-10100757.text_edition: Messiae defunctae annorum XVII parentes et sibi
EDCS-10100757.translation: To Messia, deceased at 17 years of age, her parents, and for themselves.
EDCS-10100757.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents for their deceased daughter Messia, who lived for 17 years, and also for themselves, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-10100765.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Primus Vepi libens posuit
EDCS-10100765.translation: Primus, son of Vepus, willingly set this up for Silvanus Silvester.
EDCS-10100765.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Primus, son of Vepus, to the god Silvanus Silvester.

EDCS-10100767.text_edition: Dis Manibus  filiae vixit annos XVI Plator Carvius Batonis posuit
EDCS-10100767.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Plator Carvius Batonius set this up for his daughter, who lived for 16 years.
EDCS-10100767.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Plator Carvius Batonius for his daughter, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-10100768.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulgerali Vesii Ravonius et Hermes votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-10100768.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgeralis, Vesius Ravonius and Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10100768.summary: This is a votive inscription where Vesius Ravonius and Hermes fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgeralis (Jupiter the Thunderer).

EDCS-10100769.text_edition: Valent militi legionis II adiutricis defuncto annorum XXX memoriam posuit Tata pientissima coniux
EDCS-10100769.translation: To Valens, soldier of the Second Legion Adiutrix, deceased at 30 years old, his most devoted wife Tata erected this memorial.
EDCS-10100769.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Tata for her deceased husband Valens, a soldier of the Legio II Adiutrix, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100770.text_edition: Publius Aelius Firmus filius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100770.translation: Publius Aelius Firmus, his son, erected this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-10100770.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Firmus for his meritorious father.

EDCS-10100771.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly funerary or honorific, dedicated to someone named Aelius Maximus.

EDCS-10100772.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto decreto
EDCS-10100772.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, by decree.
EDCS-10100772.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Gordian III, dating to his reign (238-244 AD), erected by decree.

EDCS-10100773.text_edition: ae Priscillae coniugi parentes vivi fecerunt
EDCS-10100773.translation: His living parents made this for Priscilla, their wife/daughter.
EDCS-10100773.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by living parents for their deceased daughter Priscilla, or by a husband's parents for his deceased wife Priscilla.

EDCS-10100774.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum T Frontoni patri pientissimo qui vixit annos LV L Marcus filius ei et sibi vivus posuit
EDCS-10100774.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Titus Frontonius, most devoted father, who lived 55 years. Lucius Marcus, his son, erected this for him and for himself while living.
EDCS-10100774.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Frontonius by his son Lucius Marcus, who also prepared a burial place for himself, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100775.text_edition: Herculi Augusto Sextus Aelius Domitianus pius posuit
EDCS-10100775.translation: Sextus Aelius Domitianus, pious, erected this for Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-10100775.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Sextus Aelius Domitianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100776.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Valerius Licinius decurio IIvir vixit annos XXXV Procilla coniunx ipsius vixit annos XX Lucius Valerius Montanus et Baebia Iulla parentes filio pientissimo et sibi vivis memoriam posuerunt
EDCS-10100776.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Valerius Licinius, decurion and duumvir, lived 35 years. Procilla, his wife, lived 20 years. Lucius Valerius Montanus and Baebia Iulla, his parents, erected this memorial for their most devoted son and for themselves while living.
EDCS-10100776.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Valerius Montanus and Baebia Iulla to their son Lucius Valerius Licinius, a decurion and duumvir, and his wife Procilla, also preparing a burial place for themselves, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100777.text_edition: Anancae pro salute Valeri Liciniani fili Lucius Coelius Valerianus pater libens laetus
EDCS-10100777.translation: To Ananca, for the health of his son Valerius Licinianus, Lucius Coelius Valerianus, father, willingly, gladly.
EDCS-10100777.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Ananca by Lucius Coelius Valerianus, made on behalf of the health of his son Valerius Licinianus.

EDCS-10100778.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Candaviensi Titus Flavius Dionysius donum posuit
EDCS-10100778.translation: Titus Flavius Dionysius placed this gift for Diana Augusta Candaviensis.
EDCS-10100778.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta Candaviensis by Titus Flavius Dionysius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100779.text_edition: Deis omnibus Casticius Tertullinus ex voto
EDCS-10100779.translation: To all the gods, Casticius Tertullinus (made this offering) in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10100779.summary: This is a votive inscription by Casticius Tertullinus dedicated to all the gods, likely on an altar or monument, made after fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100780.text_edition: Iovi Antra--rim sacrum Titus Flavius Liberalis ex voto succepto sic
EDCS-10100780.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Antra--rim. Titus Flavius Liberalis (made this) in fulfillment of a vow undertaken.
EDCS-10100780.summary: This votive inscription records Titus Flavius Liberalis's offering to Jupiter Antra--rim, fulfilling a vow, likely placed on an altar or monument during the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100781.text_edition: Iovi Cohortali Augusto Iulius Eutyches libertus pro salute patroni sui Iuli Vinciani votum  libens solvit
EDCS-10100781.translation: To Jupiter Cohortalis Augustus, Julius Eutyches, a freedman, willingly fulfilled his vow for the well-being of his patron, Julius Vincianus.
EDCS-10100781.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julius Eutyches, a freedman, dedicated to Jupiter Cohortalis Augustus for the health of his patron, Julius Vincianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100782.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Eponae Reginae Genio loci Caius Ocratius Lacon beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Crispino et Aeliano consulibus
EDCS-10100782.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Queen Epona, to the Genius of the place, Gaius Ocratius Lacon, a beneficiarius consularis of the First Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Crispinus and Aelianus.
EDCS-10100782.summary: This votive inscription, dated to the consulship of Crispinus and Aelianus (223 AD), records Gaius Ocratius Lacon, a military officer, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Epona Regina, and the Genius Loci.

EDCS-10100784.text_edition: Domno et Domnae Petronia Valentina votum libens solvit
EDCS-10100784.translation: To the Lord and Lady, Petronia Valentina willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10100784.summary: This is a votive inscription by Petronia Valentina, who willingly fulfilled a vow to 'the Lord and Lady', likely referring to specific deities or a deified couple, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100786.text_edition: Marco Antonio Euticho collegium fabrum fecit
EDCS-10100786.translation: The guild of craftsmen made this for Marcus Antonius Eutychus.
EDCS-10100786.summary: This inscription records that the collegium fabrum (guild of craftsmen) made something (likely a monument or dedication) for Marcus Antonius Eutychus, suggesting a public honor or memorial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100787.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Cassio Valerio Epidiano qui vixit annos XV menses XI dies XXVII Valerius Marcianus et Epidia Tatta parentes filio pientissimo
EDCS-10100787.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Titus Cassius Valerius Epidianus, who lived fifteen years, eleven months, and twenty-seven days. Valerius Marcianus and Epidia Tatta, his parents, (made this) for their most devoted son.
EDCS-10100787.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Titus Cassius Valerius Epidianus, erected by his parents Valerius Marcianus and Epidia Tatta, marking the grave of their son who died young, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100788.text_edition: T(ito) Flavio Titi filio Quirina Basso decurioni ex testamento eius
EDCS-10100788.translation: For Titus Flavius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, Bassus, a decurion, according to his will.
EDCS-10100788.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Titus Flavius Bassus, a decurion, made according to his will, likely a funerary monument or a public endowment, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100789.text_edition: Flavia Phoebe quae vixit nnos VI Iulia Domitia mater fecit
EDCS-10100789.translation: Julia Domitia, her mother, made this for Flavia Phoebe, who lived six years.
EDCS-10100789.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Julia Domitia for her daughter, Flavia Phoebe, who died at the age of six, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100790.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Marcio Cilici decurioni Calabro domo Lypias sic vixit annos XX Caius Marcius Firmus filio piissimo fecit
EDCS-10100790.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Marcius Cilicius, a decurion from Calabria, from the home (or town) of Lypias. He lived twenty years. Gaius Marcius Firmus made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-10100790.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorates Gaius Marcius Cilicius, a decurion from Lypias in Calabria, and was erected by his father, Gaius Marcius Firmus, for his son who died at age 20, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100791.text_edition: Dis Manibus Numeriae Clementillae coniugi Verrius Mercator maritus
EDCS-10100791.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Numeria Clementilla, Verrius Mercator, her husband, dedicated this to his wife.
EDCS-10100791.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Verrius Mercator commemorates his wife, Numeria Clementilla.

EDCS-10100792.text_edition: Tito Oclatio Severo et Laetiliae Trhepte sic et suis Dis Manibus
EDCS-10100792.translation: To Titus Oclatius Severus and Laetilia Threpte, and likewise to their own Divine Spirits.
EDCS-10100792.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits of Titus Oclatius Severus and Laetilia Threpte, likely a funerary monument for a couple.

EDCS-10100793.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to Lucius Titius Cili[...].

EDCS-10100794.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Lucio Verrio Firmo qui vixit annos XXXIII Marcia Procula marito optimo pientissimo bene merenti fecit
EDCS-10100794.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcia Procula made this for her most excellent and devoted husband, Lucius Verrius Firmus, who lived for 33 years, as he was well deserving.
EDCS-10100794.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcia Procula dedicating a monument to her beloved husband, Lucius Verrius Firmus, who died at age 33.

EDCS-10100795.text_edition: Caio Aa Felici domo Salona defuncto annorum XXX Octavia Acantis marito
EDCS-10100795.translation: To Gaius Aelius Felix, from Salona, who died at 30 years of age. Octavia Acantis [made this for her] husband.
EDCS-10100795.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Octavia Acantis to her husband, Gaius Aelius Felix, who originated from Salona and died at age 30.

EDCS-10100796.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Faustino patri piissimo qui vixit annos IX avia filia fecit
EDCS-10100796.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. The grandmother, his daughter, made this for Faustinus, her most devoted father, who lived for 9 years.
EDCS-10100796.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by a grandmother (who is also described as a daughter, possibly indicating a complex family relationship or an error) to her young father, Faustinus, who lived for 9 years.

EDCS-10100797.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum miserrimo et infelicissimo Libonio qui vixit annos LV menses VIII dies V er Procula fratri pientissimo
EDCS-10100797.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To the most wretched and unfortunate Libonius, who lived for 55 years, 8 months, and 5 days. Procula made this for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-10100797.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Procula to her most devoted brother, Libonius, detailing his age at death and emphasizing his unfortunate fate.

EDCS-10100798.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Temusa Baiula Boia.
EDCS-10100798.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Temusa Baiula Boia.

EDCS-10100803.text_edition: Plaetoriae Titullae Plaetoria Iulla matri pientissimae
EDCS-10100803.translation: Plaetoria Iulla [made this] for Plaetoria Titulla, her most devoted mother.
EDCS-10100803.summary: This inscription records Plaetoria Iulla dedicating a monument to her highly devoted mother, Plaetoria Titulla, likely a funerary commemoration.

EDCS-10100804.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Crinnetisa Harmoniae Libonia Placida munus cum natis suis fecerunt
EDCS-10100804.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Crinnetisa and Harmonia, Libonia Placida, together with their children, made this gift.
EDCS-10100804.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits, recording that Crinnetisa, Harmonia, and Libonia Placida, along with their children, made this offering or monument.

EDCS-10100806.text_edition: Marcus Caesius Epidianus vivus fecit sibi et suis sepulturam
EDCS-10100806.translation: Marcus Caesius Epidianus, while alive, made this burial place for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100806.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marcus Caesius Epidianus prepared his own burial plot for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-10100807.text_edition: Agirro Epicadi filio principi kastelli Salthua et Temeiae Glavi filiae fecit Epicadus filius ut primum aetatis complerunt tempora vitae et genitor fato conditus est tumulo protinus inscripsit pietas nomen matrisque patrisque ut legerent cuncti genitoris nomina saepe hoc decet ut nati componant ossa parentum et cineri sedem substituat pietas
EDCS-10100807.translation: Epicadus, his son, made this for Agirrus, son of Epicadus, chief of the fortress of Salthua, and for Temeia, daughter of Glavus. As soon as they completed the span of their life's ages and the father was laid to rest by fate, piety immediately inscribed the names of both mother and father on the tomb, so that all might often read the names of the parent. It is fitting that children arrange the bones of their parents and that piety establishes a resting place for their ashes.
EDCS-10100807.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tomb epigram, in which Epicadus dedicates a monument to his parents, Agirrus (a fort chief) and Temeia, after their deaths, emphasizing filial piety.
