EDCS-10100808.text_edition: Caius Epicadi filius princeps civitatis Docleatium hic situs hoc fieri iussit genitor sibi et suis set sic fili eius Plassus Epicadus Scerdis Verzo et summa adiecta effeIcit sic#effecit sic#EFFICIT istud opus est pietas natique hoc auxsisse sic videntur et decorant facto et docent esse pios
EDCS-10100808.translation: Gaius, son of Epicadus, chief of the community of Docleatium, lies here; he ordered this to be made for himself and his family. But his sons Plassus, Epicadus, Scerdis, and Verzo, with a great addition, thus completed this work. It is piety, and his sons are seen to have augmented this thus, and they honor by their deed and show themselves to be dutiful.
EDCS-10100808.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius, son of Epicadus and chief of the Docleatians, which he commissioned for himself and his family, but his sons augmented and completed the work out of piety.

EDCS-10100810.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Asellioni annorum III Alexandria icin
EDCS-10100810.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Asellio, three years old. Alexandria [made/placed this].
EDCS-10100810.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for a young child named Asellio, aged three, likely placed by Alexandria.

EDCS-10100811.text_edition: Statia Luci filia Clementia Iunoni Lucinae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100811.translation: Statia Clementia, daughter of Lucius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Juno Lucina.
EDCS-10100811.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording that Statia Clementia, daughter of Lucius, fulfilled a vow to the goddess Juno Lucina, who was associated with childbirth and light.

EDCS-10100817.text_edition: Posuit uxxor marito titulum bene morientissima
EDCS-10100817.translation: His wife, most devoted, placed this inscription for her husband.
EDCS-10100817.summary: This is a funerary inscription, placed by a very devoted wife for her deceased husband.

EDCS-10100818.text_edition: Aesculapio sacrum Marcus Caesius Marci libertus Primus Sextus Herennius Sexti Sexti libertus Eros IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem
EDCS-10100818.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius. Marcus Caesius Primus, freedman of Marcus, and Sextus Herennius Eros, freedman of Sextus Sextus, members of the college of six masters of Mercuriales, [placed this] in honor.
EDCS-10100818.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Aesculapius, erected by two freedmen who were part of the college of 'sexviri magistri Mercuriales', likely in fulfillment of a vow or as a public dedication for an honor received.

EDCS-10100819.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Atilia Tranquilla votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100819.translation: Sacred to Augusta Fortuna. Atilia Tranquilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10100819.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription in which Atilia Tranquilla fulfilled a vow to Fortuna Augusta, an imperial manifestation of the goddess of fortune.

EDCS-10100820.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo aeterno sacrum Valerius Alexander ex voto posuit pro coniuge sua
EDCS-10100820.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus. Valerius Alexander placed this according to a vow for his wife.
EDCS-10100820.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus, placed by Valerius Alexander in fulfillment of a vow made on behalf of his wife.

EDCS-10100821.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno sacrum posuerunt sacerdotes Flavius Faladus et Domitius Apollinaris pro salute Imperatoris Helvi Pertenacis sic et populi cRoloniae#coloniae#RO Iuliae Naronae
EDCS-10100821.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. The priests Flavius Faladus and Domitius Apollinaris placed this for the health of Emperor Helvius Pertinax and the people of Colonia Iulia Narona.
EDCS-10100821.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, erected by two priests, Flavius Faladus and Domitius Apollinaris, for the well-being of Emperor Helvius Pertinax and the community of Colonia Iulia Narona, likely dating to 193 AD.

EDCS-10100822.text_edition: Marcus Paenius Augurinus locus datus publice decreto decurionum
EDCS-10100822.translation: Marcus Paenius Augurinus. A plot given publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-10100822.summary: This inscription records that a burial plot was publicly granted to Marcus Paenius Augurinus by decree of the town council (decurions).

EDCS-10100824.text_edition: rnus posuit pro salute  ex voto
EDCS-10100824.translation: [---]rnus placed this for the well-being [---] from a vow.
EDCS-10100824.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication made in fulfillment of a vow for someone's well-being by a person whose name ends in '-rnus'.

EDCS-10100825.summary: This inscription is a dedication or part of a monument honoring Publius Servilius Isauricus, who held the office of consul.

EDCS-10100826.text_edition: Aulo Ducenio Gemino consuli XVviro sacris faciundis sodali Augustali curatori vectigalium publicorum legato pro praetore patrono
EDCS-10100826.translation: To Aulus Ducenius Geminus, consul, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, Augustan sodalis, curator of public revenues, propraetorian legate, patron.
EDCS-10100826.summary: This inscription lists the distinguished political and religious career of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, who served as consul, a priest, and held various administrative and patronal roles.

EDCS-10100828.text_edition: Quintus Lusius Seleucus Quintus Lusius Secundus  Lucius Afidius Pisaurio Caius Valerius Castrensis  Quintus Iulius Ursio Publius Licinius Leo  Quintus Lusius Liberalis Quintus Lusius Glycon  Quintus Stertinius Paris Publius Saenius Lupus  Tiberius Claudius Latini libertus Fortunatus Quintus Antonius Felix  Marcus Vinicius Masurius Severus Publius Annaeus Philetus  Publius Publicius Tryphon Lucius Cerennius Fortunatus  Lucius Ostorius Dionysius
EDCS-10100828.translation: Quintus Lusius Seleucus, Quintus Lusius Secundus, Lucius Afidius Pisaurio, Gaius Valerius Castrensis, Quintus Iulius Ursio, Publius Licinius Leo, Quintus Lusius Liberalis, Quintus Lusius Glycon, Quintus Stertinius Paris, Publius Saenius Lupus, Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus, freedman of Latinius, Quintus Antonius Felix, Marcus Vinicius Masurius Severus, Publius Annaeus Philetus, Publius Publicius Tryphon, Lucius Cerennius Fortunatus, Lucius Ostorius Dionysius.
EDCS-10100828.summary: This inscription is a list of names, likely members of a collegium, a group of benefactors, or simply a collection of individuals for whom a monument was erected.

EDCS-10100830.text_edition: $ Publi]cio Quinti filio Nic aedili IIIIviro  ai Quinti filiae Secundinae - Publicius Cai filius Nicephorus
EDCS-10100830.translation: To Publi[cius], son of Quintus, Nice, aedile, quattuorvir, [to Cai]a Secundina, daughter of Quintus. Publicius Nicephorus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-10100830.summary: This inscription records a dedication, possibly sepulchral, honoring Publicius Nice, an aedile and quattuorvir, and Caia Secundina, with Publicius Nicephorus also mentioned, likely as a dedicant.

EDCS-10100831.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Claudio Mascelioni annorum XXXIIII IIIIIIviro qui libertatem tus  et
EDCS-10100831.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Claudius Mascelio, 34 years old, sevir, who [received] freedom and [---].
EDCS-10100831.summary: This sepulchral inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and commemorates Claudius Mascelio, a sevir, who died at the age of 34 and had acquired his freedom.

EDCS-10100832.text_edition: Sextus Herennius Sexti libertus Eros IIIIIIvir Licinia Quinti filia Iucunda Herennia Sexti filia Tyche Herennia Sexti liberta
EDCS-10100832.translation: Sextus Herennius Eros, freedman of Sextus, sevir. Licinia Iucunda, daughter of Quintus. Herennia Tyche, daughter of Sextus. Herennia, freedwoman of Sextus.
EDCS-10100832.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, including Sextus Herennius Eros, a freedman and sevir, along with Licinia Iucunda, Herennia Tyche, and another Herennia, likely indicating family members or associates on a tombstone.

EDCS-10100833.text_edition: Caio Iunio Cai liberto Cisso IIIIIIviro collegium fabrum
EDCS-10100833.translation: To Gaius Iunius Cissus, freedman of Gaius, sevir. The college of craftsmen.
EDCS-10100833.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the college of craftsmen (collegium fabrum) to Gaius Iunius Cissus, a freedman and a sevir Augustalis.

EDCS-10100834.text_edition: Papiae Luci filiae Maximae Caius Agrius Cai libertus Fortunatus IIIIIIvir coniugi bene merenti et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-10100834.translation: Gaius Agrius Fortunatus, freedman of Gaius, sevir, made this in his lifetime for Papia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, his well-deserving wife, and for his own family.
EDCS-10100834.summary: This funerary inscription records that Gaius Agrius Fortunatus, a freedman and sevir, had a monument erected during his lifetime for his deserving wife, Papia Maxima, and for his entire family.

EDCS-10100836.text_edition: Quinto Nassio Iuliano haruspici Iulianus et Rufinus filii parenti optimo
EDCS-10100836.translation: To Quintus Nassius Julianus, soothsayer. Julianus and Rufinus, his sons, to their excellent father.
EDCS-10100836.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Julianus and Rufinus to their father, Quintus Nassius Julianus, who was a soothsayer.

EDCS-10100837.text_edition: Umbrio Lu coniugi incomparabili qui vixit annis XXXII Queta posuit
EDCS-10100837.translation: To Umbrio Lu, an incomparable husband, who lived for 32 years. Queta erected this.
EDCS-10100837.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Queta for her incomparable husband, Umbrio Lu, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-10100838.text_edition: Valeriae Piae nnorum XIIII Ravonia Procilla mater filiae pientissimae viva et sibi et posteris suis fecit
EDCS-10100838.translation: To Valeria Pia, 14 years old. Ravonia Procilla, her mother, made this for her most devoted daughter while alive, and for herself and her descendants.
EDCS-10100838.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ravonia Procilla for her devoted daughter, Valeria Pia, who died at 14, and also for herself and her future descendants.

EDCS-10100842.text_edition: o Luci filio Ti no Ae
EDCS-10100842.translation: to Lucius, son of Titus, [---] Aeneas.
EDCS-10100842.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a Lucius, son of Titus, possibly with a cognomen related to Aeneas.

EDCS-10100845.translation: to Spurius, son of Spurius, Rusticus.
EDCS-10100845.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Spurius Rusticus, son of Spurius.

EDCS-10100849.text_edition: Quintus Trebianus Auctus hic situs est
EDCS-10100849.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Quintus Trebianus Auctus.

EDCS-10100850.text_edition: Dis Manibus Amaerimno generi pientissimo bene merenti vixit nnos XXV Restitutianus et Calliste
EDCS-10100850.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Amaerimnus, most devoted and well-deserving son-in-law, who lived for 25 years. Restitutianus and Calliste [made this].
EDCS-10100850.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Restitutianus and Calliste to their beloved son-in-law, Amaerimnus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-10100852.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription mentioning the names Malaius and Messor, possibly indicating co-dedicants or subjects of commemoration.

EDCS-10100853.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Fresio Valentino veterano ex |(centurione) cohortis II miliariae Dalmatarum
EDCS-10100853.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Fresius Valentinus, veteran ex-centurion of the Second Cohort of Dalmatian Millenarii.
EDCS-10100853.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Fresius Valentinus, a veteran who served as a centurion in a Dalmatian cohort.

EDCS-10100854.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Atilia Ermetilla Iulio Urso coniugi suo bene merenti PSVDA PM annorum XXXX
EDCS-10100854.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Atilia Ermetilla to Julius Ursus, her well-deserving husband. He lived 40 years.
EDCS-10100854.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Atilia Ermetilla to her well-deserving husband, Julius Ursus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-10100855.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Flavius Fortunatus maritus Atiliae Quartae uxori bene merenti fecit vixit nnos XXXV
EDCS-10100855.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Fortunatus, her husband, made this for Atilia Quarta, his well-deserving wife. She lived for 35 years.
EDCS-10100855.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication to his deceased wife, who lived for 35 years.

EDCS-10100857.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Marciana defuncta annorum XX Aelius Dracontius maritus coniugi pientissimae posuit
EDCS-10100857.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelius Dracontius, her husband, set this up for Aelia Marciana, his most devoted wife, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-10100857.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelius Dracontius for his most devoted wife, Aelia Marciana, who passed away at twenty years old.

EDCS-10100858.text_edition: s Publi filius Tromentina Restitutus aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo  et o Hermae IIIIIIviro Augustali patri et Secundae matri testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10100858.translation: P. Restitutus, son of Publius, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile, quattuorvir for administering justice, and also a sexvir Augustalis, ordered this to be made by his will for his father Herma and his mother Secunda.
EDCS-10100858.summary: This inscription records the testamentary wishes of P. Restitutus, a local magistrate and priest, for the erection of a monument to his parents, Herma and Secunda.

EDCS-10100861.text_edition: Lucius Oppius Luci filius Sergia Segninus domo Conana veteranus legionis VII nnum LXX
EDCS-10100861.translation: Lucius Oppius Segninus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Conana, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 70 years.
EDCS-10100861.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucius Oppius Segninus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, specifying his origin, tribe, and age at seventy years.

EDCS-10100863.text_edition: Pollia  miles legionis I adiutricis annorum XXX stipendiorum X Attius Domitius miles legionis II adiutricis procurator posuit
EDCS-10100863.translation: Pollia, a soldier of the First Auxiliary Legion, aged 30, with 10 years of service. Attius Domitius, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion, a procurator, set this up.
EDCS-10100863.summary: This inscription records the death of Pollia, a soldier of the First Auxiliary Legion, and notes that Attius Domitius, a soldier of the Second Auxiliary Legion and procurator, set it up.

EDCS-10100864.text_edition: Marcus Livius Marci filius Palatina domo Aloro veteranus legionis VII annorum L stipendiorum XXVI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10100864.translation: Marcus Livius, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, from Alorus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 50, with 26 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10100864.summary: This funerary inscription for Marcus Livius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion from Alorus, states he was buried here and had the monument erected by his will.

EDCS-10100865.text_edition: Titus Varius Titi filius Velina domo Pessinunte veteranus legionis VII annorum L stipendiorum XXIX hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-10100865.translation: Titus Varius, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe, from Pessinus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 50, with 29 years of service, is buried here. His heir set this up.
EDCS-10100865.summary: This funerary inscription marks the burial place of Titus Varius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion from Pessinus, and was erected by his heir.

EDCS-10100866.text_edition: Q(uinto) Caenio Quinti filio equiti cohortis III Alpinorum domo Velauno nnorum  stipendiorum XX
EDCS-10100866.translation: To Quintus Caenius, son of Quintus, a cavalryman of the Third Alpine Cohort, from Velaunum, with [---] years and 20 years of service.
EDCS-10100866.summary: This inscription commemorates Quintus Caenius, a cavalryman of the Third Alpine Cohort from Velaunum, noting his 20 years of military service.

EDCS-10100868.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae B coniugi bene merenti quae vixit mecum annos XXII et tulit aetatis suae annos XL Flavius Aurelianus decurio cohortis I Belgarum posuit natione Pannonia
EDCS-10100868.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Aurelianus, a decurion of the First Cohort of Belgae, of Pannonian nationality, set this up for Iulia B, his well-deserving wife, who lived with him for 22 years and reached the age of 40.
EDCS-10100868.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Flavius Aurelianus, a Pannonian decurion, to his well-deserving wife, Iulia B, who lived with him for 22 years and died at 40.

EDCS-10100869.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Verecundo |(centurioni) cohortis I Belgarum vixit nnos XXXVIIIII stipendiorum XXI testamento fieri iussit ex HS mille nummum Postimia Restituta uxor pientissimo fieri curavit ex HS MDCmille sescentis nummum
EDCS-10100869.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Gaius Iulius Verecundus, centurion of the First Cohort of Belgae, who lived for 39 years and served for 21 years. He ordered this to be made by his will from 1000 sesterces. Postimia Restituta, his most devoted wife, arranged for it to be made from 1600 sesterces.
EDCS-10100869.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Gaius Iulius Verecundus, a centurion, whose monument was initially willed from 1000 sesterces, but his devoted wife, Postimia Restituta, expanded the endowment to 1600 sesterces.

EDCS-10100870.text_edition: to equiti cohortis I Belgarum stipendiorum  posuit Aurelia Marina nepoti piissimo
EDCS-10100870.translation: Aurelia Marina placed this for her most devoted grandson, a cavalryman of the First Cohort of Belgae, after [---] years of service.
EDCS-10100870.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a grandmother's dedication to her deceased grandson, a cavalryman in a cohort of Belgae, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100871.text_edition: Dassius Bastarni filius domo Maezaeus miles cohortis I Belgarum centuria Restituti signifer nnum  stipendiorum XV testamento fieri iussit curavit Valerius Maximinus heres
EDCS-10100871.translation: Dassius, son of Bastarnus, from Maeza, a soldier of the First Cohort of Belgae, standard-bearer in the century of Restitutus, [lived for ---] years, with 15 years of service. He ordered this to be made by his will. Valerius Maximinus, his heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-10100871.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Dassius, a standard-bearer in the First Cohort of Belgae, whose heir carried out his testamentary wish to have the monument erected.

EDCS-10100872.text_edition: Meduttus Caturonis filius miles cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum nnorum XXX stipendiorum
EDCS-10100872.translation: Meduttus, son of Caturonus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, 30 years old, with [---] years of service.
EDCS-10100872.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription or a dedication for Meduttus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, indicating his age and years of service, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100873.text_edition: sca miles cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum annorum X hic situs est Cambi heres posuit
EDCS-10100873.translation: [---]sca, a soldier of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, 10 years old, lies here. Cambi, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-10100873.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young soldier named [---]sca from the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, erected by his heir Cambi.

EDCS-10100874.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Maxima filio suo et Octavius signifer amico dulcissimo fecerunt nnorum duum bene merenti paremtalem sic dolente animo
EDCS-10100874.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Flavius Maximus, their son, and Octavius, the standard-bearer, they made this for their sweetest friend, deserving well, two years old, with a parent's grieving heart.
EDCS-10100874.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, erected by parents and a friend (a standard-bearer) for a beloved two-year-old child named Flavius Maximus.

EDCS-10100875.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating an individual who held the military ranks of 'imaginifer' (standard-bearer of the emperor's image) and 'vexillarius' (standard-bearer).

EDCS-10100878.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Maximo filio annorum XII Aelia Maxima mater infelicissimo filio et Aeliae Annaeae matri hoc monumentum heredem nM(on)#n(on)#M sequetur et Primus pater
EDCS-10100878.translation: For Tiberius Claudius Maximus, son, 12 years old. Aelia Maxima, his mother, [erected this] for her most unfortunate son, and for Aelia Annaea, his mother. This monument will follow no heir. And Primus, the father [did this].
EDCS-10100878.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Maxima and Primus to their unfortunate son, Tiberius Claudius Maximus, who died at age 12, and also includes a dedication to Aelia Annaea, perhaps the son's maternal grandmother, explicitly stating the monument is not for future heirs.

EDCS-10100880.text_edition: Marco Antonio Maximo signifero veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnum L stipendiorum XXX et Iuliae Sabinae Caius Antonius Augurinus filius faciendum curavit
EDCS-10100880.translation: For Marcus Antonius Maximus, standard-bearer, veteran of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, 50 years old, with 30 years of service, and for Iulia Sabina. Caius Antonius Augurinus, his son, had this made.
EDCS-10100880.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caius Antonius Augurinus to his father, Marcus Antonius Maximus, a veteran standard-bearer of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, and also to Iulia Sabina, likely his mother.

EDCS-10100881.text_edition: Aurelius Saturninus ER defunctus annorum XXAR Severus PSIS SD
EDCS-10100881.translation: Aurelius Saturninus [---] deceased, 20 years old. Severus [---]
EDCS-10100881.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aurelius Saturninus, who died at the age of 20, likely erected by Severus.

EDCS-10100883.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Iulius Cassianus miles cohortis
EDCS-10100883.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Iulius Cassianus, soldier of the cohort [---]
EDCS-10100883.summary: This is a religious dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Iulius Cassianus, a soldier of a cohort, suggesting a votive offering or altar.

EDCS-10100888.text_edition: Titiae Septimae filiae infelici Larcia Gemella marito et filiae dulcissimae posuit
EDCS-10100888.translation: To Tita Septima, her unfortunate daughter. Larcia Gemella placed this for her husband and her dearest daughter.
EDCS-10100888.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Larcia Gemella dedicated to her unfortunate daughter, Tita Septima, and also to her husband.

EDCS-10100889.text_edition: Minervae Augustae sacrum Lucius Sulpicius Proculus actuarius cohortis VIII voluntariorum ex adiutore corniculariorum connsularis libens posuit
EDCS-10100889.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augusta. Lucius Sulpicius Proculus, actuary of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, formerly an assistant to the governor's clerks, willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100889.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Minerva Augusta, set up by Lucius Sulpicius Proculus, an official from a cohort of volunteers.

EDCS-10100890.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae consuli IIII cohors VIII voluntariorum turrem ad aquam tollendam fecit Sexto Aemilio Equestre legato Augusti pro praetore Marco Caecilio Africano praefecto
EDCS-10100890.translation: To Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, consul for the fourth time, the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers built a tower for raising water, under Sextus Aemilius Equestris, imperial legate with propraetorian power, and Marcus Caecilius Africanus, prefect.
EDCS-10100890.summary: This inscription records the construction of a water tower by the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers for Emperor Antoninus Pius, under the authority of named provincial governors, likely dating to Antoninus Pius's fourth consulate (145 CE).

EDCS-10100891.text_edition: Lucius Fabius Cai filius Sergia Conana nnorum XX hic situs est Caius Fabius pater filio posuit
EDCS-10100891.translation: Lucius Fabius, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, aged 20 years, lies here. Gaius Fabius, his father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-10100891.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Gaius Fabius for his son, Lucius Fabius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10100892.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai filius Fabia Ninica miles legionis VII annorum XXXIX stipendiorum XVII hic situs est hospes resiste et tumulum contempla meum lege et moraris iam scies quae debeas vixi qua potui carus sum Opiorum hic sum sepultus hic ossua sic requiescum sic mea vale et bene facito vitae dum fatum venit Faustus libertus patrono
EDCS-10100892.translation: Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, aged 39 years, with 17 years of service, lies here. Stranger, stop and contemplate my tomb. Read and linger, and you will soon know what you owe. I lived as best I could, dear to the Opian family. Here I am buried, here my bones rest. So fare well and do good deeds in life until fate comes. Faustus, freedman, to his patron.
EDCS-10100892.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, set up by his freedman Faustus, including a philosophical address to the passerby and details of his military service and age.

EDCS-10100893.text_edition: nius Cai filius s nnorum V hic situs est iaii parens supre  qui fatis concideci  sine PVS NSS annus cui SCIDI  miseris ipse patrem II mihi natura I
EDCS-10100893.translation: [Gai]nius, son of Gaius, aged 5 years, lies here. His parents, supreme [in grief], who fell to fate without [---], whose year [---]. I myself, a father to the miserable, [had] two [children], nature [gave me] one.
EDCS-10100893.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a 5-year-old child named Gainius, placed by his grieving parents, lamenting their loss and fate.

EDCS-10100897.text_edition: ODLI annum mensem filio infelicissimo LBL et Attia Caesia parentes vivi f(ecerunt) et? s(ibi)
EDCS-10100897.translation: To [---] son, most unfortunate, who lived [---] year, [---] month. L. B. L. and Attia Caesia, parents, made this while living, and perhaps for themselves.
EDCS-10100897.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents, L. B. L. and Attia Caesia, while they were still alive, for their unfortunate son, likely also reserving space for themselves.

EDCS-10100899.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caetenio  Secundo infelicissimo defuncto annorum XV menserum sic X dierum XX Caetenius Maxsimus sic et Raia Ianuaria parentes ifEelio sic#ifelio sic#IEELIO posuerunt
EDCS-10100899.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Caetenius Secundus, most unfortunate, who died aged 15 years, 10 months, 20 days. Caetenius Maximus and Raia Ianuaria, his parents, placed this for their most beloved son.
EDCS-10100899.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Caetenius Secundus, an unfortunate youth who died at age 15, placed by his parents, Caetenius Maximus and Raia Ianuaria.

EDCS-10100901.text_edition: eriuno aberio  Flavius tiscum vivus nio tis filio
EDCS-10100901.translation: To Eriunus Aberius. Flavius Tiscus, while living, [made this] for his son, [---]nius Tiscus.
EDCS-10100901.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for Eriunus Aberius and another individual, set up by Flavius Tiscus for his son while Tiscus was still alive.

EDCS-10100903.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Priscus veteranus alae Claudiae novae testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Sexti Viniusi
EDCS-10100903.translation: Tiberius Claudius Priscus, a veteran of the New Claudian cavalry wing, ordered this to be made by his will, under the judgment of Sextus Vinusius.
EDCS-10100903.summary: This inscription records that Tiberius Claudius Priscus, a veteran cavalryman, arranged for this monument or structure to be built according to his will, supervised by Sextus Vinusius.

EDCS-10100904.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Naevio Firmo decurioni coloniae Aequensium aedili duumviro qui vixit nnis  Naevio Natalioni patri eiusdem Marci Naevi Firmi qui vixit annis LXXX Claudia Syra mater filio piissimo et marito obsequentissimo posuit
EDCS-10100904.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Naevius Firmus, decurion of the colony of the Aequenses, aedile, duumvir, who lived for [---] years. To Naevius Natalio, father of the same Marcus Naevius Firmus, who lived for 80 years. Claudia Syra, mother, placed this for her most devoted son and most obedient husband.
EDCS-10100904.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Claudia Syra to her son Marcus Naevius Firmus, a municipal magistrate, and her husband Naevius Natalio.

EDCS-10100906.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Plaetorio Quinti filio Sergia Valenti defuncto annorum XV Quintus Plaetorius Pudens et Nevia Secunda parentes filio infelicissimo posuerunt in fronte pedes  in agro pedes
EDCS-10100906.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Plaetorius Valens, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe, who died at 15 years old. Quintus Plaetorius Pudens and Nevia Secunda, his parents, placed this for their most unfortunate son. In front, [---] feet; in depth, [---] feet.
EDCS-10100906.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Quintus Plaetorius Pudens and Nevia Secunda for their deceased son, Quintus Plaetorius Valens, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-10100908.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Septimio Gratiano militi legionis VII Claudiae exacto consularis vixit nnos XXIIII dies XI Genialis Augusti nostri libertus et Apuleia Salvia filio karissimo posuerunt
EDCS-10100908.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Septimius Gratianus, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, exactus consularis, who lived 24 years, 11 days. Genialis, freedman of our Augustus, and Apuleia Salvia placed this for their dearest son.
EDCS-10100908.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Genialis, a freedman of the emperor, and Apuleia Salvia to their son Lucius Septimius Gratianus, a soldier in the Legio VII Claudia.

EDCS-10100909.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating that Valerius Messor, a standard-bearer, set up this monument, likely a dedication or a funerary stone.

EDCS-10100910.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Dassiae infilicissimae qui sic vixit annos XXII menses VII diis sic V Platon
EDCS-10100910.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelia Dassia, most unfortunate, who thus lived 22 years, 7 months, [---] days. Platon [set this up].
EDCS-10100910.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Dassia, who lived for 22 years and 7 months, likely set up by Platon.

EDCS-10100911.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ingenuiae Valeriae defunctae nnorum XXV Aurelius Rufinus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100911.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Ingenuia Valeria, who died at 25 years old. Aurelius Rufinus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10100911.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Rufinus to his wife, Ingenuia Valeria, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10100913.text_edition: Nerius Epicadus  Varonis et Su nepotis filiae dulcissimae quae vixit nnos VI parentes
EDCS-10100913.translation: Nerius Epicadus [and] [---] Varonis and Su[---] nephew [set this up] for their sweetest daughter, who lived 6 years.
EDCS-10100913.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Nerius Epicadus and [another relative] for their beloved daughter, who died at the age of six.

EDCS-10100914.text_edition: Aureliae Seioni Aurelia Maxima matri pientissimae defunctae annorum
EDCS-10100914.translation: To Aurelia Seionia. Aurelia Maxima [set this up] for her most devoted mother, who died at [---] years old.
EDCS-10100914.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelia Maxima for her most devoted mother, Aurelia Seionia.

EDCS-10100922.text_edition: AI Viatori nnorum XV menssium sic IIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100922.translation: AI Viator, of 15 years, 4 months, well-deserving, [this was] placed.
EDCS-10100922.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a person named Viator, who died at 15 years and 4 months of age, indicating a dedication for a well-deserving individual.

EDCS-10100926.text_edition: Binius Buri filius sibi et suis fecit locum privatum
EDCS-10100926.translation: Binius Buri's son made a private burial place for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100926.summary: This inscription records that Binius, son of Burus, created a private burial plot for himself and his family, indicating a funerary context.

EDCS-10100928.text_edition: Aureliae Ulpiae Aurelius Candidus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10100928.translation: To Aurelia Ulpia, Aurelius Candidus [dedicated this] to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10100928.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aurelius Candidus to his wife, Aurelia Ulpia, praising her as well-deserving.

EDCS-10100946.text_edition: Annius Lucianus Aurelia Secundina Annio Lucio marito bene merenti posuit qui vixit annos LXXXX
EDCS-10100946.translation: Annia Secundina placed this for Annius Lucius, her well-deserving husband, who lived for 90 years.
EDCS-10100946.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a wife, Annia Secundina, to her husband, Annius Lucius, noting his advanced age of 90 years.

EDCS-10100947.text_edition: Dis Manibus Plotius Maximus homo infelicissimus qui vixit nnos plus minus XL Argentia uxor ipsius con sic quo vixit annos XX
EDCS-10100947.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Plotius Maximus, a most unfortunate man, who lived approximately 40 years. Argentia, his wife, [lived] approximately 20 years.
EDCS-10100947.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plotius Maximus and his wife Argentia, emphasizing Plotius's unfortunate life and stating their approximate ages.

EDCS-10100948.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Maximus heres OCI
EDCS-10100948.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Valerius Maximus, heir.
EDCS-10100948.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Maximus, indicating that an heir was involved in its dedication.

EDCS-10100949.text_edition: Am Lucia bene merenti vixit annos LX  CI
EDCS-10100949.translation: To Am[---] Lucia, well-deserving. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-10100949.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a woman named Lucia, who lived for 60 years and is described as well-deserving.

EDCS-10100952.text_edition: Antheo sacrum Thallus Campani servus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100952.translation: Sacred to Antheus. Thallus, slave of Campanus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100952.summary: This is a votive inscription by Thallus, a slave of Campanus, dedicated to the deity Antheus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100953.text_edition: Genio coloniae Martiae Iuliae Salonitanorum Attalus Aquilinae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100953.translation: To the Genius of the Colony of Martia Iulia Salonitanorum, Attalus Aquilinae willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100953.summary: This is a votive inscription by Attalus Aquilinae to the Genius of the Roman colony Martia Iulia Salonitanorum, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100954.text_edition: Genio coloniae Salonitanorum Lucius Sextilius Marullus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10100954.translation: To the Genius of the Colony of Salonitanorum, Lucius Sextilius Marullus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100954.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Sextilius Marullus to the Genius of the Roman colony Salonitanorum, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100955.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus votum sovit libens merito
EDCS-10100955.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100955.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100956.text_edition: Ex imperio domini Iovis Optimi Maximi iussit sibi aedem fieri cum suo consentio deorum dearumque silvestrium Nymphis fontanis cum Silvano Nymphis silvestrium cum Silvano Fescenia Astice cum suo pare coniuge Tito o Fausto VIviro et Augustali a solo restituit
EDCS-10100956.translation: By the command of Lord Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Fescenia Astice with her partner and husband Titus Faustus, a sevir and Augustalis, ordered a temple to be made for herself with the consent of the gods and goddesses of the woods, the fountain Nymphs with Silvanus, the wood Nymphs with Silvanus, and restored it from the ground up.
EDCS-10100956.summary: This inscription records the reconstruction of a temple from its foundations by Fescenia Astice and her husband Titus Faustus, a sevir Augustalis, by divine command and with the consent of various deities.

EDCS-10100957.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. M. Dereccandis [set this up].
EDCS-10100957.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus, set up by M. Dereccandis.

EDCS-10100960.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Alasinio Secundo defuncto nnorum LXXXVII Caius Alasinius Secundinus miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum duplarius Alasinia Quintina patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10100960.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Alasinius Secundus, who died at 87 years old. Gaius Alasinius Secundinus, a soldier, duplicarius of the VIII cohort of volunteers, and Alasinia Quintina, set this up for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-10100960.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Alasinius Secundus by his son, a soldier, and Alasinia Quintina, likely his wife or another close relative.

EDCS-10100961.text_edition: Kapitoni defuncto nnorum XIX Attia Saturnina mater
EDCS-10100961.translation: To Capito, who died at 19 years old. Attia Saturnina, his mother [set this up].
EDCS-10100961.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Capito, aged 19, dedicated by his mother Attia Saturnina.

EDCS-10100962.text_edition: ntilla Aurelio Dassio coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100962.translation: Antilla set this up for her well-deserving husband, Aurelius Dassius.
EDCS-10100962.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antilla to her husband, Aurelius Dassius.

EDCS-10100963.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nereis mater Aureliae Primae nnorum XXVI Aurelio Iannuario sic nnorum XXVIII mater misera
EDCS-10100963.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Nereis, mother of Aurelia Prima, 26 years old, and Aurelius Iannuarius, likewise 28 years old, [set this up]. A miserable mother.
EDCS-10100963.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Nereis, a sorrowful mother, for her two deceased children, Aurelia Prima and Aurelius Iannuarius.

EDCS-10100964.text_edition: Dis Manibus A Veneria filio pientissimo Suacrio qui vicxit sic annos viginti infelicissimo posuit
EDCS-10100964.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. A. Veneria set this up for her most dutiful son, Suacrius, who lived for 20 most unfortunate years.
EDCS-10100964.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by A. Veneria for her tragically deceased son, Suacrius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10100965.text_edition: Dis Manibus Bae Firminae infanti infelicissimae quae vixit nnos
EDCS-10100965.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For the most unfortunate infant Bae Firmina, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-10100965.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tragically deceased infant named Bae Firmina.

EDCS-10100967.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodio Liberali defuncto annorum XXV
EDCS-10100967.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Clodius Liberalis, who died at 25 years old.
EDCS-10100967.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodius Liberalis, who passed away at the age of 25.

EDCS-10100968.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Fundanio Secundo et Iuliae Laeonini sic parentibus et Caio Iulio Fundanio Caius Iulius Secundus fratri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100968.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Gaius Fundanius Secundus and Iulia Laeonini, his parents, and for Gaius Iulius Fundanius. Gaius Iulius Secundus set this up for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-10100968.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Secundus to his parents, Gaius Fundanius Secundus and Iulia Laeonini, and to his brother, Gaius Iulius Fundanius.

EDCS-10100969.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Hilara Hilaro vernaculo defuncto annorum V
EDCS-10100969.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulia Hilara [set this up] for Hilarus, her home-born slave, who died at 5 years old.
EDCS-10100969.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Hilara to Hilarus, a child who was born into her household and died at the age of 5.

EDCS-10100970.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maltilio Pudenti et Aureliae Maximae Maltilia Pudentilla et Maltilia Maximina sorores parentibus dulcissimis posuerunt bbene mmerentibus
EDCS-10100970.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maltilia Pudentilla and Maltilia Maximina, sisters, set this up for their sweetest parents, Maltilius Pudens and Aurelia Maxima, who well deserved it.
EDCS-10100970.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by two sisters to their deceased parents, Maltilius Pudens and Aurelia Maxima, acknowledging their good merits.

EDCS-10100971.text_edition: collegii fabrum magistro quinquennali Publius Marcius Vitalis patri pientissimo
EDCS-10100971.translation: Publius Marcius Vitalis [set this up] for his most devoted father, a quinquennial master of the college of craftsmen.
EDCS-10100971.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Publius Marcius Vitalis to his father, commemorating his role as a quinquennial master in a college of craftsmen.

EDCS-10100972.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publiciae Epictesi Quintus Publicius Paroclus collibertae et coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10100972.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Publicius Paroclus [set this up] for Publicia Epictesis, his co-freedwoman and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10100972.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Publicius Paroclus to his wife, Publicia Epictesis, who was also his co-freedwoman, honoring her good merits.

EDCS-10100973.translation: Caius Tadusius Quartio [set this up] for his grandson Sabinus.
EDCS-10100973.summary: This short inscription is a funerary dedication by Caius Tadusius Quartio to his grandson, Sabinus.

EDCS-10100974.text_edition: Dis Manibus Decimo Balerio sic Clementi Baebia Tertulla coniugi bene mernti posuit
EDCS-10100974.translation: To the Divine Manes. Baebia Tertulla placed this for Decimus Balerius Clemens, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10100974.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Baebia Tertulla for her husband, Decimus Balerius Clemens, commending his good merits.

EDCS-10100975.text_edition: Valerius Stratco Proannorum Xvixit annorum  vixit annorum XL uxsor sic IENBRIA vicina titulum posuit state AMIC ST SITE ET
EDCS-10100975.translation: Valerius Stratco lived for 40 years. His neighbor Ien[---]bria placed this monument. Stop, friend, stand here and [---].
EDCS-10100975.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Valerius Stratco, who lived for 40 years, and was set up by his neighbor, Ien[---]bria, with an exhortation to passersby.

EDCS-10100976.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilaro defuncto nnorum V mensium XI Maximus
EDCS-10100976.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maximus [set this up] for Hilarus, who died at 5 years and 11 months old.
EDCS-10100976.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Maximus to Hilarus, who died as a young child at the age of five years and eleven months.

EDCS-10100994.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Messiae defunctae annorum XXVI Marcus Africanus Trophimus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10100994.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Africanus Trophimus [set this up] for Julia Messia, his well-deserving wife, who died at 26 years of age.
EDCS-10100994.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Africanus Trophimus to his wife, Julia Messia, who died at the age of 26, praising her merits.

EDCS-10100998.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nepoti defuncto nnorum XXXV Quinta conservo et Nepotiano filio infelicissimo nnorum IIII
EDCS-10100998.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quinta [set this up] for Nepos, her co-slave, who died at 35 years of age, and for her most unfortunate son Nepotianus, who died at 4 years of age.
EDCS-10100998.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by Quinta to her deceased co-slave, Nepos, and to her tragically deceased son, Nepotianus, who died very young.

EDCS-10100999.text_edition: Dis Manibus Salonina Suro coniugi defuncto annorum XXIIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10100999.translation: To the Divine Manes. Salonina [set this up] for Sura, her well-deserving husband, who died at 24 years of age.
EDCS-10100999.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Salonina for her husband, Sura, who died at the age of 24, commending his merits.

EDCS-10101001.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Barbaracio Hermadioni coniuci sic bene merenti
EDCS-10101001.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius Barbaracius Hermadio, his very deserving spouse.
EDCS-10101001.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Barbaracius Hermadio by his spouse, commemorating his well-deserved memory.

EDCS-10101002.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Clodio Crescenti Crescentilla fratri
EDCS-10101002.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Clodius Crescens, from Crescentilla, his sister.
EDCS-10101002.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Clodius Crescens by his sister, Crescentilla.

EDCS-10101003.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aprili Phoebus frater sorori
EDCS-10101003.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aprilis, from Phoebus, her brother, for his sister.
EDCS-10101003.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aprilis by her brother, Phoebus.

EDCS-10101004.text_edition: Dis Manibus Securo defuncto annorum XX Stratonicus et Fusca parentes posuerunt
EDCS-10101004.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Securus, who died at 20 years of age. Stratonicus and Fusca, his parents, placed this.
EDCS-10101004.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Securus, who died at 20, set up by his parents, Stratonicus and Fusca.

EDCS-10101006.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Fabio Fortunato Caius verna bene merenti
EDCS-10101006.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Fabius Fortunatus, from Gaius, a home-born slave, for his very deserving memory.
EDCS-10101006.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Fabius Fortunatus, erected by Gaius, a verna, acknowledging his merits.

EDCS-10101008.text_edition: Deo Optimo Magno  Quintus Clodius Dardanus et Veius Aurelianus nepos ipsius donum posuerunt
EDCS-10101008.translation: To the Best and Greatest God. Quintus Clodius Dardanus and Veius Aurelianus, his grandson, placed this gift.
EDCS-10101008.summary: This is a votive dedication to a supreme deity, offered by Quintus Clodius Dardanus and his grandson, Veius Aurelianus.

EDCS-10101009.text_edition: Dis Patris sacrum Titus Tarconius Catho posuit
EDCS-10101009.translation: Sacred to the Paternal Gods. Titus Tarconius Catho placed this.
EDCS-10101009.summary: This is a dedication to the Paternal Gods, set up by Titus Tarconius Catho.

EDCS-10101011.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius  Iovinus veteranus legionis X geminae exio sacrum ex voto solvit
EDCS-10101011.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Julius Iovinus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina, paid his vow from an ex voto offering, sacred to him.
EDCS-10101011.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Gaius Julius Iovinus, a veteran of Legio X Gemina, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10101013.text_edition: Iovi Adflatori Iunia Iucunda  solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101013.translation: To Jupiter Adflator. Junia Jucunda willingly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-10101013.summary: This is a votive offering to Jupiter Adflator by Junia Jucunda, fulfilling a vow willingly and deservedly.

EDCS-10101014.text_edition: Iovi Augusto sacrum Caius Arrius Narcissus collegio dono dedit
EDCS-10101014.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus. Gaius Arrius Narcissus gave this as a gift to the college.
EDCS-10101014.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Augustus, presented as a gift to a college by Gaius Arrius Narcissus.

EDCS-10101016.translation: Sacred to Father Liber. Curianus [set this up].
EDCS-10101016.summary: This is a religious dedication by Curianus to the god Liber Pater, likely on an altar or votive stone.

EDCS-10101018.text_edition: Matribus Magnis sacrum Publius Safinius Filucinus Terentiae sacerdotis filius aram supstituit sic idem ampliavit sibi et cognationi suae permissu Cai Clodi Gracilis
EDCS-10101018.translation: Sacred to the Great Mothers. Publius Safinius Filucinus, son of Terentia, the priestess, replaced the altar and likewise enlarged it for himself and his kin, with the permission of Gaius Clodius Gracilis.
EDCS-10101018.summary: This inscription records the replacement and enlargement of an altar dedicated to the Great Mothers by Publius Safinius Filucinus, son of a priestess, for himself and his family, with official permission, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101019.text_edition: AEO  Nymphis pro salute  Domnica    votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101019.translation: [Good fortune!] To the Nymphs for the well-being of Domnica, [she] willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101019.summary: This is a votive inscription by Domnica to the Nymphs, offered willingly and deservedly for her own well-being or that of another named Domnica, probably on an altar or plaque.

EDCS-10101020.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Tiberius Claudius Verus ex voto reddidit
EDCS-10101020.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Tiberius Claudius Verus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10101020.summary: This is a votive dedication by Tiberius Claudius Verus to Silvanus Augustus, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101021.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Fabius Valentinus [set this up].
EDCS-10101021.summary: This is a religious dedication by Fabius Valentinus to the god Silvanus Augustus, likely on an altar or votive stone.

EDCS-10101022.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Laberius Rufinus deo exauditus libens posuit
EDCS-10101022.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Laberius Rufinus, having been heard by the god, willingly set this up.
EDCS-10101022.summary: This is a votive dedication by Laberius Rufinus to Silvanus Augustus, set up willingly after his prayer was heard by the god, likely on an altar.

EDCS-10101023.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Communis. Alypus, IV [set this up].
EDCS-10101023.summary: This is a religious dedication by Alypus, possibly indicating a rank or order with "IV", to Silvanus Communis, likely on an altar or votive stone.

EDCS-10101025.text_edition: Cleudicia Dis Triviis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101025.translation: Cleudicia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Crossroad Gods.
EDCS-10101025.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cleudicia to the Dis Triviis, the gods of the crossroads, indicating a personal vow that was fulfilled.

EDCS-10101030.translation: Antistius Fortunius with his people [made this] vow.
EDCS-10101030.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Antistius Fortunius and his family or associates, likely part of a larger dedication.

EDCS-10101031.text_edition: RI ET LE AD et Aurelius Bastianus filius votum libens solvit
EDCS-10101031.translation: [---] RI ET LE AD [---] and Aurelius Bastianus, his son, willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10101031.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription, likely to a deity, by an unnamed individual and his son Aurelius Bastianus, who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-10101032.summary: This is a simple dedication recording that Aratus placed something, likely a tombstone or a votive offering.

EDCS-10101033.summary: This inscription identifies Marcus Lucilius Myro, likely a funerary marker or a simple ownership tag.

EDCS-10101036.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus A Augustus restituit Ino viro perfectissimo praeside provinciae
EDCS-10101036.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Augustus restored this for Ino, a most perfect man, president of the province.
EDCS-10101036.summary: This inscription records a restoration made by an Emperor Augustus for Ino, the provincial governor, likely indicating a public building or monument.

EDCS-10101038.text_edition: Diocletiano VIIIS Maximiano  VII Auggustis consulibus Aurelio Valentiniano viro perfectissimo bis praef(ecto)
EDCS-10101038.translation: In the consulship of Diocletian for the 8th time and Maximian for the 7th time, Augusti, for Aurelius Valentinianus, a most perfect man, twice prefect.
EDCS-10101038.summary: This inscription dates an event or dedication to a specific consulship of the emperors Diocletian and Maximian and mentions Aurelius Valentinianus, a high-ranking official.

EDCS-10101040.text_edition: M Titus Aurelius Marcellinus IIvir aed(ilicia)? pot(estate) Caio? Fulvio Maximo viro clarissimo legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Dalmatiae
EDCS-10101040.translation: Marcus Titus Aurelius Marcellinus, duumvir with aedilician power, for Gaius Fulvius Maximus, a most distinguished man, legate of Augustus with propraetorian power for the province of Dalmatia.
EDCS-10101040.summary: This inscription records a dedication or monument erected by a local magistrate, Marcus Titus Aurelius Marcellinus, in honor of the provincial governor of Dalmatia, Gaius Fulvius Maximus.

EDCS-10101046.text_edition: Vibius Firmus magister et quinquennalis mensam dono dedit
EDCS-10101046.translation: Vibius Firmus, master and quinquennalis, gave this table as a gift.
EDCS-10101046.summary: This inscription records Vibius Firmus, a local official (magister and quinquennalis), donating a table, likely to a collegium or for public use.

EDCS-10101048.text_edition: Maceria privata Sexti Gelli Primi cum solo suo
EDCS-10101048.translation: Private wall of Sextus Gelius Primus with its own ground.
EDCS-10101048.summary: This inscription marks a private wall belonging to Sextus Gelius Primus, indicating land ownership or boundary demarcation.

EDCS-10101049.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Augustalis speculator legionis I adiutricis infelicissimo posuit Valeria Sabina patri pientissimo pio
EDCS-10101049.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius Augustalis, speculato of the First Adiutrix legion, placed this for the most unfortunate. Valeria Sabina placed this for her most devoted and pious father.
EDCS-10101049.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Valerius Augustalis, a legionary speculator, erected by his daughter Valeria Sabina for her father.

EDCS-10101050.text_edition: Ulpius Iustinus beneficiarius consularis legionis primae Italicae defunctus annorum XXX
EDCS-10101050.translation: Ulpius Iustinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the First Italian legion, deceased at 30 years of age.
EDCS-10101050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Iustinus, a military official (beneficiarius consularis) of the Legio I Italica, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10101052.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Domitius Valens veteranus legionis V Macedonicae et A AFA B Caius Offonius Domiti Caius Domi ann
EDCS-10101052.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Domitius Valens, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian legion, and A. A. F. A. B. Gaius Offonius Domiti, Gaius Domi, [deceased] years [---].
EDCS-10101052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Domitius Valens, a veteran of Legio V Macedonica, and possibly two other individuals, Gaius Offonius Domiti and Gaius Domi.

EDCS-10101053.text_edition: Marcus Titius Marci filius Sergia domo Isinda eques legionis VII annorum XXXV stipendiorum X ex testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Luci Gelli Marci fili Sergia
EDCS-10101053.translation: Marcus Titius, son of Marcus, from the Sergian tribe, Isinda, a cavalryman of the Seventh Legion, aged 35, with 10 years of service, ordered this to be made according to his will, under the judgment of Lucius Gellius, son of Marcus, from the Sergian tribe.
EDCS-10101053.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Titius, a cavalryman of the Seventh Legion, which was erected according to his will and supervised by an executor, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101054.text_edition: Lucius Arruntius domo Pasimoae sic nnrum LV veteranus? legionis VII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-10101054.translation: Lucius Arruntius, from Pasimoa, aged 55 years, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, is buried here. He ordered this to be made according to his will.
EDCS-10101054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Arruntius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, which he arranged to be made by his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101055.text_edition: Caius Manticus Cai filius Scaptia miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XXIIII Caius Avinnius Postumius s
EDCS-10101055.translation: Gaius Manticus, son of Gaius, from the Scaptian tribe, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, with 24 years of service. Gaius Avinnius Postumius S[---]
EDCS-10101055.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription for Gaius Manticus, a soldier of the prestigious Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, likely set up by Gaius Avinnius Postumius, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101056.text_edition: signifer legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis Caius Clodius Felix libertus IIIIIIvir Augustalis
EDCS-10101056.translation: Standard-bearer of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis. Gaius Clodius Felix, freedman, Augustalis of the six leading men.
EDCS-10101056.summary: This is a commemorative or funerary inscription for Gaius Clodius Felix, a freedman who served as a standard-bearer in Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis and held the prominent civic position of *Sevir Augustalis*, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101058.text_edition: Publio Aelio Proculino veterano Aelia Proculina filia et Aelius Urbanus libertus et heres bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10101058.translation: To Publius Aelius Proculinus, a veteran, Aelia Proculina, his daughter, and Aelius Urbanus, his freedman and heir, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101058.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Proculina, daughter, and Aelius Urbanus, freedman and heir, to Publius Aelius Proculinus, a veteran, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101062.text_edition: sia Sexti filius Sec Iulio Sec nnorum XXX stipendiorum XVII T IETSS L bene merenti mille C
EDCS-10101062.translation: Sia, son of Sextus, Sec. To Julius Sec, aged 30 years, with 17 years of service. T. IETSS. L. For the well-deserving. 1100.
EDCS-10101062.summary: This appears to be a fragmentary funerary inscription for a man named Julius Sec, noting his age and years of military service, possibly erected for the well-deserving by an unspecified dedicant, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101064.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelia Messiana Aurelio Saturnino ex |(centurione) ex castris Batavum sic comNpari#compari#CONPARI incomNparabili#incomparabili#INCONPARABILI qui militavit annos XXVIII et mecum vixit nns XXXVII arcam posuit et sibi
EDCS-10101064.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aelia Messiana placed this chest for Aurelius Saturninus, former centurion from the camp of the Batavians, her incomparable partner, who served for 28 years and lived with me for 37 years, and for herself.
EDCS-10101064.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Messiana to her incomparable partner, Aurelius Saturninus, a former centurion, and for herself, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101066.translation: To Marcus Cam[---], from [---] homeland, aged 10 years.
EDCS-10101066.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a young boy named Marcus, aged 10, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101067.text_edition: ara ulio t  Quintus Ignienus Quinti filius Aniensis Ferox domo Troade
EDCS-10101067.translation: Altar [---] to Julius [---] Quintus Ignienus, son of Quintus, from the Aniensian tribe, Ferox, from his home in Troas.
EDCS-10101067.summary: This appears to be a fragmentary altar dedication or funerary inscription, possibly for Julius, made by Quintus Ignienus Ferox from Troas, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101068.text_edition: Gaio Nertonio Sperato veterano ex decurione ex Dacia vixit annis LV Gaius Nertonius Philocyrius et Gaius Nertonius Chrysanthus liberti patrono posuerunt
EDCS-10101068.translation: To Gaius Nertonius Speratus, a veteran, former decurion from Dacia, who lived 55 years. Gaius Nertonius Philocyrius and Gaius Nertonius Chrysanthus, his freedmen, placed this for their patron.
EDCS-10101068.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his freedmen, Gaius Nertonius Philocyrius and Gaius Nertonius Chrysanthus, to Gaius Nertonius Speratus, a veteran and former decurion from Dacia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101069.text_edition: Dis Manibus  ? Caerelleo sic Museo militi clTassis#classis#CTAS praetoriae Antoninianae Misenatium IIIIquadriremi Venere Victoriacare
EDCS-10101069.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Caerelleus Museus, soldier of the Antoninian praetorian fleet of Misenum, from the quadrireme Venus Victrix.
EDCS-10101069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caerelleus Museus, a soldier of the praetorian fleet, likely set up by his comrades or family.

EDCS-10101071.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Marci filio Sabino equo publico decurioni coloniae Salonitanorum IIviro iure dicundo praefecto bis collegii fabrum et centonariorum patrono collegii supra scripti ob industriam adque sic singularem eius innocentiam et integritatem defensionemque ex aere collato collegium supra scriptum
EDCS-10101071.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, son of Marcus, a public horseman, decurion of the colony of Salonitanorum, duumvir with judicial power, twice prefect of the college of craftsmen and felt-makers, patron of the aforementioned college. For his industry and singular innocence and integrity and defense, from money collected, the aforementioned college [erected this].
EDCS-10101071.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a college of craftsmen and felt-makers to Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, honoring his public service and virtues.

EDCS-10101075.text_edition: Lucius Curius Luci libertus Sosibius IIIIIIvir Augustalis sibi et Iuliae Scepsidi uxori testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Iuliae Scepsidis in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-10101075.translation: Lucius Curius Sosibius, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis (member of the college of six priests of the cult of Augustus), ordered this to be made by his will for himself and Julia Scepsis his wife, at the discretion of Julia Scepsis. Twenty feet in front, thirty feet in depth.
EDCS-10101075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Curius Sosibius and his wife Julia Scepsis, ordered by his will, detailing the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-10101078.text_edition: Dis Manibus ProtagCorae#Protagorae#PROTACORAE L AG PP notario et opsonatori Felicissima coniugi piissimo fecit
EDCS-10101078.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Protagoras, notary and caterer of Lucius Aelius Paternus Paternus. Felicissima made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-10101078.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Protagoras, a notary and caterer, erected by his wife Felicissima.

EDCS-10101080.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primo Caesaris servo librario
EDCS-10101080.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Primus, slave of Caesar, a scribe.
EDCS-10101080.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primus, a slave of the emperor who worked as a scribe.

EDCS-10101081.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Renover dispensatoris Augusti vicarius defunctus annorum XXX et Aeliae Augusti libertae defunctae annorum XXVII Aelia Eutiches! liberta Augusti filiae et  genero
EDCS-10101081.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Renover, deputy of the Augustan dispenser, who died at 30 years old, and to Aelia, freedwoman of Augustus, who died at 27 years old. Aelia Eutyches, freedwoman of Augustus, [made this] for her daughter and son-in-law.
EDCS-10101081.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Renover, an imperial official, and Aelia, an imperial freedwoman, erected by Aelia Eutyches for her daughter and son-in-law.

EDCS-10101082.text_edition: l Augusti libertus Magnus alumnae piissimae erga se bene merenti fecit
EDCS-10101082.translation: Lucius, freedman of Augustus, Magnus, made this for his most devoted foster-daughter, who was well deserving of him.
EDCS-10101082.summary: This inscription records a dedication by an imperial freedman named Magnus to his foster-daughter, honoring her devotion and merit.

EDCS-10101083.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Quintiano filio dulcissimo defuncto nnrum VIIII dierum XXXIIII mater pientissima Dexsia sic Proba
EDCS-10101083.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Titus Aelius Quintianus, most sweet son, who died at 9 years and 34 days old. His most devoted mother, Dexsia Proba, [made this].
EDCS-10101083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aelius Quintianus, a young boy, erected by his grieving mother Dexsia Proba.

EDCS-10101084.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemilio Rufo et Se Rufae
EDCS-10101084.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aemilius Rufus and to Saenia Rufa.
EDCS-10101084.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aemilius Rufus and Saenia Rufa, likely a couple.

EDCS-10101086.text_edition: Dis Manibus Armeniae Alexandriae Claudius Proculus heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101086.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Claudius Proculus, heir, placed this for Armenia Alexandria, who was well deserving.
EDCS-10101086.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Armenia Alexandria, erected by her heir, Claudius Proculus.

EDCS-10101087.text_edition: Aurelio Aeneae coniugi incomparabili natione Graeco q=CLui#qui#CLUI vixit annos XXVII posuit Cassia Decorata coniux virginio suo bene merenti
EDCS-10101087.translation: To Aurelius Aeneas, my incomparable husband, of Greek nationality, who lived 27 years. Cassia Decorata, his wife, placed this for her well-deserving husband in his youth.
EDCS-10101087.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Cassia Decorata to her husband, Aurelius Aeneas, who died at 27 years old, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101088.text_edition: Aurelio Anthigono bene merenti coll=II(egae#coll(egae#COII saciicariorum posuerunt
EDCS-10101088.translation: To Aurelius Anthigonus, well-deserving colleague, the college of 'saciicarii' placed this.
EDCS-10101088.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a professional college of 'saciicarii' to their well-deserving colleague, Aurelius Anthigonus, likely a funerary monument or an honorary dedication.

EDCS-10101089.text_edition: Aureliae Ferocillae in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XVI
EDCS-10101089.translation: For Aurelia Ferocilla, 12 feet in front, 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-10101089.summary: This inscription marks the dimensions of a burial plot or property belonging to Aurelia Ferocilla, specifying its frontage and depth, indicating a funerary context.

EDCS-10101090.text_edition: Aurelio Maximino ITLV qui vixit ter denus sic annos inplevit huic infelici hunc sarcTofacum sic#sarcofacum sic#SARTOFACUM posuit Aur(elius) Eutichianus sic nutritor
EDCS-10101090.translation: To Aurelius Maximinus, who lived and completed thirty years in this way. Aurelius Eutichianus, his nurturer, placed this unhappy sarcophagus for him in this way.
EDCS-10101090.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a sarcophagus, dedicated by Aurelius Eutichianus to his foster child, Aurelius Maximinus, who died at the age of 30, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101092.summary: This inscription specifies a measurement of 20 feet for Marcus Aurelius Polyvenus, likely indicating the size of a burial plot or a monument dedicated to him.

EDCS-10101094.text_edition: innocenti Aurelius Titus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101094.translation: Aurelius Titus placed this for the innocent, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101094.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aurelius Titus for an unnamed innocent individual, described as well-deserving, likely a child or a young person.

EDCS-10101095.text_edition: Aureliae Ursinae filiae piissimae adque infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XVIII menses VIII Aurelius Ursinus et Iulia Eucarpia parentes bene merenti posuerunt et sibi
EDCS-10101095.translation: To Aurelia Ursina, their most dutiful and most unfortunate daughter, who lived 18 years and 8 months. Aurelius Ursinus and Iulia Eucarpia, her well-deserving parents, placed this for her and for themselves.
EDCS-10101095.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelius Ursinus and Iulia Eucarpia to their daughter, Aurelia Ursina, who died at 18 years and 8 months, and also for themselves, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-10101097.text_edition: Aureliae Valentinae  puellae infelicissimae quae vixit annnos plus minus XX Aelius Valentio pater filiae dulcissimae posuit
EDCS-10101097.translation: To Aurelia Valentina, a most unfortunate girl who lived approximately 20 years. Aelius Valentio, her father, placed this for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-10101097.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Aelius Valentio to his most unfortunate daughter, Aurelia Valentina, who died around the age of 20, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101098.text_edition: tycho Aurelius Victorinus patronus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101098.translation: Tycho Aurelius Victorinus, his patron, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101098.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tycho Aurelius Victorinus to his well-deserving client or freedman, whose name is not explicitly stated in this fragment.

EDCS-10101099.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, to Lucius Baebidius Primitius.
EDCS-10101099.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes (Spirits of the Dead) and to Lucius Baebidius Primitius, indicating a grave marker or a tomb, common throughout the Roman Empire.

EDCS-10101100.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilarae defunctae nnorum II mensium II Baebius Saloninus
EDCS-10101100.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Hilara, who died at the age of 2 years and 2 months. Baebius Saloninus [set this up].
EDCS-10101100.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Baebius Saloninus to Hilara, likely a young child, who died at an early age.

EDCS-10101101.text_edition: Dis Manibus Basidius Philumenus Saccidiae Primitivae
EDCS-10101101.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Basidius Philumenus [set this up] for Saccidia Primitiva.
EDCS-10101101.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Basidius Philumenus to Saccidia Primitiva, likely a deceased relative or dependent.

EDCS-10101102.text_edition: Bellius Crescens Cerenio Grato amico carissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101102.translation: Bellius Crescens placed this for Cerenius Gratus, a most dear and well-deserving friend.
EDCS-10101102.summary: This is an inscription dedicated by Bellius Crescens to his friend Cerenius Gratus, commemorating their friendship and his merits.

EDCS-10101103.text_edition: Dis Manibus Benniusnticus Eucarpiae matri et Valeriae Respectillae sorori et
EDCS-10101103.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Benniusnticus [set this up] for Eucarpia, his mother, and for Valeria Respectilla, his sister, and...
EDCS-10101103.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Benniusnticus, commemorating his mother Eucarpia and his sister Valeria Respectilla, with the rest of the text presumably continuing to list other family members or details.

EDCS-10101107.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caetenio Satriano qui vixit annos XI
EDCS-10101107.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Caetenius Satrianus, who lived for 11 years.
EDCS-10101107.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caetenius Satrianus, a child who died at the age of eleven, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-10101108.text_edition: Cannutiae Crescentillae defunctae annorum XXXVI Vibius Anicetus coniugi incomparabili
EDCS-10101108.translation: To Cannutia Crescentilla, who died at 36 years of age. Vibius Anicetus [set this up] for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-10101108.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibius Anicetus to his deceased wife, Cannutia Crescentilla, highlighting her exceptional qualities.

EDCS-10101110.text_edition: Dis Manibus Casinia Hermione c heres C D
EDCS-10101110.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Casinia Hermione, with her heir, C. D. [set this up].
EDCS-10101110.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription, possibly for Casinia Hermione herself or for someone else, with Casinia Hermione and her heir (indicated by C. D.) being the dedicants.

EDCS-10101111.text_edition: Cassiae Flaviae defunctae annorum VI Mettia Placidia nepotiae infelicissimae I
EDCS-10101111.translation: To Cassia Flavia, who died at 6 years of age. Mettia Placidia [set this up] for her most unfortunate granddaughter.
EDCS-10101111.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Mettia Placidia for her granddaughter, Cassia Flavia, who died tragically young at the age of six.

EDCS-10101113.text_edition: Chrys Chrys filius Rhod Callityche uxor
EDCS-10101113.translation: Chrys, son of Chrys. Rhod. Callityche, wife.
EDCS-10101113.summary: This is an inscription likely indicating familial relationships on a funerary monument, identifying Chrys, son of Chrys, and Rhod. Callityche as his wife, possibly listing the deceased or other family members.

EDCS-10101115.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodiae Secundinae defunctae annorum II dierum XXX Clodia Firmina et Aurelius Secundus parentes
EDCS-10101115.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Clodia Secundina, who died at the age of 2 years and 30 days. Clodia Firmina and Aurelius Secundus, her parents [set this up].
EDCS-10101115.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Clodia Firmina and Aurelius Secundus to their very young daughter, Clodia Secundina, who died just after her second birthday.

EDCS-10101116.text_edition: Coelia Arescusa Primitivae libertae bene merenti
EDCS-10101116.translation: Coelia Arescusa [made this] for her well-deserving freedwoman Primitiva.
EDCS-10101116.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a patroness, Coelia Arescusa, dedicated to her freedwoman Primitiva.

EDCS-10101118.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Chrysidi Cornelius Didymus coniugi optumae sic cum qua vixi annis XXX
EDCS-10101118.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelius Didymus [made this] for Cornelia Chrysis, his excellent wife, with whom he lived for 30 years.
EDCS-10101118.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Cornelius Didymus commemorates his wife, Cornelia Chrysis, noting their thirty years of marriage.

EDCS-10101119.text_edition: <S=C>er#<S>er#CER Cornelio Clementi Petronia Secunda mater et Paulla soEror#soror#SEROR infelix mater quem dilexi vixit annos XXXI vitae praesidium meae
EDCS-10101119.translation: To Cornelius Clemens. His mother Petronia Secunda and his unhappy sister Paulla, his mother whom I loved, [made this]. He lived 31 years, the protector of my life.
EDCS-10101119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Clemens, dedicated by his mother Petronia Secunda and his sister Paulla, lamenting his premature death at 31.

EDCS-10101120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Euhodiae Aurelius gi
EDCS-10101120.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Cornelia Euhodia. Aurelius G[aius or Gnaeus]...
EDCS-10101120.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Cornelia Euhodia by Aurelius G[aius or Gnaeus].

EDCS-10101121.text_edition: Dis Manibus Egnatia Quinta Egnatiae Secundae filiae
EDCS-10101121.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Egnatia Quinta [made this] for her daughter Egnatia Secunda.
EDCS-10101121.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Egnatia Quinta to her daughter, Egnatia Secunda.

EDCS-10101122.text_edition: Fabia M viva fecit sibi et  Buccioni  et Fabiae N et Fabiae My et Tiberio Claudio  nepoti aditu in fronte pedes VI in agro pedes
EDCS-10101122.translation: Fabia M. made this while alive for herself and for Buccioni and for Fabia N. and for Fabia My. and for Tiberius Claudius, her grandson. Access in front, six feet; in the field, [X] feet.
EDCS-10101122.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Fabia M. during her lifetime for herself, her husband Buccioni, two other Fabiae, and her grandson Tiberius Claudius, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-10101123.text_edition: Posphoro mensium XI Titus Flavius Alexander et Horea nepoti
EDCS-10101123.translation: For Posphorus, 11 months old. Titus Flavius Alexander and Horea [made this] for their nephew.
EDCS-10101123.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Posphorus, who lived for 11 months, dedicated by his uncle Titus Flavius Alexander and Horea.

EDCS-10101125.text_edition: Dis Manibus F Flavio Ianuario Gobo fratri karissimo
EDCS-10101125.translation: To the Divine Spirits. F. Flavius Gobo [made this] for his dearest brother Ianuarius.
EDCS-10101125.summary: This is a funerary inscription by F. Flavius Gobo dedicated to his very dear brother Ianuarius.

EDCS-10101126.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Septimia Flavius ci
EDCS-10101126.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Septimia [made this for Flavius C.---]
EDCS-10101126.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription by Flavia Septimia, likely dedicated to a Flavius C.[---].

EDCS-10101129.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Aprili Caius Iulius Tacitus patri bene merenti
EDCS-10101129.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Iulius Aprilis. Gaius Iulius Tacitus [made this] for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-10101129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Tacitus to his deserving father, Gaius Iulius Aprilis.

EDCS-10101130.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Asclepiodotus et Valeria Valentina filio Iulio annuclo mensium V posuerunt
EDCS-10101130.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julius Asclepiodotus and Valeria Valentina placed this for their little son Julius, who lived for five months.
EDCS-10101130.summary: This is a funerary inscription from parents, Julius Asclepiodotus and Valeria Valentina, dedicating a monument to their deceased infant son, Julius.

EDCS-10101131.text_edition: IDIAI Firmo Iulia Clementina coniugi et sibi
