EDCS-10101131.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Clementina [made this] for Firmus, her husband, and for herself.
EDCS-10101131.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb, erected by Iulia Clementina for her husband, Firmus, and intended for her own future burial as well.

EDCS-10101132.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Crescenti Armenia Alexandria marito optimo bene merenti
EDCS-10101132.translation: To the Divine Manes. Armenia Alexandria [made this] for Julius Crescens, her most excellent and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10101132.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Armenia Alexandria dedicates a monument to her deserving late husband, Julius Crescens.

EDCS-10101133.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Dionisius sic et Caius Iulius Pamphilus II
EDCS-10101133.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Julius Dionysius, and also Gaius Julius Pamphilus II.
EDCS-10101133.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Gaius Julius Dionysius and Gaius Julius Pamphilus II, likely marking their burial place.

EDCS-10101134.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Faustinianus Iul VCO
EDCS-10101134.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julius Faustinianus. Julius, a quinquennial overseer (quinquennalis coactor) or similar official.
EDCS-10101134.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Faustinianus, mentioning another individual named Julius, who held an official position indicated by 'VCO'.

EDCS-10101135.translation: To the Divine Manes. Julia Messa [made this] for her mother.
EDCS-10101135.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Messa to her mother.

EDCS-10101136.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Nicini defunctae nnorum XIX mensium V dierum I Herculianus
EDCS-10101136.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Julia Nicini, who died at 19 years, 5 months, and 1 day old. Herculianus [made this].
EDCS-10101136.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Herculianus to Julia Nicini, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-10101137.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Panthia Veneriae ancillae
EDCS-10101137.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Panthia [made this] for Veneria, her slave woman.
EDCS-10101137.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Iulia Panthia for her deceased slave, Veneria.

EDCS-10101138.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Soteridi Iulia Tertia matri bene merenti
EDCS-10101138.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Tertia [made this] for Iulia Soteris, her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10101138.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulia Tertia dedicates a monument to her deserving late mother, Iulia Soteris.

EDCS-10101139.text_edition: ELIE Caio Iulio Turboni  Caio Iulio Magno filiis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur Alexsander sic
EDCS-10101139.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Julius Turbo and Gaius Julius Magnus, sons. This monument will not pass to an heir. Alexander [made this], likewise.
EDCS-10101139.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Alexander to his sons, Gaius Julius Turbo and Gaius Julius Magnus, with a clause indicating it is not inheritable.

EDCS-10101140.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Iulia Veneria.
EDCS-10101140.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Veneria, likely placed by a family member or associate whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-10101141.text_edition: Firmus et  Atticilla Publi Iulsimi si mea fata fuissent bona hic lapis in titulo qui premet hossua sic mea aequius iste lapis patri super hossua sic iaceret
EDCS-10101141.translation: Firmus and Atticilla. Publius Iulsimus. If my fates had been good, this stone, which will press upon these bones in the inscription, would more justly have lain upon the bones of my father. Thus.
EDCS-10101141.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, mentioning Firmus, Atticilla, and Publius Iulsimus, with a poignant reflection on an untimely death that prevented the deceased from burying his father.

EDCS-10101142.text_edition: Patri dulcissimo Iunius Lupulus et Cylnia Lupula coniunx bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10101142.translation: To the dearest father. Iunius Lupulus and Cylnia Lupula, his wife, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101142.summary: This is a funerary dedication erected by Iunius Lupulus and his wife Cylnia Lupula in honor of a beloved father.

EDCS-10101143.text_edition: Marcus Licinius Marci filius Dignus et Liciniae Marci filiae Proculae Caius Licinius et Lucius Licinius patrui titulum posuerunt  in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV aditum ad sepulchrum latum pedes IIII
EDCS-10101143.translation: Marcus Licinius Dignus, son of Marcus, and Licinia Procula, daughter of Marcus; Gaius Licinius and Lucius Licinius, paternal uncles, placed this monument. In front, 15 feet; in depth, 15 feet. Access to the tomb is 4 feet wide.
EDCS-10101143.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Licinius Dignus, Licinia Procula, and their paternal uncles Gaius and Lucius Licinius, detailing the dimensions of the plot and the access way to the tomb.

EDCS-10101144.text_edition: Lollia Victorina Lollio Calistomeno coniugi piissimo
EDCS-10101144.translation: Lollia Victorina to Lollius Calistomenus, her most devoted husband.
EDCS-10101144.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Lollia Victorina dedicating a monument to her deeply devoted husband, Lollius Calistomenus.

EDCS-10101145.text_edition: Dis Manibus Longiniae Tertullae Octavius Valens maritus et Octavius Tertullinus filius bene merenti
EDCS-10101145.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Longinia Tertulla. Octavius Valens, her husband, and Octavius Tertullinus, her son, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101145.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Longinia Tertulla, placed by her husband Octavius Valens and her son Octavius Tertullinus.

EDCS-10101146.text_edition: Publius Mescenius Dorus emit monimentum de Cincia Placida sibi et Statiae Epiteuxi uxori et suis in fronte pedes XIIX in agro pedes XIIX
EDCS-10101146.translation: Publius Mescenius Dorus bought this monument from Cincia Placida for himself and for Statia Epiteuxis, his wife, and for his family. In front, 18 feet; in depth, 18 feet.
EDCS-10101146.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a burial monument by Publius Mescenius Dorus from Cincia Placida, intended for himself, his wife Statia Epiteuxis, and his family, with the dimensions of the plot specified.

EDCS-10101147.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Mescenio Secundino fratri et Mesceniae Postuminae sorori et Lucio Cornificio Postumino nepoti et Lucio Cornificio Parthenio adfini Mescenia Rufina
EDCS-10101147.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Publius Mescenius Secundinus, her brother, and Mescenia Postumina, her sister, and Lucius Cornificius Postuminus, her nephew, and Lucius Cornificius Parthenius, her relative by marriage. Mescenia Rufina.
EDCS-10101147.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Mescenia Rufina in honor of her brother Publius Mescenius Secundinus, her sister Mescenia Postumina, her nephew Lucius Cornificius Postuminus, and her relative by marriage Lucius Cornificius Parthenius.

EDCS-10101148.text_edition: Dis Manibus Paetronio Aerasino sic defuncto nnorum XXXI
EDCS-10101148.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Paetronius Aerasinus, who died at the age of 31 years.
EDCS-10101148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Paetronius Aerasinus, recording his age at death as 31 years.

EDCS-10101149.text_edition: Lucio Plotio Ursioni defuncto annorum XXX mensium VII Plotia Prima filio pientissimo
EDCS-10101149.translation: To Lucius Plotius Ursio, who died at the age of 30 years and 7 months. Plotia Prima, to her most devoted son.
EDCS-10101149.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Plotia Prima to her most devoted son, Lucius Plotius Ursio, recording his age at death as 30 years and 7 months.

EDCS-10101150.text_edition: Statio Eutico Statia Artemisia bene merenti coniugi
EDCS-10101150.translation: Statia Artemisia to Statio Eutico, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10101150.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Statia Artemisia to her deceased husband, Statio Eutico.

EDCS-10101151.translation: Statia Marcellina set up this for Ia Secunda.
EDCS-10101151.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Statia Marcellina for Ia Secunda, likely a relative or close associate.

EDCS-10101152.text_edition: Marcus Titius Verna Fortunato alumno infelicissimo set sic et sibi vivo posuit et libertis libertabus suis
EDCS-10101152.translation: Marcus Titius Verna set this up for Fortunatus, his most unfortunate foster-child, but also for himself while living, and for his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-10101152.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Titius Verna for his deceased foster-child Fortunatus, and also pre-emptively for himself and his freed dependents.

EDCS-10101153.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caius Tafius.
EDCS-10101153.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to the underworld gods, indicating it is a tomb or memorial for Caius Tafius.

EDCS-10101154.text_edition: Dis Manibus oni DATNIAT Tarconio Diadumeno coniugi pientissimo
EDCS-10101154.translation: To the Divine Manes. Oni DATNIAT Tarconius Diadumenus to his most devoted wife.
EDCS-10101154.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Manes, set up by Tarconius Diadumenus for his very devoted wife, whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-10101155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Tarconius Fortunatus Tarconiae Lupercae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101155.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Tarconius Fortunatus set this up for Tarconia Luperca, his well-deserving (relative).
EDCS-10101155.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Titus Tarconius Fortunatus for Tarconia Luperca, a well-deserving individual, likely a family member.

EDCS-10101156.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caeteneniae Luciferae Caetenia Vibia amica
EDCS-10101156.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caetenia Vibia, friend, to Caetenenia Lucifera.
EDCS-10101156.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Caetenia Vibia for her friend, Caetenenia Lucifera.

EDCS-10101157.text_edition: Quintus Terentius Seleucianus vivus fecit sibi in agro pedes XXV in fronte pedes  et Vipsaniae sibi et  INPA SIN
EDCS-10101157.translation: Quintus Terentius Seleucianus made this while living for himself, in an area 25 feet wide and [---] feet deep, and for Vipsania for himself and INPA SIN.
EDCS-10101157.summary: This inscription records a pre-mortem funerary monument made by Quintus Terentius Seleucianus for himself and Vipsania, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-10101159.text_edition: Caius Tossius Cai filius  Tossia Cai liberta Thalassia Tossiae Primigeniae filiae carissimae
EDCS-10101159.translation: Caius Tossius, son of Caius. Tossia Thalassia, freedwoman of Caius, to Tossia Primigenia, her dearest daughter.
EDCS-10101159.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Tossia Primigenia, her dearest daughter, by Tossia Thalassia, a freedwoman, and also mentioning Caius Tossius.

EDCS-10101160.text_edition: Trosiae Celerinae filiae infelici nnorum  mensium VII MA
EDCS-10101160.translation: To Trosia Celerina, an unfortunate daughter of [---] years and 7 months.
EDCS-10101160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Trosia Celerina, an unfortunate daughter who died at a young age of 7 months, with her full age likely indicated by lost text.

EDCS-10101163.text_edition: Dis Manibus riae Crisidi filiae quae vixit annos IIII menses  dies IXX Iulia Popilia filiae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101163.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Ria Crisida, her daughter, who lived for 4 years, 7 months, and 19 days. Iulia Popilia placed this for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-10101163.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Iulia Popilia dedicated to her deceased daughter, Ria Crisida, who died at a young age.

EDCS-10101164.summary: This is a simple inscription, likely a name label or a fragment of a larger monument, identifying Gaius Valerius Dius.

EDCS-10101165.text_edition: Dis Manibus Derm puero annorum  mensium III dierum VIII Valerius Euporistus pater et Valeria mater filio infelicissimo
EDCS-10101165.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Derm, a boy of [x] years, 3 months, and 8 days. Valerius Euporistus, his father, and Valeria, his mother, [placed this] for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-10101165.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Valerius Euporistus and Valeria, dedicated to their young son, Derm, who died at a very early age.

EDCS-10101167.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vallio Primitivo def(uncto) an(norum)
EDCS-10101167.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Vallio Primitivus, deceased, of [x] years.
EDCS-10101167.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription dedicated to Vallio Primitivus, noting his death and age.

EDCS-10101168.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from Publius Vibius Victor to a woman named Egnatia.

EDCS-10101169.text_edition: ius Manertus - Laelia uxor - CV fieri iussit
EDCS-10101169.translation: Manertus and his wife Laelia ordered this to be made.
EDCS-10101169.summary: This inscription records that Manertus and his wife Laelia commissioned the monument on which it is inscribed.

EDCS-10101170.text_edition: ius Vindemiator coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101170.translation: Vindemiator placed this for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-10101170.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Vindemiator, dedicated to his deceased, well-deserving spouse.

EDCS-10101171.text_edition: etoCalliope mater infelicissima dulcissimo et piissimo natus Salonis defuncto Monte Gargano
EDCS-10101171.translation: Calliope, most unfortunate mother, [placed this] for her sweetest and most dutiful son, born in Salona, deceased at Monte Gargano.
EDCS-10101171.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Calliope, for her deceased son, noting his birthplace and place of death.

EDCS-10101172.text_edition: Pater et mater Abascanto filio bene merito
EDCS-10101172.translation: Father and mother [placed this] for their well-deserving son Abascantus.
EDCS-10101172.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a father and mother, dedicated to their deceased, well-deserving son, Abascantus.

EDCS-10101173.translation: Stratonice and Aglaus [placed this] for their son Alexander.
EDCS-10101173.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from Stratonice and Aglaus to their son, Alexander.

EDCS-10101174.text_edition: Dis Manibus Candidiano qui vixit annos III Larga  mater
EDCS-10101174.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Candidianus, who lived for 3 years. Larga, his mother, [set this up].
EDCS-10101174.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Larga, for her young son Candidianus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-10101175.text_edition: Capitoni qui vixit annis plus minus XXX Veneria conservo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101175.translation: For Capito, who lived for about 30 years. Veneria set this up for her well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-10101175.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Veneria for Capito, her deceased fellow slave, who was approximately 30 years old.

EDCS-10101176.text_edition: Chinnamis Luci Enni Clementis contubernalis bene merenti posuit hunc titulum accepi supremi temporis a te mandatu posui sit levis iste tibi
EDCS-10101176.translation: Chinnamis, the concubine of Lucius Ennius Clemens, set this up for her well-deserving partner. I received this tablet from you by mandate in your final moments, and I have placed it. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10101176.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Chinnamis, the concubine of Lucius Ennius Clemens, for her deceased partner, fulfilling a final wish.

EDCS-10101177.text_edition: Helpis Corelliae suae carissimae posuit h
EDCS-10101177.translation: Helpis set this up for her dearest Corellia. H[oc monumentum?]
EDCS-10101177.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely incomplete, set up by Helpis for her beloved Corellia.

EDCS-10101178.text_edition: Epetinae matri Aelia Faustina bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101178.translation: Aelia Faustina set this up for her well-deserving mother Epetina.
EDCS-10101178.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Faustina to her mother, Epetina.

EDCS-10101179.text_edition: Felicio Camilli Arrunti Scriboniani servus annorum XL hic situs est Guttilla conserva bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101179.translation: Felicio, a slave of Camillus Arruntius Scribonianus, 40 years old, is laid here. Guttilla, his fellow slave, set this up for her well-deserving companion.
EDCS-10101179.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felicio, a 40-year-old slave, erected by his fellow slave Guttilla.

EDCS-10101180.text_edition: Dis Manibus Feliclae defunctae nnorum XXV Hermes conservae bene merenti
EDCS-10101180.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Felicla, deceased, 25 years old. Hermes [set this up] for his well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-10101180.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Hermes to Felicla, his fellow slave, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10101181.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunatae defunctae annorum XX Tyche ancillae bene merenti
EDCS-10101181.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Fortunata, deceased, 20 years old. Tyche [set this up] for her well-deserving slave girl.
EDCS-10101181.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tyche to Fortunata, her slave girl, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10101182.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilariano defuncto annorum IIII mensium XI dierum VII Secundinus filio infelicissimo
EDCS-10101182.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Hilarianus, deceased, 4 years, 11 months, 7 days old. Secundinus [set this up] for his most unfortunate son.
EDCS-10101182.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Secundinus for his son Hilarianus, who died tragically young at just under five years old.

EDCS-10101183.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilarioni anni I mensium X Fortunata
EDCS-10101183.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Hilarion, 1 year, 10 months old. Fortunata [set this up].
EDCS-10101183.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely by a parent, for Hilarion, a child who died at one year and ten months old.

EDCS-10101184.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilaro Iuli Festini nnorum XXIII Eutyches et Speciosa filio piissimo et sanctissimo posuerunt
EDCS-10101184.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Eutyches and Speciosa placed this for Hilarus, son of Iulius Festinus, most devoted and most holy son, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-10101184.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tombstone erected by Eutyches and Speciosa for their most devoted and holy son, Hilarus, who lived 23 years.

EDCS-10101185.text_edition: Dis Manibus Irinini defunctae nnorum XVII mensium VIII parentes et fratres infelicissimae posuerunt
EDCS-10101185.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Parents and brothers placed this for the most unfortunate Irinina, who died at 17 years and 8 months.
EDCS-10101185.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents and brothers for their most unfortunate daughter and sister, Irinina, who died at the age of 17 years and 8 months.

EDCS-10101186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcia matri pientissimae Vna filia
EDCS-10101186.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Una, daughter, for Marcia, most devoted mother.
EDCS-10101186.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcia, a most devoted mother, set up by her daughter Una.

EDCS-10101187.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primae defunctae nni I mensium IIII Primus pater filiae infelicissimae posuit
EDCS-10101187.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Primus, father, placed this for his most unfortunate daughter Prima, who died at 1 year and 4 months.
EDCS-10101187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young daughter named Prima, who died at 1 year and 4 months, erected by her father Primus.

EDCS-10101188.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primulae infelicissimae quae vixit annos plus minus XXV Quintus conlibertus posuit
EDCS-10101188.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus, fellow freedman, placed this for the most unfortunate Primula, who lived approximately 25 years.
EDCS-10101188.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Quintus, a fellow freedman, for the most unfortunate Primula, who lived about 25 years.

EDCS-10101189.translation: Primula, daughter, for her most unfortunate parents.
EDCS-10101189.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Primula for her most unfortunate parents.

EDCS-10101190.translation: The sons Quartinus and Gemella placed this.
EDCS-10101190.summary: This inscription records the dedication by two sons, Quartinus and Gemella, presumably for their parents or another family member.

EDCS-10101192.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Felicioni Lupulus et Qu
EDCS-10101192.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lupulus and Qu[---] for Quintus Felicio.
EDCS-10101192.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Felicio, set up by Lupulus and another individual whose name begins with 'Qu'.

EDCS-10101194.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundae defunctae annorum X Marcius Muricus et Lucia Aphrodisia alumnae bene meritae
EDCS-10101194.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcius Muricus and Lucia Aphrodisia for their well-deserving foster daughter Secunda, who died at 10 years.
EDCS-10101194.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secunda, a well-deserving foster daughter who died at the age of 10, erected by Marcius Muricus and Lucia Aphrodisia.

EDCS-10101198.text_edition: LIASE N MEMU Urso qui vixit annos VII VTI
EDCS-10101198.translation: LIASE N MEMU, for Ursus who lived 7 years VTI.
EDCS-10101198.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription for Ursus, who lived 7 years, with some fragmentary or unusual introductory phrases.

EDCS-10101204.text_edition: Hic est posita Byrgo annorum XIII NO
EDCS-10101204.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of a deceased individual named Byrgo.

EDCS-10101205.text_edition: Felix vibus sic sibi posuit et Memmianae conpari suae
EDCS-10101205.translation: Felix, while alive, thus set this up for himself and for Memmiana, his companion.
EDCS-10101205.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Felix, while still alive, setting up a monument for himself and his companion Memmiana.

EDCS-10101207.text_edition: OPC annorumnia viva sibi  tio Stercorio virginii  vit qui vixit mecum annos XV
EDCS-10101207.translation: OPC, alive for years, for himself. [Also] for Stercorius Virginius, who lived with me for 15 years.
EDCS-10101207.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by an individual for themselves while alive, and also commemorating Stercorius Virginius, who lived with the dedicator for 15 years.

EDCS-10101223.text_edition: Diaia ex iusso sic deae Nemisi sic solvit votum libens merito
EDCS-10101223.translation: Diaia, by command, thus willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-10101223.summary: This inscription records Diaia fulfilling a vow to the goddess Nemesis as commanded.

EDCS-10101224.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Secundinus, possibly identified by his role as a 'sequutor' or follower.

EDCS-10101234.text_edition: Pinaria Cai liberta [] patrono suo Caio Pinario Scarpo veterano legionis VII et filio
EDCS-10101234.translation: Pinaria, freedwoman of Gaius, [set this up] for her patron Gaius Pinarius Scarpus, veteran of the VII Legion, and for his son.
EDCS-10101234.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Pinaria, a freedwoman, for her patron Gaius Pinarius Scarpus, a veteran of the VII Legion, and for his son.

EDCS-10101239.text_edition: Ar Uri Ar Publian qui sibi vivi fecerunt  ti iu
EDCS-10101239.translation: Ar. Uri. Ar. Publian, who while alive made this for themselves.
EDCS-10101239.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Ar. Uri. Ar. Publian, who set up a monument for themselves while still living.

EDCS-10101242.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Caesonia Primitiva.
EDCS-10101242.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caesonia Primitiva, invoking the Dis Manibus (Divine Spirits of the Dead).

EDCS-10101243.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of names, possibly including a 'Caius Iu[...]'.

EDCS-10101246.translation: Januarius [set this up] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101246.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Januarius commemorates his wife, praising her as 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-10101248.text_edition: defunct-- annorum XXV Velox bene merenti
EDCS-10101248.translation: To Velox, who died at the age of 25, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101248.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a memorial to Velox, who died at 25 years old.

EDCS-10101249.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by a grandson to a deceased individual.

EDCS-10101251.text_edition: Iuliae Aureliae Hilarae quae vixit annis XXVIIII mensibus VII diebus II Aurelius Hecatus coniugi castissimae et incomparabili posuit et sibi
EDCS-10101251.translation: To Iulia Aurelia Hilara, who lived 29 years, 7 months, 2 days. Aurelius Hecatus placed this for his most chaste and incomparable wife, and for himself.
EDCS-10101251.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a tomb by Aurelius Hecatus for his wife, Iulia Aurelia Hilara, and for himself.

EDCS-10101268.text_edition: Aurelius Fortunius petitus a coniuge sua Aurelia Vernantilla sorori suae Ursae quae vixit ex caritate eorum sene sic ullo devitum sic Aurelius Fortunius concessit locum si quis autem filio meo VernMantiano#Vernantiano#VERMATIANO post ovitum sic eorum Ursa Vernantilla Fortunium quintum ponere voluerit davit sic ecclesiae argenti pondo V
EDCS-10101268.translation: Aurelius Fortunius, requested by his wife Aurelia Vernantilla, for his sister Ursa, who lived through their love without any [debt?]. Aurelius Fortunius thus granted the place. But if anyone, after their death, wishes to place Ursa Vernantilla and Fortunius Quintus for my son Vernantianus, they shall give five pounds of silver to the church.
EDCS-10101268.summary: This complex inscription records a burial plot granted by Aurelius Fortunius for his sister Ursa, at the request of his wife, and includes a stipulation for a monetary penalty if anyone wishes to place other individuals there later.

EDCS-10101274.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101274.text_edition: Arca Quiriacetis si quis voluerit aperire det argenti pondo quinque
EDCS-10101274.translation: The chest of Quiriacetis. If anyone wishes to open it, they shall give five pounds of silver.
EDCS-10101274.summary: This inscription appears to be a warning or regulation for a chest, possibly a tomb or a valuable container belonging to Quiriacetis, demanding a payment to open it.

EDCS-10101275.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely part of a funerary monument dedicated to a wife named La[---].

EDCS-10101504.text_edition: -- Marcianus SIV V et menses II dies
EDCS-10101504.translation: --- Marcianus, aged [---] years and 2 months and [---] days.
EDCS-10101504.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription providing the partial age of a deceased individual named Marcianus.

EDCS-10101590.text_edition: Ex voto Alexandri cot sic promisit solvi(t)
EDCS-10101590.translation: From the vow of Alexander, as he promised, he paid.
EDCS-10101590.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Alexander fulfilled a vow he had made.

EDCS-10101591.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Karpophore fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-10101592.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vivio Silvestro militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum centuria Ber annorum A CINNconiugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101592.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Vivius Silvester, soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, centuria Ber[---], aged [---]. His well-deserving wife placed this.
EDCS-10101592.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vivius Silvester, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, by his deserving wife.

EDCS-10101593.text_edition: Dis Manibus Campatiae Felicitati Geminius Felix maritus fecit piissimae
EDCS-10101593.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Geminius Felix, her husband, made this for Campatia Felicitas, a most devoted woman.
EDCS-10101593.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication to his deceased wife, Campatia Felicitas.

EDCS-10101594.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Quinti filius Severus vivus fecit sibi
EDCS-10101594.translation: Lucius Cassius Severus, son of Quintus, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-10101594.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Cassius Severus for himself during his lifetime, a common practice for pre-planning burial.

EDCS-10101595.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodiae Gratillae Aelia Germana matri pientissimae bene merenti
EDCS-10101595.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Clodia Gratilla. Aelia Germana made this for her most devoted and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10101595.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Germana to her deceased mother, Clodia Gratilla, highlighting maternal virtues.

EDCS-10101596.text_edition: Corelliae Melete defunctae nnorum XXX uxori optimae
EDCS-10101596.translation: To Corellia Melete, deceased at 30 years old, an excellent wife.
EDCS-10101596.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Corellia Melete, who died at the age of 30, and is remembered as an excellent wife.

EDCS-10101598.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Veronioni Titus Flavius Quintillus
EDCS-10101598.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Iulius Veronioni. Titus Flavius Quintillus [made this].
EDCS-10101598.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Quintillus to Iulius Veronioni, likely a relative or close associate.

EDCS-10101599.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Laberia Primula, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-10101600.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mariae Primae infelicissimae defunctae annorum VIIII Valeria Procula libertae posuit
EDCS-10101600.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Maria Prima, most unfortunate, deceased at 9 years old. Valeria Procula, her freedwoman, placed this.
EDCS-10101600.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maria Prima, a child who died at age 9, dedicated by Valeria Procula, likely her former slave and now freedwoman, indicating a close bond.

EDCS-10101602.text_edition: L onia Syrillua marito dulcissimo posuit
EDCS-10101602.translation: L. Onia Syrillua placed this for her sweetest husband.
EDCS-10101602.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by L. Onia Syrillua to her beloved deceased husband.

EDCS-10101616.summary: This is a personal identification, likely from a funerary monument or official record, identifying Cornelia Rustica as the freedwoman of Aulus.

EDCS-10101618.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Gnaeus Ahius Philemo.

EDCS-10101619.text_edition: veterano legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis Neria Pieris coniugi suo et sibi
EDCS-10101619.translation: To the veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis. Neria Pieris [made this] for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-10101619.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Neria Pieris to her husband, a veteran of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, and for herself.

EDCS-10101620.text_edition: Primigenius Publi Ploti |(centurionis) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis libertus annorum XXI sibi et pat
EDCS-10101620.translation: Primigenius, freedman of Publius Plotius, centurion of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, aged 21, for himself and his father.
EDCS-10101620.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primigenius, a 21-year-old freedman of a centurion from the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, made by or for himself and his father.

EDCS-10101621.text_edition: Titi Tremeli Lepidi nnorum V hic situs est
EDCS-10101621.translation: Titus Tremelius Lepidus, aged 5 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10101621.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of a child named Titus Tremelius Lepidus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-10101622.text_edition: Licinius Capito miles cohortis I Belgarum centuria VI Severi S unato
EDCS-10101622.translation: Licinius Capito, soldier of the First Cohort of Belgae, in the century of Severus, [---].
EDCS-10101622.summary: This is an inscription for Licinius Capito, a soldier from the First Cohort of Belgae, specifying his military unit and centuria.

EDCS-10101624.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicated to an individual named Passienus, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-10101632.text_edition: Salvius Crescens et Scantia Prima Napini delicatae suae  obitae nnorum VI
EDCS-10101632.translation: Salvius Crescens and Scantia Prima [made this] for their dear Napini, who died at 6 years old.
EDCS-10101632.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Salvius Crescens and Scantia Prima to their beloved Napini, who died at the young age of six.

EDCS-10101633.text_edition: Dis Manibus Modesto Titus Aemilius Quartio discenti bene merenti
EDCS-10101633.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Modestus, a deserving student, Titus Aemilius Quartio [made this].
EDCS-10101633.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Modestus, described as a deserving student, by Titus Aemilius Quartio.

EDCS-10101634.text_edition: Dis Manibus Felici Caesaris nostri servo annorum VIII Antiochus fratri piissimo
EDCS-10101634.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Antiochus [made this] for his most devoted brother Felix, slave of our Caesar, aged 8 years.
EDCS-10101634.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Antiochus for his brother Felix, a slave of the emperor who died at age eight.

EDCS-10101635.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Coylio sic Athenioni homini bono qui vixit nnos plus minus LXX Caesius Polybius collegae bono posuit
EDCS-10101635.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caesius Polybius placed this for Coylius, also known as Athenio, a good man who lived about 70 years, his good colleague.
EDCS-10101635.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caesius Polybius to his colleague Coylius, also known as Athenio, who lived for approximately 70 years.

EDCS-10101636.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Homini pagano Thelonico quendam sic retiario qui pietate populi rude liberatus est Xustus sic amicus et Pepticius sodalis
EDCS-10101636.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Thelonius, a countryman, also known as a retiarius, who was freed from the rudis by the piety of the people. Xustus, his friend, and Pepticius, his comrade, [made this].
EDCS-10101636.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Thelonius, a countryman and retiarius (net-fighter) who gained his freedom, dedicated by his friend Xustus and comrade Pepticius.

EDCS-10101637.text_edition: Villiae Philogenis libertae Suttae Dacae Lucius Clodius Adiutor matri posuit
EDCS-10101637.translation: To Villia Philogenis Sutta Daca, a freedwoman, Lucius Clodius Adiutor set this up for his mother.
EDCS-10101637.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Clodius Adiutor to his mother, Villia Philogenis Sutta Daca, a freedwoman.

EDCS-10101638.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cominiae Lupae nnum XXI Quintus Petronius Epigonus vivus coniugi bene merenti posuit et Petroniae Bidusae matri
EDCS-10101638.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Cominia Lupa, who lived 21 years. Quintus Petronius Epigonus, while living, set this up for his well-deserving wife, and for Petronia Bidusa, his mother.
EDCS-10101638.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Petronius Epigonus, while still alive, for his wife Cominia Lupa and his mother Petronia Bidusa.

EDCS-10101639.text_edition: Lucio Cafatio Spuri filio Celeri Pomponia Philumina mater posuit
EDCS-10101639.translation: To Lucius Cafatius Celer, son of Spurius. His mother, Pomponia Philumina, set this up.
EDCS-10101639.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Pomponia Philumina to her son, Lucius Cafatius Celer.

EDCS-10101640.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Firmus.
EDCS-10101640.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to a person named Firmus.

EDCS-10101641.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iphigeniae Valeriae quae vixit annos XXV hunc titulum posuit infelicissima Domitia mater suo sic sic cum magno dolore etsi maerent superi fortasse et inferi  multi
EDCS-10101641.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Valeria Iphigenia, who lived 25 years. Her most unhappy mother Domitia set up this monument for her own, with such great sorrow, even if the gods above and perhaps many gods below are also grieving.
EDCS-10101641.summary: This is a deeply emotional funerary inscription dedicated by the grieving mother Domitia to her daughter Valeria Iphigenia, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10101642.text_edition: Dis Manibus Noeto Augusti vernae adiutori tabularii bene merenti collegae fecerunt
EDCS-10101642.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Noetus, a slave born in the household of Augustus, an assistant in the accounts office, well-deserving. His colleagues made this.
EDCS-10101642.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his colleagues to Noetus, an imperial slave who served as an assistant in the tabularium (accounts office).

EDCS-10101643.text_edition: Dis Manibus nae defE(unctae)#def(unctae)#DEE annorum I mensium VII dierum XXI Mescenio defI(uncto)#d]ef(uncto)#EI nnorum IIII men(sium) --I dieI(rum)#die(rum)#DII XXI orius nepos
EDCS-10101643.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To [---] who died, aged 1 year, 7 months, 21 days. To Mescenius who died, aged 4 years, [---] months, 21 days. [---]orius, his nephew, [set this up].
EDCS-10101643.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to two deceased individuals, including a child named Mescenius, by their nephew [---]orius.

EDCS-10101644.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia Titiana P I fratribus Titiano et Cl
EDCS-10101644.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Titiana P. I. [set this up] for her brothers, Titianus and Cl[---].
EDCS-10101644.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Titiana to her two brothers, Titianus and another individual whose name begins with Cl.

EDCS-10101645.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Coylio sic Ursino Publius Valerius Severus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101645.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Coylius Ursinus. Publius Valerius Severus set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101645.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Valerius Severus to Coylius Ursinus, whom he considered well-deserving.

EDCS-10101646.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Urs defuncto annorum  AN
EDCS-10101646.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Urs[inus] deceased, aged [---] years.
EDCS-10101646.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius Ursinus, noting his deceased status and age in years.

EDCS-10101647.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Varronius TO.
EDCS-10101647.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Varronius TO.

EDCS-10101648.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titiae Moschi carissimae feminae qui sic vixit annos XXXVIII Titus Horesius Saturninus coniugi inconparabili sic bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101648.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titia Moschus, a most dear woman, who lived for 38 years. Titus Horesius Saturninus placed this for his incomparable and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101648.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Titus Horesius Saturninus to his beloved wife, Titia Moschus, who lived for 38 years.

EDCS-10101649.text_edition: Dis Manibus Kornelia Procla Valerio Proclo filio infelicissimo qui vixit plus minus annos XVII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101649.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cornelia Procla placed this for her most unfortunate son, Valerius Proclus, who lived approximately 17 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101649.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Cornelia Procla to her son, Valerius Proclus, who died at about 17 years old.

EDCS-10101651.text_edition: M VET Sym Secu die--- XXXVII F
EDCS-10101651.translation: M. Vet[urius] Sym[machus?], Secu[ndus?], on the day [---] 37. F.
EDCS-10101651.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, recording names and an age or date.

EDCS-10101656.text_edition: Noviae Compsini Novius et Novia patroni sic bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10101656.translation: Novius and Novia, as patrons, placed this for Novia Compsina, who well-deserved it.
EDCS-10101656.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Novius and Novia, likely as former owners or benefactors, to Novia Compsina, acknowledging her good service or merit.

EDCS-10101659.text_edition: coniunx EIVsus filius  pientissim Trophimo - Claudi Pomponiani --
EDCS-10101659.translation: His wife and most dutiful son [placed this] for Trophimus [---] Claudius Pomponianus [---].
EDCS-10101659.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Trophimus by his wife and son, possibly mentioning Claudius Pomponianus in another context.

EDCS-10101661.text_edition: IIIProclo menses X posuit Antonia Soteria alumno
EDCS-10101661.translation: Antonia Soteria placed this for Proclus, her foster-child, [who lived] 10 months.
EDCS-10101661.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Soteria to her foster-child, Proclus, who died at ten months of age.

EDCS-10101663.translation: Vatinia Privata [placed this] for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-10101663.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vatinia Privata to her son.

EDCS-10101666.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Clodius Maximus.

EDCS-10101671.translation: To Gaius Carfanius [---], his home-born slave [---].
EDCS-10101671.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary text, mentioning Gaius Carfanius and a vernaculus, a home-born slave.

EDCS-10101672.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription or dedication, possibly commemorating an individual who lived for 26 years and mentioning a patroness.

EDCS-10101674.translation: To Gaius P. Herm, her husband, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101674.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by a woman for her well-deserving husband, Gaius P. Herm.

EDCS-10101675.text_edition: Publi- Su SAET fecit C DCI
EDCS-10101675.translation: Publius Su(---) made this for Saetus.
EDCS-10101675.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication or funerary inscription, recording that Publius Su(---) made something for Saetus.

EDCS-10101682.text_edition: nti pisturi sic unddecemo sic Calendas
EDCS-10101682.translation: The baker, on the eleventh, on the Kalends.
EDCS-10101682.summary: This is a fragment likely related to an event or transaction, possibly involving a baker, dated to the eleventh day of a month and the Kalends.

EDCS-10101698.translation: Duovir of Singidunum, to his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-10101698.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely by a duovir (a high-ranking magistrate) of Singidunum (modern Belgrade), made for his freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-10101700.summary: This is a fragment bearing a part of a Roman name, ]vius Gaudentius, likely from a funerary or dedicatory inscription.

EDCS-10101701.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Fabricia who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-10101712.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Accio Zosimo qui vixit nnis XXIII menssibus XI diebus XVI fecit Accia Amabilis mater filio pientissimo
EDCS-10101712.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Marcus Accius Zosimus, who lived for 23 years, 11 months, and 16 days. His mother, Accia Amabilis, made this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-10101712.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, erected by Accia Amabilis for her highly devoted son, Marcus Accius Zosimus, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-10101713.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aebutiae Ursinae defunctae aEnnorPum#annorum#ENNOPU XXX Marcius Primitivus maritus et Cusia Victoria filia eius bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10101713.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aebutia Ursina, deceased, 30 years old. Her husband, Marcius Primitivus, and her daughter, Cusia Victoria, placed this for the well-deserving one.
EDCS-10101713.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, erected by Marcius Primitivus and Cusia Victoria for their well-deserving wife and mother, Aebutia Ursina, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10101714.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliae Maximinae defunctae annorum XXI posuit frater titulum Felicianus bene merenti D L S
EDCS-10101714.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aelia Maximina, deceased, 21 years old. Her brother, Felicianus, placed this inscription for the well-deserving one. (He fulfilled) a vow willingly, gladly, and deservedly.
EDCS-10101714.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, erected by Felicianus for his well-deserving sister, Aelia Maximina, who died at the age of 21, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10101715.text_edition: Dis Manibus Albiae Cale Albii Marina et Hermes matri piissimae
EDCS-10101715.translation: To the Divine Manes. Albius Marina and Hermes [erected this] for Albia Cale, their most devoted mother.
EDCS-10101715.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a monument by Albius Marina and Hermes to their mother, Albia Cale.

EDCS-10101716.text_edition: Titus Aurelius Messianus et Clodia Maximina sibi vivi posuerunt locum concessum a Domitia Antonia
EDCS-10101716.translation: Titus Aurelius Messianus and Clodia Maximina, while still living, erected this for themselves, on a plot granted by Domitia Antonia.
EDCS-10101716.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Titus Aurelius Messianus and Clodia Maximina acquired a burial plot from Domitia Antonia and prepared their own tomb during their lifetime.

EDCS-10101717.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101717.text_edition: Aurelius Saloninus concessit arcam Barroniae sic
EDCS-10101717.translation: Aurelius Saloninus granted the burial chest to Baronia, thus.
EDCS-10101717.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Saloninus granting a burial chest to Baronia, likely a specific concession within a larger burial ground.

EDCS-10101718.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription indicating that the heirs of Baebia Felicla were responsible for her monument.

EDCS-10101719.text_edition: Caio Caecilio Vilielliano Cornelia Titia marito rarissimo
EDCS-10101719.translation: Cornelia Titia [erected this] for Gaius Caecilius Viliellianus, her most exceptional husband.
EDCS-10101719.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cornelia Titia to her husband, Gaius Caecilius Viliellianus.

EDCS-10101720.text_edition: Calpurnia Pitiana arcam se viva posuit sibi et Publio Claudio coniugi suo domum aeternalem
EDCS-10101720.translation: Calpurnia Pitiana, while still living, erected this burial chest for herself and for Publius Claudius, her husband, an eternal home.
EDCS-10101720.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Calpurnia Pitiana's dedication of an eternal burial chest for herself and her husband, Publius Claudius, while she was still alive.

EDCS-10101721.text_edition: Dis Manibus Camuraenae Afrodisiae annorum XXX Caius Salvius Cosmicus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10101721.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Salvius Cosmicus [erected this] for Camuraena Afrodisia, who lived for 30 years, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101721.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Salvius Cosmicus to his well-deserving wife, Camuraena Afrodisia, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10101722.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Camureno Lupulo defuncto nnum XXVII Vibia Doxa infelicissimo coniugi posuit
EDCS-10101722.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibia Doxa erected this for Lucius Camurenus Lupulus, her most unfortunate husband, who died at the age of 27.
EDCS-10101722.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibia Doxa to her "most unfortunate" husband, Lucius Camurenus Lupulus, who died at age 27.

EDCS-10101723.text_edition: Dis Manibus Camurenae Luci filiae Proculae
EDCS-10101723.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Camurena Procula, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-10101723.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription dedicated to Camurena Procula, identified as the daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-10101724.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caninio Tito Caninia Syra patrono bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101724.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caninia Syra erected this for Caninius Titus, her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-10101724.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caninia Syra to her well-deserving patron, Caninius Titus.

EDCS-10101725.text_edition: nnosae defunctae nnorum XVII Claudia Quarta mater
EDCS-10101725.translation: To [the long-lived] deceased, seventeen years old, Claudia Quarta, her mother, [erected this].
EDCS-10101725.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother, Claudia Quarta, dedicating a monument to her deceased seventeen-year-old daughter.

EDCS-10101726.text_edition: defuncto annorum  mensium III liberto Claudius Ursus adfini bene merenti
EDCS-10101726.translation: To the deceased, [aged ---] years, three months, his freedman Claudius Ursus [erected this] to his well-deserving relative.
EDCS-10101726.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Claudius Ursus, a freedman, for a deceased male relative, highlighting their familial connection and the deceased's merit.

EDCS-10101727.text_edition: Coe Maritima mater Coeo in=VIfelicissimo#infelicissimo#VIFELICISMO annorum XXII mensium II bene merenti
EDCS-10101727.translation: Coe Maritima, mother, [erected this] to her most unfortunate Coeus, aged twenty-two years, two months, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101727.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Coe Maritima, to her deceased son Coeus, who died young at twenty-two years and two months, lamenting his unfortunate passing.

EDCS-10101728.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Cornelio Diadumeno Caius Cornelius Venamius libertus et heres bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101728.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Cornelius Venamius, freedman and heir, erected this to the well-deserving Gaius Cornelius Diadumenus.
EDCS-10101728.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Cornelius Diadumenus by his freedman and heir, Gaius Cornelius Venamius, indicating a close relationship of patronage and inheritance.

EDCS-10101729.text_edition: Flavia Eucarpia Charmideti filio pientissimo annorum XVI mensium IIII dierum IIII
EDCS-10101729.translation: Flavia Eucarpia [erected this] to Charmides, her most devoted son, aged sixteen years, four months, four days.
EDCS-10101729.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavia Eucarpia for her deeply beloved son, Charmides, who died at the young age of sixteen.

EDCS-10101731.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marcus Iulius Epafroditus se vivo posuit sibi et Iuliae Agrippinae coniugi suae
EDCS-10101731.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Iulius Epaphroditus erected this for himself while alive, and for Iulia Agrippina, his wife.
EDCS-10101731.summary: This is a funerary inscription made during his lifetime by Marcus Iulius Epaphroditus for himself and his wife, Iulia Agrippina, anticipating their future burial.

EDCS-10101732.text_edition: Depositio si sorori et Iulio Epicteto vivo Ianuarius filius posuit
EDCS-10101732.translation: The burial site for his sister and for Iulius Epictetus, still living; Ianuarius, his son, erected this.
EDCS-10101732.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot prepared by Ianuarius for his sister and his still-living father, Iulius Epictetus, indicating a familial burial ground.

EDCS-10101733.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Eutychini Iulia Fortunata alumnae M
EDCS-10101733.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Fortunata [erected this] to Iulia Eutychine, her foster-daughter, [aged] M [years].
EDCS-10101733.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulia Fortunata for her foster-daughter, Iulia Eutychine, highlighting the bond between them.

EDCS-10101734.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Feliculae amicae bonae Septueia Ingenua posuit
EDCS-10101734.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Septueia Ingenua erected this to Iulia Felicula, her good friend.
EDCS-10101734.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Septueia Ingenua to Iulia Felicula, commemorating their friendship.

EDCS-10101735.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  ]] II(?)[] [[  Iulius Fronto filius Vitalianus filia Valentilla Vibia matri
EDCS-10101735.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulius Fronto, son, Vitalianus, daughter, Valentilla Vibia, [erected this] to their mother.
EDCS-10101735.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by multiple children—Iulius Fronto, Vitalianus, and Valentilla Vibia—in honor of their mother.

EDCS-10101737.text_edition: coniugi bene merenti Lucius Ulpius Serapφio#Serapio#SERAφIO
EDCS-10101737.translation: To his well-deserving wife, Lucius Ulpius Serapio (set this up).
EDCS-10101737.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Ulpius Serapio for his wife, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101738.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maria Ofelima Marco Memnio Primitio suo infelicissimo annorum XVIIII
EDCS-10101738.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Maria Ofelima (set this up) for her most unfortunate Marcus Memnius Primitius, who lived 19 years.
EDCS-10101738.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maria Ofelima to her deceased Marcus Memnius Primitius, who died at the age of 19, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-10101739.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Masuriae Titiae coniugi inconparabili sic quae vixit nnos XXXI menses VI Ursus tabellarius posuit coniugi bene merenti quae vixit mecum nnos XVII menses V et sibi vivus posuit
EDCS-10101739.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Ursus, a letter-carrier, placed this for his incomparable wife Masuria Titia, who lived 31 years and 6 months; for his well-deserving wife with whom he lived for 17 years and 5 months; and for himself, alive, he placed this.
EDCS-10101739.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ursus, a letter-carrier, for his wife Masuria Titia, who died at 31, and for himself while still living, detailing their years together.

EDCS-10101740.text_edition: annorum VI Titus Mescenius Euprepes maritus bene merenti posuit et mater Trofime
EDCS-10101740.translation: He lived 6 years. Titus Mescenius Euprepes, her husband, placed this for his well-deserving wife, and her mother Trofime (also contributed).
EDCS-10101740.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Mescenius Euprepes and his mother-in-law Trofime for his well-deserving wife, who died at the age of six, probably an error in transcription or a very young spouse.

EDCS-10101741.text_edition: Dis Manibus Minicius Hermes pater Minicio Felici filio pientissimo defuncto nnorum XII
EDCS-10101741.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Minicius Hermes, father, (set this up) for Minicius Felix, his most devoted son, who died at 12 years of age.
EDCS-10101741.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Minicius Hermes to his beloved son, Minicius Felix, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-10101742.text_edition: Dis Manibus Plotia Fortunata VALVEN merenti
EDCS-10101742.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Plotia Fortunata (set this up for her) well-deserving VALVEN[...].
EDCS-10101742.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Plotia Fortunata for a well-deserving individual whose name is partially preserved as VALVEN, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101743.text_edition: Marco Seppieno Aurioni defuncto annorum VIIII Marcus Seppienus Eutychus pater  posuit filio
EDCS-10101743.translation: For Marcus Seppienus Aurion, deceased at 9 years of age. Marcus Seppienus Eutychus, father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-10101743.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Seppienus Eutychus for his son, Marcus Seppienus Aurion, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-10101744.text_edition: - Seppieno Eutycheti defuncto annorum XVI Seppiena Nice mater filio infelicissimo posuit
EDCS-10101744.translation: For Seppienus Eutyches, deceased at 16 years of age. Seppiena Nice, mother, placed this for her most unfortunate son.
EDCS-10101744.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Seppiena Nice to her deceased son, Seppienus Eutyches, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-10101745.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septiena Marcia coniugi carissimo pientissimo Iulio Crispino qui et Passar miles strator viri consularis bene merenti
EDCS-10101745.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Septiena Marcia (set this up) for her dearest, most devoted husband, Julius Crispinus, also known as Passar, a cavalryman, groom of a consular, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101745.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Septiena Marcia to her beloved husband, Julius Crispinus (also Passar), a cavalryman and groom to a consular official, highlighting his service and devotion.

EDCS-10101746.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Valentinae coniugi rarissimae
EDCS-10101746.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Valeria Valentina, a most uncommon wife.
EDCS-10101746.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Valeria Valentina, praised as a most uncommon wife, likely by her husband during the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101747.text_edition: tiliae Secundinae Marcus UlIpius#Ulpius#UIP Germanus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-10101747.translation: To Tilia Secundina. Marcus Ulpius Germanus to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101747.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Marcus Ulpius Germanus to his wife, Tilia Secundina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101748.text_edition: Crescens vixit annos IIII menses VI Prima mater infelicissima filio fecit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-10101748.translation: Crescens lived for 4 years, 6 months. Prima, a most unhappy mother, made this for her son. He is laid here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-10101748.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Prima, an grieving mother, for her young son, Crescens, who died at the age of 4 years and 6 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101749.translation: To Ritma. Lebinthus to his dearest wife.
EDCS-10101749.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lebinthus for his beloved wife, Ritma, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101750.translation: To the Divine Manes. A friend placed this for Restutus.
EDCS-10101750.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a friend to Restutus, invoking the Divine Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101753.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Verae defunctae nnorum XXVII coniugi incomparabili Iulius Fortunatus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101753.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Valeria Vera, who died at 27 years old, an incomparable wife. Julius Fortunatus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101753.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Julius Fortunatus for his incomparable wife, Valeria Vera, who died at the age of 27, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101754.text_edition: Dis Manibus Epetinae defunctae annorum XXV Lucris filiae infelicissimae
EDCS-10101754.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Epetina, who died at 25 years old. To a most unhappy daughter, Lucris.
EDCS-10101754.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Epetina, who died at 25, by Lucris, her most unhappy daughter, under the protection of the Divine Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101756.text_edition: Caius Iulius Numida Apollini votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101756.translation: Gaius Julius Numida willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-10101756.summary: This inscription records Gaius Julius Numida fulfilling a vow to the god Apollo, likely a votive altar or monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101760.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudio Prisco coniugi carissimo et infelicissimo
EDCS-10101760.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Claudius Priscus, a most dear and most unhappy husband.
EDCS-10101760.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Claudius Priscus, described as a most dear and unhappy husband, under the protection of the Divine Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101761.text_edition: VIV Cupinae defunctae nnorum XVIII Corellius Cassius pater
EDCS-10101761.translation: To Cupina, who died at 18 years old. Corellius Cassius, father.
EDCS-10101761.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Corellius Cassius for his daughter, Cupina, who died at the age of 18, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101762.text_edition: Herennius Capitolinus Aemiliae Maxximillae coniugi inconparabili sic
EDCS-10101762.translation: Herennius Capitolinus to Aemilia Maxximilla, his incomparable wife. So it is.
EDCS-10101762.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Herennius Capitolinus for his incomparable wife, Aemilia Maxximilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101763.text_edition: Dis Manibus Papinio ThreIptiano#Threptiano#THRIPTIANO Papinia Festiva liberta
EDCS-10101763.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Papinius ThreIptianus (Threptianus, THRIPTIANO). Papinia Festiva, his freedwoman (set this up).
EDCS-10101763.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Papinius ThreIptianus by his freedwoman, Papinia Festiva.

EDCS-10101764.text_edition: Selia Marci liberta Chia hic ego Chia cinis fatis mea forma deleta? est mors cuncta aequat m
EDCS-10101764.translation: Selia Chia, freedwoman of Marcus. Here I, Chia, am ashes, my form destroyed by fate. Death makes all things equal.
EDCS-10101764.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Selia Chia, a freedwoman, reflecting on mortality.

EDCS-10101765.text_edition: Dis Manibus Annidi Messor conservae pientissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101765.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Annidis Messor, her most devoted fellow slave, well-deserving. (Someone) set this up.
EDCS-10101765.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Annidis Messor by an unnamed fellow slave, possibly from the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101766.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ἀχατία Λαμπάδις  Σιμπλικι Καμπαγι Πανηγορίς
EDCS-10101766.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Achatia Lampadis, Simplicius, Campagius, Panegoris.
EDCS-10101766.summary: This appears to be a funerary inscription listing several individuals, likely family members or companions, dedicated to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-10101800.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum pro salute et reditu Luci Pomponi Valentini Lucius Pomponius Restutus cliens
EDCS-10101800.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, for the health and return of Lucius Pomponius Valentinus. Lucius Pomponius Restutus, his client (set this up).
EDCS-10101800.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Lucius Pomponius Restutus, a client, on behalf of Lucius Pomponius Valentinus, likely for his safe return from a journey, possibly dating to the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101801.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Capito decurio municipi Salvio natus Starve qui vixit annos XLV Aelius Victor filius ipsius patri karissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101801.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelius Capito, decurion of the municipality of Salvia, born to Starvus, who lived 45 years. Aelius Victor, his son, set this up for his dearest, well-deserving father.
EDCS-10101801.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Capito, a decurion, set up by his son Aelius Victor, likely from the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101802.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Liciniae defunctae annorum XVIIII pat(er) filiae pientissimae posuit
EDCS-10101802.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Licinia, who died at 19 years old. Her father set this up for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-10101802.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia, aged 19, erected by her father, likely from the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101803.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Vettiae Ingenuae matri pientissimae quae vixit nnos XL Vettia Tertullina posuit
EDCS-10101803.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Vettia Ingenua, her most devoted mother, who lived 40 years. Vettia Tertullina set this up.
EDCS-10101803.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vettia Ingenua, aged 40, erected by her daughter Vettia Tertullina, likely from the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101804.text_edition: Dis Manibus Actiaco Marinus tatae baene merenti et Glaucus Filocrenus bene merenti
EDCS-10101804.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Actiacus, his well-deserving 'tata' (father), and to Glaucus Filocrenus, well-deserving. Marinus (set this up).
EDCS-10101804.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marinus to Actiacus, whom he refers to as 'tata', and to Glaucus Filocrenus, possibly his sons or other close relatives, likely from the second or third century CE.

EDCS-10101805.text_edition: Lucius Antonius Niger Antoniae Telesphoridi libertae et coniugi
EDCS-10101805.translation: Lucius Antonius Niger (set this up) for Antonia Telesphoridi, his freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-10101805.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Antonius Niger for Antonia Telesphoridi, who was both his freedwoman and wife.

EDCS-10101806.text_edition: Nymphis Cato et Lupo socii votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-10101806.translation: To the Nymphs, Cato and Lupus, their companions, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10101806.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the Nymphs by Cato and Lupus along with their companions, likely in gratitude for a wish fulfilled.

EDCS-10101807.text_edition: Arrio Calationi et Minatiae Myrine parentibus bene merentibus filia fecit
EDCS-10101807.translation: To Arrius Calatio and Minatia Myrine, well-deserving parents, their daughter made this.
EDCS-10101807.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by a daughter in honor of her deceased parents, Arrius Calatio and Minatia Myrine.

EDCS-10101808.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Iulia Saturnina ETE.
EDCS-10101808.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Iulia Saturnina, with the final letters 'ETE' possibly representing an abbreviation or an incomplete word.

EDCS-10101812.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Publius C A Abas voto posuit
EDCS-10101812.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Publius C. A. Abas placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10101812.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Silvanus Augustus made by Publius C. A. Abas, likely in gratitude or for a favor sought.

EDCS-10101813.text_edition: Dis Manibus Papirius Quintio Dionisiae sic coniugi quae vixitm mecum annis XXVI et sibi posuit
EDCS-10101813.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Papirius Quintio placed this for Dionisia, his wife, who lived with him for 26 years, and for himself.
EDCS-10101813.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dionisia, erected by her husband Papirius Quintio, who also prepared the monument for himself, indicating a marital tomb.

EDCS-10101814.text_edition: Rutiliae Zonae puellae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XXI sumptus sic eius positum et Rutiliae Augustae sorori instantia Rutiliorum Zenonis et Latinae parentum Clodius Fortunatus locum concessit  Arca Iuliano Pandurio
EDCS-10101814.translation: For Rutilia Zona, a most unhappy girl who lived for 21 years, her burial expenses were met and this monument placed, and for Rutilia Augusta, her sister; at the insistence of their parents Rutilii Zeno and Latina, Clodius Fortunatus granted the space. The coffin is for Iulianus Pandurius.
EDCS-10101814.summary: This complex funerary inscription commemorates two sisters, Rutilia Zona and Rutilia Augusta, with the plot granted by Clodius Fortunatus at the request of their parents, and separately mentions a coffin for Iulianus Pandurius, suggesting a shared or reused burial ground.

EDCS-10101816.text_edition: Turoni Statariae Turi filiae hic sitae
EDCS-10101816.translation: Here lies Turonia Stataria, daughter of Turus.
EDCS-10101816.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Turonia Stataria, daughter of Turus.

EDCS-10101817.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101817.summary: This inscription identifies a coffin belonging to Iulianus Pandurius, likely indicating its purpose within a larger funerary context.

EDCS-10101819.text_edition: Pomponius VaMlentinus#Valentinus#VMLENTINUS et Titia Rufina P C C P S fecerunt
EDCS-10101819.translation: Pomponius Valentinus and Titia Rufina made this P C C P S.
EDCS-10101819.summary: This inscription records that Pomponius Valentinus and Titia Rufina made something, with the abbreviation P C C P S likely referring to the purpose or nature of their act, possibly related to a dedication or construction.

EDCS-10101822.translation: ... to gi[...], to a companion [...] Pertenac[...]
EDCS-10101822.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or honorary, mentioning a companion and a name possibly related to Pertinax.

EDCS-10101824.text_edition: Septiminae IITEMPOLA annorum XI FIIEEGIVR O annorum OV IIIOM
EDCS-10101824.translation: To Septimina, [aged] 11 years, [and to ---], aged [---] years.
EDCS-10101824.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Septimina, aged 11, and another unnamed individual, likely children given their young ages.

EDCS-10101825.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing parts of two names, likely funerary or dedicatory, but too little remains to determine its exact purpose or context.

EDCS-10101826.translation: To Aplo Varierius Plahenius, aged 30 years.
EDCS-10101826.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aplo Varierius Plahenius, who lived to be 30 years old.

EDCS-10101828.text_edition: Aureliae Stercoriae infanti infelicissimae quae vixit nnum I mensem I dies VIII Aurelius Pat et Fabia Maxima parentes filiae dulcissimae et sibi vivi
EDCS-10101828.translation: To Aurelia Stercoria, a most unfortunate infant, who lived 1 year, 1 month, 8 days. Aurelius Patus and Fabia Maxima, her parents, [made this] for their sweetest daughter and for themselves while living.
EDCS-10101828.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Patus and Fabia Maxima for their infant daughter, Aurelia Stercoria, who died at a very young age, and also for themselves while still alive, common in anticipation of their own deaths.

EDCS-10101829.text_edition: - Antonius Martialis signum Minervae et aedem vetustate consumptam restituit
EDCS-10101829.translation: Antonius Martialis restored the statue of Minerva and the temple, which had been consumed by old age.
EDCS-10101829.summary: This inscription records Antonius Martialis' restoration of a statue of Minerva and a dilapidated temple, indicating an act of pietas or public beneficence.

EDCS-10101830.text_edition: Manibus Cai Valeri Cai fili Scaptia Secundi Altino praefecti kastrorum legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis
EDCS-10101830.translation: To the spirits of Gaius Valerius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Scaptia tribe, from Altinum, prefect of the camp of the Eleventh Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion.
EDCS-10101830.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Valerius Secundus, a camp prefect of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, detailing his name, filiation, tribe, origin, and military rank.

EDCS-10101831.text_edition: Deo p  Quintus V Ph votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101831.translation: To the god P[---]. Quintus V[---] P[---] willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10101831.summary: This inscription records Quintus V[---] P[---]'s fulfillment of a vow to an unnamed deity, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-10101832.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Billianius  filius Verus beneficiarius consularis
EDCS-10101832.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Billianius Verus, son of [---], a beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-10101832.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Billianius Verus, identifying him as a beneficiarius consularis, a soldier holding a privileged position attached to a provincial governor.

EDCS-10101833.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a military or administrative record, indicating the 'century of Betuus,' which refers to a specific military unit or land division.

EDCS-10101838.text_edition: Tertius Baebius Cai filius Scaptia domo Florentia nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XVII miles legionis XI cornicen
EDCS-10101838.translation: Tertius Baebius, son of Gaius, of the Scaptia tribe, from Florentia, aged 35 years, with 17 years of service, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, a cornicen (horn-blower).
EDCS-10101838.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tertius Baebius, a horn-blower in the Eleventh Legion, detailing his name, filiation, tribe, origin, age, and military service.

EDCS-10101839.text_edition: Lucius Bodlius Luci filius Pollia Genialis Pollentia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Iuli Secundi nnorum XXX stipendiorum X heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-10101839.translation: Lucius Bodlius, son of Lucius, of the Pollian tribe, Genialis, from Pollentia, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the centuria of Iulius Secundus, lived for 30 years and served for 10 years. His heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10101839.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Bodlius Genialis, a Roman soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected by his heir.

EDCS-10101840.text_edition: Satrius  filius Camilia Rufus Ravenna miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Fabrici Veri nnorum XXXIII stipendiorum XV testamento fieri iussit Caius Muscius Maternus et Caius Asuvius Crescens heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-10101840.translation: Satrius, son of [---], of the Camilian tribe, Rufus, from Ravenna, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the centuria of Fabricius Verus, lived for 33 years and served for 15 years. He ordered this to be made by his will. Gaius Muscius Maternus and Gaius Asuvius Crescens, his heirs, arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-10101840.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Satrius Rufus, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected by his heirs according to his will.

EDCS-10101843.text_edition: Sextus Valerius Sexti filius Voltinia Lucinus Vianna sic miles legionis VIII Augustae centuria Terenti Iuliani vixit annis XXIX miles annis VIIII
EDCS-10101843.translation: Sextus Valerius, son of Sextus, of the Voltinian tribe, Lucinus, from Vianna (?), a soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta, from the centuria of Terentius Iulianus, lived for 29 years and served as a soldier for 9 years.
EDCS-10101843.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Valerius Lucinus, a soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta.

EDCS-10101844.text_edition: Caius Vaternius Cai filius Lemonia Aprio Bononia stipeF(ndiorum)#stipe(ndiorum)#STIPF XIX heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-10101844.translation: Gaius Vaternius, son of Gaius, of the Lemonian tribe, Aprio, from Bononia, served for 19 years. His heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10101844.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Vaternius Aprio, likely a soldier given the mention of stipends, erected by his heir.

EDCS-10101845.text_edition: Dacnas Apsaei filius miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum domo Berea nnorum L stipendiorum XXIV hic situs est
EDCS-10101845.translation: Dacnas, son of Apsaeus, a soldier of the Cohors II Cyrrhestarum, from Berea, lived for 50 years and served for 24 years. He lies here.
EDCS-10101845.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dacnas, a soldier of the Cohors II Cyrrhestarum.

EDCS-10101847.text_edition: Caius Egnatius Rogatus Silvano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101847.translation: Gaius Egnatius Rogatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-10101847.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Gaius Egnatius Rogatus.

EDCS-10101848.text_edition: Tertia Claudia TIriviis#Triviis#IRIVIS votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101848.translation: Tertia Claudia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Trivia.
EDCS-10101848.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Trivia by Tertia Claudia.

EDCS-10101850.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Nepotiano decurioni municipii Varvarinorum qui vixit annos LI dies XXII Aurelia Verina
EDCS-10101850.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Nepotianus, decurion of the municipium of Varvarinorum, who lived for 51 years and 22 days. Aurelia Verina [set this up].
EDCS-10101850.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Nepotianus, a decurion, by Aurelia Verina.

EDCS-10101851.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titiae Flaviae Crescentillae sanctitatis unicae sinceritatis incomparabilis sapientiae singularis suis desideranda grata omnibus amabilis notis cuius nomen mutavit affectus ex Crescentilla enim vocata est mamma quae sola sine inimico vixit cuius casum nemo non flevit Victoria filia matri carissimae posuit quae vixit annos LXXI
EDCS-10101851.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Titia Flavia Crescentilla, of unique sanctity, incomparable sincerity, and singular wisdom, missed by her family, pleasing to all, beloved by those who knew her, whose name affection changed, for from Crescentilla she was called 'Mamma.' She alone lived without an enemy, and no one failed to weep at her passing. Victoria, her daughter, placed this for her dearest mother, who lived for 71 years.
EDCS-10101851.summary: This is an elaborate funerary inscription dedicated by Victoria to her beloved mother, Titia Flavia Crescentilla, praising her virtues and unique character.

EDCS-10101853.text_edition: Libero Patri Proculus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101853.translation: Proculus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Liber Pater.
EDCS-10101853.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Liber Pater by Proculus.

EDCS-10101854.text_edition: Publio Atilio Aebutiano praefecto praetorio clarissimo viro patrono optimo ordo Asseriatium
EDCS-10101854.translation: To Publius Atilius Aebutianus, most illustrious man, praetorian prefect, best patron, from the order of the Asseriates.
EDCS-10101854.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Asseriates to Publius Atilius Aebutianus, honoring him as an illustrious praetorian prefect and their best patron, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10101856.text_edition: Lucius Caninius Titi filius Claudia Fronto IIvir flamen divi Claudii IIvir quinquennalis
EDCS-10101856.translation: Lucius Caninius Fronto, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, duovir, flamen of the deified Claudius, quinquennial duovir.
