EDCS-10101856.summary: This inscription details the career and honors of Lucius Caninius Fronto, a local magistrate from the Claudia tribe who served as duovir, flamen of the deified Claudius, and quinquennial duovir, likely during the Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-10101859.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Daeico Aureli Fortunionis TV
EDCS-10101859.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Of Daeicus, son of Aurelius Fortunio. TV.
EDCS-10101859.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Daeicus, son of Aurelius Fortunio, suggesting a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-10101861.translation: To Lucius Clodius, son of Quintus, four times.
EDCS-10101861.summary: This fragmentary inscription is dedicated to Lucius Clodius, son of Quintus, possibly indicating an honor or a public office held four times.

EDCS-10101862.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription likely records the age of the deceased and indicates that it was dedicated by Maximina, presumably the mother, for her child.

EDCS-10101863.summary: This brief inscription records the name of Papirius Secundus, son of Gaius, likely part of a larger dedication or a property marker.

EDCS-10101864.text_edition: Dis Manibus Agathe Saecundino sic filio annorum XV SV PO
EDCS-10101864.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Agathe Saecundinus, her son, aged 15. SV PO.
EDCS-10101864.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Agathe Saecundinus, who died at the age of 15, likely erected by his mother Agathe or a relative.

EDCS-10101865.text_edition: Dis Manibus Epicatiae coniugi Geminus maritus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-10101865.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Epicatia, his wife, Geminus, her well-deserving husband, placed this.
EDCS-10101865.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Epicatia, erected by her husband Geminus, honoring her as a well-deserving spouse.

EDCS-10101867.text_edition: Titia Marci filia Rufina TAB votum solvit libens m(erito)
EDCS-10101867.translation: Titia Rufina, daughter of Marcus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101867.summary: This inscription records that Titia Rufina, daughter of Marcus, fulfilled a vow she had made, indicating a religious dedication or offering.

EDCS-10101868.text_edition: Caesaris Augusti inter Sidrinos et Asseriates Quintus Aebutius Liberalis |(centurio) legionis XI definit
EDCS-10101868.translation: Quintus Aebutius Liberalis, centurion of the eleventh legion, defines the boundary of Caesar Augustus between the Sidrini and the Asseriates.
EDCS-10101868.summary: This inscription records the official act of Quintus Aebutius Liberalis, a centurion of Legio XI, who demarcated a boundary on behalf of Caesar Augustus between the territories of the Sidrini and the Asseriates, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-10101870.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Aurelius [--]
EDCS-10101870.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, likely for a Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-10101872.text_edition: Valetudini Statae sacrum Lurnio Calpurnia votum solvit libens
EDCS-10101872.translation: Sacred to Stata Valetudo. Lurnia Calpurnia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101872.summary: This inscription records Lurnia Calpurnia's fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Stata Valetudo, likely for good health.

EDCS-10101874.text_edition: Baebilius Maxsimus sic sibi et suis
EDCS-10101874.translation: Baebilius Maximus [made this] for himself and his own.
EDCS-10101874.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription made by Baebilius Maximus for himself and his family.

EDCS-10101875.text_edition: Quintus Gellius Hermeros sibi Quinto G(ellio) Maximo? fil(io)
EDCS-10101875.translation: Quintus Gellius Hermeros [made this] for himself [and] for Quintus Gellius Maximus(?), his son.
EDCS-10101875.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Gellius Hermeros for himself and his son, Quintus Gellius Maximus.

EDCS-10101876.text_edition: Quinto Hostilio  annorum VIII Ius Lupus
EDCS-10101876.translation: To Quintus Hostilius, eight years old. Ius Lupus [made this].
EDCS-10101876.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ius Lupus to Quintus Hostilius, who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-10101877.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Procula, identified as a daughter, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-10101878.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a son of Gaius and a dedication or relationship to Quinta, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-10101880.text_edition: IA  ex testamento fratris posuit rio Celso
EDCS-10101880.translation: [---] IA, placed according to the will of his/her brother [---]rius Celsus.
EDCS-10101880.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating a monument or offering placed by someone in accordance with the will of a brother named Celsus.

EDCS-10101883.text_edition: Iovi Kalpurnia Peculiaris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101883.translation: To Jupiter. Calpurnia Peculiaris willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101883.summary: This inscription records Calpurnia Peculiaris's fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter, indicating a dedication to the chief Roman deity.

EDCS-10101884.text_edition: Libero Patri Quintus Hostilius Barcinus voto fecit
EDCS-10101884.translation: To Liber Pater. Quintus Hostilius Barcinus made this as a vow.
EDCS-10101884.summary: This inscription records Quintus Hostilius Barcinus making a dedication or monument to Liber Pater as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10101885.text_edition: finis inter Ansienses et Corinienses secundum conventionem utriusque partis derectus mensuris actis iussu Auli Duceni Gemini legati Augusti pro praetore
EDCS-10101885.translation: The boundary between the Ansienses and Corinienses was established according to an agreement between both parties, surveyed and measured by order of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, imperial legate with propraetorian power.
EDCS-10101885.summary: This inscription records the delimitation of a boundary between two local communities, the Ansienses and Corinienses, carried out under the authority of an imperial legate, likely dating from the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-10101888.text_edition: area viva fecit iae Terti Calpurnio
EDCS-10101888.translation: An area of living rock was made for Terti[us] Calpurnius.
EDCS-10101888.summary: This inscription appears to record the creation of a rock-cut area for a person named Tertius Calpurnius, possibly for a tomb or a monument.

EDCS-10101893.text_edition: Baebius Priscus Volcano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101893.translation: Baebius Priscus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Vulcan.
EDCS-10101893.summary: This is a votive inscription by Baebius Priscus to the god Vulcan, indicating the fulfillment of a promise.

EDCS-10101895.text_edition: Dis Manibus Heraclio filio dulcissimo qui vixit nnos VI menses VIII dies XXII et Heracliae quae vixit nnos XXV infelicissimis set sic sibi pientissimis ut mater filium non amplius quam unam noctem desereret ut merito filius patri et uxor marito assiduas lacrimas relinquerent Velox filio dulcissimo et coniugi incomparabilibus
EDCS-10101895.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Heraclius, sweetest son, who lived 6 years, 8 months, 22 days, and to Heraclia, who lived 25 years. Most unhappy, but so devoted to each other that the mother did not leave her son for more than one night, so that deservedly the son to the father and the wife to the husband left unending tears. Velox [set this up] for his sweetest son and incomparable wife.
EDCS-10101895.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Velox to his deceased son Heraclius and his wife Heraclia, expressing profound grief and their mutual devotion.

EDCS-10101896.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quartillae matri pientissimae quae vixit nnos plus minus LXXXV filii bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-10101896.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quartilla, most devoted mother, who lived about 85 years. Her sons placed this for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10101896.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by sons for their mother Quartilla, who lived to an old age.

EDCS-10101916.text_edition: Sentonae Sextus Aemilius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101916.translation: Sextus Aemilius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sentona.
EDCS-10101916.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Aemilius to the goddess Sentona, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10101918.text_edition: Vestae Sextus Aquilius Lucifer votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101918.translation: Sextus Aquilius Lucifer willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Vesta.
EDCS-10101918.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Aquilius Lucifer to the goddess Vesta, indicating the fulfillment of a promise.

EDCS-10101922.text_edition: In memoriam liberorum suorum Aquillia Luci filia Oculatina de pecunia sua fecit
EDCS-10101922.translation: In memory of her children, Aquillia Oculatina, daughter of Lucius, made this from her own money.
EDCS-10101922.summary: This inscription records a monument or memorial made by Aquillia Oculatina for her deceased children, funded from her personal resources.

EDCS-10101923.text_edition: Sentonae sacrum Caius Vibius Florus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101923.translation: Sacred to Sentona. Gaius Vibius Florus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10101923.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Gaius Vibius Florus to the goddess Sentona, marking the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10101924.text_edition: Sentonae sacrum Tullia Fusca votum solvit
EDCS-10101924.translation: Sacred to Sentona. Tullia Fusca fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101924.summary: This is a votive inscription by Tullia Fusca to the goddess Sentona, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-10101925.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Caius Vibius Fesius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101925.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Gaius Vibius Fesius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10101925.summary: This inscription records Gaius Vibius Fesius dedicating an altar or offering to the god Silvanus in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101926.text_edition: Acaica Hoia pro Benigna et Iadestino votum solvit
EDCS-10101926.translation: Acaica Hoia fulfilled a vow on behalf of Benigna and Iadestinus.
EDCS-10101926.summary: This inscription records Acaica Hoia fulfilling a vow on behalf of two other individuals, Benigna and Iadestinus, likely on an altar or votive tablet.

EDCS-10101927.text_edition: nama Sexti filia Matto votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-10101927.translation: Nama, daughter of Sextus, Matto, willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10101927.summary: This inscription records Nama, the daughter of Sextus, (also named) Matto, fulfilling a vow, likely on a votive monument dedicated to a deity.

EDCS-10101929.translation: To Gavillia, daughter of Lucius. To Heros.
EDCS-10101929.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Gavillia, daughter of Lucius, and also includes a dedication to Heros, likely on a tombstone or funerary monument.

EDCS-10101930.translation: To Gaius Valerius Seneca, aged 22 years.
EDCS-10101930.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Gaius Valerius Seneca, who died at the age of 22.

EDCS-10101931.text_edition: NAETI ANEN ius Quinti filius   Volt
EDCS-10101931.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to commemorate Naetus, the son of Quintus, and mentions "Volt-" which could be part of a name or origin, likely on a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-10101932.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a freedman of Quintus and a name starting with "Tro-", likely part of a funerary or manumission record.

EDCS-10101934.summary: This fragmentary inscription includes a partial word ending in "-um," the name Gaius Geminius, and the name Claudia, possibly part of a dedicatory or funerary monument involving these individuals.

EDCS-10101937.text_edition: sorori piae Iuliae Tertullinus frater fecit quae vixit annos XXII
EDCS-10101937.translation: Tertullinus, her brother, made this for his devoted sister, Iulia Tertullina, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-10101937.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Tertullinus for his sister Iulia Tertullina, who lived for 22 years, recording their sibling relationship and her age at death.

EDCS-10101940.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Leucius Asclepius patri suo infelicissimo Leucio Asclepio enpiri[co! &
EDCS-10101940.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Leucius Asclepius made this for his most unfortunate father, Leucius Asclepius, the empiric.
EDCS-10101940.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Leucius Asclepius for his father, also named Leucius Asclepius, an empiric (a type of physician), and is addressed to the Manes, the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-10101941.text_edition: Dis Manibus Servilia Prima mater Martino filio pientissimo
EDCS-10101941.translation: To the Divine Manes. Servilia Prima, the mother, to Martinus, her most devoted son.
EDCS-10101941.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Servilia Prima to her deceased son, Martinus.

EDCS-10101962.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to a woman named Campia by her spouse.

EDCS-10101970.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Super hic situs est vixit annos II menses VII
EDCS-10101970.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Super lies here. He lived for 2 years, 7 months.
EDCS-10101970.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Super, noting his brief lifespan.

EDCS-10101971.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vivio sic Traeboniano sic Gallo et Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vivio sic Atinio sic Veldumniano Volusiano Auggustis
EDCS-10101971.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus and to the Imperator Caesar Gaius Vibius Afinius Valdumianus Volusianus, Augusti.
EDCS-10101971.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, likely dating to their joint reign (251-253 CE).

EDCS-10101972.text_edition: Imperatori Gaiulo Iulio Vero Macximino sic Pio Felici et Caio Iulio Vero Macximo sic i nobilissimo Caesari Augusto Caesaris nostri filio
EDCS-10101972.translation: To Imperator Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix, and to Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, son of our Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-10101972.summary: This inscription honors the emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely erected during their joint rule (236-238 CE).

EDCS-10101974.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Claudio Constanti nobilissimo Caesari filio domini nostri Constantini Maximi Victoris sem semper Augusti
EDCS-10101974.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Constans, most noble Caesar, son of our lord Constantine the Great, Victor, always Augustus.
EDCS-10101974.summary: This inscription honors Constans as a noble Caesar and son of Constantine the Great, likely dating to the period when Constans held the title of Caesar (333-337 CE).

EDCS-10101977.text_edition: Domino nostro nobilissimo Caesari Galerio o
EDCS-10101977.translation: To our lord, the most noble Caesar Galerius.
EDCS-10101977.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Galerius as a noble Caesar, likely set up during his tetrarchic rule before he became Augustus (c. 293-305 CE).

EDCS-10101979.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Claudio Tacito Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-10101979.translation: To Imperator Caesar Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-10101979.summary: This inscription honors the Emperor Tacitus, recognizing his full imperial titles during his brief reign (275-276 CE).

EDCS-10101981.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Numerianus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-10101981.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Numerian as a noble Caesar, likely erected before his accession as Augustus (c. 282-283 CE).

EDCS-10101982.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul
EDCS-10101982.translation: Imperator Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the third time, consul.
EDCS-10101982.summary: This inscription details the full titulature and offices held by Emperor Maximinus Thrax, indicating a date early in his reign (likely late 236 CE).

EDCS-10101983.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobiElissimus#nobilissimus#NOBELISSIMUS Caesar
EDCS-10101983.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the third time, consul, father of the country; and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-10101983.summary: This inscription records the full titles of the Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely dating to 236 or 237 AD.

EDCS-10101987.translation: To the most noble Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantius.
EDCS-10101987.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantius Chlorus as Caesar, likely before his elevation to Augustus in 305 AD.

EDCS-10101988.text_edition: Impperatoribus ddominis nnostris Publio Licinio Valeriano et Publio Licinio Gallieno Piis Felicibus Auggustis ppontificibus mmaximis tribunicia potestate a Tilurio milia passuum
EDCS-10101988.translation: To the Emperors, our lords Publius Licinius Valerianus and Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate Augusti, pontifices maximi, holding tribunician power, from Tilurium, XXXXX miles.
EDCS-10101988.summary: This inscription, likely part of a milestone, dedicates to the co-emperors Valerian and Gallienus, providing their titles and a distance from Tilurium, probably dating to between 253 and 260 AD.

EDCS-10101992.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Diocletiano et Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Maximiano PPiis FFelicibus perpetuis princincpibus
EDCS-10101992.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus and Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate, perpetual princes.
EDCS-10101992.summary: This inscription honors the co-emperors Diocletian and Maximian, reflecting the early period of their joint reign, likely between 286 and 293 AD.

EDCS-10101995.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus tribunicia pontifex maximus potestas pater patriae imperator III consul II Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar
EDCS-10101995.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, father of the country, imperator for the third time, consul for the second time; Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-10101995.summary: This inscription lists the imperial titles of Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus, likely dating to 237 AD given Maximinus' second consulate.

EDCS-10102004.summary: This inscription provides the name and titles of Emperor Claudius Gothicus, likely dating to his brief reign between 268 and 270 AD.

EDCS-10102007.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII consul IIII pater patriae censor XXXXIIII
EDCS-10102007.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor for the forty-fourth time.
EDCS-10102007.summary: This inscription details the extensive titles of Emperor Claudius, likely dating to 47 or 48 AD based on his seventh tribunician power and fourth consulate.

EDCS-10102118.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Aurelius Salvianus |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae C II A ANNSVS
EDCS-10102118.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Aurelius Salvianus, centurion of the Thirteenth Gemina legion, [with illegible text].
EDCS-10102118.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aurelius Salvianus, a centurion of Legio XIII Gemina, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local genius, indicating a religious offering.

EDCS-10701891.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Saturna Caesio Castrensi marito suo fecit qui vixit annis XXXX
EDCS-10701891.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Saturna made this for her husband Caesius Castrensis, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-10701891.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Saturna for her husband, Caesius Castrensis, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-10701893.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Dometia sic vixit annos XVIII Aurelii Domitius et Tata filiae p h s s f
EDCS-10701893.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelia Dometia lived for 18 years. Aurelii Domitius and Tata made this for their daughter, she lies here.
EDCS-10701893.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aurelii Domitius and Tata for their daughter, Aurelia Dometia, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-12000832.text_edition: Annaea Decimi filia Maxima Luci Saeni Luculli Iunoni ob honorem
EDCS-12000832.translation: Annaea Maxima, daughter of Decimus, to Juno, on behalf of Lucius Saenius Lucullus, in honor of her office.
EDCS-12000832.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the goddess Juno, made by Annaea Maxima on behalf of Lucius Saenius Lucullus, likely for an honorific office held by Annaea.

EDCS-12000833.translation: Catena Tertulla, a gift to Venus.
EDCS-12000833.summary: This is a brief dedication from Catena Tertulla, offering a gift to the goddess Venus.

EDCS-12000834.text_edition: Fortunae sacrum Caius Agrius Fortunatus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-12000834.translation: Sacred to Fortuna, Gaius Agrius Fortunatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12000834.summary: This inscription records Gaius Agrius Fortunatus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-12000835.text_edition: Castori et Polluci sacrum Quintus Lusius Secundus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-12000835.translation: Sacred to Castor and Pollux, Quintus Lusius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12000835.summary: This inscription commemorates Quintus Lusius Secundus fulfilling a vow to the divine twins Castor and Pollux.

EDCS-12000836.text_edition: Veneri Augustae sacrum in memoriam Septimiae Lupulae ex testamento Septimiae Ursinae matris detracta XX ex libris LXXXXV semis Publius Umbrius Saturninus heres posuit
EDCS-12000836.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta, in memory of Septimia Lupula, in accordance with the will of Septimia Ursina, her mother, after deducting 20 (units) from 95.5 books, Publius Umbrius Saturninus, as heir, set this up.
EDCS-12000836.summary: This inscription is a commemorative monument erected by Publius Umbrius Saturninus as heir to Venus Augusta, in memory of Septimia Lupula, funded by her mother Septimia Ursina's will with a specific financial allocation.

EDCS-12000838.text_edition: Dis Manibus Turranio Clementi marito defuncto annorum LII Laelia Quinta Turranio Clementiano filio karo infelicissimo defuncto nnum XXXII multa peragrasti Germaniam terram secutus hic iacet erepta pietas pulchraque iuventus et sibi et suis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-12000838.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: to Turranius Clemens, her deceased husband, 52 years old; Laelia Quinta to Turranius Clementianus, her dearest, most unfortunate deceased son, 32 years old. You traveled much, following the land of Germania; here lies piety snatched away and beautiful youth, both for himself and for his own. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12000838.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Laelia Quinta to her deceased husband, Turranius Clemens, and her son, Turranius Clementianus, highlighting their tragic deaths and specifying that the tomb is not inheritable.

EDCS-12000839.text_edition: Aemilius Messor et Aelia Vitalis parentes et Aemilius Marcus frater piissimi et  posuerunt hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur sub ascia dederunt dedicaveruntque
EDCS-12000839.translation: Aemilius Messor and Aelia Vitalis, the most devoted parents, and Aemilius Marcus, his brother, erected this monument. This monument will not pass to an heir. They dedicated and consecrated it under the adze.
EDCS-12000839.summary: This inscription marks a funerary monument erected by Aemilius Messor, Aelia Vitalis, and Aemilius Marcus, dedicated with specific legal provisions regarding inheritance.

EDCS-12000840.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Ulpio Silvino Publi filio
EDCS-12000840.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Marcus Ulpius Silvinus, son of Publius.
EDCS-12000840.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Marcus Ulpius Silvinus, son of Publius.

EDCS-12300218.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300218.translation: Imperator Caesar Vespasian Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the sixth time, father of the country, consul for the third time, designated for the fourth, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the veterans who served in the Ravenna fleet under Sextus Lucilius Bassus, who earned twenty-six or more stipends and were discharged to Pannonia, whose names are written below. He granted citizenship to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they had when citizenship was granted to them, or, if any were bachelors, with those they might marry afterward, provided only one wife for each. On the Nones of April, during the consulship of Caesar Domitian, son of Augustus, and Gnaeus Pedius Cascus. To Plator, son of Venetus, centurion of the Maezei tribe. Transcribed and verified from the bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitol, near the altar of the Gens Iulia, on the outer side, on the left podium, tablet 1, page 2, location 44. (Repeated in a second copy with minor variations, then followed by witnesses' names): Of Titus Iulius Rufus, a Roman eques from Salona; Publius Vibius Maximus, a Roman eques from Epidaurus; Titus Fanius Celer, a decurion from Iader; Gaius Marcius Proculus, a decurion from Iader; Publius Caetennius Clemens from Salona; Publius Lurius Moderatus from Risinium; Quintus Poblicius Crescens from Iader.
EDCS-12300218.summary: This inscription is a military diploma issued by Emperor Vespasian in 71 AD, granting Roman citizenship and conubium to discharged veterans of the Ravenna fleet settled in Pannonia, with specific details of its public display and the names of attesting witnesses.

EDCS-12300242.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300242.text_edition: Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XII imperator XXII censor perpetuus consul XVI pater patriae peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohorte III Alpinorum et in VIII Voluntariorum civium Romanorum qui peregrinae condicionis probati erant et sunt in DaElmatia#Dalmatia#DELMATIA sub Quinto Pomponio Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerunt item dimisso honesta missione emeritis stipendiis quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem  dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem III Idus Iulias Marco Lollio Paullino Valerio Asiatico Saturnino Caio Antio Iulio Quadrato consulibus cohortis III Alpinorum cui praeest Caius Vibius Maximus pediti Veneto Diti filio Daverso et Madenae Plarentis filiae uxori eius Deramistae et Gaio filio eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae  Imperator Caesar divi Vespasiani filius Domitianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XII imperator XXII consul XVI censor perpetuus pater patriae peditibus et equitibus qui militant in cohorte III Alpinorum et in VIII Voluntariorum civium Romanorum qui peregrinae condicionis probati erant et sunt in DaElmatia#Dalmatia#DELMATIA sub Quinto Pomponio Rufo qui quina et vicena stipendia aut plura meruerunt item dimisso honesta missione emeritis stipendiis quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem III Idus Iulias Marco Lollio Paullino Valerio Asiatico Saturnino Caio Antio Iulio Quadrato consulibus cohortis III Alpinorum cui praeest Caius Vibius Maximus pediti Veneto Diti filio Daverso et Madenae Plarentis filiae uxori eius Deramistae et Gaio filio eius descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam  Auli Volumni Expectati Quinti Orfi Cupiti Cnaei Egnati Vitalis Sexti Manli Cinnami Luci Pulli Sperati Publi Atini Amermni Luci Pulli Verecundi
EDCS-12300242.translation: Imperator Caesar Domitian Augustus Germanicus, son of divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the twelfth time, imperator for the twenty-second time, perpetual censor, consul for the sixteenth time, father of the country, granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the foot soldiers and cavalrymen who served in the Third Cohort of Alpinorum and the Eighth Cohort of Roman Citizen Volunteers, who were of foreign condition and attested, and are in Dalmatia under Quintus Pomponius Rufus, who earned twenty-five or more stipends, as well as those honorably discharged after completing their stipends, whose names are written below. He granted citizenship to them, their children, and their descendants, and the right of marriage with the wives they had when citizenship was granted to them, or, if any were bachelors, with those they might marry afterward, provided only one wife for each. Three days before the Ides of July, during the consulship of Marcus Lollius Paullinus Valerius Asiaticus Saturninus and Gaius Antius Iulius Quadratus. To Daversus, a foot soldier of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum, commanded by Gaius Vibius Maximus, a Venetan, son of Ditio; and to Madena, daughter of Plarens, his wife from Deramista; and to Gaius, his son. Transcribed and verified from the bronze tablet fixed in Rome (repeatedly described as fixed in Rome on the wall behind the temple of divine Augustus, near Minerva). (Followed by witnesses' names): Of Aulus Volumnius Expectatus; Quintus Orfius Cupitus; Gnaeus Egnatius Vitalis; Sextus Manlius Cinnamus; Lucius Pullius Speratus; Publius Atinius Amermnus; Lucius Pullius Verecundus.
EDCS-12300242.summary: This inscription is a military diploma issued by Emperor Domitian in 92 AD, granting Roman citizenship and conubium to discharged foreign soldiers from two cohorts serving in Dalmatia, specifically detailing the recipient's family and the location of the original bronze tablet in Rome, along with the names of the witnesses.

EDCS-13300216.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Nonio Euticho sic Oristilla sic soror
EDCS-13300216.translation: To the Divine Manes of Lucius Nonius Eutychus. So Oristilla, so his sister.
EDCS-13300216.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Nonius Eutychus by Oristilla and his sister, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-13302687.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Graecus qui et Fortunatus Marci libertus libens posuit
EDCS-13302687.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Graecus, also known as Fortunatus, freedman of Marcus, willingly set this up.
EDCS-13302687.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Graecus Fortunatus, a freedman of Marcus, expressing gratitude or fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-14800164.text_edition: Veneri Augustae sacrum in memoriam Vibiae Proculae ex testamento Flaviae Tertullinae matris de libris C Flavii Severus et Pudentilla posuerunt
EDCS-14800164.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta, in memory of Vibia Procula, according to the will of Flavia Tertullina, her mother. From the funds of Gaius Flavius Severus and Pudentilla, they placed this.
EDCS-14800164.summary: This commemorative inscription is dedicated to Venus Augusta in memory of Vibia Procula, funded by her mother's will and erected by Gaius Flavius Severus and Pudentilla.

EDCS-14800165.text_edition: Divo Augusto sacrum Publius Dolabella consul Caesaris Augusti legatus pro praetore
EDCS-14800165.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Publius Dolabella, consul, legate of Caesar Augustus with propraetorian power.
EDCS-14800165.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Publius Dolabella, a consul and imperial legate, likely marking a public monument or act of veneration during the early Imperial period.

EDCS-14800166.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Gemino iuveni infelicissimo defuncto Roma VC F AC Iulia et Gemella sorores fratri pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-14800166.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Geminus, a most unfortunate young man, who died in Rome. Iulia and Gemella, his sisters, placed this for their most devoted brother.
EDCS-14800166.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Geminus, a young man who died in Rome, erected by his grieving sisters, Iulia and Gemella.

EDCS-14800167.text_edition: Publius Quintilius Fortunatus Publius Quintilius Valens filius et Quinta filia fecerunt
EDCS-14800167.translation: Publius Quintilius Fortunatus, Publius Quintilius Valens, his son, and Quinta, his daughter, made this.
EDCS-14800167.summary: This inscription records the dedication or creation of a monument by Publius Quintilius Fortunatus, his son Publius Quintilius Valens, and his daughter Quinta.

EDCS-14800168.text_edition: Stenius Fortis Lupulae collibertae bene merenti
EDCS-14800168.translation: Stenius Fortis to Lupula, his fellow freedwoman, well-deserving.
EDCS-14800168.summary: This is likely a funerary or commemorative inscription dedicated by Stenius Fortis to his deserving fellow freedwoman, Lupula.

EDCS-14800169.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sempronio Valentioni Valeria Victorina
EDCS-14800169.translation: To the Divine Manes of Sempronius Valentio. Valeria Victorina [set this up].
EDCS-14800169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sempronius Valentio, erected by Valeria Victorina.

EDCS-14800170.text_edition: Dis Manibus Agrio Dicaeo patri bene merenti et Agrio Maximo fratri infelicissimo defuncto annorum XIIII Vinicia Plotia
EDCS-14800170.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Agrius Dicaeos, his well-deserving father, and to Agrius Maximus, his most unfortunate brother, who died at 14 years of age. Vinicia Plotia [set this up].
EDCS-14800170.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Vinicia Plotia dedicated to her deserving father, Agrius Dicaeos, and her unfortunate brother, Agrius Maximus, who died young.

EDCS-14800171.text_edition: Lucius Ancharenus Quinti filius Sergia Laranda miles legionis VII nnorum XL stipendiorum XXI hic situs est Quintus Ancharenus haec tan posuit tibi dona frater quia longe a patria te cecidisse dolet optaram vivo potius dare vina  non obito care ferenda tuo si quicquam sentire potes mpore nostro damus haec o sit tibi terra levis frater fratri posuit
EDCS-14800171.translation: Lucius Ancharenus, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe, Laranda, a soldier of the VII legion, 40 years old, 21 years of service, lies here. Quintus Ancharenus placed these gifts for you, brother, because he grieves that you fell far from your homeland. I would have rather given wine to you alive, not to be carried to your untimely death. If you can feel anything in our time, we give these things, oh, may the earth be light for you. Brother placed this for his brother.
EDCS-14800171.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription for Lucius Ancharenus, a soldier of the VII Legion who died abroad, erected by his grieving brother, Quintus Ancharenus, around the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-14800172.text_edition: Lucius Cusius Luci libertus Ascanius annorum XX hic situs est patronus posuit bene merito
EDCS-14800172.translation: Lucius Cusius Ascanius, freedman of Lucius, aged 20, is buried here. His patron placed this for his well-deserving freedman.
EDCS-14800172.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of a freedman named Lucius Cusius Ascanius, set up by his former master, Lucius Cusius.

EDCS-15100101.text_edition: conservatrici  potenti  Calpurnia Luci Pisonis auguris filia Cnaei Pisonis neptis donum dedit
EDCS-15100101.translation: To the powerful preserver, Calpurnia, daughter of Lucius Piso the augur, granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, gave this gift.
EDCS-15100101.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Calpurnia, a woman of notable lineage, offering a gift to a preserving deity.

EDCS-15700109.text_edition: Veneri Ansoticae sacrum Baebia Cai filia Maximilla ex testamento Luci Corneli Bassi fecit
EDCS-15700109.translation: Sacred to Venus Ansotica, Baebia Maximilla, daughter of Gaius, made this in accordance with the will of Lucius Cornelius Bassus.
EDCS-15700109.summary: This is a votive inscription to Venus Ansotica, erected by Baebia Maximilla, fulfilling the last will of Lucius Cornelius Bassus.

EDCS-15900045.text_edition: Titus Appuleius Titi Luci libertus Antigonius Anzoticae votum solvit
EDCS-15900045.translation: Titus Appuleius Antigonius, freedman of Titus Lucius, gladly fulfilled his vow to Anzotica.
EDCS-15900045.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Appuleius Antigonius, a freedman, in fulfillment of a vow to the deity Anzotica.

EDCS-16000504.text_edition: Liberai sic Iulia Maximina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-16000504.translation: To Libera, Julia Maximina gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-16000504.summary: This is a votive inscription by Julia Maximina, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Libera.

EDCS-16100255.text_edition: Caius Utius Spuri filius testamento fieri iussit sibi et Publio Utio fratri suo et Clodiae Faustae concubinae suae multa peragratus ego terraque marique debitum reddidi in patria nunc situs hic iaceo stat lapis et nomen vestigia nulla
EDCS-16100255.translation: Gaius Utius, son of Spurius, ordered this to be made by his will for himself, and for his brother Publius Utius, and for his concubine Clodia Faustus. Having traveled much by land and sea, I have paid my debt; now I lie buried here in my homeland. A stone stands, and my name, but no other traces.
EDCS-16100255.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, for Gaius Utius, his brother, and his concubine, containing a poetic reflection on his life and travels.

EDCS-16200948.translation: To Julius P., wool merchant, Eutyches [dedicated this].
EDCS-16200948.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Eutyches to Julius P., who was a wool merchant, likely in a funerary context or as a mark of respect.

EDCS-16900021.text_edition: Quintus Marcius Quinti filius Cornelia Bassus domo Benevento miles legionis VII annorum  stipendiorum XVII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit ex arbitratu Maximi
EDCS-16900021.translation: Quintus Marcius Bassus, son of Quintus, from Beneventum, soldier of the Seventh Legion, aged [missing], of 17 years' service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will, according to the judgment of Maximus.
EDCS-16900021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Marcius Bassus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, buried at his own command via his will, with Maximus overseeing the arrangements.

EDCS-16900065.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelio Messoriano filio infelicissimo ex collegio Veneris defunctus Aterno qui vixit annos XXXV Aelius Anianus et Aurelia Prima obsequentissimo titulum posuerunt
EDCS-16900065.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aelius Messorianus, most unfortunate son, deceased from the college of Venus, at Aternum, who lived 35 years. Aelius Anianus and Aurelia Prima placed this monument for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-16900065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Messorianus, a son who died at age 35 and was a member of the 'college of Venus', placed by his parents Aelius Anianus and Aurelia Prima.

EDCS-17000067.text_edition: Iovi Fulminatori Titus Aurelius Quintus  posuit
EDCS-17000067.translation: To Jupiter the Thunderer, Titus Aurelius Quintus placed this.
EDCS-17000067.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Fulminator (Jupiter the Thunderer) by Titus Aurelius Quintus.

EDCS-20401672.text_edition: Sexto Servilio Sexti filio Titiano infelici defuncto nnum XVIII mensium III dierum IIII Aelia Maxima infelicissima mater sibi et filio piissimo posuit
EDCS-20401672.translation: To Sextus Servilius Titianus, son of Sextus, unhappy, deceased at 18 years, 3 months, 4 days. Aelia Maxima, his most unhappy mother, set this up for herself and her most dutiful son.
EDCS-20401672.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelia Maxima for her deceased son, Sextus Servilius Titianus, and for herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20401678.text_edition: Stenio Hermeti marito bene merenti Charite coniunx
EDCS-20401678.translation: To Stenius Hermes, her well-deserving husband, his wife Charite [set this up].
EDCS-20401678.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Charite for her husband, Stenius Hermes, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601174.text_edition: Caius Licinius Cai filius Fabia domo Sinope veteranus legionis VII annorum L stipendiorum XXVII hic situs est heredes posuerunt
EDCS-20601174.translation: Gaius Licinius, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, from Sinope, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, 50 years old, with 27 years of service, lies here. His heirs set this up.
EDCS-20601174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman veteran, Gaius Licinius, erected by his heirs, commemorating his military service and place of origin, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601175.text_edition: Lucius Luc Cai filius Primus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris hic situs est  testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-20601175.translation: Lucius Luc[ius], son of Caius, Primus, a veteran of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, lies here. He ordered it to be made by will.
EDCS-20601175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Luc[ius] Primus, a veteran, commissioned according to his will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601176.text_edition: elius Ma P domo R veteranus legionis VII  stipendiorum XXVIII  annos LXII filio et Patrono V posuit matri
EDCS-20601176.translation: Ma[rcus] P[---]lius, from R[---], a veteran of the Seventh Legion, with 28 years of service, 62 years old, set this up for his son and patronus V[---], and for his mother.
EDCS-20601176.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a veteran, Ma[rcus] P[---]lius, dedicated to his son, his patron, and his mother, indicating familial and patronage relationships, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601177.text_edition: AGA eques cohortis I Belgarum armorum custos tur(ma) Maximi annorum?  stipendiorum XXIII
EDCS-20601177.translation: Aga, cavalryman of the First Cohort of Belgae, armorer, of the troop of Maximus, [---] years old, with 23 years of service.
EDCS-20601177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aga, a cavalryman and armorer in a Roman auxiliary cohort, detailing his military role and length of service, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601178.text_edition: Tures Alburi filius eques cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum nnorum XXV stipendiorum VII hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-20601178.translation: Tures, son of Alburus, cavalryman of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, 25 years old, with 7 years of service, lies here. His heir set this up.
EDCS-20601178.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tures, an auxiliary cavalryman, erected by his heir, recording his age and years of service, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-20601179.text_edition: Veranus Caturonis filius eques cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum turma Tironis nnnorum XXXIIX stipendiorum XVIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heredes posuerunt Tiro decurio et Crispus Caturonis
EDCS-20601179.translation: Veranus, son of Caturus, cavalryman of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, of the troop of Tiro, 38 years old, with 18 years of service, lies here. He ordered it to be made by will. His heirs, Tiro the decurion and Crispus, son of Caturus, set this up.
EDCS-20601179.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Veranus, an auxiliary cavalryman, made according to his will and erected by his heirs, including his decurion and a relative, highlighting his military unit and family connections, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-23801439.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating a dedication or memorial made by Stratonice for a woman whose name begins with 'ronia', likely a private dedication.

EDCS-24200354.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae aedem cognatio fecit ex nummis conlatis solo suo
EDCS-24200354.translation: The family made this temple for the Great Mother of the Gods from collected funds on their own land.
EDCS-24200354.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple dedicated to the Magna Mater by a family, funded by contributions and built on their own property, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-24200355.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Maximae Publius Bennius Priscianus miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum tibicem sic consularis hospitae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-24200355.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Valeria Maxima. Publius Bennius Priscianus, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, a trumpeter, placed this for his well-deserving hostess.
EDCS-24200355.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a soldier of a volunteer cohort, Publius Bennius Priscianus, dedicating a monument to his hostess, Valeria Maxima, who was a tibicen (flute player or trumpeter).

EDCS-24200356.text_edition: Sexto Alvio sic Procusio annorum L Aufidia Maxima coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-24200356.translation: To Sextus Alvius Procusius, aged 50. Aufidia Maxima placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-24200356.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aufidia Maxima to her husband, Sextus Alvius Procusius, who lived to be 50 years old.

EDCS-24200357.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lusidenius Glicerus sic delicato suo bene merenti posuit Lusidenius Glicerus sic fecit Secundae filiae suae
EDCS-24200357.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lusidenius Glicerus placed this for his well-deserving darling. Lusidenius Glicerus made this for his daughter, Secunda.
EDCS-24200357.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly a double dedication, where Lusidenius Glicerus honors both an unnamed 'darling' and his daughter, Secunda.

EDCS-24200358.translation: Aemilia Aprulla [placed this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-24200358.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aemilia Aprulla to her husband, acknowledging his merit.

EDCS-24200359.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Fabiae Vrsae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit nnos XXXVII Basileus maritus posuit
EDCS-24200359.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Fabia Ursa, an incomparable wife, who lived 37 years. Her husband Basileus placed this.
EDCS-24200359.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Basileus to his incomparable wife, Fabia Ursa, who died at the age of 37.

EDCS-24200360.text_edition: Lucio Statio T IIIIIIviro Lucius Statius Luci libertus
EDCS-24200360.translation: To Lucius Statius, sevir. Lucius Statius, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-24200360.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Statius, a sevir (member of a board of six), made by his freedman, also named Lucius Statius.

EDCS-24200362.translation: Quintus Cominius Martialis placed this for his wife.
EDCS-24200362.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Quintus Cominius Martialis dedicates a monument to his wife.

EDCS-24200364.text_edition: Dis Manibus infelicissimo infanti Valerio  Maximiano qui  vixit annis II mensibus VI diebus XXVII VLVS Valerius Veer filio
EDCS-24200364.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the most unfortunate infant Valerius Maximianus, who lived 2 years, 6 months, 27 days. Valerius Veer [placed this] for his son.
EDCS-24200364.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated by Valerius Veer to his infant son, Valerius Maximianus, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-24200370.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Pomponio Barbario  Respectus amico
EDCS-24200370.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lucius Pomponius Barbarius. Respectus [placed this] for his friend.
EDCS-24200370.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Respectus to his friend, Lucius Pomponius Barbarius.

EDCS-24200371.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Titio Priscino aedili IIviro annorum XXXIII mensium VII dierum VIII Laetilia Fructa mater filio pientissimo fecit
EDCS-24200371.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Titius Priscinus, aedile, duumvir, aged 33 years, 7 months, 8 days. Laetilia Fructa, his mother, made this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-24200371.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Laetilia Fructa, to her son, Gaius Titius Priscinus, who held public offices as an aedile and duumvir and died at the age of 33.

EDCS-24400333.text_edition: Caius Fulvius Cai filius Voltinia Philippis miles legionis XI nnum XXX stipendiorum X
EDCS-24400333.translation: Gaius Fulvius, son of Gaius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Philippi, soldier of the Eleventh Legion, 30 years old, with 10 years of service.
EDCS-24400333.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, Gaius Fulvius, detailing his age and years of service, likely dating from the 1st century AD.

EDCS-24400345.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Voltinia domo Philippis miles legionis VII nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XV hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-24400345.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Philippi, soldier of the Seventh Legion, 35 years old, with 15 years of service. He is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-24400345.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, which was erected according to his will, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-24400842.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Magnio Victori Lucius Cluentius  Cornelianus conservo
EDCS-24400842.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Lucius Magnius Victor. Lucius Cluentius Cornelianus, to his fellow slave.
EDCS-24400842.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Magnius Victor by his fellow slave, Lucius Cluentius Cornelianus, suggesting a context of shared servitude, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-24600956.text_edition: Aureliae Messiae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XXXV Signius Lupus maritus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-24600956.translation: To Aurelia Messia, most unhappy, who lived for 35 years. Signius Lupus, her husband, placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-24600956.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Signius Lupus for his deceased wife, Aurelia Messia, expressing his sorrow and her deserving nature, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-24900088.text_edition: Quintus Vibius Luci filius Dianae votum solvit eisdem aram de suo faciendum curavit
EDCS-24900088.translation: Quintus Vibius, son of Lucius, fulfilled a vow to Diana and took care of having an altar made at his own expense for the same goddesses.
EDCS-24900088.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Quintus Vibius, son of Lucius, fulfilling a vow to Diana and funding the construction of an altar, likely from the 1st century BC or 1st century AD.

EDCS-24900089.text_edition: Publius Annaeus Quinti libertus Epicadus aedem Leiberi Patrus faciundam coiravit lubens merito
EDCS-24900089.translation: Publius Annaeus Epicadus, freedman of Quintus, willingly and deservedly took care of having a temple for Liber Pater built.
EDCS-24900089.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the construction of a temple to Liber Pater, undertaken by Publius Annaeus Epicadus, a freedman, likely from the 1st century BC or 1st century AD.

EDCS-24900091.text_edition: Quintus Safinius Quinti filius Sextus Marius Luci libertus magistri Naronae Quintus Marcius Quinti filius Publius Annaeus Quinti libertus  Epicadus  quaestores turrim faciendam coiraverunt
EDCS-24900091.translation: Quintus Safinius, son of Quintus, and Sextus Marius, freedman of Lucius, as magistrates of Narona; Quintus Marcius, son of Quintus, and Publius Annaeus Epicadus, freedman of Quintus, as quaestors, took care of having a tower built.
EDCS-24900091.summary: This is a public inscription recording the construction of a tower by local magistrates and quaestors in Narona, likely dating to the Augustan or early Imperial period.

EDCS-24900093.text_edition: Lucius Pontius Cnaei filius Mircurio dedit  Λεύκιος Πόντιος Γναίου υἱὸς Ἑρμᾶι δῶρον
EDCS-24900093.translation: Lucius Pontius, son of Gnaeus, gave this to Mercury. (Greek:) Leúkios Póntios Gnaíou huiós Hermãi dôron (Lucius Pontius, son of Gnaeus, a gift to Hermes).
EDCS-24900093.summary: This is a bilingual dedicatory inscription to Mercury (Hermes) by Lucius Pontius, son of Gnaeus, offering a gift, likely from the 1st century BC or 1st century AD.

EDCS-24900124.text_edition: Turus Patalius Granp Opiavi filius Venetus Lastimeis Hospolis filius praetores murum locaverunt longum pedes CXI altum pedes XX eisdem probaverunt
EDCS-24900124.translation: Turus Patalius, son of Granp Opiavus, and Venetus Lastimeis, son of Hospolis, as praetors, contracted for a wall, 111 feet long and 20 feet high, and approved it themselves.
EDCS-24900124.summary: This is a public inscription recording the construction and approval of a wall, specifying its dimensions, by two praetors, likely from the 1st century BC or 1st century AD.

EDCS-25002168.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Sicilia recepta Caius Papius Celsus Marcus Papius Kanus fratres
EDCS-25002168.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of the Divine, for the recovery of Sicily. Gaius Papius Celsus and Marcus Papius Canus, brothers.
EDCS-25002168.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by two brothers, Gaius Papius Celsus and Marcus Papius Canus, honoring Imperator Caesar (likely Octavian/Augustus) for the recovery of Sicily, specifically dating to after 36 BC.

EDCS-26001142.translation: Lucius Caecilius [built] the wall of the tower.
EDCS-26001142.summary: This inscription records the construction of a tower wall by Lucius Caecilius.

EDCS-26600638.translation: Aedile of X years. Curtia of X years.
EDCS-26600638.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly for an aedile and a woman named Curtia, with their ages indicated.

EDCS-26600639.text_edition: Dis Manibus  bene merenti Festio sic patri qui vixit annos CX Conse sic filia posuit
EDCS-26600639.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To the well-deserving Festio, his father, who lived 110 years, Conse his daughter thus placed this.
EDCS-26600639.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Conse for her father Festio, who lived to an impressive age.

EDCS-26600640.text_edition: [] Cai liberta Fortunata annorum hic sita est XIIX si pietas prodest cQuiquam#cuiquam#QUIQUAM vixisse modeste vos precor o Manes sit mihi terra levis libertas cui olim fuerat promissa est ante ditis sub fatum venit in arbitrium vivite felices quibus  est Fortuna superstes spenque sic meam oppressit fatus in Hiluricum sic
EDCS-26600640.translation: [Here] lies Cai's freedwoman Fortunata, 18 years old. If piety profits anyone to have lived modestly, I beg you, O Spirits, may the earth be light for me, whose freedom was once promised before fate came to the power of the gods. Live happily, for whom Fortune is still alive, and thus fate oppressed my hope in Illyricum.
EDCS-26600640.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fortunata, a freedwoman who died at 18, expressing a wish for light earth and reflecting on the brevity of her promised freedom and the role of fate.

EDCS-26600641.text_edition: Lusia Gemellina filia Lusio Gemellino patri pio fecit
EDCS-26600641.translation: Lusia Gemellina, daughter, made this for Lusius Gemellinus, her devoted father.
EDCS-26600641.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lusia Gemellina for her pious father, Lusius Gemellinus.

EDCS-26600642.text_edition: Haline   nnorum XV Sextus Lusius   Leonas   coniugi
EDCS-26600642.translation: Haline, 15 years old. Sextus Lusius Leonas [made this] for his wife.
EDCS-26600642.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sextus Lusius Leonas for his wife Haline, who died at age 15.

EDCS-26600643.text_edition: Iulia Rufina mater infelicGissimae#infelicissimae#INFELIGISSIME filiae Viviae Valeriae quae vixit annos duos menses quattuor dies novem
EDCS-26600643.translation: Julia Rufina, most unfortunate mother, for her daughter Vivia Valeria, who lived two years, four months, nine days.
EDCS-26600643.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from Julia Rufina, a grieving mother, for her very young daughter Vivia Valeria.

EDCS-26600644.text_edition: ] Marciae Maritimae Nicephorus Aprilis Augusti liberti servus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-26600644.translation: [For] Marcia Maritima. Nicephorus Aprilis, freedman of Augustus, servant, [made this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600644.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Nicephorus Aprilis, a freedman and servant of Augustus, for his deserving wife, Marcia Maritima.

EDCS-26600645.summary: This is a simple nominative inscription likely identifying Domatia Prima, daughter of Spurius, possibly from a funerary monument or statue base.

EDCS-26600646.text_edition: Lucius Pomponius Spuri filius Longinus ex testamento situs
EDCS-26600646.translation: Lucius Pomponius Longinus, son of Spurius, buried according to his will.
EDCS-26600646.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Pomponius Longinus, son of Spurius, in accordance with his last will.

EDCS-26600647.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Primus Phileroti filio bene merenti
EDCS-26600647.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Primus [made this] for Phileros, his well-deserving son.
EDCS-26600647.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Primus dedicating a monument to his son, Phileros, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-26600648.text_edition: Satrius Secundus Primitivae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit nnos XVII menses VII dies X
EDCS-26600648.translation: Satrius Secundus [made this] for Primitiva, his most devoted wife, who lived 17 years, 7 months, 10 days.
EDCS-26600648.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Satrius Secundus for his wife, Primitiva, detailing her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600649.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Saturio coniugi Titiae Titi filiae
EDCS-26600649.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Saturio [made this] for Titia, daughter of Titus, his wife.
EDCS-26600649.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Saturio for his wife, Titia, daughter of Titus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600650.text_edition: Caius Amerinus Publi libertus Latinus situs
EDCS-26600650.translation: Gaius Amerinus Latinus, freedman of Publius, lies here.
EDCS-26600650.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Amerinus Latinus, a freedman of Publius, indicating his place of burial, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-26600652.text_edition: Augusto sacrum Caius Iulius Macrini libertus Martialis IIIIIIvir magister Mercurialis ob honorem idem ludos scaenicos per triduum dedit et cantharum argenteum pondo |Sunciarum septem
EDCS-26600652.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Gaius Iulius Martialis, freedman of Macrinus, a sevir (member of the college of six) and master of the Mercuriales, in honor of his office, likewise gave theatrical games for three days and [donated] a silver cantharus weighing seven ounces.
EDCS-26600652.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Gaius Iulius Martialis, a freedman and a sevir Augustalis, recording his benefactions (games and a silver vessel) in honor of his office, dedicated to Augustus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-26600654.text_edition: Divo Augusto sacrum Quintus Sextilius Corinthus Caius Stertinius Synegdemus Lucius Vibius Amaranthus Lucius Aquillius Aptus Lucius Titusidius Chryseros Caius Valerius Herma IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem
EDCS-26600654.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Quintus Sextilius Corinthus, Gaius Stertinius Synegdemus, Lucius Vibius Amaranthus, Lucius Aquillius Aptus, Lucius Titusidius Chryseros, Gaius Valerius Herma, seviri and masters of the Mercuriales, in honor of their office.
EDCS-26600654.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by six seviri Augustales and masters of the Mercuriales, made in honor of their office and dedicated to the Divine Augustus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-26600656.text_edition: Dianae Nemorensi sacrum Tiberius Claudius Claudianus praefectus cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum ex voto suscepto de suo
EDCS-26600656.translation: Sacred to Diana Nemorensis. Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, prefect of the First Cohort of Bracaraugustani, [made this] from his own funds in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600656.summary: This is a religious dedication by Tiberius Claudius Claudianus, a prefect, to Diana Nemorensis, made in fulfillment of a vow and at his own expense, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600657.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Aeterno Titus Flavius Magnus ex voto
EDCS-26600657.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Aeternus. Titus Flavius Magnus [made this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600657.summary: This is a religious dedication by Titus Flavius Magnus to Jupiter Optimus Aeternus, made in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the later Imperial period.

EDCS-26600658.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  pro salute Impperatorum Severi et Antonini Auggustorum et Getae nobilissimi Caesaris et Iuliae Augustae matris Auggustorum et castrorum Gaius Statius Tacitianus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens merito Pompeiano et Avito consulibus
EDCS-26600658.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the well-being of the Emperors Severus and Antoninus Augusti, and Geta Nobilissimus Caesar, and Julia Augusta, mother of the Augusti and of the camps. Gaius Statius Tacitianus, beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-26600658.summary: This is a religious dedication by Gaius Statius Tacitianus, a beneficiarius consularis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made for the well-being of the Imperial family (Severus, Caracalla, Geta, Julia Domna) and the army, precisely dated to 207 CE.

EDCS-26600659.summary: This is a short religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600660.text_edition: Deo sancto Iovi Optimo Maximo aeterno sacrum Claudius Marcus factus ex optione beneficiarius Senecionis consularis
EDCS-26600660.translation: Sacred to the holy, best, greatest, eternal god Jupiter. Claudius Marcus, having been made a beneficiarius (optio) of the consular Senecio.
EDCS-26600660.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Claudius Marcus, who was a beneficiarius under a consular named Senecio.

EDCS-26600661.text_edition: Quintus Pliseniius Severianus |(centurio) legionis XI Claudiae templum Liberi Patris vetustate corruptum porticibus adiectis restituit
EDCS-26600661.translation: Quintus Plisenius Severianus, centurion of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, restored the temple of Liber Pater, which had been ruined by age, adding porticoes.
EDCS-26600661.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple of Liber Pater, with added porticoes, by Quintus Plisenius Severianus, a centurion of the Legio XI Claudia.

EDCS-26600662.text_edition: Templum Liberi Patris et Liberae vetustate dilabsum sic restituit cohors I Belgarum adiectis porticibus curam agente Flavio Victore |(centurione) legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Severo et Pompeiano II consulibus
EDCS-26600662.translation: The temple of Liber Pater and Libera, which had fallen into ruin due to age, was thus restored by the First Cohort of Belgae, with added porticoes, under the care of Flavius Victor, centurion of the First Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, in the consulship of Severus and Pompeianus for the second time.
EDCS-26600662.summary: This inscription commemorates the restoration of a temple dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera by the Cohors I Belgarum, under the supervision of centurion Flavius Victor, during the second consulship of Severus and Pompeianus (241 CE).

EDCS-26600665.text_edition: Marcus Lusius Trofimas Marci libertus IIIIIIvir magister Mercurialis ob honorem
EDCS-26600665.translation: Marcus Lusius Trophimas, freedman of Marcus, quinquennalis and master of the Mercuriales, in honor.
EDCS-26600665.summary: This inscription records a dedication made in honor by Marcus Lusius Trophimas, a freedman and a quinquennalis (chief of the six seviri Augustales) who was also a master of the Mercuriales.

EDCS-26600666.text_edition: A(ulus) Obultronius Grat(us) Publius nnus Stamitus Lucius Battius  Lucius Safinius Quartus Caius Licinius Telamo seviri magistri Mercuriales aere accepto libentes a in solo privato aedem aidificaverunt de sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-26600666.translation: Aulus Obultronius Gratus, Publius [---]nnus Stamitus, Lucius Battius, Lucius Safinius Quartus, Gaius Licinius Telamo, seviri and masters of the Mercuriales, having received payment, willingly built a shrine on private land and saw to its construction with their own money.
EDCS-26600666.summary: This inscription details the construction of a shrine on private land, funded by a group of seviri and masters of the Mercuriales, including Aulus Obultronius Gratus, Publius [---]nnus Stamitus, Lucius Battius, Lucius Safinius Quartus, and Gaius Licinius Telamo.

EDCS-26600667.publication: CIL 03, 01802a = CIL 03, *00301ca = CINar-01, 00016a = CILGM p 108 = CINar-02, p 135
EDCS-26600667.summary: This short inscription records a dedication made in honor by Titus Haterius Hilarus.

EDCS-26600669.text_edition: Thermas rei publicae hiemales rogante populo in ruinam conlapsas Marcus Aurelius Valerius vir perfectissimus ducenarius ex protectoribus lateris divini de frugalitate sua item atque suorum saxis et caementis denuo aedificavit et lavantes rei publicae tradidit epulum quoque civibus suis ea die praebuit Messala et Grato consulibus dedicante Marco Aurelio Tiberiano viro perfectissimo praeside provinciae Delmatiae sic
EDCS-26600669.translation: The public winter baths, having collapsed into ruin, at the request of the people, Marcus Aurelius Valerius, a most perfect man, ducenarius ex protectoribus lateris divini, rebuilt them anew with his own and his family's frugality, using stones and cement, and handed them over to the public for bathing. He also provided a banquet for his citizens on that day, in the consulship of Messala and Gratus, with Marcus Aurelius Tiberianus, a most perfect man and governor of the province of Dalmatia, dedicating them thus.
EDCS-26600669.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of public winter baths by Marcus Aurelius Valerius, a high-ranking imperial official, funded by his own resources and including a banquet for citizens, dedicated by the provincial governor Marcus Aurelius Tiberianus during the consulship of Messala and Gratus (214 CE).

EDCS-26600670.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemilio Forti beneficiario cohortis VIII voluntariorum Lucius Aemilius Hermes IIIIIIvir et Aemilia Saturnina patrono bene merenti sibiE#sibi#SIBE et suis et posterisque aeorum sic
EDCS-26600670.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aemilius Fortis, beneficiarius of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers. Lucius Aemilius Hermes, quinquennalis, and Aemilia Saturnina, to their well-deserving patron, for themselves and their family and their descendants, thus.
EDCS-26600670.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aemilius Fortis, a beneficiarius of the Cohors VIII Voluntariorum, by Lucius Aemilius Hermes (a quinquennalis) and Aemilia Saturnina, for their patron and their own family's future remembrance.

EDCS-26600673.text_edition: Marcus Heredius Marci filius Palatina veteranus legionis VII hic situs est fecit Faustus libertus
EDCS-26600673.translation: Marcus Heredius, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe, veteran of the Seventh Legion, is buried here. Faustus, his freedman, made this.
EDCS-26600673.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Heredius, a veteran of the Legio VII, erected by his freedman, Faustus.

EDCS-26600674.text_edition: Cnaeus Magius Cnaei filius Palatina veteranus situs
EDCS-26600674.translation: Gnaeus Magius, son of Gnaeus, of the Palatine tribe, veteran, is buried.
EDCS-26600674.summary: This is a concise funerary inscription for Gnaeus Magius, a veteran of the Palatine tribe.

EDCS-26600675.text_edition: patrono iterum itenque!  sacerdoti et Gaio Statio Verecundo e Quintus Umbrius
EDCS-26600675.translation: To the patron again and again! To the priest and Gaius Statius Verecundus and Quintus Umbrius.
EDCS-26600675.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Quintus Umbrius to a patron, who is also identified as a priest and Gaius Statius Verecundus.

EDCS-26600676.text_edition: Publio Annaeo seviro magistro Mercuriali Lucio Lacutulano Luci liberto P Lucio Lacutulano patri Lacutulanae Maximae Annaeae Cai filiae Secundae Lacutulana Luci filia  viva sibi et suis
EDCS-26600676.translation: To Publius Annaeus, sevir, master Mercurialis, to Lucius Lacutulanus, freedman of Lucius, to Publius Lucius Lacutulanus, father of Lacutulana Maxima, and to Annaea, daughter of Gaius, Secunda Lacutulana, daughter of Lucius, while alive, for herself and her family.
EDCS-26600676.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Annaea Secunda Lacutulana to herself and her family, including Publius Annaeus, Lucius Lacutulanus, and her father, during her lifetime.

EDCS-26600677.translation: To Lucius Cali Fusc for the college of artisans.
EDCS-26600677.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Calius Fuscus from the college of artisans, likely a guild of craftsmen.

EDCS-26600678.text_edition: Caius Catellius Cai libertus Stabilis Caius Catellius Cai libertus Licinus IIIIIIviri testamento fieri iusserunt
EDCS-26600678.translation: Gaius Catellius Stabilis, freedman of Gaius, and Gaius Catellius Licinus, freedman of Gaius, sevir augustales, ordered this to be made by testament.
EDCS-26600678.summary: This inscription indicates that two freedmen, Gaius Catellius Stabilis and Gaius Catellius Licinus, who were seviri augustales, arranged for the monument's creation through their will.

EDCS-26600679.text_edition: o Luci liberto Hyllo ? IIIIII Augustali Maximae coniugi eius Turbo aedilis IIIIvir testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-26600679.translation: To Lucius, freedman Hyllo, a sevir Augustalis, and to Maxima, his wife; Turbo, aedile and duumvir, ordered this to be made by testament.
EDCS-26600679.summary: This inscription records that Turbo, an aedile and duumvir, commissioned a monument through his will for Lucius Hyllo, a freedman and sevir Augustalis, and his wife Maxima.

EDCS-27400524.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Proculo Kapitonis defuncto annorum XXVI Aurelia Severina marito et Aelia Proculina patri pientissimo pro pietate posuerunt
EDCS-27400524.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Severina and Aelia Proculina placed this for Aelius Proculus, son of Capito, who died at 26 years of age, for her most devoted husband, and for her most devoted father, out of piety.
EDCS-27400524.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelius Proculus, who died at age 26, by his wife Aurelia Severina and his daughter Aelia Proculina, expressing their profound devotion.

EDCS-27400525.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription, identifying Flavia Gentilis as the wife of Flavius Asper.

EDCS-27400526.text_edition: Titus Aurelius Marcus decurio municipii defunctus Salona annorum L AurI(elii)#Aur(elii)#AUI Albanus LIus LupIus#Lupus#LUIUS patri pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-27400526.translation: To Titus Aurelius Marcus, a decurion of the municipality, who died in Salona at 50 years of age. Aurelii Albanus and Lupus placed this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-27400526.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Aurelius Marcus, a municipal decurion who died at age 50 in Salona, erected by his sons Aurelius Albanus and Lupus.

EDCS-27400527.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Quintiliano decurioni municipii Malvesatii IIvirali defuncto nnorum LXV Publii Aelii Maximus et Silvanus et Tattaia patri pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-27400527.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Aelius Maximus and Silvanus and Tattaia placed this for Publius Aelius Quintilianus, a decurion of the municipality of Malvesatium, who held duumviral office, and died at 65 years of age, for their most devoted father.
EDCS-27400527.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Quintilianus, a municipal decurion and former duumvir of Malvesatium who died at 65, dedicated by his children Publius Aelius Maximus, Silvanus, and Tattaia.

EDCS-27400544.text_edition: Lucius Gaviarius augur IIvir quinquennalis decurionum decreto murum reficiendum coeravit idemque probavit
EDCS-27400544.translation: Lucius Gaviarius, augur and quinquennial duumvir, saw to the repair of the wall by decree of the decurions, and likewise approved it.
EDCS-27400544.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Gaviarius, an augur and quinquennial duumvir, oversaw and approved the repair of a wall, a public work commissioned by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-27400545.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Gallieno Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae consuli III res publica Docleatium
EDCS-27400545.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, father of his country, consul for the third time, the Republic of Doclea [set this up].
EDCS-27400545.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Republic of Doclea to the Emperor Gallienus, likely in honor of his reign, during his third consulship (around 265 AD).

EDCS-27400546.text_edition: Flavia Cai filia libens posuit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27400546.translation: Flavia, daughter of Gaius, willingly placed this. The plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27400546.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Flavia, daughter of Gaius, for herself or a loved one, on land granted by the municipal council.

EDCS-27400547.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Flaviae Eutiae pien=AItissimae#pientissimae#PIEAITISSIME quae vixit annos plus minus XXX Epidius Filippus sic maritus posuit
EDCS-27400547.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Epidius Filippus, her husband, placed this for Flavia Eutia, most devoted, who lived about 30 years.
EDCS-27400547.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Epidius Filippus to his most devoted wife, Flavia Eutia, who died at approximately 30 years old.

EDCS-27400548.text_edition: Tito Aurelio Sextiano equiti Romano decurioni municipii S Titus Aurelius Lupercus filio pientissimo in memoriam posuit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27400548.translation: To Titus Aurelius Sextianus, Roman knight, decurion of the municipality of S[---]. Titus Aurelius Lupercus, his most devoted son, placed this in his memory. The plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27400548.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Aurelius Lupercus to his father, Titus Aurelius Sextianus, a Roman knight and municipal decurion, on land provided by the city council.

EDCS-27400549.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Dexter et Amavilis! vivi sibi posuerunt et Masimillae! filiae quae vixit annos XXV
EDCS-27400549.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Dexter and Amabilis, alive, placed this for themselves and for their daughter Masimilla, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-27400549.summary: This is a funerary monument that Dexter and Amabilis, while still living, prepared for themselves and for their deceased daughter Masimilla, who lived to be 25.

EDCS-27400550.text_edition: Sexto Cipio Cai filio Sergia Firminiano Gaius Cipius Aper pater titulum poni iussit et epulo dedicari Gaius Cipius Aper consobrinus heres posuit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27400550.translation: To Sextus Cipius Firminianus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe. Gaius Cipius Aper, his father, ordered this monument to be placed and dedicated with a banquet. Gaius Cipius Aper, his cousin and heir, placed it. The plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27400550.summary: This is a funerary monument for Sextus Cipius Firminianus, dedicated by his father and cousin/heir, Gaius Cipius Aper, on land provided by the decurions, with an accompanying banquet for its dedication.

EDCS-27400551.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27400551.text_edition: Caio Clodio Cai filio Sergia Vitellino IIviro iure dicundo iudici ex V decuriis equo publico huic defuncto ordo decurionum locum sepulturae inpensam funeris  decrevit
EDCS-27400551.translation: To Gaius Clodius Vitellinus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, duovir with judicial power, judge from the five decuriae, with public horse. For this man, deceased, the order of decurions decreed a burial plot and funeral expenses.
EDCS-27400551.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Clodius Vitellinus, a high-ranking municipal official, for whom the local council decreed a burial plot and covered funeral expenses upon his death.

EDCS-27400552.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caesonia Nardis Caesonio Hermeti pientissimo coniugi viva fecit nnorum XLV et suis omnibus sub ascia dedicavit
EDCS-27400552.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Caesonia Nardis, living, made this for Caesonian Hermet, her most devoted husband, aged 45, and for all her own, dedicated under the adze.
EDCS-27400552.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Caesonia Nardis for her most devoted husband, Caesonian Hermet, who died at 45, and for her entire family, dedicated with a traditional ceremony.

EDCS-27400553.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Clodiae Euphrosynae nnorum XXIIII Clodius Euphrosynus et Clodia Frequentilla parentes vivi fecerunt et Clodio Eucarpo magistro
EDCS-27400553.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Clodius Euphrosynus and Clodia Frequentilla, parents, while living, made this for Clodia Euphrosyna, aged 24, and for Clodius Eucarpus, their teacher.
EDCS-27400553.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Clodius Euphrosynus and Clodia Frequentilla for their daughter, Clodia Euphrosyna, who died at 24, and for Clodius Eucarpus, their teacher.
