EDCS-27400554.text_edition: Sextus Publius Gracilis annorum LX Quintus Publius Gracilis filius annorum XX Marcus Aemilius Celer annorum XXV hic siti sunt
EDCS-27400554.translation: Sextus Publius Gracilis, aged 60; Quintus Publius Gracilis, his son, aged 20; Marcus Aemilius Celer, aged 25, are buried here.
EDCS-27400554.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of three individuals: Sextus Publius Gracilis, his son Quintus Publius Gracilis, and Marcus Aemilius Celer, with their respective ages.

EDCS-27400555.text_edition: Dis Manibus Statiliae Ianuariae coniugi dulcissimae Publius Lurius Stephanus  amoris  mei S pientis
EDCS-27400555.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Statilia Ianuaria, my sweetest wife. Publius Lurius Stephanus, from my devoted love.
EDCS-27400555.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a husband, Publius Lurius Stephanus, dedicating a memorial to his beloved wife, Statilia Ianuaria.

EDCS-27400556.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Luci filius Quadratus nnorum LX hic situs est
EDCS-27400556.translation: Quintus Valerius Quadratus, son of Lucius, aged 60, is buried here.
EDCS-27400556.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Quintus Valerius Quadratus, noting his age at death.

EDCS-27400557.text_edition: Quintus Manlius Quinti filius Sergia Rufus decurio iudex ex quinque decuriis equo publico testamento fieri iussit et epulo dedicari in hoc opus Statia Sexti filia Fida mater adiecit HS XXXVtriginta quinque milia et summae operi et epulo relictae XX fisco intulit HS XIIItredecim miliaCC solo publico
EDCS-27400557.translation: Quintus Manlius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe, decurion, judge from the five decuriae with public horse, ordered this to be made by his will and dedicated with a public banquet. For this work, his mother Statia Fida, daughter of Sextus, added 35,000 sesterces; and of the sum for the work and the banquet, the remaining 20 sesterces she paid to the treasury, along with 13,200 sesterces for public land.
EDCS-27400557.summary: This inscription records the testamentary wishes of Quintus Manlius Rufus for a monument and a celebratory banquet, with his mother, Statia Fida, contributing additional funds and managing the payments for the work and public land.

EDCS-27400559.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesia [---] made this.
EDCS-27400559.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, indicating that Caesia was involved in its creation, likely for a family member.

EDCS-27400560.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Tertullae vixit annum I menses VIII Hordionius Cyranus pater
EDCS-27400560.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Julia Tertulla, who lived 1 year and 8 months. Hordionius Cyranus, father.
EDCS-27400560.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Hordionius Cyranus, commemorating his young daughter, Julia Tertulla, who died at the age of one year and eight months.

EDCS-27400561.text_edition: Publia Luci filia Saecunda sic annorum XXVI hic sita est
EDCS-27400561.translation: Publia Saecunda, daughter of Lucius, aged 26, is buried here.
EDCS-27400561.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publia Saecunda, noting her age at death and her filiation.

EDCS-27400562.text_edition: Publio Lurio  Urso  vixit annos XX menses II
EDCS-27400562.translation: To Publius Lurius Ursus, who lived 20 years and 2 months.
EDCS-27400562.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Publius Lurius Ursus and stating his age at death.

EDCS-27400563.translation: To Luria, daughter of Publius, M[---].
EDCS-27400563.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Luria, daughter of Publius, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-27400564.text_edition: Naeus Minucius Lentinus nnorum L hic situs est
EDCS-27400564.translation: Gnaeus Minucius Lentinus, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-27400564.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Minucius Lentinus, indicating his age at death and burial location.

EDCS-27400565.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum mater Hordionia filio Hordionio carissimo qui vixit annos XXVII
EDCS-27400565.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Mother Hordionia to her dearest son Hordionius, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-27400565.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Hordionia, dedicating a memorial to her beloved son, also named Hordionius, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-27400567.text_edition: Statio  filio Sergia Valenti patri nnorum LXIII et Egnatiae Luci filiae Bucculae matri nnorum XXXXVII Valentinus et Crassus  parentibus suis fecerunt
EDCS-27400567.translation: Statio, son of Sergia Valens, to his father, aged 63, and to Egnatia Buccula, daughter of Lucius, his mother, aged 47. Valentinus and Crassus made this for their parents.
EDCS-27400567.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by Valentinus and Crassus for their parents, Statio Valens and Egnatia Buccula, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27400568.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Statiae Paulinae annorum VII
EDCS-27400568.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Statia Paulina, aged 7.
EDCS-27400568.summary: This short inscription is a funerary dedication to the Divine Manes for a young girl named Statia Paulina, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-27400571.text_edition: Diocas Diocaenu sic Cilix nnorum XXXV Lup bene merenti
EDCS-27400571.translation: Diocas, also known as Diocaenus, a Cilician, aged 35. To Lupus, who deserved well.
EDCS-27400571.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Diocas Diocaenus, a Cilician, to Lupus, described as deserving well, likely a tombstone for a freedman or a person of humble status.

EDCS-27400572.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Caesius Licentinus vixit annos XXXX
EDCS-27400572.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Caesius Licentinus lived 40 years.
EDCS-27400572.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Marcus Caesius Licentinus, indicating a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-27400573.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Caesius Niger qui vinxit et vixit L
EDCS-27400573.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Caesius Niger, who bound and lived 50 [years].
EDCS-27400573.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Marcus Caesius Niger, likely a tombstone, mentioning an occupation of 'binding' (perhaps a bookbinder or someone who tied things) and his age.

EDCS-27400575.text_edition: Marcus Plaetorius Marci filius Sergia  hic ille situs est vixit annos XXV
EDCS-27400575.translation: Marcus Plaetorius, son of Marcus, of the Sergian tribe. Here he lies, he lived 25 years.
EDCS-27400575.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Plaetorius, son of Marcus, a member of the Sergian tribe, who died at the age of 25, indicating a tombstone.

EDCS-27400576.text_edition: Tifatiae Amatae Secunda filia matri nnum XXXXX
EDCS-27400576.translation: To Tifatia Amata. Secunda, her daughter, to her mother, aged 50.
EDCS-27400576.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Secunda for her mother, Tifatia Amata, who lived to be 50 years old, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-27400577.text_edition: Valerio Fortunato et Valerio Felici et Valeriae Crescentillae Valeria Finita parentibus bene merentibus
EDCS-27400577.translation: To Valerius Fortunatus and Valerius Felix and Valeria Crescentilla. Valeria Finita to her well-deserving parents.
EDCS-27400577.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valeria Finita to her well-deserving parents, Valerius Fortunatus, Valerius Felix, and Valeria Crescentilla, likely a family tombstone.

EDCS-27400578.text_edition: Caio Egnatio Cai filio Sergia Marcello decurioni Iulia Tertulla mater Iulium Philinum et Iulium Crescentem libertos testamento ponere iussit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27400578.translation: To Gaius Egnatius Marcellus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, a decurion. Iulia Tertulla, his mother, ordered that Iulius Philinus and Iulius Crescens, her freedmen, be placed here by testament. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27400578.summary: This is a funerary inscription and testament record for Gaius Egnatius Marcellus, a decurion, erected by his mother Iulia Tertulla, who also arranged for her freedmen, Iulius Philinus and Iulius Crescens, to be interred there, with the burial plot formally granted by a decree of the decurions, suggesting a municipal context and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27400601.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Epidiae infelicissimae defunctae nnorum XXX Publius Aelius Cassianus maritus coniugi pientissimae posuit
EDCS-27400601.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelia Epidia, most unfortunate, who died aged 30. Publius Aelius Cassianus, her husband, placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-27400601.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Aelia Epidia, who died at 30, erected by her husband Publius Aelius Cassianus for his devoted wife, indicating a tombstone.

EDCS-27400602.text_edition: Lucio Calenio Apro Lucius Calenius Rufus pater filio nnorum V posuit
EDCS-27400602.translation: Lucius Calenius Rufus, father, placed this for his son Lucius Calenius Aper, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-27400602.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a father for his deceased son, Lucius Calenius Aper, who passed away at the age of five.

EDCS-27400603.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Severus se vivo sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-27400603.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Ulpius Severus made this for himself and his family during his lifetime.
EDCS-27400603.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb, that Marcus Ulpius Severus prepared for himself and his family while he was still alive.

EDCS-27400605.text_edition: Timotheo Lucio homini infelicissimo Fulvinia Tertia cognato inconparabili sic posuit
EDCS-27400605.translation: Fulvinia Tertia thus placed this for Timotheus Lucius, a most unfortunate man, an incomparable kinsman.
EDCS-27400605.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fulvinia Tertia to Timotheus Lucius, her beloved kinsman, described as a man of great misfortune.

EDCS-27400606.text_edition: Dis Manibus Spedia MarBcelIlina#Marcellina#MABCEIINA Spedio PaulIino#Paulino#PAUIINO annorum IX filio bene merenti titulum posuit
EDCS-27400606.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Spedia Marcellina placed this titulus for her well-deserving son, Spedius Paulinus, who lived 9 years.
EDCS-27400606.summary: This is a funerary monument, a titulus, dedicated by Spedia Marcellina to her nine-year-old son, Spedius Paulinus, who was considered well-deserving.

EDCS-27400607.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Nobilis mater bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27400607.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nobilis, his mother, placed this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-27400607.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother named Nobilis to her well-deserving son.

EDCS-27400608.text_edition: Dis Manibus FVorbiae#Forbiae#VORBIAE Aphrodisiae filiae sanctissimae Lucius Forbius Verinus pater
EDCS-27400608.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Forbius Verinus, the father, [placed this] for his most sacred daughter, Forbia Aphrodisia.
EDCS-27400608.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Forbius Verinus, dedicated to his extremely revered daughter, Forbia Aphrodisia.

EDCS-27500001.text_edition: S Fortunae Reduci Titus Flavius Sabinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27500001.translation: To Sacred Fortuna Redux. Titus Flavius Sabinus, a consular beneficiarius of the First Italic Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500001.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the goddess Fortuna Redux, made by Titus Flavius Sabinus, a military official, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27500002.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Atilius Gen miles legionis XIII geminae legionis I adiutricis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27500002.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Atilius Gen, a soldier of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion and a beneficiarius consularis of the First Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500002.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, offered by Gaius Atilius Gen, a soldier who served in two different legions.

EDCS-27500003.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio municipii Novensium salvis civibus Novensibus Aurelius Donatus votum posuit
EDCS-27500003.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Municipality of Novensium, with the citizens of Novensium safe, Aurelius Donatus placed this vow.
EDCS-27500003.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the guardian spirit of the municipality of Novensium, offered by Aurelius Donatus for the safety of its citizens.

EDCS-27500005.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio municipii Novensium Gaius Vibius Pomptina Ianuarius beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito  IIviris quinquennalibus Aurreliis Maximo et Anneo
EDCS-27500005.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the Municipality of Novensium. Gaius Vibius Pomptina Ianuarius, a consular beneficiarius of the First Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the quinquennial duumvirate of Aurelius Maximus and Annius.
EDCS-27500005.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the municipality of Novensium, made by Gaius Vibius Pomptina Ianuarius, a military officer, and dated to the term of two specific quinquennial duumvirs.

EDCS-27500006.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Acutianus bReneficiarius#beneficiarius#RF consularis legionis XIIII geminae Gordianae domino nostro Gordiano Augusto et Aviola consulibus libens animo
EDCS-27500006.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Acutianus, beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Gordiana, to our lord Gordian Augustus, and with Aviola as consul, gladly and with willing spirit.
EDCS-27500006.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by Acutianus, a beneficiarius consularis, likely dating to the consulship of Gordian Augustus and Aviola in 240 AD.

EDCS-27500008.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco Medico Parthico maximo tribunicia potestate XXIV imperatori V consuli III patri patriae pecunia publica decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500008.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus Medicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva; holding tribunician power for the 24th time, hailed imperator 5 times, consul 3 times, father of the country; with public money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500008.summary: This is a public dedication, funded by the decurions, honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus (Caracalla) during his 24th year of tribunician power, dating to 221 AD.

EDCS-27500009.text_edition: Caius Appuleius Etruscus speculator veteranus legionis XI decurio allectus Salonae et Gaius Appuleius Etruscus filius annorum XIIII hic siti sunt Cuparia Lupula p(osuit)
EDCS-27500009.translation: Gaius Appuleius Etruscus, a veteran speculator of the Eleventh Legion, decurion elected at Salona, and Gaius Appuleius Etruscus, his son, 14 years old, are buried here. Cuparia Lupula placed this.
EDCS-27500009.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Cuparia Lupula for Gaius Appuleius Etruscus, a veteran and decurion, and his 14-year-old son, Gaius Appuleius Etruscus.

EDCS-27500010.text_edition: Scaptia Florentia veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis testamento fieri iussit hic situs est in fronte pedes X in agrum sic pedes X
EDCS-27500010.translation: Scaptia Florentia, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, ordered this to be made by will. He is buried here. The plot is ten feet in front, and ten feet in depth.
EDCS-27500010.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Scaptia Florentia, a veteran, who ordered his tomb to be made by will, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-27500011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hermeti nnorum XXIIII Crescentilla soror bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27500011.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Hermes, 24 years old. Crescentilla, his sister, placed this for her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-27500011.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Crescentilla to her well-deserving brother, Hermes, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-27500012.translation: For himself and for his unfortunate children.
EDCS-27500012.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, dedicated by an individual to himself and his children who died young.

EDCS-27500013.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sulpicius Calvio centurio legionis I Minerviae praepositus cohortis I Belgarum hoc in loco maiestate et numine eius servatus
EDCS-27500013.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Sulpicius Calvio, centurion of the First Legion Minervia, commander of the First Cohort of Belgae, having been saved in this place by his majesty and divine power.
EDCS-27500013.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sulpicius Calvio, a centurion and cohort commander, in gratitude for being saved by the god's divine power.

EDCS-27500014.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Artorius Castus |(centurio) legionis III Gallicae item |(centurio) legionis VI ferratae item |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis item |(centurio) legionis V Macedonicae item primus pilus eiusdem legionis praeposito classis Misenatium praeff(ectus) legionis VI victricis duci sic leggionum alarum Britannicimiarum adversus Armoricanos procurator centenario sic provinciae Liburniae iure gladii vivus ipse sibi et suis  ex testamento
EDCS-27500014.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Artorius Castus, centurion of the Third Legion Gallica, likewise centurion of the Sixth Legion Ferrata, likewise centurion of the Second Legion Adiutrix, likewise centurion of the Fifth Legion Macedonica, likewise primus pilus of the same legion, commander of the Misenum fleet, prefect of the Sixth Legion Victrix, leader of the British legions and alae against the Armoricans, centenarian procurator of the province of Liburnia with the power of the sword. He himself, while living, for himself and his family, by will.
EDCS-27500014.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Artorius Castus, a distinguished Roman officer who held numerous military commands and procuratorial positions, which he prepared for himself and his family during his lifetime by will.

EDCS-27500015.text_edition: Gellio Dextro auguri IIIIviro iure dicundo   Sexto Gellio Eroni IIIIIIviro
EDCS-27500015.translation: To Gellius Dexter, augur, quattuorvir with judicial power. To Sextus Gellius Eron, sevir.
EDCS-27500015.summary: This inscription honors two men, Gellius Dexter, who held an augural and judicial magistracy, and Sextus Gellius Eron, who was a sevir.

EDCS-27500016.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Vindemiae annorum XXXV Rubellius Pisticus et Rubellia Vindemia filiae pientissimae
EDCS-27500016.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Cornelia Vindemia, 35 years old. Rubellius Pisticus and Rubellia Vindemia, her most devoted daughters, [placed this].
EDCS-27500016.summary: This is a tombstone for Cornelia Vindemia, who died at 35, erected by her devoted daughters, Rubellius Pisticus and Rubellia Vindemia.

EDCS-27500018.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Florio Crispino defuncto annorum II mensium III Publius Florius Severus filio infelici
EDCS-27500018.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Publius Florius Crispinus, who died at the age of 2 years, 3 months. Publius Florius Severus to his unfortunate son.
EDCS-27500018.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Florius Severus to his young, deceased son, Publius Florius Crispinus.

EDCS-27500019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Lucretio Marcello defuncto annorum XXVI Avidia Luculla fratri infelici
EDCS-27500019.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Marcus Lucretius Marcellus, who died at the age of 26. Avidia Luculla to her unfortunate brother.
EDCS-27500019.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Avidia Luculla in memory of her brother, Marcus Lucretius Marcellus.

EDCS-27500020.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lupae defunctae nnorum XV Dracontilla mater infelix bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27500020.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lupa, who died at the age of 15. Her unfortunate mother Dracontilla placed this for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-27500020.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Dracontilla, the mother, for her deceased daughter, Lupa, who was fifteen years old.

EDCS-27500021.text_edition: Dis Manibus Mercurialis Silvinae conservae con sic qua vixi annos XLV ex qua habeo natos V bene merenti
EDCS-27500021.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Mercurialis Silvina, my fellow slave and my partner with whom I lived for 45 years, from whom I have 5 children, well-deserving.
EDCS-27500021.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Mercurialis Silvina by her partner, also a slave, commemorating their long life together and their five children.

EDCS-27500022.text_edition: Dis Manibus Messor Ditoni coniugi defunctae annorum XX bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27500022.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Messor Ditoni placed this for his well-deserving wife, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-27500022.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Messor Ditoni to his wife, who died at the young age of twenty.

EDCS-27500023.text_edition: Murcidia Maxima viva fecit sibi et Publio Aelio Epidio marito suo et suis hoc monumentum heredem sequetur
EDCS-27500023.translation: Murcidia Maxima, while living, made this monument for herself and for Publius Aelius Epidius, her husband, and for their dependents; an heir will succeed to it.
EDCS-27500023.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Murcidia Maxima during her lifetime for herself, her husband Publius Aelius Epidius, and their family, specifying that an heir will succeed its possession.

EDCS-27500024.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Suricina.
EDCS-27500024.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Suricina.

EDCS-27500025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Terentiae Valeriae defunctae annorum
EDCS-27500025.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Terentia Valeria, who died at the age of [---].
EDCS-27500025.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to Terentia Valeria, whose age at death is missing.

EDCS-27500026.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Vipsanio Marcello Vipsanius Lupus consubrino sic bene merenti
EDCS-27500026.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Vipsanius Marcellus. Vipsanius Lupus to his well-deserving cousin.
EDCS-27500026.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vipsanius Lupus to his cousin, Lucius Vipsanius Marcellus.

EDCS-27500028.text_edition: Lucio Aelio Caesare II Publio Coelio Balbino Vibullio Pio consulibus VII Idus Octobres Caius Domitius Valens IIvir iure dicundo praeeunte Caio Iulio Severo pontifice legem dixit in ea verba quae infra scripta sunt Iuppiter Optime Maxime quandoque tibi hodie hanc aram dabo dedicaboque ollis legibus ollisque regionibus dabo dedicaboque quas hic hodie palam dixero uti infimum solum huius arae est si quis hic hostia sacrum faxit quod magmentum nec protollat it circo tamen probe factum esto ceterae leges huic arae eaedem sunto quae arae Dianae sunt in Aventino monte dictae hisce legibus hisce regionibus sic uti dixi hanc tibi aram Iuppiter Optime Maxime do dico dedicoque uti sis volens propitius mihi collegisque meis decurionibus colonis incolis coloniae Martiae Iuliae Salonae coniugibus liberisque nostris
EDCS-27500028.translation: In the consulship of Lucius Aelius Caesar for the second time and Publius Coelius Balbinus Vibullius Pius, on the 7th Ides of October, Gaius Domitius Valens, duumvir with judicial power, with Gaius Julius Severus, pontiff, leading the way, declared the law in the words written below: 'Jupiter Optimus Maximus, when I today give and dedicate this altar to you, I will give and dedicate it with those laws and those conditions which I shall openly declare here today, that the lowest ground of this altar is thus: if anyone here makes a sacrifice with a victim, that which is a 'magmentum' (part of entrails) he may not set forth, yet because of this it shall be considered properly done; the other laws for this altar shall be the same as those declared for the altar of Diana on the Aventine Hill. With these laws, with these conditions, just as I have said, I give, dedicate, and consecrate this altar to you, Jupiter Optimus Maximus, so that you may be willing and propitious to me, my colleagues, the decurions, the colonists, the inhabitants of the colony Martia Iulia Salona, and to our wives and children.'
EDCS-27500028.summary: This inscription records the formal dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in the colony of Martia Iulia Salona on October 9th, 137 AD, by the duumvir Gaius Domitius Valens, outlining the specific religious laws and conditions of the dedication for the welfare of the community.

EDCS-27500029.text_edition: Caesia Titi filia Secunda Boati Aesculapio
EDCS-27500029.translation: Caesia Secunda, daughter of Titus, to Boatus and Aesculapius.
EDCS-27500029.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Caesia Secunda to the deities Boatus and Aesculapius.

EDCS-27500030.text_edition: Fortunae Conservatrici pro salute Marci Aureli Iuli viri clarissimi auguris praesidis provinciae Dassius notarius votum solvit
EDCS-27500030.translation: To Fortuna the Preserver, for the health of Marcus Aurelius Iulius, most distinguished man, augur, and governor of the province, Dassius, a notary, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500030.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Dassius, a notary, to Fortuna Conservatrix for the well-being of the provincial governor Marcus Aurelius Iulius.

EDCS-27500031.text_edition: Fortunae Domesticae sacrum Aulus Obultronius Beryllus
EDCS-27500031.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Domestica, Aulus Obultronius Beryllus.
EDCS-27500031.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Fortuna Domestica by Aulus Obultronius Beryllus.

EDCS-27500032.text_edition: Herculi  Sextus Aquillius Severus |(centurio) cohortis V donis donatus ab Imperatore Traiano bello Dacico decurio Salonis et Flanonae locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500032.translation: To Hercules. Sextus Aquillius Severus, a centurion of the Fifth Cohort, honored with gifts by Emperor Trajan in the Dacian War, a decurion of Salona and Flanona, was granted this plot by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500032.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules by Sextus Aquillius Severus, a veteran centurion of the Dacian War and a decurion, who was granted land by municipal decree.

EDCS-27500033.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Lucius Egnatius Luci filius Cnaei nepos Cnaei pronepos Tromentina Clemens decurio augur
EDCS-27500033.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Egnatius Clemens, son of Lucius, grandson of Gnaeus, great-grandson of Gnaeus, of the Tromentina tribe, a decurion and augur.
EDCS-27500033.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Egnatius Clemens, a decurion and augur.

EDCS-27500034.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Maximus Germani votum posuit
EDCS-27500034.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Maximus, son of Germanus, placed his vow.
EDCS-27500034.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maximus, son of Germanus.

EDCS-27500036.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et dis deabus sacrum Tiberius  Severus
EDCS-27500036.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the gods and goddesses, Tiberius Severus.
EDCS-27500036.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication by Tiberius Severus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to all gods and goddesses.

EDCS-27500037.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et divo Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico tribunicia potestate XIIII Publius Anteius Publi Antei Syntrophi libertus Herma IIIIIIvir et Augustalis porticum voto soluto libens merito loco accepto decurionum decreto
EDCS-27500037.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the deified Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, in his 14th tribunician power. Publius Anteius Herma, freedman of Publius Anteius Syntrophus, a sevir and Augustalis, built this portico having willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, having received the plot by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500037.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a portico by Publius Anteius Herma, a freedman and Augustalis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the deified Emperor Claudius, fulfilling a vow and building on land granted by the decurions, likely dating to 54 AD during Claudius's 14th tribunician power.

EDCS-27500038.text_edition: Iovi Opt=Eimo#Optimo#OEIMO Maximo caelesti patrono Gaius Caesius Corymbus et Faberia Cara votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-27500038.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, celestial patron, Gaius Caesius Corymbus and Faberia Cara willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-27500038.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Gaius Caesius Corymbus and Faberia Cara to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Caelestis.

EDCS-27500039.text_edition: Messor Cai I S votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27500039.translation: Messor, son of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500039.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Messor, son of Gaius.

EDCS-27500041.text_edition: Curia Prisca Matri Magnae fanum reIfecit#refecit#RIFECIT signa posuit Larophorum cymbala tynpana sic catillum forfices aram dat libens animo
EDCS-27500041.translation: Curia Prisca rebuilt the sanctuary for the Great Mother, set up statues, a Larophorum, cymbals, tympana, also a bowl, scissors, and a voluntary altar, with willing spirit.
EDCS-27500041.summary: This inscription records Curia Prisca's restoration and dedication of a sanctuary to the Great Mother, including several cultic objects.

EDCS-27500042.text_edition: Publius Iulius Rufus aedem Matri Magnae de sua pecunia faciendum curavit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-27500042.translation: Publius Iulius Rufus took care of the construction of a temple for the Great Mother with his own money and also dedicated it.
EDCS-27500042.summary: This inscription records Publius Iulius Rufus's personal financing and dedication of a temple to the Great Mother.

EDCS-27500043.text_edition: Lucius Statius Luci filius Facula quinquennalis Matri Deum aedem de pecunia sua fecit ex voto
EDCS-27500043.translation: Lucius Statius Facula, son of Lucius, quinquennalis, built a temple for the Mother of the Gods with his own money, in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-27500043.summary: This inscription records Lucius Statius Facula, a quinquennalis, fulfilling a vow by building a temple for the Mother of the Gods with his own funds.

EDCS-27500044.text_edition: Deo Invicto pro salute et incolumitate Pamphili dispensatoris Auggustorum nnostrorum Fortunatus arcarius
EDCS-27500044.translation: To the Unconquered God, for the health and safety of Pamphilus, our Augusti's dispenser, Fortunatus, the treasurer (dedicates this).
EDCS-27500044.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Fortunatus, a treasurer, to the Unconquered God for the well-being of Pamphilus, the imperial dispenser.

EDCS-27500045.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis  sacrum pro salute Liberalis nostri Coetonicus servus
EDCS-27500045.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs, for the health of our Liberalis, Coetonicus, a slave (dedicates this).
EDCS-27500045.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Coetonicus, a slave, to the Augustan Nymphs for the health of his master Liberalis.

EDCS-27500046.summary: This short inscription is a simple dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus.

EDCS-27500047.text_edition: Mescenia Valentina imperio Veneris Iunonem sic donum dedit
EDCS-27500047.translation: Mescenia Valentina, by the command of Venus, thus gave a gift to Juno.
EDCS-27500047.summary: This inscription records Mescenia Valentina making a votive gift to Juno at the command of Venus.

EDCS-27500048.text_edition: Prima Fulviniae Urbana Curtiae Oecumene Iuliae magistrae Veneris donum dederunt
EDCS-27500048.translation: Prima, Fulvinia Urbana, Curtia Oecumene, and Iulia gave a gift to the mistress of Venus.
EDCS-27500048.summary: This inscription records a dedication of a gift to the mistress of Venus by Prima, Fulvinia Urbana, Curtia Oecumene, and Iulia.

EDCS-27500049.summary: This very brief inscription is a dedication to Venus Victrix.

EDCS-27500050.text_edition: Veneri Victrici in memoriam Albuciae Cai filiae Pontiae uxoris Marcus Marius Paulus locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500050.translation: To Venus Victrix, in memory of Albucia Pontia, daughter of Gaius, his wife, Marcus Marius Paulus (dedicated this). The location was given by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500050.summary: This inscription records Marcus Marius Paulus's dedication to Venus Victrix in memory of his wife, Albucia Pontia, with the site granted by local decurions.

EDCS-27500052.text_edition: Constantio IIII et Maximiano IIII nobilissimis Caesaribus conssulibus praefecto Aurelio Valentiniano qui miEniEstrabit!#ministrabit!#MENESTBI at sic Tritones Kalendis Februariis Volusius Adiutor Aurelius Asiaticus Aurelius Armentius Aurelius Vates Aurelius Antonianus Aurelius Ursilianus Aurelius Lucianus Aurelius Lapns sic Aurelius Dalmatius Aurelius MerPcurPius#Mercurius#MEPCUPIUS  Sabino et Rufino consulibus IRAN NAEC RIDV MAE IEAF (Hadrian[us &
EDCS-27500052.translation: When Constantius for the fourth time and Maximian for the fourth time were the most noble Caesars and consuls, to the prefect Aurelius Valentinianus, who will serve at the Tritons on the Kalends of February: Volusius Adiutor, Aurelius Asiaticus, Aurelius Armentius, Aurelius Vates, Aurelius Antonianus, Aurelius Ursilianus, Aurelius Lucianus, Aurelius Lapsus, Aurelius Dalmatius, Aurelius Mercurius. When Sabinus and Rufinus were consuls, [further illegible text mentions Hadrian].
EDCS-27500052.summary: This inscription records the names of individuals who served under the prefect Aurelius Valentinianus on specific dates during the consulships of Constantius and Maximian (305 AD) and Sabinus and Rufinus (310 AD), likely related to an official duty or collegium activity involving the 'Tritons'.

EDCS-27500053.text_edition: DDominis nnostris Diocletiano VIII et Maximiano VII Auggustis conssulibus  Aurelio Valentiniano viro perfectissimo bis praefecto Kalendis Februariis miEniEstravimus#ministravimus#MENESTRAVIMUS at sic TritoneIs#Tritones#TRITONIS Aurelius Mercurius Aurelius Secundinus Aurelius Fortunius Aurelius Severianus Aurelius Sarmatio Aurelius Antonius Aurelius Ursus Iulius Secundus Aurelius Uranius Aurelius Forensis  DDominis nnostris Constantino Augusto V et Licinio iuniore  Caesare consulibus Aurelio Xenone bis  praefecto Kalendis Februariis miEniEstrabimus sic#ministrabimus sic#MENESTRABIMUS at sic Tritones Aurelius Lucentius Aurelius ius Aurelius Gregorius Aurelius Firminus Aurelius Marcellinus Aurelius Dalmatius  Valentinus  Aurelius Vincentius Aurelius Messor  PECVS  ORVS  Dominis nostris Constantino Augusto set Constantino nobRilissimo#nobilissimo#NORIISSIMO Caesare praefecOito#praefecito#PREFEOITO Quint ilio FaintillIo#Faintillo#FAINTILIO at sic Tritones KalendOis#Kalendis#ALENOIS Februariis Aurelius Martinus Aurelius Donatus Varius Terentianus Aurelius Stercorius Aurelius Exuperius Aurelius Terentianus Aurelius Alexander Aurelius Heraclianus Aurelius Reditus  Aurelius Maurenus Aurelius Balbinus  Aurelius Euticius  Aurelius Leuntius Aurelius Senator  Ministri ad Tritones Aelius Valerianus Varius Sabinus Iulius Silvius Aurelius Fortunius Papirius Crescenti--- Claudius Barbianus Dirrutius Crescenti--- Aelius Dalmatius ex permissu Nocturni Novelli patroni collegii  feliciter
EDCS-27500053.translation: When our Lords Diocletian for the eighth time and Maximian for the seventh time were Augusti and consuls, we served at the Tritons on the Kalends of February for Aurelius Valentinianus, a most perfect man and twice prefect: Aurelius Mercurius, Aurelius Secundinus, Aurelius Fortunius, Aurelius Severianus, Aurelius Sarmatio, Aurelius Antonius, Aurelius Ursus, Iulius Secundus, Aurelius Uranius, Aurelius Forensis. When our Lords Constantine Augustus for the fifth time and Licinius the younger Caesar were consuls, we will serve at the Tritons on the Kalends of February for Aurelius Xeno, twice prefect: Aurelius Lucentius, Aurelius Ius, Aurelius Gregorius, Aurelius Firminus, Aurelius Marcellinus, Aurelius Dalmatius, Valentinus, Aurelius Vincentius, Aurelius Messor, Pecus, Orus. When our Lords Constantine Augustus and Constantine the most noble Caesar were consuls, at the Tritons on the Kalends of February for the prefect Quintilius Faintillo: Aurelius Martinus, Aurelius Donatus, Varius Terentianus, Aurelius Stercorius, Aurelius Exuperius, Aurelius Terentianus, Aurelius Alexander, Aurelius Heraclianus, Aurelius Reditus, Aurelius Maurenus, Aurelius Balbinus, Aurelius Euticius, Aurelius Leuntius, Aurelius Senator. Ministers at the Tritons: Aelius Valerianus, Varius Sabinus, Iulius Silvius, Aurelius Fortunius, Papirius Crescentius, Claudius Barbianus, Dirrutius Crescentius, Aelius Dalmatius, by permission of Nocturnus Novellus, patron of the collegium. Successfully.
EDCS-27500053.summary: This lengthy inscription chronicles the names of individuals who served at a location known as 'the Tritons' on the Kalends of February in various years, corresponding to the consulships of Diocletian and Maximian (303 AD), Constantine and Licinius (318 AD), and Constantine and Constantine II (320 AD), under the prefects Aurelius Valentinianus, Aurelius Xeno, and Quintilius Faintillo, likely detailing membership or service in an official collegium.

EDCS-27500054.translation: Aurelia Renata placed this, in her own magistracy.
EDCS-27500054.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Aurelia Renata, commemorating her tenure as a magistrate.

EDCS-27500055.text_edition: magister II inM#in#IM memoriam Luriae Hygiae filiae donum posuit cuius dedicatione collegio epulum dedit loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500055.translation: The master (magister II) placed this gift in memory of Luria Hygiae, his daughter, for whose dedication he gave a feast to the collegium; the plot was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500055.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory monument placed by a 'master' (magister II) for his daughter, Luria Hygiae, detailing a feast given to a collegium and the official grant of land by municipal officials.

EDCS-27500056.translation: Species gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-27500056.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that a person named Species fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-27500057.text_edition: Pro salute domini nostri  Caius A I posuit
EDCS-27500057.translation: For the health of our lord, Gaius A. I. placed this.
EDCS-27500057.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Gaius A. I. for the well-being of a reigning emperor or another important figure, referred to as 'our lord'.

EDCS-27500059.text_edition: IANAM VI CON Flavius Primitivos sic Mius Agrius Liberalis  Pomponio Lupio Obultronia Nicia
EDCS-27500059.translation: Flavius Primitivus, Mius Agrius Liberalis, Pomponius Lupius, Obultronia Nicia.
EDCS-27500059.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely listing names of individuals, possibly patrons, officials, or members of a collegium, with some reference to an official capacity or group (IANAM VI CON).

EDCS-27500060.text_edition: Tiberio Claudio Drusi filio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XI imperatori XXIIII consuli V censori patri patriae Publio Anteio legato pro praetore
EDCS-27500060.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of Drusus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 11th time, Imperator for the 24th time, Consul for the 5th time, Censor, Father of the Fatherland; and to Publius Anteius, legate pro praetore.
EDCS-27500060.summary: This inscription is a public dedication honoring the Emperor Claudius, detailing his full titulature and a brief mention of his legate, Publius Anteius, likely dating to 51 AD based on the tribunician power and consulship.

EDCS-27500061.text_edition: Divo Nervae Lucius Vettius Luci filius Sergia Catulus IIvir iure dicundo ex pecunia honoraria IIviratus sui locus datus publice decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500061.translation: To the Deified Nerva, Lucius Vettius, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, Catulus, duumvir with judicial power, from the honorary money of his duumvirate; the plot was given publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500061.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Vettius Catulus, a duumvir, to the deified Emperor Nerva, funding it from his official stipend, and indicating that the land for the monument was granted by the local council, likely dating from after Nerva's death (98 AD).

EDCS-27500062.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIIII consule III patre patriae vexillationes legionum II piae et III Concordiae pedes CC sub cura Publi Aeli Amyntiani |(centurionis) frumentarii legionis II Traianae
EDCS-27500062.translation: When the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 24th time, Consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, was in power, vexillations of Legions II Pia and III Concordia, 200 foot soldiers, under the care of Publius Aelius Amyntianus, centurion frumentarius of Legion II Traiana.
EDCS-27500062.summary: This inscription details the presence of vexillations (detachments) of Roman legions, specifically 200 infantry from Legions II Pia and III Concordia, under the command of Centurion Publius Aelius Amyntianus, during the reign of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), dating to 221 AD based on his titulature.

EDCS-27500063.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli honorarii, tituli sacri
EDCS-27500063.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constanti victori ac triumfatori semper Augusto Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius vir clarissimus praeses provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-27500063.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, victorious and triumphant, ever Augustus, Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-27500063.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Flavius Constantius by Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius, the governor of Dalmatia, likely for a statue base or monument, dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-27500064.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli honorarii, tituli sacri
EDCS-27500064.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constantio victoriosissimo semper Augusto Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius vir clarissimus praeses provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-27500064.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, most victorious, ever Augustus, Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-27500064.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Emperor Flavius Constantius by Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius, the governor of Dalmatia, likely for a statue base or monument, dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-27500066.text_edition: Publio Balsamio Sabiniano clarissimo pueri tribuno laticlavio filio Aureli Sabiniani viri egregii procuratoris ducis provinciae Dalmatiae tribuni protectorum Augusti N vigor Concordius Victorinianus patrono praestantissimo
EDCS-27500066.translation: To Publius Balsamius Sabinianus, most distinguished youth, tribune with the broad stripe, son of Aurelius Sabinianus, eminent man, procurator and duke of the province of Dalmatia, tribune of the emperor's protectors, N. Vigor Concordius Victorinianus, to his most outstanding patron.
EDCS-27500066.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Publius Balsamius Sabinianus, a young man of senatorial rank, by N. Vigor Concordius Victorinianus, likely a client or subordinate, in recognition of his patronage.

EDCS-27500067.text_edition: Casconiae Marcellinae clarissimae feminae matri pientissimae quae vixit nnos LVII Casconia Marcellina clarissima femina filia et heres si quis hoc sepulchrum tentaverit aperire dederit fisco SS L milia
EDCS-27500067.translation: To Casconia Marcellina, most distinguished woman, most devoted mother, who lived 57 years; Casconia Marcellina, most distinguished woman, daughter and heir, made this. If anyone attempts to open this tomb, they shall pay 50,000 sesterces to the treasury.
EDCS-27500067.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Casconia Marcellina, erected by her daughter and heir, including a curse to protect the tomb from disturbance.

EDCS-27500068.text_edition: Caio Valerio Cai filio Tromentina Respecto Terentiano clarissimo iuveni Xviro stlitibus iudicandis filio et Caediciae Luci filiae Lucillae  Crispinilla mater infelicissima ave vale
EDCS-27500068.translation: To Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, Respectus Terentianus, most distinguished youth, decemvir for judging lawsuits, her son; and to Caedicia, daughter of Lucius, Lucilla: Crispinilla, most unhappy mother, farewell.
EDCS-27500068.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Gaius Valerius Respectus Terentianus, a young man of senatorial rank, and Caedicia Lucilla, likely his sister, erected by their mother Crispinilla.

EDCS-27500079.summary: This brief inscription is likely a simple funerary dedication or a goodwill wish, possibly indicating a place of rest for Mescenia Tertia.

EDCS-27500091.text_edition: Aesculapio Augusto sacrum Publius Servilius  IIIIIIvir Augustalis Flavialis Titialis Nervialis testamento ponendum iussit ex HS et ob dedicationem
EDCS-27500091.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius Augustus. Publius Servilius, Augustalis, Flavialis, Titialis, Nervialis sexvir, ordered this to be erected from his will from HS [funds] and for the dedication.
EDCS-27500091.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Aesculapius by Publius Servilius, a member of multiple priestly colleges, specifying that it was erected from his will and associated funds, likely after his death.

EDCS-27500093.text_edition: Genio plebis sacrum Lucius Septimulenus Vitalis Quintus Lusius Acrabanus Quintus Iulius Daphnus Marcus Curtius Sporus IIIIIIviri ob honorem magistri Mercuriales
EDCS-27500093.translation: Sacred to the Genius of the Plebs. Lucius Septimulenus Vitalis, Quintus Lusius Acrabanus, Quintus Iulius Daphnus, Marcus Curtius Sporus, sexviri, in honor of the master Mercuriales.
EDCS-27500093.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the Plebs by four sexviri, who are likely civic officials or members of a collegium, made in honor of the 'master Mercuriales,' probably a group associated with the god Mercury.

EDCS-27500094.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Maximus lapidarius ex voto aram posuit
EDCS-27500094.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Maximus the stonemason placed this altar as a vow.
EDCS-27500094.summary: This inscription is a votive altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maximus, a stonemason, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-27500095.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Publius Mescenius Severianus votum solvit lI(ibens)#l(ibens)#I merito
EDCS-27500095.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Mescenius Severianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500095.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Mescenius Severianus, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-27500096.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Munnius Imnis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27500096.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Munnius Imnis willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500096.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter by Lucius Munnius Imnis.

EDCS-27500097.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute domini nostri Marcus Ulpius Kalendinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae Fusco II et Dextro consulibus
EDCS-27500097.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the well-being of our master, Marcus Ulpius Kalendinus, beneficiarius consularis of the First Italian Legion, during the consulship of Fuscus II and Dextrus.
EDCS-27500097.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter for the health of an unnamed master, made by a military official, Marcus Ulpius Kalendinus, and dated to 225 AD by the consuls mentioned.

EDCS-27500098.text_edition: Marci Lusi Severi clarissimi viri Modestus libertus templum ex voto restituit
EDCS-27500098.translation: Modestus, freedman of Marcus Lusius Severus, a most distinguished man, restored the temple according to a vow.
EDCS-27500098.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple by Modestus, a freedman, in fulfillment of a vow on behalf of his patron, Marcus Lusius Severus.

EDCS-27500099.text_edition: Libero Augusto sacrum Lusius Felicio Luci Lusi Severini libertus posuit
EDCS-27500099.translation: Sacred to Liber Augustus, Lusius Felicio, freedman of Lucius Lusius Severinus, placed this.
EDCS-27500099.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Liber Augustus, erected by Lusius Felicio, a freedman of Lucius Lusius Severinus.

EDCS-27500100.text_edition: Invicto Deo Soli  Marcus Lusius Trofimas libertus posuit
EDCS-27500100.translation: To the Unconquered God Sol, Marcus Lusius Trofimas, freedman, placed this.
EDCS-27500100.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Sol Invictus, set up by Marcus Lusius Trofimas, a freedman.

EDCS-27500101.text_edition: Lucius Magius Princeps Mercurio votum solvit
EDCS-27500101.translation: Lucius Magius Princeps fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-27500101.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Mercury by Lucius Magius Princeps.

EDCS-27500102.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Marcus Ulpius Augusti libertus Nedymus Caius Pollius Albanus Titus Vetulenus Titi libertus Abascantus Quintus Cornelius Augustalis Lucius Volceius Cerdo IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem
EDCS-27500102.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Marcus Ulpius Nedymus, freedman of Augustus, Caius Pollius Albanus, Titus Vetulenus Abascantus, freedman of Titus, Quintus Cornelius Augustalis, Lucius Volceius Cerdo, se[x]viri magistri Mercuriales, on account of the honor.
EDCS-27500102.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mercury Augustus, set up by a group of six magistrates (seviri magistri Mercuriales), likely in gratitude for an honor.

EDCS-27500103.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Lucius Volceius Luci libertus Silvester IIIIIIvir ob honorem
EDCS-27500103.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Lucius Volceius Silvester, freedman of Lucius, se[x]vir, on account of the honor.
EDCS-27500103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mercury Augustus, set up by Lucius Volceius Silvester, a freedman and a sevir, likely in gratitude for an honor.

EDCS-27500104.text_edition: Ninfis sic Augustis sacrum I M P librarius
EDCS-27500104.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, thus Augustae, I. M. P., scribe.
EDCS-27500104.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs, made by a scribe identified only by his initials I. M. P.

EDCS-27500105.text_edition: Saturno Augusto sacrum Claudia Aesernina sacerdos divae Augustae testamento poni iussit
EDCS-27500105.translation: Sacred to Saturn Augustus, Claudia Aesernina, priestess of the divine Augusta, ordered this to be placed by testament.
EDCS-27500105.summary: This inscription is a posthumous dedication to Saturn Augustus, ordered in the will of Claudia Aesernina, a priestess of a deified empress.

EDCS-27500106.text_edition: Lucius Lusius Corpio Lucius Lusius Quintio Luci liberti IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem
EDCS-27500106.translation: Lucius Lusius Corpio, Lucius Lusius Quintio, freedmen of Lucius, *seviri*, masters of the Mercuriales, in honor of (their office).
EDCS-27500106.summary: This inscription records an offering made by two freedmen, Lucius Lusius Corpio and Lucius Lusius Quintio, who held the offices of *seviri* and masters of the Mercuriales collegium, likely in recognition of their magistracies.

EDCS-27500107.text_edition: Publius Mescenius Trophimus Titus Flavius Asiaticus Titus Flavius Surus Lucius Bovianius Pareus IIIIIIviri ob honorem magistri Mercuriales
EDCS-27500107.translation: Publius Mescenius Trophimus, Titus Flavius Asiaticus, Titus Flavius Surus, Lucius Bovianius Pareus, *seviri*, in honor of (their office) as masters of the Mercuriales.
EDCS-27500107.summary: This inscription lists four individuals, Publius Mescenius Trophimus, Titus Flavius Asiaticus, Titus Flavius Surus, and Lucius Bovianius Pareus, who were *seviri* and masters of the Mercuriales, presumably making an offering to mark their office.

EDCS-27500109.summary: This brief inscription expresses devotion to an individual named Constantius, possibly an emperor or a local dignitary.

EDCS-27500110.text_edition: Marcus Lusius  PON ob dedicationem balnei EXVOS CENT
EDCS-27500110.translation: Marcus Lusius PON, on account of the dedication of the bath, EXVOS CENT.
EDCS-27500110.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Lusius, or someone associated with 'PON', made a dedication upon the inauguration of a bath, possibly an offering or a payment.

EDCS-27500112.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marco Avilio Nummio |(centurioni) cohortis III Alpinorum
EDCS-27500112.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes, for Marcus Avilius Nummius, centurion of the III Alpine cohort.
EDCS-27500112.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Avilius Nummius, a centurion of the III Alpine cohort.

EDCS-27500114.text_edition: Publius Lastus Auli filius Scaeva domo Florentia eques veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis nnum LX stipendiorum XXX et Lastae Publi libertae Apiculae Publio Lastio Felici Publio Lasto Diodoro liberto testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-27500114.translation: Publius Lastus, son of Aulus, Scaeva, from Florentia, veteran cavalryman of the Seventh Praetorian, Pious, Faithful Legion, 60 years old, with 30 years of service; and Lastae Apicula, freedwoman of Publius; Publius Lastius Felix; Publius Lastus Diodorus, freedman. He ordered this to be made by will.
EDCS-27500114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Lastus Scaeva, a veteran cavalryman from Florentia, and includes other individuals, likely family or freedmen, indicating it was made posthumously according to his will.

EDCS-27500115.summary: This is a very short and fragmentary inscription mentioning Mantus, son of Eantis, with an unclear abbreviation.

EDCS-27500116.text_edition: Marcius VS miles um APLA  stipendiorum VII F
EDCS-27500116.translation: Marcius VS, soldier (from) APLA, 7 years of service, F.
EDCS-27500116.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription for a soldier named Marcius, noting his place of origin or unit and years of service, likely part of a tombstone.

EDCS-27500118.text_edition: Aulo Annaeo Auli filio Aniensi Flacco flamini Augustali IIIIviro II quinquennali IIIIviro iure dicundo praefecto iure dicundo ex decreto decurionum pontifici Aninia Decimi filia uxor
EDCS-27500118.translation: To Aulus Annaeus Flaccus, son of Aulus, of the Aniensis tribe, Augustan flamen, *quattuorvir* and *quattuorvir quinquennalis* for the second time, *quattuorvir* for administering justice, prefect for administering justice by decree of the decurions, pontifex. His wife, Aninia, daughter of Decimus (made this).
EDCS-27500118.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Aninia, daughter of Decimus, to her husband, Aulus Annaeus Flaccus, detailing his extensive political and religious offices, including Augustan flamen, multiple *quattuorvir* roles, and pontifex.

EDCS-27500120.text_edition: enio CI o IIIIIIviro s Acceptus filius patri  votum fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-27500120.translation: enio CI, (a) *sevir* for the second time. Acceptus, his son, made this vow for his father, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-27500120.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Acceptus for his father, who held the office of *sevir* for the second time, and also for himself and his family, likely a dedication or an offering to a deity.

EDCS-27500121.text_edition: Lucio Aconio Agathopo IIIIIIvir convictor Concordiae
EDCS-27500121.translation: To Lucius Aconius Agathopus, sevir, fellow member of Concordia.
EDCS-27500121.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Aconius Agathopus, a sevir Augustalis, likely by an association or colleague of Concordia.

EDCS-27500122.text_edition: Lucio Aemilio Eutycheti IIIIIIviro Lucius Aemilius    locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27500122.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Eutyches, sevir. Lucius Aemilius. A plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500122.summary: This inscription records a burial plot granted to Lucius Aemilius Eutyches, a sevir, by decree of the town council (decurions), with Lucius Aemilius as the dedicant.

EDCS-27500123.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Annaeo Palaestrico IIIIIIviro thiasus iuventutis fecit
EDCS-27500123.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. For Lucius Annaeus Palaestricus, sevir. The youth thiasus made this.
EDCS-27500123.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Annaeus Palaestricus, a sevir, by a youth association (thiasus iuventutis).

EDCS-27500124.text_edition: A---ius Luci libertus Eutropus IIIIIIvir vivos sic posuit
EDCS-27500124.translation: A---ius Eutropus, freedman of Lucius, sevir, placed this while alive.
EDCS-27500124.summary: This inscription is a self-dedication for a burial monument, erected by A---ius Eutropus, a freedman of Lucius and a sevir, during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500125.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai libertus Martialis IIIIIIvir vivos sic fecit sibi  et suis
EDCS-27500125.translation: Gaius Iulius Martialis, freedman of Gaius, sevir, made this while alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-27500125.summary: This is a self-dedication for a burial monument, made by Gaius Iulius Martialis, a freedman of Gaius and a sevir, for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500126.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Pomp Urso IIIIIIviro nnorum XLV marito bene merenti Terentia Dafine uxor cum sic nnorum XXV
EDCS-27500126.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Lucius Pompeius Ursus, sevir, aged 45 years, a well-deserving husband. Terentia Dafine, his wife, made this, being 25 years old.
EDCS-27500126.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Pompeius Ursus, a sevir, aged 45, by his well-deserving wife, Terentia Dafine, who was 25 years old.

EDCS-27500127.translation: Olybius Amen, freedman of Lucius, sevir.
EDCS-27500127.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Olybius Amen, a freedman of Lucius and a sevir Augustalis.

EDCS-27500128.text_edition: lo infanti issimo qui vixit --- dies XX Publius V IIIIIIvir pater et sibi
EDCS-27500128.translation: To the most infant ---, who lived --- 20 days. Publius V---, sevir, his father, and for himself.
EDCS-27500128.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from a father, Publius V---, a sevir, to his infant child who lived for 20 days, also establishing a place for himself.

EDCS-27500130.text_edition: Dis Manibus Atimeti Ulpi Menophanis Augusti dispensatoris marito bene merenti Aelia Nice coniux
EDCS-27500130.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Atimetus Ulpius Menophanes, imperial procurator. His wife Aelia Nice made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27500130.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Nice to her well-deserving husband, Atimetus Ulpius Menophanes, who was an imperial procurator (Augusti dispensatoris).

EDCS-27500131.text_edition: Victori Flavi Secundi conspectori Epaphroditus eiusdem
EDCS-27500131.translation: To Victor, inspector of Flavius Secundus. Epaphroditus, of the same.
EDCS-27500131.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Victor, an inspector for Flavius Secundus, made by Epaphroditus, who appears to be associated with the same Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-27500132.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, recording the beginning of the name Lucius Annaeus.

EDCS-27500133.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Anneae An filiae Victorinae pietatis incomparabili vixit annos XX mensem I dies XV Iulius Victor et Annea Sura parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-27500133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Annea, daughter of Annius, Victorina, of incomparable devotion, who lived 20 years, 1 month, 15 days. Julius Victor and Annea Sura, her most unhappy parents, erected this.
EDCS-27500133.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the most unhappy parents, Julius Victor and Annea Sura, to their daughter, Annea Victorina, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-27500134.text_edition: N S TAIVS   Anteroni L S
EDCS-27500134.translation: N. S. Taius to Antero, son of Lucius S.
EDCS-27500134.summary: This fragmented inscription, likely a funerary dedication, records a dedication by N. S. Taius to Antero, son of Lucius S.

EDCS-27500135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Aprilla.
EDCS-27500135.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Aprilla.

EDCS-27500137.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Artorius FeFlicissimus#Felicissimus#FFLICISSIMUS Aemiliae Barbarae coniugi  pientissimae quae vixit mecum annos LVI queius sic  maculam non habui queius sic beneficio me exportavi Salona et ab omnibus meis dunc sic  ---? potestatem facere
EDCS-27500137.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Artorius Felicissimus to Aemilia Barbara, his most devoted wife, who lived with me for 56 years, whose flaw I never found, whose beneficence allowed me to escape Salona and from all my people, while [---] to make power.
EDCS-27500137.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Artorius Felicissimus to his most devoted wife, Aemilia Barbara, reflecting on her character and their life together.

EDCS-27500138.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-27500138.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus made this for himself and his family while living.
EDCS-27500138.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500139.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Claudiae Engenuae sic Horatius Vivianus uxsori sic bene merenti et suis fecit
EDCS-27500139.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Horatius Vivianus made this for Claudia Engenua, his well-deserving wife, and for his own family.
EDCS-27500139.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Horatius Vivianus to his well-deserving wife, Claudia Engenua, and his family.

EDCS-27500140.text_edition: RIOCO filio annorum VI et Hermae annorum IIII
EDCS-27500140.translation: To Rioco, son, 6 years old, and to Herma, 4 years old.
EDCS-27500140.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to two young children, Rioco and Herma, who died at the ages of six and four respectively.

EDCS-27500142.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, recording the beginning of the name Gaius Julius Mar[---].

EDCS-27500143.text_edition: Iuliae Secundinae et Iulio Maservae infelicissimis qui vixerunt annos XXIIII menses IIII dies VIIII Secunda mater filiis infelicissimis
EDCS-27500143.translation: To Julia Secundina and Julius Maserva, most unhappy, who lived 24 years, 4 months, 9 days. Secunda, their mother, to her most unhappy children.
EDCS-27500143.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Secunda, the mother, to her most unhappy children, Julia Secundina and Julius Maserva, who died at a young age.

EDCS-27500144.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, recording the name of Gaius (?) Lusius.

EDCS-27500145.text_edition: Lucius Lusius Severinus Curiae Proculae et Lusiae Proculae et Lucio Lusio Maximo filiis sacrum
EDCS-27500145.translation: Lucius Lusius Severinus, to his children Curia Procula and Lusia Procula, and Lucius Lusius Maximus, sacred.
EDCS-27500145.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Lusius Severinus to his children Curia Procula, Lusia Procula, and Lucius Lusius Maximus.

EDCS-27500146.text_edition: Pomponio Stercorio infanti infelicissimo non clausa in tumulo requiescunt ossa sepulcro istius nunc infantis nec corpus morte sacratum cessit ad infernos
EDCS-27500146.translation: For Pomponius Stercorius, a most unfortunate infant. His bones do not rest enclosed in a tomb, nor has the body of this infant, consecrated by death, descended to the underworld.
EDCS-27500146.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription for an infant, Pomponius Stercorius, expressing the sorrow and the lack of proper burial for the child, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500147.text_edition: Publius Publicius Publi libertus Tychicus se vivo fecit sibi et filiis suis Publio Publicio Restituto et Publio Publicio Germano
EDCS-27500147.translation: Publius Publicius Tychicus, freedman of Publius, made this for himself while alive and for his sons Publius Publicius Restitutus and Publius Publicius Germanus.
EDCS-27500147.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Publius Publicius Tychicus, a freedman, for himself during his lifetime and for his sons Publius Publicius Restitutus and Publius Publicius Germanus.

EDCS-27500148.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Salvio Saturnino marito optimo et Probae libertae et Lusiae Publi filiae Memoriae nnorum XXXII mensium VI Claudia Probilla mater infelicissima filiae pientissimae et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-27500148.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Salvius Saturninus, a most excellent husband, and for Proba, his freedwoman, and for Lusia Memoria, daughter of Publius, who lived 32 years, 6 months. Claudia Probilla, a most unhappy mother, made this for her most devoted daughter and for herself while alive.
EDCS-27500148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed by Claudia Probilla, a grieving mother, for her daughter Lusia Memoria (who lived 32 years and 6 months), her husband Lucius Salvius Saturninus, and her freedwoman Proba, also preparing it for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-27500149.text_edition: Publio Spedio Publi filio Viro Proculo
EDCS-27500149.translation: To Publius Spedius, son of Publius, the worthy Proculus.
EDCS-27500149.summary: This is a funerary or honorary inscription for Publius Spedius Proculus, son of Publius.

EDCS-27500150.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Eutychiano pater infelicissimus qui vixit nnos V menses II dies VIII filio dulcissimo et
EDCS-27500150.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Valerius Eutychianus, a most unhappy father, who lived 5 years, 2 months, 8 days, for his sweetest son, and...
EDCS-27500150.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Eutychianus, made by his grieving father for his sweet son, who lived only 5 years, 2 months, and 8 days.

EDCS-27500158.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27500158.translation: Marturius made this memorial for himself.
EDCS-27500158.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marturius for himself during his lifetime, common in the Roman world.

EDCS-27500159.text_edition: Cereri sanctae Caius Frisius Marinus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-27500159.translation: To holy Ceres, Gaius Frisius Marinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500159.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Ceres, where Gaius Frisius Marinus offers thanks and fulfills a vow, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-27500161.text_edition: Veneri Victrici Augustae sacrum Caius Vesius Cerdo IIIIIIvir TRIB
EDCS-27500161.translation: Sacred to Venus Victrix Augusta, Gaius Vesius Cerdo, sevir (of the Augustales) for the third time, TRIB (?).
EDCS-27500161.summary: This is a religious dedication to Venus Victrix Augusta, made by Gaius Vesius Cerdo, who was a sevir Augustalis (a priest of the imperial cult) for the third time, possibly related to a tribus or military unit.

EDCS-27500162.text_edition: Lucius Riccius Luci filius Velina Pessinunte nnorum LX veteranus Marcia Maxima uxor Lucius Riccius Luci filius Valens Lucius Riccius Luci filius Gallus Riccia Luci filia Bulla hic siti sunt Lucius Atilius Luci filius Velina veteranus legionis VII avunculo suo posuit et sibi
EDCS-27500162.translation: Lucius Riccius, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, from Pessinus, a veteran aged 60. Marcia Maxima, his wife. Lucius Riccius Valens, son of Lucius. Lucius Riccius Gallus, son of Lucius. Riccia Bulla, daughter of Lucius. They are buried here. Lucius Atilius, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, set this up for his uncle and for himself.
EDCS-27500162.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial place of Lucius Riccius, his family, and also commemorating Lucius Atilius, a veteran, who erected the monument for his uncle and himself.

EDCS-27500163.text_edition: Atiliae Quartae libertae karissimae sacrum Publius Atilius Cognatus
EDCS-27500163.translation: Sacred to Atilia Quarta, a most dear freedwoman. Publius Atilius Cognatus.
EDCS-27500163.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Publius Atilius Cognatus to his beloved freedwoman, Atilia Quarta.

EDCS-27500164.text_edition: Caius Feresius Cai filius Tromentina Venustus magister Augustalis poni curavit locus datus decreto decurionum E annorum XCX F RER LI HS CXIII DC
EDCS-27500164.translation: Gaius Feresius Venustus, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, Augustalis magistrate, took care to have this erected. The plot was granted by a decree of the decurions. He lived 90 years. [Illegible] The sum of 113,600 sesterces.
EDCS-27500164.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by Gaius Feresius Venustus, an Augustalis magistrate, with the plot granted by the city council, and mentions a sum of money and his age at death.

EDCS-27500165.text_edition: Caio Masurio Masuriano ex testamento ipsius Cassia Laetina heres
EDCS-27500165.translation: To Gaius Masurius Masurianus, according to his own will. Cassia Laetina, heir.
EDCS-27500165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Masurius Masurianus, erected by his heir, Cassia Laetina, in accordance with his will.

EDCS-27500166.text_edition: Tito Mevio Titi filio Tromentina Celso aedili IIIIviro     Lupercus
EDCS-27500166.translation: To Titus Mevius Celsus, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile, quattuorvir. [Dedicant:] Lupercus.
EDCS-27500166.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Titus Mevius Celsus, a man who held the municipal offices of aedile and quattuorvir, set up by Lupercus.

EDCS-27500167.text_edition: Papiae Luci filiae Cale Caius Cominius Fuscus uxori vivos sic fecit
EDCS-27500167.translation: To Papia Cale, daughter of Lucius. Gaius Cominius Fuscus made this while alive for his wife.
EDCS-27500167.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Cominius Fuscus to his wife, Papia Cale, indicating it was made during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500168.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pomponiae Messillae coniugi karissimae nnorum XXV et Lusiae Proculae coniugi et Lusio Rufino adfini Lucius Caerellius Proculus vivus fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque suis
EDCS-27500168.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Pomponia Messilla, most dear wife, aged 25 years, and to Lusia Procula, wife, and to Lusius Rufinus, relative. Lucius Caerellius Proculus made this while alive for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-27500168.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Caerellius Proculus to his two wives, Pomponia Messilla and Lusia Procula, a relative Lusius Rufinus, and also for himself and his freedpeople, suggesting an Augustan or Julio-Claudian date based on the age of one wife.

EDCS-27500169.text_edition: Caio Vibio Severo IIIIIIviro nnum XXV Caius Vibius Ingenuus pater IIIIIIvir Augustalis Flavialis Titialis Nervialis vivos sic fecit et sibi et Vibiae Rhodope uxori libertae vivae et Caio Vibio Primigenio IIIIIIviro liberto optimo
EDCS-27500169.translation: To Gaius Vibius Severus, sevir, aged 25 years. Gaius Vibius Ingenuus, his father, a sevir Augustalis, Flavialis, Titialis, Nervialis, made this while alive for himself and for Vibius Rhodope, his wife, a freedwoman, while alive, and for Gaius Vibius Primigenius, sevir, best freedman.
EDCS-27500169.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by Gaius Vibius Ingenuus, a distinguished priest of several imperial cults, for his son Gaius Vibius Severus, his wife Vibius Rhodope, and his freedman Gaius Vibius Primigenius, as well as for himself, indicating a date after the Flavian dynasty due to the priestly titles.

EDCS-27500170.summary: This appears to be a fragment of an inscription mentioning a woman named Poplicia Clymene, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-27500172.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Pulchra et Ursus parentes Viperino filio pientissimo posuerunt bene merenti sibi et suis
EDCS-27500172.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Pulchra and Ursus, parents, placed this for their most dutiful son Viperinus, well-deserving, for themselves and their family.
EDCS-27500172.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents, Pulchra and Ursus, for their devoted son Viperinus, and also for themselves and their broader family.

EDCS-27500173.text_edition: Aurelio Veturio decurioni municipii Novensium defuncto nnorum XXX Aurelia Veturia fratri piissimo posuit
EDCS-27500173.translation: To Aurelius Veturius, decurion of the municipality of Novensium, who died at 30 years of age. Aurelia Veturia placed this for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-27500173.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aurelia Veturia dedicating a monument to her brother, Aurelius Veturius, a municipal decurion, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-27500174.text_edition: Dis Manibus Viselliae Paulinae lia Marcellina matri carissimae posuit
EDCS-27500174.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Visellia Paulina, [Aure]lia Marcellina placed this for her dearest mother.
EDCS-27500174.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Aurelia Marcellina dedicates a monument to her mother, Visellia Paulina.

EDCS-27500175.text_edition: Litorea praessus sic scruposae margine rupis inriguus gelido defluit amne latex cuius perspicuo per levia saxa meatu praedulcis salsam perluit unda Thetyn sic indigenis gratus preterlabentibus! almus incola delicias advena laudat opes salve Nymfa sic meos dignata invisere finis sic et celebrem cunctis conciliare locum nostra salutifero tu mactas praedia fonte Licinianus ego carmine te dominus diversum sortita capis finemque caputque Nymfa sic caput cautes terminus unda tibi est quis queat arcanum sapiens pernoscere fontis nasceris e scopulis fons moriture fretis hoc Pelagia suos fontes epigrammate donat magne tui pignus Liciniane tori
EDCS-27500175.translation: Pressed by a stony cliff, a watery stream flows down from the edge of a jagged rock, with its clear current through smooth stones, whose sweet wave washes the salty Tethys. Thus, pleasing to the native inhabitants, the life-giving stranger praises the delights and riches. Hail, Nymph, thus deemed worthy to visit my boundaries, and thus to make this place famous among all! You enrich our properties with your health-giving spring. I, Licinianus, your master, receive you in song, allotted a distinct end and source. Nymph, thus a cliff is your source, and water your end. Who, being wise, can fully know the secret of the spring? You are born from rocks, a spring destined to die in the sea. Pelagia dedicates her springs with this epigram, a great pledge of your marriage, Licinianus.
EDCS-27500175.summary: This is an elaborate poetic inscription, likely for a nymphaeum or sacred spring, dedicated by Licinianus and Pelagia, praising the spring and its Nymph.

EDCS-27500180.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Allius Firminus Ursino filio Cai Septimi Carpophori delicato infelicissimo puero naufragio obito annorum XI cuius membra consumpsit maris sic per se quot nomen titulus praestat suisque dolorem
EDCS-27500180.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Allius Firminus placed this for Ursinus, son of Gaius Septimius Carpophorus, a most unfortunate and beloved boy, who died by shipwreck at the age of 11, whose limbs the sea consumed. Thus, his name provides a title and sorrow to his own.
EDCS-27500180.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Marcus Allius Firminus dedicating a monument to Ursinus, the 11-year-old son of Gaius Septimius Carpophorus, who tragically died in a shipwreck.

EDCS-27500181.text_edition: Dis Manibus Allia M A P ET coniugi IMET P
EDCS-27500181.translation: To the Divine Manes. Allia M. A. P. E. T. and her husband I. M. E. T. P.
EDCS-27500181.summary: This is an incomplete or highly abbreviated funerary inscription, likely placed by Allia for herself and her husband.

EDCS-27500182.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Messilla filio infelicissimo suo qui vixxit sic plus minus annos III Aurelia Messoria titulum posuit
EDCS-27500182.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Messoria placed this title for her most unfortunate son, Aurelius Messilla, who lived for about 3 years.
EDCS-27500182.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aurelia Messoria dedicating a monument to her son, Aurelius Messilla, who lived for approximately three years.

EDCS-27500183.text_edition: Dis Manibus Firminae infelici inconparabili sic uxsori sic Victor posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27500183.translation: To the Divine Manes. Victor placed this for Firmina, his unfortunate and incomparable wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-27500183.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Victor dedicating a monument to his beloved and unfortunate wife, Firmina.

EDCS-27500184.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerius Maximus et Cornelia Vera cum dolum tantum haberent de Valerio delicato quam ut filio infelicissimo iuveni dulcissimo nnorum XXIIII dunc sic dolum haberit sic Cornelia Vendemia de marito Valerio et socero puero pientissimo titulum posuit
EDCS-27500184.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valerius Maximus and Cornelia Vera, having so much grief for Valerius, their beloved and most unfortunate sweetest young son, 24 years old.
