While Cornelia Vendemia thus had grief for her husband Valerius and her most devoted paternal uncle, she placed this title.
EDCS-27500184.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely a shared monument, placed by Valerius Maximus and Cornelia Vera for their 24-year-old son Valerius, and by Cornelia Vendemia for her husband Valerius and her paternal uncle (implicitly the same Valerius, the father).

EDCS-27500185.text_edition: Aurelius Nigrinus Herculi Augusto crateram fecit
EDCS-27500185.translation: Aurelius Nigrinus made a bowl for Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-27500185.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Aurelius Nigrinus making and dedicating a crater (bowl) to Hercules Augustus.

EDCS-27500186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victori qui vixit annos VIIII Valeria Severina et Messor Firmionis delicato in<feli>cissimo
EDCS-27500186.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Victor, who lived 9 years. Valeria Severina and Messor Firmionis [placed this] for their most unfortunate and beloved [son].
EDCS-27500186.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Valeria Severina and Messor Firmionis dedicating a monument to their beloved son, Victor, who lived for nine years.

EDCS-27601564.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Diogeni Athicti filio Diogenes Augusti libertus tabularius provinciae Dalmatiae nepoti posuit
EDCS-27601564.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Diogenes, son of Athictus. Diogenes, freedman of Augustus, tabularius of the province of Dalmatia, set this up for his grandson.
EDCS-27601564.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Diogenes, an Augustan freedman and provincial record-keeper, to his grandson, Diogenes, son of Athictus.

EDCS-27601565.text_edition: Phrygio Auggustorum nnostrorum ab instrumentis defuncto nnorum XXXVIII Lucius Aurelius Castus et Gaius Iulius Honoratus filii patri bene merenti posuerunt et Iuliae Valeriae matri defunctae nnorum XXX
EDCS-27601565.translation: To Phrygio, in charge of instruments for our Augusti, who died at 38 years old. Lucius Aurelius Castus and Gaius Julius Honoratus, sons, set this up for their well-deserving father, and for Julia Valeria, their mother, who died at 30 years old.
EDCS-27601565.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Lucius Aurelius Castus and Gaius Julius Honoratus for their father Phrygio, an imperial servant, and their mother Julia Valeria.

EDCS-27601566.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintiano vernae Augusti vilico et arcario XX hereditatium Iulia Helpis coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27601566.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julia Helpis set this up for her well-deserving husband Quintianus, imperial slave born in the household, overseer and treasurer of the 20th inheritance tax.
EDCS-27601566.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Julia Helpis for her husband Quintianus, an imperial slave who served as an overseer and treasurer of the 20th inheritance tax.

EDCS-27601567.text_edition: Dis Manibus Thaumasto Augusti conmentariensi sic aurariarum Delmatarum felicissimus dispensator titulum posuit
EDCS-27601567.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Thaumastus, imperial clerk, most fortunate financial agent of the Dalmatian gold mines, set up this monument.
EDCS-27601567.summary: This funerary monument was set up by Thaumastus, an imperial clerk and financial agent for the Dalmatian gold mines, for himself.

EDCS-27601569.text_edition: Publio Aelio Lupo beneficiario consularis et Vibiae Forti Aelia Tethis coniugi et filiae suis bene merenti et Aeliae Ursillae filiae nnorum XXV
EDCS-27601569.translation: To Publius Aelius Lupus, beneficiary of the consular, and to Vibia Fortis. Aelia Tethis set this up for her well-deserving husband and daughter, and for Aelia Ursilla, her daughter, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-27601569.summary: This inscription honors Publius Aelius Lupus, a consular's beneficiary, and his wife Vibia Fortis and daughter Aelia Ursilla, erected by Aelia Tethis, likely the mother and wife.

EDCS-27601570.text_edition: Aelio Ursacio militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum qui vixit annis XXV mensibus VIIII marito pientissimo posuit
EDCS-27601570.translation: To Aelius Ursacius, soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, who lived 25 years and 9 months. His most devoted wife set this up for her husband.
EDCS-27601570.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a devoted wife for her husband, Aelius Ursacius, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers.

EDCS-27601571.text_edition: Aurelio Apollinari veterano Aurelius Delmatius filius patri pientissimo
EDCS-27601571.translation: To Aurelius Apollinaris, veteran. Aurelius Delmatius, son, set this up for his most devoted father.
EDCS-27601571.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Delmatius for his father, Aurelius Apollinaris, a veteran.

EDCS-27601572.text_edition: Tito Aurelio Apollonio fratri eius |(centurioni) cohortis I |(milliariae) defuncto Sirmi nnorum XXXIII Aurelius Aquila pater infelicissimus vivus fecit et  Aureli sic FElavi sic#Flavi sic#ELAVI negotiantis sic natione Suri sic defuncto annorum LV Sirmi et  Aurelio Luciano defuncto annorum amico eorum merenti
EDCS-27601572.translation: To his brother Titus Aurelius Apollonius, centurion of the 1st cohort (milliaria), who died at Sirmium at 33 years old. Aurelius Aquila, most unhappy father, made this in his lifetime, and for Aurelius Flavus, a merchant of Syrian nationality, who died at Sirmium at 55 years old, and for Aurelius Lucianus, who died at years old, their well-deserving friend.
EDCS-27601572.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelius Aquila for his brother Titus Aurelius Apollonius, a centurion, for Aurelius Flavus, a Syrian merchant, and for Aurelius Lucianus, a friend, indicating a complex family and social dedication.

EDCS-27601573.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Eutichiano sic infelicissimo ex cursore pravato sic qui confecit sub die milia XCIV qui vixit annos XXV menses duos dies VIIII Aurelius Graecio fratri pientissimo hanc memoriam posuit
EDCS-27601573.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To the most unfortunate Aurelius Eutychianus, an abused courier, who covered 94,000 miles in one day, and lived 25 years, two months, and nine days. Aurelius Graecio set up this memorial for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-27601573.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Graecio for his brother, Aurelius Eutychianus, an ill-fated courier who accomplished an extraordinary journey before his death at a young age.

EDCS-27601574.text_edition: Aurelius Martialis miles legionis I Italici sic stipendiorum XXVII et Aelia coniux aeius sic et Martiali filio eorum uno fato decepti Disdozi frater et heres eorum posuit
EDCS-27601574.translation: Aurelius Martialis, soldier of the 1st Italic Legion, with 27 years of service, and Aelia his wife, and Martialis their son, having been struck by a single fate. Disdozus, their brother and heir, set this up.
EDCS-27601574.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Disdozus, the brother and heir, for Aurelius Martialis, a soldier, his wife Aelia, and their son Martialis, who all died together.

EDCS-27601575.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Mucatrae militi legionis I Italicae stipendiorum XIIII Aurelius Sabasianus miles legionis supra scriptae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27601575.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Mucatra, soldier of the First Italian Legion, with fourteen years of service. Aurelius Sabasianus, soldier of the aforesaid legion, placed this for his well-deserving comrade.
EDCS-27601575.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a soldier to his well-deserving comrade, a soldier of the First Italian Legion, who served for fourteen years.

EDCS-27601576.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaio Baebidio Marcello militi cohortis III Alpinorum vexillario equitum defuncto nnorum XXIII Anna alumno infelicissimo titulum posuit
EDCS-27601576.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Baebidius Marcellus, soldier of the Third Alpine Cohort, standard-bearer of the cavalry, who died at 23 years of age. Anna placed this monument for her most unfortunate foster-child.
EDCS-27601576.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young cavalry standard-bearer of the Third Alpine Cohort, dedicated by Anna to her foster-child.

EDCS-27601577.text_edition: Marcus Blaesius Tertullus miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis Papiriae Pyrallidi bene merenti hospitae suae annorum XXV de suo fecit
EDCS-27601577.translation: Marcus Blaesius Tertullus, soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, made this out of his own funds for Papiria Pyrallis, his well-deserving hostess, aged 25.
EDCS-27601577.summary: This is an inscription dedicated by a soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion to his hostess, Papiria Pyrallis, who died at age 25.

EDCS-27601578.text_edition: Lucius Caesius Luci filius Camilia Bassus domo Pisauri veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis annorum LIII stipendiorum XXXII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres posuit  in fronte pedes VI in agro pedes X
EDCS-27601578.translation: Lucius Caesius Bassus, son of Lucius, of the Camilia tribe, from Pisaurum, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, aged 53, with 32 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will. His heir placed it. Six feet in front, ten feet in depth.
EDCS-27601578.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caesius Bassus, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, placed by his heir according to his will, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-27601579.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tiberio Claudio Ianuario speculatori legionis XIIII geminae commentariensi consularis provinciae Delmatiae stipendiorum XIII Aebutia Ianuaria filio piissimo et Visellia Iulia marito
EDCS-27601579.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, 'speculator' of the Fourteenth Gemina Legion, 'commentariensis' of the provincial governor of Dalmatia, with thirteen years of service. Aebutia Ianuaria [placed this] for her most dutiful son, and Visellia Iulia for her husband.
EDCS-27601579.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, a soldier and administrative assistant to the governor of Dalmatia, dedicated by his mother and wife.

EDCS-27601580.text_edition: Cloutius Clutami filius duplicarius alae nnum Susarrus domo Curunniace annorum XXXV stipendiorum XI- hic situs est posuit CA
EDCS-27601580.translation: Cloutius, son of Clutamus, a duplicarius of the alae [---] Susarrus, from Curunniace, aged 35, with eleven years of service, is buried here. CA placed this.
EDCS-27601580.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cloutius, a cavalry soldier of auxiliary forces, who died at age 35 after eleven years of service, placed by an individual whose name is partially preserved.

EDCS-27601581.text_edition: Lucius Cornelius Luci fE(ilius)#f(ilius)#E Maecia Pelagonia vetR(eranus)#vet(eranus)#VER legionis XI nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est filii posuerunt
EDCS-27601581.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius, of the Maecia tribe, Pelagonia, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, aged 45, with 25 years of service, is buried here. His sons placed this.
EDCS-27601581.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, placed by his sons.

EDCS-27601583.text_edition: Caius Curiatius Titi filius Sergia Secundus domo Augusta Troade veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis scriba Salonis nnum LXV hic situs est in fronte pedes  in agro pedes XX
EDCS-27601583.translation: Gaius Curiatius Secundus, son of Titus, of the Sergia tribe, from Augusta Troas, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, a scribe at Salona, aged 65, is buried here. In front [---] feet, in depth 20 feet.
EDCS-27601583.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Curiatius Secundus, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion and a scribe, specifying the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-27601584.text_edition: Lucius Fabius Luci filius Saturninus veteranus legionis VII pia Claudiae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Tiberi Claudi Alypi liberti Caesaris libertae Euche hic siti sunt locum donavit Caius Titius Restitutus amicus hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-27601584.translation: Lucius Fabius Saturninus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, made this for himself and for Claudia Euche, freedwoman of Tiberius Claudius Alypus, freedman of Caesar, while he was alive. They are buried here. Gaius Titius Restitutus, a friend, donated the plot. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-27601584.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared during the lifetime of Lucius Fabius Saturninus, a veteran, for himself and his freedwoman, located on a plot donated by a friend, explicitly stating it is not inheritable.

EDCS-27601585.text_edition: Tito Flavio Titi filio Tromentina Agricolae decurioni coloniae Salonitanae aedili IIviro iure dicundo decurioni coloniae Aequitatis IIviro quinquennali dispunctori municipii Riditarum praefecto et patrono collegii fabrum ob merita eius collegium fabrum ex aere conlato curatori rei publicae Splonisstarum tribuno legionis X geminae piae fidelis  Tito Vettio Augustali decurioni coloniae Salonitanae quaestori aedili IIviro iure dicundo praefecto et patrono collegii fabrum ob merita eius collegium fabrum ex aere conlato
EDCS-27601585.translation: To Titus Flavius Agricola, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, decurion of the colony of Salona, aedile, duumvir with judicial power, decurion of the colony of Aequitas, quinquennial duumvir, dispunctor of the municipium of Riditae, prefect and patron of the guild of craftsmen, on account of his merits, the guild of craftsmen, from collected funds [placed this]; curator of the republic of Splonistae, tribune of the Tenth Gemina Legion, loyal and faithful. To Titus Vettius Augustalis, decurion of the colony of Salona, quaestor, aedile, duumvir with judicial power, prefect and patron of the guild of craftsmen, on account of his merits, the guild of craftsmen, from collected funds [placed this].
EDCS-27601585.summary: This is a public inscription honoring two prominent citizens, Titus Flavius Agricola and Titus Vettius Augustalis, both holding numerous municipal and military offices, dedicated by the guild of craftsmen from collected funds in recognition of their merits.

EDCS-27601586.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Caroso veterano Attigia Ursacia compatri benignissimo
EDCS-27601586.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Flavius Carosus, veteran. Attigia Ursacia, his very kind compatriot.
EDCS-27601586.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a veteran named Flavius Carosus by his compatriot, Attigia Ursacia.

EDCS-27601587.text_edition: Lucio Praecilio Luci filio Clementi Iuliano pontifici nni designato flamini patrono coloniae primipilari legionis V Macedonicae praefecto castrorum legionis eiusdem veterani qui militaverunt sub Publio Memmio Regulo legato Augusti et missi sunt Quinto FEutetio#Futetio#EUTETIO Lusio Saturnino Marco Seio Verano consulibus curam gerentibus Tiberio Claudio Celso Tiberi Augusti COMBIO Marco Tauritio Marcellino locus datus publice
EDCS-27601587.translation: To Lucius Praecilius, son of Lucius, Clemens Iulianus, designated pontiff, flamen, patron of the colony, chief centurion of the V Macedonian Legion, prefect of the camp of the same legion. Veterans who served under Publius Memmius Regulus, legate of Augustus, and were discharged when Quintus Eutetius Lusius Saturninus and Marcus Seius Veranus were consuls. With Tiberius Claudius Celsus, son of Tiberius, COMBIO, Marcus Tauritius Marcellinus overseeing, the place was granted publicly.
EDCS-27601587.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Praecilius Clemens Iulianus, detailing his distinguished military and civic career, and records that a burial plot was publicly granted to him by veterans under the supervision of local officials.

EDCS-27601588.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Pomponiano |(centurioni) legionis II Traianae fortis |(centurioni) legionis IIII Flaviae |(centurioni) legionis XII fulminatae |(centurioni) legionis XVI Flaviae |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae |(centurioni) legionis II TraO(ianae)#Tra(ianae)#TRO fortis heredes
EDCS-27601588.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titus Flavius Pomponianus, centurion of the II Traiana Fortis Legion, centurion of the IIII Flavia Legion, centurion of the XII Fulminata Legion, centurion of the XVI Flavia Legion, centurion of the XIIII Gemina Martia Legion, centurion of the II Traiana Fortis Legion. His heirs [erected this].
EDCS-27601588.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his heirs to Titus Flavius Pomponianus, a career centurion who served in numerous legions.

EDCS-27601589.translation: RM Herennius, of the I Italica Legion.
EDCS-27601589.summary: This brief inscription likely identifies a member of the I Italica Legion named Herennius, possibly from a funerary monument.

EDCS-27601590.text_edition: Quinto Hortensio Quinti filio veterano legionis VII hic situs est Hortensia Quinti filia Quarta et Lucius Corellius Iucundus patri posuere
EDCS-27601590.translation: To Quintus Hortensius, son of Quintus, veteran of the VII Legion, here he lies. Hortensia Quarta, daughter of Quintus, and Lucius Corellius Iucundus set this up for their father.
EDCS-27601590.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Hortensius, a veteran of the VII Legion, erected by his daughter Hortensia Quarta and Lucius Corellius Iucundus.

EDCS-27601591.text_edition: Diophantus Alexandri filius de liburna Murena vixit annos XXXVIII militavit annos XVI hic situs
EDCS-27601591.translation: Diophantus, son of Alexander, from the Murena liburna. He lived 38 years, served 16 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-27601591.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Diophantus, a sailor who served on a liburna (a type of warship) named Murena, indicating his age and length of service.

EDCS-27601592.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Marci filius Voltinia Paternus Aquis Sextiis miles legionis VI victricis |(centurio) legionis VIII Augustae |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis |(centurio) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis testamento fieri iussit ex HS Xdecem milibus Iulia Titi filia Maxima uxor et Marcus Iulius Marci libertus Docimus heredes faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-27601592.translation: Marcus Iulius, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe, Paternus, from Aquae Sextiae, soldier of the VI Victrix Legion, centurion of the VIII Augusta Legion, centurion of the XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix Legion, centurion of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion. He ordered this to be made by testament from ten thousand sesterces. Iulia Maxima, daughter of Titus, his wife, and Marcus Iulius Docimus, his freedman and heir, took care of the execution.
EDCS-27601592.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius Paternus, a career centurion from Aquae Sextiae, whose monument was erected by his wife and freedman according to his will.

EDCS-27601593.text_edition: Tito Laelio Titi filio Severo |(centurioni) legionis III Cyrenaicae
EDCS-27601593.translation: To Titus Laelius Severus, son of Titus, centurion of the III Cyrenaica Legion.
EDCS-27601593.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Titus Laelius Severus, a centurion of the III Cyrenaica Legion, likely a funerary dedication or an honorific monument.

EDCS-27601594.text_edition: Caius Lucretius signifer legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona annorum XLV stipendiorum XXVI de suo peculio faciendum iussit sibi et Primo suo curam egerunt frater et Chrestus libertus in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XX
EDCS-27601594.translation: Gaius Lucretius, standard-bearer of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, from Verona, 45 years old, 26 years of service. He ordered this to be made from his own funds for himself and his Primus. His brother and his freedman Chrestus took care of it. 10 feet in front, 20 feet in depth.
EDCS-27601594.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Gaius Lucretius, a standard-bearer of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, for himself and his Primus, and erected by his brother and freedman.

EDCS-27601595.text_edition: Caius Lucretius veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona vivus fecit sibi et Provinciali filio et Firmillae et Tyche libertae suisque omnibus in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XX
EDCS-27601595.translation: Gaius Lucretius, veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, from Verona, made this during his lifetime for himself and his son Provincialis, and for Firmilla and Tyche, his freedwomen, and all his people. 10 feet in front, 20 feet in depth.
EDCS-27601595.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Lucretius, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, during his lifetime for himself, his son, his freedwomen, and his entire household.

EDCS-27601596.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Masclinio Romulo veterano integerrimo  GERENTI AVTORITE nnorum
EDCS-27601596.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius Masclinius Romulus, a most upright veteran, holding authority, of [---] years.
EDCS-27601596.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Masclinius Romulus, a veteran who held some position of authority, likely set up by his family or associates, dating to the Roman period.

EDCS-27601597.text_edition: XII bixit sic annos XXX Caius Memmius Hierax optio fratri bene merente sic posuit
EDCS-27601597.translation: He lived 12 [years], thus he lived 30 years. Gaius Memmius Hierax, an optio, thus placed this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-27601597.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Gaius Memmius Hierax, an optio (military officer), for his brother, who lived to be 30 years old, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601598.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio P Ver militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum annorum XVIIII Helius domino bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27601598.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius P. Ver[---], a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, 19 years old. Helius placed this for his well-deserving master.
EDCS-27601598.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Helius to his master, Gaius P. Ver[---], a 19-year-old soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601599.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Sabinio Niciati equiti ex singularibus homini verecundissimo stipendiorum XXV Flavia Tertia coniugi inconparabili sic cum quo vixit nnos XXV bene merenti
EDCS-27601599.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius Sabinius Niciatus, a horseman from the Imperial Horse Guards, a most modest man, with 25 years of service. Flavia Tertia placed this for her incomparable husband, with whom she lived for 25 years, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27601599.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Flavia Tertia for her husband, Gaius Sabinius Niciatus, a modest horseman of the Imperial Horse Guards with 25 years of service, with whom she lived for 25 years.

EDCS-27601600.text_edition: Titus Sabinius Titi filius Fabia domo Sebaste veteranus legionis VII nnorum LVI stipendiorum XXXII hic situs est
EDCS-27601600.translation: Titus Sabinius, son of Titus, of the Fabian tribe, from Sebaste, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, 56 years old, with 32 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-27601600.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Titus Sabinius, a 56-year-old veteran of the Seventh Legion from Sebaste, who served 32 years.

EDCS-27601601.text_edition: Quinto Servilio Marci filio Palatina Pacuviano aedili praefecto cohortis I Morinorum Marcus Servilius Copiensis Pacuvianus pater fecit et sibi
EDCS-27601601.translation: To Quintus Servilius Pacuvianus, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe, aedile, prefect of the First Cohort of Morini. Marcus Servilius Copiensis Pacuvianus, his father, made this for him and for himself.
EDCS-27601601.summary: This inscription records a monument built by Marcus Servilius Copiensis Pacuvianus for his son, Quintus Servilius Pacuvianus, who served as an aedile and prefect of the First Cohort of Morini, and also for himself.

EDCS-27601602.text_edition: Tito Statilio Maximo militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum adiutori cornicularii consularis defuncto annorum XXV Iulia Fructula hospita et sibi
EDCS-27601602.translation: To Titus Statilius Maximus, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, assistant to the governor's horn-blower, deceased at 25 years old. Julia Fructula, his hostess, [placed this] for him and for herself.
EDCS-27601602.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Julia Fructula, his hostess, to Titus Statilius Maximus, a 25-year-old soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers who served as an assistant to a consular horn-blower, and also for herself.

EDCS-27601603.text_edition: Titi Titiseni Convictoriani decurionis coloniae Salonitanae sacerdotis Titus Titisenus Valentinus |(centurio) legionis filius
EDCS-27601603.translation: To Titus Titisenus Convictorianus, decurion of the colony of Salona, priest. Titus Titisenus Valentinus, centurion of the legion, his son [made this].
EDCS-27601603.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Titus Titisenus Convictorianus, a decurion and priest of Salona, by his son, Titus Titisenus Valentinus, a legionary centurion.

EDCS-27601604.text_edition: Caio Valerio  veterano legionis XI pia fidelis Valerius I patrono
EDCS-27601604.translation: To Gaius Valerius [---], veteran of the Eleventh Legion, 'Pia Fidelis'. Valerius I. [---] to his patron.
EDCS-27601604.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Valerius, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, 'Pia Fidelis', likely set up by Valerius I. [---] to his patron.

EDCS-27601605.text_edition: Gaio Valerio Gai filio Claudia Marcello III militi cohortis
EDCS-27601605.translation: To Gaius Valerius Marcellus, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, III [---], soldier of the Cohort [---].
EDCS-27601605.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Valerius Marcellus, a soldier of a Roman cohort, whose exact military rank or unit is partially missing.

EDCS-27601606.text_edition: Lucio Vario Luci filio Sergia  domo Augusta Praetoria legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis |(centurio) cohortis VI vigilum Lucius Varius Laetus Pu et Benniae Sabinae vivae  in fronte pedes [in agro pedes &
EDCS-27601606.translation: To Lucius Varius, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, from Augusta Praetoria, centurion of the Sixth Cohort of Vigiles, of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful. Lucius Varius Laetus and Bennia Sabina (set this up) for themselves, while living. In front, X feet; in the field, X feet.
EDCS-27601606.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Varius Laetus and Bennia Sabina for Lucius Varius, a centurion of the Cohort of Vigiles and the Legion XI Claudia, presumably during the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27601607.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titus Varronius Maro frumentIarius#frumentarius#FRUMENIARIUS legionis III Quirenaricae sic qui cucurrit fTrumentarius#frumentarius#TRUM nnos XL et centurio frumentariorum factus modo Firminus libertus eius posuit locus concessus
EDCS-27601607.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Titus Varronius Maro, a grain-supplier of the Third Legion 'Cyrenaica', who worked as a grain-supplier for 40 years and was recently made a centurion of grain-suppliers. Firminus, his freedman, placed this. The plot was granted.
EDCS-27601607.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead by Firminus, a freedman, for his patron Titus Varronius Maro, a veteran grain-supplier and centurion of the Legio III Cyrenaica, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27601608.text_edition: Virdomarus Thartontis filius domo Biturix missicius alae Claudiae novae testamento fieri iussit et Pamae sorori
EDCS-27601608.translation: Virdomarus, son of Thartontis, from Biturix, a discharged soldier of the new Claudian ala, ordered this to be made by will, also for his sister Pama.
EDCS-27601608.summary: This is a funerary inscription ordered by will by Virdomarus, a discharged cavalryman from the new Claudian ala, for himself and his sister Pama, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27601609.text_edition: Marco Uttedio Sallubiano Caio Petilio Amando domo Iguvio veterano legionis XIIII geminae decurioni coloniae Salonitanae quaestori pontifici Petilia Secundina coniux
EDCS-27601609.translation: To Marcus Uttedius Sallubianus, Caius Petilius Amandus, from Iguvium, a veteran of the Fourteenth Legion 'Gemina', decurion of the colony of Salona, quaestor, pontiff. Petilia Secundina, his wife (set this up).
EDCS-27601609.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Petilia Secundina for her husband, Marcus Uttedius Sallubianus, a veteran of Legio XIIII Gemina and a prominent municipal official in Salona, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27601610.text_edition: Zosime Statilius Pulcher miles cohortis I Belgarum ex stratore consularis uxori bene merenti duplicarius
EDCS-27601610.translation: Zosimus, a duplicarius and soldier of the First Cohort of Belgae, formerly a consular groom, (set this up) for his deserving wife Statilia Pulchra.
EDCS-27601610.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Zosimus, a soldier and duplicarius of Cohors I Belgarum, for his wife Statilia Pulchra, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27601612.text_edition: Pomptina Arretio miles legionis VII anniorum VM stipendiorum IX hic situs
EDCS-27601612.translation: Pomptina Arretio, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 5 years old, with 9 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-27601612.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pomptina Arretio, a soldier of Legio VII, containing a likely scribal error or unusual naming convention given the recorded age and years of service, presumably from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27601621.text_edition: Titus Fuficius Cai filius Pollia veteranus legionis XX  Titus Fuficius Titi libertus Privatus Fuficia |(mulieris) liberta Prisca Fuficia Titi liberta Prima
EDCS-27601621.translation: Titus Fuficius, son of Caius, of the Pollian tribe, a veteran of the Twentieth Legion. Titus Fuficius Privatus, freedman of Titus. Fuficia Prisca, freedwoman of a woman. Fuficia Prima, freedwoman of Titus.
EDCS-27601621.summary: This is likely a funerary or commemorative inscription mentioning Titus Fuficius, a veteran of Legio XX, along with several of his freedmen and freedwomen, suggesting a family monument from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27601625.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27601625.text_edition: Maurentius fabricensis una cum Concordia uxore sua sarcofacum sic vivi sibi ordinaverunt
EDCS-27601625.translation: Maurentius, a craftsman, together with his wife Concordia, ordered this sarcophagus for themselves while living.
EDCS-27601625.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a sarcophagus, commissioned by Maurentius, a craftsman, and his wife Concordia for themselves during their lifetimes, likely from the 2nd to 4th century AD.

EDCS-27601630.text_edition: Locho Augusti dispensatori DelImatiae#Delmatiae#DEIMATIAE Himer Augusti dispensator Moesiae patri piissimo
EDCS-27601630.translation: To Locho, imperial dispensor of Dalmatia. Himer, imperial dispensor of Moesia, (set this up) for his most devoted father.
EDCS-27601630.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription, set up by Himer, an imperial dispensor in Moesia, for his father Locho, who was an imperial dispensor in Dalmatia, indicating a family of imperial freedmen holding administrative posts, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27601632.text_edition: Faniae Maximae innocentissimae puellae quae vixit nnos II menses VIII Aelius Martinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae dulcissimae
EDCS-27601632.translation: To Fania Maxima, an most innocent girl, who lived 2 years, 8 months. Aelius Martinus, a beneficiarius consularis of the First Italic Legion, (set this up for his) sweetest (daughter).
EDCS-27601632.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fania Maxima, a young girl who died at two years and eight months old, set up by her father Aelius Martinus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Italica, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27601633.translation: ius Firmus, son of, from the home of Ar[---]
EDCS-27601633.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, recording part of a name and an origin.

EDCS-27601634.text_edition: SeFsti#Sesti#SFSTI Nepotis veteFrani#veterani#VETFRANI ex signifero Aurelia Calliste marito pii=Essimo#piissimo#PESSIMO sibi
EDCS-27601634.translation: To Sextus Nepos, veteran, former standard-bearer. Aurelia Calliste placed this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-27601634.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Calliste to her deceased husband, Sextus Nepos, a veteran and former standard-bearer.

EDCS-27601635.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anthistia Anterotis Publio Domitio Potenti coniugi bene merenti posuit veterano cohortis III Alpinorum in suo
EDCS-27601635.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Anthistia Anterotis placed this for Publius Domitius Potens, her well-deserving husband, a veteran of the Third Alpine Cohort, on his own plot.
EDCS-27601635.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Anthistia Anterotis for her husband, Publius Domitius Potens, a veteran of the Cohors III Alpinorum.

EDCS-27601636.text_edition: Aurelius Priscus optio equitum singularium Aurelio Prisco filio infelicissimo qui vixit nnos II menses VIII
EDCS-27601636.translation: Aurelius Priscus, optio of the imperial mounted bodyguard, to his most unfortunate son, Aurelius Priscus, who lived 2 years, 8 months.
EDCS-27601636.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aurelius Priscus, an optio of the equites singulares Augusti, for his young, deceased son, also named Aurelius Priscus.

EDCS-27601637.text_edition: Co=QVeliae#Coeliae#QVELIE Calpurniae quae vixit annos XXVI Aurelius Vitalis centurio legionis V Macedonicae maritus bene merenti posuit Coeliae Calpurniae sorori in hac etiam Aeliae Pomponiae locum concessit Iulia Vettia amica
EDCS-27601637.translation: To Coelia Calpurnia, who lived 26 years. Aurelius Vitalis, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, her well-deserving husband, placed this. Julia Vettia, a friend, also granted a place for Aelia Pomponia here.
EDCS-27601637.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Vitalis, a centurion of Legio V Macedonica, to his wife Coelia Calpurnia, with an additional grant of burial space for Aelia Pomponia by Julia Vettia.

EDCS-27601638.text_edition: Caius Valerius Marcellinus eques cohortis III Alpinorum turmae
EDCS-27601638.translation: Gaius Valerius Marcellinus, horseman of the Third Alpine Cohort, of the troop [---]
EDCS-27601638.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, identifying Gaius Valerius Marcellinus as a cavalryman of the Cohors III Alpinorum.

EDCS-27601639.text_edition: Lucius Tettennius Titi filius StIellatina#Stellatina#SIE Pansa veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis hic situs est
EDCS-27601639.translation: Lucius Tettennius Pansa, son of Titus, of the Stellatina tribe, a veteran of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, lies here.
EDCS-27601639.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Lucius Tettennius Pansa, a veteran of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-27601640.text_edition: Marcus Valerius -- Rufus veteranus  militavit SP testamento posuit heredes faciendum curaverunt in fronte pedes X? in agro pedes
EDCS-27601640.translation: Marcus Valerius [---] Rufus, veteran [---] served. By his testament, he placed this. His heirs saw to its construction. In front, 10 feet; in depth, [---] feet.
EDCS-27601640.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Rufus, a veteran, erected by his heirs according to his will, also specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-27700003.text_edition: Lucio Aemilio Martialis sic Aemilia Bauce posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27700003.translation: To Lucius Aemilius Martialis. Aemilia Bauce placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700003.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aemilia Bauce to Lucius Aemilius Martialis.

EDCS-27700004.text_edition: Caius Aemilius Theophilus vivos sic fecit sibi et Flaviae Hecyoni concubinae defunctae nnum XX Caius Aemilius Theophilus posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27700004.translation: Gaius Aemilius Theophilus made this while alive for himself and for Flavia Hecyone, his deceased concubine, who lived 20 years. Gaius Aemilius Theophilus placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700004.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Aemilius Theophilus for himself and for his deceased concubine, Flavia Hecyone.

EDCS-27700005.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintus Aeronius Adiutor et Aeronia Delicia Quinti liberta fecerunt se vivi posterisque suis hoc monumentum heredem non s(equetur)
EDCS-27700005.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Aeronius Adiutor and Aeronia Delicia, freedwoman of Quintus, made this monument for themselves while living and for their descendants. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-27700005.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Quintus Aeronius Adiutor and his freedwoman Aeronia Delicia to themselves and their descendants, explicitly stating it is not for an heir, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700006.text_edition: L D D Marci Albini Raspaleti domum perpetuam hanc sibi et suis posteris anno XXVIII aetatis suae
EDCS-27700006.translation: L(ocus) D(atus) D(ecreto) Marci Albini Raspaleti, his perpetual home, for himself and his descendants, in the 28th year of his age.
EDCS-27700006.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a perpetual burial plot by Marcus Albinus Raspaletus for himself and his descendants, at the age of 28, indicating a funerary monument.

EDCS-27700007.text_edition: Caio Albucio Trophimo Caius Albucius Vitalis collibertus bene merenti
EDCS-27700007.translation: To Gaius Albucius Trophimus. Gaius Albucius Vitalis, his fellow freedman, to his well-deserving colleague.
EDCS-27700007.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Albucius Vitalis to Gaius Albucius Trophimus, his well-deserving fellow freedman, suggesting a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-27700008.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a personal name on a funerary marker or an object.

EDCS-27700009.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Anchario Abellioni defuncto annorum XV Iulia Marcina mater filio infelicissimo
EDCS-27700009.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Ancharius Abellio, deceased at 15 years. Julia Marcina, his mother, to her most unfortunate son.
EDCS-27700009.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from Julia Marcina, dedicating a monument to her deceased son, Titus Ancharius Abellio, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-27700010.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anniae defunctae annorum XXV dominus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700010.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Annia, deceased at 25 years. Her master placed this for his well-deserving servant.
EDCS-27700010.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Annia, aged 25, erected by her master for his deserving servant, suggesting a tombstone from the Imperial era.

EDCS-27700011.text_edition: Publius Anteius Secundinus et Titia Artemisia eius vivi sibi posuerunt
EDCS-27700011.translation: Publius Anteius Secundinus and Titia Artemisia, his wife, placed this for themselves while living.
EDCS-27700011.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by Publius Anteius Secundinus and his wife Titia Artemisia for their future use while they were still alive.

EDCS-27700012.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anteiae Publi filiae Crispinae cineribus sacrum  Τρίτος Δαματρίου Δαμάτριος Τρίτου Δάμων Τρίτου Δάμων Δαματρίου
EDCS-27700012.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sacred to the ashes of Anteia Crispina, daughter of Publius. Tritus son of Damatrius, Damatrius son of Tritus, Damon son of Tritus, Damon son of Damatrius.
EDCS-27700012.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Anteia Crispina, daughter of Publius, with an added list of several Greek names, possibly indicating shared family members or individuals involved in the monument's creation.

EDCS-27700013.text_edition: Antonius Anicetus et Verginia Marcellina parentes infelicissimi Antoniae
EDCS-27700013.translation: Antonius Anicetus and Verginia Marcellina, most unfortunate parents, to Antonia.
EDCS-27700013.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Antonius Anicetus and Verginia Marcellina, expressing their grief for their daughter Antonia.

EDCS-27700014.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonia Primitiva et Antonia Eutychia et Publio Antonio Restituto bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-27700014.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antonia Primitiva and Antonia Eutychia placed this for well-deserving Publius Antonius Restitutus.
EDCS-27700014.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Primitiva and Antonia Eutychia to the well-deserving Publius Antonius Restitutus, likely a family member.

EDCS-27700015.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoniae De filiae Severae
EDCS-27700015.translation: To the Divine Manes of Antonia De, daughter of Severa.
EDCS-27700015.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Antonia De, daughter of Severa, likely erected by a relative or guardian.

EDCS-27700016.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aprofennio Magno defuncto nnorum XXIII Aprofennia Flora liberta bene merenti
EDCS-27700016.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aprophennius Magnus, who died at 23 years of age. Aprofennia Flora, a freedwoman, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700016.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aprofennia Flora, a freedwoman, for Aprophennius Magnus, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-27700017.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aquesillae annorum III Melissa mater posuit
EDCS-27700017.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aquesilla, three years old. Melissa, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-27700017.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Melissa, the mother, for her three-year-old daughter Aquesilla.

EDCS-27700018.text_edition: Dis Manibus Arethusae nnorum II mensium VIII dierum IX
EDCS-27700018.translation: To the Divine Manes of Arethusa, two years, eight months, nine days old.
EDCS-27700018.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arethusa, who died at the very young age of two years, eight months, and nine days.

EDCS-27700019.text_edition: Lucius Arrius SaeculariEs#Saecularis#SECLARES Protiani fratri pientissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700019.translation: Lucius Arrius Saecularis placed this for Protianus, his most devoted and well-deserving brother.
EDCS-27700019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Arrius Saecularis to his deceased brother Protianus.

EDCS-27700020.text_edition: Atilius Marcellus Atilio Luciano filio infelicissimo qui vixit nnos II menses II posuit
EDCS-27700020.translation: Atilius Marcellus placed this for Atilius Lucianus, his most unfortunate son, who lived for two years and two months.
EDCS-27700020.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Atilius Marcellus for his son, Atilius Lucianus, who died at the age of two years and two months.

EDCS-27700021.text_edition: Atiliae Quintinae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit nnos XXVIII menses X dies X Clodius Salutaris maritus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700021.translation: To Atilia Quintina, his most devoted wife, who lived 28 years, 10 months, 10 days. Clodius Salutaris, her husband, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700021.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Clodius Salutaris to his most devoted wife, Atilia Quintina, who lived for 28 years, 10 months, and 10 days.

EDCS-27700023.text_edition: Marcus Attius Marci libertus Faustus vivus fecit sibi et Attiae Marci libertae Secundae coniugi sanctissimae et Marco Attio Marci filio Crispo et Marco Attio Marci filio Secundo coniugis amissae lacrumas satis esse putavi me fudisse meae vimque tulisse deum et tuleram graviter sed nunc et solus et orbus amissis natis maxima damna fleo et testor superos patri quae ponere nati debuerant natis ponere dona patrem  Attia Marci liberta Ampliata nnum XXIII hic sita est florente aetate depressere veneficiae mensesque quinque et annum cum aegrotaverit abreptam aetate in inferi Ditis specus hunc titulum posuit Faustus libertae suae
EDCS-27700023.translation: Marcus Attius Faustus, freedman of Marcus, made this while alive for himself, and for Attia Secunda, freedwoman of Marcus, his most holy wife, and for Marcus Attius Crispus, son of Marcus, and for Marcus Attius Secundus, son of Marcus. I thought it enough that I had shed tears for my lost wife and endured the violence of the gods, and I had endured it gravely, but now, alone and bereft, having lost my children, I weep for the greatest losses, and I call upon the gods above to witness that a father had to place gifts for his children which his children ought to have placed for their father. Attia Ampliata, freedwoman of Marcus, 23 years old, is laid here; witchcraft cut her down in the prime of life. After being ill for five months and a year, torn away in her prime, she lies in the underworld caves of Dis. Faustus placed this inscription for his freedwoman.
EDCS-27700023.summary: This is a complex funerary monument made by Marcus Attius Faustus for himself, his wife Attia Secunda, and his sons Marcus Attius Crispus and Marcus Attius Secundus, with an additional inscription and poetic lamentation by Faustus for his freedwoman Attia Ampliata, who died at 23 due to alleged witchcraft.

EDCS-27700024.text_edition: Dis Manibus Attiae Gratillae coniugi incomparabili quondam nnorum plus minus XL Iulius Timotheus maritus cum quo nnos XVIIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700024.translation: To the Divine Manes of Attia Gratilla, his incomparable wife, formerly about 40 years old. Iulius Timotheus, her husband, with whom she lived 19 years, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700024.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Timotheus to his incomparable wife, Attia Gratilla, with whom he lived for 19 years and who died around the age of 40.

EDCS-27700025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aufidiae Quartae Caesienus Achilleus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-27700025.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aufidia Quarta. Caesienus Achilleus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27700025.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Caesienus Achilleus for his well-deserving wife, Aufidia Quarta.

EDCS-27700026.text_edition: Avidiae Titi libertae Ingenuae matri et Caeciliae Cai filiae Tertullae sorori Avidia Titi filia Luculla posuit
EDCS-27700026.translation: Avidia Luculla, daughter of Titus, placed this for Avidia Ingenua, freedwoman of Titus, her mother, and for Caecilia Tertulla, daughter of Gaius, her sister.
EDCS-27700026.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Avidia Luculla, daughter of Titus, dedicated a monument to her mother, Avidia Ingenua, and her sister, Caecilia Tertulla.

EDCS-27700027.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Anniano iuveni qui vixit annos XXVI
EDCS-27700027.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Annianus, a young man who lived 26 years.
EDCS-27700027.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Annianus, a young man who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-27700028.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700028.text_edition: Aurelius Aprilianus se vivo donavet sic arcam Aurelio Amuro et Aureliae Quintae quae est in horto Metrodori vivi sibi posuerunt IIarnum quod si quis post mortem eorum super eos alia corpora velet sic inponere  inferet! solidos
EDCS-27700028.translation: Aurelius Aprilianus, while alive, donated this chest to Aurelius Amurus and Aurelia Quinta, which is in Metrodorus' garden. While alive, they placed for themselves a burial chamber. If anyone after their death wishes to place other bodies upon theirs, they shall pay solidi.
EDCS-27700028.summary: This inscription records the donation of a burial chest by Aurelius Aprilianus to Aurelius Amurus and Aurelia Quinta, who also prepared a burial chamber for themselves, and includes a clause against subsequent interments.

EDCS-27700029.text_edition: Aurelius Burius [posvI]iVt#[posv]it#VT sibi et matri suae piae Horaniae sic qui sibi vivi pRosuerunt#posuerunt#ROSUERUN quot sic si quis voluerit sene sic iussione nostra aperire inferet
EDCS-27700029.translation: Aurelius Burius placed this for himself and for his devoted mother Horania, for those who, while alive, built this for themselves. If anyone wishes to open it without our command, they shall pay.
EDCS-27700029.summary: This inscription records that Aurelius Burius prepared a monument for himself and his mother, Horania, while they were alive, including a penalty for unauthorized opening.

EDCS-27700030.text_edition: VIV nno--- L infelicissimo Aurelius Cassius patronus
EDCS-27700030.translation: To the unhappy [deceased] who lived for [---] years, Aurelius Cassius, his patron.
EDCS-27700030.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Cassius, the patron, to an unnamed deceased individual.

EDCS-27700031.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Deucalioni AurBelia#Aurelia#AUBEL Ursina patri
EDCS-27700031.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Deucalion. Aurelia Ursina, his father.
EDCS-27700031.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Ursina to Aurelius Deucalion, indicating a familial relationship (though the role of 'father' for a female dedicant is unusual and might be a scribal error or a specific family convention).

EDCS-27700033.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Emeritianus vixit annis XXIIII Flavia Valentina mater filio carissimo posuit locus emptus cum vaso a Caesonio Primo
EDCS-27700033.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Emeritianus lived 24 years. Flavia Valentina, his mother, placed this for her dearest son. The place was bought with the urn from Caesonio Primus.
EDCS-27700033.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Emeritianus, aged 24, dedicated by his mother Flavia Valentina, noting the purchase of the burial plot and urn from Caesonius Primus.

EDCS-27700034.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Fortunio defuncto annorum XXX Aurelia Panthera mater et patrona filio infelicissimo posuit
EDCS-27700034.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Titus Aurelius Fortunius, deceased at 30 years old. Aurelia Panthera, his mother and patrona, placed this for her most unhappy son.
EDCS-27700034.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Panthera, mother and patrona, to her son Titus Aurelius Fortunius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-27700035.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Hermeti Filia marito bene merenti
EDCS-27700035.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Hermes. Filia, to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27700035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Filia to her husband, Aurelius Hermes, acknowledging his merit.

EDCS-27700036.text_edition: Aurelio Iulio Titi Aureli Nepos sic Nepos posuit
EDCS-27700036.translation: To Aurelius Iulius. Nepos, son of Titus Aurelius, placed this. So Nepos placed it.
EDCS-27700036.summary: This inscription records that Nepos, son of Titus Aurelius, dedicated a monument to Aurelius Iulius.

EDCS-27700037.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximino defuncto annorum III dierum VIII parentes posuerunt filio infelicissimo
EDCS-27700037.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Maximinus, who died at 3 years and 8 days old. His parents placed this for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-27700037.summary: This is a funerary inscription from parents dedicating a monument to their young, deceased son, Aurelius Maximinus, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700038.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Veneriano qui vixit annos CVIII Aurelius Polensis nepos
EDCS-27700038.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelius Venerianus, who lived for 108 years. Aurelius Polensis, his grandson, [placed this].
EDCS-27700038.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aurelius Polensis, a grandson, dedicating a monument to his exceptionally long-lived grandfather, Aurelius Venerianus.

EDCS-27700040.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Crescentillae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXV Iulius Fortunius maritus et sibi
EDCS-27700040.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelia Crescentilla, my wife, who lived for 25 years. Iulius Fortunius, her husband, and for himself.
EDCS-27700040.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Iulius Fortunius for his wife, Aurelia Crescentilla, also reserving a space for himself in the same monument.

EDCS-27700041.text_edition: M Aurelius H et Aurelia Eulogia filiis infelicissimis
EDCS-27700041.translation: M. Aurelius H[erculanus/ilarus] and Aurelia Eulogia for their most unfortunate children.
EDCS-27700041.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by M. Aurelius H. and Aurelia Eulogia for their deceased children, emphasizing their sorrow.

EDCS-27700042.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700042.text_edition: Aureliae Iulianae puellae innocentissimae qui sic vixit annos decem septem mensis sic undecim dies duodecim Flavius Iulius et Aurelia Emerius arcam posuerunt filiae deposita die XVII Kalendas Ianuarias
EDCS-27700042.translation: To Aurelia Iuliana, a most innocent girl, who lived thus for seventeen years, eleven months, and twelve days. Flavius Iulius and Aurelia Emerius placed this sarcophagus for their daughter, interred on the 17th day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-27700042.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a sarcophagus by Flavius Iulius and Aurelia Emerius for their young, innocent daughter, Aurelia Iuliana, noting her age and burial date (December 16).

EDCS-27700043.translation: Aurelia Sabina for her husband, PERSEOMAIOCNissim.
EDCS-27700043.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Sabina to her husband, whose name is partially illegible or unusually spelled, expressing her devotion.

EDCS-27700044.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureliae Saturninae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit annis XXVII Quintus Iulius Vilanesis maritus posuit
EDCS-27700044.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Aurelia Saturnina, an incomparable wife, who lived for 27 years. Quintus Iulius Vilanesis, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-27700044.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Quintus Iulius Vilanesis dedicating a monument to his deceased wife, Aurelia Saturnina, praising her as incomparable.

EDCS-27700045.text_edition: Aureliae Valentinae defunctae nnorum XXXVII Crispinius Felix coniugi inconparabili sic
EDCS-27700045.translation: For Aurelia Valentina, who died at 37 years old. Crispinius Felix for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-27700045.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Crispinius Felix to his incomparable wife, Aurelia Valentina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-27700046.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700046.text_edition: Aurelia Victorina quae emit arca ex proprio suo et vixit annus sic XLV et dies tres et post obitum ipseius sic fecit Fortunatus alonnus sic ipseius sic ex boto sic ipseius fecit si quis voluerit corpus inponere dabit in fiscum |(denariorum folles |(mille)
EDCS-27700046.translation: Aurelia Victorina, who bought this sarcophagus from her own property and lived for 45 years and three days. After her death, Fortunatus, her foster-son, carried out her wish. If anyone wishes to place a body [here], they will pay 1000 folles into the treasury.
EDCS-27700046.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Victorina, who purchased her own sarcophagus, with provisions made for its use after her death by her foster-son Fortunatus, and including a financial penalty for unauthorized burials, dating to a period when currency was counted in folles (likely 4th-5th century AD).

EDCS-27700047.text_edition: Lucio Baebio I patri optimo Minatiae FirmillaeAELus FirNIAIP
EDCS-27700047.translation: To Lucius Baebius I[---], best father. To Minatia Firmilla AELus FirNIAIP.
EDCS-27700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a 'best father', Lucius Baebius, and also mentioning Minatia Firmilla, likely from their family, though parts of the names are obscured.

EDCS-27700048.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Baebio Vindematori Baebia Secura patri bene merenti
EDCS-27700048.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Baebius Vindemator, Baebia Secura (set this up) for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-27700048.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Baebia Secura to her father, Lucius Baebius Vindemator.

EDCS-27700049.text_edition: Lucius Baebidius Vitalis et Iulia Quarta Lucio Baebidio Primitivo delicio defuncto nnorum XII et Baebidiae Amandae coniugi bene merentibus posuerunt
EDCS-27700049.translation: Lucius Baebidius Vitalis and Iulia Quarta placed this for Lucius Baebidius Primitivus, their deceased darling, who lived for 12 years, and for Baebidia Amanda, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27700049.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Baebidius Vitalis and Iulia Quarta for their beloved deceased son, Lucius Baebidius Primitivus, and for Baebidia Amanda, who is also referred to as a well-deserving wife, suggesting a family tomb.

EDCS-27700050.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Baebilius Euryalus et Valeria Lais vivi fecerunt sibi et libertis libertabusque suis et amico Statio Crescenti aditum ad sepulcrum et unam DIO FIIIC
EDCS-27700050.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Baebilius Euryalus and Valeria Lais, while living, made this for themselves and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and for their friend Statius Crescens, with access to the tomb and one [Dio Fiiic, possibly a mistake or abbreviation for part of the tomb].
EDCS-27700050.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Baebilius Euryalus and Valeria Lais during their lifetime for themselves, their freedpersons, and a friend, Statius Crescens, including access to the tomb and a specific feature of the tomb.

EDCS-27700051.text_edition: Basilissae infelicissimae defunctae nnorum XXX et Surae filiae defunctae nnorum II verna filius bene merentibus
EDCS-27700051.translation: To Basilissa, most unfortunate, deceased, who lived for 30 years, and to Sura, deceased daughter, who lived for 2 years, (her) son, Verna, (set this up) for the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700051.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Verna, a son, for his deceased mother Basilissa and his deceased sister Sura.

EDCS-27700052.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription or dedication to Gaius Bennius Valentinus, possibly part of a larger monument where the dedicant and full context are missing.

EDCS-27700053.text_edition: Dis Manibus Buccioni nnrum XX Sextus S M pius posuit
EDCS-27700053.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Buccio, who lived for 20 years. Sextus S. M. Pius placed this.
EDCS-27700053.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sextus S. M. Pius for Buccio, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-27700054.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius C S posuit P Veneriae matri pientissimae
EDCS-27700054.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius C. S. placed this for P. Veneria, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-27700054.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius C. S. for his most devoted mother, P. Veneria.

EDCS-27700055.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesia Romana Caesiae Luci filiae Ducatrici filiae bene merenti
EDCS-27700055.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesia Romana (set this up) for Caesia Ducatrix, daughter of Lucius, her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-27700055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caesia Romana to her well-deserving daughter, Caesia Ducatrix.

EDCS-27700056.text_edition: Lucio Aemilio Ursino fratri eius posuit Titus Aemilius Protogenes cum Stergio liberto
EDCS-27700056.translation: Titus Aemilius Protogenes, with his freedman Stergius, placed this for Lucius Aemilius Ursinus, his brother.
EDCS-27700056.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Aemilius Protogenes, accompanied by his freedman Stergius, for his brother Lucius Aemilius Ursinus.

EDCS-27700078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Cassia Aelio Victorino marito pientissimo con sic quo vixit nnos XXV bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Cassia placed this for Aelius Victorinus, her most devoted husband, with whom she lived for 25 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-27700078.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Cassia to her most devoted husband, Aelius Victorinus, commemorating their 25 years of marriage.

EDCS-27700079.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Cyerema C P
EDCS-27700079.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Cyerema, C. P.
EDCS-27700079.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Cyerema to the Divine Manes, the spirits of the dead.

EDCS-27700083.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Veneriae coniugi carissimae quae vixit annos LII#LI#II Tertullianus Augusti libertus maritus posuit
EDCS-27700083.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelia Veneria, his dearest wife, who lived fifty-two years, Tertullianus, freedman of the Emperor, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-27700083.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Tertullianus, an imperial freedman, for his beloved wife, Aurelia Veneria, who died at the age of 52, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27700086.text_edition: Aemilia Clementilla Tertullae Annae et duobus Apris fratribus infelicissimis annum quaternum mensium trium bene merenti
EDCS-27700086.translation: Aemilia Clementilla, to Tertulla Anna and to her two most unhappy brothers, Apris, who deserved well, having lived four years and three months.
EDCS-27700086.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aemilia Clementilla to the unfortunate children Tertulla Anna and her two brothers, Apris, who died young at four years and three months old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700087.text_edition: Dis Manibus Agriae Annuclae mensium III Cinnamis mater infelix
EDCS-27700087.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Agria Annucla, three months old, Cinnamis, her unhappy mother, dedicates this.
EDCS-27700087.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unhappy mother, Cinnamis, for her infant daughter, Agria Annucla, who died at three months of age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700088.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sexto Atilio Niconi defuncto nnorum VIII Atilia Hediste et Nicon Nicostratu filio infelicissimo locum Pacuvia Tryfera donabit sic
EDCS-27700088.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Sextus Atilius Nikon, who died at eight years of age. Atilia Hediste and Nikon Nicostratus to their most unhappy son. Pacuvia Tryphera donated this place thus.
EDCS-27700088.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Atilius Nikon, an eight-year-old boy, erected by his parents Atilia Hediste and Nikon Nicostratus, with the burial plot donated by Pacuvia Tryphera, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700089.text_edition: Dis Manibus Attio Magno defuncto nnorum VII mensium II Privata filio infelicissimo
EDCS-27700089.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Attius Magnus, who died at seven years and two months of age. Privata, to her most unhappy son.
EDCS-27700089.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a mother, Privata, for her very unfortunate son, Attius Magnus, who died at seven years and two months old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700090.text_edition: Albucia Felicula Acte filiae annorum XVII et sibi
EDCS-27700090.translation: Albucia Felicula, to her daughter Acte, seventeen years old, and for herself.
EDCS-27700090.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a tomb by Albucia Felicula for her seventeen-year-old daughter, Acte, and also for herself, indicating a family burial plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700091.text_edition: Aurelio Annio et Liciniae Iuventillae Aurelia Annia filia parentibus
EDCS-27700091.translation: To Aurelius Annius and Licinia Iuventilla, Aurelia Annia, their daughter, to her parents.
EDCS-27700091.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Annia to her parents, Aurelius Annius and Licinia Iuventilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700092.text_edition: Aurelius Castus et Aurelia Iuliane Aurelio Casto filio qui decidit mensium quattuor et dierum novem
EDCS-27700092.translation: Aurelius Castus and Aurelia Iuliane, to Aurelius Castus, their son, who passed away at four months and nine days old.
EDCS-27700092.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aurelius Castus and Aurelia Iuliane for their infant son, Aurelius Castus, who died at a very young age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700093.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius C Arg Turrania Aprilis marito
EDCS-27700093.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius C. Arg. Turrania Aprilis to her husband.
EDCS-27700093.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Turrania Aprilis to her husband, Lucius C. Arg., to the Divine Manes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700094.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Achillaeo liberto fidelissimo Titus Aurelius Pompeius patronus posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27700094.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Pompeius, his patron, placed this for Titus Aurelius Achillaeus, his most faithful freedman, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700094.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Aurelius Pompeius to his most faithful freedman, Titus Aurelius Achillaeus.

EDCS-27700095.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700095.text_edition: Aurelius Vindemius qui vixit annus! sexsaginta sic arcam sibi et coniugi suae carissimae Luciae posuit si qui autem de heredibus meis se ipsum vel alios  suorum suae   rum posuerit hered
EDCS-27700095.translation: Aurelius Vindemius, who lived sixty years, thus set up this tomb for himself and his dearest wife Lucia. If any of my heirs, however, places himself or others of his own [here], he shall be an heir...
EDCS-27700095.summary: This is a funerary inscription and will set up by Aurelius Vindemius for himself and his wife Lucia, including a clause regarding unauthorized burials by his heirs.

EDCS-27700124.text_edition: Caio Aemilio Ingenuo aedili et IIviro iure dicundo et Aemiliae Hippolytae matri  Vestibulum et ambitus monumenti a bathro clatrorum intra loricam quae spectat in meridie ad viam munitam longa pedes XXXII lata pedes LV et a bathro clatrorum a parte dextra et sinistra et a postico omnium angulorum IIII lata pedes quinos denos  Vestibulum et ambitus monumenti a bathro clatrorum intra loricam quae spectat in meridie ad viam munitam longa pedes XXXII lata pedes LV et a bathro clatrorum a parte dextra et sinistra et a postico omnium angulorum IIII lata pedes quinos denos
EDCS-27700124.translation: To Gaius Aemilius Ingenuus, aedile and duumvir with judicial power, and to Aemilia Hippolyta, his mother. The vestibule and surrounds of the monument, from the base of the railings within the enclosure wall facing south towards the paved road, thirty-two feet long, fifty-five feet wide, and from the base of the railings on the right and left sides and at the back of all four corners, fifteen feet wide.
EDCS-27700124.summary: This inscription details the dimensions and construction of a monument, including a vestibule and surrounds, dedicated to Gaius Aemilius Ingenuus, an aedile and duumvir, and his mother Aemilia Hippolyta.

EDCS-27700125.text_edition: Caio Albio Nigro Lucio Albio Cai filio Nigro Lucio Albio Luci filio Leugae aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo Caio Albio Luci filio Lepido decurioni Salonitanorum Albia Cai filia Lepida
EDCS-27700125.translation: To Gaius Albius Niger, to Lucius Albius, son of Gaius, Niger, to Lucius Albius, son of Lucius, Leuga, aedile, quattuorvir with judicial power, to Gaius Albius, son of Lucius, Lepidus, decurion of the Salonitans. Albia Lepida, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-27700125.summary: This inscription lists multiple members of the Albius family, including Gaius Albius Niger, Lucius Albius Leuga (an aedile and quattuorvir), and Gaius Albius Lepidus (a decurion of the Salonitans), likely indicating a family monument or dedication.

EDCS-27700126.text_edition: Caio Albucio Cai filio Tromentina Menippo decurioni Salonis aedili marito et Caio Albucio Cai filio Tromentina Prociliano decurioni Salonis et Issae aedili defuncto nnorum IXXX et Caio Albucio Cai filio Sergia Menippo decurioni Issae defuncto nnorum IXX filiis et Caio Ligurio Titiano defuncto nnorum XXX fratri Liguria Procilla quae et Albucia et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-27700126.translation: To Gaius Albucius, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, Menippus, decurion of Salona, aedile, his husband; and to Gaius Albucius, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, Procilianus, decurion of Salona and Issa, aedile, deceased at 80 years; and to Gaius Albucius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, Menippus, decurion of Issa, deceased at 70 years, her sons; and to Gaius Ligurius Titianus, deceased at 30 years, her brother. Liguria Procilla, who is also Albuccia, erected this for herself while living.
EDCS-27700126.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Liguria Procilla (also known as Albuccia) for herself while living, and for her husband, two sons, and brother, all of whom held various civic offices and are noted as deceased with their ages.

EDCS-27700127.text_edition: Tertullae annorum XIIX Antoniae Primigeniae filius Lucio Antonio Luci filio Tromentina Firmo aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo praefecto fabrum procuratori Augusti filius Lucio Antonio Expectato frater vivus fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-27700127.translation: To Tertulla, 18 years old. Antonia Primigenia, his daughter. For Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, Firmus, aedile, quattuorvir with judicial power, prefect of engineers, procurator of Augustus, his son. Lucius Antonius Expectatus, his living brother, made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-27700127.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Antonius Expectatus for himself and his family, commemorating Tertulla (aged 18), Antonia Primigenia (his daughter), and Lucius Antonius Firmus, who held several prominent civic and imperial positions.

EDCS-27700128.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Aufidio Epicteto decurioni et
EDCS-27700128.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Aufidius Epictetus, decurion and...
EDCS-27700128.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Aufidius Epictetus, who held the rank of decurion.

EDCS-27700129.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Aureli Hermogenis equitis Romani decurionis aedilis curulis defuncti annorum XX dierum III Marcus Aurelius Auggustorum libertus Hermes procurator piisimo filio
EDCS-27700129.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Marcus Aurelius Hermogenes, Roman knight, decurion, curule aedile, deceased at 20 years and 3 days. Marcus Aurelius Hermes, freedman of the Augusti and procurator, [made this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-27700129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Hermes, a procurator and freedman of the Augusti, to his most dutiful son, Marcus Aurelius Hermogenes, who was a Roman knight, decurion, and curule aedile, deceased at a very young age.

EDCS-27700130.text_edition: Aurelio Severiano decurioni Salonitanorum et Aufidiae Soteridi parentibus Aufidia Rustica et Aurelius Rufinus filii
EDCS-27700130.translation: To Aurelius Severianus, decurion of the Salonitans, and to Aufidia Soteris, their parents. Aufidia Rustica and Aurelius Rufinus, their children.
EDCS-27700130.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aufidia Rustica and Aurelius Rufinus to their parents, Aurelius Severianus, a decurion of the Salonitans, and Aufidia Soteris.

EDCS-27700131.text_edition: Caio Clodio Cai filio Sergia Fadieno IIIIviro iure dicundo Fadia Cai filia Marcellina mater locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-27700131.translation: To Gaius Clodius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, Fadienus, quattuorvir with judicial power. Fadia Marcellina, daughter of Gaius, his mother. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27700131.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Gaius Clodius Fadienus, a quattuorvir with judicial power, by his mother Fadia Marcellina, noting that the plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-27700132.text_edition: Cai libertus Eupor  Cai fil- decuri C THEVR COI
EDCS-27700132.translation: Gaius Eupor, freedman of Gaius, son of Gaius, decurion. C. Theur. Coi.
EDCS-27700132.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Gaius Eupor, a freedman and decurion, likely on a public monument or funerary inscription.

EDCS-27700133.text_edition: Papiria Rhome viva fecit sibi et Papiriae Cladillae nnorum XXIX et Publio Papirio Proculo nnorum XIX filiis condidit hic miseri mater duo funera partus ossaque non iustis intulit exequiis tegula nam Romae Proculum prolapsa peremit pressit Sipunti sic pressa Cladilla rogum vota parens nocuere tibi qui numina saeva ut plura eriperent plura dedere bona et Publio Papirio Clado viro et Publio Papirio Celerioni et Papiriae Hispanillae libertis  Quintus Nassius Quinti filius Tromentina Certus Draco aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo vivus fecit sibi et Tulliae Intumae matri  hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-27700133.translation: Papiria Rhome, while living, made this for herself and for Papiria Cladilla, 29 years old, and for Publius Papirius Proculus, 19 years old, her children. Here the wretched mother entombed the two funerals of her offspring and brought their bones in unjust obsequies, for a tile falling in Rome killed Proculus, and Cladilla, thus pressed, perished at Sipontum. O parent, your vows harmed you, you who gave more good things, so that the cruel deities might snatch more away. And for Publius Papirius Cladus, her husband, and for Publius Papirius Celerio and Papiria Hispanilla, her freedpersons. Quintus Nassius, son of Quintus, of the Tribe Tromentina, Certus Draco, aedile, quattuorvir with judicial power, while living, made this for himself and for Tullia Intuma, his mother. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-27700133.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated by Papiria Rhome for herself, her two children who died tragically, and her husband and freedpersons, with an additional dedication by Quintus Nassius for himself and his mother, explicitly stating the monument is not inheritable, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27700134.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Terentio Caesiano decurioni aedili defuncto annorum XXIII Terentius Primus libertus
EDCS-27700134.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Titus Terentius Caesius, decurion, aedile, deceased at 23 years of age. Terentius Primus, his freedman.
EDCS-27700134.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a freedman, Terentius Primus, to his deceased patron, Titus Terentius Caesius, who held the public offices of decurion and aedile, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700135.text_edition: Tito Turranio Titi filio Vero aedili Scardonae Arria Cai filia Maxima filio pientissimo fecit et sibi et Caio Maecio Maximo coniugi libertis libertabusque suis
EDCS-27700135.translation: To Titus Turranius Verus, son of Titus, aedile at Scardona. Arria Maxima, daughter of Gaius, made this for her most dutiful son, and for herself, and for Gaius Maecius Maximus, her husband, and for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-27700135.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Arria Maxima for her son, Titus Turranius Verus, an aedile in Scardona, as well as for herself, her husband, and their freedpersons, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700136.text_edition: DDis MManibus Valeriae Ursinae Titi filiae coniugi incomparabili defunctae annorum  mensium VI dierum V Aurelius Aquila decurio Patavissensis negotiator ex provincia Dacia bene merenti posuit et sibi cum qua vixit annos VII sine ulla querella
EDCS-27700136.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Valeria Ursina, daughter of Titus, an incomparable wife, deceased at [X] years, 6 months, 5 days. Aurelius Aquila, decurion of Patavissum, merchant from the province of Dacia, placed this for her, well-deserving, and for himself, with whom she lived for seven years without any complaint.
EDCS-27700136.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelius Aquila, a decurion and merchant from Dacia, to his deceased wife, Valeria Ursina, praising her as incomparable and noting their harmonious marriage, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700138.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a decurion and a person named Quintus, likely part of a public or funerary monument, but is too incomplete to provide specific details.

EDCS-27700140.text_edition: Titus Ancharius Anthus IIIIIIvir Augustalis titulum fieri iussit ex HS VIsex milibus sibi et Petroniae Prothesi uxori et libertis heredibusque Fortunato Tito Zosimo VIviris Zopyro Epaphrodito Substituto Abascanto Petroniae Iucundae Anchario Iusto et omnibus libertis libertabusque
EDCS-27700140.translation: Titus Ancharius Anthus, sevir Augustalis, ordered this inscription to be made at a cost of six thousand sesterces for himself and for Petronia Prothesis, his wife, and for their freedmen and heirs: Fortunatus, Titus, Zosimus (seviri), Zopyrus, Epaphroditus, Substitutus, Abascantus, Petronia Iucunda, Ancharius Iustus, and all their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-27700140.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Titus Ancharius Anthus, a sevir Augustalis, for himself, his wife, and a list of his freedpersons and heirs, indicating a substantial investment in the monument, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27700141.text_edition: Tito Flavio Augusti liberto Basso Augustali Claudia Tiberi f(ilia) Thetis marito bene merenti et Titus Flavius Felix pro parte quinta patrono bono hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes I-X in agro pedes XXV
EDCS-27700141.translation: To Titus Flavius Bassus, freedman of Augustus, Augustalis. Claudia Thetis, daughter of Tiberius, made this for her well-deserving husband, and Titus Flavius Felix, for his fifth share, to his good patron. This monument shall not pass to an heir. In front, 10 feet; in depth, 25 feet.
EDCS-27700141.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus Flavius Bassus, a freedman of Augustus and Augustalis, erected by his wife Claudia Thetis, with a contribution from his freedman Titus Flavius Felix, explicitly stating the monument is not inheritable and providing its dimensions, likely dating to the early Imperial period.
