EDCS-27700142.text_edition: Caius Iulius Sceptus Admeti Augusti liberti libertus IIIIIIvir Augustalis vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Coetonidi uxori Caio Iulio Cai filio Tromentina Admeto filio Caio Iulio Cai filio Tromentina Aquilae filio Lucio Iulio Cai filio Tromentina Scepto filio Iuliae Cai filiae Tromentina Admetidi filiae  Caio Iulio |(mulieris) liberto Triumphali filio Iuliae Cai libertae Romanae filiae Iuliae |(mulieris) libertae Scepsidi filiae Iuliae |(mulieris) libertae Primae uxori defunctae  Niso Tiberi Claudi Augusti servo genero defuncto in hoc monumentum veto aliter ossua deponi quam Primae et nisi sunt  in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-27700142.translation: Gaius Iulius Sceptus, freedman of Admetus, freedman of Augustus, sevir Augustalis, while living, made this for himself and for Iulia Coetonis, his wife; for Gaius Iulius Admetus, son of Gaius, of the Tribe Tromentina, his son; for Gaius Iulius Aquila, son of Gaius, of the Tribe Tromentina, his son; for Lucius Iulius Sceptus, son of Gaius, of the Tribe Tromentina, his son; for Iulia Admetis, daughter of Gaius, of the Tribe Tromentina, his daughter. For Gaius Iulius Triumphalis, freedman of a woman, his son; for Iulia Romana, freedwoman of Gaius, his daughter; for Iulia Scepsis, freedwoman of a woman, his daughter; for Iulia Prima, freedwoman of a woman, his deceased wife. To Nisus, slave of Tiberius Claudius Augustus, his deceased son-in-law. On this monument, I forbid any bones to be placed other than those of Prima and unless they are theirs. In front, 20 feet; in depth, 30 feet.
EDCS-27700142.summary: This extensive funerary inscription was prepared by Gaius Iulius Sceptus, a sevir Augustalis and an Augustan freedman, for himself, his wife Iulia Coetonis, his children, his freedperson children, his deceased wife Iulia Prima, and his deceased son-in-law Nisus, with a strict clause regarding who may be interred, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27700143.text_edition: Decimus Petronius Dasius IIIIIIvir Decimus Petronius Cilo IIIIIIvir
EDCS-27700143.translation: Decimus Petronius Dasius, sevir. Decimus Petronius Cilo, sevir.
EDCS-27700143.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Decimus Petronius Dasius and Decimus Petronius Cilo, both holding the office of sevir, likely from a public or funerary monument and dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700144.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Pomponius Draco Augustalis vivus posuit sibi et Iuliae Anniae coniugi suae veto autem in hac arca alium corpus inferri aut ossua poni si quis autem intulerit dabit rei publicae Salonitanorum HS nummum Xmilia et  ne quis ante arcam hanc aliam ponat
EDCS-27700144.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Pomponius Draco, Augustalis, while living, set this up for himself and for Julia Annia, his wife. However, it is forbidden for any other body to be placed or bones to be laid in this sarcophagus. If anyone should place them, they will give ten thousand sesterces to the Republic of the Salonitani, and no one may place another sarcophagus in front of this one.
EDCS-27700144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a sarcophagus, set up by Lucius Pomponius Draco for himself and his wife, Julia Annia, with a legal clause prohibiting further interments and imposing a fine.

EDCS-27700145.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Vettius Augustalis Vettia  coniugi  posuit
EDCS-27700145.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Vettius Augustalis set this up for Vettia, his wife.
EDCS-27700145.summary: This short inscription records a funerary dedication by Titus Vettius Augustalis to his wife, Vettia.

EDCS-27700146.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Vettio Eutycheti IIIIIIviro Augustali Caius Vettius Eucharistus patri pientissimo et sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-27700146.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Vettius Eutyches, a sevir Augustalis, Gaius Vettius Eucharistus, while living, made this for his most devoted father and for himself.
EDCS-27700146.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Vettius Eucharistus to his father, Gaius Vettius Eutyches, a sevir Augustalis, and also for himself.

EDCS-27700147.text_edition: Tito Vettio Titi liberto Fortunato Augustali Vettia Agape marito bene merenti
EDCS-27700147.translation: To Titus Vettius Fortunatus, freedman of Titus, an Augustalis. Vettia Agape [made this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27700147.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Vettia Agape to her deserving husband, Titus Vettius Fortunatus, a freedman and Augustalis.

EDCS-27700148.text_edition: Lucius Naevius Luci libertus Diogenes magister conlegii sevirum quinquennalis aedem  de sua pecunia fecit dedicavitque
EDCS-27700148.translation: Lucius Naevius Diogenes, freedman of Lucius, master of the college of seviri quinquennales, built and dedicated this temple at his own expense.
EDCS-27700148.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Naevius Diogenes, a freedman and a high-ranking official within a college of seviri, funded the construction and dedication of a temple.

EDCS-27700149.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700149.text_edition: Aurelius Ursacius Salonitanus ex collegio Veneris et Aurelia Vitalia vivis sibi posuerunt hanc arca tradita ab Aurelia Lupa  Atilio Primo
EDCS-27700149.translation: Aurelius Ursacius, a Salonitan from the college of Venus, and Aurelia Vitalia, while living, set up this sarcophagus for themselves, entrusted by Aurelia Lupa to Atilius Primus.
EDCS-27700149.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a sarcophagus, set up by Aurelius Ursacius and Aurelia Vitalia for themselves while they were alive, with a note about its entrustment by Aurelia Lupa to Atilius Primus.

EDCS-27700150.text_edition: Dis Manibus Augustiano magistro symforariorum bene merenti
EDCS-27700150.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Augustianus, master of the symphorarii, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700150.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Augustianus, identified as a 'master of the symphorarii', praised as well-deserving.

EDCS-27700151.text_edition: Aurelia Vernilla plumbaria sibi et Aurelio Lucio marito suo et Aureliae Stercoriae filiae posuit quod si qui supeper haec corpora posuerit inferet rei publicae |(denarios) Ccentum milia
EDCS-27700151.translation: Aurelia Vernilla, a plumbaria (lead worker), set this up for herself and for Aurelius Lucius, her husband, and for Aurelia Stercoria, her daughter. If anyone should place bodies on top of these, they shall pay one hundred thousand denarii to the Republic.
EDCS-27700151.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelia Vernilla for herself, her husband Aurelius Lucius, and her daughter Aurelia Stercoria, including a penalty clause for anyone disturbing the graves.

EDCS-27700152.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caniae Urbanae vilicae quaeF#quae#QUAF vixit nnos LV Cania Ursina filia et Attius Verecundianus gener matri incomparabili
EDCS-27700152.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cania Urbana, the vilica (estate manager), who lived 55 years. Cania Ursina, her daughter, and Attius Verecundianus, her son-in-law, [made this] for their incomparable mother.
EDCS-27700152.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Cania Urbana, an estate manager who lived 55 years, by her daughter Cania Ursina and son-in-law Attius Verecundianus.

EDCS-27700153.text_edition: Caius Cassius Urbanus Cassia Valentina vivi fecerunt siTbi#sibi#STB concessum locum a Flavio Marcellino magistro introito sic libero
EDCS-27700153.translation: Gaius Cassius Urbanus and Cassia Valentina, while living, made this for themselves, a place granted by Flavius Marcellinus, the master of the entrance, thus free.
EDCS-27700153.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Cassius Urbanus and Cassia Valentina, while alive, created this monument for themselves on land granted by Flavius Marcellinus, the master of the entrance, implying it was a burial plot.

EDCS-27700154.text_edition: Decimus Cornelius Herma et Cornelia Tyche domo Ravenna parentes infelicissimi filiabus pientissimis Corneliae Helpidi filiae defunctae nnorum XVIII Corneliae Helpiste filiae defunctae nnorum XVIIII Restutae filiae defunctae nnorum VII libertis libertabusque suis posterisque eorum
EDCS-27700154.translation: Decimus Cornelius Herma and Cornelia Tyche, from Ravenna, most unhappy parents, to their most devoted daughters: Cornelia Helpidis, daughter, who died at 18 years; Cornelia Helpiste, daughter, who died at 19 years; Restuta, daughter, who died at 7 years; and for their freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-27700154.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Decimus Cornelius Herma and Cornelia Tyche to their three deceased daughters, Cornelia Helpidis, Cornelia Helpiste, and Restuta, and also for their freedpersons and their posterity.

EDCS-27700155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Ursiliano iuveni infelicissimo defuncto nnorum plus minus XXVIII artis medicinae industriae primae Aemiliea Spifarilla conilugi#coniugi#CONLUGI pientissimo et infelicissimo cum quo vixit annis IIII et sibi posuit
EDCS-27700155.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Flavius Ursilianus, a most unfortunate young man, who died at approximately 28 years of age, of the art of medicine, of prime diligence. Aemilia Spifarilla placed this for her most devoted and most unfortunate husband, with whom she lived for 4 years, and for herself.
EDCS-27700155.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aemilia Spifarilla to her deceased husband, Flavius Ursilianus, a diligent practitioner of medicine, and also for herself.

EDCS-27700156.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Aprilis mensor
EDCS-27700156.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Aprilis, surveyor.
EDCS-27700156.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Aprilis, identifying him by his name and profession as a surveyor.

EDCS-27700157.text_edition: Lucius Iulius Cai filius Tromentina Clemens negotiator titulum fieri iussit sibi et Caio Iulio Cai filio Tromentina Maximo patri et Noniae Lacri filiae Marullae matri et Lucio Iulio Luci filio Tromentina Maximo filio et Iuliae Luci filiae Marullae filiae et Iuliae Luci filiae Clementillae filiae
EDCS-27700157.translation: Lucius Iulius Clemens, son of Gaius, of the Tribus Tromentina, a merchant, ordered this monument to be made for himself and for Gaius Iulius Maximus, son of Gaius, of the Tribus Tromentina, his father; and for Nonia Lacra, his daughter; Marulla, his mother; and Lucius Iulius Maximus, son of Lucius, of the Tribus Tromentina, his son; and Iulia Marulla, daughter of Lucius, his daughter; and Iulia Clementilla, daughter of Lucius, his daughter.
EDCS-27700157.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Lucius Iulius Clemens, a merchant, for himself and multiple members of his family, including his father, mother, daughters, and a son.

EDCS-27700158.text_edition: Novesis servus agens et Iuventilla Suriscae filiae infelicissimae quae vixit annis II mensibus III bene merenti
EDCS-27700158.translation: Novesis, a slave, acting, and to Iuventilla, daughter of Surisca, most unfortunate, who lived for 2 years and 3 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-27700158.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Novesis, a slave, for the deceased Iuventilla, daughter of Surisca, who died at a young age.

EDCS-27700159.text_edition: Placentino retiario qui vicxit sic annos XX posuit retiaarius Bacchius colliberto de suo sibi
EDCS-27700159.translation: To Placentinus, the retiarius, who lived thus for 20 years. Bacchius, a retiarius, placed this for his fellow freedman, from his own expense.
EDCS-27700159.summary: This is a funerary monument for Placentinus, a retiarius (a type of gladiator), dedicated by his fellow freedman and retiarius, Bacchius.

EDCS-27700160.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Publicius Aemilianus rhetor nationem Afer vixit annos XLVII menses VIIII dies VII horas noctis V
EDCS-27700160.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Quintus Publicius Aemilianus, a rhetorician of African origin, lived for 47 years, 9 months, 7 days, and 5 hours of the night.
EDCS-27700160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Publicius Aemilianus, an African rhetorician, detailing his age at death with unusual precision.

EDCS-27700161.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Sempronio Fortunato mensori aedificiorum natione Kampanus defuncto nnorum XXVIII mensium IIII dierum XI Appia Victoria coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700161.translation: To the Divine Manes. Appia Victoria placed this for her well-deserving husband Sempronius Fortunatus, a surveyor of buildings, of Campanian origin, who died at 28 years, 4 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-27700161.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Appia Victoria for her deceased husband, Sempronius Fortunatus, a Campanian surveyor of buildings.

EDCS-27700162.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a simple name label or record of two individuals, Venustus and Phareus.

EDCS-27700163.text_edition: Caius Villius Sexti fTilius#filius#TILIUS domo Aquileia monumentum vivos fecit
EDCS-27700163.translation: Gaius Villius, son of Sextus, from Aquileia, made this monument while living.
EDCS-27700163.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Villius, from Aquileia, for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-27700164.text_edition: Publio Aelio Claudiano defuncto nnorum IIII dierum XLVIII Publius Aelius Ursinus et Claudia Festiva parentes filio infelicissimo
EDCS-27700164.translation: To Publius Aelius Claudianus, who died at the age of 4 years and 48 days. Publius Aelius Ursinus and Claudia Festiva, his parents, [set this up] for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-27700164.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents for their young son who died at an early age.

EDCS-27700165.text_edition: Dis Manibus Abanto defuncto conserva posuit
EDCS-27700165.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. A fellow slave set this up for Abantus, who died.
EDCS-27700165.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a fellow slave to a deceased individual named Abantus.

EDCS-27700166.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Fresia Lamia Fresiae Acceptae alumnae vixit nnos II menses III dies XV
EDCS-27700166.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Fresia Lamia [set this up] for Fresia Accepta, her foster daughter, who lived for 2 years, 3 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-27700166.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fresia Lamia to her young foster daughter, Fresia Accepta, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-27700167.text_edition: Dis Manibus Actes Lupulus et Felix et Firminus matri piissimae
EDCS-27700167.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Acte, Lupulus, Felix, and Firminus [set this up] for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-27700167.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by four individuals for their beloved and devoted mother.

EDCS-27700168.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aebutio Tertullino defuncto nnorum XXV Amindianus filius posuit
EDCS-27700168.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Amindianus, his son, set this up for Aebutius Tertullinus, who died at the age of 25.
EDCS-27700168.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Amindianus for his father, Aebutius Tertullinus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-27700169.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aebutriae Quartae defunctae annorum XXIIII Barbius Vindemio coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27700169.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Barbius Vindemio set this up for Aebutria Quarta, his well-deserving wife, who died at the age of 24 years.
EDCS-27700169.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Barbius Vindemio to his wife, Aebutria Quarta, who died at age 24.

EDCS-27700170.text_edition: Lucio Aelio Pederoti Amaranthus Luci Aelii filius clementissimus bene merenti hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-27700170.translation: Amaranthus, the most kind son of Lucius Aelius, [set up] this monument for the well-deserving Lucius Aelius Pederos; this monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-27700170.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Amaranthus to his father, Lucius Aelius Pederos, with a clause restricting its inheritance.

EDCS-27700171.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi liberto Satyro Lyris defuncto nnorum XL Papiria Helpidys coniugi rarissimo et incomparabilis pietatis loco concesso a Fulvio Hermete in fronte pedes IIII semissem et sibi
EDCS-27700171.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lyris [set this up] for Publius Aelius Satyrus, freedman of Publius, who died at the age of 40; Papiria Helpidys [set this up] for her most rare husband, of incomparable devotion, in a place granted by Fulvius Hermes, four and a half feet in front, and for herself.
EDCS-27700171.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated by Lyris to her husband, Publius Aelius Satyrus, with additional mention of Papiria Helpidys, likely his wife, who also dedicates to him and reserves space for herself, on a plot granted by Fulvius Hermes.

EDCS-27700172.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aelia...
EDCS-27700172.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a woman named Aelia.

EDCS-27700173.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Marciae coniugi dulcissimae bene merenti quae bene vixit annis XXXV Turranius Firminianus maritus et Aelia Saturnina filia matri piissimae
EDCS-27700173.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Turranius Firminianus, her husband, and Aelia Saturnina, her daughter, [set this up] for Aelia Marcia, their most sweet and well-deserving wife and most devoted mother, who lived well for 35 years.
EDCS-27700173.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a husband and daughter to Aelia Marcia, who lived for 35 years and is celebrated as a sweet, well-deserving wife and devoted mother.

EDCS-27700174.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Salviae maritae Ulpius Primus et Ulpia Supera matri infelicissimae quae vicsit! cum Aelia Felicula annorum LV bene merenti
EDCS-27700174.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aelia Salvia, wife. Ulpius Primus and Ulpia Supera to their most unfortunate mother, who lived with Aelia Felicula for 55 years, to the well-deserving.
EDCS-27700174.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Primus and Ulpia Supera to their mother, Aelia Salvia, who lived for 55 years, presumably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27700175.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Tertullinae filiae piae defunctae annorum XII mensium VIII C Ursulus et Aelia Telesphoris parentes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-27700175.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aelia Tertullina, a dutiful daughter who died at 12 years and 8 months. C. Ursulus and Aelia Telesphoris, her parents, placed this for their well-deserving child.
EDCS-27700175.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by parents, C. Ursulus and Aelia Telesphoris, for their dutiful daughter, Aelia Tertullina, who died at the age of 12 years and 8 months.

EDCS-27700179.text_edition: Caius Clodius Felix IIIIIIvir Augustalis vivus fecit sibi et Clodiae Primigeniae filiae posterisque suis
EDCS-27700179.translation: Gaius Clodius Felix, Sevirus Augustalis, made this while living for himself and for his daughter Clodia Primigenia, and for his descendants.
EDCS-27700179.summary: This is a self-made funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Clodius Felix, a Sevirus Augustalis, for himself, his daughter Clodia Primigenia, and his future descendants, indicating a pre-planning for burial.

EDCS-27700180.text_edition: Publius Fundanius Philologus Augustalis titulum fieri iussit sibi et Fundaniae Egloge libertae ex uxori Zosimus et Felix liberti et heredes faciundum curaverunt
EDCS-27700180.translation: Publius Fundanius Philologus, Augustalis, ordered this inscription to be made for himself and for Fundania Egloge, his freedwoman and former wife. Zosimus and Felix, his freedmen and heirs, oversaw its construction.
EDCS-27700180.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Publius Fundanius Philologus, an Augustalis, for himself and his freedwoman and former wife Fundania Egloge, with his freedmen and heirs, Zosimus and Felix, completing the work.

EDCS-27700194.text_edition: Lucius Varius Luci libertus Ver IIIIIIvir Augustalis libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum hoc monumentum sive sepulcrum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes
EDCS-27700194.translation: Lucius Varius, freedman of Lucius, Ver, Sevirus Augustalis, to his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. This monument or tomb shall not pass to an heir. In front, 20 feet; in depth, [---] feet.
EDCS-27700194.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Varius, a freedman and Sevirus Augustalis, for his own freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, explicitly stating it will not be inherited and giving dimensions.

EDCS-27700195.text_edition: Eutycheti Vetti Bathylli servo domo Aquileiae Menelaus et Peculiaris conservo bene merenti
EDCS-27700195.translation: To Eutyches, slave of Vettius Bathyllus, from Aquileia. Menelaus and Peculiaris, to their well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-27700195.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by fellow slaves, Menelaus and Peculiaris, to Eutyches, a slave of Vettius Bathyllus, who originated from Aquileia.

EDCS-27700196.text_edition: Dis Manibus Saturnino Augusti nsotri vernae mensori Florentina coniux bene merenti
EDCS-27700196.translation: To the Divine Manes of Saturninus, our emperor's home-born slave and measurer. Florentina, his wife, to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27700196.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Florentina to her husband, Saturninus, a *verna* (home-born slave) of the emperor and a measurer, indicating his service in the imperial household.

EDCS-27700197.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valentinae alumnae et delicatae filiae Valentionis serbi sic vilici infelicissimae quae vixit nnos IIII Celerinus dominus posuit
EDCS-27700197.translation: To the Divine Manes of Valentina, foster-daughter and beloved daughter of Valentio, a slave and thus a bailiff, most unfortunate, who lived 4 years. Celerinus, her master, placed this.
EDCS-27700197.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Celerinus, the master, for Valentina, his foster-daughter and beloved child, who died at the age of 4, and whose father, Valentio, was a slave and bailiff.

EDCS-27700198.text_edition: Caio Valerio Cai filio Restituto filio Caio Valerio Cai liberto Restituto patri negotiatori vinario Marcus Nassius Sotericus amico bene merenti
EDCS-27700198.translation: To Gaius Valerius Restitutus, son of Gaius, his son. To Gaius Valerius Restitutus, freedman of Gaius, his father, a wine merchant. Marcus Nassius Sotericus to his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-27700198.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Nassius Sotericus to his friend, Gaius Valerius Restitutus the elder (a wine merchant and freedman of Gaius), and the younger Gaius Valerius Restitutus, son of Gaius.

EDCS-27700199.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Primitivo marito pientissimo Calventia Septimina bene merenti
EDCS-27700199.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aelius Primitivus, a most devoted husband. Calventia Septimina to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27700199.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Calventia Septimina to her most devoted and well-deserving husband, Aelius Primitivus.

EDCS-27700200.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Rufinae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit nnos XXXVII Iulius Menander et sibi
EDCS-27700200.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aelia Rufina, an incomparable wife, who lived for 37 years. Julius Menander (erected this) for himself and her.
EDCS-27700200.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius Menander to his incomparable wife Aelia Rufina and also for himself, indicating a shared burial monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700201.text_edition: Aeliae Ursinae Umbria Maxima matri piissimae posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27700201.translation: Umbria Maxima placed this for Aelia Ursina, a most devoted mother, who well deserved it.
EDCS-27700201.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Umbria Maxima in honor of her most devoted mother, Aelia Ursina, acknowledging her merits.

EDCS-27700203.text_edition: Aelia Candida se viva posuit sibi et coniugi Publio Oct(avio) Fortunato
EDCS-27700203.translation: Aelia Candida, while living, placed this for herself and for her husband Publius Octavius Fortunatus.
EDCS-27700203.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Candida during her lifetime for herself and her husband, Publius Octavius Fortunatus, indicating a pre-prepared burial plot or monument.

EDCS-27700204.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Constantilla et Aelia Concordia hAeredes#heredes#AEREDES Gnaeo Pompeio Threptioni posuaerunt vixit annos XXXX
EDCS-27700204.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Constantilla and Aelia Concordia, his heirs, placed this for Gnaeus Pompeius Threption. He lived for 40 years.
EDCS-27700204.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Constantilla and Aelia Concordia, as heirs, to Gnaeus Pompeius Threption, who lived to be 40 years old.

EDCS-27700205.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Victoriae defunctae annorum VIIII dierum XXX mater filiae pientissimae
EDCS-27700205.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aelia Victoria, who died at 9 years and 30 days old. Her mother (erected this) for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-27700205.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother to her most devoted daughter, Aelia Victoria, who passed away at a very young age.

EDCS-27700206.text_edition: N VLRF INF E annorum XXVI Fulvius Hermes collega et consacranius bene merenti et Eucida soror
EDCS-27700206.translation: [---] who lived 26 years. Fulvius Hermes, his colleague and fellow worshipper, (erected this) for the well-deserving, and Eucida, his sister.
EDCS-27700206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who lived 26 years, dedicated by Fulvius Hermes, a colleague and fellow worshipper, and by Eucida, the sister, acknowledging their merits.

EDCS-27700207.text_edition: Aurelius Peculiaris magister conquiliarius et Aurelia Urbica vivi sibi posuerunt si quis autem post obitum nostrum aliut sic corpus ponere voluerit inferat ecclesiae argenti pondo quinquaginta
EDCS-27700207.translation: Aurelius Peculiaris, master of shell-fish, and Aurelia Urbica, while living, placed this for themselves. If anyone, however, after our death, wishes to place any other body here, they shall pay fifty pounds of silver to the church.
EDCS-27700207.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Peculiaris, a master shellfish dealer, and Aurelia Urbica during their lifetimes for themselves, including a clause stipulating a fine to the church for unauthorized burials after their deaths, suggesting a later Roman or early Christian context.

EDCS-27700208.text_edition: Lucio Fabio Cai filio Clementi scribae nnorum XXX Cordia Auli filia Quarta mater posuit
EDCS-27700208.translation: Cordia Quarta, daughter of Aulus, his mother, placed this for Lucius Fabius Clemens, son of Gaius, a scribe, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-27700208.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cordia Quarta, daughter of Aulus, in memory of her son, Lucius Fabius Clemens, a scribe who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-27800055.text_edition: Marco Veratio Severino defuncto annorum V Marcus Veratius Severus veteranus legionis XXX Ulpiae victricis et Licinia vivi posuerunt filio suo et sibi
EDCS-27800055.translation: For Marcus Veratius Severinus, who died at 5 years old. Marcus Veratius Severus, a veteran of the Thirtieth Legion Ulpia Victrix, and Licinia, while living, placed this for their son and for themselves.
EDCS-27800055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Veratius Severus, a veteran of the Legio XXX Ulpia Victrix, and Licinia to their son, Marcus Veratius Severinus, and also for themselves, indicating a shared family tomb.

EDCS-27800515.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Lusius Cerdo sibi et coniugi suae vivis posuit
EDCS-27800515.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Lusius Cerdo, while living, placed this for himself and for his wife.
EDCS-27800515.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Lusius Cerdo during his lifetime for himself and his wife, indicating a pre-arranged burial plot or monument.

EDCS-27800686.translation: Marcus Domitius Salvianus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-27800686.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription from the 1st-2nd century AD, identifying Marcus Domitius Salvianus, son of Lucius, likely as the deceased.

EDCS-27800689.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27800689.text_edition: Titulum positum est Suetonio Iovino domNo#domo#DONO Durrachio nnorum LX ovit sic mense Ianuario III
EDCS-27800689.translation: This tombstone was placed for Suetonius Iovinus from Dyrrachium, a man of 60 years, who died on the 3rd of January.
EDCS-27800689.summary: This funerary inscription records the death and burial of Suetonius Iovinus, a 60-year-old man from Dyrrachium, who died on January 3rd, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-27800690.text_edition: Dis Ascaenis et Matri Magnae Ianae libens merito
EDCS-27800690.translation: To the Ascaenian gods and to the Great Mother Iana, willingly, deservedly.
EDCS-27800690.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Ascaenian gods and the Great Mother Iana, offered by an unnamed dedicator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-27900001.text_edition: Caecinia Calpurnia vixit annos VI dies XXX Calpurnius et Rufina parentes filiae dulcissimae posuerunt
EDCS-27900001.translation: Caecinia Calpurnia lived for 6 years and 30 days. Calpurnius and Rufina, her parents, placed this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-27900001.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caecinia Calpurnia at the age of six years and thirty days, set up by her parents Calpurnius and Rufina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900002.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesidiae Romanae Pomponius Crescens coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-27900002.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Pomponius Crescens [placed this] for Caesidia Romana, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27900002.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Caesidia Romana, set up by her husband Pomponius Crescens, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900003.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Caesionio Felici Coelia Firma marito bene merenti
EDCS-27900003.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Coelia Firma [placed this] for Gaius Caesionius Felix, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27900003.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Caesionius Felix, dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead by his wife Coelia Firma, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900004.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesoniae Firminae coniugi karissimae defunctae annorum XXI Urgulanius Faustus maritus bene merenti
EDCS-27900004.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Urgulanius Faustus [placed this] for Caesonia Firmina, his dearest wife, who died at 21 years of age, well-deserving.
EDCS-27900004.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Caesonia Firmina, who died at age 21, erected by her husband Urgulanius Faustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900005.text_edition: Publius Caetennius Publi libertus Aeschines vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Ithaces libertae Pantherae filiae defunctae nnorum XXVIII Marcus Subulnius Aprio
EDCS-27900005.translation: Publius Caetennius Aeschines, freedman of Publius, made this for himself while alive, and for Iulia Ithaces Panthera, his freedwoman daughter, who died at 28 years of age. Marcus Subulnius Aprio [completed or added this].
EDCS-27900005.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Caetennius Aeschines building a tomb for himself and his freedwoman daughter Iulia Ithaces Panthera, who died at 28, with Marcus Subulnius Aprio possibly completing or adding to it, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-27900006.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Caetennio Caeliano Publius Caetennius Caetennianus
EDCS-27900006.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publius Caetennius Caetennianus [placed this] for Publius Caetennius Caelianus.
EDCS-27900006.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Publius Caetennius Caelianus, set up by Publius Caetennius Caetennianus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900007.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. [Placed for] Publius Caetennius Onesimus.
EDCS-27900007.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Publius Caetennius Onesimus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900008.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Caetennio Primo Publio Caetennio Pudenti filiis Publius Caetennius Firmus
EDCS-27900008.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Caetennius Firmus [erected this] for his sons, Publius Caetennius Primus and Publius Caetennius Pudens.
EDCS-27900008.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a father, Publius Caetennius Firmus, for his two sons, Publius Caetennius Primus and Publius Caetennius Pudens.

EDCS-27900009.text_edition: Publius Caetennius Ursio Marciae Veneriae coniugi et Caetennio Ursino filio pientissimo
EDCS-27900009.translation: Publius Caetennius Ursio [erected this] for Marcia Veneria, his wife, and for Caetennius Ursinus, his most devoted son.
EDCS-27900009.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Publius Caetennius Ursio to his wife, Marcia Veneria, and his son, Caetennius Ursinus.

EDCS-27900010.text_edition: Dis Manibus Callisto nnorum II Cassius Faventinus alumno bene merenti
EDCS-27900010.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cassius Faventinus [erected this] for Callistus, his well-deserving foster-child, who lived for two years.
EDCS-27900010.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cassius Faventinus to his foster-child, Callistus, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-27900011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Callisteni Caius Aelius Epictetianus matri bene merenti
EDCS-27900011.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Callistenis. Gaius Aelius Epictetianus [erected this] for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-27900011.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gaius Aelius Epictetianus for his mother, Callistenis.

EDCS-27900012.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cameriae Heroidi defunctae annorum XVIII Maltinia Bonosa filiae infelicissimae
EDCS-27900012.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maltinia Bonosa [erected this] for her most unfortunate daughter, Cameria Herois, who died at the age of eighteen.
EDCS-27900012.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maltinia Bonosa to her daughter, Cameria Herois, who died at the age of eighteen.

EDCS-27900015.text_edition: Dis Manibus Candidae coniugi bene merenti nnorum plus minus XXX quae mecum vixit nnos plus minus VII quae est cruciata ut pariret diebus IIII et non peperit et est ita vita functa Iustus conservus posuit
EDCS-27900015.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Justus, fellow-slave, erected this for Candida, his well-deserving wife, who lived for more or less thirty years, who lived with me for more or less seven years, who was in labor for four days and did not give birth, and so passed from life.
EDCS-27900015.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription dedicated by a fellow-slave, Justus, to his wife, Candida, who died tragically in childbirth after living with him for about seven years.

EDCS-27900016.text_edition: Dis Manibus Carpophorus Soteriae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900016.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Carpophorus erected this for Soteria, his well-deserving [wife].
EDCS-27900016.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Carpophorus to Soteria, likely his wife.

EDCS-27900018.text_edition: Quinto Cassio Eutichi sic Cassia Eutichia sic mater
EDCS-27900018.translation: To Quintus Cassius Eutychus. So [also] Cassia Eutychia, so [also] mother.
EDCS-27900018.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription, possibly for a family plot, mentioning Quintus Cassius Eutychus, Cassia Eutychia, and their mother.

EDCS-27900019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Cassio Felicioni Quintus Cassius Tertullinus alumnus posuit
EDCS-27900019.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Quintus Cassius Felicio. Quintus Cassius Tertullinus, his foster-child, erected this.
EDCS-27900019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Cassius Tertullinus, a foster-child, to Quintus Cassius Felicio.

EDCS-27900022.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassiae Concordiae Caelia Bonosa ex testamento ipsius
EDCS-27900022.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Cassia Concordia. Caelia Bonosa [erected this] according to her will.
EDCS-27900022.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cassia Concordia, erected by Caelia Bonosa in accordance with Cassia Concordia's last will.

EDCS-27900023.text_edition: Cassia Constantilla Cassiae Secundae matri posuit in fronte pedes IV in agro pedes V
EDCS-27900023.translation: Cassia Constantilla set this up for Cassia Secunda, her mother. Four feet in front, five feet in depth.
EDCS-27900023.summary: This is a funerary inscription from an unspecified date, where Cassia Constantilla commemorates her mother, Cassia Secunda, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-27900026.translation: Cervia Siliciana [set this up] for Maritima, her foster-daughter.
EDCS-27900026.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from the Imperial period, where Cervia Siliciana commemorates her foster-daughter, Maritima.

EDCS-27900027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cingius Iustinus fecit piscinam in horto Aeliae Liciniae coniugi incomparabili et sibi
EDCS-27900027.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cingius Iustinus made this pool in the garden for Aelia Licinia, his incomparable wife, and for himself.
EDCS-27900027.summary: This inscription records the construction of a garden pool by Cingius Iustinus for his wife, Aelia Licinia, and for himself, suggesting a dedicatory or memorial purpose from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900028.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cirrotis Patrinus M R I posuit
EDCS-27900028.translation: To the Divine Manes. Patrinus M. R. I. set this up for Cirrotis.
EDCS-27900028.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes for Cirrotis, set up by Patrinus M. R. I., from an indeterminate Imperial date.

EDCS-27900030.text_edition: ori C PRI  Publi Claudi Severi
EDCS-27900030.translation: [To the Mem]ory of Publius Claudius Severus.
EDCS-27900030.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, dedicated to Publius Claudius Severus.

EDCS-27900031.text_edition: Claudiae Bonosae defunctae nnorum plus minus XXX Gelasius maritus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900031.translation: Gelasius, her husband, set this up for Claudia Bonosa, who died aged about 30 years, for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27900031.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Imperial period, where Gelasius commemorates his wife, Claudia Bonosa, who died around age 30.

EDCS-27900032.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudia CamNpana#Campana#CANPANA Claudio Peregrino marito qui annos LXXX bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900032.translation: To the Divine Manes. Claudia Campana set this up for Claudius Peregrinus, her husband, who lived 80 years, for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27900032.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes from the Imperial period, where Claudia Campana commemorates her husband, Claudius Peregrinus, who lived to the age of 80.

EDCS-27900033.translation: To the Divine Manes of Claudia Ianuaria.
EDCS-27900033.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription to the Divine Manes, dedicated to Claudia Ianuaria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-27900035.text_edition: annis VIII Clodius Adiutor pater infelicissimus filio piissimo bene merenti
EDCS-27900035.translation: After eight years, Clodius Adiutor, the most unhappy father, [set this up] for his most dutiful and well-deserving son.
EDCS-27900035.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Imperial period, where the grieving father, Clodius Adiutor, commemorates his pious and well-deserving son who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-27900036.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodio Hermeti Clodia Felicia patri
EDCS-27900036.translation: To the Divine Manes. Clodia Felicia [set this up] for Clodius Hermes, her father.
EDCS-27900036.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes from the Imperial period, where Clodia Felicia honors her father, Clodius Hermes.

EDCS-27900037.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodio Philargo Clodia Chara patrono bene merenti
EDCS-27900037.translation: To the Divine Manes. Clodia Chara to her patron, Clodius Philargus, who deserved well.
EDCS-27900037.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Clodia Chara to her deserving patron, Clodius Philargus.

EDCS-27900038.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodio Secundino defuncto annorum XXVI infelicissimo Aurelius Secundus et Clodia Firmina parentes
EDCS-27900038.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Secundus and Clodia Firmina, parents, to their most unhappy son, Clodius Secundinus, who died at 26 years of age.
EDCS-27900038.summary: This is a funerary inscription from parents, Aurelius Secundus and Clodia Firmina, for their deceased son, Clodius Secundinus, who died tragically at age 26.

EDCS-27900039.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodia Helpiduti matri Ancharia posuit bene merenti
EDCS-27900039.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ancharia placed this for her mother, Clodia Helpidus, who deserved well.
EDCS-27900039.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Ancharia for her deserving mother, Clodia Helpidus.

EDCS-27900041.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Claudia Primitiva, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-27900042.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Coelio Quintiano infanti amantissimo defuncto nnorum III mensium IX Obultronia Corinthia alumno dulcissimo
EDCS-27900042.translation: To the Divine Manes. Obultronia Corinthia to her dearest foster-child, Publius Coelius Quintianus, a most loving infant, who died at 3 years and 9 months.
EDCS-27900042.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Obultronia Corinthia for her beloved foster-child, Publius Coelius Quintianus, a young infant who died at 3 years and 9 months.

EDCS-27900043.text_edition: Coeliae Peregrinae Publi libertae defunctae nnum XVII Saecularis et Maria Coeli Balbini parentes filiae piissimae fecerunt
EDCS-27900043.translation: Saecularis and Maria Coeli Balbini, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter, Coelia Peregrina, freedwoman of Publius, who died at 17 years of age.
EDCS-27900043.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Saecularis and Maria Coeli Balbini for their exceptionally pious daughter, Coelia Peregrina, a freedwoman, who died at age 17.

EDCS-27900044.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Considius Viator qui et Gargilius vixit plus minus nnos XL memoriam posuit Amantius signu sic Simplici
EDCS-27900044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Considius Viator, also known as Gargilius, lived for approximately 40 years. Amantius placed this memorial, the sign thus to Simplex.
EDCS-27900044.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Considius Viator, also known as Gargilius, who lived about 40 years, with a memorial placed by Amantius.

EDCS-27900046.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cornelia Aquilina Felicissimo defuncto nnorum
EDCS-27900046.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Aquilina to Felicissimus, who died at [---] years of age.
EDCS-27900046.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription by Cornelia Aquilina for Felicissimus, whose age at death is mostly illegible.

EDCS-27900047.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning Cornelius Dom[---] and a foster-child.

EDCS-27900049.text_edition: Curio Ingenuo - Curio Secundo Caio Curio Ami Curia Syntyche
EDCS-27900049.translation: To Curius Ingenuus, to Curius Secundus, to Gaius Curius, to Syntyche Curia.
EDCS-27900049.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or record listing multiple individuals from the Curia family: Curius Ingenuus, Curius Secundus, Gaius Curius, and Curia Syntyche.

EDCS-27900051.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Curtio Eutycho Caesonia Thalassa coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900051.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesonia Thalassa placed this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Curtius Eutychus.
EDCS-27900051.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by Caesonia Thalassa to her deceased husband, Gaius Curtius Eutychus.

EDCS-27900052.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Dalmati qui vixit annos    Dis Manibus  Valentinae defunctae annorum plus minus trium Claudia L mater FNPAR
EDCS-27900052.translation: To the Divine Manes of Dalmatius, who lived for [---] years. To the Divine Manes of Valentina, who died at approximately three years of age. Claudia L. mater FNPAR.
EDCS-27900052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia L. as a mother to her deceased children, Dalmatius and Valentina.

EDCS-27900053.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dassio homini infelicissimo defuncto nnorum XXVI mensium VIII Honesime sic soror posuit
EDCS-27900053.translation: To the Divine Manes. Honesime, his sister, placed this for Dassius, a most unfortunate man, who died at 26 years and 8 months of age.
EDCS-27900053.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Honesime to her brother, Dassius, marking his untimely death at 26 years and 8 months old.

EDCS-27900054.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dato filio bene merenti parentes fecerunt Severa etI#et#EI Datus qui vixit annis X mensibus IIII
EDCS-27900054.translation: To the Divine Manes. His parents, Severa and Datus, made this for their well-deserving son, Datus, who lived for 10 years and 4 months.
EDCS-27900054.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by parents, Severa and Datus, for their deceased son, Datus, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-27900055.text_edition: Dis Manibus Decembro Iucunda amico incomparabili qui vixit plus minus nnos XXXV
EDCS-27900055.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iucunda placed this for her incomparable friend, December, who lived for approximately 35 years.
EDCS-27900055.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iucunda to her friend December, who lived to about 35 years of age.

EDCS-27900057.text_edition: Dis Manibus Donatae filiae quae vixit nnos XXII mater infelicissima posuit
EDCS-27900057.translation: To the Divine Manes. Her most unfortunate mother placed this for her daughter Donata, who lived for 22 years.
EDCS-27900057.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a grieving mother to her daughter, Donata, who died at 22 years old.

EDCS-27900058.text_edition: Dis Manibus Donatae Ursinae coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-27900058.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Donata Ursina, a well-deserving wife.
EDCS-27900058.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Donata Ursina, likely by her spouse, acknowledging her as a well-deserving wife.

EDCS-27900061.text_edition: Dis Manibus Epiteuxi defunctae annorum?  Marcus Arruntius Zosimus Rufo filio pientissimo
EDCS-27900061.translation: To the Divine Manes of Epiteuxis, who died at [---] years of age. Marcus Arruntius Zosimus [placed this] for his most dutiful son, Rufus.
EDCS-27900061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Arruntius Zosimus to his dutiful son, Rufus, with an additional mention of Epiteuxis.

EDCS-27900062.text_edition: Dis Manibus Heuodia sic alumnae Scriboniae Corneliae annorum XVIII
EDCS-27900062.translation: To the Divine Manes. Heuodia [placed this] for her foster-daughter, Scribonia Cornelia, who was 18 years old.
EDCS-27900062.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Heuodia to her foster-daughter, Scribonia Cornelia, who passed away at eighteen.

EDCS-27900063.text_edition: Dis Manibus Heusebio sic defuncto nnorum XXX Sura conservo piissimo
EDCS-27900063.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sura [placed this] for his most dutiful fellow slave, Eusebius, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-27900063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sura to his fellow slave, Eusebius, commemorating his death at age thirty.

EDCS-27900064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Eutyches vixit nnos XIII menses II dies XX Fabricius Asclepiades alumni sic bene merenti
EDCS-27900064.translation: To the Divine Manes. Eutyches lived 13 years, 2 months, 20 days. Fabricius Asclepiades, his alumnus, dedicated this to his well-deserving foster child.
EDCS-27900064.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fabricius Asclepiades to his deceased foster child, Eutyches, who lived for 13 years.

EDCS-27900066.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Faberia Eutychia Faberiae Fortunatae patronae bene merenti titulum posuit
EDCS-27900066.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Faberia Eutychia set up this monument for Faberia Fortunata, her well-deserving patroness.
EDCS-27900066.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Faberia Eutychia to her patroness, Faberia Fortunata, likely a tombstone for a freedwoman by her former slave.

EDCS-27900067.text_edition: Fabricius Agatho posuit Agathino filio suo annuculo et mensum sic sex
EDCS-27900067.translation: Fabricius Agatho set this up for his son Agathinus, who was one year and six months old.
EDCS-27900067.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fabricius Agatho to his infant son, Agathinus, who died at the age of one year and six months.

EDCS-27900069.text_edition: Feresiae Quinti et Cai libertae Speratae Quintus Feresius Quinti libertus Chrestus fecit et
EDCS-27900069.translation: Quintus Feresius Chrestus, freedman of Quintus, made this for Feresia Sperata, freedwoman of Quintus and Gaius, and...
EDCS-27900069.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, set up by Quintus Feresius Chrestus for Feresia Sperata, both freedpersons sharing patrons.

EDCS-27900070.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marronia Firma, wife of Aplus.
EDCS-27900070.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marronia Firma, identified as the wife of Aplus.

EDCS-27900071.translation: A. Falx Firmanus is buried here.
EDCS-27900071.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of A. Falx Firmanus.

EDCS-27900072.text_edition: Dis Manibus Firmiae Soteridi nnorum XXXI Exuperatus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900072.translation: To the Divine Manes. Exuperatus dedicated this to Firmia Soteris, his well-deserving wife, who lived 31 years.
EDCS-27900072.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Exuperatus to his wife, Firmia Soteris, who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-27900074.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Primitivos sic Paramone matri bene merenti
EDCS-27900074.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Flavius Primitivos dedicated this to his well-deserving mother, Paramone.
EDCS-27900074.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Primitivos to his mother, Paramone.

EDCS-27900075.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flabius sic Saecundinus Iuliae Maiae
EDCS-27900075.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Secundinus to Iulia Maia.
EDCS-27900075.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavius Secundinus dedicated to Iulia Maia.

EDCS-27900077.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Thallioni Cossonia Eutychia coniugi bene m(erenti) p(osuit)
EDCS-27900077.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cossonia Eutychia set this up for Flavius Thallio, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27900077.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cossonia Eutychia to her husband, Flavius Thallio.

EDCS-27900078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Vero defuncto nnorum XXIII Rufus fratri pientissimo posuit
EDCS-27900078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Rufus placed this for his most devoted brother, Titus Flavius Verus, who died at 23 years of age.
EDCS-27900078.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Rufus for his brother, Titus Flavius Verus, who died at age 23.

EDCS-27900082.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunato obsequentissimo et innocentissimo alumno annorum plus minus XVII Marcus Lectinius Cornelianus bene merenti
EDCS-27900082.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Lectinius Cornelianus [placed this] for the well-deserving Fortunatus, his most obedient and most innocent foster-son, who was about 17 years old.
EDCS-27900082.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Marcus Lectinius Cornelianus to his deserving foster-son, Fortunatus, who was approximately 17 years old.

EDCS-27900083.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortum Fortunato Caesaris nostri vernae Chreste contubernalis cum filio bene merenti
EDCS-27900083.translation: To the Divine Manes. Chreste, his messmate, with her son, [placed this] for the well-deserving Fortunatus, our Caesar's home-born slave.
EDCS-27900083.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication by Chreste and her son to Fortunatus, a home-born slave of the emperor and Chreste's messmate.

EDCS-27900085.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunata coniugi Parthinopeo et filiis infelicissimis posuit
EDCS-27900085.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fortunata placed this for her husband Parthinopaeus and for her most unfortunate children.
EDCS-27900085.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Fortunata, commemorating her husband Parthinopaeus and their children.

EDCS-27900087.translation: Gaius Fuficius Sabinus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-27900087.summary: This brief inscription identifies Gaius Fuficius Sabinus as a freedman of a man named Gaius Fuficius.

EDCS-27900089.text_edition: Dis Manibus Galliae Secundae Baebilius Genethlius Iulius Zmaragdus heredes bene merenti
EDCS-27900089.translation: To the Divine Manes. Baebilius Genethlius [and] Iulius Zmaragdus, heirs, [placed this] for the well-deserving Gallia Secunda.
EDCS-27900089.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the heirs Baebilius Genethlius and Iulius Zmaragdus to Gallia Secunda.

EDCS-27900090.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gamus Laeliae Liberali coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-27900090.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gamus placed this for his well-deserving wife, Laelia Liberalis.
EDCS-27900090.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Gamus for his wife, Laelia Liberalis.

EDCS-27900091.text_edition: Sextus Gellius Primus et Caetennia Artimisia Gelliae Ursae libertae suae bene meritae posuerunt annorum XX hic sita est
EDCS-27900091.translation: Sextus Gellius Primus and Caetennia Artimisia placed this for their well-deserving freedwoman, Gellia Ursa. She lived for 20 years. She lies here.
EDCS-27900091.summary: This funerary inscription was placed by Sextus Gellius Primus and Caetennia Artimisia for their freedwoman, Gellia Ursa, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-27900095.text_edition: Herennio Apelti Iulia Herbula marito posuit
EDCS-27900095.translation: Julia Herbula placed this for her husband, Herennius Apeltes.
EDCS-27900095.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Julia Herbula dedicated to her husband, Herennius Apeltes.

EDCS-27900096.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Herennio Papirio defuncto annorum XXVI sodales sodali bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-27900096.translation: To the Divine Manes. The members of the association placed this for their well-deserving fellow member, Quintus Herennius Papirius, who died at 26 years of age.
EDCS-27900096.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by the members of an association for their fellow member, Quintus Herennius Papirius, who died at age 26.

EDCS-27900099.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titio Hilarioni marito bene merenti Cassia Maxima coniux
EDCS-27900099.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cassia Maxima, his wife, to her well-deserving husband Titius Hilario.
EDCS-27900099.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cassia Maxima to her deceased husband Titius Hilario.

EDCS-27900100.text_edition: Homonoea Zosime viva sibi posuit et Septimio Lucio marito suo
EDCS-27900100.translation: Homonoea Zosime, while living, set this up for herself and for her husband, Septimius Lucius.
EDCS-27900100.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Homonoea Zosime for herself and her husband Septimius Lucius during their lifetime.

EDCS-27900102.translation: Anemia set this up for her most loving husband Honorius.
EDCS-27900102.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Anemia to her deceased husband Honorius.

EDCS-27900103.translation: To Horia Prima, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-27900103.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Horia Prima, who was a freedwoman of Marcus.

EDCS-27900105.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ianuarius infantem sic infelici lapidem fecit
EDCS-27900105.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ianuarius, unhappily, made this stone for his infant child.
EDCS-27900105.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating an infant child, erected by the grieving father Ianuarius.

EDCS-28000001.text_edition: Dis Manibus Philadelphi qui et Polydapanus Stratonice conserva bene merenti
EDCS-28000001.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Philadelphus, also known as Polydapanus. Stratonice, his fellow slave, to her well-deserving (companion).
EDCS-28000001.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Stratonice, a fellow slave, to Philadelphus (also known as Polydapanus).

EDCS-28000002.text_edition: Dis Manibus Filinae defunctae nnorum XVIII nondum libertae Cassia Constantilla posuit
EDCS-28000002.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Filina, who died at 18 years old, not yet a freedwoman. Cassia Constantilla placed this.
EDCS-28000002.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Filina, who died at 18 years old while still enslaved, set up by Cassia Constantilla.

EDCS-28000003.text_edition: Lucio Plotio Luci filio Pollia Basso
EDCS-28000003.translation: To Lucius Plotius Bassus, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe.
EDCS-28000003.summary: This is an inscription commemorating Lucius Plotius Bassus, son of Lucius, indicating his Roman citizenship and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-28000004.text_edition: Plotiae Secundinae Lucius Plotius Eutyches libertus patronae bene merenti
EDCS-28000004.translation: Lucius Plotius Eutyches, freedman, to his well-deserving patroness Plotia Secundina.
EDCS-28000004.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Plotius Eutyches, a freedman, to his deceased patroness Plotia Secundina.

EDCS-28000006.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Pomponius C. P.
EDCS-28000006.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Pomponius, whose full name is abbreviated as C. P.

EDCS-28000010.text_edition: Pomponiae Alce Pomponia Arisbe sorori et libertae carissimae
EDCS-28000010.translation: To Pomponia Alce, Pomponia Arisbe [set this up] for her dearest sister and freedwoman.
EDCS-28000010.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Pomponia Arisbe to her sister and freedwoman, Pomponia Alce.

EDCS-28000011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pomponiae Sabinae defunctae annorum IIII Flaviae Aciliae  et Pomponiae Thalassiae alumnae infelicissimae
EDCS-28000011.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Pomponia Sabina, who died at the age of 4, [and for] Flavia Acilia and Pomponia Thalassia, her most unfortunate foster-parents.
EDCS-28000011.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Pomponia Sabina, who died at age four, and also mentions her sorrowful foster-parents, Flavia Acilia and Pomponia Thalassia.

EDCS-28000012.text_edition: Pomponia Semne et Pomponia Quarta libertae patron- bene merenti
EDCS-28000012.translation: Pomponia Semne and Pomponia Quarta, freedwomen, [set this up] for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-28000012.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two freedwomen, Pomponia Semne and Pomponia Quarta, to their deserving patron.

EDCS-28000014.text_edition: PontianHae#Pontianae#PONTIAHE PANONESA sibi etI#et#EI coniug=NSi#coniugi#CONNSI suo  postIerP(is)qO(ue)#poster(is)q(ue)#POSIEPO eorum
EDCS-28000014.translation: Pontiana Panonesa [made this] for herself and for her husband and for their descendants.
EDCS-28000014.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Pontiana Panonesa for herself, her husband, and their descendants, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-28000016.text_edition: Pontiena Cale viva fecit sibi et Tito Flavio Augusti liberto Suavi viro Quinto Pontieno Suavi filio nnorum X
EDCS-28000016.translation: Pontiena Cale, while living, made this for herself and for Titus Flavius Suavis, freedman of Augustus, her husband, and for Quintus Pontienus Suavis, her son, aged 10.
EDCS-28000016.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Pontiena Cale during her lifetime for herself, her husband Titus Flavius Suavis, and her ten-year-old son Quintus Pontienus Suavis.

EDCS-28000018.translation: Praesentilla, most obedient, set this up for her father.
EDCS-28000018.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Praesentilla to her father, whom she describes as most obedient.

EDCS-28000019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primillae infelici defunctae nnorum XVIII infelicissimus coniunx
EDCS-28000019.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Primilla, unfortunate, who died at the age of 18; her most unfortunate husband [set this up].
EDCS-28000019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a grieving husband to his unfortunate wife, Primilla, who died at the age of eighteen.

EDCS-28000022.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Sextus Barbius Proculus.
EDCS-28000022.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Sextus Barbius Proculus.

EDCS-28000024.text_edition: Prostinia Cai filia Procula viva fecit sibi et Faventinae delicatae defunctae annorum X
EDCS-28000024.translation: Prostinia Procula, daughter of Gaius, while living, made this for herself and for Faventina Delicata, who died at the age of 10.
EDCS-28000024.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Prostinia Procula, daughter of Gaius, for herself and for Faventina Delicata, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-28000026.text_edition: Prudenti nnorum XX Proba mater posuit bene merenti
EDCS-28000026.translation: Proba, his mother, set this up for Prudentius, aged 20, well-deserving.
EDCS-28000026.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother, Proba, to her well-deserving son, Prudentius, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-28000031.translation: Rupia Crescentilla, while alive, set this up for herself.
EDCS-28000031.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Rupia Crescentilla built her own tomb while still living.

EDCS-28000032.text_edition: Quintae alumnae defunctae nnorum XXVI Lollius Cerdo
EDCS-28000032.translation: To Quinta, my deceased foster daughter, who lived 26 years. Lollius Cerdo [erected this].
EDCS-28000032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lollius Cerdo to his foster daughter Quinta, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-28000035.text_edition: Raia Valeria Vi nn marito pientissimo defuncto nnorum plus minus L cum quo vixit innocentissime
EDCS-28000035.translation: Raia Valeria Vi[ctorina] to her most devoted husband, who died at approximately 50 years of age, with whom she lived most blamelessly.
EDCS-28000035.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a wife, Raia Valeria Vi[ctorina], to her late husband, commemorating their blameless life together.

EDCS-28000036.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ravoniae Eucarpiae Ravonia Placontis matri et Niceni delicatae defunctae nnrum XIII
EDCS-28000036.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Ravonia Eucarpia. Ravonia Placontis [erected this] for her mother, and Niceni [erected this] for her beloved daughter, who died at 13 years of age.
EDCS-28000036.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ravonia Placontis to her mother, Ravonia Eucarpia, and also by Niceni to her beloved daughter, who died young at 13.

EDCS-28000038.text_edition: Romania Sabina Titi liberta Firmioni et Martiali et Quintioni filiis viva se fecit
EDCS-28000038.translation: Romania Sabina, freedwoman of Titus, built this for herself while alive, and for her sons Firmio, Martial, and Quintio.
EDCS-28000038.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Romania Sabina, a freedwoman, for herself and her three sons while she was still living.

EDCS-28000039.text_edition: Romania Thalia viva fecit sibi et Romanio Secundino et Romanio Surioni filio et Romaniae Saloninae filiae et Chrissio liberto Lupae libertae et Sabinae Sabiniano Secundae filiis
EDCS-28000039.translation: Romania Thalia, while alive, made this for herself, and for Romanio Secundino, and for her son Romanio Surio, and for her daughter Romania Salonina, and for her freedman Chrissius, and for her freedwoman Lupa, and for her daughters Sabina and Sabiniana Secunda.
EDCS-28000039.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Romania Thalia while alive, for herself and a large family group including her husband, children, freedpeople, and grandchildren.

EDCS-28000041.translation: Rufina to Secundinus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28000041.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Rufina to her husband Secundinus, honoring him as well-deserving.

EDCS-28000042.text_edition: Rusticia Clodiana filio carissimo Rusticiano qui vixit annos XXI ego misera mater emi ex proprio e meo peto bos sic fratres ne qui sic alium ponatis super
EDCS-28000042.translation: Rusticia Clodiana to her dearest son Rusticianus, who lived 21 years. I, his miserable mother, bought this from my own funds. I ask you, brothers, that no one places anything else on top of this.
EDCS-28000042.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a grieving mother, Rusticia Clodiana, for her son Rusticianus, who died at 21, with an appeal to prevent further burials on the tomb.

EDCS-28000043.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sabinus Sabinae matri dulcissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000043.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Sabinus placed this for Sabina, his sweetest, well-deserving mother.
EDCS-28000043.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sabinus to his mother Sabina, honoring her as sweetest and well-deserving.

EDCS-28000045.text_edition: Saccario ? coniugi inconparabili --- sic  Hic ego --- sum perpetuus  SEMICI qui MEDLI ux ADI vivos sic autem SA et HIIhospitia vestra A BAINTRA et benefac E si vos at sic me venietis   cum Leptiana
EDCS-28000045.translation: To Saccarius, my incomparable spouse. Here I am, perpetual. SEMICI, who... lived with ME… and HII, your hospices… and do good. If you approach me in this way with Leptiana.
EDCS-28000045.summary: This is a complex and partially illegible funerary inscription, possibly for Saccarius, invoking the concept of perpetual presence and offering blessings to visitors who approach with kindness or with Leptiana.

EDCS-28000046.text_edition: Satrenio Victorino filio infelicissimo qui vixit nnos V menses V dies VI Satrenius Victor et Sempronia Maxima parentes
EDCS-28000046.translation: To Satrenius Victorinus, their most unfortunate son, who lived 5 years, 5 months, 6 days. Satrenius Victor and Sempronia Maxima, his parents, [set this up].
EDCS-28000046.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating a young boy, Satrenius Victorinus, erected by his grieving parents Satrenius Victor and Sempronia Maxima, likely from the late 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28000048.text_edition: Dis Manibus Scantiae Zoini Fyrmia sic Paeraegrina sic ANNAE bene merenti
EDCS-28000048.translation: To the Divine Manes of Scantia Zoina. Fyrmia Paeraegrina and Anna, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28000048.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Scantia Zoina by Fyrmia Paeraegrina and Anna, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000049.text_edition: Secundae filiae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos plus minus Dassius et Rosula parentes posuerunt
EDCS-28000049.translation: To Secunda, their most unfortunate daughter, who lived more or less [X] years. Dassius and Rosula, her parents, placed this.
EDCS-28000049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Secunda, erected by her parents Dassius and Rosula, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000050.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundinus et Florentina parentes Severo filio annorum IIII infelicissimo posuerunt
EDCS-28000050.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundinus and Florentina, parents, placed this for their most unfortunate son Severus, aged 4 years.
EDCS-28000050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 4-year-old boy named Severus, set up by his parents Secundinus and Florentina, typical of the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-28000051.text_edition: annorum XVIII fratri piissimo Secundinus item Secundino bene merenti Miliesis fecit
EDCS-28000051.translation: For [X] years old. To his most devoted brother, Secundinus also made this for the well-deserving Secundinus. Miliesis made it.
EDCS-28000051.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a brother, also named Secundinus, by Secundinus and Miliesis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000052.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundioni iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit nnos XXVII Secundus posuit
EDCS-28000052.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Secundio, a most unfortunate youth who lived 27 years. Secundus placed this.
EDCS-28000052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man named Secundio, who lived 27 years, erected by Secundus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000053.text_edition: Seio Dalmatae homini bono qOui#qui#OUI incendio oppressus est Aurelia Victorina uxor
EDCS-28000053.translation: To Seius Dalmata, a good man who was overcome by fire. Aurelia Victorina, his wife.
EDCS-28000053.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Victorina to her husband Seius Dalmata, who tragically died in a fire, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000054.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Gellio Artorio defuncto nnrum VIII mensium VIIII Artoria Secundina et Gellius Felix pro pietate filio infelicissimo
EDCS-28000054.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Gellius Artorius, deceased, aged 8 years, 9 months. Artoria Secundina and Gellius Felix, for their most unfortunate son, out of piety.
EDCS-28000054.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Lucius Gellius Artorius, erected by his parents Artoria Secundina and Gellius Felix, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28000057.text_edition: Seppienae Philomenae vivae Seppiena Restituta liberta VI M matri fecit
EDCS-28000057.translation: To Seppiena Philomena, while living. Seppiena Restituta, her freedwoman, made this for her mother.
EDCS-28000057.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a freedwoman, Seppiena Restituta, to her patroness and 'mother' Seppiena Philomena, while the latter was still alive, indicating a dedicatory or pre-mortem tomb construction from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000059.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimo bene merenti Urgulanius Profuturus et Vesidia Cassia amico posuerunt
EDCS-28000059.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Septimus, well-deserving. Urgulanius Profuturus and Vesidia Cassia placed this for their friend.
EDCS-28000059.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Septimus by his friends Urgulanius Profuturus and Vesidia Cassia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000060.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Servilio Apolloniano et Caio Servilio Proculo Iulia Restuta filio et nepoti
EDCS-28000060.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Servilius Apollonianus and Gaius Servilius Proculus. Julia Restuta (set this up) for her son and grandson.
EDCS-28000060.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Restuta to her son, Gaius Servilius Apollonianus, and her grandson, Gaius Servilius Proculus.

EDCS-28000061.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Servilio Eutycheti defuncto nnorum XXXIII Aurelia Maxima coniugi inconparabili sic bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000061.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Servilius Eutyches, who died at 33 years of age. Aurelia Maxima placed this for her incomparable and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28000061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Maxima to her deceased husband, Marcus Servilius Eutyches, who lived for 33 years.

EDCS-28000063.text_edition: Caio Sevio Tertio Caio Sevio Tempestivo filio Celandus
EDCS-28000063.translation: To Gaius Sevius Tertius, (and) to Gaius Sevius Tempestivus, his son. Celandus (set this up).
EDCS-28000063.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Celandus dedicated to Gaius Sevius Tertius and his son, Gaius Sevius Tempestivus.

EDCS-28000064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sextiliae Secundinae coniugi sanctissimae Lucius Baebilius Hyginus
EDCS-28000064.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Sextilia Secundina, his most holy wife. Lucius Baebilius Hyginus (set this up).
EDCS-28000064.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Baebilius Hyginus dedicated to his deceased wife, Sextilia Secundina.

EDCS-28000065.text_edition: Caio Sossio Cai liberto Philomuso testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Cai Sossi Cai liberti Severi et Luci Genuci Luci liberti Rustici
EDCS-28000065.translation: Gaius Sossius Philomus, freedman of Gaius, ordered this to be made by his will, under the judgment of Gaius Sossius Severus, freedman of Gaius, and Lucius Genucus Rusticus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-28000065.summary: This inscription records the testamentary instructions of Gaius Sossius Philomus, a freedman, for the erection of a monument, supervised by his fellow freedman and another freedman.

EDCS-28000066.text_edition: Lucius Egnatius Sotericus hic situs est
EDCS-28000066.translation: Lucius Egnatius Sotericus is buried here.
EDCS-28000066.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Lucius Egnatius Sotericus.

EDCS-28000067.translation: To the Genius of the place. Statius Clementianus placed this.
EDCS-28000067.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Statius Clementianus to the Genius (protective spirit) of a specific location.

EDCS-28000069.text_edition: Dis Manibus Statia Prima Statio Ursino coniugi bene merenti ex pietate posuit cum quo vixit annis XXX
EDCS-28000069.translation: To the Divine Manes. Statia Prima, out of piety, placed this for her well-deserving husband, Statius Ursinus, with whom she lived for 30 years.
EDCS-28000069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Statia Prima to her well-deserving husband, Statius Ursinus, with whom she shared 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-28000071.text_edition: Steniae Securae Lucius Publicius Trophimus uxori optimae
EDCS-28000071.translation: To Stenia Secura, his excellent wife. Lucius Publicius Trophimus (set this up).
EDCS-28000071.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Publicius Trophimus to his excellent wife, Stenia Secura.

EDCS-28000074.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sym defunct- annorum VIIII Artemisia filio infelicissimo
EDCS-28000074.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Sym, who died at 9 years of age. Artemisia (set this up) for her most unhappy son.
EDCS-28000074.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Artemisia to her deceased son, Sym, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-28000075.text_edition: Dis Manibus Symforus Primae sorori bene merenti
EDCS-28000075.translation: To the Divine Shades. Symforus to Prima, his well-deserving sister.
EDCS-28000075.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Symforus to his sister, Prima, who was well-deserving.

EDCS-28000077.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaio T B Pon Zo C bene merenti SO EIP infelicissim-
EDCS-28000077.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius T.B. Pon. Zo. C., well-deserving, [S.O.E.I.P.] most unfortunate.
EDCS-28000077.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius T.B. Pon. Zo. C., described as well-deserving and most unfortunate, likely by a bereaved individual or group.

EDCS-28000082.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tar Tertia Nigrino filio pientissimo infelicissimo vixit annos XVI menses  dies XV mater filio posuit
EDCS-28000082.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tar. Tertia to Nigrinus, her most dutiful and most unfortunate son, who lived 16 years, 7 months, 15 days. His mother placed this for her son.
EDCS-28000082.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a mother, Tar. Tertia, for her deeply beloved and unfortunate son, Nigrinus, who died at the age of sixteen.

EDCS-28000083.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tattiae Laemotinae defunctae nnorum XXIII Caetennia Amullina bene merenti
EDCS-28000083.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tattia Laemotina, deceased at 23 years of age. Caetennia Amullina [placed this] for her well-deserving [relative/friend].
EDCS-28000083.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caetennia Amullina to Tattia Laemotina, who passed away at 23 years old.

EDCS-28000087.translation: To Tertius, [who lived] 7 years. His father [placed this] for his son.
EDCS-28000087.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription erected by a father for his son, Tertius, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-28000088.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tertius.
EDCS-28000088.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription dedicated to an individual named Tertius.

EDCS-28000089.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tertulliano vixit nnos V Aurelius Tertullus nepoti posuit
EDCS-28000089.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tertullianus, who lived 5 years. Aurelius Tertullus placed this for his grandson.
EDCS-28000089.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Tertullus to his grandson, Tertullianus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-28000090.text_edition: Tertullino dedefuncto nnorum VLLI Diogenes pater filio infelicissimo
EDCS-28000090.translation: To Tertullinus, deceased at 8 years. Diogenes, his father, [placed this] for his most unfortunate son.
EDCS-28000090.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Diogenes, the father, for his unfortunate son, Tertullinus, who died at the age of eight.

EDCS-28000094.text_edition: Lucio Tigidio Proculo Tigidia Tertulla pa=ERtri#patri#ERTRI posuit
EDCS-28000094.translation: Tigidia Tertulla placed this for Lucius Tigidius Proculus, her father.
EDCS-28000094.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tigidia Tertulla to her father, Lucius Tigidius Proculus.

EDCS-28000095.text_edition: CESIOInae alumnae Titi Firmi defunctae nnorum XXIIX Publius Coelius Eutyches uxori bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000095.translation: To Caesionia, the foster-daughter of Titus Firmus, deceased at 28 years. Publius Coelius Eutyches placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28000095.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Coelius Eutyches to his well-deserving wife, Caesionia, who was the foster-daughter of Titus Firmus and died at the age of 28.

EDCS-28000096.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titiae Heliadi Lucius Titius Callistus collibertae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000096.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Titius Callistus placed this for his well-deserving freedwoman, Titia Heliad.
EDCS-28000096.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Titius Callistus dedicating a monument to his freedwoman, Titia Heliad.

EDCS-28000099.text_edition: Dis Manibus Threptioni Papinia Fesstia sic coniugi
EDCS-28000099.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Papinia Fesstia placed this for her husband Threption.
EDCS-28000099.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Papinia Fesstia dedicating a monument to her husband, Threption.
