EDCS-28000100.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Turpinae uxori Tertius maritus et Eutyches frater posuerunt animae innocenti sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-28000100.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Tertius, her husband, and Eutyches, her brother, placed this for their wife Turpina, an innocent soul. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-28000100.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Tertius and Eutyches dedicating a monument to Turpina, Tertius's wife and Eutyches's sister.

EDCS-28000105.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Adaucto patrono bene merenti Caius Valerius Euplus libertus posuit
EDCS-28000105.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Valerius Euplus, a freedman, placed this for his well-deserving patron, Gaius Valerius Adauctus.
EDCS-28000105.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a freedman, Gaius Valerius Euplus, dedicating a monument to his patron, Gaius Valerius Adauctus.

EDCS-28000133.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iucunda Tarconio Agathemero conservo defuncto annorum XXX infelici posuit
EDCS-28000133.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iucunda placed this for her unfortunate fellow-slave, Tarconius Agathemerus, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-28000133.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Iucunda dedicating a monument to her fellow-slave, Tarconius Agathemerus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-28000136.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Atiliano posuerunt parentes filio carissimo bene merenti
EDCS-28000136.translation: To the Divine Spirits. His parents placed this for their dearest and well-deserving son, Iulius Atilianus.
EDCS-28000136.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the parents of Iulius Atilianus dedicating a monument to their beloved son.

EDCS-28000137.text_edition: Iulio Crescenti infanti Marcus Iulius Cronius? ---
EDCS-28000137.translation: For the infant Iulius Crescens, Marcus Iulius Cronius (?) [placed this].
EDCS-28000137.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely incomplete, dedicated to the infant Iulius Crescens by Marcus Iulius Cronius, possibly his father.

EDCS-28000138.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Epiforeto defuncto filia patri pientissimo
EDCS-28000138.translation: To the Divine Spirits. His daughter placed this for her most devoted father, Iulius Epiforetus, who has died.
EDCS-28000138.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the daughter of Iulius Epiforetus dedicating a monument to her deceased father.

EDCS-28000141.text_edition: CTL anTnorum#annorum#ATNORUM VIIII pater Iulius Ianuarius et Urbulania Secuindina filiae infelicissimae posuerunt
EDCS-28000141.translation: Iulius Ianuarius, the father, and Urbulania Secuindina placed this for their most unfortunate daughter, who lived for 9 years.
EDCS-28000141.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Iulius Ianuarius and Urbulania Secuindina dedicating a monument to their daughter, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-28000142.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Iasoni Pomponia Ammia coniugi pientissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000142.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pomponia Ammia placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving husband, Iulius Iason.
EDCS-28000142.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Pomponia Ammia dedicating a monument to her husband, Iulius Iason.

EDCS-28000143.text_edition: Iulio Messori Phoebus avomculo sic bene merenti
EDCS-28000143.translation: To Julius Messor, Phoebus [dedicates this] to his avunculus, so well-deserving.
EDCS-28000143.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Phoebus to his maternal uncle, Julius Messor, indicating a familial relationship and esteem.

EDCS-28000146.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Primitivo Ravonia Placontis marito bene merenti
EDCS-28000146.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Julius Primitivus, Ravonia Placontis [dedicates this] to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28000146.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ravonia Placontis to her deceased husband, Julius Primitivus.

EDCS-28000147.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Primitivo defuncto annorum V Primus et Septimina filio piissimo et Ursula alumno
EDCS-28000147.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Julius Primitivus, who died at the age of 5. Primus and Septimina [dedicate this] to their most dutiful son, and Ursula to her foster child.
EDCS-28000147.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Julius Primitivus, who died at age five, erected by his parents Primus and Septimina, and also by Ursula, likely his nurse or guardian.

EDCS-28000149.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Iulius S Psalmus Iuliae Stratoniceni coniugi
EDCS-28000149.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Julius S. Psalmus [dedicates this] to his wife, Julia Stratoniceni.
EDCS-28000149.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Julius S. Psalmus to his wife, Julia Stratoniceni.

EDCS-28000150.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Valentino nutrix et Flavia Iustina virginia ipsius pientissimo defuncto annorumN#annorum#ANNORUN plus minus XXV
EDCS-28000150.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Julius Valentinus, who died most dutifully, approximately 25 years old. His nurse and Flavia Iustina, his virgin, [dedicate this].
EDCS-28000150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julius Valentinus, who died around age 25, dedicated by his nurse and Flavia Iustina, described as his 'virginia', possibly indicating a specific familial or dependent relationship.

EDCS-28000151.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuli Vari Saturisci defuncti nnorum XI Lucius Varius Saturiscus et Avidia Bestia filio pientissimo fecerunt
EDCS-28000151.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Iulius Varius Saturiscus, who died at 11 years old. Lucius Varius Saturiscus and Avidia Bestia made this for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-28000151.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Varius Saturiscus, who died at age 11, erected by his parents Lucius Varius Saturiscus and Avidia Bestia.

EDCS-28000152.text_edition: Lucillae defunctae nnorum XXVIII Iulius Ursulus coniugi rarissimae et infelicissimae  S
EDCS-28000152.translation: To Lucilla, who died at 28 years old. Julius Ursulus [dedicates this] to his most rare and most unhappy wife.
EDCS-28000152.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Julius Ursulus for his wife Lucilla, who died at age 28, expressing deep affection and sorrow.

EDCS-28000153.text_edition: Iulio I Urso Anulia Tegiviva coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000153.translation: Anulia Tegiviva placed this for her well-deserving husband, Julius Ursus.
EDCS-28000153.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Anulia Tegiviva to her husband, Julius Ursus.

EDCS-28000155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Ursus Rosulae coniugi quem duxi annorum XII vixit mecum annos XIIII bene merenti posui
EDCS-28000155.translation: To the Divine Spirits. I, Julius Ursus, placed this for Rosula, my well-deserving wife, whom I married at 12 years old and who lived with me for 14 years.
EDCS-28000155.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Julius Ursus for his wife Rosula, noting her young age at marriage (12) and their 14 years together, likely indicating she died at 26.

EDCS-28000156.text_edition: e sibi et Iulipatrronae  cognato filio Publio Turpilio o et suis
EDCS-28000156.translation: [Dedicated] to himself and to Iulipa, his patroness, to his relative Publius Turpilius, his son, and to his own [family].
EDCS-28000156.summary: This is likely a funerary or commemorative inscription dedicated by an individual to himself, his patroness Iulipa, his relative and son Publius Turpilius, and his wider family.

EDCS-28000157.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Aquilinae defunctae nnorum XVIIII
EDCS-28000157.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Julia Aquilina, who died at 19 years old.
EDCS-28000157.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Julia Aquilina, a young woman who died at the age of nineteen, presumably erected by her family or associates.

EDCS-28000158.text_edition: Iuliae Aquilinae Dionisius sic coniugi incomparabili con sic qua vixit annos L et sibi
EDCS-28000158.translation: To Julia Aquilina. Dionysius thus (dedicates this) to his incomparable wife, with whom he lived for 50 years, and for himself.
EDCS-28000158.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Dionysius for his incomparable wife, Julia Aquilina, with whom he lived for 50 years, and also for himself, indicating a shared tomb.

EDCS-28000159.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Chariteni locum donaverunt qui pertinebat at sic Cassios et Atiutorem sic et Saecularem et Fidelem loco qui appellatur in Epetina in fronte pedes numero XXXIII in agro pedes numero XXXIII praecipio autem post obitum meum ut liberti libertasque ponantur sine ulla controversia
EDCS-28000159.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Julia Chariteni. They donated a plot of land that belonged to the Cassii, and to Atiutor and Saecularis and Fidelis, at the place called 'in Epetina', with a front of 33 feet and a depth of 33 feet. I also stipulate that after my death, my freedmen and freedwomen are to be buried here without any dispute.
EDCS-28000159.summary: This inscription records the donation of a burial plot for Julia Charitenis and others in Epetina, specifying its dimensions and including a will-like clause ensuring the burial rights of the donor's freedpersons after their death.

EDCS-28000160.text_edition: Iuliae Chreste Agatho amicae bene merenti
EDCS-28000160.translation: To Julia Chreste. Agatho (dedicated this) to his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-28000160.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Agatho to Julia Chreste, recognizing her as his well-deserving friend.

EDCS-28000161.text_edition: Iuliae - f(iliae) Epetinae matri Iulia Tina bene m(erenti) p(osuit)
EDCS-28000161.translation: To Julia Epetina, daughter of ---, her well-deserving mother. Julia Tina placed this.
EDCS-28000161.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Julia Tina for her mother, Julia Epetina, recognizing her as well-deserving.

EDCS-28000163.text_edition: Iuliae Gratae nnorum XXV Caius Iulius Nerva contubernali et libertae bene merenti et sibi
EDCS-28000163.translation: To Julia Grata, 25 years old. Gaius Julius Nerva (erected this) for his well-deserving life partner and freedwoman, and for himself.
EDCS-28000163.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius Julius Nerva for Julia Grata, his 25-year-old life partner and freedwoman, and also for himself, indicating a shared burial plot.

EDCS-28000164.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Heliadi defunctae nnorum VIII mensium II dierum XX Iulius Helius pater filiae infelicissimae et Corellia Diceosyne mater
EDCS-28000164.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Julia Heliadi, who died at 8 years, 2 months, and 20 days old. Julius Helius, her father, and Corellia Diceosyne, her mother, (erected this) for their most unfortunate daughter.
EDCS-28000164.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Julius Helius and Corellia Diceosyne for their young daughter, Julia Heliadi, who died at just over eight years old.

EDCS-28000167.text_edition: Iuliae Lucidae libertae Veranilla patrona fecit
EDCS-28000167.translation: To Julia Lucida, freedwoman. Veranilla, her patroness, made this.
EDCS-28000167.summary: This is an inscription dedicated by Veranilla, a patroness, to her freedwoman, Julia Lucida, likely commemorating her or marking her burial.

EDCS-28000168.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Lupulae matri Sexti
EDCS-28000168.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Julia Lupula, mother of Sextus.
EDCS-28000168.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Lupula, identified as the mother of Sextus.

EDCS-28000169.text_edition: Iulia Maritima Popilio Popiliano pientissimo marito posuit
EDCS-28000169.translation: Julia Maritima placed this for Popilius Popilianus, her most dutiful husband.
EDCS-28000169.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Julia Maritima for her husband, Popilius Popilianus, whom she describes as most dutiful.

EDCS-28000170.text_edition: Iulia Muntana sibi viva posuit et Aurelio Patienti coniugi suo inter areas in medio in horto et viam prospicientem inter Aurelium Vettium et Calventiam Ingenuam loco concesso ab Aelio Marullino
EDCS-28000170.translation: Iulia Muntana, while living, set this up for herself and for Aurelius Patiens, her husband, between the plots, in the middle of the garden and facing the road, between Aurelius Vettius and Calventia Ingenua, in a place granted by Aelius Marullinus.
EDCS-28000170.summary: This inscription records a burial plot set up by Iulia Muntana for herself and her husband, Aurelius Patiens, indicating a private funerary monument in a garden, on a plot granted by Aelius Marullinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28000173.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Sabina nepoti infelicissimo et Raia Marcella filio infelicissimo Marco Ampeio Sabino filio nnorum VII mensium=ER#mensium#MENSER VI
EDCS-28000173.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sabina to her most unfortunate grandson, and Raia Marcella to her most unfortunate son, Marcus Ampeius Sabinus, son, aged 7 years, 6 months.
EDCS-28000173.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Marcus Ampeius Sabinus, a child who died at age seven and a half, set up by his grandmother Iulia Sabina and his mother Raia Marcella, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000174.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Sarapia Claudiae Antonillae filia matri piissimae
EDCS-28000174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Sarapia, daughter of Claudius Antonilla, to her most devoted mother.
EDCS-28000174.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed for a mother, Claudius Antonilla, by her daughter Iulia Sarapia, indicating a tombstone or memorial from the Imperial era.

EDCS-28000175.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Cai filiae Severinae Iulia Vindemia matronae
EDCS-28000175.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Vindemia, a matron, to Iulia, daughter of Gaius, Severina.
EDCS-28000175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Severina, daughter of Gaius, set up by Iulia Vindemia, a matron, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000178.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Valentinae Serenus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28000178.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Serenus to Iulia Valentina, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28000178.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Iulia Valentina by her husband Serenus, commemorating his well-deserving wife on a tombstone or monument, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-28000180.text_edition: Lucio Iunio Epicteto Iunia Trofime delicato suo posuit annorum V
EDCS-28000180.translation: Iunia Trofime set this up for her darling Lucius Iunius Epictetus, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-28000180.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication set up by Iunia Trofime for her beloved child, Lucius Iunius Epictetus, who died at the age of five, suggesting a tombstone or memorial for a child, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000183.text_edition: Dis Manibus Laelia Laudice Laelio Messio fratri carissimo qui vixit annos XXXIIII menses V
EDCS-28000183.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Laelia Laudice to Laelius Messius, her dearest brother, who lived 34 years, 5 months.
EDCS-28000183.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Laelius Messius, who lived for 34 years and 5 months, set up by his sister Laelia Laudice, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000184.text_edition: Laelia Maximina posuit delicato Felicissimo defuncto annorum III
EDCS-28000184.translation: Laelia Maximina set this up for her darling Felicissimus, who died at the age of 3 years.
EDCS-28000184.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication set up by Laelia Maximina for her beloved child, Felicissimus, who died at the age of three, indicating a tombstone or memorial for a young child, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000185.text_edition: Dis Manibus Laelia Sexta Vitali annorum VII dierum X delicatae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000185.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Laelia Sexta set this up for Vitalis, her well-deserving darling, aged 7 years, 10 days.
EDCS-28000185.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Vitalis, a beloved child who lived 7 years and 10 days, set up by Laelia Sexta, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28000186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Laeliae Zoe Coelius Euvodus sic coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000186.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Coelius Euvodus thus set this up for Laelia Zoe, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28000186.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Laelia Zoe, set up by her husband Coelius Euvodus, commemorating his well-deserving wife on a tombstone or monument, likely from the Imperial era.

EDCS-28000190.text_edition: Caius Ligustius Felix vivus posuit sibi et Agriae Hermogeniae coniugi piissimae
EDCS-28000190.translation: Gaius Ligustius Felix set this up while alive for himself and for Agria Hermogenia, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-28000190.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Ligustius Felix's pre-mortem dedication of a tomb for himself and his wife, Agria Hermogenia.

EDCS-28000191.text_edition: Dis Manibus Livio Pontiano Tribonia Eutychis coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28000191.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Livius Pontianus, Tribonia Eutychis [set this up] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28000191.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Tribonia Eutychis to her deceased husband, Livius Pontianus.

EDCS-28000192.text_edition: Lollia Luci liberta Musa hic sita
EDCS-28000192.translation: Lollia Musa, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-28000192.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Lollia Musa, a freedwoman of Lucius.

EDCS-28000194.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to a daughter named Lollia Secunda.

EDCS-28000195.translation: To the sons, Iotinus and Neritus.
EDCS-28000195.summary: This appears to be a funerary dedication to two sons named Iotinus and Neritus.

EDCS-28000197.translation: To Lucius Lupulanus Lybius, most devoted husband.
EDCS-28000197.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Lupulanus Lybius by his spouse, praising him as most devoted.

EDCS-28000198.text_edition: Maltinia Pudentilla Publio Aelio Catandioni marito pientissimo con sic quo sic vixit annos XXX
EDCS-28000198.translation: Maltinia Pudentilla [set this up] for Publius Aelius Catandion, her most devoted husband, with whom she lived thus for 30 years.
EDCS-28000198.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maltinia Pudentilla to her most devoted husband, Publius Aelius Catandion, commemorating their 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-28000199.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cai Manii Monimuani vixit annos XV dies XVII Sexaria Primitiva mater filio piissimo
EDCS-28000199.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Gaius Manius Monimuanus, who lived for 15 years and 17 days. Sexaria Primitiva, his mother, [set this up] for her most devoted son.
EDCS-28000199.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sexaria Primitiva to her most devoted son, Gaius Manius Monimuanus, who died at the age of 15 years and 17 days.

EDCS-28000200.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcio Artemisio Marcia Fortunata marito bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000200.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Marcius Artemisus, Marcia Fortunata set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28000200.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcia Fortunata to her deceased, well-deserving husband, Marcius Artemisus.

EDCS-28000202.text_edition: Caio Mario Cai liberto Herme Mariae Cai libertae Prepusae Vitoria Cai et Marci liberta Severa posuit
EDCS-28000202.translation: Vitoria Severa, freedwoman of Gaius and Marcus, set this up for Gaius Marius Herme, freedman of Gaius, and for Maria Prepusa, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-28000202.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Vitoria Severa, a freedwoman, for two other freedpersons, Gaius Marius Herme and Maria Prepusa, indicating familial or close social ties among freed individuals.

EDCS-28000206.text_edition: - Egnatio Maximo filio et Egnatiae Liberali filiae et Egnatiae Verae filiae et Egnatio Luciensi filio et Egnatiae Secundae filiae in agro pedes XX
EDCS-28000206.translation: To Egnatius Maximus, son, and to Egnatia Liberalis, daughter, and to Egnatia Vera, daughter, and to Egnatius Luciensis, son, and to Egnatia Secunda, daughter. In the field, 20 feet.
EDCS-28000206.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates several members of the Egnatius family, noting the extent of their burial plot.

EDCS-28000207.text_edition: Dis Manibus mater Melitinini filiae defunctae annorum VII
EDCS-28000207.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Mother Melitinini to her daughter, who died at 7 years of age.
EDCS-28000207.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Melitinini, dedicated to her deceased seven-year-old daughter.

EDCS-28000208.text_edition: Gaius Metellius Restutus et Noniae sic Euhodiae sic coniugi sic vivis sibi posuerunt
EDCS-28000208.translation: Gaius Metellius Restutus and Nonia, also Euhodia, also his wife, erected this for themselves while living.
EDCS-28000208.summary: This inscription records a tomb set up by Gaius Metellius Restutus and his wife Nonia Euhodia for themselves during their lifetime.

EDCS-28000209.text_edition: Dis Manibus Moenius Herma Murcidiae Hilarae coniugi rarissimae posuit et sibi con sic qua per multos annos bene vixit
EDCS-28000209.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Moenius Herma set this up for Murcidia Hilara, his most rare wife, and for himself, with whom he lived well for many years.
EDCS-28000209.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Moenius Herma for his exceptionally beloved wife, Murcidia Hilara, and for himself, commemorating their long and happy life together.

EDCS-28000210.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Murrecius Valentinus se vivo posuit sibi et coniugi Valeriae Secundinae et Murrecio Valerio filio infelicissimo defuncto nnrum XL
EDCS-28000210.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Murrecius Valentinus set this up for himself while living, and for his wife Valeria Secundina, and for his most unfortunate son Murrecius Valerius, who died at 40 years of age.
EDCS-28000210.summary: This inscription records a tomb erected by Murrecius Valentinus for himself, his wife Valeria Secundina, and his son Murrecius Valerius who tragically died at age 40.

EDCS-28000212.text_edition: homini incomparabili qui vixit nnos XXXV locus concessus a Naevio Aulo Mario Fortunato signo Asterio
EDCS-28000212.translation: To an incomparable man, who lived 35 years. The place was granted by Naevius Aulus Marius Fortunatus, also known as Asterius.
EDCS-28000212.summary: This inscription records a burial plot granted by Naevius Aulus Marius Fortunatus (also known as Asterius) for an outstanding man who lived 35 years.

EDCS-28000213.text_edition: Dis Manibus Neulae puellae quae vixit annos V menses V dies IIII Anteus pater
EDCS-28000213.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. For Neula, a girl who lived 5 years, 5 months, 4 days. Anteus, father.
EDCS-28000213.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the father Anteus to his young daughter, Neula, who died at just over five years old.

EDCS-28000214.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nigrina CONSTRO bene merenti
EDCS-28000214.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. To Nigrina, CONSTRO, well deserving.
EDCS-28000214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Nigrina, who is described as 'CONSTRO' and well-deserving, likely by an individual or group.

EDCS-28000219.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Oclatio Socrati Lucius Iulius Primus patri dulcissimo et Iuliae Agatetyche
EDCS-28000219.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. To Lucius Oclatius Socratis. Lucius Julius Primus to his most sweet father, and to Julia Agatetyche.
EDCS-28000219.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Julius Primus to his beloved father, Lucius Oclatius Socratis, also mentioning Julia Agatetyche, likely the deceased's mother or wife.

EDCS-28000221.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Octavius Menodotus se vivo et Aeliae Apuleiae coniugCi#coniugi#CONIUCI in fronte pedes X in aggro pedes X
EDCS-28000221.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Octavius Menodotus, for himself while living, and for Aelia Apuleia, his wife. In front, 10 feet; in the field, 10 feet.
EDCS-28000221.summary: This inscription records a burial monument set up by Lucius Octavius Menodotus for himself during his lifetime and for his wife, Aelia Apuleia, specifying the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-28000222.text_edition: testamento fieri iussit sibi et suis arbitratu Octaviae Thallusae uxoris
EDCS-28000222.translation: He ordered this to be made by his will, for himself and his family, at the discretion of his wife Octavia Thallusa.
EDCS-28000222.summary: This inscription records a testamentary instruction for the construction of a monument for the deceased and his family, to be managed by his wife, Octavia Thallusa.

EDCS-28000223.text_edition: Dis Manibus Oppiae Trofimae defunctae nnorum XXII coniugi bene merenti Flavius Castor et sibi
EDCS-28000223.translation: To the Divine Manes of Oppia Trofima, who died at 22 years of age, Flavius Castor made this for his well-deserving wife, and for himself.
EDCS-28000223.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Castor to his deceased wife, Oppia Trofima, and also reserving a place for himself.

EDCS-28000225.text_edition: Pacuvio Lucido defuncto annorum VIII Pacuvia Tryfera alumno
EDCS-28000225.translation: To Pacuvius Lucidus, who died at eight years of age, Pacuvia Tryfera made this for her foster-child.
EDCS-28000225.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Pacuvia Tryfera to her deceased foster-child, Pacuvius Lucidus.

EDCS-28000226.text_edition: Dis Manibus Paeani Cavari Frontonis Synodus fecit
EDCS-28000226.translation: To the Divine Manes of Paean Cavarus Fronto, Synodus made this.
EDCS-28000226.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Synodus to Paean Cavarus Fronto.

EDCS-28000229.text_edition: Lucius Papinius Hermes Lucio Papinio Luci filio Martiali defuncto infanti annuculo fecit
EDCS-28000229.translation: Lucius Papinius Hermes made this for Lucius Papinius Martialis, son of Lucius, a deceased infant one year old.
EDCS-28000229.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Lucius Papinius Hermes to Lucius Papinius Martialis, an infant who died at one year of age.

EDCS-28000234.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeria Musa Lucio Petronio Diomanio bene merenti
EDCS-28000234.translation: To the Divine Manes, Valeria Musa made this for Lucius Petronius Diomede, her well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-28000234.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valeria Musa to her well-deserving husband, Lucius Petronius Diomede.

EDCS-28000236.text_edition: Dis Manibus Petronio Primitivo filio pientissimo RPVVDI et Iuliae Probae filiae infelicissimae parentes posuerunt defunctis annorum XX
EDCS-28000236.translation: To the Divine Manes, parents placed this for their most devoted son Petronius Primitivus, and for their most unfortunate daughter Iulia Proba, who died at 20 years of age.
EDCS-28000236.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by parents for their deceased children, Petronius Primitivus and Iulia Proba.

EDCS-28000237.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Petronius Secundus Primitivae uxori bene merenti
EDCS-28000237.translation: To the Divine Manes, Lucius Petronius Secundus made this for Primitiva, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28000237.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Lucius Petronius Secundus to his well-deserving wife, Primitiva.

EDCS-28000238.text_edition: Dis Manibus Petronio Turanniano iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit annos XXVII Quarta coniux et mater pientissima
EDCS-28000238.translation: To the Divine Manes of Petronius Tyrannianus, a most unfortunate young man who lived 27 years, his wife Quarta and his most devoted mother made this.
EDCS-28000238.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated to Petronius Tyrannianus by his wife Quarta and his mother.

EDCS-28000239.text_edition: Dis Manibus Petronia Pastorilla Masurio Baiano coniugi pientissimo Petroniae Floe matri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28000239.translation: To the Divine Manes, Petronia Pastorilla placed this for Masurius Baianus, her most devoted husband, and for Petronia Floe, her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-28000239.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Petronia Pastorilla to her deceased husband, Masurius Baianus, and her mother, Petronia Floe.

EDCS-28100080.text_edition: Valerio Hilaro defuncto annorum XVII Valeria Euthycala alumno posuit et sibi
EDCS-28100080.translation: To Valerius Hilarus, deceased at 17 years old. Valeria Euthycala placed this for her foster child and for herself.
EDCS-28100080.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Valeria Euthycala for her foster child, Valerius Hilarus, and for herself.

EDCS-28100082.text_edition: Caio Valerio Primigenio Caio Valerio Cai filio Metallico Plotia Amanda viro et filio
EDCS-28100082.translation: To Gaius Valerius Primigenius, to Gaius Valerius Metallicus, son of Gaius. Plotia Amanda [placed this] for her husband and son.
EDCS-28100082.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Plotia Amanda to her husband, Gaius Valerius Primigenius, and her son, Gaius Valerius Metallicus.

EDCS-28100083.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Valerio Protati Valeria Marcia patrono bene merenti
EDCS-28100083.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Valerius Protatus. Valeria Marcia [placed this] for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-28100083.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, dedicated by Valeria Marcia to her well-deserving patron, Gaius Valerius Protatus.

EDCS-28100084.text_edition: Caio Valerio Vero vixit nnos XVI menses V dies XVI posuerunt Caius Valerius E Valeria V parentes filio pientissimo et sibi
EDCS-28100084.translation: For Gaius Valerius Verus, who lived 16 years, 5 months, 16 days. Gaius Valerius and Valeria, his parents, placed this for their most devoted son and for themselves.
EDCS-28100084.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Valerius and Valeria for their most devoted son, Gaius Valerius Verus, and for themselves.

EDCS-28100085.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Valerio Zosimo Publius Valerius Zosimus fratri et Felicissima coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28100085.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Publius Valerius Zosimus. Publius Valerius Zosimus [placed this] for his brother and Felicissima, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28100085.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, dedicated by Publius Valerius Zosimus for his brother and his well-deserving wife, Felicissima.

EDCS-28100086.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Bassillae et Caio Iulio Sereno Caius Iulius Valerianus filius parentibus bene merenti
EDCS-28100086.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Valeria Bassilla and Gaius Julius Serenus. Gaius Julius Valerianus, their son, [placed this] for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-28100086.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, erected by Gaius Julius Valerianus for his well-deserving parents, Valeria Bassilla and Gaius Julius Serenus.

EDCS-28100087.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Clementinae defunctae nnorum XXII mensium III Cincia Histrica filiae pientissimae
EDCS-28100087.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Valeria Clementina, deceased at 22 years, 3 months. Cincia Histrica [placed this] for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-28100087.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, dedicated by Cincia Histrica to her most devoted daughter, Valeria Clementina.

EDCS-28100088.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Crescentillae defunctae nnorum XXX Ulpia Severa patrona una cum P
EDCS-28100088.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Valeria Crescentilla, deceased at 30 years. Ulpia Severa, her patrona, together with P. [placed this].
EDCS-28100088.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Departed, dedicated by Ulpia Severa, her patrona, and another individual (P.), for Valeria Crescentilla.

EDCS-28100091.text_edition: Varius Valentinus et Flavia Licentina Iulio Cyrano et Variae Flaviae Saloniae filiis infelicissimis qui vixerunt Iulius annos XXVIII menses II et Salonia annos XVI menses VIII parentes infelicissimi  filiis carissimis posuerunt
EDCS-28100091.translation: Varius Valentinus and Flavia Licentina, most unfortunate parents, placed this for their dearest and most unfortunate children, Julius Cyranus and Varia Flavia Salonia, who lived: Julius 28 years, 2 months, and Salonia 16 years, 8 months.
EDCS-28100091.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Varius Valentinus and Flavia Licentina for their two deceased children, Julius Cyranus and Varia Flavia Salonia.

EDCS-28100092.text_edition: Variae Barbarae Atilia Prima mater filiae piissimae fecit
EDCS-28100092.translation: To Varia Barbara. Atilia Prima, her mother, made this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-28100092.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Atilia Prima for her most devoted daughter, Varia Barbara.

EDCS-28100093.text_edition: Dis Manibus Variae Leonini matri pientissimae Firmus filius posuit
EDCS-28100093.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Firmus, her son, placed this for Varia Leonini, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-28100093.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Firmus for his mother, Varia Leonini, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100094.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, likely a simple tombstone epitaph, for a woman named Varia Maxima, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100095.text_edition: Dis Manibus Variae Regillae matri piissimae
EDCS-28100095.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Varia Regilla, a most pious mother.
EDCS-28100095.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Varia Regilla by an unnamed child, honoring her as a devoted mother, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100096.text_edition: Varoniae Germanae et quae vixit nnos XXVII Aurelia Caesia mater filiae infelicissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28100096.translation: For Varonia Germana, who lived 27 years. Aurelia Caesia, her mother, placed this for her most unhappy and well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-28100096.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Caesia for her daughter, Varonia Germana, who died at the age of 27, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100097.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vediae Ursinae defunctae nnorum LXXX Vedius Augustalis matri bene merenti
EDCS-28100097.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Vedia Ursina, who died at 80 years of age. Vedius Augustalis [placed this] for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-28100097.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vedius Augustalis to his mother, Vedia Ursina, who lived to the age of 80, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100098.text_edition: Lucius Verulanus Ampliatus et Bellia Chrysorhoe vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-28100098.translation: Lucius Verulanus Ampliatus and Bellia Chrysorhoe made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-28100098.summary: This is a funerary monument, likely a tomb or sarcophagus, built by Lucius Verulanus Ampliatus and Bellia Chrysorhoe for themselves and their descendants during their lifetime, indicative of Imperial period practices.

EDCS-28100101.text_edition: Vettiae Marcellae coniugi rarissimae Publius Aelius Philippus maritus eius posuit bene merenti
EDCS-28100101.translation: Publius Aelius Philippus, her husband, placed this for Vettia Marcella, his most rare wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-28100101.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Philippus for his wife, Vettia Marcella, honoring her exceptional qualities, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100103.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Vibio Primitivo Vibia Sabina
EDCS-28100103.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Vibius Primitivus. Vibia Sabina [placed this].
EDCS-28100103.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Vibius Primitivus, dedicated by Vibia Sabina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100104.text_edition: Vibio Restituto defuncto annorum XIX Vibia Tallusa mater imfelicissuma sic filio pientissimo posuit
EDCS-28100104.translation: For Vibius Restitutus, who died at 19 years of age. Vibia Tallusa, his most unhappy mother, thus placed this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-28100104.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vibia Tallusa to her son, Vibius Restitutus, who died young at 19, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100107.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibiae Tychini Veianius Turranianus C
EDCS-28100107.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Vibia Tychine. Veianius Turranianus C[---].
EDCS-28100107.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vibia Tychine by Veianius Turranianus, with the dedicant's full name possibly incomplete, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28100111.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vincentio Hilariano homini bono qui vixit annis plus minus XXXV Florentia uxor proprio sumptu ATEED
EDCS-28100111.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Vincentius Hilarianus, a good man, who lived for about 35 years. Florentia, his wife, at her own expense.
EDCS-28100111.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Vincentius Hilarianus, erected by his wife Florentia at her own expense.

EDCS-28100112.text_edition: Viria Secundina viva fecit sibi et Sestio Victori coniugi et libertis libertabusque suis  Aelia Quintina viva fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque suis
EDCS-28100112.translation: Viria Secundina, in her lifetime, made this for herself and for Sestius Victor, her husband, and for her freedmen and freedwomen. Aelia Quintina, in her lifetime, made this for herself and for her freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28100112.summary: This is a joint funerary monument, built by Viria Secundina for herself, her husband, and her freedpeople, and also by Aelia Quintina for herself and her freedpeople during their lifetimes.

EDCS-28100113.text_edition: Dis Manibus Visciae Maximinae Annia Maxima mater
EDCS-28100113.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Viscia Maximina. Annia Maxima, her mother.
EDCS-28100113.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Viscia Maximina by her mother, Annia Maxima.

EDCS-28100114.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vitalioni conservo qui vixit nnos plus minus XXXX Maritima coniugi posuit
EDCS-28100114.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Vitalio, my fellow slave, who lived for about 40 years. Maritima placed this for her husband.
EDCS-28100114.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vitalio, a fellow slave, by his wife Maritima.

EDCS-28100116.inscription_type: carmina, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28100116.text_edition: Vitalis Pieridi Luci Pomponi Pietatis vernae bene merenti Pieris hoc tumulo tegitur de matre Venusta sexto nata loco quae fuit a reliquis nondum viginti iuvenis compleverat annos quoi quoque virginitas nuper adempta fuit grataque florebat cunctis mortalibus aetas quam Fors ad superos noluit esse diu non pudor huic aberat pietas non grata parenti non amor in fratres eius avarus erat  cara fuit mater fuerant caraeque sorores et pia coniugio grataque semper erat invida Pieridi cum venit letifera hora qua cubuit molli languida saepe toro hanc Atropos rapuit Lachesisque et tertia Clotho infelix mater tollit ad astra manus incusatque deos incusat denique Parcas quae vitam pensant quaeque futura canunt inplerunt fratres magnis mugitibus auras et cuncti flebant nec minus ante rogum haec fuit at sic tumulum miserae vox ultima matris ossa simul vidit tabida Pieridis hanc humus excepit leviter precor illa prematque infantem ex utero quae quoque sustinuit coniunx Pieridi supremum munus amatae hunc titulum scripsit pro pietate sua
EDCS-28100116.translation: Vitalis Pieris, a home-born slave of Lucius Pomponius Pietas, deserving well. Pieris is covered by this tomb; born of mother Venusta, she was sixth in place among the others. She had not yet completed twenty years, and her virginity had only recently been taken from her. Her age, pleasing to all mortals, was flourishing, but Fortune did not wish her to be long among the living. She lacked neither modesty nor gratitude to her parent, nor was her love for her brothers avaricious. She was dear to her mother and her sisters were dear, and she was always dutiful and pleasing in marriage. When the death-bringing hour came for Pieris, she lay often languid on her soft couch. Atropos, Lachesis, and the third, Clotho, snatched her away. Her unhappy mother lifts her hands to the stars and accuses the gods, and finally accuses the Fates, who weigh life and prophesy future events. Her brothers filled the air with loud wailing, and all wept, and no less before the pyre. This was her state, and thus the last voice of her miserable mother, as she saw the decaying bones of Pieris. May the earth receive her lightly, I pray, and press lightly upon her who also bore the infant from her womb. Her husband wrote this inscription for Pieris, his beloved, as a last dutiful tribute, out of his devotion.
EDCS-28100116.summary: This is a lengthy funerary inscription with poetic epitaph, dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, Vitalis Pieris, a young freedwoman, lamenting her untimely death and praising her virtues, including a dedication by her mother and a final wish for the earth to lie lightly upon her.

EDCS-28100117.text_edition: Marco Vivio Obelliano Samanna et Caio LucretioCX
EDCS-28100117.translation: To Marcus Vivius Obellianus Samanna and Gaius Lucretius CX.
EDCS-28100117.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Vivius Obellianus Samanna and Gaius Lucretius.

EDCS-28100118.translation: To Vivia Honorata, her mother, well-deserving.
EDCS-28100118.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vivia Honorata, a deserving mother.

EDCS-28100121.text_edition: Ulpia Ingenua Ulpio Ingenuo filio infelicissimo defuncto nnorum XVI
EDCS-28100121.translation: Ulpia Ingenua to Ulpius Ingenuus, her most unfortunate son, deceased at 16 years of age.
EDCS-28100121.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpia Ingenua to her most unfortunate son, Ulpius Ingenuus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-28100123.text_edition: Condidit hic natum infelix Bocontia sic mater functa EEPQVON nimis SVOSSOCIVM magno dAolore#dolore#AOLORE nomine et ipsuo hic situs est Bocontius sic infans qui tulit bis binos me=VFnseFs#menses#VFSFS Q quatEtuor=Eque#quattuorque#QUAETUEQUE I di=INes#dies#INES queL#que#QUL effecit HVLASTISBATI suae domu nVe#ne#VE ulla cVorpora=CR#corpora#VORPOCR iac=Tent#iacent#TENT
EDCS-28100123.translation: Here, unhappy mother Bocontia buried her child, who departed life. Here lies the infant Bocontius, who lived for eight months and four days, having completed his last breath in his own home. Let no other bodies lie here.
EDCS-28100123.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Bocontius, buried by his unhappy mother Bocontia, specifying his short lifespan and the desire that no other bodies be laid in the monument.

EDCS-28100124.text_edition: Volsiae Pyrallidi Mammio Quartioni viro Volsio Saturnino filio Iuliae Craterillae filiae Octavio Mammiano nepoti Volsio Pyramo filio et Octavio Nardo adfini in fronte pedes XXV in agro pedes XXX hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28100124.translation: To Volsia Pyrallis, to Mammius Quartio, her husband, to Volsius Saturninus, her son, to Iulia Craterilla, her daughter, to Octavius Mammianus, her grandson, to Volsius Pyramus, her son, and to Octavius Nardus, her kinsman. In front, 25 feet; in depth, 30 feet. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28100124.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to multiple family members by Volsia Pyrallis, specifying its dimensions and stating that it is not intended to be inherited.

EDCS-28100128.text_edition: Dis Manibus Urgulaniae Valentinae Publius Urgulanius Primitivus matri rarissimae bene merenti
EDCS-28100128.translation: To the Divine Manes of Urgulania Valentina. Publius Urgulanius Primitivus [made this] for his most uncommon and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-28100128.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Urgulanius Primitivus in honor of his mother, Urgulania Valentina.

EDCS-28100129.text_edition: Ursae Castrici Cladi libertae Lupa verna
EDCS-28100129.translation: To Ursa, freedwoman of Castricius Cladus. Lupa, home-born slave, [made this].
EDCS-28100129.summary: This short inscription is a funerary monument for Ursa, a freedwoman, set up by Lupa, a domestic slave, likely her companion or relative.

EDCS-28100131.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursulae annorum XXX Muntana mater fecit bene merenti
EDCS-28100131.translation: To the Divine Manes of Ursula, who lived 30 years. Muntana, her mother, made this for her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-28100131.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the mother Muntana to her deceased daughter Ursula, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-28100133.text_edition: Zotico ILulia#Iulia#LULIA Fortunata coniugi inconparabili sic qui vixit mecum nnos plus minus XXX bene merenti posuit  et sibi
EDCS-28100133.translation: To Zoticus. Iulia Fortunata placed this for her incomparable husband, who lived with her for approximately 30 years, well-deserving, and for herself.
EDCS-28100133.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iulia Fortunata for her husband Zoticus, with whom she lived for about 30 years, and also for herself, indicating a shared tomb.

EDCS-28100134.text_edition: Uxor fecit marito suo bene merenti titulum fecit
EDCS-28100134.translation: His wife made this inscription for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28100134.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a wife for her husband, likely for a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-28100150.text_edition: qui vixit annis XXVI diebuu(s) XXXX MEPIA posuerunt MIRCODRLEO EXAEIRA
EDCS-28100150.translation: Who lived 26 years, 40 days. Mepia and Mircodrleo Exaeira placed this.
EDCS-28100150.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual who lived 26 years and 40 days, erected by Mepia and Mircodrleo Exaeira, likely relatives or companions.

EDCS-28100155.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nyurus socrae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28100155.translation: To the Divine Manes. Nyurus placed this for his well-deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-28100155.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Nyurus for his well-deserving mother-in-law.

EDCS-28100173.text_edition: VITREA voque et natae nata uti comite OVTVIMRC comitata necavit um sprevit nulloque cupidine eius MVC fuit uno contenta marito  nomen erit a summo NVII VERS bene meritae Sabinus et sibi
EDCS-28100173.translation: Vitrea, and also for her daughter, the daughter accompanied by Utvimrc, did not kill, but spurned by no desire of his, was content with one husband. Her name will be from the highest NVII VERS. Sabinus made this for the well-deserving woman and for himself.
EDCS-28100173.summary: This complex funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Vitrea, celebrates her virtue and monogamy, and was erected by Sabinus for her and for himself.

EDCS-28300001.text_edition: Pullia Cai filia Surisca Nimphis sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300001.translation: Pullia Surisca, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-28300001.summary: This inscription records Pullia Surisca, daughter of Gaius, fulfilling a vow to the Nymphs, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-28300002.text_edition: Lucius Stallius Secundus VIvir Augustalis et Stallia Callirhoe uxor cum liberis aedem Matri Magnae ex voto suscepto fecerunt sua pecunia
EDCS-28300002.translation: Lucius Stallius Secundus, Seviri Augustalis, and Stallia Callirhoe, his wife, with their children, built a temple to the Magna Mater, from a vow undertaken, at their own expense.
EDCS-28300002.summary: This inscription records the dedication and construction of a temple to the Magna Mater by Lucius Stallius Secundus, a Seviri Augustalis, and his family, in fulfillment of a vow and at their own cost.

EDCS-28300003.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Titulenio Luci filio Respecto veterano beneficiario legionis X geminae Titia Myrsine marito karissimo
EDCS-28300003.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Titulenius Respectus, son of Lucius, a veteran beneficiary of the X Gemina Legion. Titia Myrsine [dedicated this] to her dearest husband.
EDCS-28300003.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titia Myrsine to her deceased husband, Lucius Titulenius Respectus, a veteran of the X Gemina Legion, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300004.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Vibius Cai filius Pomptina Pudens domo Arretio |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis vivos sic fecit sibi et Iuliae Cai filiae Pollae uxori
EDCS-28300004.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Vibius Pudens, son of Gaius, of the Pomptina tribe, from Arretium, centurion of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, made this for himself while living, and for his wife, Julia Polla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-28300004.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Vibius Pudens, a centurion, for himself and his wife, Julia Polla, during their lifetimes, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300005.text_edition: Aurelio Longiniano decurioni coloniae Drobetensium Aelia Balbina coniux obsequentissima qui liberos suos SCERISTANVPATITIOSS mater quae vixit cum eo annis XX
EDCS-28300005.translation: To Aurelius Longinianus, decurion of the colony of Drobeta. Aelia Balbina, his most devoted wife, [dedicated this], who [lived] with him for XX years, and who [bears] his children SCERISTANVPATITIOSS.
EDCS-28300005.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Aelia Balbina to her husband, Aurelius Longinianus, a decurion of the colony of Drobeta, recording their shared life and children, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300006.text_edition: Titus Rubrius Titi libertus Philonomus IIIIIIvir testamento fieri iussit sibi et Baureae ||(mulierum) libertae Rufae et Tito Rubrio Titi filio Albae
EDCS-28300006.translation: Titus Rubrius Philonomus, freedman of Titus, a sevir, ordered by testament that this be made for himself and for Baurea Rufa, freedwoman of a woman, and for Titus Rubrius Albus, son of Titus.
EDCS-28300006.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Titus Rubrius Philonomus, a freedman and sevir, by his will for himself, his freedwoman, and his son, likely from the 1st-2nd century AD.

EDCS-28300007.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliis Geminae matri et Adiutori Aeliis Valentinae Primo fratribus Tertia posuit
EDCS-28300007.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tertia placed this for her mother Aelia Gemina, and for her brothers Aelius Adiutor, Aelia Valentina, and Primus.
EDCS-28300007.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Tertia for her mother, Aelia Gemina, and her siblings, Aelius Adiutor, Aelia Valentina, and Primus, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300008.text_edition: Aeliae Ursae quae vixit annos XXII menses VII dies XVI Iulius Hermes pater posuit et Statius Dionysocles maritus infelicissimae
EDCS-28300008.translation: To Aelia Ursa, who lived for 22 years, 7 months, and 16 days. Julius Hermes, her father, and Statius Dionysocles, her husband, placed this for the most unfortunate [woman].
EDCS-28300008.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Julius Hermes and Statius Dionysocles to Aelia Ursa, their daughter and wife respectively, who died at a young age, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300009.text_edition: Antonio Vibiano nnorum XXX Antonia Valeria soror pientissimo posuit
EDCS-28300009.translation: To Antonius Vibianus, 30 years old. Antonia Valeria, his most devoted sister, placed this.
EDCS-28300009.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Antonia Valeria to her most devoted brother, Antonius Vibianus, who died at the age of 30, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300010.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Firminus et Flavia Prima parentes infelicissimi filiabus suis Stercoriae annorum VIII Iovino annorum IIII Lucio annorum IIII indulgentissimis
EDCS-28300010.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Firminus and Flavia Prima, most unfortunate parents, [placed this] for their most indulgent daughters: Stercoria, 8 years old; Iovinus, 4 years old; and Lucius, 4 years old.
EDCS-28300010.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Firminus and Flavia Prima, grieving parents, for their three young children, Stercoria, Iovinus, and Lucius, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Saturninae defunctae nnorum LXV Mucius Crescis uxori bene merenti et sibi posuit loco concesso a Ulpio Varrone
EDCS-28300011.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelia Saturnina, deceased at 65 years old. Mucius Crescis placed this for his well-deserving wife and for himself, on a plot of land granted by Ulpius Varrus.
EDCS-28300011.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Mucius Crescis to his deceased wife, Aurelia Saturnina, and for himself, on a plot granted by Ulpius Varrus, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300012.text_edition: Caius Bennius uxori suae Caesiae Optatae non lubens sic fecit sed fatum sic fecit
EDCS-28300012.translation: Gaius Bennius made this not willingly for his wife, Caesia Optata, but fate made it so.
EDCS-28300012.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from Gaius Bennius for his wife, Caesia Optata, expressing sorrow and resignation to fate, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300013.text_edition: Lucius Caesellius Publi filius Tromentina Maximus Caesellia Publi filia Quarta
EDCS-28300013.translation: Lucius Caesellius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Tromentina tribe. Caesellia Quarta, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-28300013.summary: This inscription records the names of two individuals, likely a father and daughter or other relatives, possibly from a tombstone, noting their filiation and tribe.

EDCS-28300014.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Coelius Probus coniugi Mariae Marcellae bene merenti et Marius Marcellus matri piissimae defunctae annorum XXXV
EDCS-28300014.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Coelius Probus to his wife Maria Marcella, who well deserved it, and Marius Marcellus to his most devoted mother, deceased at 35 years of age.
EDCS-28300014.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Publius Coelius Probus dedicates a monument to his deserving wife, Maria Marcella, and Marius Marcellus dedicates it to his most devoted mother, also Maria Marcella, who died at age 35.

EDCS-28300015.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunio defuncto nnorum III mensium XI Caetennianus et Victorina alumno carissimo
EDCS-28300015.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Fortunio, deceased at 3 years and 11 months, Caetennianus and Victorina [erected this] to their dearest foster-child.
EDCS-28300015.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Caetennianus and Victorina to their beloved foster-child, Fortunio, who died at the young age of three years and eleven months.

EDCS-28300016.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Iulius Alexander vivus fecit sibi et Sextiae Prim(a)e coniugi rariissimae
EDCS-28300016.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Julius Alexander made this in his lifetime for himself and for Sextia Prima, his most rare wife.
EDCS-28300016.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Julius Alexander during his lifetime for himself and for his exceptionally rare wife, Sextia Prima.

EDCS-28300017.text_edition: Quintus Tullius Crescens Liberali nepotiae defunctae nnorum XXV
EDCS-28300017.translation: Quintus Tullius Crescens to Liberalis, his granddaughter, deceased at 25 years of age.
EDCS-28300017.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Tullius Crescens to his granddaughter, Liberalis, who passed away at the age of 25.

EDCS-28300018.text_edition: Gai Iuli Queti verna nomine Primitivus nnorum V posuit Abascantus pater
EDCS-28300018.translation: For Gaius Julius Quetus, his home-born slave named Primitivus, 5 years old. Abascantus, his father, placed this.
EDCS-28300018.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primitivus, a 5-year-old home-born slave of Gaius Julius Quetus, erected by his father Abascantus.

EDCS-28300019.text_edition: Iuliae Lascivae et Iuliae Lupae nnorum XVIII Iulia Panthera posuit sorori et matri
EDCS-28300019.translation: To Julia Lasciva and Julia Lupa, 18 years old. Julia Panthera placed this for her sister and mother.
EDCS-28300019.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Panthera to her sister, Julia Lasciva, and her mother, Julia Lupa, who was 18 years old at the time of her death.

EDCS-28300020.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Musculo Orestes suo delicato annorum V posuit
EDCS-28300020.translation: To the Divine Manes. Orestes placed this for his beloved Musculus, 5 years old.
EDCS-28300020.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Orestes to Musculus, his beloved child or pet, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-28300021.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publio Ovio Maximo viro honesto marito optimo Valeria Pomponia marito suo et sibi fecit et titulum posuit et lorici(am) inpendio suo fecit
EDCS-28300021.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Publius Ovius Maximus, an honorable man, a most excellent husband. Valeria Pomponia made this for her husband and for herself, and she placed the inscription and made the burial garment at her own expense.
EDCS-28300021.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Valeria Pomponia to her most excellent and honorable husband, Publius Ovius Maximus, also preparing it for herself, and funding the inscription and burial garment from her own expense.

EDCS-28300022.text_edition: Pompeiae Maximinae vixit nnos XI Valeria Titia filiae infelicissimae et Valeriae Titiae vixit nnos XXXV Caius Pompeius Cerdo coniugi incomparabili bene merenti
EDCS-28300022.translation: To Pompeia Maximina, who lived 11 years. Valeria Titia to her most unhappy daughter. And to Valeria Titia, who lived 35 years. Gaius Pompeius Cerdo to his incomparable wife, who well deserved it.
EDCS-28300022.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two women, Pompeia Maximina, who died at 11 and was commemorated by her mother Valeria Titia, and Valeria Titia herself, who died at 35 and was commemorated by her incomparable husband, Gaius Pompeius Cerdo.

EDCS-28300023.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pompeiae Phyebae sic Lucius Epidius Valens posuit sibi et uxori bene merenti et libertis libertabusque suis  in fronte pedes VIII in agro pedes VIIII
EDCS-28300023.translation: To the Divine Manes of Pompeia Phyebae, Lucius Epidius Valens thus placed this for himself and his deserving wife, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. Eight feet in front, nine feet in the field.
EDCS-28300023.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Epidius Valens dedicating a tomb for himself, his wife, and his freedpeople, detailing the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-28300024.text_edition: Primitivae filiae defunctae annorum XXXIII parentes Lartius et Hermogenia miserrimae filiae posuerunt
EDCS-28300024.translation: To Primitiva, their deceased daughter, who lived thirty-three years, her parents Lartius and Hermogenia placed this for their most unfortunate daughter.
EDCS-28300024.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lartius and Hermogenia commemorate their deceased daughter Primitiva, who died at the age of 33.

EDCS-28300025.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito T Victori defuncto nnorum XVI Titus T Privatus et Servilia Chrysis parentes infelicissimi posuerunt
EDCS-28300025.translation: To the Divine Manes of Titus T. Victor, deceased at sixteen years of age, his most unhappy parents Titus T. Privatus and Servilia Chrysis placed this.
EDCS-28300025.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Titus T. Privatus and Servilia Chrysis dedicating a monument to their deceased son, Titus T. Victor, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-28300026.text_edition: TeFrentia#Terentia#TFRENTIA  viva fecit sibi  Quinto Statio Publi filio Crescenti? --- Marco Statio Publi filio SeFv[ero#Severo?#SFV ---
EDCS-28300026.translation: Terentia made this for herself while living, for Quintus Statius Crescens, son of Publius, and for Marcus Statius Severus, son of Publius.
EDCS-28300026.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Terentia prepared a tomb for herself and two men, Quintus Statius Crescens and Marcus Statius Severus, during her lifetime.

EDCS-28300027.text_edition: Turiae Artemisiae quae vixit nnos XXXVII menses VIII Iulius Artemisianus matri dulcissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28300027.translation: To Turia Artemisia, who lived 37 years and 8 months, Julius Artemisianus placed this for his sweetest and most deserving mother.
EDCS-28300027.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Julius Artemisianus dedicates a monument to his mother, Turia Artemisia, who lived for 37 years and 8 months.

EDCS-28300028.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vitalioni posuit Maxima coniux qOui#qui#OUI vixit cuO(m)#cu(m)#CO eLa#ea#LA annus sic XXIII posuit bene merenti
EDCS-28300028.translation: To the Divine Manes of Vitalio, Maxima, his wife, placed this, who lived with him for 23 years. She placed this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-28300028.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Maxima dedicates a monument to her husband, Vitalio, with whom she lived for 23 years.

EDCS-28300030.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28300030.text_edition: Sarcofagum Ursicli et Tertiae depositio Ursicli die III Idus Decembres indictione XIIII si quis vero voluerit sine conscientia supra sibi praesumere det poenae sic nomine sanctae aecclesiae sic ante litis ingressum auri uncias IIII
EDCS-28300030.translation: Sarcophagus of Ursiclus and Tertia. The burial of Ursiclus was on the 3rd day before the Ides of December, in the 14th indiction. If anyone should presume to claim it without permission, they shall pay a fine to the Holy Church before litigation begins, amounting to four ounces of gold.
EDCS-28300030.summary: This is a late Roman funerary inscription on a sarcophagus for Ursiclus and Tertia, detailing Ursiclus's burial date and imposing a fine for unauthorized use or appropriation of the tomb, likely from the late 4th or 5th century AD.

EDCS-28300031.text_edition: Dis Manibus Drosus Charini conservae bene merenti posuuit
EDCS-28300031.translation: To the Divine Manes, Drosus placed this for Charinus, his deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-28300031.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Drosus dedicates a monument to Charinus, a fellow slave, indicating a burial or memorial by one slave for another.

EDCS-28300032.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio Marcus Ippius Luci filius Stellatina Benevento Vitalis |(centurio) cohortis VIII voluntariorum [Phi][lippianae] votum libens posuit Imperatore domino nostro [Philippo] et Tittiano consulibus III Nonas Ianuarias signo Equitii
EDCS-28300032.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius, Marcus Ippius Vitalis, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, from Benevento, centurion of the 8th Cohort of Philippian Volunteers, willingly placed this vow. In the consulship of our lord Emperor Philippus and Tittianus, on the 3rd day before the Nones of January, under the sign of Equitius.
EDCS-28300032.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius, dedicated by Marcus Ippius Vitalis, a centurion, dated to January 3rd, 245 AD, during the consulship of Emperor Philip and Tittianus.

EDCS-28300034.text_edition: Lucius Allius Luci filius Fabia signifer legionis XI nnum XXX stipendiorum X hic situs est Lucius Statienus Luci filius Fabia Catulus posuit
EDCS-28300034.translation: Lucius Allius, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, standard-bearer of the 11th Legion, aged 30, with 10 years of service, is buried here. Lucius Statienus Catulus, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, placed this.
EDCS-28300034.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Allius, a standard-bearer of the 11th Legion, dedicated by Lucius Statienus Catulus, likely a relative, detailing Allius's age and years of military service.

EDCS-28300035.text_edition: Titus Ancharenus Titi filius Sergia domo Laranda miles legionis VII annorum XLV stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est
EDCS-28300035.translation: Titus Ancharenus, son of Titus, from the Sergian tribe, home Laranda, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 45 years old, with 23 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-28300035.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Ancharenus, a soldier of the Legio VII, detailing his name, filiation, origin, age, and military service.

EDCS-28300036.text_edition: Cnaeus Domitius Cnaei filius Velina Pessinunte annorum XLIV stipendiorum XXV veteranus ex legione VII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-28300036.translation: Gnaeus Domitius, son of Gnaeus, from the Velina tribe, home Pessinus, 44 years old, with 25 years of service, a veteran from the Seventh Legion, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-28300036.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Domitius, a veteran of the Legio VII, indicating his name, filiation, origin, age, service, and that the monument was erected according to his will.

EDCS-28300037.text_edition: Publius Apulanus Publi filius Pollia Sabinus domo Eporedia tribunus militum legionis XI N IM
EDCS-28300037.translation: Publius Apulanus, son of Publius, from the Pollian tribe, Sabinus, home Eporedia, military tribune of the Eleventh Legion, N. I. M.
EDCS-28300037.summary: This is an inscription for Publius Apulanus, a military tribune of the Legio XI, specifying his name, filiation, tribe, cognomen, and place of origin, but incomplete.

EDCS-28300038.text_edition: Melvadius Macri filius eques alae Claudiae novae domo Cugernus nnorum XXX stipendiorum XII testamento fieri iussit Tiberius Claudius Aurelius heres posuit
EDCS-28300038.translation: Melvadius, son of Macer, a cavalryman of the new Claudia ala, from Cugernus, 30 years old, with 12 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Tiberius Claudius Aurelius, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-28300038.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Melvadius, a cavalryman, erected by his heir Tiberius Claudius Aurelius, as stipulated in Melvadius's will, detailing his name, filiation, unit, origin, age, and years of service.

EDCS-28300039.text_edition: Caius Helvius Cai filius Macer UltP(inia) sic#Ult(inia) sic#ULP miles
EDCS-28300039.translation: Gaius Helvius, son of Gaius, Macer, from the Ulpina tribe, a soldier.
EDCS-28300039.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription for Gaius Helvius Macer, a soldier, identifying his name, filiation, and tribe.

EDCS-28300040.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai filius Ultinia sic Clistinna miles legionis VII anorum XL stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est
EDCS-28300040.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, from the Ultinia tribe, Clistinna, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 40 years old, with 18 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-28300040.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius, a soldier of the Legio VII, providing his name, filiation, tribe, origin, age, and military service.

EDCS-28300041.text_edition: Marcus Iulius Marci filius Trernahensis miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XXXXV stipendiorum XX Marcus Arruntius frater et heres posuit hic situs est
EDCS-28300041.translation: Marcus Iulius, son of Marcus, from Trerna, a soldier of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, 45 years old, with 20 years of service. Marcus Arruntius, his brother and heir, set this up. He is buried here.
EDCS-28300041.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Iulius, a soldier of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected by his brother and heir, detailing his name, filiation, origin, unit, age, and years of service.

EDCS-28300042.text_edition: Quintus Oppius Quinti filius Pollia Foro Cornelii signifer legC(ionis)#leg(ionis)#LEC VII nnorum XXX stipendiorum VIII hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-28300042.translation: Quintus Oppius, son of Quintus, from the Pollian tribe, from Forum Cornelii, a standard-bearer of the Seventh Legion, 30 years old, with 8 years of service, is buried here. His heir set this up.
EDCS-28300042.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Oppius, a standard-bearer of the Legio VII, erected by his heir, listing his name, filiation, tribe, origin, rank, age, and years of service.

EDCS-28300043.text_edition: Marcus Vireius Titi filius Celer Leo domo Augusta Bagiennorum  donatus a Tiberio Caesare torquibus armillis faleris sic
EDCS-28300043.translation: Marcus Vireius, son of Titus, Celer, Leo, from Augusta Bagiennorum, honored by Tiberius Caesar with torques, armillae, and phalerae.
EDCS-28300043.summary: This is an inscription honoring Marcus Vireius Celer Leo, from Augusta Bagiennorum, who received military decorations (torques, armillae, phalerae) from Emperor Tiberius Caesar.

EDCS-28300046.text_edition: Marcus Annonius AELIAS C IV nnrum XXX aetate infelix SITAS  et filii duo Caius Iulius posuit coniugi suae bene meritae de suo
EDCS-28300046.translation: Marcus Annonius Aelias, 30 years old, unhappy in age. His two sons, Gaius Iulius, placed this for their well-deserving wife, at their own expense.
EDCS-28300046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the sons, Gaius Iulius and an unnamed brother, to their well-deserving mother, Marcus Annonius Aelias, noting her age and unfortunate death.

EDCS-28300047.text_edition: Caius Laberius filius es nnorum VII hic situs est hunc titulum posuit tibi fidus amicus ultuma sic quae potui debita persolvi non dolere mater faciundum fuit properavit aetas voluit hoc astrum meum vale et priores aetate te tollant hunc luctum tibi
EDCS-28300047.translation: Gaius Laberius, his son, is 7 years old. He lies here. A loyal friend placed this inscription for you. Thus, I have paid the last debts that I could. Do not grieve, mother; it had to be done. Life hastened, my star willed this. Farewell, and may those older than you take this grief from you.
EDCS-28300047.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 7-year-old boy, Gaius Laberius, placed by a loyal friend, with an address to his grieving mother, possibly dating to the Imperial period given the emotional tone.

EDCS-28300048.text_edition: Honesimus sic Titi Septumi sic servus nnorum XV hic situs est dominus bene merito posuit
EDCS-28300048.translation: Honesimus, slave of Titus Septumius, is 15 years old. He lies here. His master placed this for a well-deserving individual.
EDCS-28300048.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Honesimus, a 15-year-old slave, erected by his master, Titus Septumius, indicating a master's recognition of a deserving slave, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300049.text_edition: Caio Statio Syriaco et Iuliae Natali
EDCS-28300049.translation: To Gaius Statius Syriacus and Julia Natalis.
EDCS-28300049.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription, likely funerary, for Gaius Statius Syriacus and Julia Natalis, indicating a memorial or honor, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-28300050.text_edition: Dis Manibus e TertullinaNae#Tertullinaae#TERTULLINNE coniugi simae et incomparabili et Aureliae Acutae filiae simae atque pientissimae parum felicibus Aurelius Maximus coniugi iimae et filiae dulcissimae posuit et sibi
EDCS-28300050.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Tertullina, his most beloved and incomparable wife, and to Aurelia Acuta, his most beloved and devoted daughter, whose lives were too short. Aurelius Maximus placed this for his dearest wife and sweetest daughter, and for himself.
EDCS-28300050.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Tertullina and Aurelia Acuta, a wife and daughter, placed by Aurelius Maximus for them and for himself, suggesting a family tomb from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300051.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titia Procilla, who was 36 years old, likely marking her burial place during the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300053.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Plaetorius Albanus posuit
EDCS-28300053.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Plaetorius Albanus placed this.
EDCS-28300053.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Marcus Plaetorius Albanus, indicating an act of piety or a fulfilled vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300054.translation: To Jupiter Best [and Greatest, Lucius Se---].
EDCS-28300054.summary: This is a fragmentary religious dedication to Jupiter, initiated by a Lucius Se[...], likely part of a votive offering or public monument.

EDCS-28300055.text_edition: Libero Augusto sacrum Lucius Aebutius Luci filius Sergia Celer aedilis IIvir iuri dicundo ex pecunia publica
EDCS-28300055.translation: Sacred to Liber Augustus. Lucius Aebutius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, aedile, duumvir with judicial power, from public funds.
EDCS-28300055.summary: This is a religious dedication to Liber Augustus, made by Lucius Aebutius Celer, an aedile and duumvir, financed by public funds, indicating a public work or shrine, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28300057.text_edition: Cnaeo Iulio Cnaei filio Vero consuli designato II auguri legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Syriae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Brittanniae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Germaniae inferioris praefecto aerarii Saturni legato legionis XXX Ulpiae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori Augusti tribuno laticlavio legionis X fretensis triumviro aere argento auro flando feriundo Aequenses municipes
EDCS-28300057.translation: To Gnaeus Julius Verus, son of Gnaeus, consul designate for the second time, augur, imperial legate pro praetore of the province of Syria, imperial legate pro praetore of the province of Britannia, imperial legate pro praetore of the province of Lower Germania, prefect of the Saturnian treasury, legate of the Thirtieth Ulpian Legion, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the emperor, laticlavian tribune of the Tenth Fretensis Legion, triumvir for minting bronze, silver, and gold. The Aequensian citizens [set this up].
EDCS-28300057.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Gnaeus Julius Verus, detailing his extensive senatorial career, including several high-ranking military and provincial appointments, erected by the Aequensian citizens, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300059.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai libertus Crescens IIIIIIvir vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Anthusae coniugi Caio Iulio Maximo filio
EDCS-28300059.translation: Gaius Julius Crescens, freedman of Gaius, sevir, made this during his lifetime for himself and for his wife Julia Anthusa, and for his son Gaius Julius Maximus.
EDCS-28300059.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a family tomb, made by Gaius Julius Crescens, a freedman and sevir, during his lifetime for himself, his wife Julia Anthusa, and his son Gaius Julius Maximus, common in the Imperial period for freedmen to establish their family memorials.

EDCS-28300060.text_edition: Publius Aelius Verus Aeliae Trioni matri pientissimae defunctae annorum LXX et sibi posuit
EDCS-28300060.translation: Publius Aelius Verus erected this monument for Aelia Trio, his most devoted mother, who died at the age of 70, and for himself.
EDCS-28300060.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a son dedicating a tombstone to his deceased mother and for himself.

EDCS-28300061.text_edition: omnibus Publii nnii Nepos et Surus
EDCS-28300061.translation: To all... Publius [...] Nepos and Surus.
EDCS-28300061.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to a dedication or funerary context, made by Publius Nepos and Surus.

EDCS-28300062.text_edition: Cornelia Ferocilla Corneliae Ferocillae obsequentissimae filiae mater et matertera deposuerunt
EDCS-28300062.translation: Cornelia Ferocilla, her mother and maternal aunt, laid this for Cornelia Ferocilla, her most obedient daughter.
EDCS-28300062.summary: This is a funerary inscription, where a mother and maternal aunt jointly dedicate a monument to their deceased daughter/niece.

EDCS-28300063.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cornelia Ferocilla et Coelia Oclatia Coeliae Marcellae defunctae nnorum XXXV mater et matertera filiae infelicissimae et obsequentissimae posuerunt
EDCS-28300063.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Ferocilla and Coelia Oclatia, her mother and maternal aunt, erected this for Coelia Marcella, their most unfortunate and obedient daughter, who died at the age of 35.
EDCS-28300063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, by a mother and maternal aunt for their deceased daughter, Coelia Marcella.

EDCS-28300064.text_edition: Dis Manibus  virginius Secundinus defunctus annorum XL Stercorina annorum II Tertulla incomparabilibus RAVENVR
EDCS-28300064.translation: To the Divine Manes. Virginius Secundinus, who died at age 40, Stercorina, at age 2, Tertulla, incomparable... RAVENUR.
EDCS-28300064.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Virginius Secundinus, Stercorina, and Tertulla, possibly family members.

EDCS-28300065.text_edition: Vettia Cai filia Tertullina viva fecit sibi et Sexto Iulio Nostro marito optimo hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28300065.translation: Vettia Tertullina, daughter of Gaius, made this monument for herself and for Sextus Iulius Noster, her excellent husband, while still alive. It will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28300065.summary: This is a funerary monument made by a woman for herself and her husband while she was still living, explicitly stating it is not for an heir.

EDCS-28300066.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vipsanius Terentius Aureliae Victorinae coniugi carissimae virginiae posuit et sibi quae vixit mecum annos VIIII menses V defunctae annorum XXIII
EDCS-28300066.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vipsanius Terentius placed this for Aurelia Victorina, his dearest, chaste wife, and for himself; she lived with me for 9 years and 5 months and died at the age of 23.
EDCS-28300066.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the spirits of the dead, dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife and for himself.

EDCS-28300068.text_edition: Castori et Polluci sacerum Fabericia Pieris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300068.translation: To Castor and Pollux. Fabericia Pieris willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28300068.summary: This is a votive inscription, where Fabericia Pieris dedicates an offering to the deities Castor and Pollux in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28300069.text_edition: Turranius Firmus vexillarius cohortis I Belgarum Mercurio votum solvit libens merito
