EDCS-28300069.translation: Turranius Firmus, standard-bearer of the First Cohort of Belgae, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-28300069.summary: This is a votive inscription, recording a standard-bearer of a military cohort dedicating an offering to the god Mercury in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28300070.text_edition: Lucio Terentio Luci filio Claudia Severo Celeia militi cohortis VIII vexillario nnorum XXX stipendiorum XIV Lucius Aebutius Felix posuit
EDCS-28300070.translation: Lucius Aebutius Felix erected this for Lucius Terentius Severus, son of Lucius, from Celeia, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort, standard-bearer, who died at age 30, having served for 14 years.
EDCS-28300070.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Aebutius Felix to Lucius Terentius Severus, a deceased soldier and standard-bearer.

EDCS-28300071.text_edition: Marco Valerio Donico natus domo Celeiae |(centurioni) cohortis III Alpinorum fecit Herennia Pudentilla coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28300071.translation: To Marcus Valerius Donicus, born in Celeia, centurion of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum, Herennia Pudentilla made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28300071.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Herennia Pudentilla for her husband, Marcus Valerius Donicus, a centurion, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28300072.text_edition: Iulia Aelia rus vetustate conlapsam sic suo aere restituit
EDCS-28300072.translation: Iulia Aelia thus restored, at her own expense, a country house collapsed from old age.
EDCS-28300072.summary: This inscription records Iulia Aelia's personal initiative to restore a dilapidated country house at her own expense, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300073.text_edition: Aurelio Maximo militi cohortis III AlTpinorum#Alpinorum#ATPINR defuncto nnorum XXVII Aurelia Valentina uxor posuit
EDCS-28300073.translation: To Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum, who died at the age of 27, Aurelia Valentina, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-28300073.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Valentina for her deceased husband, Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of the Cohors III Alpinorum, who died at age 27, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28300074.text_edition: Batonis sic Baracionis Tato filius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28300074.translation: Tato, son of Bato and Baracio, set this up for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-28300074.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Tato to his well-deserving father, Bato, and his mother, Baracio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300075.text_edition: Panes fecit sibi et suis se vivo fecit
EDCS-28300075.translation: Panes made this for himself and his family while he was alive.
EDCS-28300075.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely for a tomb or monument) created by Panes for himself and his family during his lifetime, a common practice to ensure proper burial arrangements, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300076.text_edition: Platori Varronis Panes Titi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28300076.translation: Panes, son of Platorius Varro and Titus, set this up for his well-deserving relative.
EDCS-28300076.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Panes, son of Platorius Varro and Titus, to a well-deserving relative, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300077.text_edition: fili-- et Varroni Pantoni marito suo posuit viva
EDCS-28300077.translation: [She] set this up for her son and for Varro Panto, her husband, while alive.
EDCS-28300077.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a woman (while still living) for her son and her husband, Varro Panto, indicating a family burial plot or monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300078.text_edition: Dis Manibus Surus Panenti Muteli fecit sibi et Buomi coniugi suae posuit
EDCS-28300078.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Surus, son of Panto Mutellus, made this for himself and for Buomis, his wife.
EDCS-28300078.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, set up by Surus, son of Panto Mutellus, for himself and his wife Buomis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300079.text_edition: Turo et Sextoni parentibus bene merentibus
EDCS-28300079.translation: To Turo and Sexto, well-deserving parents.
EDCS-28300079.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Turo and Sexto, described as well-deserving parents, likely set up by their children, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28300080.text_edition: MAT D D Vitali et Maximus frater
EDCS-28300080.translation: To the Mother Goddesses. Vitalis and Maximus, brothers, dedicated this.
EDCS-28300080.summary: This is a religious dedication made by brothers Vitalis and Maximus to the Mother Goddesses (Matres), likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300081.text_edition: Zanosia Lisenonis nepotie suae annorum VIIII et sibi inreparabile possuit
EDCS-28300081.translation: Zanosia placed this for her irreplaceable granddaughter, nine years old, and for herself.
EDCS-28300081.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Zanosia, likely a grandmother, erected a monument for her nine-year-old granddaughter and for herself.

EDCS-28300082.text_edition: Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus Titoni et Sextoni sororibus bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-28300082.translation: Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus placed this for his well-deserving sisters, Titona and Sextona.
EDCS-28300082.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus dedicated a monument to his deserving sisters, Titona and Sextona.

EDCS-28300084.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Iunius Fadenus decurio equitum cohortis III Alpinorum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300084.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Iunius Fadenus, decurion of the cavalry of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300084.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording Marcus Iunius Fadenus, a decurion of an auxiliary cavalry unit, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28300085.text_edition: Dis Manibus VVO Iucundo mereInti#merenti#MERINTI LPSVVLI Valentinus socro suo
EDCS-28300085.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valentinus placed this for his deserving father-in-law Iucundus.
EDCS-28300085.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, indicating Valentinus erected a monument for his father-in-law, Iucundus.

EDCS-28300086.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Titus ex protectore votum libens solvit
EDCS-28300086.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Titus, formerly a protector, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300086.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording Aelius Titus, a former protector (ex protectore), fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28300087.text_edition: Ilarus Gra--- f(ilius) posuit sepulcrum Lavoni filiae defunctae annorum septem sibi et suis
EDCS-28300087.translation: Ilarus, son of Gra---, placed this tomb for his deceased daughter Lavona, who was seven years old, for himself, and for his family.
EDCS-28300087.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Ilarus erected a tomb for his seven-year-old deceased daughter, Lavona, and for himself and his family.

EDCS-28300088.text_edition: S Batun sic mihi et meis
EDCS-28300088.translation: S. Batun: thus for me and mine.
EDCS-28300088.summary: This is a brief and somewhat cryptic inscription, possibly a funerary or personal dedication by S. Batun, expressing a wish for himself and his family.

EDCS-28300089.text_edition: Marcus Antonius  Dis Manibus Aurelius Licinianus vivus sibi et      memoriam fecit
EDCS-28300089.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Antonius Aurelius Licinianus, while living, made this memorial for himself and...
EDCS-28300089.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Manes, recording that Marcus Antonius Aurelius Licinianus prepared a memorial for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-28300090.text_edition: Dis Manibus Firmus Surionis vixit annos XXX posuit frater et posteris
EDCS-28300090.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Firmus, son of Surio, lived thirty years. His brother placed this for him and for his descendants.
EDCS-28300090.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Manes, recording that Firmus, son of Surio, lived for thirty years, and his brother erected a monument for him and future generations.

EDCS-28300091.text_edition: Quartiniano caro coniugi Elpis titulum posuit qui Salona in fata ruit
EDCS-28300091.translation: Elpis placed this title for her dear husband Quartinianus, who perished in Salona.
EDCS-28300091.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Elpis dedicated a monument (titulus) to her beloved husband, Quartinianus, who died in Salona.

EDCS-28300092.text_edition: In locis peregrinis monumentum fecerunt infelices parentes vivi Valentio iini
EDCS-28300092.translation: In foreign lands, unhappy parents, while alive, made this monument for Valentius iinius.
EDCS-28300092.summary: This is a tombstone erected by grieving parents, while still living, for their deceased son Valentius in a foreign land.

EDCS-28300093.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tonitratori Titus Aurelius Maximus VI Idus Auggustas
EDCS-28300093.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Thunderer, Titus Aurelius Maximus, on the sixth day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-28300093.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Thunderer made by Titus Aurelius Maximus on a specific date in August.

EDCS-28300094.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Claudio Maximo decurioni coloniae Ris defuncto an(norum) LV T
EDCS-28300094.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Titus Claudius Maximus, decurion of the colony of Ris, who died at 55 years old. T.
EDCS-28300094.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to the memory of Titus Claudius Maximus, a decurion of the colony of Ris, who died at age 55.

EDCS-28300095.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Maximus et Aelia Tatta parentes Aeliae Marcellae filiae defunctae annorum XX vivi sepulcrum fecerunt et sibi et suis
EDCS-28300095.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Maximus and Aelia Tatta, parents, while alive, made this tomb for their daughter Aelia Marcella, who died at 20 years old, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-28300095.summary: This is a tombstone and family burial plot made by parents, Aelius Maximus and Aelia Tatta, for their deceased daughter Aelia Marcella and for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-28300096.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Arrius Agaevivus fecit
EDCS-28300096.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Arrius Agaevivus made this.
EDCS-28300096.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Gaius Arrius Agaevivus.

EDCS-28300097.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Iettus Dasantis
EDCS-28300097.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Iettus Dasantis.
EDCS-28300097.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, offered by Iettus Dasantis.

EDCS-28300098.translation: Maxima [daughter] of Turi, to the Nymphs.
EDCS-28300098.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs, likely made by a woman named Maxima, daughter of Turis.

EDCS-28300099.text_edition: Veneri Victrici Parthicae Augustae sacrum in memoriam Antoniae Decimi filiae Paulae Decimus Antonius Proculus decurio Salonae et Coponia parentes locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300099.translation: Sacred to Venus Victrix Parthica Augusta, in memory of Antonia Paula, daughter of Decimus. Decimus Antonius Proculus, decurion of Salona, and Coponia, her parents, were granted this plot by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300099.summary: This is a commemorative dedication and tomb for Antonia Paula, erected by her parents, Decimus Antonius Proculus and Coponia, on a plot granted by decree of the decurions, honoring Venus Victrix Parthica Augusta.

EDCS-28300100.text_edition: Virtute et felicitate omnes retro principes supergresso domino nostro Flavio Iulio Constanti victori ac triumfatori semper Augusto Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius vir clarissimus praesis sic provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eIius#eius#IIUS
EDCS-28300100.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, conqueror and triumphator, always Augustus, who surpassed all previous emperors in virtue and good fortune. Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-28300100.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, governor of Dalmatia, to the Emperor Flavius Julius Constantius, praising his virtues and victories, likely dating to the 4th century CE.

EDCS-28300101.text_edition: Quinto Petronio Valenti militiavit legione VII CG(laudia)#C(laudia)#G pia fideli hic situs est
EDCS-28300101.translation: Quintus Petronius Valens served in the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis. He lies here.
EDCS-28300101.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Petronius Valens, a veteran who served in the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-28300102.text_edition: Pantoni Lavinciae Platoris filiae Apludi Statici decurionis uxori hic est sita annorum XLV
EDCS-28300102.translation: Here lies Pantonia Lavincia, daughter of Plator, wife of Apludus Staticus, a decurion, 45 years old.
EDCS-28300102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pantonia Lavincia, erected by her husband Apludus Staticus, a decurion, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28300104.text_edition: NOI Scenobaro Tizio decurioni patri suo fecit bene merito
EDCS-28300104.translation: NOI made this for Scenobar Tizius, her father, a decurion, who deserved well.
EDCS-28300104.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by NOI to her father, Scenobar Tizius, a decurion, acknowledging his merits.

EDCS-28300105.text_edition: principi Delmatarum nnorum XXII Claudia Tiberi filia
EDCS-28300105.translation: To the princeps of the Delmatae, 22 years old. Claudia, daughter of Tiberius.
EDCS-28300105.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for a young leader (princeps) of the Delmatae, dedicated by Claudia, daughter of Tiberius, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28300106.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliis Feliculae et Malcioni filiis Iulius Cosmus bene merenti
EDCS-28300106.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Cosmus [made this] for his deserving sons, Aelius Felicula and Aelius Malcio.
EDCS-28300106.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Cosmus to the memory of his two sons, Aelius Felicula and Aelius Malcio.

EDCS-28300107.text_edition: Aplis Ledietis Sini filius annorum VIII
EDCS-28300107.translation: Aplus Ledietis, son of Sinus, 8 years old.
EDCS-28300107.summary: This is a short funerary inscription for Aplus Ledietis, an eight-year-old boy.

EDCS-28300108.text_edition: Aplo Darmoca Turi filia nnorum XXV hic sita est
EDCS-28300108.translation: Here lies Apla Darmoca, daughter of Turus, 25 years old.
EDCS-28300108.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Apla Darmoca, a woman who died at 25 years old.

EDCS-28300109.text_edition: Aurelia Trita N CVNTINI MIL ENO ARP PA
EDCS-28300109.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aurelia Trita and a soldier, likely funerary given the context of other inscriptions from this area, but too incomplete to fully interpret.

EDCS-28300110.text_edition: Baezo Cursulavia Lavi filia annorum LXV
EDCS-28300110.translation: Baezo Cursulavia, daughter of Lavus, 65 years old.
EDCS-28300110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baezo Cursulavia, a woman who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-28300111.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary monument prepared by Bizo during their lifetime, as indicated by 'vivus fecit'.

EDCS-28300113.text_edition: Neriae Helpidi Eutychus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28300113.translation: Eutychus [made this] for his deserving spouse, Neria Helpis.
EDCS-28300113.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Eutychus to his wife, Neria Helpis, honoring her merits.

EDCS-28300114.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nomedito Benni posuit viva hic - def(uncto MA dat
EDCS-28300114.translation: To the Divine Manes. Benni, while alive, placed this for Nomedito, deceased here. MA gives [this]
EDCS-28300114.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Benni for Nomedito, likely a family member, while Benni was still living, with an additional note about a donor.

EDCS-28300115.translation: Panto, daughter of Madocus, while alive, made this for herself and for her daughter Postuma, who was 2 years old.
EDCS-28300115.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Panto for herself and her deceased two-year-old daughter Postuma.

EDCS-28300116.text_edition: Pladomenus Sera Turi filius annorum LXXV hic situs est
EDCS-28300116.translation: Pladomenus, son of Sera Turis, aged 75 years, lies here.
EDCS-28300116.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Pladomenus, son of Sera Turis, who lived to be 75 years old.

EDCS-28300117.text_edition: Platino Platoris Tizi filia viva fecit sibi
EDCS-28300117.translation: Platino, daughter of Plator Tizus, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-28300117.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Platino, daughter of Plator Tizus, for her own future burial while she was still living.

EDCS-28300118.text_edition: cis viva et Quartae et Quintae filiabus defunctis
EDCS-28300118.translation: --- while alive, and for the deceased daughters Quarta and Quinta.
EDCS-28300118.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a woman and her two deceased daughters, Quarta and Quinta.

EDCS-28300119.text_edition: Sextus Statinius Aplinis filius nnrum LXX hic situs est
EDCS-28300119.translation: Sextus Statinius, son of Aplin, aged 70 years, lies here.
EDCS-28300119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Statinius, son of Aplin, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-28300120.text_edition: VGAEG et lea filiae riae infelicissimae posuerunt anorum plus minus II
EDCS-28300120.translation: VGAEG and Lea, the most unfortunate daughters, placed this for their mother, who was about 2 years old.
EDCS-28300120.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by daughters VGAEG and Lea for their mother, who died at a very young age, possibly indicating a misreading or symbolic age.

EDCS-28300121.text_edition: Tritanoni Lavi filiae annorum LX et Aploni Tritanonis annorum XII Tritano Acali matri et filiae fecit
EDCS-28300121.translation: Tritano Acali made this for her mother Tritanoni, daughter of Lavus, aged 60 years, and for her daughter Aplo, daughter of Tritanon, aged 12 years.
EDCS-28300121.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Tritano Acali to her mother Tritanoni (60 years old) and her daughter Aplo (12 years old).

EDCS-28300123.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, starting with the name Turo.

EDCS-28300124.text_edition: Turo Statica Aplinis filia annorum XXI h=II(ic)#h(ic)#II sita est
EDCS-28300124.translation: Turo Statica, daughter of Aplin, aged 21 years, lies here.
EDCS-28300124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Turo Statica, daughter of Aplin, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-28300125.text_edition: Vendo Tritaneria Pinsi filia nnorum XXX hic sita est
EDCS-28300125.translation: Vendo Tritaneria, daughter of Pinsus, aged 30, lies here.
EDCS-28300125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vendo Tritaneria, placed by an unspecified dedicant, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28300126.text_edition: Vendo Tudania Pladomeni filia annorum XXI hic sita est
EDCS-28300126.translation: Vendo Tudania, daughter of Pladomenus, aged 21, lies here.
EDCS-28300126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vendo Tudania, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28300127.text_edition: CICDETDMRIIARO R sibi et compari et heredibus suis vivis vivus sibi vivus posuit et Tertullinae nepotiae annorum XII mensium II
EDCS-28300127.translation: CICDETDMRIIARO R set this up while alive for himself, his partner, and his heirs, and for his grandniece Tertullina, aged 12 years and 2 months.
EDCS-28300127.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a living individual, CICDETDMRIIARO R, for himself, his partner, his heirs, and his grandniece Tertullina.

EDCS-28300128.text_edition: Infelicissimo infanti Cornelio Urso qui vixit annos II menses II dies XX Birrius Fortunius et Iulia Firmina filio incomparabili
EDCS-28300128.translation: To the most unfortunate infant Cornelius Ursus, who lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 20 days. Birrius Fortunius and Iulia Firmina [set this up] for their incomparable son.
EDCS-28300128.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Birrius Fortunius and Iulia Firmina to their young, deceased son, Cornelius Ursus.

EDCS-28300129.text_edition: Dis Manibus Surae defunctae plus minus nnorum LX Verna filius pientissimus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28300129.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Sura, deceased, aged more or less 60 years. Her most devoted son Verna placed this for her, well-deserving.
EDCS-28300129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the devoted son Verna to his deceased mother, Sura.

EDCS-28300131.text_edition: Genio municipii Flavi Scardonae Caius Petronius Firmus ob honorem auguratus locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300131.translation: To the Genius of the Flavian municipality of Scardona. Gaius Petronius Firmus [set this up] on account of the honor of the augurate. The place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300131.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Genius of the municipality of Scardona, set up by Gaius Petronius Firmus in honor of his augurate, with the location granted by the city council.

EDCS-28300133.text_edition: Iovi Taranuco Arria Successa votum solvit
EDCS-28300133.translation: To Jupiter Taranucus, Arria Successa willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28300133.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Taranucus by Arria Successa in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28300135.text_edition: Mutilia Publi filia Nertilla votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300135.translation: Mutilia Nertilla, daughter of Publius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28300135.summary: This is a votive inscription by Mutilia Nertilla, daughter of Publius, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28300136.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Claudi filius Postumus Iupiter ex imperio libens merito
EDCS-28300136.translation: Marcus Aurelius Postumus, son of Claudius, Jupiter, by command, willingly and deservedly [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-28300136.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Postumus, son of Claudius, to Jupiter, stating it was done by divine command and willingly.

EDCS-28300137.text_edition: Neroni Caesari Germanici filio Tiberi Augusti nepoti divi Augusti pronepoti flamini Augustali civitates Liburniae
EDCS-28300137.translation: To Nero Caesar, son of Germanicus, grandson of Tiberius Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, Flamen Augustalis. The communities of Liburnia [set this up].
EDCS-28300137.summary: This is a honorific inscription dedicated by the communities of Liburnia to Nero Caesar, recognizing his imperial lineage and priestly office as Flamen Augustalis, likely dating from early in his reign.

EDCS-28300139.text_edition: Tito Turranio Titi filio Sergia Sedato decurioni IIviro Scardonis sacerdoti ad aram Augusti Liburnorum huic ordo Scardonitanorum statuam decrevit Iulia Sexti filia Maxima inpensa remissa de sua pecunia fecit loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300139.translation: To Titus Turranius Sedatus, son of Titus, of the Sergian tribe, decurion, duumvir of Scardona, priest at the Altar of Augustus of the Liburni. To him, the order of the Scardonians decreed a statue. Iulia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, at her own expense, having remitted the cost, made it with her own money, the place having been granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300139.summary: This inscription records a statue dedicated to Titus Turranius Sedatus, a local magistrate and priest, by Iulia Maxima, funding it herself after the town council of Scardona had decreed it.

EDCS-28300140.text_edition: Titus Septumius sic Titi Luci libertus Charito qulinam sic de sua pecunia fecit
EDCS-28300140.translation: Titus Septumius Charito, freedman of Titus Lucius, made this [---] from his own money.
EDCS-28300140.summary: This inscription, likely an epitaph or dedication, records Titus Septumius Charito, a freedman, funding the construction of an object or monument.

EDCS-28300141.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dracontius compari Aureliae Sabinillae coniugi dulcissimae et obsequentissimae quae vixit mecum annos XI menses VIII utrisque hoc sepulcrum fieri voluit
EDCS-28300141.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Dracontius for his companion Aurelia Sabinilla, his sweetest and most dutiful wife, who lived with him for 11 years and 8 months. He wanted this tomb to be made for both of them.
EDCS-28300141.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Dracontius's dedication of a tomb to his beloved wife, Aurelia Sabinilla, which was intended for both of them.

EDCS-28300142.text_edition: Dis Manibus Oppiae Martiae Oppius Cosmus filius matri bene merenti
EDCS-28300142.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Oppius Cosmus, son, for his well-deserving mother Oppia Martia.
EDCS-28300142.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Oppius Cosmus dedicating a tomb to his mother, Oppia Martia.

EDCS-28300143.text_edition: Dis Manibus Retinia Queta Retinio Felicissimo fratri carissimo qui vixit plus minus annos XXV
EDCS-28300143.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Retinia Queta for Retinius Felicissimus, her dearest brother, who lived approximately 25 years.
EDCS-28300143.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Retinia Queta dedicating a tomb to her brother, Retinius Felicissimus.

EDCS-28300145.text_edition: Caius Turranius Cai filius Severus evocatus Augusti Latrae aram posuit
EDCS-28300145.translation: Gaius Turranius Severus, son of Gaius, an evocatus Augusti, placed an altar at Latrae.
EDCS-28300145.summary: This inscription records Gaius Turranius Severus, a veteran soldier recalled to service, dedicating an altar to an unspecified deity at Latrae.

EDCS-28300146.text_edition: Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus |(centurio) veteranorum legionis IIII Macedonicae annos natus XL bis huc pietas ossa bene quiescant
EDCS-28300146.translation: Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus, centurion of veterans of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, 40 years old. Twice to this place, may his pious bones rest well.
EDCS-28300146.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus, a veteran centurion of the Legio IIII Macedonica, recording his age and a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-28300147.text_edition: Titus Cillius Titi filius Fabia domo Laranda veteranus legionis XI nnorum LXX stipendiorum XXXIIX E
EDCS-28300147.translation: Titus Cillius, son of Titus, of the Fabia tribe, from Laranda, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, 70 years old, with 38 years of service.
EDCS-28300147.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Cillius, a veteran of Legio XI from Laranda, detailing his age and length of military service.

EDCS-28300148.text_edition: Dis Manibus SMEITIOM annorum LVIIII V Maria coniux posuit
EDCS-28300148.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] 59 years old. Maria, his wife, placed this.
EDCS-28300148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maria, a wife, to her husband, who was 59 years old, though his name is illegible.

EDCS-28300149.text_edition: Iovi votum solvit Gaius Calius libens laetus merito
EDCS-28300149.translation: Gaius Calius gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-28300149.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Calius happily fulfilling a vow he made to Jupiter.

EDCS-28300150.text_edition: Caius Helvius Maximinus Iovi Optimo Maximo ex viso aram posuit
EDCS-28300150.translation: Caius Helvius Maximinus placed this altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus after a vision.
EDCS-28300150.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Helvius Maximinus, prompted by a vision, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-28300151.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Tertius Alexander
EDCS-28300151.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Tertius Alexander.
EDCS-28300151.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Tertius Alexander, likely a private votive offering from the imperial era.

EDCS-28300152.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Vibius Iulianus miles legionis I adiutricis frumentarius
EDCS-28300152.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Vibius Iulianus, soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, grain provisioner.
EDCS-28300152.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Vibius Iulianus, a soldier and frumentarius of Legio I Adiutrix, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-28300153.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Publius Aelius Andes Barcini votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300153.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Preserver, Publius Aelius Andes Barcini willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300153.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Publius Aelius Andes Barcini, likely a private dedication from the imperial era.

EDCS-28300154.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Lucius S P posuit
EDCS-28300154.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Preserver, Lucius S.P. placed this.
EDCS-28300154.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator placed by Lucius S.P., likely a private votive offering from the imperial period.

EDCS-28300155.text_edition: Mercurio Augusto sacrum Quintus B R F N V S votum libens solvit
EDCS-28300155.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus, Quintus B.R.F.N.V.S. willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300155.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Mercury Augustus by Quintus B.R.F.N.V.S., indicating a private dedication from the imperial period.

EDCS-28300156.text_edition: Neptuno sacrum Lucius Lipo votum solvit libens
EDCS-28300156.translation: Sacred to Neptune, Lucius Lipo willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300156.summary: This inscription records the willing fulfillment of a vow to Neptune by Lucius Lipo, likely a private votive offering from the imperial era.

EDCS-28300157.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300157.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, father of the country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300157.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the decurions to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his second year of tribunician power and second consulship (around 118 CE), likely for a public monument.

EDCS-28300158.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication or marker for the Emperor Flavius Constans Augustus, likely dating to the mid-4th century CE.

EDCS-28300159.text_edition: Cnaeo Minicio Faustino Sex(to) Iulio Cai? filio Sergia Severo seviro turmae V equitum Romanorum IIIIviro viarum curandarum XVviro sacris faciundis tribuno militum legionis XIII geminae quaestori provinciae Macedoniae candidato divi Traiani Parthici tribuno plebis candidato eiusdem praetori legato legionis XIIII geminae legato pro praetore Imperatoris Traiani Hadriani Augusti provinciae Daciae consuli legato pro praetore provinciae Moesiae inferioris legato pro praetore provinciae Brittanniae legato pro praetore provinciae Iudaeae legato pro praetore provinciae SyVriae#Syriae#SVRIAE huic senatus auctore Imperatore Caesare Traiano Hadriano Augusto ornamenta triumphalia decrevit ob res in Iudaea prospere gestas decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300159.translation: To Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Sextus Iulius Severus, son of Gaius, sevir of the fifth troop of Roman equites, quattuorvir for caring for roads, quindecimvir for performing sacred rites, military tribune of the Thirteenth Gemina Legion, quaestor of the province of Macedonia, candidate of the deified Trajan Parthicus, tribune of the plebs, candidate of the same, praetor, legate of the Fourteenth Gemina Legion, propraetorian legate of Imperator Trajan Hadrian Augustus for the province of Dacia, consul, propraetorian legate for the province of Lower Moesia, propraetorian legate for the province of Britannia, propraetorian legate for the province of Judaea, propraetorian legate for the province of Syria: to him the Senate, with the authority of Imperator Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, decreed triumphal ornaments for deeds successfully performed in Judaea, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300159.summary: This inscription, likely a statue base, details the extensive senatorial and military career of Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Sextus Iulius Severus, culminating in the award of triumphal ornaments for his success in Judaea under Emperor Hadrian, and was erected by decree of the decurions, probably dating to the early-to-mid 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300161.text_edition: CI AVI INI legionis XI nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXI Caius Aemilius Cai filius Marcellus nnorum XIIX pater et filius  hic situs! est sic
EDCS-28300161.translation: C(aius) A(e)milius C(aii) f(ilius) Marcellus, of the 11th legion, 45 years old, 21 years of service. Caius Aemilius, son of Caius, Marcellus, 18 years old. Father and son lie here, so it is.
EDCS-28300161.summary: This is a tombstone for a father, Caius Aemilius Marcellus, a veteran of the 11th legion, and his son, also named Caius Aemilius Marcellus, who died young.

EDCS-28300162.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Luci filius Tromentina Martialis Aquae Statiellae miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Pulpidi Silvestris stipendiorum XII annorum XXXV titulum fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-28300162.translation: Lucius Cassius, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, Martialis, from Aquae Statiellae, soldier of the 11th legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the centuria of Pulpidius Silvester, with 12 years of service, 35 years old. He ordered this titulus to be made. His heir took care of its construction.
EDCS-28300162.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Cassius Martialis, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, who ordered it made during his lifetime, with his heir overseeing the work.

EDCS-28300163.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus) Aulus? ubius Auli f(ilius) Celer |(centurio) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona nnorum LXIII stipendiorum XXXXIII titulum fieri iussit Panthagathus libertus faciendum curavit
EDCS-28300163.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aulus Ubrius, son of Aulus, Celer, centurion of the 11th legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from Verona, 63 years old, with 43 years of service. He ordered this titulus to be made. Panthagathus, his freedman, took care of its construction.
EDCS-28300163.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Aulus Ubrius Celer, a centurion of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis from Verona, whose freedman Panthagathus erected it as ordered.

EDCS-28300164.text_edition: Titus Cominius Cai filius Romilia Ateste miles legionis XI annorum XL stipendiorum XVI hic situs est frater fratri posuit vixsi sic quoad potui semper bene pauper honeste fraudavi nullum nunc iuvat ossa mea
EDCS-28300164.translation: Titus Cominius, son of Caius, of the Romilia tribe, from Ateste, soldier of the 11th legion, 40 years old, with 16 years of service, lies here. His brother placed it for his brother. I lived thus, as I could, always well, poor but honest. I defrauded no one. Now it pleases my bones.
EDCS-28300164.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Cominius, a soldier of the 11th legion, erected by his brother, including a short epitaph reflecting on his life.

EDCS-28300165.text_edition: Salvius Frebranus Titi filius Quirina Baculus hastatus prior legionis XX
EDCS-28300165.translation: Salvius Frebranus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, Baculus, hastatus prior of the 20th legion.
EDCS-28300165.summary: This is an inscription for Salvius Frebranus Baculus, a hastatus prior of the Legio XX, likely part of a tombstone or military record.

EDCS-28300167.text_edition: IMA NAEV Quinti liberti Primi Quintus Naevius Cai filius Voltinia Ponticus signifer
EDCS-28300167.translation: [---] to Naevius, freedman of Quintus Primus. Quintus Naevius, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, Ponticus, standard-bearer.
EDCS-28300167.summary: This fragment appears to be a dedication or record related to Quintus Naevius Ponticus, a standard-bearer and a freedman, probably from a funerary monument.

EDCS-28300168.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Aniensis Niger domo Foro Iulii veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Caius Pontius et Quintus Gussius heredes fecerunt
EDCS-28300168.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, Niger, from Forum Iulii, veteran of the 11th legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, 45 years old, with 24 years of service, lies here. He ordered it to be made by testament. Caius Pontius and Quintus Gussius, his heirs, made it.
EDCS-28300168.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Valerius Niger, a veteran of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected by his heirs according to his will.

EDCS-28300169.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Cai Maeloni Cai liberto Celeris domo Arretio annorum LXX Maelonius Erastus patrono posuit
EDCS-28300169.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Caius Maelonius Celer, freedman of Caius, from Arretium, 70 years old. Maelonius Erastus placed it for his patron.
EDCS-28300169.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated to Caius Maelonius Celer, a freedman from Arretium, erected by his own freedman, Maelonius Erastus, for his patron.

EDCS-28300170.text_edition: Folnia Auli filia Spes Annia Spes Aulus Folnius parentes posuerunt
EDCS-28300170.translation: Folnia, daughter of Aulus, Spes. Annia Spes. Aulus Folnius. The parents placed this.
EDCS-28300170.summary: This is an inscription, likely a tombstone, placed by Aulus Folnius and presumably Folnia (or Annia) Spes, for their children or other family members.

EDCS-28300173.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate  consuli III decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300173.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power [---], consul for the third time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300173.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicated to Emperor Hadrian, likely on a public monument, commissioned by the decurions of a municipality, and dates to when he held the consulate for the third time (AD 119).

EDCS-28300174.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli II decreto decurionum
EDCS-28300174.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300174.summary: This inscription is a public dedication, likely for a statue or monument, to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, erected by a municipal council (decurions) around 163 CE, based on his tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-28300175.text_edition: Quinto Cestio Quinti filio Proculo annorum XXXX IIviro bis Cestia Quinti filia Quintina patri pientissimo
EDCS-28300175.translation: To Quintus Cestius Proculus, son of Quintus, 40 years old, duovir twice. Cestia Quintina, daughter of Quintus, to her most devoted father.
EDCS-28300175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cestia Quintina to her father, Quintus Cestius Proculus, who was a local magistrate (duovir) in his community.

EDCS-28300176.text_edition: Memoriai sic Dentonis patris Sextus Thorius Capito fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-28300176.translation: To the memory of Denton, his father. Sextus Thorius Capito made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-28300176.summary: This inscription records the construction of a tomb or memorial by Sextus Thorius Capito for his father, Denton, and also for himself and his family, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-28300177.text_edition: Titius Caper Silvano solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300177.translation: Titius Caper willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-28300177.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Titius Caper dedicated an offering to the god Silvanus, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28300178.translation: When our Lords Constantius and Caesar were consuls.
EDCS-28300178.summary: This inscription dates an event by naming the consuls of the year, Constantius and Caesar, likely referring to Constantius Chlorus as consul for the first time in 293 CE, or for his later consulships alongside a junior Caesar.

EDCS-28300179.text_edition: Tito Iulio Clementi decurioni Asseriae Iulia Titi filia Proclina fratri posuit
EDCS-28300179.translation: To Titus Julius Clemens, decurion of Asseria. Julia Proclina, daughter of Titus, placed this for her brother.
EDCS-28300179.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Proclina to her brother, Titus Julius Clemens, who was a decurion (town councilor) in the Roman municipality of Asseria.

EDCS-28300180.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Cornelio Eutychiano Cornelia Paulina patri pientissimo fecit
EDCS-28300180.translation: To the Divine Manes. Cornelia Paulina made this for Marcus Cornelius Eutychianus, her most devoted father.
EDCS-28300180.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cornelia Paulina in honor of her father, Marcus Cornelius Eutychianus.

EDCS-28300181.text_edition: Dis Manibus Laetiliae Aprillae Laetilia Fructa matri fEecit#fecit#EECIT
EDCS-28300181.translation: To the Divine Manes. Laetilia Fructa made this for Laetilia Aprilla, her mother.
EDCS-28300181.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Laetilia Fructa to her mother, Laetilia Aprilla.

EDCS-28300182.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely a dedication to Publius Papirius, though its original context (e.g., funerary, votive) is lost due to its brevity.

EDCS-28300183.text_edition: CAC LIANC pientissimo vixit plus minus annis X inMfelix#infelix#IMELIX mater T
EDCS-28300183.translation: [To ---] most devoted [---] who lived for approximately 10 years. His unhappy mother, T[---], [made this].
EDCS-28300183.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a child who lived about 10 years, dedicated by his sorrowful mother, whose name began with T.

EDCS-28300186.text_edition: Calpurnia Cai filia Ceuna Latrae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300186.translation: Calpurnia Ceuna, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Latra.
EDCS-28300186.summary: This inscription records Calpurnia Ceuna's fulfillment of a vow to the deity Latra, likely on a votive altar or plaque.

EDCS-28300187.text_edition: Pupillorum Moicorum liberta Dumma Latrae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300187.translation: Dumma, freedwoman of the wards of Moicus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Latra.
EDCS-28300187.summary: This inscription records Dumma, a freedwoman, fulfilling a vow to the deity Latra, likely on a votive altar or plaque.

EDCS-28300188.text_edition: Caius Iulius Picusi filius Ceunus Latrae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28300188.translation: Gaius Iulius Ceunus, son of Picus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Latra.
EDCS-28300188.summary: This inscription records Gaius Iulius Ceunus's fulfillment of a vow to the deity Latra, likely on a votive altar or plaque.

EDCS-28300189.translation: To the deified Nerva, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300189.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the deified Emperor Nerva, made by decision of the local town council (decurions), suggesting a public monument from the early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28400114.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400114.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400114.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), acknowledging his father Septimius Severus, made by the local town council (decurions) in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400115.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Valeriano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli III patri patriae decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400115.translation: To Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400115.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Emperor Valerian, detailing his imperial titles and honors, made by the local town council (decurions) during his reign (253-260 AD).

EDCS-28400116.text_edition: Caio Octavio Tidio Tossiaano Lucio Iavoleno Prisco legato legionis IV Flaviae legato legionis III Augustae iuridico provinciae Brittanniae legato consuari provinciae Germaniae superioris legato consulari provinciae Syriae proconsuli provinciae Africae pontifici Publius Mutilius Publi filius Claudia Crispinus testamento poni iussit amico carissimo
EDCS-28400116.translation: To Gaius Octavius Tidus Tossianus Lucius Iavolenus Priscus, legate of the Fourth Flavian Legion, legate of the Third Augustan Legion, legal counsel of the province of Britannia, consular legate of the province of Upper Germania, consular legate of the province of Syria, proconsul of the province of Africa, pontifex. Publius Mutilius Crispinus, son of Publius, of the Claudian tribe, ordered this to be erected in his will for his dearest friend.
EDCS-28400116.summary: This funerary inscription records the illustrious military and administrative career of Gaius Octavius Tidus Tossianus Lucius Iavolenus Priscus, erected posthumously by his friend Publius Mutilius Crispinus as stipulated in his will, likely dating to the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400117.text_edition: veteranus legionis VIII Lucio Octavio  filio Claudia Frontino fratri
EDCS-28400117.translation: A veteran of the Eighth Legion, to Lucius Octavius Frontinus, son of Lucius, of the Claudian tribe, his brother.
EDCS-28400117.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a veteran of the Eighth Legion, dedicated to his brother, Lucius Octavius Frontinus.

EDCS-28400118.text_edition: Cocceio Umbriano decurioni auguri et pontifici civitatis Paralissensium sic provinciae Daciae Cocceius Severus filius patri pientissimo
EDCS-28400118.translation: To Cocceius Umbriano, decurion, augur, and pontifex of the city of Paralissenses in the province of Dacia. Cocceius Severus, his son, [erected this] to his most devoted father.
EDCS-28400118.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cocceius Severus to his father, Cocceius Umbriano, who held several municipal offices in Paralissum, Dacia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400119.text_edition: Marco Fulvio  filio Claudia Rufo aedili
EDCS-28400119.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Rufus, son of [---], of the Claudian tribe, aedile.
EDCS-28400119.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Marcus Fulvius Rufus, an aedile, indicating a public or funerary monument for a local magistrate.

EDCS-28400120.text_edition: Caio Lartio Sabiniano officiali Naeditarum sic rarissimo vitae incorruptae nnorum XXXII mensium III patri filiorum II Magia Maximina coniugi incomparabili cum quo vixit nnos XV virginio infelicissimo mater posuit
EDCS-28400120.translation: To Gaius Lartius Sabinianus, official of the Naeditarii, a man most rare for his blameless life, aged 32 years, 3 months, father of two sons, Magia Maximina, his incomparable wife, with whom he lived for 15 years, and his most unfortunate virgin son, his mother set this up.
EDCS-28400120.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother dedicated to her deceased husband, Gaius Lartius Sabinianus, an official of the Naeditarii, and their young son, presumably by his wife, Magia Maximina, and possibly by his own mother as well.

EDCS-28400121.text_edition: Sextus Octavius Claudia Constans IIvir pontifex testamento fieri iussit epRuloque#epuloque#ERULOQUE dedicari
EDCS-28400121.translation: Sextus Octavius Constans, son of Claudia, duumvir and pontifex, ordered this to be made by his will and dedicated with a feast.
EDCS-28400121.summary: This inscription records that Sextus Octavius Constans, a duumvir and pontifex, ordered a monument to be made and dedicated with a feast, as per his will.

EDCS-28400122.text_edition: Octavia Quinti filia Secunda viva fecit sibi et Octaviae Titi filiae Gracillae filiae Octaviae Sexti filiae Celsinae nepti Quinto Octavio patri aedili IIviro III Quintiae Voltisae matri Tito Octavio Sexti filio Gracili viro aedili IIviro pontifici Caio Octavio Rufo fratri aedili IIviro pontifici Sexto Octavio Sexti filio Celso genero
EDCS-28400122.translation: Octavia Secunda, daughter of Quintus, made this while living for herself and for Octavia Gracilla, daughter of Titus, her daughter; Octavia Celsina, daughter of Sextus, her granddaughter; Quintus Octavius, her father, aedile, duumvir for the third time; Quintia Voltisa, her mother; Titus Octavius Gracilis, son of Sextus, her husband, aedile, duumvir, pontifex; Gaius Octavius Rufus, her brother, aedile, duumvir, pontifex; Sextus Octavius Celsus, son of Sextus, her son-in-law.
EDCS-28400122.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Octavia Secunda during her lifetime for herself and a large number of her family members, including parents, children, a grandchild, a husband, a brother, and a son-in-law, many of whom held political offices.

EDCS-28400123.text_edition: Titus Turranius Luci filius Claudia Rufus ob honorem aedilitatis porticum longam pedes C latam pedes XX item clatra cum Lucio Turranio Proculo et Tito Turannio Frontone filiis fecit
EDCS-28400123.translation: Titus Turranius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, for the honor of his aedileship, built a portico 100 feet long and 20 feet wide, and also gratings, with his sons Lucius Turranius Proculus and Titus Turannius Fronto.
EDCS-28400123.summary: This inscription records that Titus Turranius Rufus, in honor of his aedileship, constructed a portico and gratings with his two sons, Lucius Turranius Proculus and Titus Turannius Fronto.

EDCS-28400124.text_edition: Octaviae Cai filiae Quintilliae Iulia Quinti filia Paula mater fecit
EDCS-28400124.translation: Julia Paula, daughter of Quintus, made this for Octavia Quintilla, daughter of Gaius, her daughter.
EDCS-28400124.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Julia Paula, dedicated to her daughter, Octavia Quintilla.

EDCS-28400125.text_edition: a Cai filia Maxima Titi Iulii
EDCS-28400125.translation: To Maxim[a], daughter of Gaius, of Titus Julius.
EDCS-28400125.summary: This appears to be a fragmentary dedication to a woman named Maxima, daughter of Gaius, associated with Titus Julius.

EDCS-28400126.translation: To Maximus Octavius Fronto, son of Marcus.
EDCS-28400126.summary: This is a dedication to Maximus Octavius Fronto, son of Marcus, likely part of a larger monument or epitaph.

EDCS-28400127.text_edition: Opiniae Marci filiae Neptillae nnum XIIII virgini desperatae prope diem nuptiarum defunctae Marcus Opinius Rufus et Gellia Neptilla parentes
EDCS-28400127.translation: To Opinia Neptilla, daughter of Marcus, 14 years old, a desperate virgin who died shortly before her wedding, her parents Marcus Opinius Rufus and Gellia Neptilla [set this up].
EDCS-28400127.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from Marcus Opinius Rufus and Gellia Neptilla, lamenting their daughter Opinia Neptilla, who died as a virgin at age 14 just before her wedding.

EDCS-28400128.text_edition: Marcus Papisius Marci filius Claudia Maximus vivus fecit sibi et suis Marco Papisio Marci filio Claudia Nigro Aemiliae Volsci filiae Pietati parentibus hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28400128.translation: Marcus Papisius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, made this while living for himself and his family, for Marcus Papisius Niger, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, and Aemilia Pietas, daughter of Volscus, his parents. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28400128.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument made by Marcus Papisius Maximus during his lifetime for himself, his parents Marcus Papisius Niger and Aemilia Pietas, and indicates that the monument is not to be inherited by others.

EDCS-28400129.text_edition: Sentia Cai filia Prima viva fecit sibi et Caio Tario Cai filio Sextioni viro Laecaniae Cai filiae Maxumae matri Marco Tario Cai filio Clementi filio Publio Tario Cai filio Rufo et Lucio Tario Cai filio Celso filio
EDCS-28400129.translation: Sentia Prima, daughter of Gaius, made this while living for herself and for Gaius Tari[us] Sextio, son of Gaius, her husband; Laecania Maxuma, daughter of Gaius, her mother; Marcus Tari[us] Clemens, son of Gaius, her son; Publius Tari[us] Rufus, son of Gaius, and Lucius Tari[us] Celsus, son of Gaius, her sons.
EDCS-28400129.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Sentia Prima during her lifetime for herself and multiple family members, including her husband, mother, and three sons.

EDCS-28400130.text_edition: Marcus Tarius Cai filius Triera Tariae Sexto Tario Cai filio fratri Tariae Cai filiae Tertiae sorori Quinto Tario Cai filio fratri vivos sic fecit et suis filiis
EDCS-28400130.translation: Marcus Tarius, son of Gaius, for Triera Taria; for Sextus Tarius, son of Gaius, his brother; for Taria, daughter of Gaius, his sister Tertia; for Quintus Tarius, son of Gaius, his brother; while living, thus made this for himself and his children.
EDCS-28400130.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marcus Tarius, son of Gaius, had this monument made for himself, his brothers, and his sisters while still alive, and also for his children.

EDCS-28400131.text_edition: Dis Manibus  piissimo   dies   Severus   parentes posuerunt
EDCS-28400131.translation: To the Divine Manes. Most pious. Days. Severus. His parents placed this.
EDCS-28400131.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by parents to the Divine Manes for their most pious son, Severus.

EDCS-28400132.text_edition: Dis deabusque secundum interpretrationem Clarii Apollinis
EDCS-28400132.translation: To the gods and goddesses, according to the interpretation of Clarian Apollo.
EDCS-28400132.summary: This is a religious dedication made to the gods and goddesses following the interpretation of the oracle of Clarian Apollo.

EDCS-28400133.text_edition: Iano Patri Quintus Ennius  filius Agrippa votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400133.translation: To Father Janus. Quintus Ennius Agrippa, son, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400133.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Quintus Ennius Agrippa, son, fulfilling a vow to Father Janus.

EDCS-28400136.text_edition: Titus Caninius Titi filius Sergia Maximus veteranus cohortis VI praetoriae sibi testamento poni iussit Marcus Maesius Paulus heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-28400136.translation: Titus Caninius Maximus, son of Titus, of the Sergian tribe, a veteran of the Sixth Praetorian Cohort, ordered this to be placed for himself by his will. Marcus Maesius Paulus, his heir, saw to its placement.
EDCS-28400136.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Caninius Maximus, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, erected by his heir Marcus Maesius Paulus as per his will.

EDCS-28400137.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Sergia Agrippae militi legionis VII piae fidelis centuria Agrippae p Avita marito Agrippina patri
EDCS-28400137.translation: To Gaius Julius Agrippa, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Pia Fidelis, from Agrippa's century. Avita for her husband. Agrippina for her father.
EDCS-28400137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Agrippa, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, dedicated by his wife Avita and his daughter Agrippina.

EDCS-28400138.text_edition: Marco Iulio Severo militi cohortis VII urbanae Calpurnia Volaesa mater posuit
EDCS-28400138.translation: To Marcus Julius Severus, a soldier of the Seventh Urban Cohort. Calpurnia Volaesa, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-28400138.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Julius Severus, a soldier of the Urban Cohorts, erected by his mother Calpurnia Volaesa.

EDCS-28400141.text_edition: Aprilis Gemellae bene merenti conservae posuit Dis Manibus
EDCS-28400141.translation: Aprilis placed this for Gemella, a well-deserving fellow slave. To the Divine Manes.
EDCS-28400141.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes, dedicated by Aprilis for her well-deserving fellow slave, Gemella.

EDCS-28400142.text_edition: Baebiae Luci filiae Tertullinae nnorum XXIX Calpurnia Tertulla mater viva posuit
EDCS-28400142.translation: To Baebia Tertullina, daughter of Lucius, 29 years old. Calpurnia Tertulla, her mother, placed this while living.
EDCS-28400142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baebia Tertullina, aged 29, erected by her mother Calpurnia Tertulla while still alive.

EDCS-28400143.text_edition: Calpurnia Cai filia Ceuna viva fecit sibi et Oppiae  filiae Secundae matri et Oppiae Oepli filiae Voltisae amitae et Calpurniae Cai filiae Oeplae sorori
EDCS-28400143.translation: Calpurnia Ceuna, daughter of Gaius, made this while living for herself and for Oppia Secunda, her daughter; and for Oppia Voltisa, daughter of Oelpus, her paternal aunt; and for Calpurnia Oepla, daughter of Gaius, her sister.
EDCS-28400143.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Calpurnia Ceuna, daughter of Gaius, made this monument while living for herself, her daughter, her paternal aunt, and her sister.

EDCS-28400145.text_edition: Dis Manibus DionyVsiae#Dionysiae#DIONVS infelicissimae defunctae annorum III mensium X Atticilla alumnae posuit
EDCS-28400145.translation: To the spirits of the dead, to the most unfortunate Dionysia, who died at the age of 3 years and 10 months. Atticilla placed this for her alumna.
EDCS-28400145.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Atticilla for her alumna, Dionysia, who died young, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400146.text_edition: Marco Iulio Titi filio Proculo patri Lucio Iulio Proculo fratri Marcus Iulius Severus
EDCS-28400146.translation: To Marcus Iulius Proculus, son of Titus, his father; to Lucius Iulius Proculus, his brother. Marcus Iulius Severus (set this up).
EDCS-28400146.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Iulius Severus to his father, Marcus Iulius Proculus, and his brother, Lucius Iulius Proculus.

EDCS-28400147.text_edition: Iuliae Cai filiae Oiae Iulia  filia Patrinia matri posuit
EDCS-28400147.translation: To Iulia Oia, daughter of Gaius. Iulia Patrinia, her daughter, placed this for her mother.
EDCS-28400147.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a daughter, Iulia Patrinia, for her mother, Iulia Oia.

EDCS-28400148.translation: To Iulia Secunda, daughter of Iaetus.
EDCS-28400148.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Secunda, identified by her father's name.

EDCS-28400149.text_edition: Iulia Valentilla sibi et Iuliae Virnoni patronae viva fecit
EDCS-28400149.translation: Iulia Valentilla made this for herself and for Iulia Virno, her patroness, while she was alive.
EDCS-28400149.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Iulia Valentilla during her lifetime for herself and for her patroness, Iulia Virno, indicating a relationship of freedperson and former owner.

EDCS-28400150.text_edition: Octavia Titi filia Tertulla viva sibi et suis fecit in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-28400150.translation: Octavia Tertulla, daughter of Titus, made this while alive for herself and her family. In front, 20 feet; in depth, 30 feet.
EDCS-28400150.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Octavia Tertulla during her lifetime for herself and her family, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-28400151.text_edition: Dis Manibus s(acrum) Rutilio Amoeno conservo Parthenopeus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400151.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. Parthenopeus placed this for Rutilius Amoenus, his fellow slave, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400151.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Parthenopeus for his fellow slave, Rutilius Amoenus, honoring a well-deserving individual.

EDCS-28400152.text_edition: Tullia Oepli filia Voltisa sibi et Caio Iulio Ceuni filio Acirrio filio et Orpiae Quinti filiae Opiavae matri viva fecit in fronte pedes V |(triens) in agro pedes X |(dextans)
EDCS-28400152.translation: Tullia Voltisa, daughter of Oeplus, made this while alive for herself and for Gaius Iulius Acirrius, son of Ceunus, her son, and for Orpia Opiava, daughter of Quintus, her mother. In front, 5 and 1/3 feet; in depth, 10 and 5/6 feet.
EDCS-28400152.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Tullia Voltisa during her lifetime for herself, her son Gaius Iulius Acirrius, and her mother Orpia Opiava, with specific dimensions for the plot.

EDCS-28400223.text_edition: VNESIC centuria Octavi F Volso et F Octavio - filio Balbo
EDCS-28400223.translation: VNESIC, from the centuria of Octavius F. Volso, and F. Octavius Balbus, his son.
EDCS-28400223.summary: This is a military inscription, likely a dedication or record, referencing Vnesic and members of the Octavian family, identifying a centurion and his son.

EDCS-28400224.text_edition: Paula Lucii filia annorum septem Iulia Caii filia vivens
EDCS-28400224.translation: Paula, daughter of Lucius, aged seven years. Iulia, daughter of Gaius, living.
EDCS-28400224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Paula, who died at seven years old, alongside a mention of Iulia, who was still living at the time the inscription was made.

EDCS-28400225.text_edition: Iano Augusto sacrum Cinius Genialis pro salute ordinis sui et civium suorum simulacrum eTi#ei#TI reformavit atDque#atque#ADQUE restituitD#restituit#RESTITUID
EDCS-28400225.translation: Sacred to Janus Augustus. Cinius Genialis, for the well-being of his order and his fellow citizens, rebuilt and restored the statue.
EDCS-28400225.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Cinius Genialis's restoration of a statue to Janus Augustus for the benefit of his community.

EDCS-28400226.text_edition: Neptuno Dianae propter maiestatem Lucius Cincius Trophimus ex viso posuit quod Halis  vidit
EDCS-28400226.translation: To Neptune and Diana, on account of their majesty. Lucius Cincius Trophimus placed this according to a vision which he saw at Halis.
EDCS-28400226.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Lucius Cincius Trophimus to Neptune and Diana, erected after a divine vision he experienced at Halis.

EDCS-28400227.text_edition: Veneri Augustae sacrum Egnatia Cai filia Paullina solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400227.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta. Egnatia Paullina, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28400227.summary: This is a votive inscription by Egnatia Paullina to Venus Augusta, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28400228.text_edition: Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus pontifex maximus imperator tribunicia potestate XVIII consul designatus tertius
EDCS-28400228.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the divine Augustus, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Imperator, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, consul elect for the third time.
EDCS-28400228.summary: This inscription details the full titulature and significant offices held by the Emperor Tiberius, likely dating to 16-17 CE.

EDCS-28400229.text_edition: Publio Silio Publi filio proconsuli patrono decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400229.translation: To Publius Silius, son of Publius, proconsul, patron, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400229.summary: This inscription honors Publius Silius, a proconsul and patron, erected by the decree of the local council of decurions.

EDCS-28400233.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Appuleio Publi filio Frontoni aedili IIviro quinquennali pontifici Saenia Maxima marito de se bene merito
EDCS-28400233.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Appuleius Fronto, son of Publius, aedile, duovir quinquennalis, pontiff. Saenia Maxima (erected this) for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28400233.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Appuleius Fronto, a local magistrate and priest, erected by his wife Saenia Maxima.

EDCS-28400234.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Tullio Ursioni IIIIIIviro Augustali Tullia Victorina marito optimo
EDCS-28400234.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Tullius Ursio, Augustalis sevir. Tullia Victorina (erected this) for her excellent husband.
EDCS-28400234.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Tullius Ursio, an Augustalis sevir, set up by his wife Tullia Victorina.

EDCS-28400235.text_edition: Lucius Barbius Crapus et Quintus Barbius Rufus sibi et suis vivi fecerunt in fronte pedes XXXV in agro pedes X
EDCS-28400235.translation: Lucius Barbius Crapus and Quintus Barbius Rufus made this for themselves and their family while living. In front, 35 feet; in depth, 10 feet.
EDCS-28400235.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb or burial plot prepared by Lucius Barbius Crapus and Quintus Barbius Rufus for themselves and their family during their lifetimes, specifying its dimensions.

EDCS-28400236.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead of Caecianus.
EDCS-28400236.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicating a monument to the Spirits of the Dead for an individual named Caecianus.

EDCS-28400237.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum inM#in#IM memoriam Iuli Frontoni qui tulit annos XXXXVII Secundina coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28400237.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, in memory of Julius Fronto, who lived 47 years. Secundina (erected this) for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28400237.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Julius Fronto, who lived 47 years, erected by his wife Secundina.

EDCS-28400238.translation: Gaius Licinius Thalamus, a wild ox.
EDCS-28400238.summary: This is a brief and unusual inscription, possibly marking a grave or property, dedicated to Gaius Licinius Thalamus, with 'urus' (wild ox) potentially being an epithet or a symbol, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28400239.text_edition: Titus OctIavius#Octavius#OCIAVIU Capito vivus fecit sibi TAVIO Octavi- T filiae patri SERT II VI L O I S VII D M
EDCS-28400239.translation: Titus Octavius Capito made this for himself while alive. To Octavia, his daughter, to his father, [...] VI L O I S VII D M.
EDCS-28400239.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Octavius Capito for himself while still alive, and also for his daughter Octavia and his father, with some parts missing, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400240.translation: Lucius Septumius Frunitus thus restored this.
EDCS-28400240.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an unspecified monument or structure by Lucius Septumius Frunitus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400241.text_edition: Vetillius N Volsi Potisus VI L C
EDCS-28400241.translation: Vetillius N. Volsi Potitus VI L C.
EDCS-28400241.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Vetillius N. Volsi Potitus, with the final letters 'VI L C' possibly indicating 'vixit libens curavit' (lived, willingly took care of) or similar, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400242.text_edition: Millae uxori III et suis viv- fec--- in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX
EDCS-28400242.translation: To Milla, his wife, also to his own (family), he made this while alive. In the front, twenty feet; in the field, twenty feet.
EDCS-28400242.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the creation of a burial plot by an unnamed individual for his wife Milla and his family while he was still alive, specifying the dimensions of the plot, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400243.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or legal in nature, mentioning a 'testament' (will), and an individual with a name fragment 'Tura', dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400245.translation: The unhappy wife to her well-deserving (husband/partner).
EDCS-28400245.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a grieving wife to her well-deserving deceased husband or partner, expressing her sorrow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400248.text_edition: Dis Manibus Anius Rufinus sibi et Gratulae uxori suae et Aelio Scorpioni filio defuncto annorum XXVIII vivus posuit
EDCS-28400248.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Anius Rufinus, while alive, placed this for himself and for Gratula, his wife, and for Aelius Scorpio, his son, who died at 28 years of age.
EDCS-28400248.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, erected by Anius Rufinus while alive, for himself, his wife Gratula, and his deceased son Aelius Scorpio, who died at 28, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400249.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Quinto annorum XXX veterano Flavia QuietIa#Quieta#QIA V marito
EDCS-28400249.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aurelius Quintus, 30 years old, a veteran. Flavia Quieta (set this up) for her husband.
EDCS-28400249.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Aurelius Quintus, a 30-year-old veteran, erected by his wife Flavia Quieta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400250.text_edition: Dis Manibus Herennia Rufina POIA SVVE annorum LXX
EDCS-28400250.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Herennia Rufina, 70 years old.
EDCS-28400250.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead for Herennia Rufina, who lived to be 70 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400252.text_edition: D(is) Manibus IIVISEI sibi et Catae coniugi MAEVI posuerunt nnrum LXXVIII
EDCS-28400252.translation: To the Divine Shades. IIVISEI and Catae, his wife, MAEVI, placed this for themselves. 78 years.
EDCS-28400252.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument by IIVISEI and Catae for themselves, possibly indicating Catae's age at death or the monument's construction after 78 years.

EDCS-28400253.text_edition: Publius Aelius  vivus sibi et Aeliae Iuliae nnorum XLV uxori pientissimae posuit
EDCS-28400253.translation: Publius Aelius, while living, placed this for himself and for Aelia Iulia, his most devoted wife of 45 years.
EDCS-28400253.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Aelius for himself and his wife, Aelia Iulia, during his lifetime, commemorating her age of 45 years.

EDCS-28400255.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Priscus annorum LX vivus fecit sibi et Aeliae Mellitoni uxori pientissimae annorum LX et Aelio Frontoni filio et suis
EDCS-28400255.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Priscus, 60 years old, while living, made this for himself and for Aelia Mellito, his most devoted wife, 60 years old, and for Aelius Frontonus, his son, and for his family.
EDCS-28400255.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Publius Aelius Priscus during his lifetime for himself, his wife Aelia Mellito, his son Aelius Frontonus, and his family, noting their ages of 60 years.

EDCS-28400256.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vit Stennas sibi et Stennatoni coniugi quae vixit annos LX vivi posuerunt
EDCS-28400256.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vit Stennas and Stennato, his wife, who lived 60 years, placed this for themselves while living.
EDCS-28400256.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Vit Stennas and his wife Stennato during their lifetimes for themselves, recording Stennato's age of 60 years.

EDCS-28400257.text_edition: Dis Manibus Andes et Cleus vivi posuerunt sibi annorum LXX Caius MON
EDCS-28400257.translation: To the Divine Shades. Andes and Cleus, while living, placed this for themselves. 70 years old. Caius MON.
EDCS-28400257.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Andes and Cleus during their lifetimes for themselves, indicating an age of 70 years and the name Caius MON, possibly another dedicant or an individual related to the monument.

EDCS-28400261.text_edition: Titus Auius AV NI IT XXXX viv- fecit et I P P P M
EDCS-28400261.translation: Titus Auius AV NI IT, 40 years old, made this while living and I P P P M.
EDCS-28400261.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Auius during his lifetime, noting his age of 40 years, with an unclear abbreviated phrase at the end.

EDCS-28400262.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Nervae Augusto patri patriae consuli III decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400262.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Nerva Augustus, father of his country, consul for the third time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400262.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Emperor Nerva, listing his titles and indicating that it was erected by the municipal council (decurions), likely dating to 97-98 CE based on his consulship.

EDCS-28400263.text_edition: Marco Aelio Aurelio Vero Caesari Imperatoris Titi Aeli Caesaris Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti consuli II decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400263.translation: To Marcus Aelius Aurelius Verus Caesar, son of Emperor Titus Aelius Caesar Hadrian Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of his country, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, consul for the second time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400263.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Marcus Aurelius Verus Caesar, detailing his imperial lineage and titles, erected by the local decurions, likely dating to 140-145 CE based on his second consulship and adoptive parentage.

EDCS-28400266.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Domitius titulum IVS fecit sibi vivus FANO EINESVO CLVAIA et coniugi carissimae suae castae bene merentique quae vixit annos LXXIIII
EDCS-28400266.translation: Tiberius Claudius Domitius, while living, made this title IVS for himself FANO EINESVO CLVAIA and for his most dear, chaste, and deserving wife, who lived 74 years.
EDCS-28400266.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Tiberius Claudius Domitius during his lifetime for himself and for his wife, commemorating her virtuous qualities and age of 74 years, with some unclear text related to the monument's purpose or location.

EDCS-28400267.text_edition: Caio Iulio Rufino filio patri A
EDCS-28400267.translation: To Gaius Iulius Rufinus, son. To father A.
EDCS-28400267.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Iulius Rufinus, identified as a son, and also mentioning a father with the initial 'A'.

EDCS-28400268.text_edition: Marcus Livius Lucius C R O
EDCS-28400268.translation: Marcus Livius, son of Lucius, of the Colline tribe.
EDCS-28400268.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Marcus Livius.

EDCS-28400276.text_edition: Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus ex fPrumV(entario)#frum(entario)#PRUV beneficiarius consularis et Cnaeus SAV  ROB CIVM D I M POBI M A MVNC FILI D
EDCS-28400276.translation: Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, a frumentarius, beneficiarius consularis, and Gnaeus. SAV ROB CIVM D I M POBI M A MVNC FILI D.
EDCS-28400276.summary: This inscription records the names and military ranks of Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus and Gnaeus, likely a funerary monument given the other elements.

EDCS-28400277.text_edition: Numini maiestatique domini nostri Gordiani Augusti et Genio loci Aurelius Valerianus speculator legionis XI Claudiae referens gratiam  votum solvit
EDCS-28400277.translation: To the divinity and majesty of our lord Gordian Augustus and to the Genius of the place, Aurelius Valerianus, a speculatore of the Eleventh Legion Claudia, gratefully fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400277.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Aurelius Valerianus, a scout from the Eleventh Legion Claudia, to the Emperor Gordian and the local Genius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400278.text_edition: Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Gallieni Augusti decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400278.translation: To Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of our lord Gallienus Augustus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400278.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by the decurions to Empress Cornelia Salonina, wife of Emperor Gallienus.

EDCS-28400279.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Niger and Aurelia.

EDCS-28400281.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a daughter.

EDCS-28400282.text_edition: Sentonae Eutychus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400282.translation: To Sentona, Eutychus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400282.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Eutychus to the goddess Sentona, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400283.text_edition: Caio Livio Cai filio Sergia Clementi militi cohortis VIII praetoriae centuria Cai Marci Gemellini Livius Obsequens libertus vivus fecit
EDCS-28400283.translation: To Gaius Livius Clemens, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, soldier of the Eighth Praetorian Cohort, in the century of Gaius Marcus Gemellinus. Livius Obsequens, his freedman, made this during his lifetime.
EDCS-28400283.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Livius Obsequens for his patron, Gaius Livius Clemens, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard.

EDCS-28400284.text_edition: Caio Notario Vettidiano Secundo IIviro iure dicundo sacerdoti Augusti fratri Titus Vettidius Nepos testamento fieri iussit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400284.translation: To Gaius Notarius Vettidianus Secundus, duumvir with judicial power, priest of Augustus, his brother, Titus Vettidius Nepos ordered this to be made by his will; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
