EDCS-28400284.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Notarius Vettidianus Secundus, a high-ranking local official, erected by his brother Titus Vettidius Nepos according to his will, on land granted by the decurions.

EDCS-28400285.text_edition: Vettidio Nepoti patri IIviro iure dicundo Titus Vettidius Nepos testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-28400285.translation: To Vettidius Nepos, his father, duumvir with judicial power, Titus Vettidius Nepos ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-28400285.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Vettidius Nepos for his father, Vettidius Nepos, a duumvir with judicial power, according to his will.

EDCS-28400287.text_edition: Marcus Vipsanus Marci libertus Faustus Icae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400287.translation: Marcus Vipsanus Faustus, freedman of Marcus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Ica.
EDCS-28400287.summary: This is a votive inscription where Marcus Vipsanus Faustus, a freedman, dedicates an offering to the deity Ica.

EDCS-28400288.text_edition: Iriae Augustae in memoriam Vibiae Portiae matris Aquilia Quinti filia Colatina donum dedit
EDCS-28400288.translation: Aquilia Colatina, daughter of Quintus, gave this gift to Iria Augusta in memory of her mother, Vibia Portia.
EDCS-28400288.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Iria Augusta by Aquilia Colatina in memory of her mother, Vibia Portia, likely on a tomb or funerary monument.

EDCS-28400289.text_edition: Iriae Veneri Caia Valeria Optati fE(ilia)#f(ilia)#E Felicula votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400289.translation: Caia Valeria Felicula, daughter of Optatus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Iria Venus.
EDCS-28400289.summary: This is a votive inscription where Caia Valeria Felicula dedicates an offering to the deity Iria Venus.

EDCS-28400291.translation: Nobilis, the dispenser, placed this for Oni.
EDCS-28400291.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Nobilis, a 'dispensator' (steward or treasurer), to someone or something named Oni.

EDCS-28400292.text_edition: Acilia Luci filia Quincta viva fecit sibi et Sexto Aquillio viro suo heredi et Lucio Aquillio Sexti filio Claudia Longo filio decurioni
EDCS-28400292.translation: Acilia Quincta, daughter of Lucius, made this while living for herself and for Sextus Aquillius, her husband and heir, and for Lucius Aquillius Claudia Longus, son of Sextus, a decurion.
EDCS-28400292.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Acilia Quincta while alive, for herself, her husband, and their son, a decurion, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-28400293.text_edition: Sexto Aquillio Sexti filio Lucio Aquillio Sexti filio Rufo
EDCS-28400293.translation: For Sextus Aquillius, son of Sextus; for Lucius Aquillius Rufus, son of Sextus.
EDCS-28400293.summary: This inscription lists two male individuals, Sextus Aquillius and Lucius Aquillius Rufus, likely for a funerary monument dedicated to them.

EDCS-28400294.text_edition: Avita Suioca Vesclevesis filia viva fecit sibi et V   Volsounae Suiocae Vesclevesis filiae   Avita Aquillia Luci filia viva fecit sibi et Vol
EDCS-28400294.translation: Avita Suioca, daughter of Vesclevesis, made this while living for herself and for V[---] Volsouna Suioca, daughter of Vesclevesis. Avita Aquillia, daughter of Lucius, made this while living for herself and for Vol[---].
EDCS-28400294.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by two women, Avita Suioca and Avita Aquillia, for themselves and other family members while they were still alive.

EDCS-28400295.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a Gaius Lanius, likely part of a larger dedication or record.

EDCS-28400296.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory text, mentioning individuals with names like 'Olso' and 'Graesimo'.

EDCS-28400297.text_edition: Flaviae Titiae coniugi incomparabili Flavius Felicissimus fecit
EDCS-28400297.translation: Flavius Felicissimus made this for Flavia Titia, his incomparable wife.
EDCS-28400297.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Felicissimus to his wife, Flavia Titia, praising her as incomparable.

EDCS-28400298.translation: To Pleson, for their parents and for themselves, they made this.
EDCS-28400298.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that an unnamed person or people made a monument for Pleson, their parents, and themselves.

EDCS-28400300.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Lucius Thorius.

EDCS-28400301.text_edition: uxori C Cai filia Maxuma Sex Cai filia CaP Turrania
EDCS-28400301.translation: To his wife, Maxuma, daughter of Gaius, daughter of Gaius, and Turrania, daughter of Sextus Gaius.
EDCS-28400301.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to two women, Maxuma and Turrania, mentioning their fathers and family relationships.

EDCS-28400302.text_edition: Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Lucius Volumnius Pudens votum solvit libens merito fecit D
EDCS-28400302.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Lucius Volumnius Pudens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. He made it.
EDCS-28400302.summary: This is a religious dedication by Lucius Volumnius Pudens to Liber Pater Augustus, made to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-28400303.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis sacrum ex voto suscepto pro salute municipii balineo effecto Tiberius Gavillius Cai filius Claudia Lambicus aedilis IIvir posuit
EDCS-28400303.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs, from a vow undertaken for the safety of the municipality, with the bathhouse having been completed, Tiberius Gavillius Lambicus, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, aedile, duumvir, placed this.
EDCS-28400303.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Tiberius Gavillius Lambicus, an aedile and duumvir, to the Augustan Nymphs, fulfilling a vow for the safety of the municipality after the completion of a bathhouse.

EDCS-28400304.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti
EDCS-28400304.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus.
EDCS-28400304.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to an emperor, likely Marcus Aurelius or Lucius Verus, tracing his imperial lineage back to Antoninus Pius, Hadrian, and Trajan.

EDCS-28400305.text_edition: Marco Iulio Severo Filippo sic nobilissimo Caesari nobilissimo principi iuventutis res publica Albonessium sic
EDCS-28400305.translation: To Marcus Iulius Severus Philippus, most noble Caesar, most noble Prince of Youth, the Republic of Albonessium (made this).
EDCS-28400305.summary: This is an honorific inscription by the Republic of Albonessium dedicated to Marcus Iulius Severus Philippus, likely Philip II, referring to him as most noble Caesar and Prince of Youth, dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28400308.text_edition: VIIII Lucius Veratius Luci filius Claudia Otho miles legionis XI annorum XX dedicavit
EDCS-28400308.translation: Ninth (year of service). Lucius Veratius Otho, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, twenty years old, dedicated this.
EDCS-28400308.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Veratius Otho, a 20-year-old soldier of the Legio XI, made after nine years of service, dedicated by himself or someone on his behalf.

EDCS-28400309.text_edition: us Hostiduces filius Volso hic LES in SyVria#Syria#SVRIA D Titius fratri fecit
EDCS-28400309.translation: Hostiduces, son of Volso, here in Syria, Titius made this for his brother.
EDCS-28400309.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Syria, made by Titius for his brother Hostiduces, son of Volso.

EDCS-28400310.text_edition: Sexto Gavilio Sexti filio Claudia Germo aedili IIviro Gavilia Sexti filia Maxima filia fecit huic ob merita locus sepulturae datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400310.translation: To Sextus Gavilius Germo, son of Sextus, of the Claudia tribe, aedile, duumvir. Gavilia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, his daughter, made this. Due to his merits, a place of burial was given to him by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400310.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gavilia Maxima to her father, Sextus Gavilius Germo, an aedile and duumvir, who was granted a burial plot by decree of the decurions for his merits.

EDCS-28400311.text_edition: Publio Gavillio Sexti filio Claudia Prisco aedili IIviro patri huic decreto decurionum d(atum) est funus publicum Taeliae Volsetis filiae Quartae matri Titiae Luci filiae Proculae uxori Publius Gavillius Publi filius Claudia Maximus aedilis IIvir fecit
EDCS-28400311.translation: To Publius Gavillius Priscus, son of Sextus, of the Claudia tribe, aedile, duumvir, his father, to whom a public funeral was given by decree of the decurions. To Taelia Quarta, daughter of Volsetus, his mother. To Titia Procula, daughter of Lucius, his wife. Publius Gavillius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, aedile, duumvir, made this.
EDCS-28400311.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Publius Gavillius Maximus for his father Publius Gavillius Priscus, who received a public funeral, his mother Taelia Quarta, and his wife Titia Procula, likely dating to the early Imperial period given the mention of aedile and duumvir offices.

EDCS-28400312.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely dedicated by a husband holding the offices of aedile and duumvir to his wife, though most of the text is lost.

EDCS-28400313.text_edition: ER AN  CONSVI RAT DEIA TATM L MAR
EDCS-28400313.translation: [---] er an consu[l] [---] rat deia tatum L. Mar[---]
EDCS-28400313.summary: This inscription is a severely fragmented text, making its precise content and purpose impossible to determine, though it may contain parts of names, titles (like 'consul'), or administrative terms.

EDCS-28400314.text_edition: Vesclevesi Petronio Triti filio is in provincia defunctus est iturus
EDCS-28400314.translation: To Vesclevesis Petronius, son of Tritus, who died in the province, about to depart.
EDCS-28400314.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Vesclevesis Petronius, son of Tritus, who passed away in a province while preparing to travel, likely erected by his family or associates.

EDCS-28400315.text_edition: Sextus Ceionius Voltimesis filius Claudia Loiscus annorum LXXX
EDCS-28400315.translation: Sextus Ceionius Loiscus, son of Voltimesis, of the Claudia tribe, 80 years old.
EDCS-28400315.summary: This inscription is a simple funerary epitaph recording the name, filiation, tribe, and age of Sextus Ceionius Loiscus.

EDCS-28400316.text_edition: Lucius Ceionius Luci libertus Claudia Priamus vivus fecit sibi et Tariae  libertae Primigeniae uxori carissimae
EDCS-28400316.translation: Lucius Ceionius Priamus, freedman of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, made this alive for himself and for Taria Primigenia, his most beloved wife, a freedwoman.
EDCS-28400316.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Ceionius Priamus, a freedman, for himself and his beloved wife, Taria Primigenia, also a freedwoman, indicating they prepared their tomb while still living.

EDCS-28400317.text_edition: Locus sepulturae Sexti Gavilli Titi fili in fronte pedes XXV in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-28400317.translation: Burial plot of Sextus Gavillius, son of Titus: 25 feet in front, 30 feet in depth.
EDCS-28400317.summary: This inscription marks the dimensions and ownership of a burial plot belonging to Sextus Gavillius, son of Titus, serving as a boundary marker for his tomb.

EDCS-28400318.text_edition: Lucius Vibius Luci libertus Crescens Aur- V
EDCS-28400318.translation: Lucius Vibius Crescens, freedman of Lucius, Aur[elia?] V[---].
EDCS-28400318.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, identifying Lucius Vibius Crescens, a freedman of Lucius, with an unclear further association or designation.

EDCS-28400319.text_edition: Dis Manibus Volumniae Luci filiae Proculae miseri parentes qui in spem tollunt filias si spes decepit cum viderunt mortuas
EDCS-28400319.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Volumnia Procula, daughter of Lucius. Wretched parents who raise their daughters in hope, if hope deceives them when they see them dead.
EDCS-28400319.summary: This poignant funerary inscription is dedicated by grief-stricken parents to their deceased daughter, Volumnia Procula, expressing the sorrow of lost hope in their child's future.

EDCS-28400321.text_edition: Libero Patri Torclesi fecit P Saturninus ex voto pro P Clemente filio suo ex suo cum suis
EDCS-28400321.translation: To Liber Pater, Torclesi made this, by vow for his son Publius Clemens, from his own funds with his family.
EDCS-28400321.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Torclesi to Liber Pater, fulfilling a vow on behalf of his son, Publius Clemens, using his own and his family's resources.

EDCS-28400323.translation: nius, freedman of Lucius, six-time sevir.
EDCS-28400323.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription recording a freedman named nius, a sevir Augustalis.

EDCS-28400324.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Coelia Crescentino patri pientissimo filia cum matre in memoriam
EDCS-28400324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Coelia Crescentino, a daughter, with her mother, placed this for her most devoted father in memory.
EDCS-28400324.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Coelia Crescentino and her mother to her most devoted father.

EDCS-28400325.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Pardalati posuit Queta quonserva sic qui vicxit sic annos XL
EDCS-28400325.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Queta, his fellow slave, placed this for Pardalatus, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-28400325.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Queta, a fellow slave, to Pardalatus, who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-28400326.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Urbana -- fratri suoQ#suo#SQ Martiali bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28400326.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iulia Urbana placed this for her well-deserving brother Martialis.
EDCS-28400326.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Urbana to her well-deserving brother Martialis.

EDCS-28400327.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Ursinus et Eutychia parentes infelices Ursino filio pientissimo posuerunt annorum XXV
EDCS-28400327.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Ursinus and Eutychia, unhappy parents, placed this for their most devoted son Ursinus, who lived for 25 years.
EDCS-28400327.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ursinus and Eutychia, the parents, to their most devoted son Ursinus, who died at 25 years old.

EDCS-28400329.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a larger text, with only a few letters preserved, including parts of a name like 'Dent-'.

EDCS-28400330.text_edition: Quintus Blassius Firmus Dianae conservatrici posuit libens merito
EDCS-28400330.translation: Quintus Blassius Firmus willingly and deservedly placed this for Diana the Preserver.
EDCS-28400330.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Quintus Blassius Firmus to the goddess Diana Conservatrix.

EDCS-28400331.text_edition: Sacerdos Quintus Baronius Quinti filius templum et aras Iovi Herculi de <s=I>(ua#s(ua#I pecunia facienda curavit
EDCS-28400331.translation: Quintus Baronius, son of Quintus, a priest, took care to have the temple and altars made for Jupiter and Hercules from his own money.
EDCS-28400331.summary: This is an honorary inscription recording that Quintus Baronius, a priest, financed the construction of a temple and altars for Jupiter and Hercules.

EDCS-28400334.text_edition: Lucius Serrvilius Publi filius Sergia Dalmasius  S F --
EDCS-28400334.translation: Lucius Serrvilius Dalmasius, son of Publius, of the Sergian tribe...
EDCS-28400334.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or honorary, mentioning Lucius Serrvilius Dalmasius and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-28400336.text_edition: Caius Fadius Cai Fadi Cerdonis libertus Epictetus vivos sic fecit sibi et patrono et suis omnibus
EDCS-28400336.translation: Gaius Fadius Epictetus, freedman of Gaius Fadius Cerdo, made this while living for himself and his patron and all his household.
EDCS-28400336.summary: This is a funerary inscription, set up by Gaius Fadius Epictetus while still alive, for himself, his patron, and his entire family.

EDCS-28400337.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication by a father to his daughter, Numonia Maxima.

EDCS-28400338.text_edition: Gaius Octavius Marcus Octavius Marcus Octavius  Gaius Octavius N et fratribus faciendum curavit
EDCS-28400338.translation: Gaius Octavius, Marcus Octavius, Marcus Octavius, Gaius Octavius N. had this made for themselves and their brothers.
EDCS-28400338.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Octavius, Marcus Octavius, Marcus Octavius, and Gaius Octavius N. oversaw the construction of a monument for themselves and their brothers, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-28400339.text_edition: Clodiae Severae Clodius Gallicanus et Vipsana Surilla filiae locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400339.translation: To Clodia Severa. Clodius Gallicanus and Vipsana Surilla, her daughter. The ground was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400339.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Clodia Severa, likely set up by Clodius Gallicanus and her daughter Vipsana Surilla, noting that the plot of land was officially granted by the town council.

EDCS-28400340.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caetenniae Hermioni Caetennius V patronus posuit
EDCS-28400340.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caetennius V, her patron, placed this for Caetennia Hermio.
EDCS-28400340.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, erected by Caetennius V, a patron, for his freedwoman Caetennia Hermio.

EDCS-28400341.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Flavio Vementi annorum XXV bene merenti liberta posuit
EDCS-28400341.translation: To the Divine Manes. A freedwoman placed this for Lucius Flavius Vemens, who lived 25 years and was well-deserving.
EDCS-28400341.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Lucius Flavius Vemens, a 25-year-old, erected by a freedwoman to honor him.

EDCS-28400342.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gloriam sic annorum V UrsulIa#Ursula#URSUIA pientissima mater posuit
EDCS-28400342.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ursula, a most devoted mother, placed this for Gloria, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-28400342.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, erected by a most devoted mother, Ursula, for her daughter Gloria, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-28400343.text_edition: Dibus Manibus Lucius Statius Marcellus Lucio Statio Marcellino patri piientissimo Laelia Chrisis sic Lucio Statio Marcellino coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28400343.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Statius Marcellus to his most devoted father Lucius Statius Marcellinus. Laelia Chrisis to her well-deserving husband Lucius Statius Marcellinus.
EDCS-28400343.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, erected by Lucius Statius Marcellus for his father Lucius Statius Marcellinus, and by Laelia Chrisis for her husband Lucius Statius Marcellinus.

EDCS-28400344.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titiae Nevillae defunctae nnorum L Flavii Florentini filius et pater matri dulcissimae et coniugi posuerunt bene merenti
EDCS-28400344.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Florentinus, her son and father, placed this for Tita Nevilla, who died at 50 years, his sweetest mother and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-28400344.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead for Tita Nevilla, who died at age 50, erected by Flavius Florentinus for his mother and his wife, both of whom were Tita Nevilla.

EDCS-28400346.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Sal<v=N>ius#Salvius#ALNIUS Obultronius Dexter
EDCS-28400346.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Salvius Obultronius Dexter.
EDCS-28400346.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a sacred object or monument to Hercules Augustus, made by Salvius Obultronius Dexter.

EDCS-28400347.text_edition: Vesellia sic Felicetas sic Libero Magno Patri Torculensi ex voto
EDCS-28400347.translation: Vesellia Felicetas to Liber Pater Magnus Torculensis, as a vow.
EDCS-28400347.summary: This is a votive inscription made by Vesellia Felicetas to Liber Pater Magnus Torculensis, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400348.text_edition: Libero Patri  Aristio votum libens solvit
EDCS-28400348.translation: To Liber Pater, Aristius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400348.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory offering made by Aristius to the god Liber Pater, likely a private vow fulfillment.

EDCS-28400349.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Julius Bubalus.
EDCS-28400349.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Julius Bubalus to Sol Invictus Mithras, indicating a religious offering, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400350.text_edition: Nymphis sacrum Quintus Silvius Speratus centurio cohortis I Belgarum curagens theatri donum dedit
EDCS-28400350.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, Quintus Silvius Speratus, centurion of the First Cohort of Belgae, took charge of the theater and gave this gift.
EDCS-28400350.summary: This inscription records a donation made to the Nymphs by Quintus Silvius Speratus, a centurion, for a theater, likely in a public context.

EDCS-28400351.text_edition: TRO ?A3 G3 S3 Quintus Corellius Threptus locum constituit
EDCS-28400351.translation: TRO ?A3 G3 S3. Quintus Corellius Threptus established this place.
EDCS-28400351.summary: This inscription indicates that Quintus Corellius Threptus established a particular location, possibly for a grave or a monument, with some unclear initial abbreviations.

EDCS-28400353.text_edition: Arriae Florae matri Arriae Tertullae sorori
EDCS-28400353.translation: To Arria Flora, mother, to Arria Tertulla, sister.
EDCS-28400353.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, honoring Arria Flora, a mother, and Arria Tertulla, a sister, likely placed by a family member.

EDCS-28400354.text_edition: Ianuario Ianuari liberto filio ann(orum) XL
EDCS-28400354.translation: To Januarius, freedman of Januarius, son, 40 years old.
EDCS-28400354.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Januarius, a freedman and son of another Januarius, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-28400356.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli Nigellionis nnorum XXXX Petilia Secunda bene merenti  posuit
EDCS-28400356.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Aurelius Nigellio, 40 years old. Petilia Secunda set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28400356.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Petilia Secunda for Aurelius Nigellio, who lived for 40 years, dedicating it to the Manes.

EDCS-28400357.text_edition: Pomponio Serapioni carissimo Lucilia Aufidia mater bene merenti L posuit
EDCS-28400357.translation: To Pomponius Serapion, most beloved. Lucilia Aufidia, his mother, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28400357.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucilia Aufidia for her most beloved son, Pomponius Serapion, as a tribute to the well-deserving.

EDCS-28400359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Victor Messillae conpari infelici quae vicxit sic annos plus mN(inus)#m(inus)#N XXX titulum posuit
EDCS-28400359.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Victor placed this titulus for his unfortunate companion Messilla, who lived about 30 years.
EDCS-28400359.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aurelius Victor for his deceased companion, Messilla, who lived approximately 30 years, dedicating it to the Manes.

EDCS-28400363.text_edition: Aurelius Kamines natione Armenius qui vixit annos XXVIII
EDCS-28400363.translation: Aurelius Kamines, of Armenian nationality, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-28400363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Kamines, noting his Armenian origin and that he lived for 28 years.

EDCS-28400366.text_edition: Helviae Saturninae coniugi Publius Titienus Nepos
EDCS-28400366.translation: To Helvia Saturnina, his wife, Publius Titienus Nepos [erected this].
EDCS-28400366.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Publius Titienus Nepos to his wife, Helvia Saturnina.

EDCS-28400368.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Quinctilius Quinti filius Seneca veteranus cohortis IIII praetoriae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28400368.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Sextus Quinctilius Seneca, son of Quintus, a veteran of the Fourth Praetorian Cohort, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400368.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication by Sextus Quinctilius Seneca, a praetorian veteran, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28400370.text_edition: Nymphis Augustis sacrum Caius Raecius Leo aquam quam nullus antiquorum in civitate fuisse meminerit inventam impendio ex volunt=TN(ate)#volunt(ate)#VOLUTN Cai Raeci Rufi clarissimi viri patroni sui dedicavit Severo et Pompeiano II consulibus VI Idus Novembres
EDCS-28400370.translation: Sacred to the Augustan Nymphs, Gaius Raecius Leo dedicated the water, which no one among the ancients is remembered to have found in the city, discovered at the expense and by the will of Gaius Raecius Rufus, most distinguished man, his patron, on the 6th day before the Ides of November, when Severus and Pompeianus were consuls for the second time.
EDCS-28400370.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a newly discovered water source to the Augustan Nymphs by Gaius Raecius Leo, funded by his patron Gaius Raecius Rufus, dated to November 8, 207 AD.

EDCS-28400372.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco tribunicia potestate XVIIII consuli III imperatori III decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400372.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, with tribunician power for the 19th time, consul for the 3rd time, imperator for the 3rd time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400372.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), listing his imperial titles and filiation, erected by decree of the decurions, likely dating to 216 AD.

EDCS-28400373.text_edition: Iuliae Dominae Augustae matri castrorum decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400373.translation: To Julia Domna Augusta, Mother of the Camps, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400373.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Empress Julia Domna, recognizing her title 'Mother of the Camps,' erected by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-28400374.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Parthico
EDCS-28400374.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Parthicus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus.
EDCS-28400374.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), identifying him as the son of Septimius Severus.

EDCS-28400375.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Augusto divi Magni Antonini filio divi Severi nepoti pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III patri patriae decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400375.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus, son of the deified Great Antoninus, grandson of the deified Severus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400375.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander, listing his extensive imperial titles and lineage, erected by decree of the decurions, likely dating to 230 AD.

EDCS-28400376.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Augusto decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400376.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Augustus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400376.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, erected by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-28400378.text_edition: Magia Lugulla nnum XVIIII hic sita est
EDCS-28400378.translation: Magia Lugulla, aged 19, is buried here.
EDCS-28400378.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Magia Lugulla, noting her age and burial place.

EDCS-28400380.text_edition: ILII XI urbanae tribuno cohortis VI praetoriae et protectori Auggustorum nnnostrum patroni splendidissimae civitatis Curictarum ob insignem eius benevolentiam statuam poni sanxerunt feliciter
EDCS-28400380.translation: To Ilii XI urban, tribune of the Sixth Praetorian Cohort and protector of our Augusti, patron of the most splendid city of the Curictae, because of his remarkable benevolence, they sanctioned the placement of a statue, successfully.
EDCS-28400380.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue for Ilii XI, a tribune of the praetorian guard and protector of the emperors, honored by the city of Curictae for his benevolence.

EDCS-28400382.text_edition: Publius Pitius Publi filius Marullus decurionum decreto publice elatus sepultusque est
EDCS-28400382.translation: Publius Pitius Marullus, son of Publius, was publicly carried out and buried by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400382.summary: This inscription records the public funeral and burial of Publius Pitius Marullus, granted by a decree of the decurions, likely on a tombstone or funerary monument.

EDCS-28400383.text_edition: Raeciae Cai filiae Pollae decurionum decreto publice
EDCS-28400383.translation: To Raecia Polla, daughter of Gaius, publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400383.summary: This inscription fragment indicates a public honor or burial for Raecia Polla, granted by a decree of the decurions, likely part of a larger funerary monument.

EDCS-28400385.text_edition: Dis Manibus Oppiae Luci filiae Proclae Aurelia Publi filia Maxima matri pientissimae viva fecit
EDCS-28400385.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Maxima, daughter of Publius, made this in her lifetime for Oppia Procla, daughter of Lucius, her most devoted mother.
EDCS-28400385.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aurelia Maxima's dedication of a monument to her deceased mother, Oppia Procla, during the dedicator's lifetime.

EDCS-28400387.text_edition: Quinto Kapio Maximo  Kapiae   Kapiae Maximae  Kapiae Postumae   et
EDCS-28400387.translation: To Quintus Capius Maximus, to Capia Maxima, to Capia Postuma, and...
EDCS-28400387.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription listing multiple individuals, likely family members, for whom a monument was erected.

EDCS-28400388.text_edition: Turi fili-  sibi et  Volsoni viro  is filio Turo patri Volsonis filiae Fatae matri us faciundum curavit
EDCS-28400388.translation: To Turo, the son; for himself and to Volso, his husband; this son, Turo, for his father; for Volsonia, his daughter; for Fata, his mother. He arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-28400388.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, likely on a family tomb, recording its construction by an unnamed individual for himself, his husband Volso, his son Turo, his daughter Volsonia, and his mother Fata.

EDCS-28400389.text_edition: II  Tullia Publi filia Maxima matri  locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-28400389.translation: II. Tullia Maxima, daughter of Publius, for her mother. The plot was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400389.summary: This funerary inscription records Tullia Maxima dedicating a monument to her mother, noting that the burial plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.

EDCS-28500007.translation: Of Tiberius, from the Pansiana estate.
EDCS-28500007.summary: This is a short inscription, likely an ownership or origin mark, indicating an object belongs to Tiberius and comes from the Pansiana estate.

EDCS-28500008.translation: Of Tiberius, from the Pansiana estate.
EDCS-28500008.summary: This is a short inscription, likely an ownership or origin mark, indicating an object belongs to Tiberius and comes from the Pansiana estate.

EDCS-28500009.translation: Of Gaius Caesar, from the Pansiana estate.
EDCS-28500009.summary: This is a short inscription, likely an ownership or origin mark, indicating an object belongs to Gaius Caesar and comes from the Pansiana estate.

EDCS-28500044.translation: Of Nero Caesar, L.A. I[---] from the Pansiana estate.
EDCS-28500044.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely an ownership or origin mark, indicating an object belongs to Nero Caesar and comes from the Pansiana estate, with some illegible intermediate text.

EDCS-28500058.summary: This inscription marks an object as the property of Gaius Caesar, possibly referring to a specific estate or item.

EDCS-28500424.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto
EDCS-28500424.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian.
EDCS-28500424.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring the emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, detailing his imperial titles and lineage.

EDCS-28600106.text_edition: Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto pontifici maximo Caius Aemilius Volsoni filius Oclatinus Lucius Fonteius Quinti filius Rufus IIviri porticum curiam decreto decurionum faciundum curavere idemque probavere
EDCS-28600106.translation: Under Tiberius Caesar, son of Augustus, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus: Gaius Aemilius Oclatinus, son of Volso, and Lucius Fonteius Rufus, son of Quintus, as duumvirs, saw to the construction of the portico and curia by decree of the decurions, and also approved them.
EDCS-28600106.summary: This inscription records the construction and approval of a portico and curia by two duumvirs, Gaius Aemilius Oclatinus and Lucius Fonteius Rufus, under the emperor Tiberius Caesar, likely dating to his reign (14-37 CE).

EDCS-28600107.text_edition: Quintus Fonteius Raeci filius vivus fecit sibi et Volsounae Oplicae Plaetoris filiae coniugi suae
EDCS-28600107.translation: Quintus Fonteius, son of Raecus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Volsouna Oplica, daughter of Plaetor, his wife.
EDCS-28600107.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Quintus Fonteius prepared a tomb in his lifetime for himself and his wife, Volsouna Oplica.

EDCS-28600108.text_edition: Laeliae Pyramidi contubernali suae et Laeli?
EDCS-28600108.translation: To Laelia Pyramis, his companion, and to Laelius [---].
EDCS-28600108.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laelia Pyramis, described as a companion, and partially for a male named Laelius.

EDCS-28600109.text_edition: Quintus Nigidius Turi filius vivus fecit sibi et Avitae Nigidiae Volsouni filiae uxori
EDCS-28600109.translation: Quintus Nigidius, son of Turus, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Avita Nigidia, daughter of Volso, his wife.
EDCS-28600109.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Quintus Nigidius prepared a tomb in his lifetime for himself and his wife, Avita Nigidia.

EDCS-28600110.translation: To Lucius, son [---], and to Paula [---].
EDCS-28600110.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely commemorating a son named Lucius and a woman named Paula.

EDCS-28600111.text_edition: Primitivo filio nnorum XXVI mensium V Priscilla et Successus parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-28600111.translation: To Primitivus, their son, 26 years and 5 months old. Priscilla and Successus, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-28600111.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Priscilla and Successus, commemorating their son Primitivus, who died at the age of 26 years and 5 months.

EDCS-28600112.text_edition: Vinioca Spuri filia Maximilla sibi et Marco Vinioco Primigenio viro libertis libertabusque viva fecit
EDCS-28600112.translation: Vinioca Maximilla, daughter of Spurius, made this in her lifetime for herself and for Marcus Viniocus Primigenius, her husband, and for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28600112.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Vinioca Maximilla prepared a tomb in her lifetime for herself, her husband Marcus Viniocus Primigenius, and their freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-28600116.translation: Maximianus thus fulfills his vow, thus [---].
EDCS-28600116.summary: This is a votive inscription by Maximianus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28600117.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum  Valerius Valens vetereranus  ex |(centurione) limitem publicum  praeclusum  ob decessum  Aureli Galli  legati suo inpendio aperuit  Imperatore Commodo II et Martio Vero  II consulibus VI  Kalendas  Maias
EDCS-28600117.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Valerius Valens, a veteran, formerly a centurion, at his own expense reopened the public road, which had been closed due to the death of Aurelius Gallus, the legate, on the 6th Kalends of May, in the consulship of Imperator Commodus II and Martius Verus II.
EDCS-28600117.summary: This inscription records the reopening of a public road by Valerius Valens, a veteran, dedicated to Hercules Augustus, in 179 AD.

EDCS-28600118.text_edition: Iano Patri Augusto sacrum Caius Iulius Cai filius Sergia Aetor aedilis donatus ab Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto torque maiore bello Delmatico ob honorem IIviratus cum liberis suis posuit
EDCS-28600118.translation: Sacred to Father Janus Augustus. Gaius Julius, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, Aetor, aedile, honored by Tiberius Caesar, son of Augustus, with a large torque in the Dalmatian War, in honor of his duumvirate, set this up with his children.
EDCS-28600118.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Janus Pater Augustus by Gaius Julius Aetor, an aedile, commemorating honors received from Tiberius Caesar for his military service and duumvirate, set up with his children.

EDCS-28600120.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto omnipotenti Mithrae aeterno sacrum Atilius Tertius ex voto libiens posuit
EDCS-28600120.translation: Sacred to the unconquered, omnipotent, eternal god Sol Invictus Mithras. Atilius Tertius willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28600120.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the god Sol Invictus Mithras by Atilius Tertius.

EDCS-28600128.text_edition: Surus Sparuci filius domo Tribocus eques alae Claudiae novae nnorum XXX stipendiorum XIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Celati sesquiplicarii heredis
EDCS-28600128.translation: Surus, son of Sparux, from the Triboci, a horseman of the new Ala Claudia, 30 years old, having served for 13 years, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will, at the discretion of Celatus, a sesquiplicarius, his heir.
EDCS-28600128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Surus, a cavalryman from the Triboci, buried according to his will by his heir Celatus.

EDCS-28600138.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoniae Falernae nepotillae et Claudio Proculo filio eius defuncto annorum VIII et Antoniae Clucerae sic filiae et Octavio Papiriano nepoti defuncto annorum VII Antonia Fencis sic mater infelicissima ob pietatem posuit
EDCS-28600138.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Antonia Fencis, a most unhappy mother, out of piety, placed this for Antonia Falerna, her granddaughter, and for her son Claudius Proculus, who died at 8 years of age, and for her daughter Antonia Glucera, and for her grandson Octavius Papirianus, who died at 7 years of age.
EDCS-28600138.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Antonia Fencis, a grieving mother and grandmother, for her deceased daughter, son, granddaughter, and grandson.

EDCS-28600143.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Caesellius ChrysogConus#Chrysogonus#CHRYSOCONUS filius Lucio Caesellio Chrysantiano patri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28600143.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Caesellius Chrysogonus, son, set this up for Lucius Caesellius Chrysantianus, his well-deserving father.
EDCS-28600143.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Caesellius Chrysogonus to his deserving father, Lucius Caesellius Chrysantianus.

EDCS-28600149.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Flavio Primo Flavius Epictetus patrono bene merenti
EDCS-28600149.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Flavius Epictetus [set this up] for Marcus Flavius Primus, his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-28600149.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Epictetus to his patron, Marcus Flavius Primus.

EDCS-28600168.text_edition: Marcus Poblicius Marci filius Aquila annorum II
EDCS-28600168.translation: Marcus Poblicius, son of Marcus, Aquila, 2 years old.
EDCS-28600168.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for a young child named Marcus Poblicius Aquila.

EDCS-28600182.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Vesidiae Crescentillae AE defunctaeI#defunctae#DEFUNCTAI annorum XXV Vesidia Cassia mater filiae infelicissimaeI#infelicissimae#INFELICISSIMAI
EDCS-28600182.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vesidia Cassia, a most unhappy mother, [set this up] for her daughter Vesidia Crescentilla, who died at 25 years of age.
EDCS-28600182.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a grieving mother, Vesidia Cassia, for her deceased daughter, Vesidia Crescentilla.

EDCS-28600185.text_edition: Caius Volusius Primigenius VIvir vivos sic fecit sibi et Caio Volusio Euhemero conliberto VIviro Augustali et Caio Volusio Primigenio filio defuncto nnum VIII libertis libertabusque suis ossibus inferendis extraniorum nequis ossa inferre velit et tu et tibi in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-28600185.translation: Gaius Volusius Primigenius, a sevir, made this for himself while alive, and for Gaius Volusius Euhemerus, his fellow freedman and Augustalis sevir, and for his son Gaius Volusius Primigenius, who died at 8 years of age, and for his freedmen and freedwomen, for the burial of their bones. Let no one else desire to inter bones here. You have and for yourself, 30 feet in front, 30 feet in the field.
EDCS-28600185.summary: This is a funerary inscription and an explicit grave marker established by Gaius Volusius Primigenius for himself, his freedman, his deceased son, and his freedpeople, detailing the dimensions of the plot and forbidding others from using it.

EDCS-28600193.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus maximus Brittannicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator VI consul IIII pater patriae pontem Hippi fluminis vetustate corruptum restituit sumptum et operas subministrantibus Novensibus Delminensibus Riditis curante et dedicante Lucio Iunio Rufino Proculiano legato pro praetore
EDCS-28600193.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Sarmaticus Germanicus Maximus Britannicus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the ninth time, acclaimed Imperator for the sixth time, consul for the fourth time, Father of the Country, restored the bridge over the River Hippus, which had been ruined by age, with the Novenses, Delminenses, and Riditae providing expenses and labor, under the supervision and dedication of Lucius Junius Rufinus Proculianus, propraetorian legate.
EDCS-28600193.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a bridge over the Hippus River by Emperor Commodus around 184 CE, with local communities contributing labor and expenses, overseen by the provincial legate.

EDCS-28600194.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul II proconsul pater patriae et Marcus Iulius Philippus nobilissimus Caesar
EDCS-28600194.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, proconsul, Father of the Country, and Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-28600194.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II, the former with a full imperial titulature indicating a date around 247 CE.

EDCS-28600197.text_edition: Gaio Valerio Constantio et Galerio Valerio Maximiano nobilissimis Caesaribus
EDCS-28600197.translation: To Gaius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the most noble Caesars.
EDCS-28600197.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Constantius Chlorus and Galerius, as Caesars of the First Tetrarchy, dating to between 293 and 305 CE.

EDCS-28600198.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Maximinus Daia as Caesar, likely between 305 and 309 CE.

EDCS-28600203.text_edition: DDominis nnostris Galerio Valerio Maximiano et Flavio Severo invictis Auggustis
EDCS-28600203.translation: To our lords Galerius Valerius Maximianus and Flavius Severus, unconquered Augusti.
EDCS-28600203.summary: This inscription honors Galerius and Severus as Augusti, indicating a date between 306 and 307 CE.

EDCS-28700936.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Aurelio Varroni  veterano cohortis I |(miliariae) Aurelius Victorinus  et Iulia filii heredes posuerunt
EDCS-28700936.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Marcus Aurelius Varro, veteran of the First Cohort (milliaria). His sons Aurelius Victorinus and Iulia, as heirs, placed this.
EDCS-28700936.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Victorinus and Iulia to their father, Marcus Aurelius Varro, a veteran of a thousand-strong cohort.

EDCS-28700938.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Vitalis qui vixit nnos plus minus XIIII Aurelius Ianuarius miles legionis Italicae et Quirilla mater parentes filio infelicissimo miserrimio posuuerunt
EDCS-28700938.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius Vitalis, who lived about 14 years. Aurelius Ianuarius, soldier of the Italic Legion, and Quirilla, his mother, his most unfortunate, most miserable parents, placed this.
EDCS-28700938.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier, and Quirilla, the parents of Aelius Vitalis, who died at the young age of 14.

EDCS-28700940.text_edition: Asclepiade Domestico et Crescentilla adito libero in agro pedes VI
EDCS-28700940.translation: To Asclepiades, Domesticus, and Crescentilla, with a free burial plot six feet into the ground.
EDCS-28700940.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot for Asclepiades, Domesticus, and Crescentilla, specifying the dimensions of their designated free ground.

EDCS-28700941.text_edition: Caius Asurius miles legionis VII centuria Trini nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XIV domo Florentia Severa liberta posuit
EDCS-28700941.translation: Gaius Asurius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, from the century of Trinus, aged 35, with 14 years of service, from Florentia. Severa, his freedwoman, placed this.
EDCS-28700941.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Asurius, a 35-year-old soldier of the Legio VII who served 14 years, from Florentia, placed by his freedwoman Severa.

EDCS-28700942.translation: Sextus Aelius?, princeps of the legion...
EDCS-28700942.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely pertains to Sextus Aelius, a high-ranking centurion or 'princeps' of an unnamed legion.

EDCS-28700943.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28700943.text_edition: Aurelius Alexsander sic beneficiarius numero frumentariorum legionis XI Claudiae vibus sic hanc sibi suo nomine iussit testamento arcam poni
EDCS-28700943.translation: Aurelius Alexander, beneficiary of the frumentarii of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, by his own wish, ordered this sarcophagus to be placed for himself in his name by his will.
EDCS-28700943.summary: This is a funerary inscription on a sarcophagus, recording that Aurelius Alexander, a soldier from the Eleventh Claudian Legion, willed its placement for himself.

EDCS-28700945.text_edition: $ Au]reliae Ianuariae infelicissimae fEiliae#filiae#EILIAE quae vixit nnos III menses X Aurelius Ianuarius miles cohortis VIII pater eius Aurelia Ursina mater posueIrunt#posuerunt#POSUIRUNT
EDCS-28700945.translation: To Aurelia Ianuaria, a most unhappy daughter, who lived for 3 years and 10 months. Aurelius Ianuarius, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort, her father, and Aurelia Ursina, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-28700945.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by parents to their young daughter, Aurelia Ianuaria, who died at the age of three years and ten months.

EDCS-28700946.text_edition: Veneria Quartilla Aurelio Materno coniugi incomparabili et innocentissimo veterano legionis II Italicae natione Norico qui vixit nnos XLVIIII posuit coniunx bene merenti
EDCS-28700946.translation: Veneria Quartilla placed this for Aurelius Maternus, her incomparable and most innocent husband, a veteran of the Second Italic Legion, of Norican nationality, who lived for 49 years; his well-deserving wife set this up.
EDCS-28700946.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Veneria Quartilla to her husband, Aurelius Maternus, a veteran of the Second Italic Legion from Noricum, who died at 49.

EDCS-28700947.text_edition: Aureli legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XXX  filia Ioventia
EDCS-28700947.translation: Ioventia, daughter of Aurelius, of the Seventh Claudian Legion, Pious and Faithful, with 30 years of service.
EDCS-28700947.summary: This is likely a funerary or dedicatory inscription, recording Ioventia as the daughter of a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion who served for 30 years.

EDCS-28700948.text_edition: Publio Bennio Sabino IIIIviro iure dicundo auguri IIIIviro iure dicundo nni flamini Augustali praefecto cohortis II Lusitanorum equitatae
EDCS-28700948.translation: To Publius Bennius Sabinus, quattuorvir for administering justice, augur, quattuorvir for administering justice, perpetual priest of Augustus, prefect of the Second Lusitanian Cohort of cavalry.
EDCS-28700948.summary: This is an honorific inscription detailing the extensive civil and military career of Publius Bennius Sabinus, including multiple magistracies and military commands.

EDCS-28700949.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of an archer named Beres.

EDCS-28700952.text_edition: Quintus Cassius Quinti filius Tromentina Constans aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo augur praefectus fabrum tribunus militum cohortis IIII voluntariorum civium Romanorum Quintus Cassius Cerdo libertus et heres faciundum curavit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28700952.translation: Quintus Cassius Constans, son of Quintus, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile, quattuorvir for administering justice, augur, prefect of engineers, military tribune of the Fourth Cohort of Roman Citizen Volunteers. Quintus Cassius Cerdo, freedman and heir, took care to have this monument made. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28700952.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Cassius Constans, an official with various civic and military roles, set up by his freedman and heir, with a clause restricting its inheritance.

EDCS-28700953.text_edition: decurioni equitum praefecto cohortis III Alpinae et Quinto Ennio Severo nnorum XV fecit Lutatia Veneria viro et filio orba queror genetrix miseri post funera nati raptaque et ante premit me mea cara soror intra ter quinos infelix occidit annos sic illi coniunx sic toga pura data est nullaque propago manibus succepta parentis nullus honos fasces in mea tecta tulit omnia Ditis habet praeterquam nomen eorum nec comitem nati me sinit esse mei o genitor felix qui nec tua funera vidit nec filium filiam FRIII
EDCS-28700953.translation: To the decurion of cavalry, prefect of the Third Alpine Cohort, and to Quintus Ennius Severus, 15 years old. Lutatia Veneria, widowed and bereft of her son, I, the mother, lament after the death of my unhappy child, and my dear sister, also snatched away before him, weighs heavily upon me. Unhappy, he died before three times five years; thus was a wife given to him, thus a toga pura, yet no offspring received by his parent's hands, no honor brought fasces into my house. Pluto has everything except their name, nor does he allow me to be a companion to my son. Oh, happy father, who saw neither your own death nor that of your son or daughter!
EDCS-28700953.summary: This is a deeply emotional funerary inscription by Lutatia Veneria, mourning the loss of her husband (a decurion and prefect) and her 15-year-old son, Quintus Ennius Severus, along with her sister, reflecting on the tragedies that befell her family.

EDCS-28700954.text_edition: Quinto Fabio Pacato Voltinia Viennae militi legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnum XXXVII Fabius Fabianus frater posuit
EDCS-28700954.translation: To Quintus Fabius Pacatus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Vienna, a soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, Pious and Faithful, aged 37 years. Fabius Fabianus, his brother, placed this.
EDCS-28700954.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Fabius Fabianus to his brother, Quintus Fabius Pacatus, a soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion who died at 37.

EDCS-28700955.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Apuleio Montano veterano ex ordinato decurionum et IIviro coloniae Aequensium homini bono Octavia Rufina marito incomparabili et sibi posuit
EDCS-28700955.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Apuleius Montanus, a veteran promoted from centurion to decurion, and duumvir of the colony of Aequensium, a good man. Octavia Rufina placed this for her incomparable husband and for herself.
EDCS-28700955.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Apuleius Montanus, a veteran and municipal official, erected by his wife Octavia Rufina for him and for herself.

EDCS-28701027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iu<l>ius Probinu(s QVVV F C NBI posuitI#posuit#POSUII M V CXXX sibi et suis natis LX SN YZS |XSXZ X
EDCS-28701027.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Probinus, P V CXXX, placed this for himself and his children, LX.
EDCS-28701027.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Iulius Probinus dedicating a monument for himself and his children.

EDCS-28701300.text_edition: Marcus Attius Cinna  ob honorem Augustalitatis
EDCS-28701300.translation: Marcus Attius Cinna, on account of the honor of Augustalitas.
EDCS-28701300.summary: This is a fragment likely commemorating Marcus Attius Cinna's public office as an Augustalis, a priest of the imperial cult.

EDCS-28701301.text_edition: Petronius Cai filius Rufus IIIIvir testamento fieri iussit sibi et Caio Petronio
EDCS-28701301.translation: Petronius, son of Gaius, Rufus, Quattuorvir, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Gaius Petronius.
EDCS-28701301.summary: This inscription records Petronius Rufus, a quattuorvir (a magistrate), having a monument made for himself and Gaius Petronius according to his will.

EDCS-28701302.text_edition: Dis Manibus infelicissimae et Publius Atilius  matri
EDCS-28701302.translation: To the Divine Manes of the most unfortunate and Publius Atilius to his mother.
EDCS-28701302.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Publius Atilius dedicates a monument to his mother, implying her death was sudden or tragic.

EDCS-28701304.text_edition: Lucius Cervius Restitutus vivus fecit Antoniae Secundae uxori et Statiae Ingenuae matri eius
EDCS-28701304.translation: Lucius Cervius Restitutus made this while living, for his wife Antonia Secunda and her mother Statia Ingenua.
EDCS-28701304.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Cervius Restitutus during his lifetime for his wife and mother-in-law.

EDCS-28701305.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Cincio Fortunato Cincia Vitalis coniugi et Cincia Valeria filia patri piissimo
EDCS-28701305.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Cincius Fortunatus. Cincia Vitalis to her husband and Cincia Valeria, his daughter, to her most devoted father.
EDCS-28701305.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cincia Vitalis to her husband Gaius Cincius Fortunatus and by Cincia Valeria to her father.

EDCS-28701306.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescenti defuncto annorum IIII dierum IV Messia Paula alumno bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28701306.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Crescentius, who died at the age of 4 years and 4 days. Messia Paula placed this for her most deserving foster-child.
EDCS-28701306.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Messia Paula to her deceased foster-child Crescentius, who died very young.

EDCS-28701307.text_edition: Lucius Domitius Agathopus testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-28701307.translation: Lucius Domitius Agathopus ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-28701307.summary: This is an inscription indicating that Lucius Domitius Agathopus arranged for a monument or construction to be made through his will.

EDCS-28701308.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Florius Cornelianus Corneliae Satriae matri carissimae posuit
EDCS-28701308.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Florius Cornelianus placed this for Cornelia Satria, his dearest mother.
EDCS-28701308.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Florius Cornelianus to his beloved mother, Cornelia Satria.

EDCS-28701309.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fuscioni Floria Hilara mater filio
EDCS-28701309.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Fuscio. Floria Hilara, mother, to her son.
EDCS-28701309.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Floria Hilara to her deceased son, Fuscio.

EDCS-28701310.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilarae matri bene merenti
EDCS-28701310.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Hilara, a well-deserving mother.
EDCS-28701310.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Hilara by her child, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701311.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Cai filio Nin
EDCS-28701311.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Iulius Nin, son of Gaius.
EDCS-28701311.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulius Nin by his family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701313.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Valentino qui vixit annos XXXXV Iulia Patrona
EDCS-28701313.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Iulius Valentinus, who lived forty-five years. Iulia Patrona.
EDCS-28701313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Valentinus, aged 45, set up by Iulia Patrona, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701314.text_edition: Iulia Ide Iulio Primigenio coniugi et patrono bene merenti fecit
EDCS-28701314.translation: Iulia Ida made this for Iulius Primigenius, her well-deserving husband and patron.
EDCS-28701314.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulia Ida for her husband and patron, Iulius Primigenius, indicating a social relationship often seen between freedwomen and their former masters (patrons), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701315.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Licinius Messalinus bene merens a liberto merenti
EDCS-28701315.translation: To the Divine Manes, Lucius Licinius Messalinus, well-deserving, from his deserving freedman.
EDCS-28701315.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Licinius Messalinus, set up by his freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701318.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Petronio Urso Lucius Petronius Ursus filius Petronius Augustio filius patri pio posuerunt
EDCS-28701318.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Lucius Petronius Ursus. Lucius Petronius Ursus, his son, and Petronius Augustio, his son, placed this for their pious father.
EDCS-28701318.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Petronius Ursus by his two sons, Lucius Petronius Ursus and Petronius Augustio, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701320.text_edition: Dis Manibus patronae alumnae carae pientissimae Secundinae posuerunt annorum XVIII
EDCS-28701320.translation: To the Divine Manes, for the most dutiful patroness and dear foster-daughter Secundina, who was eighteen years old, they placed this.
EDCS-28701320.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundina, aged 18, dedicated by individuals who viewed her as both a patroness and a foster-daughter, suggesting complex social ties, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701321.text_edition: Dis Manibus Stenio Tacito Byrria sic Lupula coniugi sanctissimo
EDCS-28701321.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Stenius Tacitus. Byrria, thus Lupula, [placed this] for her most sacred husband.
EDCS-28701321.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Stenius Tacitus by his wife, Byrria Lupula, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701322.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Stercorio infelicissimo annorum plus minus annorum VIIII mater et pater posuerunt
EDCS-28701322.translation: To the Divine Manes, for the most unhappy Stercorius, who lived about nine years. His mother and father placed this.
EDCS-28701322.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Stercorius, a child who died at approximately nine years old, set up by his grieving parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701323.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Tigrini Tiburtinus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28701323.translation: To the Divine Manes, Tiburtinus placed this for Tigrinus, well-deserving.
EDCS-28701323.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Tigrinus by Tiburtinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28701324.text_edition: Dis Manibus Varro Luci defunctus annorum XXX Panico mater et Dasto matertera pientissimo posuerunt
EDCS-28701324.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Varro, son of Lucius, deceased at thirty years old. Panicus, his mother, and Dasto, his maternal aunt, placed this for their most devoted son.
EDCS-28701324.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely on a tombstone) dedicated by a mother and maternal aunt to their deceased son, Varro, who died at the age of thirty.

EDCS-28701325.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Veneriae Petroniae Sucundus sic coniugi suae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos plus minus XL bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28701325.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sucundus placed this for Veneria Petronia, his most unfortunate wife, who lived for about 40 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-28701325.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Sucundus for his wife, Veneria Petronia, who died around the age of 40.

EDCS-28701326.text_edition: Vipsanae Lupae Vivius Hyla filius bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28701326.translation: Vivius Hyla, son, placed this for Vipsana Lupa, well-deserving.
EDCS-28701326.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by a son, Vivius Hyla, to his well-deserving mother, Vipsana Lupa.

EDCS-28701327.text_edition: Dis bonis Manibus Ursinae conservae Silvinus conservus bene merenti posu annorum XX it
EDCS-28701327.translation: To the good Divine Spirits. Silvinus, fellow slave, placed this for Ursina, his fellow slave, well-deserving, who was 20 years old.
EDCS-28701327.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a 20-year-old female slave named Ursina, erected by her fellow slave, Silvinus.

EDCS-28701334.text_edition: Apollini sancto Antonius Castor IIvir coloniae Salonae ex praecepto dei posuit
EDCS-28701334.translation: To holy Apollo. Antonius Castor, duumvir of the colony of Salona, placed this by the instruction of the god.
EDCS-28701334.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo by Antonius Castor, a duumvir of Salona, indicating a public or official dedication made on divine command.

EDCS-28701335.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Quintus Valerius [placed this].
EDCS-28701335.summary: This is a religious dedication to the goddess Diana Augusta, made by Quintus Valerius.

EDCS-28701338.text_edition: Iovi --- Titus Rom vir perfectissimus MV M
EDCS-28701338.translation: To Jupiter [---]. Titus Rom[anus], most perfect man, [made this] M.V. M.
EDCS-28701338.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Jupiter by a 'vir perfectissimus' named Titus Romanus, likely indicating a votive offering.

EDCS-28701339.text_edition: Iovi Aeterno Flavius Apoleius sic Verocia Marcella et filia eorum et Appuleia Marcella votum libentes solverunt
EDCS-28701339.translation: To Eternal Jupiter. Flavius Apoleius, Verocia Marcella, and their daughter, and Appuleia Marcella willingly and joyfully fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-28701339.summary: This is a votive inscription where a family group (Flavius Apoleius, Verocia Marcella, their daughter, and Appuleia Marcella) records the joyful fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Aeternus.

EDCS-28701340.text_edition: Titus Pinarius Eros Iovi Caelesti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701340.translation: Titus Pinarius Eros willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Caelestis.
EDCS-28701340.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Pinarius Eros, recording the willing and deserved fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Caelestis (Celestial Jupiter).

EDCS-28701343.text_edition: V V B sacrum Marcus Ulpius Attalus collector cognationis ex pecunia multaticia
EDCS-28701343.translation: Sacred to V.V.B. Marcus Ulpius Attalus, collector of the corporation, from money collected as fines.
EDCS-28701343.summary: This is a dedication made by Marcus Ulpius Attalus, an official of a 'cognatio' (likely a collegium or association), using funds derived from fines, to an unknown deity or entity abbreviated as V.V.B.

EDCS-28701345.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae Lucius Cornelius Apalaustus pro salute Marci Vivi Cresti amici karissimi ex voto posuit
EDCS-28701345.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Lucius Cornelius Apalaustus, for the health of Marcus Vivius Crestus, his dearest friend, placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28701345.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Cornelius Apalaustus to the god Mithras, made in fulfillment of a vow for the health of his friend Marcus Vivius Crestus, likely on a cult monument like an altar or shrine.

EDCS-28701347.text_edition: Nymphis Marcus Thorius Valens votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701347.translation: To the Nymphs, Marcus Thorius Valens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28701347.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Thorius Valens to the Nymphs, made in willing and deserving fulfillment of a vow, likely on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-28701348.translation: To Priapus, Sempronia Ursa placed an altar.
EDCS-28701348.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Priapus by Sempronia Ursa.

EDCS-28701349.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum voto suscepto pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Nervae Traiani optimi Augusti Germanici Dacici nostri Trophimus servus Amandianus dispensator a solo fecit et aquam induxit loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-28701349.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. A vow was undertaken for the health of our Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus. Trophimus, an Amandian slave, the dispenser, made this from the ground up and brought in water, the location having been granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28701349.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Trophimus, a slave and dispenser, for the health of Emperor Trajan, detailing the construction of a monument and water supply on land granted by local officials, likely dating to Trajan's reign (98-117 CE).

EDCS-28701350.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, C. Sabinus [placed this].
EDCS-28701350.summary: This inscription records a simple dedication to Silvanus Augustus by C. Sabinus, likely on an altar or shrine.

EDCS-28701351.text_edition: Soli deo Sextus Cornelius Antiochus stellam et fructiferam ex visu libens posuit
EDCS-28701351.translation: To the Sun god, Sextus Cornelius Antiochus willingly placed a star and a fruit-bearing object, by vision.
EDCS-28701351.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Sextus Cornelius Antiochus to the Sun god, involving a star and a fruit-bearing object, placed in response to a vision, likely on a cult monument.

EDCS-28701352.text_edition: miles legionis VII Veneri donum ob magisterium cognationis Clodiorum CVI
EDCS-28701352.translation: A soldier of the Seventh Legion [gave this] as a gift to Venus for the magistracy of 106 members of the Clodius family.
EDCS-28701352.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a gift to Venus by a soldier of the Seventh Legion, likely in honor of a magistracy held by 106 members of the Clodius family, possibly on a statue or altar.

EDCS-28701353.text_edition: Veneri Bacchae sacrum Aurelia Primula ex voto posuit
EDCS-28701353.translation: Sacred to Venus Baccha, Aurelia Primula placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28701353.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelia Primula to Venus Baccha, made in fulfillment of a vow, likely on an altar or statue.

EDCS-28701356.translation: C. Aquilinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28701356.summary: This inscription records that C. Aquilinus willingly fulfilled a vow, likely on an altar or votive tablet dedicated to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-28701357.text_edition: Clodia Maxima votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701357.translation: Clodia Maxima willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28701357.summary: This inscription records that Clodia Maxima willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely on an altar or votive offering to an unspecified deity.

EDCS-28701358.text_edition: Publius Cloelius miles cohortis Campanae custos Traguri votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701358.translation: Publius Cloelius, a soldier of the Campanian cohort, guard of Trogir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28701358.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Cloelius, a soldier and guard, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28701359.translation: Iucunda willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28701359.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Iucunda in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28701360.text_edition: Paxaea Elpis cum suisque filiabus hoc monumentum ex voto posuit
EDCS-28701360.translation: Paxaea Elpis, with her daughters, erected this monument according to a vow.
EDCS-28701360.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Paxaea Elpis and her daughters, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28701361.text_edition: Caius Seius Nym  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701361.translation: Gaius Seius Nym willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28701361.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Seius Nym in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28701365.text_edition: ]I  [temporibus ] Philippianis pro vota nnalia et vicennalia res publica Salonitana
EDCS-28701365.translation: [In the times] of the Philips, for the decennial and vicennial vows, the Republic of Salona [---].
EDCS-28701365.summary: This inscription records an official dedication by the Republic of Salona related to decennial and vicennial vows during the reign of the Philips, likely around the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-28701366.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio ValerioC#Valerio#VALERIC Diocletiano Pio Felici Augusto res publica Salonitanorum
EDCS-28701366.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, the Republic of the Salonitans [dedicates this].
EDCS-28701366.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Republic of Salona to Emperor Diocletian, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-28701367.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli honorarii, tituli sacri
EDCS-28701367.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constantio victoriosissimo semper Augusto Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius vir clarissimus praeses provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-28701367.translation: To our Lord Flavius Constantius, most victorious, forever Augustus, Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divinity and majesty [dedicates this].
EDCS-28701367.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, governor of Dalmatia, to an emperor named Flavius Constantius, indicating a date in the 4th century AD.

EDCS-28701368.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, with tribunician power [---].
EDCS-28701368.summary: This inscription records a dedication to an unnamed Roman Emperor, with reference to his tribunician power, a common dating formula for imperial inscriptions.

EDCS-28701369.text_edition: Antonio Tauro ex duabRus#duabus#DUARUS centenariis ducenario post facto qui vixit annis LV Aelia Saturnina clarissima femina marito benignissimo
EDCS-28701369.translation: To Antonius Taurus, from two centenarii, later a ducenarius, who lived for 55 years, Aelia Saturnina, a most distinguished woman, [made this] for her most kind husband.
EDCS-28701369.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Aelia Saturnina for her husband, Antonius Taurus, who held military or administrative ranks and lived to age 55.

EDCS-28701370.text_edition: Caeciliae Logianae eminentissimi viri filiae et Tullio Callipiano equiti Romano Tullia Logiana filia parentibus suis fecit in fronte pedes XXVI in agro pedes XX
EDCS-28701370.translation: Tullia Logiana made this for her parents, Caecilia Logiana, daughter of a most eminent man, and Tullius Callipianus, a Roman knight; in front, 26 feet; in depth, 20 feet.
EDCS-28701370.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Tullia Logiana for her parents, Caecilia Logiana and Tullius Callipianus, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-28701371.text_edition: Caio Pontilio Fregelliano consuli patrono decreto decurionum publice
EDCS-28701371.translation: To Gaius Pontilius Fregellianus, consul, patron, by decree of the decurions, at public expense.
EDCS-28701371.summary: This inscription records a dedication made at public expense to Gaius Pontilius Fregellianus, a consul and patron, by order of the local council (decurions).

EDCS-28701375.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication to Jupiter, noting Certus as the son of the dedicator.

EDCS-28701376.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Caius Beriu Aurelius Maximus venator immunis cohortis V Mil numini eius cum suis posuit donum dedit libens merito
EDCS-28701376.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, Gaius Berius Aurelius Maximus, an exempt hunter of the Vth cohort, placed this gift for his divinity with his family, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28701376.summary: This inscription records a dedicatory gift made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator by Gaius Berius Aurelius Maximus, an exempt hunter from the 5th cohort, on behalf of himself and his family.

EDCS-28701377.text_edition: Laribus Augustis familia Publi Ulpi Publi Valentini
EDCS-28701377.translation: To the Augustan Lares, the household of Publius Ulpius Valentinus.
EDCS-28701377.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Augustan Lares by the household of Publius Ulpius Valentinus.

EDCS-28701379.text_edition: Matri Magnae cognationis Caius Turranius Cronius IIIIIIvir Augustalis voto suscepto aedem et aram de sua pecunia fecit et expolivit idemque dedicavit
EDCS-28701379.translation: To the Great Mother of the cognation, Gaius Turranius Cronius, a six-times Augustalis, having undertaken a vow, built and adorned this temple and altar from his own money, and likewise dedicated it.
EDCS-28701379.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and altar to the Great Mother by Gaius Turranius Cronius, a sexvir Augustalis, who financed and dedicated the monument himself in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28701380.text_edition: Prima Nimphis sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-28701380.translation: Prima willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-28701380.summary: This inscription records Prima's fulfillment of a vow to the Nymphs.

EDCS-28701381.summary: This inscription records Mummia Glycinna's fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-28701382.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning a vow by Gaius V. M.

EDCS-28701385.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco sic Aurelio Severo Antonino Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae Magno
EDCS-28701385.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, father of his country, Great.
EDCS-28701385.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus, also known as Caracalla, likely dating from his reign (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-28701386.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae proconsuli Aurelius Marcianus vir perfectissimus praeses provinciae Delmatiae sic devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-28701386.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the second time, Consul, Father of his Country, Proconsul; Aurelius Marcianus, vir perfectissimus, governor of the province of Dalmatia, thus devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-28701386.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Probus, listing his imperial titles and dating to 277 AD based on his second tribunician power, made by Aurelius Marcianus, a 'vir perfectissimus' and governor of Dalmatia.

EDCS-28701387.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Sorianus, a freedman.

EDCS-28701389.translation: Symphorus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28701389.summary: This inscription records Symphorus fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity, as a votive offering.

EDCS-28800844.text_edition: Flavio Iuliano ex protectore et ex praepositis qui vixit annis LI et Aureliae Sapriciae uxori obseqeuntissimae quae vixit annis XXXV
EDCS-28800844.translation: To Flavius Iulianus, former protector and former chief, who lived for 51 years, and to Aurelia Sapricia, his most devoted wife, who lived for 35 years.
EDCS-28800844.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Flavius Iulianus, a veteran with military administrative titles, and his devoted wife Aurelia Sapricia, likely erected by their family or associates.

EDCS-28800846.text_edition: Caius Iulius Dolens veteranus legionis X geminae ex beneficiario consularis et Caelia Bonosa vivi sibi posuerunt
EDCS-28800846.translation: Gaius Iulius Dolens, veteran of the Tenth Legion Gemina, formerly a beneficiarius consularis, and Caelia Bonosa, while alive, set this up for themselves.
EDCS-28800846.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Iulius Dolens, a veteran of Legio X Gemina with a specific military rank, and his partner Caelia Bonosa, for themselves while still living.

EDCS-28800847.text_edition: Publi fili-- Malleol?   et Placentina?  XXI
EDCS-28800847.translation: Sons of Publius, Malleol[us?] and Placentina? [lived?] 21 years.
EDCS-28800847.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, possibly for two individuals, Malleolus and Placentina, who were sons of Publius, with one living to age 21.

EDCS-28800848.text_edition: Caius Vatinius Sexti filius Capito veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis domo Arretio vivus fecit sibi et Vatiniae Feliculae libertae
