EDCS-28800848.translation: Gaius Vatinius Capito, son of Sextus, veteran of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from Arretium, while alive made this for himself and for Vatinia Felicula, his freedwoman.
EDCS-28800848.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Gaius Vatinius Capito, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis from Arretium, for himself and his freedwoman, Vatinia Felicula, while he was still alive.

EDCS-28800849.text_edition: filius veteranus legionis  domo Ateste testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-28800849.translation: [---] son, veteran of the legion, from Ateste, ordered this to be made by will.
EDCS-28800849.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely for a veteran from Ateste, which was ordered to be erected posthumously according to his will.

EDCS-28800855.text_edition: egiae Longinae rius beneficiarius onianus DVL et sibi  posuit
EDCS-28800855.translation: [---]egia Longina. [---]rius, beneficiarius. [---]onianus DVL and set this up for himself.
EDCS-28800855.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a woman named Longina, a beneficiarius, and an individual named Onianus who set up the monument for himself, indicating a possible mid-imperial date for the title beneficiarius.

EDCS-28800856.text_edition: II CHI corniculario consularis marito incomparabili Lucilia na si quis in hoc aliut sic corpus
EDCS-28800856.translation: To the incomparable husband, [---] Cornelius, cornicularius consularis. Luciliana [set this up]. If anyone places another body in this [tomb].
EDCS-28800856.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Luciliana to her 'incomparable' husband, a cornicularius consularis, with a clause forbidding further burials in the tomb.

EDCS-28800857.text_edition: Dis Manibus T Nepos miles? cohortis VIII [voluntariorum &
EDCS-28800857.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To T. Nepos, soldier? of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers [---].
EDCS-28800857.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to T. Nepos, a soldier in the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, indicating a military burial.

EDCS-28800858.text_edition: Octaviae Carae dominae et tecusae rarissimae summae sanctimoniae et benignitatis feminae coniugi Saloni Sabiniani veterani ex corniculario consularis legionis I adiutricis signo Scammati eqO(uitis)#eq(uitis)#EO Romani cum quo concorditer vixit nnos XXX ob merita Ulpius Asclepius confraterfrater |(Christo posuit
EDCS-28800858.translation: To Octavia Cara, mistress and unique companion, a woman of the highest sanctity and kindness, wife of Salonius Sabinianus, a veteran, formerly a cornicularius (aide) of the consular legate of Legion I Adiutrix, with the standard of Scammatus, a Roman knight, with whom he lived harmoniously for 30 years. For her merits, Ulpius Asclepius, her confrater (brother in Christ), placed this.
EDCS-28800858.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ulpius Asclepius to Octavia Cara, wife of a Roman veteran, praising her virtues and their long harmonious marriage, likely dating from the Christian era due to the "frater in Christo" phrasing.

EDCS-28800859.text_edition: Quintiae C stolatae feminae Quinti quondam Germani primipilaris filiae quae ddidit nnum XXX Flavius Valens beneficiarius consularis nniae superioris coniugi karissimae
EDCS-28800859.translation: To Quintia, a matron, daughter of Quintus Germanus, formerly a primipilaris (chief centurion), who lived for 30 years. Flavius Valens, a beneficiarius consularis (aide to a governor) of the superior province, to his dearest wife.
EDCS-28800859.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavius Valens, a provincial official, for his beloved wife Quintia, noting her lineage and age.

EDCS-28800860.text_edition: Dis Manibus Calpurniae Nymphae Quintus Servilius Statianus |(centurio) cohortis I Belgarum coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-28800860.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Servilius Statianus, centurion of Cohort I Belgarum, to his well-deserving wife Calpurnia Nympha.
EDCS-28800860.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Quintus Servilius Statianus, a centurion, dedicated to his deserving wife Calpurnia Nympha.

EDCS-28800861.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Sornius C miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum Calpurnia Pro mater filio ponendum curavit
EDCS-28800861.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Sornius, soldier of Cohort VIII of Volunteers. Calpurnia Pro, his mother, had this placed for her son.
EDCS-28800861.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Sornius, a soldier, placed by his mother Calpurnia Pro.

EDCS-28800862.text_edition: Torentio signifero qui vixit annos XXV piissimo Catulus spathario suo posuit
EDCS-28800862.translation: To Torentius, the standard-bearer, who lived for 25 years, a most dutiful man. Catulus placed this for his spatharius (sword-bearer or guard).
EDCS-28800862.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Catulus for his spatharius Torentius, a standard-bearer who died young, praising his piety.

EDCS-28800863.text_edition: Lucio Trebonio Valenti militi legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis et Lucio Trebonio Constanti filio et Treboniae Tertullae sorori Lucius Trebonius Successus libertus patronis suis bene merentibus vivus fecit sibi et suis libertis libertabusque in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XII
EDCS-28800863.translation: To Lucius Trebonius Valens, soldier of Legion VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, and to Lucius Trebonius Constans, his son, and to Trebonia Tertulla, his sister. Lucius Trebonius Successus, freedman, made this while living for his well-deserving patrons, for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen. The plot measures 20 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-28800863.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Trebonius Successus, a freedman, while still living, for his patrons (a soldier, his son, and sister), and also for himself and his own freedpersons, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-28800864.text_edition: Caius Valerius  filius Azinas Proculus eques cohortis III Alpinorum vexillarius equitum cohortis I Belgarum decurio equitum cohortis eiusdem hunc sic sepulcrum vivos sic paravi mihi et Apuleiae Sabinae uxori meae libertis libertabusque hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28800864.translation: Gaius Valerius, son of Azinas, Proculus, cavalryman of Cohort III Alpinorum, vexillarius (standard-bearer) of the cavalry of Cohort I Belgarum, decurion (commander) of the cavalry of the same cohort, prepared this tomb while living for myself and for Apuleia Sabina, my wife, and for my freedmen and freedwomen. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28800864.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Gaius Valerius Proculus, a cavalry officer with a distinguished career, for himself, his wife, and his freedpersons, explicitly stating that it is not intended for his heirs.

EDCS-28800867.text_edition: tius Quinti filius Galeria?   veteranus legionis  sibi et suis
EDCS-28800867.translation: [---]tius, son of Quintus, of the Galerian tribe, a veteran of Legion [---], for himself and his family.
EDCS-28800867.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for a veteran of an unnamed legion, set up by himself for his family.

EDCS-28800875.text_edition: enus  Augustalis vivus fecit --- sibi? etArbusculae coniugi et Caio Iulio
EDCS-28800875.translation: [---]enus Augustalis made this while living [---] for himself and for Arbuscula, his wife, and for Gaius Julius [---].
EDCS-28800875.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely for a monument, prepared by an Augustalis (a member of the college of Augustales) for himself, his wife Arbuscula, and another individual named Gaius Julius.

EDCS-28800876.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Germanus Barlaha sacerdos Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni vivus sibi posuit et Syrae coniugi
EDCS-28800876.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Germanus Barlaha, priest of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, placed this while living for himself and for Syra, his wife.
EDCS-28800876.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aurelius Germanus Barlaha, a priest of Jupiter Dolichenus, for himself and his wife Syra, while he was still alive.

EDCS-28800877.text_edition: Caius Caesius Cai libertus Amaranthus IIIIIIvir Augustalis vivus fecit sibi et Clodiae Publi libertae Blandae uxori et Caesiae Lepidillae defunctae nnum trium et Caio Caesio Cai filio Tromentina Vindici aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo et Corneliae Publi filiae Clementillae uxori
EDCS-28800877.translation: Gaius Caesius Amaranthus, freedman of Gaius, a sevir Augustalis, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Clodia Blanda, freedwoman of Publius, his wife, and for Caesia Lepidilla, deceased at three years old, and for Gaius Caesius Vindex, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile, duumvir with judicial power, and for Cornelia Clementilla, daughter of Publius, his wife.
EDCS-28800877.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by a sevir Augustalis for himself, his wife, his deceased daughter, his son who was a local magistrate, and his daughter-in-law.

EDCS-28800878.text_edition: Decimo Campanio Publi filio Tromentina Varo aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo IIIIviro iure dicundo quinquennali auguri flamini praefecto fabrum publice
EDCS-28800878.translation: To Decimus Campanius Varus, son of Publius, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile, duumvir with judicial power, quinquennial duumvir with judicial power, augur, flamen, prefect of craftsmen, by public decree.
EDCS-28800878.summary: This inscription is a public dedication honoring Decimus Campanius Varus, listing his numerous civic and religious offices, including aedile, duumvir, augur, and flamen.

EDCS-28800879.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Canius Placidus Augustalis vivus fecit sibi et suis posterisque eorum in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX
EDCS-28800879.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Canius Placidus, an Augustalis, made this in his lifetime for himself and his family and their descendants. In front, 20 feet; in depth, 30 feet.
EDCS-28800879.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by Titus Canius Placidus, an Augustalis, in his lifetime for himself, his family, and their descendants, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-28800880.text_edition: Sexto? Fulvio Sexti filio Tromentina Alfiano filio decurioni Sexto? Fulvio Pietati filio Sexto? Fulvio Pietati patri ae Proculae matri iae Syrelae coniugi Sextus? Fulvius Alfianus suis
EDCS-28800880.translation: To Sextus Fulvius Alfianus, son of Sextus, of the Tromentina tribe, a decurion, and to Sextus Fulvius Pietas, his son, and to Sextus Fulvius Pietas, his father, and to Procula, his mother, and to Syrela, his wife. Sextus Fulvius Alfianus (made this) for his own.
EDCS-28800880.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sextus Fulvius Alfianus for himself and his family members, including his father, mother, son, and wife.

EDCS-28800882.text_edition: Iulia Agathe MERIS Nassio Iuliano defuncto  VIviro Augustali
EDCS-28800882.translation: Iulia Agathe MERIS (set this up) for Nassius Iulianus, deceased, a sevir Augustalis.
EDCS-28800882.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Iulia Agathe for Nassius Iulianus, who was a sevir Augustalis.

EDCS-28800883.summary: This fragment of an inscription mentions Malleolus, holding the magistracy of aedile.

EDCS-28800884.text_edition: ntius Mer IIIIIIvir Augustalis se vivo fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum in fronte pedes XXXV
EDCS-28800884.translation: ntius Mer, a sevir Augustalis, made this in his lifetime for himself and his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants. In front, 35 feet.
EDCS-28800884.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a tomb built by a sevir Augustalis, whose name ends in '-ntius Mer', in his lifetime for himself, his freedmen and freedwomen, and their descendants, specifying the front dimension of the plot.

EDCS-28800885.text_edition: Ti(berius) Iulius Maximus Cai Iuli Thiridatis filius decurio alae Pharthorum annorum XXVI domo Roma hic situs est Sextus Coelius NAVIB
EDCS-28800885.translation: Tiberius Iulius Maximus, son of Gaius Iulius Thiridatis, decurion of the Parthian cavalry wing, aged 26, from Rome, lies here. Sextus Coelius NAVIB.
EDCS-28800885.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Iulius Maximus, a Roman cavalry officer from Rome, who died at age 26, set up by Sextus Coelius.

EDCS-28800887.text_edition: Lucio Veturioo equiti cohortis octavae praetoriae nnorum XVIII Aelia Maximina mater
EDCS-28800887.translation: To Lucius Veturius, a knight of the eighth praetorian cohort, aged 18. Aelia Maximina, his mother (made this).
EDCS-28800887.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Maximina to her son, Lucius Veturius, a praetorian cavalryman who died at 18 years old.

EDCS-28900124.text_edition: IO OC NO  coloniae aedili IIviro patrono coloniae  sacerdoti designato
EDCS-28900124.translation: IO OC NO of the colony, aedile, duumvir, patron of the colony, designated priest.
EDCS-28900124.summary: This fragment records the various civic and religious offices held by an individual, including aedile, duumvir, patron of the colony, and designated priest, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28900125.text_edition: Orbia Sexti filia Paulla titulum fieri iussit sibi et Sexto Orbio Paullo IIIIIIviro patri Cordiae Afae matri Sexto Orbio Praeconino pontifici fratri Publio Grattio Campano filio
EDCS-28900125.translation: Orbia Paulla, daughter of Sextus, ordered this monument to be made for herself and for Sextus Orbius Paullus, her father, a sevir Augustalis; for Cordia Afa, her mother; for Sextus Orbius Praeconinus, her brother, a pontifex; and for Publius Grattius Campanus, her son.
EDCS-28900125.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Orbia Paulla for herself and several family members, including her father, mother, brother, and son, likely dating to the imperial period given the mention of a sevir Augustalis and pontifex.

EDCS-28900126.text_edition: isidio? ---icto quaestori  defuncto annorum Papinia Filumena uxor cum filiis suis marito pientissimo posuit
EDCS-28900126.translation: Papinia Filumena, his wife, with her children, placed this for her most devoted husband, [---] Isidio? [---]icto, a quaestor, who died at [---] years old.
EDCS-28900126.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Papinia Filumena and her children to her deceased husband, a quaestor, highlighting his devotion.

EDCS-28900127.text_edition: ? Petronio Auli filio Tromentina Postumo aedili  cunda mater fecit
EDCS-28900127.translation: [---] Petronius Postumus, son of Aulus, of the tribe Tromentina, an aedile, his mother [---]cunda made this.
EDCS-28900127.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument made by a mother for her son, Petronius Postumus, who held the public office of aedile.

EDCS-28900128.text_edition: Quintilium Mescenium  menses III dies XV decessit Salonis  astrosum Marcus Quintilius us pater infelix H
EDCS-28900128.translation: Quintilius Mescenius died at Salona, having lived 3 months, 15 days. Marcus Quintilius, his unhappy father, [made this].
EDCS-28900128.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from a grieving father, Marcus Quintilius, for his young son, Quintilius Mescenius, who died in Salona.

EDCS-28900130.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely from a funerary monument, identifying an aedile and his wife.

EDCS-28900131.text_edition: Caius Saufeius Moderatus Augustalis testamento fieri iussit sibi et Luciliae Rhodiae libertis libertabusque suis Caius Saufeius Thalamus et Caius Saufeius Moderatianus heredes faciundum curaverunt in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes LXV
EDCS-28900131.translation: Gaius Saufeius Moderatus, an Augustalis, ordered in his will that this be made for himself and for Lucilia Rhodia, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. Gaius Saufeius Thalamus and Gaius Saufeius Moderatianus, his heirs, saw to its construction. In front, 30 feet; in depth, 65 feet.
EDCS-28900131.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Saufeius Moderatus, an Augustalis, through his will for himself, his wife, and his freedpeople, with his heirs overseeing the work and specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-28900132.text_edition: $ cum co]mpare suo annis XXX - Silicius Tertius IIIIIIvir Augustalis  coloniae Salonitanorum
EDCS-28900132.translation: [---] with his companion for 30 years. [---] Silicius Tertius, a sevir Augustalis of the colony of Salonitani.
EDCS-28900132.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely from a funerary monument, mentioning a sevir Augustalis of Salona, Silicius Tertius, and possibly commemorating a long-standing partnership.

EDCS-28900134.text_edition: Caius Vibius Leo IIIIIIvir vivus fecit sibi et Caeliae Tertiae coniugi et Antestiae Messiae libertis libertabusque suis  hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-28900134.translation: Gaius Vibius Leo, a sevir Augustalis, made this while living for himself and for Caelia Tertia, his wife, and for Antestia Messia, his freedwoman, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-28900134.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Vibius Leo, a sevir Augustalis, during his lifetime for himself, his wife, and his freedpeople, explicitly stating it is not for his heirs.

EDCS-28900144.text_edition: F  Q F  ia Publi filia F---  A P  Aedem et signa de sua pecunia fecerunt voto suscepto
EDCS-28900144.translation: F[---] Q[---] F[---]ia, daughter of Publius, F[---] A[---] P[---]. They made the temple and statues from their own money, having undertaken a vow.
EDCS-28900144.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and statues, funded by individuals (likely a family, including a woman named F[---]ia) from their own money, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28900145.text_edition: Aebu L erius  ACCEP magister collegii fabrum D PIN castra SLCANI et S P V AFESIEI
EDCS-28900145.translation: Aebulius [---]orius, master of the guild of craftsmen [---] ACCEP [---] D[---] PIN [---] the camps [---] SLCANI and S P V AFESIEI.
EDCS-28900145.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aebulius [---]orius, a master of a guild of craftsmen, and several other obscure references, possibly related to military camps or local administration.

EDCS-28900146.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aeliae Soterae obstetrici defunctae annorum XXXV Aelius Antonianus Themistocles libertae bene merenti
EDCS-28900146.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aelia Sotera, midwife, who died at 35 years of age. Aelius Antonianus Themistocles placed this for his well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-28900146.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aelius Antonianus Themistocles for his freedwoman, Aelia Sotera, a midwife, who died at a relatively young age.

EDCS-28900147.text_edition: Agathopus rcae Primigeni  annorum duorum S Ravennae
EDCS-28900147.translation: Agathopus for Primigenius, two years old, in Ravenna.
EDCS-28900147.summary: This short inscription appears to be a funerary dedication from Agathopus to a two-year-old named Primigenius, located in Ravenna.

EDCS-28900151.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Crinito secutori natione Afer annorum XX pugnarum IIL#II#IL @(obito) Afidia posuit de sua sibi frugalitate bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28900151.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Crinitus, a secutor of African origin, 20 years old, with 52 victories, who died. Afidia placed this from her own resources for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28900151.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Afidia to Crinitus, a secutor (a type of gladiator) of African origin, noting his young age, numerous victories, and manner of death.

EDCS-28900152.translation: To Epictesis, the freedman of Fortunatus Augustalis.
EDCS-28900152.summary: This inscription identifies Epictesis as a freedman associated with Fortunatus Augustalis, likely a dedicatory or ownership mark.

EDCS-28900155.text_edition: Maximiano qCui#qui#CUI et Aureo secutori nnorum XXII pugnarum V deceptus a latroniEbuOs#latronibus#LATRONEBOS frater fratri carissimo  Gemina coniux desideratissimo
EDCS-28900155.translation: To Maximianus and Aureus, a secutor, 22 years old, with 5 victories, ambushed by robbers. His brother placed this for his dearest brother. Gemina, his wife, placed this for her most missed husband.
EDCS-28900155.summary: This is a funerary inscription jointly dedicated by a brother and wife (Gemina) to Maximianus, also known as Aureus, a secutor gladiator who was killed by robbers.

EDCS-28900156.summary: This inscription records that Miscenius Ampliatus is responsible for creating something for the city of Salona, possibly a monument or a public work.

EDCS-28900157.text_edition: Onesimo Marci Iuli Macrini dispensatori Successus vicarius
EDCS-28900157.translation: To Onesimus, the dispenser of Marcus Iulius Macrinus. Successus, his deputy.
EDCS-28900157.summary: This inscription acknowledges Onesimus as the dispenser for Marcus Iulius Macrinus, with Successus serving as his deputy, indicating an administrative relationship.

EDCS-28900159.text_edition: Dis Manibus Secundo servo bono defuncto nnorum XX bene merenti posuit
EDCS-28900159.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Secundus, a good slave who died at 20 years of age. Placed for the well-deserving.
EDCS-28900159.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Secundus, a slave who died young, acknowledging him as 'good' and 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-28900161.text_edition: Sossius hic situs est Successus iure peritus Ravvennae genitus Salonis funere raptus libera quemN#quem#QUEN ratio semper deduxit ad aequum
EDCS-28900161.translation: Sossius lies here. Successus, skilled in law, born in Ravenna, snatched by death in Salona, whom free reason always led to justice.
EDCS-28900161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sossius, also known as Successus, a jurist born in Ravenna who died in Salona, praised for his adherence to justice.

EDCS-29000267.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Terentio Mercuriali annorum XXXI vexxillario collegii fabrum Terentius Mercurius pater et patronus filio infelicissimo dolens posuit
EDCS-29000267.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Titus Terentius Mercurialis, 31 years old, vexillarius of the college of craftsmen. Terentius Mercurius, his father and patron, sadly placed this for his most unfortunate son.
EDCS-29000267.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Terentius Mercurius, acting as both father and patron, to his son, Titus Terentius Mercurialis, a standard-bearer of a craftsmen's guild, who died at 31.

EDCS-29000268.text_edition: $ ---]rio natione  qui vixit annos plus minus XXVI S intravi simul et exxivi eptus OCS
EDCS-29000268.translation: [---]rio by nation, who lived about 26 years. I entered and exited at the same time, taken OCS.
EDCS-29000268.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual named [---]rius, indicating their nationality and a short lifespan, possibly from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-29000269.text_edition: Quinto Turranio Mercurio liberto Turrani Successi toreuticensis ab urbe qui vixit nnos  filio bene merenti
EDCS-29000269.translation: To Quintus Turranius Mercurius, freedman of Turranius Successus, a silversmith from the city, who lived for [---] years, to a well-deserving son.
EDCS-29000269.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an unknown person to Quintus Turranius Mercurius, a freedman and silversmith, likely from Rome, and recognized as a well-deserving son.

EDCS-29000280.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a possible Gaius and other incomplete names, the full purpose of which cannot be determined.

EDCS-29000282.text_edition: matri annorum Acubio Maximo annorum XVIII et Acubio Valentino fratri Acubius Sabidianus suis  posuit
EDCS-29000282.translation: Acubius Sabidianus placed this for his mother, aged [---] years, for Acubius Maximus, aged 18 years, and for Acubius Valentinus, his brother.
EDCS-29000282.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Acubius Sabidianus for his mother, his 18-year-old brother Acubius Maximus, and his other brother Acubius Valentinus.

EDCS-29000283.text_edition: Caio Aebutio Secundino defuncto nnorum V mensium III Caius Aebutius Crescens et Octavia Secundina parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-29000283.translation: To Gaius Aebutius Secundinus, deceased at 5 years and 3 months, Gaius Aebutius Crescens and Octavia Secundina, his most unhappy parents, [set this up].
EDCS-29000283.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Gaius Aebutius Crescens and Octavia Secundina for their young son, Gaius Aebutius Secundinus, who died at the age of five years and three months.

EDCS-29000284.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, possibly for a woman named Aetia, daughter of Lucius, but its full context is lost.

EDCS-29000285.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, mentioning parts of two names, which does not allow for a clear determination of its purpose.

EDCS-29000286.text_edition: Aeliae Proculae defunctae annorum XXX posuit Alasinius FeIrox#Ferox#FIRX coniugi incomparabili
EDCS-29000286.translation: To Aelia Procula, deceased at 30 years old, Alasinius Ferox placed this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-29000286.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Alasinius Ferox to his incomparable wife, Aelia Procula, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-29000287.text_edition: Aeliae I L defunctae annorum  Anicetus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29000287.translation: To Aelia I. L., deceased at [---] years old, Anicetus [placed this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29000287.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Anicetus for his well-deserving wife, Aelia I. L., whose age at death is not fully preserved.

EDCS-29000288.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Primo us  tia
EDCS-29000288.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, to Publius Aelius Primus, [---]
EDCS-29000288.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manibus for Publius Aelius Primus, the remainder of which is lost.

EDCS-29000291.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelia  ARTO nnorum V Iuli--
EDCS-29000291.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Arto, aged 5 years, Iuli[---].
EDCS-29000291.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelia Arto, who died at the age of five, with the name of the dedicator or further details being incomplete.

EDCS-29000292.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29000292.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aeliae Alexandriae infelicissimae virgini quae vixit nnos plus minus XV parentes infelicissimi infelicissimae filiae memoriam
EDCS-29000292.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelia Alexandria, a most unhappy maiden, who lived for about 15 years. Her most unhappy parents placed this memorial for their most unhappy daughter.
EDCS-29000292.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by grieving parents to the memory of their daughter, Aelia Alexandria, who died at approximately 15 years of age.

EDCS-29000293.text_edition: mno annorum I mensium II dierum  Aelia Fortunata filio pientissimo IFEIAO
EDCS-29000293.translation: In her first year, two months, [and an unstated number of] days. Aelia Fortunata [set this up] for her most dutiful son. IFEIAO.
EDCS-29000293.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelia Fortunata for her beloved son, who died as an infant in his first year of life.

EDCS-29000294.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument, mentioning Aeronia Primigenia and Atticus, with its full context missing.

EDCS-29000295.text_edition: Dis Manibus Agathini defuncti annorum XVI mater posuit
EDCS-29000295.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Agathinus, who died at the age of 16 years. His mother placed this.
EDCS-29000295.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother for her son, Agathinus, who passed away at the age of 16.

EDCS-29000296.text_edition: Dis Manibus Agria Primitiva fratri et filio Iu
EDCS-29000296.translation: To the Divine Manes. Agria Primitiva [placed this] for her brother and son, Iu[---].
EDCS-29000296.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Agria Primitiva to her brother and son, whose full name or details are incomplete.

EDCS-29000298.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly indicating a freedman of Gaius, with insufficient text to determine its purpose or full meaning.

EDCS-29000302.translation: Amullina placed this for Servilius Primitivus.
EDCS-29000302.summary: This is an inscription, likely funerary, dedicated by Amullina to Servilius Primitivus.

EDCS-29000306.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonio Filocalo patrono et Antoniae Glycerae sorori Antonia Fingis  bene merenti
EDCS-29000306.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Antonius Philocalus, her patron, and to Antonia Glycera, her sister. Antonia Fingis [placed this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29000306.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonia Fingis to her patron, Antonius Philocalus, and her sister, Antonia Glycera.

EDCS-29000307.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antoniae Hedone maritus uxsori sic infelicissimae defunctae annorum XVIIII
EDCS-29000307.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Antonia Hedone, his most unhappy wife, who died at the age of 19 years. Her husband [placed this].
EDCS-29000307.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a husband for his wife, Antonia Hedone, who died at the young age of 19.

EDCS-29000308.text_edition: Antoniae Sabinae <quae=CVI>#<quae>#CVI et MegNHmae defunctae annorum XVIIII Antonius Sabinianus et Octavia Eyagria parentes filiae dulcissimae posuerunt et sibi
EDCS-29000308.translation: To Antonia Sabina, who passed away at 19 years old, Antonius Sabinianus and Octavia Eyagria, her parents, placed this for their sweetest daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-29000308.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents, Antonius Sabinianus and Octavia Eyagria, for their deceased daughter, Antonia Sabina, and for themselves, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000309.text_edition: MAP me A libert-- V sibi et marito
EDCS-29000309.translation: MAP, a freedwoman, placed this for herself and her husband.
EDCS-29000309.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a freedwoman named MAP for herself and her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000310.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Apollonius, son of Aulus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000312.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Apro, likely a funerary monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000314.text_edition: Dis Manibus Apuleiae Iucundae Lucius Petilius Luci libertus Privatus sorori bene merenti
EDCS-29000314.translation: To the Divine Manes of Apuleia Iucunda. Lucius Petilius Privatus, freedman of Lucius, [placed this] for his well-deserving sister.
EDCS-29000314.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Apuleia Iucunda by her brother, Lucius Petilius Privatus, a freedman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000315.text_edition: IIIRO Aquiliae Marci libertae Chresti concubinae suae
EDCS-29000315.translation: To Aquilia Chreste, freedwoman of Marcus, his concubine.
EDCS-29000315.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aquilia Chreste, a freedwoman, by her concubine, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000316.translation: To the Divine Manes of Arat...
EDCS-29000316.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of an individual named Arat, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000318.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a spouse, likely part of a funerary monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29000319.translation: To the Divine Manes of Arethusa, [wife] of Zosimas.
EDCS-29000319.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Arethusa, likely the wife of Zosimas, by an unspecified individual, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100352.text_edition: Arriae Venaeriae sic matri dulcissimae Arrius Venerianus  filius
EDCS-29100352.translation: To Arria Venaeria, his sweetest mother, Arrius Venerianus, her son, [placed this].
EDCS-29100352.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Arrius Venerianus for his dearest mother, Arria Venaeria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100354.text_edition: Dis Manibus Artemisiae Restutus matri bene merenti
EDCS-29100354.translation: To the Divine Shades of Artemisia. Restutus [made this] for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29100354.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Restutus to his mother, Artemisia, praising her as well-deserving.

EDCS-29100355.text_edition: Aselia Lucida Lucio Aselio Halemo et Steniae Tychini parentibus posuit
EDCS-29100355.translation: Aselia Lucida placed this for her parents, Lucius Aselius Halemus and Stenia Tychina.
EDCS-29100355.summary: This inscription records Aselia Lucida dedicating a monument to her parents, Lucius Aselius Halemus and Stenia Tychina.

EDCS-29100358.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Asinio Vitali Lollia Privata coniugi pientissimo
EDCS-29100358.translation: To the Divine Shades of Gaius Asinius Vitalis. Lollia Privata [made this] for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-29100358.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lollia Privata in memory of her most devoted husband, Gaius Asinius Vitalis.

EDCS-29100359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Astrio Secundino qui et Salto TO
EDCS-29100359.translation: To the Divine Shades of Gaius Astrius Secundinus, who was also called Saltus. TO
EDCS-29100359.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Gaius Astrius Secundinus, also known as Saltus, likely marking his tomb.

EDCS-29100360.text_edition: ἀσεβῶν καὶ (π)ῇ (ἐ)μὲ (τ)άσ(σ)ων (ἐ)μοὶ δί(κ)ην διδοίη  Dis Manibus  κοῦρον πρωθήβην σφετέρων μέγ’ ἄγαλμα τοκήων Ἀστέριν ἐκ βιοτῆς βάσκανος ἦρ’ Ἀίδης κεῖται δ’ ἐν ληνῷ τῇδ’ ᾗ λεύσεις, παροδεῖτα δάκρυα καὶ στοναχὰς οἷσι λιπὼν γενέταις  εὐθύμει Ἀστέρει πολλοὶ πρὸ σοῦ, πολλοὶ μετὰ σέ οὐδεὶς ἀθάνατος
EDCS-29100360.translation: May whoever maliciously arranges me suffer punishment from me. To the Divine Shades. The envious Hades snatched Aster, a youth in the prime of life, a great ornament to his parents, from life. He lies in this vat, which you will see, passerby, leaving tears and groans for his parents. Be cheerful, Aster. Many before you, many after you. No one is immortal.
EDCS-29100360.summary: This is a bilingual funerary inscription, with both Greek and Latin components, for Aster, a young man taken by Hades, emphasizing the grief of his parents and the inevitability of death.

EDCS-29100361.text_edition: ticus Atiliae uxori suae bene merenti et sibi
EDCS-29100361.translation: Ticus [made this] for his well-deserving wife Atilia and for himself.
EDCS-29100361.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Ticus to his deserving wife Atilia and also for himself, indicating a shared tomb.

EDCS-29100363.text_edition: $ A]tilia Hospita E Lereia  Ceniavia sibi et viro  Luci filius Verecundus et fratri  FEirmo#Firmo#EIRMO obito et Vitali filiae suae  hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-29100363.translation: Atilia Hospita, daughter of Lucius, from Lereia Ceniavia, [made this] for herself and for her husband, and for her son Verecundus and her brother Firmo, who passed away, and for her daughter Vitalis. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-29100363.summary: This inscription records Atilia Hospita dedicating a funerary monument for herself, her husband, her son Verecundus, her deceased brother Firmo, and her daughter Vitalis, specifying it is not for an heir.

EDCS-29100364.text_edition: Dis Manibus Atiliae Quartae Atilius Basilides filius matri pientissimae
EDCS-29100364.translation: To the Divine Shades of Atilia Quarta. Atilius Basilides, her son, [made this] for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-29100364.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Atilius Basilides to his most devoted mother, Atilia Quarta.

EDCS-29100365.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sexto Attio Tertullo defuncto nnorum III mensium II dierum XIII parentes filio dulcissimo
EDCS-29100365.translation: To the Divine Shades of Sextus Attius Tertullus, who died at 3 years, 2 months, and 13 days old. His parents [made this] for their sweetest son.
EDCS-29100365.summary: This is a funerary inscription by parents for their sweetest son, Sextus Attius Tertullus, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-29100366.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicated to, or by, a woman named Ienia Ugurina.

EDCS-29100368.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication, with parts of names like Aurelius preserved.

EDCS-29100370.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, preserving parts of names including 'Aure[...]' and 'Iber[...]'.

EDCS-29100371.text_edition: naufragio exisse annum vertentem vixisse post annum manuO#manu#MANO humana sublatum esse Aurelius Aeladi pater filio pientissimo posuit
EDCS-29100371.translation: He escaped shipwreck, lived for a year and was snatched away by human hand after a year. Aurelius Aeladi, his father, set this up for his most devoted son.
EDCS-29100371.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Aeladi for his young son who, having survived a shipwreck, died shortly after at the age of one, possibly due to human intervention.

EDCS-29100372.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Epicteto defuncto filia patri pientissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29100372.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelius Epictetus, deceased. His daughter set this up for her most devoted father, who deserved it well.
EDCS-29100372.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Epictetus by his daughter, honoring her father for his merits and devotion.

EDCS-29100373.text_edition: O viva   dierum XX Aureli-- Eutych3  Aureli-- Eutychi
EDCS-29100373.translation: Oh, alive for 20 days. Aureli[...]. Eutych[...]. Aureli[...]. Eutychi[...]
EDCS-29100373.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Aurelius Eutyches who lived for only 20 days, likely erected by his parents.

EDCS-29100374.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aurelius Germanus and Caius P., likely a personal dedication or a funerary monument.

EDCS-29100375.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Augusti liberto Hermeroti vixit annos X menses VI dies V filio dulcissimo et pientissimo et Germanio et Stefanae parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-29100375.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Hermeros, freedman of Augustus. He lived for 10 years, 6 months, 5 days. His most unfortunate parents [set this up] for their sweetest and most devoted son, and for Germanus and Stephana.
EDCS-29100375.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by unfortunate parents to their sweetest and most devoted son, Marcus Aurelius Hermeros, a freedman of Augustus, who died at a young age, and also mentioning Germanus and Stephana.

EDCS-29100376.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aurelius Lucius Aurelius and Daphne, likely a personal dedication or a funerary monument.

EDCS-29100377.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Macedonia, likely a funerary or honorific monument.

EDCS-29100379.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Maximus vixit annos plus minus IIII dies Aurelius VISanus et Roscia Maximina pRarentes#parentes#RAENTES filio infEelicissimo#infelicissimo#INEELICISSIMO posuerunt
EDCS-29100379.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Maximus lived approximately 4 years. Aurelius Visanus and Roscia Maximina, his parents, set this up for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-29100379.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Maximus, who lived for approximately four years, set up by his parents Aurelius Visanus and Roscia Maximina.

EDCS-29100380.text_edition: Aurelius Q defunctus annorum  Messia bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29100380.translation: Aurelius Q., deceased, aged [---] years, placed this for Messia, who deserved well.
EDCS-29100380.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelius Q. to Messia, expressing gratitude for her good deeds.

EDCS-29100381.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary, mentioning Aurelius R[---], but too incomplete to determine its full purpose or context.

EDCS-29100382.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Satrio qui vixit annos VIIII menses IIII et Aureliae Maximae quae vixit nnos VI menses III filiis piissimis Aurelii Flavus et Ceres parentes et is sic Vic Ursa con sic alumnis suis P Hipetiu sic et
EDCS-29100382.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Satrius, who lived 9 years, 4 months, and to Aurelia Maxima, who lived 6 years, 3 months, their most dutiful children. Aurelius Flavus and Ceres, parents, and in this way Vic Ursa, with their foster children P. Hipetius [---] and [---].
EDCS-29100382.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aurelius Flavus and Ceres to their two deceased children, Aurelius Satrius and Aurelia Maxima, with an additional mention of foster children by Vic Ursa.

EDCS-29100383.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Saturnino Auirelia Messorina uxor et sibi fecit
EDCS-29100383.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Messorina, wife, made this for Aurelius Saturninus and for herself.
EDCS-29100383.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone or mausoleum, erected by Aurelia Messorina for her husband, Aurelius Saturninus, and for herself, indicating a shared burial place.

EDCS-29100384.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Sextilio filio innocentissimo et infelicissimo qui vixit anno sic uno! eFt#et#FT menses sex et dies VIIII Aurelii Lupus et Privata parentes posuerunt
EDCS-29100384.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Sextilius, a most innocent and unfortunate son, who lived one year, six months, and nine days. Aurelii Lupus and Privata, his parents, placed this.
EDCS-29100384.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelii Lupus and Privata to their deceased infant son, Aurelius Sextilius.

EDCS-29100385.text_edition: Aurelius Silvinus et Quadratia Urbana Aureliae Felicissimae filiae infelicissimae quae vixit annos XXI menses V posuerunt
EDCS-29100385.translation: Aurelius Silvinus and Quadratia Urbana placed this for Aurelia Felicissima, their most unfortunate daughter, who lived 21 years, 5 months.
EDCS-29100385.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelius Silvinus and Quadratia Urbana to their deceased daughter, Aurelia Felicissima.

EDCS-29100386.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Sodali Aurelius Corinthus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29100386.translation: Aurelius Corinthus placed this for Marcus Aurelius Sodalis, who deserved well.
EDCS-29100386.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelius Corinthus to Marcus Aurelius Sodalis, acknowledging his good deeds or merits.

EDCS-29100387.text_edition: $ infan]ti infEelicissimo#infelicissimo#INEELICIS vixit annumN#annum#ANUN menses VIIII Aurelius Tiberianus pater et Basilia mNater#mater#NATER filio fecerunt
EDCS-29100387.translation: [---] for a most unfortunate infant, who lived one year, nine months. Aurelius Tiberianus, father, and Basilia, mother, made this for their son.
EDCS-29100387.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a deceased infant son, erected by his parents, Aurelius Tiberianus and Basilia.

EDCS-29100388.text_edition: Dis Manibus - Aurelio Valentino Aelia Valentine mater bene merenti
EDCS-29100388.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Valentinus. Aelia Valentine, mother, [placed this] for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-29100388.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aelia Valentine to her deceased son, Aurelius Valentinus, recognizing his merits.

EDCS-29100391.text_edition: Aurel MA V Maximannum I menses V Plat
EDCS-29100391.translation: Aurelius M. A. V. Maximus, one year, five months. Plat[---]
EDCS-29100391.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely for an infant named Aurelius M. A. V. Maximus, recording his age at death, with 'Plat[---]' possibly being the name of a relative or the dedicator.

EDCS-29100392.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Crescentillae coniugi quae vixit annos XXV Iulius
EDCS-29100392.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Crescentilla, my wife, who lived for 25 years. Julius [set this up].
EDCS-29100392.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julius to his wife, Aurelia Crescentilla, who died at the age of 25, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100393.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Eutychis Aurelio Callistioni alumno bene merenti de suo posuit annorum XXX
EDCS-29100393.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Eutychis, for Aurelius Callistion, her well-deserving foster-son, set this up from her own means. He was 30 years old.
EDCS-29100393.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Eutychis for her well-deserving foster-son, Aurelius Callistion, who died at the age of 30, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100394.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcella Aureliae Vinniae libertae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29100394.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Marcella set this up for Aurelia Vinnia, her well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-29100394.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Marcella to her well-deserving freedwoman, Aurelia Vinnia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100395.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Messia filio defuncto raptus esst posit(um) arbitratu Quinti Pr Urani Criscentioni sic infanti annorum XI
EDCS-29100395.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Messia [set this up] for her deceased son. He was snatched away, a child, at 11 years old. It was set up according to the judgment of Quintus Pr[imus] Uranius Crescentio.
EDCS-29100395.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Messia for her son, who died at the age of 11, with the monument erected under the arbitration of Quintus Primus Uranius Crescentio, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100396.text_edition: Aurelia Parasceue hic est posita vixit nnos LX cQuius#cuius#QUI memoriam fabric(avit) vivus maritus eius Aurelius Isidorus qui et Edasterius
EDCS-29100396.translation: Aurelia Parasceue is laid here. She lived 60 years. Her living husband, Aurelius Isidorus, also known as Edasterius, built this memorial.
EDCS-29100396.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Parasceue, who lived 60 years, erected by her still-living husband, Aurelius Isidorus (also known as Edasterius), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100397.text_edition: Aureliae Rufinae defunctae annorum plus minus LXX Publius Maximus matri posuit
EDCS-29100397.translation: To Aurelia Rufina, deceased, aged approximately 70 years. Publius Maximus set this up for his mother.
EDCS-29100397.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publius Maximus to his deceased mother, Aurelia Rufina, who was approximately 70 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100398.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureliae Veneriae quae vixit annos LX menses quinque Aurelius Secundus fi=ELlius#filius#ELLIUS pRosuit#posuit#ROSUIT
EDCS-29100398.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia Veneria, who lived 60 years and five months. Aurelius Secundus, her son, set this up.
EDCS-29100398.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Secundus to his mother, Aurelia Veneria, who lived for 60 years and five months, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100399.text_edition: Manlius sibi et Aureliae Urbanae coniugi infelicissimae defunctae cum qua concorditer vixit
EDCS-29100399.translation: Manlius [set this up] for himself and for Aurelia Urbana, his most unfortunate wife, deceased, with whom he lived in harmony.
EDCS-29100399.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Manlius for himself and his deceased wife, Aurelia Urbana, with whom he shared a harmonious life, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100400.translation: Axilius Primus [set this up] for Marcellinus.
EDCS-29100400.summary: This is a short funerary inscription dedicated by Axilius Primus to Marcellinus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100401.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Baebilio Epaphrodito Baebilii Eutyches et Ianuarius liberti
EDCS-29100401.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus. Baebilius Eutyches and Ianuarius, his freedmen [set this up].
EDCS-29100401.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by his freedmen, Baebilius Eutyches and Ianuarius, to Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100402.text_edition: Baebiliae Charidi defunctae annorum VIIII mensium IIII dierum V Baebilii Satyrus et Prepusa alumnae karissimae
EDCS-29100402.translation: To Baebilia Charis, who died at 9 years, 4 months, and 5 days old. Baebilius Satyrus and Prepusa to their dearest foster-child.
EDCS-29100402.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Baebilius Satyrus and Prepusa to their beloved foster-daughter Baebilia Charis, who died as a child, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100403.text_edition: Dis Manibus Baebiliae iarini defunctae nnorum plus minus III
EDCS-29100403.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Baebilia Iarinus, who died at approximately 3 years old.
EDCS-29100403.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Baebilia Iarinus, a young child, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100404.text_edition: Baebio Alexanddri Baebio to Caius Baebius Sabinus Baebi-- He conlibertis
EDCS-29100404.translation: To Baebius, son of Alexander. To Baebius [---]. Gaius Baebius Sabinus [---]. To Baebius [---]. He [---] to his fellow freedmen.
EDCS-29100404.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial by Gaius Baebius Sabinus to several individuals named Baebius, possibly his fellow freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100405.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Baebio Quartioni et ? Baebio Epicteto colliberto optimo eius Valeria Euphrosyne coniugi piissimo bene merito
EDCS-29100405.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Baebius Quartionus and perhaps Baebius Epictetus, his best fellow freedman. Valeria Euphrosyne to her most devoted and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29100405.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Valeria Euphrosyne to her most devoted husband, Baebius Quartionus, and possibly to his fellow freedman Baebius Epictetus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100432.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Antonini et Getae Caesaris
EDCS-29100432.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all gods and goddesses, for the well-being of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Antoninus, and Caesar Geta.
EDCS-29100432.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and other deities for the health and welfare of the Imperial family, specifically mentioning Septimius Severus, Caracalla (Antoninus), and Geta, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29100433.text_edition: Iovi Conservatori Dolicheno sacrum Octavius Euphemus
EDCS-29100433.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Conservator Dolichenus. Octavius Euphemus.
EDCS-29100433.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Conservator Dolichenus by Octavius Euphemus, indicating the worship of this specific deity, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200199.text_edition: Dis Manibus Baebiliae Luci filiae Proculae defunctae nnorum III mensium VIII
EDCS-29200199.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Baebilia Procula, daughter of Lucius, who died at 3 years and 8 months old.
EDCS-29200199.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Baebilia Procula, a young daughter of Lucius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200200.text_edition: Dis Manibus Barbaroni defuncto anni I mensium III Barbara mater
EDCS-29200200.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Barbaronus, who died at 1 year and 3 months old. Barbara, his mother.
EDCS-29200200.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the mother Barbara to the Divine Spirits of her son Barbaronus, who died as an infant, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200201.translation: To the dear boy [---] of Basilia.
EDCS-29200201.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a dear young boy, probably set up by Basilia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200203.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning names such as Publius and Priscianus, and likely a collection number 'INS VI', with its original purpose now unclear due to its poor state of preservation, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200204.text_edition: Baebidiae Salvianeti quae vixit nnos III menses V dies XIIII Tertius et Donata parentes filiae infelicisimae posuerunt
EDCS-29200204.translation: To Baebidia Salvianetis, who lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 14 days, Tertius and Donata, her parents, placed this for their most unhappy daughter.
EDCS-29200204.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Tertius and Donata for their young, deceased daughter, Baebidia Salvianetis.

EDCS-29200205.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Bibbius Herma et Licinia Ianuaria Marco Bibbio Primigenio liberto bene merito posuerunt defuncto annorum XII mensium VII
EDCS-29200205.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Bibbius Herma and Licinia Ianuaria placed this for Marcus Bibbius Primigenius, their well-deserving freedman, who died at 12 years and 7 months of age.
EDCS-29200205.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Spirits by Marcus Bibbius Herma and Licinia Ianuaria for their freedman, Marcus Bibbius Primigenius, who died young.

EDCS-29200206.text_edition: C Acrestina Valerio Var coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29200206.translation: C. Acrestina made this for Valerius Var, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29200206.summary: This inscription records C. Acrestina's dedication to her husband, Valerius Var, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-29200207.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius CalEpetanus#Calpetanus#CAEPETANUS Martialis vivus fe[cit &
EDCS-29200207.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Calpetanus Martialis made this while alive.
EDCS-29200207.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Gaius Calpetanus Martialis for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-29200208.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a person who lived for 15 years and someone named Aesidius.

EDCS-29200209.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caesidiae Nicadiae Caesidia Tertia patronae
EDCS-29200209.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Caesidia Nicadia. Caesidia Tertia [made this] for her patroness.
EDCS-29200209.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Caesidia Nicadia by Caesidia Tertia, likely a freedwoman honoring her former mistress.

EDCS-29200210.text_edition: Caesonius Ianuarius Valeriae Victorinae quae vixit annos LXV
EDCS-29200210.translation: Caesonius Ianuarius [made this] for Valeria Victorina, who lived for 65 years.
EDCS-29200210.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Caesonius Ianuarius for Valeria Victorina, who lived to an age of 65.

EDCS-29200211.text_edition: Publius Caetennius Publi libertus Ch Caius Fuficius Cai libertus Felicianus  COX
EDCS-29200211.translation: Publius Caetennius, freedman of Publius, Ch. Gaius Fuficius, freedman of Gaius, Felicianus. COX.
EDCS-29200211.summary: This inscription lists two freedmen, Publius Caetennius and Gaius Fuficius Felicianus, alongside their former masters' names, possibly indicating shared ownership or a joint dedication, with additional abbreviations.

EDCS-29200212.text_edition: Marcus Caetennius Mar Satriae Rhodepeni matri piae fecit
EDCS-29200212.translation: Marcus Caetennius made this for Satria Rhodepene, his devoted mother.
EDCS-29200212.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Caetennius to his pious mother, Satria Rhodepene.

EDCS-29200213.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a Publius Caetennius Pater[nus or Paternus/Paterna].

EDCS-29200214.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Caltilio Luci filio Myroni Aemilia Nebris coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29200214.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Lucius Caltilius Myron, son of Lucius, Emilia Nebris placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29200214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Emilia Nebris to her deceased husband, Lucius Caltilius Myron.

EDCS-29200216.summary: This is a short, possibly funerary, inscription by Celadus dedicated to Campester.

EDCS-29200217.text_edition: Canuleia Cai filia Procula Publio Petio Marci filio Mariti[mo &
EDCS-29200217.translation: Canuleia Procula, daughter of Gaius, to Publius Petius Maritimus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-29200217.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Canuleia Procula to Publius Petius Maritimus, likely funerary given the context of surrounding inscriptions.

EDCS-29200218.text_edition: Cassio bene merenti pientissimo posuit Tigris coniunx eius
EDCS-29200218.translation: Tigris, his wife, placed this for the well-deserving and most dutiful Cassius.
EDCS-29200218.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Tigris for her deceased husband, Cassius.

EDCS-29200219.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Quinto Cassio Caeionio qui vixit nnos III menses X dies XV filio suo caro posuit Quintus Cassius Silvester et Caeionia Sexta mater
EDCS-29200219.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Cassius Caeionius, who lived for three years, ten months, and fifteen days, his dear son. Quintus Cassius Silvester and Caeionia Sexta, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-29200219.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Cassius Silvester and Caeionia Sexta to their young son, Quintus Cassius Caeionius.

EDCS-29200220.text_edition: Quinto Cassio Quinti liberto Eumeneti Quintus Cassius Firmus et Cassia Maecenatia et Cassia Constantilla patri
EDCS-29200220.translation: To Quintus Cassius Eumenetus, freedman of Quintus. Quintus Cassius Firmus and Cassia Maecenatia and Cassia Constantilla placed this for their father.
EDCS-29200220.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Cassius Firmus, Cassia Maecenatia, and Cassia Constantilla to their father, Quintus Cassius Eumenetus, a freedman.

EDCS-29200221.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Cassio Firmino defuncto annorum XVIIII parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-29200221.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Cassius Firminus, deceased at nineteen years old. His most unfortunate parents placed this.
EDCS-29200221.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by grief-stricken parents for their son, Lucius Cassius Firminus, who died at age 19.

EDCS-29200222.text_edition: Cassio Iuniano et Iuliae Veneriae parentibus Cassi Pa
EDCS-29200222.translation: To Cassius Iunianus and Iulia Veneria, his parents. Cassius Pa...
EDCS-29200222.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cassius P. to his parents, Cassius Iunianus and Iulia Veneria.

EDCS-29200223.text_edition: Lucius Cassius Theodorus vivus fecit sibi et Papiniae Italicae uxori et Cassio Italico et Cassiae Tertullinae filiis  in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX
EDCS-29200223.translation: Lucius Cassius Theodorus made this while living for himself and for Papinia Italica, his wife, and for Cassius Italicus and Cassia Tertullina, his children. Twenty feet in front, twenty feet in depth.
EDCS-29200223.summary: This is a self-dedicatory funerary inscription by Lucius Cassius Theodorus, made while he was alive, for himself, his wife, and his children, also specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-29200224.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Cassiae Secundinae defunctae annorum III mensium III Cassianus et Maxssimuna sic parentes posuaerunM(t) sic#posuaerun(t) sic#POSUAERUM
EDCS-29200224.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Cassia Secundina, deceased at three years and three months old. Cassianus and Maxssimuna, her parents, placed this.
EDCS-29200224.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cassianus and Maxssimuna to their very young daughter, Cassia Secundina.

EDCS-29200225.text_edition: CPAOTRAE  Catia Iu et Caius Catius
EDCS-29200225.translation: C. Paotra, Catia Iu[lia] and Gaius Catius [set this up].
EDCS-29200225.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names of Catia Iulia and Gaius Catius, possibly for C. Paotra, indicating a family burial or dedication.

EDCS-29200227.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Catttio Terentio Iulia Nepotilla marito posuit et sibi
EDCS-29200227.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Nepotilla placed this for her husband, Lucius Cattius Terentius, and for herself.
EDCS-29200227.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulia Nepotilla dedicating a monument to her husband, Lucius Cattius Terentius, and reserving a place for herself.

EDCS-29200228.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Cattia Baebilia, deceased.
EDCS-29200228.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the deceased Cattia Baebilia.

EDCS-29200229.text_edition: Ceionia Ferocilla Ceioniae Hilarae libertae et collactanaeae sic infelicissimae
EDCS-29200229.translation: Ceionia Ferocilla [placed this] for Ceionia Hilara, her freedwoman and foster-sister, most unfortunately.
EDCS-29200229.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Ceionia Ferocilla for her freedwoman and foster-sister, Ceionia Hilara, expressing sorrow.

EDCS-29200231.text_edition: Dis Manibus rtiae Celerinae uxori et Quinto o Cele[rino &
EDCS-29200231.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Artia Celerina, his wife, and Quintus [Catius] Celerinus.
EDCS-29200231.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Artia Celerina, a wife, and Quintus Catius Celerinus, likely a son, by an unnamed individual.

EDCS-29200232.text_edition: Dis Manibus nte  pia I  defunct-- Celsinus Celsi Maela Netis bene maerenti sic posuerunt
EDCS-29200232.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [For] Pia I[ulia], deceased. Celsinus Celsi Maela Netis and [---] placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29200232.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deceased Pia Iulia by Celsinus Celsi Maela Netis and another, honoring a deserving individual.

EDCS-29200233.translation: Caenia Quarta placed this out of piety.
EDCS-29200233.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription, possibly funerary, placed by Caenia Quarta as an act of piety.

EDCS-29200234.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ceriali defuncto annorum XVIII mensium VI dierum XVII Genialis filius et Deutaer sic mater posuerunt
EDCS-29200234.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Cerialis, deceased, who lived eighteen years, six months, and seventeen days. Genialis, his son, and Deutaer, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-29200234.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cerialis, who died at 18, dedicated by his son Genialis and his mother Deutaer.

EDCS-29200235.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to someone named Charis as 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-29200236.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Charitoni filio infelicissimo qui vixit annis XVIII Aurelius Maximus et Aurelia Gemella parentes posuerunt
EDCS-29200236.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Maximus and Aurelia Gemella, his parents, placed this for their most unfortunate son Chariton, who lived eighteen years.
EDCS-29200236.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelius Maximus and Aurelia Gemella for their son Chariton, who died at the age of eighteen, expressing their grief.

EDCS-29200237.text_edition: Dis Manibus Chresimo defuncto annorum VII Cornelia Tertulla filio
EDCS-29200237.translation: To the Divine Shades, to Chresimus, who died at seven years of age. Cornelia Tertulla to her son.
EDCS-29200237.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cornelia Tertulla to her deceased son, Chresimus, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-29200238.translation: To Vivius Chrestus and to the approaches.
EDCS-29200238.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to Vivius Chrestus and possibly the access points to a monument or area.

EDCS-29200239.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Cinciae Campanae posuit memoria Statius Victorinus matIri#matri#MAIRI dulIcissimae#dulcissimae#DUIISSIME
EDCS-29200239.translation: To the Divine Shades of Cincia Campana, Statius Victorinus placed this in memory of his sweetest mother.
EDCS-29200239.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Statius Victorinus in memory of his beloved mother, Cincia Campana.

EDCS-29200240.translation: Castricius Victor to his excellent sister.
EDCS-29200240.summary: This is a dedication by Castricius Victor to his sister, praising her as 'excellent'.

EDCS-29200241.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedicatory vow by someone named Claudius.

EDCS-29200243.text_edition: Claudio Dalmatio homini incomparabili qui vicxit sic annos plus minus L lia Ilara marito pientissimo posuit et sibi
EDCS-29200243.translation: To Claudius Dalmatius, an incomparable man, who lived approximately 50 years. Ilia Ilara placed this for her most devoted husband and for herself.
EDCS-29200243.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ilia Ilara to her 'incomparable' and 'most devoted' husband, Claudius Dalmatius, who lived around 50 years, and also for herself, implying a shared tomb.

EDCS-29200245.text_edition: Claudi-- H  et Iulia I Iulio
EDCS-29200245.translation: Claudius H and Iulia to Iulius.
EDCS-29200245.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication by Claudius H. and Iulia to someone named Iulius.

EDCS-29200246.translation: Of Claudius Musculosus, who died at fourteen years.
EDCS-29200246.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the death of Claudius Musculosus at the age of fourteen.

EDCS-29200247.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudio Superstiti iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit nnos XXX menses X dies XIIII Claudius Super et Victrix parentes
EDCS-29200247.translation: To the Divine Shades of Claudius Superstes, a most unfortunate young man who lived 30 years, 10 months, 14 days. Claudius Super and Victrix, his parents.
EDCS-29200247.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Claudius Super and Victrix for their son, Claudius Superstes, who died at the age of 30 years, 10 months, and 14 days.

EDCS-29200249.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Dione Antonius Themistocles     pietatis causa
EDCS-29200249.translation: To the Divine Shades of Claudia Dione. Antonius Themistocles, for reasons of piety.
EDCS-29200249.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antonius Themistocles to Claudia Dione, out of filial or dutiful devotion.

EDCS-29200251.text_edition: s Claudiae Maximinae quae vixit annos XXX coniugi rarissimae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29200251.translation: To the spirits of Claudia Maximina, who lived for 30 years. To a most rare and well-deserving spouse, they erected this.
EDCS-29200251.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by unknown individuals in memory of Claudia Maximina, a rare and well-deserving spouse who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-29200253.text_edition: AV Clodio Charino marito bene merenti sibi et IISI fecit
EDCS-29200253.translation: AV Clodius Charinus made this for his well-deserving husband and for himself and IISI.
EDCS-29200253.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by AV Clodius Charinus for his well-deserving husband, and for himself and IISI, likely indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-29200254.summary: This short inscription indicates that Clodia Nice HO placed this monument, likely a funerary marker, though the recipient is not stated.

EDCS-29200255.text_edition: Gaius Clodius Popilianus qui Cursor coniugi dulcissimae quae vixit con sic coniuge suo annos XII defunctae annorum XXII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29200255.translation: Gaius Clodius Popilianus, also known as Cursor, placed this for his sweetest wife, who lived with her husband for 12 years and died at the age of 22, well-deserving.
EDCS-29200255.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Gaius Clodius Popilianus, also known as Cursor, for his sweet, well-deserving wife, who died at 22 after 12 years of marriage.

EDCS-29200256.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Clodio Zoillo filio karissimo defuncto nnorum XXXII ClIodia#Clodia#CIODIA Zotice mater infelicissima et Obultronia Fortunata fratri dulcissimo posuerunt
EDCS-29200256.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For Clodius Zoillus, a most beloved son who died at 32 years. Clodia Zotice, his most unhappy mother, and Obultronia Fortunata placed this for their sweetest brother.
EDCS-29200256.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Clodius Zoillus, a beloved son who died at 32, placed by his unhappy mother Clodia Zotice and his sister Obultronia Fortunata.

EDCS-29200257.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodiae Partenopini Gaius coniugi
EDCS-29200257.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Clodia Partenopini, from Gaius, her husband.
EDCS-29200257.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius to his wife, Clodia Partenopini.

EDCS-29200258.text_edition: s Luci filio Tromentina  sibi et Lucio Co  et Aponiae  tri et AV
EDCS-29200258.translation: To the spirits of Lucius, son of Tromentina. For himself and Lucius Co... and Aponia... and AV.
EDCS-29200258.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius, son of Tromentina, and also serving as a burial place for himself, Lucius Co..., Aponia..., and AV.

EDCS-29200260.text_edition: NI  Tromentina  IQ CO defuncto annorum pientissimo coniugi
EDCS-29200260.translation: ... Tromentina ... to the deceased, a most devoted husband, aged ...
EDCS-29200260.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription by an unknown dedicant (likely a spouse) in memory of a deceased, most devoted husband, with 'Tromentina' possibly indicating a gens or origin.

EDCS-29200262.text_edition: Dis Manibus Coelio GQaudentio#Gaudentio#QAUDETIO infanti infelicissimo
EDCS-29200262.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Coelius Gaudentius, a most unhappy infant.
EDCS-29200262.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Coelius Gaudentius, a deceased infant, indicating a lament for a very young life lost.

EDCS-29200263.text_edition: Dis Manibus Coeliae Secundae defunctae annorum XXIIII Ulpia Saturnina matIer#mater#MAIER filiae iLnfelici#infelici#LNFELICI et orius sorori pientissimae posuerunt
EDCS-29200263.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Coelia Secunda, who died at 24 years. Ulpia Saturnina, her unhappy mother, and [---] orius, her most devoted brother, placed this.
EDCS-29200263.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Coelia Secunda, who died at 24, placed by her unhappy mother Ulpia Saturnina and her devoted brother [---] orius.

EDCS-29200265.text_edition: Constans patri posuit et Auxesis coniunx
EDCS-29200265.translation: Constans placed this for his father, and Auxesis his wife.
EDCS-29200265.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by Constans for his father, with his wife Auxesis joining in the dedication.

EDCS-29200266.text_edition: ca Cornelio Trofimo coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29200266.translation: To Caius Cornelius Trophimus, his well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29200266.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Caius Cornelius Trophimus, likely set up by his wife.

EDCS-29200267.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Cornelio Iullo defuncto nnorum VII Caius Cornelius Staphylus pater
EDCS-29200267.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Caius Cornelius Iullus, who died at the age of 7 years. Caius Cornelius Staphylus, father.
EDCS-29200267.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caius Cornelius Staphylus to his son, Caius Cornelius Iullus, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-29200268.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Marcus Cornelius Iuvent[...], likely part of a larger dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-29200269.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cornelio Maximo bene merenti Coelia Gemella uxor et sibi viva fecet sic
EDCS-29200269.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Cornelius Maximus, well-deserving. Coelia Gemella, his wife, made this for herself while living. Thus.
EDCS-29200269.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Cornelius Maximus by his wife, Coelia Gemella, who also prepared the monument for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-29200270.text_edition: REI TONI Cornelius Symphorus filius et heres? bene merenti
EDCS-29200270.translation: To Rei Toni (an unknown deity). Cornelius Symphorus, son and heir, to the well-deserving.
EDCS-29200270.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Cornelius Symphorus, as son and heir, to an unknown deity named Rei Toni, honoring someone 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-29200272.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescentillae defunctae nnorum XI mensium V dierum II Aurelius Maximinus alumnae
EDCS-29200272.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Crescentilla, who died at the age of 11 years, 5 months, 2 days. Aurelius Maximinus to his foster-daughter.
EDCS-29200272.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Maximinus to his foster-daughter, Crescentilla, who died as a child.

EDCS-29200273.text_edition: VIR SIRIIAus Aliae Crescentillae coniugi bene merenti con sic qC(ua)#q(ua)#C vixsi sic annos XXX posuit
EDCS-29200273.translation: To Vir Siriiaus Aliae Crescentillae, his well-deserving wife, who lived for 30 years. He placed this.
EDCS-29200273.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument erected by Vir Siriiaus for his well-deserving wife, Aliae Crescentillae, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-29200275.text_edition: Quintus Crispuleius Hermes Quinto Crispuleio Maritimo patrono optime merito
EDCS-29200275.translation: Quintus Crispuleius Hermes [placed this] for Quintus Crispuleius Maritimus, his most deserving patron.
EDCS-29200275.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Quintus Crispuleius Hermes to his patron, Quintus Crispuleius Maritimus, praising him as 'most deserving'.

EDCS-29200276.text_edition: Dazomenus <et=F>#<et>#F Panto filio suo Fuscino memoriam DI
EDCS-29200276.translation: Dazomenus and Panto [made this] for their son Fuscinus, in memoriam.
EDCS-29200276.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument erected by Dazomenus and Panto in memory of their son, Fuscinus.

EDCS-29200277.text_edition: Dis Manibus Decidio Germano CRESINI CF
EDCS-29200277.translation: To the Divine Manes of Decidius Germanus, son of Cresinus.
EDCS-29200277.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Decidius Germanus by his father, Cresinus.

EDCS-29200280.text_edition: Aga  D  Desidienae Profuturae coniugi incomparabili Suellius Septiminus maritus bene merenti posuit et Suellio Septimino filio dulcissimo posuit
EDCS-29200280.translation: Aga D. Suellius Septiminus, the husband, placed this for his incomparable wife Desidiena Profutura, who well deserved it, and placed this for his dearest son Suellius Septiminus.
EDCS-29200280.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Suellius Septiminus for his incomparable wife Desidiena Profutura and his dearest son Suellius Septiminus.

EDCS-29200281.text_edition: D(is) Manibus Dexter frater sorori Sosemini annorum IIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29200281.translation: To the Divine Manes. Dexter, a brother, placed this for his sister Soseminis, who lived four years and well deserved it.
EDCS-29200281.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the brother Dexter to his sister Soseminis, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-29200282.text_edition: Dirutia Restuta testamento fieri iussit sibi et Lucio Dirutio Phoebo fratri et Dirutiae Damale sorori Dirutia Ianilla heres faciundum curavit et Dirutiae Threpte matri
EDCS-29200282.translation: Dirutia Restuta ordered by will that this be made for herself and for her brother Lucius Dirutius Phoebus and for her sister Dirutia Damale. Dirutia Ianilla, the heir, took care to have it made, also for Dirutia Threpte, her mother.
EDCS-29200282.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Dirutia Restuta in her will for herself, her brother Lucius Dirutius Phoebus, and her sister Dirutia Damale, and was carried out by her heir Dirutia Ianilla, who also included Dirutia Threpte, the mother.

EDCS-29200283.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Diteio mater filio pientissimo defuncto nnorum plus minus XVIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29200283.translation: To the Divine Manes. Diteia, the mother, placed this for her most devoted son, who died at approximately eighteen years of age and well deserved it.
EDCS-29200283.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Diteia, to her very devoted son who died at around eighteen years old.

EDCS-29200286.summary: This fragment of an inscription likely pertains to the Rufii family or their property, but too much is missing to determine its precise purpose or date.

EDCS-29200287.translation: Publius Domitius Marcellinus took care to have this placed.
EDCS-29200287.summary: This inscription records that Publius Domitius Marcellinus was responsible for placing the monument, likely a funerary or dedicatory one.

EDCS-29200289.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Domitio Saturnino Domitius Flavianus filius patri posuit bene merenti
EDCS-29200289.translation: To the Divine Manes. Domitius Flavianus, the son, placed this for his father Domitius Saturninus, who well deserved it.
EDCS-29200289.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Domitius Flavianus for his father, Domitius Saturninus.

EDCS-29200290.summary: This fragment appears to be part of an inscription mentioning individuals named Domitius Secundus (or similar) and Aurelius, likely indicating ownership or dedication.

EDCS-29200291.text_edition: a et Dom  Valenti et Ulpio Luci filio?  parieti inscriberentur
EDCS-29200291.translation: ...and for Domitius Valens and for Ulpius, son of Lucius, it should be inscribed on the wall.
EDCS-29200291.summary: This inscription fragment indicates that a text should be inscribed on a wall for Domitius Valens and Ulpius, son of Lucius, possibly commemorating them or recording a legal act.

EDCS-29200292.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Domitia Arethusa, my wife.
EDCS-29200292.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by an unnamed individual to his wife, Domitia Arethusa.

EDCS-29200293.text_edition: et Domitiae Ianuariae filiae defunctae nnum XVIII Caius Domitius Nicias pater
EDCS-29200293.translation: And to Domitia Ianuaria, his deceased daughter, 18 years old. Gaius Domitius Nicias, father.
EDCS-29200293.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Gaius Domitius Nicias to his deceased daughter, Domitia Ianuaria, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-29200294.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Domitia Lacena.
EDCS-29200294.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Domitia Lacena.

EDCS-29200295.text_edition: TA ETI Linfelicissimae defunctae nnorum XV mensium VI Egnatius Afrodisius pater posuit
EDCS-29200295.translation: To [the Divine Spirits of] [---]taeti, most unhappy, deceased at 15 years and 6 months. Egnatius Afrodisius, father, set this up.
EDCS-29200295.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Egnatius Afrodisius to his most unhappy child, who died at the age of 15 years and 6 months.
