EDCS-29200297.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ephebo et Synerusae bene merentibus S F P
EDCS-29200297.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Ephebus and Synerusa, well-deserving. S. F. P. [perhaps 'Sibiq(ue) Fecit Posuit', 'made and set it up for himself/herself'].
EDCS-29200297.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Ephebus and Synerusa, who were considered well-deserving.

EDCS-29200298.text_edition: Epictesi Hymnus conservae nnorum XX hic sita est
EDCS-29200298.translation: Epictesis, a fellow slave of Hymnus, 20 years old, lies here.
EDCS-29200298.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Epictesis, a female slave of Hymnus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-29200299.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by an unnamed individual to his wife, Leppia A[---].

EDCS-29200300.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, mentioning a son and the Palatine tribe.

EDCS-29200302.text_edition: Quinto Etuvio Quinti filio Tromentina Capriolo nnum XIIII Quintus Etuvius Erastus et Etuvia Hediste filio piissimo et Etuviae Quintae filiae
EDCS-29200302.translation: To Quintus Etuvius Capriolus, son of Quintus, of the Tromentina tribe, 14 years old. Quintus Etuvius Erastus and Etuvia Hediste [set this up] for their most devoted son and for their daughter Etuvia Quinta.
EDCS-29200302.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Quintus Etuvius Erastus and Etuvia Hediste to their most devoted son, Quintus Etuvius Capriolus, who died at 14, and also to their daughter, Etuvia Quinta.

EDCS-29200304.text_edition: Euplo nnorum XXV occiso a viatoribus
EDCS-29200304.translation: To Euplus, 25 years old, killed by travelers.
EDCS-29200304.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph for Euplus, who was killed by travelers at the age of 25.

EDCS-29200305.translation: To the Divine Manes of Eutyches B.
EDCS-29200305.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Eutyches B.

EDCS-29200306.text_edition: LIaus Eutichus Latoni coniugi Titiae Verzoni
EDCS-29200306.translation: LIaus Eutichus to Latoni, his wife Titia Verzonis.
EDCS-29200306.summary: This is a funerary inscription by LIaus Eutichus for his wife, Titia Verzonis.

EDCS-29200307.text_edition: Marco Fabio Firmo annorum XVIII Fabiae Marcellae sororis filiis
EDCS-29200307.translation: To Marcus Fabius Firmus, 18 years old, son of Fabia Marcella, his sister.
EDCS-29200307.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Fabius Firmus, who died at 18, set up by his sister Fabia Marcella for her son.

EDCS-29200309.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning several individuals including Gaius Va[---].

EDCS-29200313.text_edition: Dis Manibus  FEelicissimo#Felicissimo#EELICISSIMO defuncto nnorum XXVIIII Fortunius cognatus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29200313.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Felicissimus, deceased, 29 years old. Fortunius, his kinsman, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29200313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felicissimus, who died at 29, set up by his kinsman Fortunius.

EDCS-29200316.text_edition: illae  ann--- XXX Felix coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29200316.translation: To illa, [---] years old. Felix to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29200316.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Felix for his well-deserving wife, whose name and age are partially lost.

EDCS-29200317.translation: Felix placed this for his well-deserving kinsman.
EDCS-29200317.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Felix for a well-deserving kinsman.

EDCS-29200318.text_edition: miseri parentes filio infelicissimo Felici qui vixit nnos XIIII bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29200318.translation: The grieving parents placed this for their most unfortunate son Felix, who lived 14 years, to the well-deserving.
EDCS-29200318.summary: This is a funerary inscription by sorrowful parents for their son Felix, who died at age 14.

EDCS-29200322.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pedio Felici Aelia Telesphoris patri bene merenti fecit
EDCS-29200322.translation: To the Divine Manes of Pedius Felix. Aelia Telesphoris made this for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-29200322.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Aelia Telesphoris for her well-deserving father, Pedius Felix.

EDCS-29200323.text_edition: OR A ndus PAT tilius Felix
EDCS-29200323.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Andus Patilius Felix, possibly part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-29200324.text_edition: FEL Titus Flavius Maximus  Laeva  amico
EDCS-29200324.translation: Happy. Titus Flavius Maximus Laeva, to his friend.
EDCS-29200324.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Titus Flavius Maximus Laeva to his friend, possibly marking a tomb or a votive offering.

EDCS-29200325.text_edition: dulcissimae   et pientissimae A lii Felix Quintus infelicissima mater bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29200325.translation: To the sweetest and most devoted Alius Felix. Quintus, his most unhappy mother, placed this for her well-deserving child.
EDCS-29200325.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Quintus, an grieving mother, dedicating a monument to her beloved and deserving child, Alius Felix.

EDCS-29200326.translation: To Sextus Festivus, a delight. Venustus.
EDCS-29200326.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Venustus to Sextus Festivus, likely a funerary marker expressing affection for the deceased.

EDCS-29300103.text_edition: Flavi sorori defunctae annorum  mater infelicissima
EDCS-29300103.translation: To Flavia, my deceased sister, [who lived] for [an unspecified number] of years. Her most unhappy mother.
EDCS-29300103.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a grieving mother for her deceased daughter, Flavia.

EDCS-29300106.text_edition: Marcus Flavius Eutychus se vivo posuit
EDCS-29300106.translation: Marcus Flavius Eutychus placed this during his lifetime.
EDCS-29300106.summary: This inscription records Marcus Flavius Eutychus commissioning or erecting his own monument while still alive, likely a tombstone or funerary altar.

EDCS-29300108.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Flavius Marcellinus, possibly on a funerary monument or a votive offering.

EDCS-29300111.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Tam [---].
EDCS-29300111.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead (Di Manes) for Flavius, whose full name is partially lost.

EDCS-29300112.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Valens.
EDCS-29300112.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead (Di Manes) for Flavius Valens.

EDCS-29300113.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Flavius Valerius, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-29300114.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavia Apra Gemellino Crescenti nnorum plus minus XIIII infelicissimo ob memoriam posuit
EDCS-29300114.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Apra placed this in memory of the most unhappy Gemellinus Crescens, who lived about 14 years.
EDCS-29300114.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Flavia Apra dedicated to the spirits of the dead (Di Manes) for Gemellinus Crescens, who died around the age of 14.

EDCS-29300116.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Nice filiae dulcissimae quae vixit annos II menses IIII Flavia Epictus mater fecet sic
EDCS-29300116.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Epictus, mother, made this for her sweetest daughter Flavia Nice, who lived for 2 years and 4 months. So be it.
EDCS-29300116.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a tombstone by a mother, Flavia Epictus, to her deceased young daughter, Flavia Nice, who passed away at the age of two years and four months, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29300117.text_edition: Flaviae Rhopeni defunctae annorum XIII Flavius Charito et Eutych
EDCS-29300117.translation: To Flavia Rhope, deceased at 13 years old. Flavius Charito and Eutych[es made this].
EDCS-29300117.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Rhope, who died at the age of 13, set up by Flavius Charito and Eutyches, likely her parents or relatives, dating probably from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-29300118.text_edition: Lucius Folnius Favor vivus fecit sibi et Lolliae Basiliae coniugi et Iuliae Noemae et Tiberio Iulio Stephano et Attiae Soteridi  Lucius Iulius Luci libertus Herma vivus fecit sibi et Terentiae Haidi coniugi
EDCS-29300118.translation: Lucius Folnius Favor, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for his wife Lollia Basilia, and for Julia Noema, and for Tiberius Julius Stephanus, and for Attia Soteris. Lucius Julius Herma, freedman of Lucius, in his lifetime, made this for himself and for his wife Terentia Haidi.
EDCS-29300118.summary: This is a double funerary monument, likely a tomb, prepared by two individuals, Lucius Folnius Favor and Lucius Julius Herma (a freedman), in their lifetimes for themselves, their wives, and other family members, indicating a common practice of pre-purchasing family burial plots during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300121.text_edition: defunct- annorum X mensium XI Claudia Fortunata frat(ri) b(ene)? m(erenti)
EDCS-29300121.translation: [To him who] died at 10 years and 11 months. Claudia Fortunata [made this for her] brother, well-deserving.
EDCS-29300121.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Fortunata to her well-deserving brother, who died at the age of 10 years and 11 months, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300122.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunatae Quintus conservae bene merenti
EDCS-29300122.translation: To the Divine Manes of Fortunata. Quintus [made this for his] fellow slave, well-deserving.
EDCS-29300122.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus to his well-deserving female fellow slave, Fortunata, highlighting a bond within the enslaved community, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300123.translation: [Someone] placed this for Fortunata Ridi Delpia, most devoted.
EDCS-29300123.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated to a very devoted woman named Fortunata Ridi Delpia by an unnamed individual, probably a family member, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300124.text_edition: Apronio Fronton?  ex HS CLmilibus nummum
EDCS-29300124.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or an epitaph, mentioning Apronius Frontonius and a substantial sum of 150,000 sesterces, which might indicate a public work, a bequest, or a significant financial contribution, probably dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300125.inscription_type: carmina, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29300125.text_edition: Fulloniae Amaryllidi aequius hunc fuerat titulum me ponere matri quem miserae mater illa mihi posuit bis ternos denos complevi mensibus annos Parcae crudeles nimium properastis rumpere fata mea
EDCS-29300125.translation: To Fullonia Amaryllis. It would have been more fitting for me to place this epitaph for my mother, which that mother, alas, placed for me. I completed two times three and ten years in months. Cruel Fates, you hastened too much to break my destiny.
EDCS-29300125.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from a daughter, who died young, to her mother Fullonia Amaryllis, lamenting the reversal of the natural order of life and death, where the mother had originally set up a tomb for her daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300127.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Gavio Benedicto et Statiae Hilarae Gavius Amabilis parentibus bene merentibus
EDCS-29300127.translation: To the Divine Manes of Titus Gavius Benedictus and Statia Hilara. Gavius Amabilis [made this] for his well-deserving parents.
EDCS-29300127.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gavius Amabilis to his well-deserving parents, Titus Gavius Benedictus and Statia Hilara, likely a tombstone or an altar, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300130.text_edition: ? Satrius Genneus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29300130.translation: [?] Satrius Genneus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29300130.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription by Satrius Genneus, dedicating a monument to his well-deserving wife, whose name is not preserved, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300131.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely dedicated to Glycera and her children, although the exact type of monument is not specified.

EDCS-29300135.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a woman named Gymnasia, although its exact purpose and date are unclear due to its incomplete state.

EDCS-29300139.text_edition: Dis Manibus Helviae Feliculae Caius Iulius Diadochus coniugi
EDCS-29300139.translation: To the Divine Manes of Helvia Felicula. Gaius Iulius Diadochus [erected this] for his wife.
EDCS-29300139.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Iulius Diadochus to his wife, Helvia Felicula.

EDCS-29300140.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hermeroti D D innocenti et infIelici#infelici#INIELICI nnorum XXV Baebilia Prima mater posuit
EDCS-29300140.translation: To the Divine Manes of Hermeros, innocent and unhappy, 25 years old. Baebilia Prima, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-29300140.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Baebilia Prima for her son, Hermeros, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-29300141.translation: To the Divine Manes. Hermes F. MAT.
EDCS-29300141.summary: This is a brief and fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Hermes, with his full identity or relationship unclear.

EDCS-29300142.text_edition: E Gaius Porcius Hermes coniugi rarissimae cum qua convixit annis XV
EDCS-29300142.translation: Gaius Porcius Hermes [erected this] for his most rare wife, with whom he lived for 15 years.
EDCS-29300142.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Porcius Hermes to his exceptional wife, commemorating their 15 years of shared life.

EDCS-29300144.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely honoring several freedmen named Hermes, Norbanus, and Symphorus, though its full context is lost.

EDCS-29300151.translation: On the fourth of January, to a well-deserving father...
EDCS-29300151.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, honoring a well-deserving father, with the date of January 4th possibly marking his death or dedication.

EDCS-29300153.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iasoni Grapthe sic et Agathangelis sorores fratri bene merenti fecerunt defuncto annorum XVI
EDCS-29300153.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iason. Grapthe and Agathangelis, his sisters, made this for their well-deserving brother, who died at 16 years of age.
EDCS-29300153.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Grapthe and Agathangelis for their brother Iason, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-29300154.translation: On the ninth day, to Iason, well-deserving.
EDCS-29300154.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely funerary, commemorating a well-deserving individual named Iason, possibly on the ninth day after his death or on the ninth day of a month.

EDCS-29300156.summary: This inscription records Irene dedicating something to the gods.

EDCS-29300160.text_edition: Dis Manibus nicius Philetus vivus fecit sibi et Osulniae Iucundae coniugi et libertis libertabusque in fronte pedes X in agro pedes
EDCS-29300160.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Nicius Philetus, while living, made this for himself and for Osulnia Iucunda, his wife, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. In the front, 10 feet; in the field, [X] feet.
EDCS-29300160.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Nicius Philetus creating a tomb for himself, his wife Osulnia Iucunda, and his freedmen and freedwomen, also specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-29300161.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Iulius, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-29300163.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary monument, where Iulius dedicated something to his brother, and Marcus is also mentioned.

EDCS-29300165.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Aristonico patri qui vixit annos LXV Iulius Aristonicus filius Iulia Viratia soror Viratia Hilara  bene merenti
EDCS-29300165.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Iulius Aristonicus, father, who lived 65 years. Iulius Aristonicus, son, Iulia Viratia, sister, Viratia Hilara [placed this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29300165.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Aristonicus, set up by his son Iulius Aristonicus, his sister Iulia Viratia, and Viratia Hilara.

EDCS-29300166.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius BeFuzas#Beuzas#BFUZAS Maxsimae sic Valeri matri pientissimae
EDCS-29300166.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Beuzas [placed this] for Maxsima, also for Valeri, his most devoted mother.
EDCS-29300166.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Beuzas to his most devoted mother, Maxsima, also mentioning Valeri.

EDCS-29300167.text_edition: VIN posuit sibi coniugi suae Iuli-- Calpurni
EDCS-29300167.translation: Vin[icius?] placed this for himself and his wife, Iuli[a] Calpurni[a].
EDCS-29300167.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a couple, where a man (possibly Vinicius) set up a tomb for himself and his wife, Iulia Calpurnia.

EDCS-29300168.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning parts of names like Iulius and other unclear terms, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-29300170.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Fortunato Helvius Sabinus fratri carissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29300170.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Helvius Sabinus placed this for his dearest brother Iulius Fortunatus, who well-deserved it.
EDCS-29300170.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Helvius Sabinus to his dearest brother, Iulius Fortunatus.

EDCS-29300173.text_edition: Dis Manibus Gaius Iulius Mercurialis Cantiae Surae obitae pientissimae M CON
EDCS-29300173.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Mercurialis [placed this] for Cantia Sura, his most devoted deceased [wife/relative]. M. Con[stantinus?].
EDCS-29300173.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius Iulius Mercurialis for Cantia Sura, described as most devoted and deceased, with an additional abbreviation M. Con.

EDCS-29300175.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Caio Iulio Tyrheno puero studiosissimo qui vixit annis XIII Caius tenius I Fortunat?
EDCS-29300175.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Gaius Iulius Tyrhenus, a most diligent boy who lived 13 years. Gaius Tenius Fortunatus [set this up].
EDCS-29300175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Tenius Fortunatus to Gaius Iulius Tyrhenus, a diligent boy who died at 13 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300176.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription for Gaius Iulius Zorii, likely a funerary dedication given the common pattern of such texts.

EDCS-29300178.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, preserving the name Iulia and some illegible characters, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-29300180.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Antigone Iulius Florentinus
EDCS-29300180.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Florentinus [set this up] for Iulia Antigone.
EDCS-29300180.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Florentinus to Iulia Antigone, likely a relative, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300181.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Fabriciae Stenius Lupulus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29300181.translation: To the Divine Manes. Stenius Lupulus set this up for Iulia Fabricia, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29300181.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Stenius Lupulus for his well-deserving wife, Iulia Fabricia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300182.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Felicissimae co(n)iugi incomparabili m Niceta maritus
EDCS-29300182.translation: To the Divine Manes. Niceta, her husband, [set this up] for Iulia Felicissima, his incomparable wife.
EDCS-29300182.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the husband Niceta to his incomparable wife, Iulia Felicissima, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300183.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Felicia posuit titulum Iulio Liberto annorum V mensium VII dierum V bene merenti
EDCS-29300183.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Felicia set up this titulus for Iulius Libertus, who lived 5 years, 7 months, and 5 days, a well-deserving individual.
EDCS-29300183.summary: This is a funerary inscription (titulus) erected by Iulia Felicia for Iulius Libertus, a well-deserving child who died at a young age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300184.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Feliculae Vettius Hermes coniugi
EDCS-29300184.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vettius Hermes [set this up] for Iulia Felicula, his wife.
EDCS-29300184.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vettius Hermes to his wife, Iulia Felicula, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300186.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Pudentilla et Iulia Ania matri dulcissimae
EDCS-29300186.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulia Pudentilla and Iulia Ania [set this up] for their sweetest mother.
EDCS-29300186.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulia Pudentilla and Iulia Ania to their beloved mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300187.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Secundae coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29300187.translation: To the Divine Manes. [Set up for] Iulia Secunda, a well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29300187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Secunda, a well-deserving wife, likely set up by her husband, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300188.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Silvinae nnorum XXIIII coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit viro suo annos XII Pomponius Afenodorus sic coniugi karissimae
EDCS-29300188.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Iulia Silvina, 24 years old, a most holy wife who lived with her husband for 12 years. Pomponius Afenodorus (dedicates this) thus to his dearest wife.
EDCS-29300188.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Pomponius Afenodorus to his deceased wife, Iulia Silvina, recording her age and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-29300189.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iuliae Ursinae matri pientissimae defunctae
EDCS-29300189.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Iulia Ursina, a most devoted mother, deceased.
EDCS-29300189.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Ursina, identifying her as a very devoted mother.

EDCS-29300190.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Zosimini matri MV EME
EDCS-29300190.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Iulia Zosimini, mother. MV EME.
EDCS-29300190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Zosimini, identifying her as a mother, with additional obscure abbreviations.

EDCS-29300194.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marco Iunio Clementiano Galliena Clementilla patri et Galliena Selene coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29300194.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Marcus Iunius Clementianus, father, and to Galliena Selene, most deserving wife (or for Galliena Clementilla, mother, and Galliena Selene, most deserving wife).
EDCS-29300194.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Iunius Clementianus (father) and Galliena Selene (wife) by Galliena Clementilla.

EDCS-29300195.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription preserving parts of two names, Iunius and possibly Terpnus.

EDCS-29300197.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuniae Epagathinari Iunilla et Martialis matri piissimae
EDCS-29300197.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Iunia Epagathina. Iunilla and Martialis (dedicate this) to their most devoted mother.
EDCS-29300197.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iunilla and Martialis to their deceased, most devoted mother, Iunia Epagathina.

EDCS-29300198.text_edition: o et Iuniae Romulae parentibus pientissimis posu---
EDCS-29300198.translation: [...] and to Iunia Romula, most devoted parents. They placed this [---].
EDCS-29300198.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Iunia Romula and another unnamed individual, identified as most devoted parents.

EDCS-29300199.translation: To the Divine Spirits. VN Tertull-- Valerius? V.
EDCS-29300199.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription with some names and abbreviations that are largely illegible or incomplete.

EDCS-29300200.text_edition: Dis Manibus matri Iustae filius eius incomparabili posuit
EDCS-29300200.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To mother Iusta, her son placed this for his incomparable mother.
EDCS-29300200.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a son to his incomparable deceased mother, Iusta.

EDCS-29300201.text_edition: Dis Manibus NIIINT VIAEDVS C VRAV M V  bene merenti posu---
EDCS-29300201.translation: To the Divine Spirits. NIIINT VIAEDVS CURAV M V, most deserving, placed this [---].
EDCS-29300201.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a deserving individual, with the dedicator's name largely illegible.

EDCS-29300203.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory text, mentioning a son and names like Sergia and Aeto.

EDCS-29300204.text_edition: Dis Manibus Leoni conservo qui vixit nnos plus minus XXXX Maritima coniugi posuit
EDCS-29300204.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Leo, my fellow slave, who lived about 40 years. Maritima placed this for her husband.
EDCS-29300204.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Maritima for her husband, Leo, who was a fellow slave and died around 40 years of age.

EDCS-29300205.text_edition: Lucius Calenius Luci filius Libo Caius FITICETEA IIIIIX N
EDCS-29300205.translation: Lucius Calenius, son of Lucius, Libo. Gaius FITICETEA 9 N.
EDCS-29300205.summary: This is a partially preserved inscription, possibly a funerary monument or public record, mentioning Lucius Calenius Libo and Gaius.

EDCS-29300206.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name label on a monument or object, identifying Publius Licinius Erastus.

EDCS-29300208.text_edition: Dis Manibus Licinia Etnio Cereali marito infelicissimo
EDCS-29300208.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Licinia Etnio for her most unfortunate husband, Cerealis.
EDCS-29300208.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Licinia Etnio to her late husband, Cerealis.

EDCS-29300209.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription from Tarconius to Licinia Stephana.

EDCS-29300212.text_edition: Lollio Pastori Titia Donata coniugi pientissimo
EDCS-29300212.translation: To Lollius Pastor. Titia Donata for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-29300212.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Titia Donata for her deeply devoted husband, Lollius Pastor.

EDCS-29300213.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Longinia Victoria coniugi bene merenti posuit Publio Censorio Civili
EDCS-29300213.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Longinia Victoria placed this for her well-deserving husband, Publius Censorus Civilis.
EDCS-29300213.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Longinia Victoria to her esteemed husband, Publius Censorus Civilis.

EDCS-29300214.text_edition: VX annis  Lucius Lucilius V Cornelia Pa parentes filio
EDCS-29300214.translation: Years. Lucius Lucilius V. Cornelia Pa, parents, for their son.
EDCS-29300214.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Lucius Lucilius and Cornelia Pa to their son, mentioning his age in years.

EDCS-29300215.text_edition: Frugi Caio Lucilio iae Cai filiae Secundae anio Quinti filio Gal
EDCS-29300215.translation: Frugi. To Gaius Lucilius. To Caius' daughter, Secunda. To Quinti's son, Gal[...]
EDCS-29300215.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a family funerary monument, mentioning Frugi, Gaius Lucilius, Secunda (daughter of Caius), and a son of Quintus.

EDCS-29300218.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a mother, E. Lupcorum, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-29300219.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning the name Lupercia, likely the deceased or the dedicant.

EDCS-29300220.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lusia Victoria posuit karo coniugi Tito Fabio Euticheti sic qui fuit annorum XXX
EDCS-29300220.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lusia Victoria placed this for her dear husband, Titus Fabius Eutiches, who lived for thirty years.
EDCS-29300220.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lusia Victoria for her beloved husband, Titus Fabius Eutiches, who died at the age of 30, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-29300221.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to Lucius Lutatius Restutus, possibly for his genius or a local deity, but the text is fragmentary.

EDCS-29300226.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, only preserving part of a name, 'Marcel', and other illegible characters.

EDCS-29300227.summary: This inscription fragment refers to someone who lived for ten years and mentions Marcellinus as their brother, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-29300228.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, indicating that an heir set up a monument for a deceased individual.

EDCS-29300229.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a son and someone deserving, likely from a funerary context.

EDCS-29300230.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Mariae Secundinae defunctae annorum VII mensium V Marius Secundus alumnae infelicissimae
EDCS-29300230.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Maria Secundina, deceased, who lived for 7 years and 5 months. Marius Secundus (set this up) for his most unfortunate foster-daughter.
EDCS-29300230.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marius Secundus to his tragically deceased foster-daughter, Maria Secundina, who died at the young age of seven years and five months, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-29300235.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, preserving only parts of words like 'Maxim' and 'day', making its purpose unclear.

EDCS-29300236.text_edition: Maximinianus coniugi suae ne bene merenti memoria posuit locum concessum Artoriam FErontinMam#Frontinam#ERONTIMAM
EDCS-29300236.translation: Maximinianus placed this memorial for his well-deserving wife, Artoria Frontina, on a granted plot.
EDCS-29300236.summary: This is a tombstone recording a husband's dedication of a memorial to his deceased wife on a plot of land that was granted for the purpose.

EDCS-29300237.text_edition: Memmiae Rufinae filiae annorum Memmia  mater posuit
EDCS-29300237.translation: Memmia, the mother, placed this for her daughter, Memmia Rufina, who lived for [---] years.
EDCS-29300237.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother's dedication to her deceased daughter.

EDCS-29300238.text_edition: Dis Manibus Messorinae filiae incomparavili sic defunctae nnorum VII inMfelicissimi#infelicissimi#IMFE parentes posuIerunt#posuerunt#POSIERUNT
EDCS-29300238.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The most unfortunate parents placed this for their incomparable daughter, Messorina, who died at the age of 7.
EDCS-29300238.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a tombstone by grieving parents to their young daughter, Messorina, who died at age seven.

EDCS-29300240.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Narcissus Septimae conservae defunctae nnorum plus minus XXX bene merenti
EDCS-29300240.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Narcissus [placed this] for his well-deserving fellow slave, Septima, who died at approximately 30 years of age.
EDCS-29300240.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Narcissus to his fellow slave, Septima, who lived to be around 30 years old.

EDCS-29300243.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Nicodromus Lupulae coniugi infelicissimae
EDCS-29300243.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Nicodromus [placed this] for his most unfortunate wife, Lupula.
EDCS-29300243.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Nicodromus to his deceased wife, Lupula.

EDCS-29300246.text_edition: Nonia Felete sic mater et Hylla pater Annio Taurioni filio nnrum VI infelicis sic locus datus a Fabricia Ianuaria
EDCS-29300246.translation: Nonia Felete, the mother, and Hylla, the father, placed this for their unfortunate son, Annius Taurio, who lived for 6 years. This plot was given by Fabricia Ianuaria.
EDCS-29300246.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording parents' dedication to their young son, Annius Taurio, and noting that the burial plot was provided by Fabricia Ianuaria.

EDCS-29300247.text_edition: Dis Manibus l Nonnae VAE et Fortunatae  TI
EDCS-29300247.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed of Nonna [---] and Fortunata [---].
EDCS-29300247.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Nonna and Fortunata.

EDCS-29300248.text_edition: Publio Novio Platoni Menandri liberto Philippus Balbini amico bene merenti
EDCS-29300248.translation: Philippus Balbini [placed this] for Publius Novius Plato, freedman of Menander, his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-29300248.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Philippus Balbini to his friend, Publius Novius Plato, a freedman of Menander.

EDCS-29300249.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli possessionis, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29300249.text_edition: Numeriae Irenini matri dulcissimae Coelius Euhippius posuit
EDCS-29300249.translation: Coelius Euhippius placed this for Numeria Ireninis, his most sweet mother.
EDCS-29300249.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a son's dedication to his deceased mother, Numeria Ireninis.

EDCS-29300251.text_edition: NFIDIO S qui vixit annos III parentes filio posuerunt
EDCS-29300251.translation: The parents placed this for their son, [---]fidius, who lived for 3 years.
EDCS-29300251.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication of a monument by parents to their young son who lived for three years.

EDCS-29300253.translation: [...] to Claudius Ap[---], to Titus Octavius [---].
EDCS-29300253.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a dedication, mentioning the names Claudius Ap[---] and Titus Octavius [---].

EDCS-29300254.text_edition: Lucius Oct(avius) Onesimus  Iuliae Primae coniugi bene merenti se vivo posuit
EDCS-29300254.translation: Lucius Octavius Onesimus, while still alive, placed this for Julia Prima, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29300254.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Octavius Onesimus erecting a monument for his wife, Julia Prima, while he was still living, indicating a pre-mortem dedication.

EDCS-29300255.translation: Gaius Octavius Rufus, freedman of Gaius.

EDCS-29400081.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning the name Iulianus.

EDCS-29500189.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that parents erected a monument for their child, Paternus.

EDCS-29500190.text_edition: Publius Decimius Gratus vivus fecitsibi et libertis libertabusq(ue) suis  loco empto in fronte pedes XXII in agro pedes XVIII  hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-29500190.translation: Publius Decimius Gratus made this during his lifetime for himself and for his freedmen and freedwomen. The plot was bought, 22 feet in front and 18 feet deep in the field. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-29500190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Decimius Gratus, who prepared a tomb for himself and his freed dependents during his lifetime, explicitly stating it would not be inherited, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500194.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Petronia, who lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Satrenius Eutyches placed this for his little native-born daughter.

EDCS-29500195.text_edition: Dis Manibus  ? Philadelpho ? ann XXX VNO DE

EDCS-29500195.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Philadelphus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-29500196.text_edition: Phileto Salvia Sollemnis marito bene merenti
EDCS-29500196.translation: Salvia Sollemnis (placed this) for Philetus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29500196.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Salvia Sollemnis to her deserving husband, Philetus.

EDCS-29500199.text_edition: Dis Manibus Philumeno defuncto annorum XXV

EDCS-29500202.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Pinnio Ianuario defuncto nnorum XXX Papiria Musa marito piissimo bene merenti

EDCS-29500206.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Plaetoria Ursina vixit annos
EDCS-29500206.translation: To the Divine Manes. Plaetoria Ursina lived for years.
EDCS-29500206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plaetoria Ursina, likely erected by a relative or loved one, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500207.text_edition: CILI SI et Luci- Po F PRAES
EDCS-29500207.translation: CILI SI and Luci- Po F PRAES

EDCS-29500210.translation: Pomponia Lupa, daughter of IDAE FIIO (or dedicated to her son).
EDCS-29500210.summary: This is a funerary inscription for or by Pomponia Lupa, with an unclear filiation or dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500211.text_edition: Pomponiae IIE Pomponiae Semne et Quarta matri et Pomponiusus
EDCS-29500211.translation: To Pomponia IIE, to Pomponia Semne and Quarta her mother, and Pomponiusus.
EDCS-29500211.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely dedicated to several female members of the Pomponia family, including Pomponia Semne and her mother Quarta, and possibly a male Pomponius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500212.text_edition: O - Popilius Philus coniugi annorum XXIV
EDCS-29500212.translation: Popilius Philus to his wife of 24 years.
EDCS-29500212.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Popilius Philus for his wife, noting her age at death as 24 years, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500213.text_edition: M Pothinae vixit nnos XVII - Collius Pothinus filiae pientissimae
EDCS-29500213.translation: To M. Pothina, who lived 17 years. Collius Pothinus to his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-29500213.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Collius Pothinus to his deceased daughter, M. Pothina, who lived 17 years, expressing his devotion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500215.text_edition: $ Aure]liae Primulae coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29500215.translation: He placed this for his well-deserving wife, Aurelia Primula.
EDCS-29500215.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by an unnamed individual for his wife, Aurelia Primula, whom he praises as well-deserving, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500216.translation: Titus Priminius Potus, her husband, lived.
EDCS-29500216.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription, possibly for Titus Priminius Potus, or mentioning his survival in the context of his wife's death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500217.text_edition: Dis Manibus Primitio et Primitae Primus et Callistus amicis bene merentibus

EDCS-29500218.translation: To A. Primitivus, so well deserving.

EDCS-29500219.translation: To Primitiva, Primitiva, llien-- Valerius-- her foster-child.

EDCS-29500220.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary dedication, mentioning a son named [---]mi and a woman named Prisca.

EDCS-29500221.text_edition: Proba filiae infelicissimae parentes posuerunt defunctae nnrum XX
EDCS-29500221.translation: To their most unfortunate daughter Proba, her parents erected this, she having died at [---] years of age.
EDCS-29500221.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents for their most unfortunate daughter Proba, who died at a young age, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500222.text_edition: Proculae filiae et Caio Caesio Donato et Autonoe vernulae

EDCS-29500222.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, likely by a family, commemorating their daughter Procula, Gaius Caesius Donatus, and their household slave Autonoe.

EDCS-29500224.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Publicio Asclepio coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29500224.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Titus Publicius Asclepius, my well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29500224.summary: This is a funerary dedication, set up by a spouse for her well-deserving husband, Titus Publicius Asclepius, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500225.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Publicius Trophimus heres institutus ab Valeria Philete uxore mea in memoriam karae coniugis et mi posui stilibatam causa arae viatoribus bene concordes vivite vivendo fit minus una dies commemora tecum nil stomachandi haec nobis domus perpetua
EDCS-29500225.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. I, Lucius Publicius Trophimus, the heir appointed by my wife Valeria Philete, erected this for my dear spouse, and for myself, a stilibata (a specific type of monument, likely a column or pillar) in the form of an altar for travelers. Live in good harmony; by living, one day less remains. Remember with yourself to grumble at nothing. This house is perpetual for us.
EDCS-29500225.summary: This is a complex funerary monument, likely a tomb with an altar-like structure, erected by Lucius Publicius Trophimus for his wife Valeria Philete and for himself, including a philosophical message for passersby, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500228.text_edition: Dis Manibus ianiae Quarta matri - bene merenti
EDCS-29500228.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Iania Quarta, my well-deserving mother.
EDCS-29500228.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Iania Quarta, a well-deserving mother, likely erected by her child, probably in the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500229.text_edition: Quartae  vixit annos XXVI C MAR
EDCS-29500229.translation: To Quarta, who lived 26 years. C. MAR.
EDCS-29500229.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a woman named Quarta, who lived 26 years, with the dedicator or family indicated by the initials C. MAR., likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500230.text_edition: Quintae coniugi pientissimae Verna maritus posuit bene merenti
EDCS-29500230.translation: To Quintae, his most devoted wife, her husband Verna erected this, she being well-deserving.
EDCS-29500230.summary: This is a funerary dedication erected by Verna for his most devoted and well-deserving wife, Quintae, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500234.text_edition: lius Publi filius Restutus vivus sibi fecit
EDCS-29500234.translation: lius Restutus, son of Publius, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-29500234.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that a man named [---]lius Restutus, son of Publius, prepared his own tomb during his lifetime.

EDCS-29500235.text_edition: Dis Manibus Romula mater Amantillae filiae infelicissimae
EDCS-29500235.translation: To the Divine Manes. Romula, a most unhappy mother, [erected this] for her daughter Amantilla.
EDCS-29500235.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a grieving mother, Romula, to the Divine Manes for her deceased daughter, Amantilla.

EDCS-29500238.text_edition: Finis loci qui est Quinti S C in fronte secus flumen pedes XXXXV item secus monimentum sic in agro pedes XXXXII item a capite sinistro longum pedes XLVII semis item ab aquilone in agro pedes XVI at quem locum iter ambitum actum aditum sacrificiique causa quibus esse oportet oportebit D AP HOR
EDCS-29500238.translation: Boundary of the place belonging to Quintus S. C., forty-five feet on the front along the river, likewise along the monument. In the field, forty-two feet. Likewise, from the left end, forty-seven and a half feet long. Likewise from the north in the field, sixteen feet. To which place there is a path, a circuit, a passage, and access for the purpose of sacrifice, for those who must or will have it. D. AP. HOR.
EDCS-29500238.summary: This inscription precisely defines the boundaries and access rights of a specific plot of land belonging to Quintus S. C., likely for religious or funerary purposes, given the mention of sacrifices.

EDCS-29500241.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely naming a Gaius Sa[---], and providing only the initial letters of other names or words.

EDCS-29500244.text_edition: Dis Manibus SatIria#Satria#SAIRIA Flora Onesimeni NVMM DE
EDCS-29500244.translation: To the Divine Manes. Satria Flora, of Onesimus. [---] NVMM DE [---]
EDCS-29500244.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Manes for a woman named Satria Flora, presumably the freedwoman of Onesimus, with additional fragmentary text.

EDCS-29500258.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a name associated with 'Septimius' and preserving only a few letters.

EDCS-29500277.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Titius Charelaos, set up by Aurelia Pudens, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29500286.text_edition: Lucio Titio Iucundo M Titia GClyicenna#Glyicenna#CLYICENNA patrono suo fecit

EDCS-29500289.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titia Secunda.

EDCS-29500293.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Tyche ATI AI.

EDCS-29500295.translation: [To the Divine Spirits of Prude]ntia Valentina, who died.

EDCS-29500299.text_edition: Manibus Dis  Valerio Frontoni veterano defuncto annorum LXXX Claudia Nigrina coniugi carissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29500299.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Valerius Fronto, a veteran, who died at 80 years of age. Claudia Nigrina placed this for her dearest and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29500299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Nigrina to her husband, Valerius Fronto, a veteran who lived to eighty years old.

EDCS-29500301.text_edition: Valeriae defunctae nnorum IIII dierum XLV Marcus Aurelius Seneca delicatae

EDCS-29500301.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Marcus Aurelius Seneca for Valeria, a child who died very young, likely his daughter or a beloved dependant.

EDCS-29500303.text_edition: Dis Manibus  incomparabili feminae Valeriae Feliculae coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit annos AI
EDCS-29500303.translation: To the Divine Shades. For the incomparable woman, Valeria Felicula, my most sacred wife, who lived 41 years.
EDCS-29500303.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Valeria Felicula by her husband, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29500304.text_edition: Valeria Trofime Flavio Victori marito pientissimo et sibi
EDCS-29500304.translation: Valeria Trophime [made this] for Flavius Victor, her most devoted husband, and for herself.
EDCS-29500304.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a tomb, set up by Valeria Trophime for her husband, Flavius Victor, and reserving a place for herself.

EDCS-29500305.text_edition: AMII V -- defunct- annorum XXIII Valeria Valerilla fili-
EDCS-29500305.translation: [To the Divine Shades...] for Valeria Valerilla, her daughter, who died at 23 years old.
EDCS-29500305.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated by Valeria Valerilla's parents to their daughter, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-29500306.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucius Varius Euhodus CE
EDCS-29500306.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Varius Euhodus.
EDCS-29500306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Varius Euhodus, likely a freedman, set up by an unspecified dedicant.

EDCS-29500307.text_edition: Dis Manibus   Variae  libertae lius posuit bene merenti
EDCS-29500307.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Varia, his freedwoman, ...lius set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29500307.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Varia, a freedwoman, set up by her former master, whose name ends in '-lius'.

EDCS-29500309.text_edition: Dis Manibus Varroni nnorum X-VIIII mensium VI Carpia Apra mater filio infelicissimo
EDCS-29500309.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Varrō, 19 years and 6 months old. Carpia Apra, his mother, [made this] for her most unfortunate son.
EDCS-29500309.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Carpia Apra, a grieving mother, to her young son, Varrō, who died at 19 years and 6 months old.

EDCS-29500313.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vedio Secundino Aulus Vedius A CaeIcilia#Caecilia#CAICILIA Fortunata parentes  nnorum III mensium IIII infelicissimi
EDCS-29500313.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Vedius Secundinus, Aulus Vedius and Caecilia Fortunata, his most unfortunate parents, [made this for their son] 3 years and 4 months old.
EDCS-29500313.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vedius Secundinus, a young child who died at the age of 3 years and 4 months, set up by his grieving parents, Aulus Vedius and Caecilia Fortunata.

EDCS-29500314.text_edition: Cnaeus Veiedius Iuvenalis vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Stochadi coniugi et Iuliae Theonoe sorori eius et Cnaeo Veiedio Cnaei filio Maximo nnorum XIIX
EDCS-29500314.translation: Gnaeus Veiedius Iuvenalis made this during his lifetime for himself and for Iulia Stochas, his wife, and for Iulia Theonoe, her sister, and for Gnaeus Veiedius Maximus, son of Gnaeus, 18 years old.
EDCS-29500314.summary: This is a funerary monument made during his lifetime by Gnaeus Veiedius Iuvenalis for himself, his wife Iulia Stochas, her sister Iulia Theonoe, and his son Gnaeus Veiedius Maximus, who died at 18 years old.

EDCS-29500315.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Velius Mescenius Posidonius Mesceniae Tycheitini coniugi pientissimae bene merenti
EDCS-29500315.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Velius Mescenius Posidonius [made this] for Mescenia Tycheitina, his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29500315.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Velius Mescenius Posidonius to his beloved and deserving wife, Mescenia Tycheitina.

EDCS-29500321.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Tito Vettio Tertullino  Coelius Gemellinus amico incomparabili
EDCS-29500321.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Titus Vettius Tertullinus, Coelius Gemellinus [made this] for his incomparable friend.
EDCS-29500321.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Coelius Gemellinus to his incomparable friend, Titus Vettius Tertullinus.

EDCS-29500323.text_edition: eculatori legionis XIIII Pontius Viator fratri bene merenti
EDCS-29500323.translation: To the *speculator* of the Fourteenth Legion, Pontius Viator [set this up] for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-29500323.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a brother, Pontius Viator, dedicated to his well-deserving brother, a *speculator* (scout or orderly) of the Legio XIIII.

EDCS-29500325.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Vibio Firmo qui et Artorius Caius Vibius F
EDCS-29500325.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Gaius Vibius Firmus, also known as Artorius. Gaius Vibius F[---].
EDCS-29500325.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Vibius Firmus, who also went by the name Artorius, set up by Gaius Vibius F[...], likely a relative, around the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-29500327.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Vibi[---].
EDCS-29500327.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to an individual named Vibi[...].

EDCS-29500328.text_edition: Vibia S  fecit sibi et Himero filio
EDCS-29500328.translation: Vibia S[---] made this for herself and for Himerus, her son.
EDCS-29500328.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Vibia S[...] for herself and her son Himerus.

EDCS-29500329.text_edition: vixit AVI item vixit nnos XVI  Vibullis
EDCS-29500329.translation: He lived 6 years; likewise, he lived 16 years. Vibullis [---].
EDCS-29500329.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for two individuals, or referring to different periods of life, recording ages of 6 and 16 years, and mentioning 'Vibullis'.

EDCS-29500332.text_edition: Victorino defuncto annorum VI dierum XV Iulianus et Ca
EDCS-29500332.translation: For Victorinus, who died at 6 years and 15 days of age. Julianus and Ca[---].
EDCS-29500332.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Victorinus, who died at a young age, set up by Julianus and Ca[...], likely his parents or relatives.

EDCS-29500333.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Victorinus C[...], stating that he lived, but the age is missing.

EDCS-29500335.text_edition: Lucio Vipsanio Luci filio Valenti defuncto nnorum XLVII Lucius Vipsanius Valens filius et Baebidia Balbina uxor bene merenti
EDCS-29500335.translation: To Lucius Vipsanius Valens, son of Lucius, who died at 47 years of age. Lucius Vipsanius Valens, his son, and Baebidia Balbina, his wife, [set this up] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-29500335.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Vipsanius Valens, who died at 47, set up by his son Lucius Vipsanius Valens and his wife Baebidia Balbina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500339.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vivio Privationi Papinia Tyche coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29500339.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Vivius Privatio. Papinia Tyche [set this up] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29500339.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vivius Privatio by his wife Papinia Tyche, who describes him as well-deserving.

EDCS-29500340.text_edition: Dis Manibus Viviae Secundinae Iulius Polynices EC
EDCS-29500340.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Vivia Secundina. Iulius Polynices EC[---].
EDCS-29500340.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vivia Secundina, set up by Iulius Polynices, with an abbreviated final word or title.

EDCS-29500343.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpio Dacioni Aurelia Victorina marito bene merenti posuit
EDCS-29500343.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Victorina set this up for Ulpius Dacionus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-29500343.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Victorina for her deceased husband, Ulpius Dacionus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29500344.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, recording parts of names, Ulpius, son of Lucius, and Domitius, of uncertain date.

EDCS-29500345.text_edition: Vibullius T legionis VII et Lucius Salvius Marci Sueto centuriones legionis XI iudices dati ex conMventione#conventione#MVENTIONE a Lucio Volusio Saturnino legato pro praetore Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici inter  RV
EDCS-29500345.translation: Vibullius T., of the Seventh Legion, and Lucius Salvius, son of Marcus Sueto, centurions of the Eleventh Legion, appointed as judges by agreement by Lucius Volusius Saturninus, propraetorian legate of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, between RV.
EDCS-29500345.summary: This inscription records the appointment of two centurions, Vibullius and Lucius Salvius, as judges by Lucius Volusius Saturninus, a legate of the emperor Gaius (Caligula), likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-29600214.translation: NAM Alexander I, minister of God.
EDCS-29600214.summary: This is a brief inscription likely commemorating Alexander I as a minister of God, suggesting a Christian context and an unknown date.

EDCS-29600222.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600222.text_edition: Aurelius Secundus qui cunparabid sic ab Aurelio Alexsio sic piscinam at sic dua corpura sic deponenda meum et coniugem meam Renatam et nefas quadrabRit#quadrabit#QUADRARIT nobis parentibus ut pon=VReremus#poneremus#PVREREMU filiam nostram in hanc piscinam sane coiiurabit sic ut supra birginiam sic suam null[lum aliud corpus inferatur &
EDCS-29600222.translation: Aurelius Secundus, who bought this basin from Aurelius Alexsius, to deposit two bodies, mine and my wife Renata's; and should it happen that our daughter, whom we, her parents, placed in this basin, swore by her virginity, that no other body shall be brought in here.
EDCS-29600222.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a funerary basin by Aurelius Secundus for himself, his wife Renata, and their daughter, including a unique oath to prevent further burials, indicating a Christian context and a late Roman date.

EDCS-29600231.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600231.text_edition: Euassu in pace Valeria coniugi suo fecit memoria annis vixit LX
EDCS-29600231.translation: Euassus made this in peace for his wife Valeria, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-29600231.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Euassus for his wife Valeria, who lived 60 years, suggesting a Christian context due to the phrase 'in peace' and a late Roman date.

EDCS-29600237.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600237.text_edition: Flavius Virgilianus qui bixit sic annus sic XXX et Aurelia Ursilla oxor sic eius qui se vivi sibi urdenaverunt sic hunc sarcTofagum#sarcofagum#SARTOFAGUM si quis autem voluerit super haec duo corpora punere sic inferetH#inferet#INFEREH sanctae ecclisiae sic argenti libras quinquem
EDCS-29600237.translation: Flavius Virgilianus, who lived 30 years, and Aurelia Ursilla, his wife, who while living ordered this sarcophagus for themselves. If anyone wishes to place any other bodies above these two, they shall pay five pounds of silver to the holy church.
EDCS-29600237.summary: This inscription records the pre-purchase of a sarcophagus by Flavius Virgilianus and Aurelia Ursilla for themselves, with a penalty clause for illicit interments, indicating a Christian context and a late Roman date.

EDCS-29600238.text_edition: Flaviae infanti dulcissimae quae sana mente salutifero die Paschae gloriosi fontis gratiam consecuta est super vixitque post baptismum sanctum mensibus quinque vixit nnos III menses X dies VII Flavianus et Archelais parenteIs#parentes#PARENTIS filiae piissimae depositio XV Kalendas Septembres
EDCS-29600238.translation: To Flavia, the sweetest infant, who, with sound mind, on the saving day of glorious Easter, received the grace of the font, and lived after holy baptism for five months. She lived 3 years, 10 months, 7 days. Flavianus and Archelais, parents, to their most devoted daughter. Deposition: 15th Kalends of September (August 18).
EDCS-29600238.summary: This is a Christian funerary inscription from Flavianus and Archelais for their daughter Flavia, commemorating her baptism and short life, with a precise date of death (August 18th), likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-29600240.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600240.text_edition: Piscina Flavi Sabbati Flavia Sexta compari et Flavia Regulia fE(ilia)#f(ilia)#E sua qui communiter consentientes comparaverunt
EDCS-29600240.translation: The basin of Flavius Sabbatus. Flavia Sexta, his companion, and Flavia Regulia, his daughter, who jointly agreed to purchase this.
EDCS-29600240.summary: This inscription records the purchase of a funerary basin by Flavius Sabbatus, his companion Flavia Sexta, and his daughter Flavia Regulia, suggesting a late Roman and likely Christian context.

EDCS-29600241.summary: This is a very short dedicatory inscription by Gaudentius, simply stating 'giving thanks', suggesting a religious context and an unknown date.

EDCS-29600245.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the memory of an individual named Iohannes, likely on a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-29600248.text_edition: ius Iubentius sic patri bene merenti
EDCS-29600248.translation: Iubentius thus [erected this] for his deserving father.
EDCS-29600248.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Iubentius to his well-deserving father, suggesting a filial act of remembrance.

EDCS-29600249.text_edition: Viventiae coniugi suae dilectissimae et sibi Valerius Felix depositus VII Idus Novembres qui vixit annos LXV quod si quis eam arcam post obitum eius aperire voluerit inferre debebit ecclesiae de(nariorum) folles mille in qua sunt filiae Graecina et Proculina
EDCS-29600249.translation: For Viventia, his most beloved wife, and for himself, Valerius Felix. He was buried on the 7th day before the Ides of November, having lived 65 years. If anyone wishes to open this sarcophagus after his death, they must pay to the church one thousand folles of denarii. Within it are his daughters Graecina and Proculina.
EDCS-29600249.summary: This is a funerary inscription and will, likely from a sarcophagus, recording the burial of Valerius Felix and his wife Viventia, specifying a penalty for disturbing the tomb and naming their daughters.

EDCS-29600258.summary: This is a brief and affectionate funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, bidding Palladi to rest in peace.

EDCS-29600262.text_edition: Deposita Petronia die III Kalendas Augustas  Depositus Nereus die II Nonas Augustas  Deposita Sofronia die XII Kalendas Iulias  Pro nefas iste tuum loquitur Petronia nomen iam titulus nono vixdum lacrimabilis anno nata diu dulcis et longi pignus amoris vita brevis sed nunc istic iam longa parentum vulnera Nereus genitor genetrixque funesti Sofronia brevibus tumuli flevere querellis tu tamen hinc spera caelum pia mente fidelis
EDCS-29600262.translation: Petronia was laid to rest on the 3rd day before the Kalends of August. Nereus was laid to rest on the 2nd day before the Nones of August. Sofronia was laid to rest on the 12th day before the Kalends of July. O shame! Petronia, this title of yours speaks, a tearful monument barely in its ninth year, long-born sweet pledge of a long love, a short life, but now here a long wound for her parents. Nereus the father and Sofronia the mother, mournful, wept with brief complaints at the tomb. But you, faithful, hope for heaven with a pious mind.
EDCS-29600262.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription commemorating the burials of Petronia, Nereus, and Sofronia, expressing the parents' grief for Petronia and offering a message of hope.

EDCS-29600267.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600267.text_edition: XNI arcam I et Aureli-- S
EDCS-29600267.translation: XNI, sarcophagus I and Aureli... S...
EDCS-29600267.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely related to a sarcophagus and possibly mentioning individuals named Aurelius or related to the family, with other illegible details.

EDCS-29600271.text_edition: rit Valeri i Valer et Valeria Merope filia annorum V
EDCS-29600271.translation: ...Valerius, and Valerius and Valeria Merope, daughter, 5 years old.
EDCS-29600271.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely from a family tomb, mentioning Valerius and his 5-year-old daughter, Valeria Merope.

EDCS-29600272.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29600272.text_edition: Valeria Hermogenia honesta femina die V Kalendarum Iuniarum quinquagesimo octavo anno finita est viva se arcam de suo poni iussit
EDCS-29600272.translation: Valeria Hermogenia, an honorable woman, finished her life on the 5th day before the Kalends of June, in her fifty-eighth year. While alive, she ordered this sarcophagus to be placed at her own expense.
EDCS-29600272.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Hermogenia, an honorable woman who, while still alive, arranged for her own sarcophagus to be placed, recording her age and date of death.

EDCS-29601484.text_edition: Praecipio libertis libertabusque meis nequis vestrum in hunc monumentum corpus aut ossa exterae inferat si quis contemserit sic tunc conlibertis dare devebet sic poenae nominae sic |(denarios) miliae sic
EDCS-29601484.translation: I instruct my freedmen and freedwomen that none of you shall place the body or bones of a stranger in this monument. If anyone disregards this, then they must give to my fellow freedmen a fine of one thousand denarii.
EDCS-29601484.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a private tomb, containing a testamentary clause prohibiting the burial of strangers and stipulating a monetary penalty for any violation.

EDCS-29601485.text_edition: IMAI vivi fecerunt sibi et filiis suis pientissimis quot si di aequi fuissent illi nobis facere debuerant parentibus infelicissimis quod heredum non fiet
EDCS-29601485.translation: We, while living, made this for ourselves and for our most dutiful children, who, had the gods been just, ought to have made it for us, their most unfortunate parents, a task that will not be done by heirs.
EDCS-29601485.summary: This is a funerary monument made by parents for themselves and their pious children while still alive, expressing a poignant lament that their children's untimely deaths meant the usual filial duty of tomb construction was reversed.

EDCS-29601486.text_edition: III Kalendas Iulias  VVMVV NVS III VII pientIissimo#pientissimo#PIENIISSIMO maritIo#marito#MARIIO  dis Manibus
EDCS-29601486.translation: The third day before the Kalends of July... to my most devoted husband... to the divine Manes.
EDCS-29601486.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, likely for a husband, mentioning the date of the third day before the Kalends of July, and is addressed to the Manes.

EDCS-29601503.text_edition: RO o fili VI E lviae  tae C  bene merent---
EDCS-29601503.translation: Ro... son... of Elvia... to the well-deserving...
EDCS-29601503.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a funerary dedication, possibly by a son, to a well-deserving individual named Elvia.

EDCS-29601505.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sulliae Maximae defunctae annorum plus minus XXXV Sullius Felix
EDCS-29601505.translation: To the divine Manes. To Sullia Maxima, deceased, aged about 35 years. Sullius Felix.
EDCS-29601505.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Sullius Felix for Sullia Maxima, who died at approximately 35 years of age, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-29601506.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely a dedication by or to Metia concerning a foster child.

EDCS-29601519.text_edition: D O QV RIVRI ILIS coniugi pro meritis
EDCS-29601519.translation: To O. Qurius, to my deserving husband.
EDCS-29601519.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a wife to her deserving husband, O. Qurius.

EDCS-29601523.text_edition: SEL HOSPI  patrono VO III posuit
EDCS-29601523.translation: Sel. Hospi... to the patron... placed...
EDCS-29601523.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Sel. Hospi to a patron.

EDCS-29601524.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to a well-deserving spouse.

EDCS-29601529.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, preserving only the word 'friend'.

EDCS-29601571.text_edition: Septimius Maximianus vir perfectissimus principalis coloniae Salon(itanorum) vivo sibi et coniugi suae dulcissimae posuit qui vicsit sic annis LXV depositus die III Nonas Februarias
EDCS-29601571.translation: Septimius Maximianus, most perfect man, principal of the colony of Salona, placed this for himself while living and for his sweetest wife, who lived for 65 years, buried on the 3rd day before the Nones of February.
EDCS-29601571.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Septimius Maximianus, a high-ranking official of Salona, for himself and his wife, who died at 65 and was buried on February 3rd.

EDCS-29700325.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursioni defuncto annorum XXIII parentes posuerunt
EDCS-29700325.translation: To the divine Manes. To Ursio, deceased, aged 23 years. His parents placed this.
EDCS-29700325.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes by parents for their son Ursio, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-29900010.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Eponae Regin(a)e Genio loci Publius Bennius Egregius miles cohortis voluntariorum adiutor principis beneficiarius consularis votum solvit l(ibens)
EDCS-29900010.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, to Epona Regina, and to the Genius of the place, Publius Bennius Egregius, a soldier of the cohort of volunteers, assistant to the *princeps*, *beneficiarius consularis*, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900010.summary: This is a religious dedication made by a Roman soldier to several deities and a local spirit, likely as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29900011.text_edition: Otaciliae Severae Augustae coniugi Philippi Augusti matri Philippi nobilissimi Caesaris res publica Docleatium decreto decurionum
EDCS-29900011.translation: To Otacilia Severa Augusta, wife of Philip Augustus, mother of Philip, most noble Caesar, the Republic of Doclea by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29900011.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the city of Doclea to the empress Otacilia Severa, wife and mother of emperors, likely dating to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-29900013.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Gordio Maximiano artis grammaticae Graecae peritissimo Quintus Flavius Helenus amico inconparabili sic
EDCS-29900013.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Gordius Maximianus, most skilled in the Greek grammatical art, Quintus Flavius Helenus set this for his incomparable friend. So be it.
EDCS-29900013.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus Flavius Helenus to his friend Gaius Gordius Maximianus, who was highly skilled in Greek grammar.

EDCS-29900014.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Silvio Aestivo sodali ROMP Iulius Iadestinus et Flavius Chresimus collegae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-29900014.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Silvius Aestivus, fellow member of ROMP, Julius Iadestinus and Flavius Chresimus, his colleagues, placed this for their well-deserving comrade.
EDCS-29900014.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Julius Iadestinus and Flavius Chresimus for their colleague Silvius Aestivus, a member of an association or college known as ROMP.

EDCS-29900015.text_edition: Soli Invicto sacrum Sextus Baberius Ianuarius votum posuit merito
EDCS-29900015.translation: Sacred to Sol Invictus. Sextus Baberius Ianuarius justly placed this vow.
EDCS-29900015.summary: This is a religious dedication made to the god Sol Invictus by Sextus Baberius Ianuarius, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-29900018.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Lucio Domitio Aureliano Au[gusto] II et Capitolino consulibus Aurelius Verecundus vir egregius procurator argentariarum balneum vetustate conlapsum ad pristinam faciem reformare curavit
EDCS-29900018.translation: When Imperator Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Augustus for the second time and Capitolinus were consuls, Aurelius Verecundus, an eminent man, procurator of the silver mines, took care to restore a bathhouse, which had collapsed from old age, to its former state.
EDCS-29900018.summary: This inscription records the restoration of an old bathhouse by Aurelius Verecundus, a high-ranking financial administrator, during the second consulate of Emperor Aurelian in 275 AD.

EDCS-29900021.text_edition: I Mascelio maritus coniugi carissimae et sibi posterisque suis bene merentibus
EDCS-29900021.translation: I, Mascelius, [set this] for my dearest wife and for myself and for my well-deserving descendants.
EDCS-29900021.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Mascelius for his beloved wife, himself, and his descendants, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-29900022.text_edition: inter Nerastinos et Pituntinos termini recogniti et restituti a Pisone legato pro praetore Tiberi Claudi Caesaris Augusti Germanici per Caium Marium Maternum |(centurionem) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis quos Lucius Volusius Saturninus legatus pro praetore statuendos curaverat
EDCS-29900022.translation: Boundaries between the Nerastini and the Pituntini recognized and restored by Piso, legate pro praetore of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, through Gaius Marius Maternum, centurion of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, which Lucius Volusius Saturninus, legate pro praetore, had taken care to establish.
EDCS-29900022.summary: This inscription records the re-establishment and recognition of boundaries between two communities, the Nerastini and Pituntini, by a governor under Emperor Claudius, referencing an earlier boundary demarcation by another governor.

EDCS-29900027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Aemilio Rufo beneficiario consularis Aemilia Aphrodite patrono
EDCS-29900027.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Quintus Aemilius Rufus, *beneficiarius consularis*, Aemilia Aphrodite [set this] for her patron.
EDCS-29900027.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aemilia Aphrodite to Quintus Aemilius Rufus, who held the military rank of *beneficiarius consularis*, indicating a patron-client relationship.

EDCS-29900028.text_edition: cohortis VIII voluntariorum mp Firmi coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-29900028.translation: Of the eighth cohort of volunteers. mp Firmi [set this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29900028.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a man named Firmi, likely a member of the 8th cohort of volunteers, for his deceased wife.

EDCS-29900029.text_edition: Marcus Pomponius Zosimus negotians materiarius vivus fecit sibi et Pomponiae Semnae filiae optimae defunctae et Pomponio Secundino defuncto bene merentibus hic lapide lusit ponderibus his XXXX L C
EDCS-29900029.translation: Marcus Pomponius Zosimus, a timber merchant, made this while alive for himself and for Pomponia Semna, his most excellent daughter, who died, and for Pomponius Secundinus, who died, both well-deserving. With this stone, he played with these weights: 40, 50, 100.
EDCS-29900029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Marcus Pomponius Zosimus, a timber merchant, preparing a tomb for himself and his deceased daughter Pomponia Semna and Pomponius Secundinus, possibly including a game or riddle related to weights.

EDCS-29900033.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Marnus qui vixit annos  Aurelia Tertulla coniugi incomparabili posuit et sibi
EDCS-29900033.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Tertulla placed this for her incomparable husband Aurelius Marnus, who lived for [---] years, and for herself.
EDCS-29900033.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelia Tertulla dedicating a tomb to her husband Aurelius Marnus and also for herself.

EDCS-29900034.text_edition: o maritus  quae vixit nnos XV dies XVIII
EDCS-29900034.translation: O husband, who lived 15 years, 18 days.
EDCS-29900034.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by a wife to her deceased husband, stating his age at death.

EDCS-29900035.translation: To Severa, a most devoted mother.
EDCS-29900035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a mother named Severa, praised for her devotion.

EDCS-29900043.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29900043.text_edition: Aurelii Marcianus IIAHIN civis Afer et Quintina uxor eius vivi sibi hanc piscinam virginem a se conparatam constituerunt
EDCS-29900043.translation: Aurelius Marcianus IIAHIN, an African citizen, and his wife Quintina, while alive, built this virgin pool, acquired by them, for themselves.
EDCS-29900043.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Marcianus, an African citizen, and his wife Quintina constructing a new private pool for their own use during their lifetimes.

EDCS-29900048.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, containing a partial name or abbreviation.

EDCS-29900049.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, to a woman named Valeria Emerita.

EDCS-29900053.text_edition: Invicto patri  Aelius Aelianus eques praetorianus et Ulpius Licinius a scriniis praefecti sancxerunt sic
EDCS-29900053.translation: To the unconquered father. Aelius Aelianus, praetorian knight, and Ulpius Licinius, from the prefect's writing office, sanctioned this thus.
EDCS-29900053.summary: This inscription records Aelius Aelianus, a praetorian knight, and Ulpius Licinius, a clerk in the prefect's office, sanctioning something, possibly a dedication to an 'unconquered father,' suggesting an imperial or divine recipient.

EDCS-29900054.text_edition: Terrae MatriMI NOII ES calinio  sup sic cura  procuratoris Mercurius vilicus officinae ferrariae XI Kalendas Maias
EDCS-29900054.translation: To Mother Earth, [---] Calinio. Supervised thus, under the care of the procurator. Mercurius, overseer of the iron workshop, on the 11th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-29900054.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mother Earth, possibly related to an offering or work completed under the supervision of a procurator, recorded by Mercurius, the foreman of an iron workshop, dated to April 21st.

EDCS-29900056.text_edition: Aplo Curbania Kabaleti filia annorum IIII
EDCS-29900056.translation: Aplo Curbania, daughter of Kabaletus, four years old.
EDCS-29900056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Aplo Curbania, daughter of Kabaletus, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-29900057.text_edition: Aplo Aplinis Tizi filia annorum II
EDCS-29900057.translation: Apla, daughter of Aplinis Tizi, aged 2 years.
EDCS-29900057.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Apla, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-29900058.text_edition: Sexto Licinia Pinsi filia aHnnorum#annorum#HNN XX hic sita est
EDCS-29900058.translation: Licinia, daughter of Sextus Pinsus, aged 20 years, lies here.
EDCS-29900058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia, daughter of Sextus Pinsus, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-29900059.text_edition: Caius Lucilius Cai filius Ma C(aio) Lucilio Cai filio Volso patri et Luciliae
EDCS-29900059.translation: Gaius Lucilius, son of Gaius, for Gaius Lucilius Volso, his father, and for Lucilia.
EDCS-29900059.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Lucilius, son of Gaius, to his father Gaius Lucilius Volso and to Lucilia, likely a mother or other female relative, possibly a funerary monument.

EDCS-29900060.text_edition: Sub hac inscriptione Marcus Cupitius Marci filius Pollia Paulus Amasia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVII Cupitius Pollio frater faciundum curavit
EDCS-29900060.translation: Beneath this inscription, Marcus Cupitius Paulus Amasia, son of Marcus, of the Pollia tribe, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, with 17 years of service. Cupitius Pollio, his brother, had this made.
EDCS-29900060.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cupitius Paulus Amasia, a veteran soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, set up by his brother Cupitius Pollio.

EDCS-29900061.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae spelaeum cum omne impensa Hermes Cai Antoni Rufi praefecti vehiculorum et conductoris publici portorii servus vilicus Fortunatianus fecit
EDCS-29900061.translation: For the undefeated Mithras, Hermes Fortunatianus, a slave and bailiff of Gaius Antonius Rufus, prefect of transport and public tax collector, made this shrine with all expenses paid.
EDCS-29900061.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a Mithraic shrine (spelaeum) to the god Mithras by Hermes Fortunatianus, a slave and bailiff, on behalf of his master Gaius Antonius Rufus.

EDCS-29900062.summary: This is likely a funerary or dedicatory inscription for Vallia Veneria, a freedwoman of Lucius.

EDCS-29900103.text_edition: Cnaeo Sertorio Cai filio Broccho Aquilio Agricolae Pedanio Fusco Salinatori Iulio Serviano
EDCS-29900103.translation: To Gnaeus Sertorius Brocchus Aquilius Agricola Pedanius Fuscus Salinator Iulius Servianus, son of Gaius.
