EDCS-29900103.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a high-ranking individual with a lengthy name, Gnaeus Sertorius Brocchus Aquilius Agricola Pedanius Fuscus Salinator Iulius Servianus, indicating his aristocratic status and possible cursus honorum.

EDCS-29900104.text_edition: ius Quirina Genialis viator consulum et praetorum sacerdos at sic aram Caesaris decurio testamento poni iussit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-29900104.translation: Genialis Iustus, of the Quirina tribe, a lictor of the consuls and praetors, a priest, ordered in his will that an altar to Caesar be placed here; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29900104.summary: This inscription records that Genialis Iustus, a lictor and priest, ordered an altar to Caesar to be placed in a location granted by the decree of the decurions, likely a public monument from the Augustan or Julio-Claudian period.

EDCS-29900105.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelio Verzaiio liberto qui vixsit sic annis LXXXV
EDCS-29900105.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, for Aurelius Verzalius, a freedman, who lived for 85 years.
EDCS-29900105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead (Dis Manibus) for Aurelius Verzalius, a freedman who lived to the age of 85.

EDCS-29900106.summary: This inscription simply lists the names Aemilius Maximus and Marcus Antonius, possibly as co-dedicants, witnesses, or individuals commemorated on a monument.

EDCS-29900107.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali Aurelius Lautus ex voto libens posuit
EDCS-29900107.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, Aurelius Lautus willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-29900107.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis by Aurelius Lautus, likely a private individual, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-29900108.text_edition: Invicto Augusto Aurelius Argyrianus procurator Aug(usti) Orfito cons(ule) laetus libens merito posuit
EDCS-29900108.translation: To the Invincible Augustus, Aurelius Argyrianus, procurator of Augustus, joyfully, willingly, and deservedly placed this when Orfitus was consul.
EDCS-29900108.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Invincible Augustus by Aurelius Argyrianus, an imperial procurator, dated to the consulship of Orfitus (likely 270 AD).

EDCS-29900109.text_edition: Servilia Marci filia Copiesilla aediculam Matris Magnae faciendam curavit ipsa inpensa sua quam voverat pro ea Marcus Cottius Certus vir eius
EDCS-29900109.translation: Servilia, daughter of Marcus, Copiesilla, had this small shrine of the Great Mother built at her own expense, which her husband Marcus Cottius Certus had vowed for her.
EDCS-29900109.summary: This inscription records the construction of a small shrine to the Great Mother by Servilia Copiesilla, funded by herself to fulfill a vow made by her husband, Marcus Cottius Certus, on her behalf.

EDCS-29900110.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Coulio Leporio qui vixit nnos XII menses X dies VII Coulius Zoticus et Publicia Ursa filio infelicissimo posuerunt qui HACARIS
EDCS-29900110.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Coulius Leporius, who lived 12 years, 10 months, 7 days. Coulius Zoticus and Publicia Ursa placed this for their most unfortunate son, who [---].
EDCS-29900110.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Coulius Leporius, erected by his parents, Coulius Zoticus and Publicia Ursa.

EDCS-29900112.text_edition: Publius Attecius Publi filius Venimarus miles cohortis VIII centuria Attili domo Claudia Viruno annorum XXI stipendiorum II Marcus Ressius Bricomarus heres ex testamento ponendum curavit
EDCS-29900112.translation: Publius Attecius, son of Publius, Venimarus, a soldier of the eighth cohort, from the century of Attilius, from the Claudia tribe, from Virunum, aged 21 years, with 2 years of service. Marcus Ressius Bricomarus, his heir, saw to its placement according to his will.
EDCS-29900112.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young Roman soldier, Publius Attecius Venimarus, erected by his heir, Marcus Ressius Bricomarus, according to the soldier's will.

EDCS-29900113.text_edition: LLV matri optimae et pientissimae vivae posuerunt locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-29900113.translation: L.L.V. placed this for their best and most devoted living mother. The location was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-29900113.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for a living mother, erected by her children (indicated by the initials L.L.V.), with the burial plot granted by a decree of the local council (decurions).

EDCS-29900114.text_edition: Aelii Nepos et Dasas Arbonis patri carissimo qui vixit  nnos LXX bene merenti memoriam posuerunt
EDCS-29900114.translation: Aelius Nepos and Dasas, sons of Arbo, placed this memorial for their dearest father, who lived 70 years, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-29900114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Arbo, erected by his sons Aelius Nepos and Dasas, commemorating their beloved father who lived to the age of 70.

EDCS-29900116.text_edition: Toitmio Iomeneta Ledri filia annorum XX
EDCS-29900116.translation: Toitmio Iomeneta, daughter of Ledrus, aged 20 years.
EDCS-29900116.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a young woman named Toitmio Iomeneta, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-29900117.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Maxa annorum L infelicissimis posu=VS(it)#posu(it)#POVS  Dis Manibus Aurelia Clemaentina sic annorum XL Sicu filius
EDCS-29900117.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Maxa, aged 50, placed this for the most unfortunate. To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Clementina, aged 40, and Sicu her son.
EDCS-29900117.summary: This is a double funerary inscription, commemorating Aurelius Maxa and also Aurelia Clementina with her son Sicu, likely placed by Sicu for his mother and possibly Maxa.

EDCS-29900788.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Ravonius Fortunatus C.I.
EDCS-29900788.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for a person named Ravonius Fortunatus, with an unclear abbreviation following his name.

EDCS-30000155.translation: Iustus to his wife Ulpia Ianuaria.
EDCS-30000155.summary: This is a funerary inscription, where Iustus dedicates a memorial to his wife, Ulpia Ianuaria.

EDCS-30000156.text_edition: Marco Ulpio Onesimiano qui vixit annos XIII menses VIII dies III Marcus Ulpius Onesimus et Aurelia Lucilla parentes infelicissimi filio pientissimo et dulcissimo posuerunt
EDCS-30000156.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Onesimianus, who lived for 13 years, 8 months, and 3 days. Marcus Ulpius Onesimus and Aurelia Lucilla, his most unhappy parents, set this up for their most dutiful and sweetest son.
EDCS-30000156.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Ulpius Onesimus and Aurelia Lucilla for their beloved son, Marcus Ulpius Onesimianus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-30000160.text_edition: Dis Manibus Urvicae sic defunctae nnorum XVII Urbana sororPi#sorori#SOROPI infelicisiimae
EDCS-30000160.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Urvica, who thus died at seventeen years of age. Urbana, her most unhappy sister, for her sister.
EDCS-30000160.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Urbana for her sister Urvica, who passed away at the age of seventeen.

EDCS-30000161.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Urgulania Valentina matri et Tarquitius
EDCS-30000161.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Urgulania Valentina to her mother, and Tarquitius.
EDCS-30000161.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Urgulania Valentina to her mother, with Tarquitius also mentioned as a dedicator.

EDCS-30000162.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---] Ursin[---].
EDCS-30000162.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, mentioning a person named Ursinus or Ursina.

EDCS-30000163.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursino nnum VII Naebia sic <T=I>uta#<T>uta#IUTA bene merenti
EDCS-30000163.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Ursinus, aged seven, Naebia Tuta, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-30000163.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Naebia Tuta to Ursinus, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-30000165.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ursus Iulio patri defuncto annorum XXXX bene merenti
EDCS-30000165.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ursus to Iulius, his father, who died at forty years of age, well-deserving.
EDCS-30000165.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ursus to his well-deserving father, Iulius, who died at the age of forty.

EDCS-30000167.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---] Urso V [---].
EDCS-30000167.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, mentioning a person named Urso.

EDCS-30000184.text_edition: s qui vixit annos nonaginta et lia Ammi qui sic vixit annos quod si quis supra ea corpora aliud voluerit ponere inferet  AVR PRIMA LIB
EDCS-30000184.translation: [---] who lived for ninety years and [---]lia Ammi who thus lived for [---] years. If anyone wishes to place anything else above these bodies, they shall pay [a fine]. Aurelia Prima, freedwoman.
EDCS-30000184.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a family tomb, which details the ages of the deceased, specifies a penalty for disturbing the burial plot, and names Aurelia Prima as the dedicator.

EDCS-30000205.text_edition: Rutilia  viva fecit sibi et Ar Tusco patri legionis VII et A Primioni filio et Rutiliae Festae defunctae
EDCS-30000205.translation: Rutilia, while living, made this for herself and for Aurelius Tuscus, her father, of the Seventh Legion, and for Aurelius Primio, her son, and for Rutilia Festa, who has passed away.
EDCS-30000205.summary: This is a funerary inscription, created by Rutilia during her lifetime, for herself and members of her family: her father Aurelius Tuscus (a legionary veteran), her son Aurelius Primio, and her deceased family member Rutilia Festa.

EDCS-30000206.text_edition: Dis Manibus Novellia  sibi et Lucio Iulio  marito optimo veterano cohortis VIII voluntariorum et cognationi suae et libertis liberisque eorum
EDCS-30000206.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Novellia [made this] for herself and for Lucius Iulius, her excellent husband, a veteran of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, and for her own kin, and for their freedmen and children.
EDCS-30000206.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb by Novellia for herself, her husband, her family, and their freedmen and children.

EDCS-30000207.text_edition: Titus Octavius Titi filius Velina Hister veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis testamento fieri iussit et Acrini contubernali in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XI
EDCS-30000207.translation: Titus Octavius Hister, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe, a veteran of the 11th Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, ordered this to be made by his will, and for Acrinus, his tent-mate, ten feet in front and eleven feet in the field.
EDCS-30000207.summary: This funerary inscription records a will-ordered dedication by Titus Octavius Hister, a veteran, for himself and his contubernalis Acrinus, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-30000208.text_edition: Lucius Pescennius Luci filius Falerna Saturninus veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Mariae Spuri filiae Quintae coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-30000208.translation: Lucius Pescennius Saturninus, son of Lucius, of the Falerna tribe, a veteran of the 11th Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, made this while living for himself and for Maria Quarta, daughter of Spurius, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30000208.summary: This funerary inscription records a pre-mortem dedication by Lucius Pescennius Saturninus, a veteran, for himself and his deserving wife, Maria Quarta.

EDCS-30000209.text_edition: Lucius Vegnonius -- Luci filius veteranus legionis VII domo Florentia opaena Fabricia se viva sibi et viro E hic situs est
EDCS-30000209.translation: Lucius Vegnonius, son of Lucius, a veteran of the 7th Legion, from Florentia, and Fabricia Opaena, while alive, made this for herself and her husband. He lies here.
EDCS-30000209.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made while living by Fabricia Opaena for herself and her husband, Lucius Vegnonius, a veteran from Florentia, marking their burial place.

EDCS-30000211.text_edition: Himero Himer pater et Stephane mater et Tranquillae filiae et Tranquillae matri
EDCS-30000211.translation: To Himerus, Himer his father and Stephane his mother, and to their daughter Tranquilla, and to Tranquilla his mother.
EDCS-30000211.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Himerus, made by his parents Himer and Stephane, and also references their daughter and mother Tranquilla.

EDCS-30000212.text_edition: Dis Manibus vivus fecit Quintus Agrius Eutychides Agrio Barbaro fEilio#filio#EIL et Agrio Eutycho patri et Flaviae Maximae coniugi bene merentibus
EDCS-30000212.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Quintus Agrius Eutychides made this while living for Agrius Barbarus, his son, and for Agrius Eutychus, his father, and for Flavia Maxima, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-30000212.summary: This funerary inscription records a pre-mortem dedication by Quintus Agrius Eutychides for his son, father, and well-deserving wife.

EDCS-30100396.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Marcia Helpis.
EDCS-30100396.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Marcia Helpis.

EDCS-30100397.text_edition: Sev ANI et Terentiae Marci filiae Te annorum  Marcus Terentius Vale
EDCS-30100397.translation: To Sevani and to Terentia, daughter of Marcus, aged years. Marcus Terentius Vale.
EDCS-30100397.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Sevani and Terentia, daughter of Marcus, and includes the name of Marcus Terentius Vale, likely the dedicator.

EDCS-30100398.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cnaeo Stenio Securo Stenia
EDCS-30100398.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Gnaeus Stenius Securus. Stenia.
EDCS-30100398.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Gnaeus Stenius Securus, likely by Stenia.

EDCS-30100402.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Maximae defunctae nnorum X dierum XXX Aurelius Tertius miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum Antoninianae et Aurelia Apuleia parentes filiae infelicissimae bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-30100402.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aurelia Maxima, who died at 10 years and 30 days old. Aurelius Tertius, a soldier of the 8th Antoninian Cohort of Volunteers, and Aurelia Apuleia, her parents, placed this for their most unfortunate, well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-30100402.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tombstone by Aurelius Tertius, a soldier, and Aurelia Apuleia for their tragically deceased daughter, Aurelia Maxima, who died at a young age.

EDCS-30100403.text_edition: Quintus Baebius Quinti filius Sergia Conanis miles legionis VII nnorum XXXXV stipendiorum XX hic situs est quamvis lasse viator rogo ne graveris et tumulum contempla meum lege et moraris iam aliquid resciveris dum vixi hilaris iucundus amicis nunc torpens iaceo hic ossa requiescunt mea natus sum CoAnaOnis#Conanis#CANONIS conditus in IllyVrico#Illyrico#ILLVRICO vale et bene facito vitae dum fatum venit frater fratri
EDCS-30100403.translation: Quintus Baebius Conanis, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe, a soldier of the 7th legion, 45 years old, with 20 years of service, lies here. However weary you are, traveler, I ask you not to begrudge it and to contemplate my tomb. Read and linger, and you will learn something. While I lived, I was cheerful and pleasant to friends; now, lifeless, I lie here. My bones rest. I was born in Conanis, buried in Illyricum. Farewell and fare well in life until fate comes. Brother to brother.
EDCS-30100403.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Baebius Conanis, a soldier of the 7th legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-30100404.text_edition: Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Fabia Heraclea miles legionis VII nnorum XXXIII stipendiorum XI testamento fieri iussit Secundus heres posuit hic situs est
EDCS-30100404.translation: Lucius Cornelius Heraclea, son of Lucius, of the Fabian tribe, a soldier of the 7th legion, 33 years old, with 11 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Secundus, his heir, placed it. He lies here.
EDCS-30100404.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Heraclea, a soldier of the 7th legion, set up by his heir, Secundus, as per his will.

EDCS-30100405.text_edition: agenio Eucisi filio equiti alae Frontonianae annorum XL stipendiorum XX Nemis Sige hic situs est frateres sic frater sic fecerunt
EDCS-30100405.translation: Agenio, son of Eucisus, a cavalryman of the Frontonian wing, 40 years old, with 20 years of service, lies here at Nemis Sige. His brothers, brother to brother, made this.
EDCS-30100405.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Agenio, a cavalryman, set up by his brothers.

EDCS-30100406.text_edition: Caius Longinius Cai filius Cornelia Amblada miles legionis VII annorum XL stipendiorum XXI hic situs est frater fratri
EDCS-30100406.translation: Gaius Longinius Amblada, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, a soldier of the 7th legion, 40 years old, with 21 years of service, lies here. Brother to brother.
EDCS-30100406.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Longinius Amblada, a soldier of the 7th legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-30100407.text_edition: EMANS  Platoris Daesitias vexillarius equitum cohortis I Belgarum turma Valeri Proculi nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXIV hic situs est fieri curavit Iulia Ves coniunx
EDCS-30100407.translation: Emans Platoris, a Daesitian standard-bearer of the cavalry of the 1st Cohort of Belgae, of the troop of Valerius Proculus, 45 years old, with 24 years of service, lies here. Julia Ves, his wife, had this made.
EDCS-30100407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Emans Platoris, a Daesitian vexillarius, commissioned by his wife, Julia Ves.

EDCS-30100408.text_edition: Dis Manibus Severo infelicissimo qOui#qui#OUI decidit in Pannonia cum duobRus#duobus#DUORUS filiis Misaucis Varronianus tata patronis ben=IVe#bene#BEIVE meritIis#meritis#RIIIS pDosuit#posuit#DOSUIT
EDCS-30100408.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To the most unfortunate Severus, who perished in Pannonia with his two sons Misaucus. Varronianus, his father, placed this for his well-deserving patrons.
EDCS-30100408.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Spirits of the Dead for Severus and his two sons, Misaucus, who died in Pannonia, erected by Varronianus, presumably the father or a patron.

EDCS-30100409.text_edition: Marcus Vibius Marci filius Aemilia DyVrrachio#Dyrrachio#DVRRA miles legionis VII nnorum XXX stipendiorum VII hic situs est lius heres posuit
EDCS-30100409.translation: Marcus Vibius Dyrrachio, son of Marcus, of the Aemilian tribe, a soldier of the 7th legion, 30 years old, with 7 years of service, lies here. Lius, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-30100409.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Vibius Dyrrachio, a soldier of the 7th legion, set up by his heir, Lius.

EDCS-30100410.text_edition: Pollia Aese miles legionis VII nnorum XXX stipendiorum VII hic situs est
EDCS-30100410.translation: Pollia Aese, a soldier of the 7th legion, 30 years old, with 7 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-30100410.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pollia Aese, a soldier of the 7th legion.

EDCS-30100411.text_edition: ITNAI LIVOS annorum et Faustus  nnorum XII
EDCS-30100411.translation: Itnai Livos, of [---] years, and Faustus, 12 years old.
EDCS-30100411.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely for two individuals, Itnai Livos and Faustus, possibly a child.

EDCS-30100412.text_edition: Iulio Menandro Iulius infanti mensum sic Menander pater
EDCS-30100412.translation: To Iulius Menander, the infant of [---] months. Thus Menander, his father.
EDCS-30100412.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Iulius Menander, erected by his father, Menander.

EDCS-30100413.translation: Lucius Signius potus Firmus placed this.
EDCS-30100413.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lucius Signius Firmus as the one who placed a monument, possibly for himself or another.

EDCS-30100416.text_edition: Iunoni  Aurelius Nepos ob honorem duumveratus sic Firmi et Maximi filiorum votum pater libens solvit
EDCS-30100416.translation: To Juno. Aurelius Nepos, on account of the honor of the duumvirate of his sons Firmus and Maximus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow as a father.
EDCS-30100416.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Nepos dedicating a monument to Juno in gratitude for his sons, Firmus and Maximus, achieving the office of duumvir.

EDCS-30100418.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Titius Euthimus sic Gabalus votum solvit libens
EDCS-30100418.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Titius Euthimus Gabalus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100418.summary: This is a votive inscription to Liber Pater, dedicated by Titius Euthimus Gabalus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30100421.text_edition: Triviis sacrum Titia Elpis votum libens posuit
EDCS-30100421.translation: Sacred to the Cross-roads. Titia Elpis willingly placed her vow.
EDCS-30100421.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Titia Elpis to the Triviae (goddesses of the cross-roads), indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30100423.translation: Lucius Eburnus Tatianus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100423.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording Lucius Eburnus Tatianus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30100425.text_edition: Marco Annio Vero II Cnaeo Arrio Augure consulibus Aelius Fronto Charito Nassius Iettus StaC Lucio Iulio Serviano Lucio Sollio
EDCS-30100425.translation: When Marcus Annius Verus was consul for the second time, and Gnaeus Arrius Augur was consul. Aelius Fronto Charito Nassius Iettus, StaC, Lucius Iulius Servianus, Lucius Sollius...
EDCS-30100425.summary: This is a consular dating formula, providing the names of the consuls (Marcus Annius Verus II and Gnaeus Arrius Augur) for a specific year (likely 121 AD), followed by a list of names, potentially officials or witnesses for an unknown event.

EDCS-30100426.text_edition: Burrius Betuloni filius Trebocus miles cohortis Aquitanorum annorum LV stipendiorum XXIX hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-30100426.translation: Burrius Trebocus, son of Betulo, a soldier of the cohort of Aquitanians, 55 years old, with 29 years of service, lies here. His heir placed this.
EDCS-30100426.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Burrius Trebocus, a soldier from the cohort of Aquitanians, erected by his heir, detailing his age and years of military service.

EDCS-30100427.text_edition: Marcus Varenus Marci filius Voltinia Valens veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Marci Vareni Secundi liberti idemque heredis
EDCS-30100427.translation: Marcus Varenus Valens, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and loyal, ordered this to be made by testament, under the arbitration of Marcus Varenus Secundus, his freedman and also his heir.
EDCS-30100427.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the posthumous dedication for Marcus Varenus Valens, a veteran of Legio VII Claudia, erected by his freedman and heir, Marcus Varenus Secundus, according to the deceased's will.

EDCS-30100429.text_edition: IIviro designato Calpurnia Crispina marito pientissimo
EDCS-30100429.translation: Calpurnia Crispina [placed this] for her most devoted husband, the duumvir elect.
EDCS-30100429.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Calpurnia Crispina to her very devoted husband, who was a duumvir elect (designatus).

EDCS-30100430.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iulius Valerianus haruspex vivus sibi posuit
EDCS-30100430.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Iulius Valerianus, a soothsayer, placed this for himself while living.
EDCS-30100430.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Valerianus, an haruspex (soothsayer), which he had prepared for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-30100431.text_edition: Marcus Marcius Anthus IIIIIIvir ex pecunia honoraria  coloniae
EDCS-30100431.translation: Marcus Marcius Anthus, a *sevir* of the six Augustales, from honorary money given by the colony.
EDCS-30100431.summary: This is a dedication by a *sevir Augustalis* (a member of a board of six priests of the imperial cult) named Marcus Marcius Anthus, funded by money received from the colony.

EDCS-30100432.text_edition: Munatia Primigenia nomine Munatiae Publi filiae Attianillae ob honorem Publi Munati Rustici auguris
EDCS-30100432.translation: Munatia Primigenia, in the name of Munatia Attianilla, daughter of Publius, in honor of Publius Munatius Rusticus, augur.
EDCS-30100432.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Munatia Primigenia on behalf of Munatia Attianilla to honor Publius Munatius Rusticus, an augur.

EDCS-30100664.translation: To Lusia Rufina, daughter of Maximus, in her fourth year.
EDCS-30100664.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lusia Rufina, who died at the age of four, likely erected by her father Maximus.

EDCS-30100681.text_edition: Quintus Vettius Hospes miles cohortis Campanorum domo Suessa annorum XLV stipendiorum XXII testamento iussit fieri
EDCS-30100681.translation: Quintus Vettius Hospes, a soldier of the cohort of Campanians, from Suessa, 45 years old, with 22 years of service, ordered this to be made by will.
EDCS-30100681.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Vettius Hospes, a soldier of the Cohort of Campanians, who ordered its erection in his will after 22 years of service and dying at age 45.

EDCS-30100684.translation: To Titia Procula, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-30100684.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titia Procula, the daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-30100685.text_edition: Aelius MAus et Aelia Victoria mater infelicissimo filio qui vixit annos II
EDCS-30100685.translation: Aelius Maus and Aelia Victoria, his mother, to their most unfortunate son who lived two years.
EDCS-30100685.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Aelius Maus and his mother Aelia Victoria for their young son who lived for only two years.

EDCS-30101114.text_edition: Sexto Aurelio Lupiano Lupi filio principi decuriones collegae et populares et peregrini incolae civi optimo ob merita posuerunt epulo dedicata loco dato decreto decurionum
EDCS-30101114.translation: To Sextus Aurelius Lupianus, son of Lupus, *princeps*. The decurions, colleagues, and local citizens and foreign residents erected this to the most excellent citizen for his merits, with a banquet dedicated and a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-30101114.summary: This monument was erected by the decurions, colleagues, and both local and foreign residents to Sextus Aurelius Lupianus, a *princeps* and excellent citizen, in recognition of his merits, with a public banquet and allocated space by decurional decree.

EDCS-30101160.summary: This is likely a funerary or property inscription indicating ownership or dedication by Sextus C. Iucundus related to someone named Iucundus.

EDCS-30101161.text_edition: Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Quirina domo Iconio veteranus legionis VII annorum LV stipendiorum XXVIII hic situs est Quintus Portorius et Quintus Valerius Anteros posuerunt
EDCS-30101161.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, from Iconium, a veteran of the VII Legion, 55 years old, with 28 years of service, is buried here. Quintus Portorius and Quintus Valerius Anteros erected this.
EDCS-30101161.summary: This is a funerary monument for Quintus Valerius, a veteran of the Legio VII who died at 55 after 28 years of service, erected by Quintus Portorius and Quintus Valerius Anteros.

EDCS-30101163.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Epetinus Caius Avillius Clemens Lucius Coelius Capella Publius Raecius Libo Publius Valerius Secundus iudices dati a Marco Pompeio Silvano legato Augusti pro praetore inter rem publicam Asseriatium et inter rem publicam Alveritarum in re praesenti per sententiam suam determinaverunt
EDCS-30101163.translation: Tiberius Claudius Epetinus, Caius Avillius Clemens, Lucius Coelius Capella, Publius Raecius Libo, Publius Valerius Secundus, judges appointed by Marcus Pompeius Silvanus, legate of Augustus pro praetore, determined by their judgment in the present matter between the commonwealth of the Asseriates and the commonwealth of the Alveritae.
EDCS-30101163.summary: This inscription records a legal judgment made by five appointed judges, settling a dispute between the communities of the Asseriates and the Alveritae, under the authority of the imperial legate Marcus Pompeius Silvanus.

EDCS-30101164.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Plaetorio Titi filio Postumino Iulia Severina filio
EDCS-30101164.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Titus Plaetorius Postuminus, son of Titus. Iulia Severina [made this] for her son.
EDCS-30101164.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Plaetorius Postuminus by his mother, Iulia Severina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101165.text_edition: Clodia Turi filia Aeta sibi et Tito Safinio Septumi filio Rufo Safiniae Septumi filiae Secundae filiis suis viva fecit
EDCS-30101165.translation: Clodia Aeta, daughter of Turus, made this in her lifetime for herself and for Titus Safinius Rufus, son of Septumus, and for Safinia Secunda, daughter of Septumus, her children.
EDCS-30101165.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Clodia Aeta in her lifetime for herself and her two children, Titus Safinius Rufus and Safinia Secunda, probably from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-30101166.text_edition: nae PCLI SPVBLVS filius  vivus fecit  M
EDCS-30101166.translation: [---]na PCLI SPVBLVS, his son, made this in his lifetime. M[---]
EDCS-30101166.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription made in life by a son, likely named SPVBLVS, for a person whose name ends in -na, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101167.text_edition: Sexto Livio Maximo Iulia Maximilla posuit
EDCS-30101167.translation: To Sextus Livius Maximus. Iulia Maximilla placed this.
EDCS-30101167.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sextus Livius Maximus by Iulia Maximilla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101168.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription for Quintus Trosius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101169.text_edition: Trosiae Cai filiae Secundillae Veratia Luci filia Maximilla mater infelicissima posuit
EDCS-30101169.translation: To Trosia Secundilla, daughter of Gaius. Veratia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius, her most unhappy mother, placed this.
EDCS-30101169.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Trosia Secundilla, placed by her deeply grieving mother, Veratia Maximilla, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101170.text_edition: Caeliae Facundae Caelius Facundus pater filiae infelicissimae posuit
EDCS-30101170.translation: To Caelia Facunda. Caelius Facundus, her father, placed this for his most unhappy daughter.
EDCS-30101170.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caelia Facunda, erected by her grieving father, Caelius Facundus, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101171.text_edition: ae inae Caesia Tertulla mater infelicissima posuit
EDCS-30101171.translation: [---]a [---]ina. Caesia Tertulla, her most unhappy mother, placed this.
EDCS-30101171.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, dedicated by a very sorrowful mother, Caesia Tertulla, to her child, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101172.text_edition: fuius Titi filius Claudia uter testamento fieri iussit io   sibi et suis
EDCS-30101172.translation: [---]fuius, son of Titus. Claudia [---] (his) wife, ordered this to be made by will for himself and his family.
EDCS-30101172.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, posthumously commissioned by will by Claudia, likely the wife of the deceased, for himself and his family, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30101173.translation: To our lords, Flavius Valerius Constan[tine...
EDCS-30101173.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to the emperors Flavius Valerius Constantine and likely others, dating to the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-30200122.text_edition: Caius Seccius Cnaei filius Sergia Marcellus decurio IIvir iure dicundo Caius Seccius Cai filius Sergia Aper decurio IIvir iure dicundo ex pecunia honoraria IIviratus porticus et trichlinam sic dederunt locus datus publice decreto decurionum
EDCS-30200122.translation: Gaius Seccius Marcellus, son of Gnaeus, of the Sergian tribe, decurion, duumvir with judicial power; Gaius Seccius Aper, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, decurion, duumvir with judicial power, from the honorary money of their duumvirate, thus gave a portico and a dining room. The location was granted publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-30200122.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a portico and dining room by two duumvirs, Gaius Seccius Marcellus and Gaius Seccius Aper, using honorary funds from their office, on a site publicly granted by the decurions.

EDCS-30200125.text_edition: defuncta annorum Aemilius Secundinus maritus qui vixit cum aea sic annos XXX CONIRA IP
EDCS-30200125.translation: To the deceased, Aemilius Secundinus, her husband, who lived with her for 30 years, thus (erected this).
EDCS-30200125.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aemilius Secundinus for his deceased wife, commemorating their 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-30200126.text_edition: Cornelio Claudia no Cornelia  patrono testamento fieri iussit Cornelii   Verecundus Pudens  liberti et heredes Publi Quinti Liciniani  hoc testamento reliquit coloniae Claudiae Aequensium HS nummum LXmilia ex cuius reditu
EDCS-30200126.translation: Cornelia, to Cornelius, her patron, ordered this to be made by testament. Cornelius' freedmen and heirs, Verecundus and Pudens, by this testament, left 60,000 sesterces to the colony of Claudia Aequensium, from the income of which...
EDCS-30200126.summary: This inscription records a testamentary bequest made by Cornelia to her patron Cornelius, with her freedmen Verecundus and Pudens as heirs, who were to provide 60,000 sesterces to the colony of Claudia Aequensium from its proceeds.

EDCS-30200127.text_edition: Dis Manibus  D V Naevenae Salviae defunctae nnorum XXXII Dieus Ursus coniugi piissimae posuit
EDCS-30200127.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Naevena Salvia, deceased, aged 32 years. Dieus Ursus placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-30200127.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Naevena Salvia, aged 32, by her husband Dieus Ursus.

EDCS-30200128.translation: The place was assigned by Lucius Iulius C[---].
EDCS-30200128.summary: This fragment appears to be part of an inscription recording the assignment of a place by a Lucius Iulius.

EDCS-30200131.text_edition: Cnaeo Seccio Aucto Cnaeo Seccio Felici Cnaeo Seccio Crescenti Cnaeo Seccio Diadumeno Cnaeo Seccio Mysti Secciae
EDCS-30200131.translation: To Gnaeus Seccius Auctus, to Gnaeus Seccius Felix, to Gnaeus Seccius Crescens, to Gnaeus Seccius Diadumenus, to Gnaeus Seccius Mystes, to Seccia [---].
EDCS-30200131.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, likely members of the Seccia family, possibly as recipients of a dedication or as patrons.

EDCS-30200132.translation: To her patrons. Seccia Silvia, freedwoman.
EDCS-30200132.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Seccia Silvia, a freedwoman, to her patrons.

EDCS-30200133.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Secundino iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit annos XXVII VitIalini#Vitalini#IIALINI sorori eius posuit T   N vixit annos XVIIII parentes posuerunt pro pietate
EDCS-30200133.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Secundinus, a most unfortunate young man, who lived 27 years. Vitalina, his sister, placed this. To T. N., who lived 19 years, his parents placed this out of devotion.
EDCS-30200133.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two young individuals: Secundinus, aged 27, by his sister Vitalina, and another individual, aged 19, by their parents out of piety.

EDCS-30200134.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vivius Maximus Viviae Primae Maximi filiae annorum XII infelicissimae
EDCS-30200134.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Vivius Maximus (to) Vivia Prima, daughter of Maximus, a most unfortunate girl of 12 years.
EDCS-30200134.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vivius Maximus to his most unfortunate 12-year-old daughter, Vivia Prima.

EDCS-30200136.text_edition: Servius Ennius Servi filius Claudia Fuscus domo Cemeneli miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum stipendiorum XVIIII nnorum XXXIIX Fulvia Vitalis viva fecit sibi et coniugi bene merenti f(ecit) hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-30200136.translation: Servius Ennius Fuscus, son of Servius, of the Claudia tribe, from Cemenelum, a soldier of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, with 19 years of service, 38 years old. Fulvia Vitalis, while alive, made this for herself and her well-deserving husband. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30200136.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Fulvia Vitalis for herself and her husband, Servius Ennius Fuscus, a veteran soldier, specifying that it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-30200138.summary: This fragmentary inscription provides only a partial name and an abbreviation, likely from a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-30200139.text_edition: $ miles legionis eiusd]em frater faciendum curavit
EDCS-30200139.translation: His brother, a soldier of the same legion, saw to the erection.
EDCS-30200139.summary: This inscription records that a brother, serving in the same legion, arranged for the construction of a monument, likely a tombstone for a fellow soldier.

EDCS-30200140.text_edition: QV LXIII SI annorum XIIII pater et mater posuerunt
EDCS-30200140.translation: QV LXIII SI. For their son, 14 years old, his father and mother erected this.
EDCS-30200140.summary: This inscription is a tombstone erected by parents for their son who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-30200144.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Aper
EDCS-30200144.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Publius Aelius Aper.
EDCS-30200144.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Publius Aelius Aper to the god Silvanus Augustus, likely for a shrine or altar.

EDCS-30200145.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Agricola Iuli servus posuit libens merito
EDCS-30200145.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Agricola, slave of Iulius, willingly and deservedly set this up.
EDCS-30200145.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Silvanus Augustus, willingly set up by Agricola, a slave belonging to Iulius.

EDCS-30200146.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum  Marcus Iulius Tigrinus votum libens solvit
EDCS-30200146.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Marcus Iulius Tigrinus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200146.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Augustus, made by Marcus Iulius Tigrinus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200148.translation: Saturninus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-30200148.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Saturninus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200149.text_edition: Vercaius Mendi filius eques ala nova Claudia t(urma)? Licini domo Varcianus annorum stupendiorum sic XX hic situs est posuit Maximus Regini filius
EDCS-30200149.translation: Vercaius, son of Mendus, a cavalryman of the new Claudia ala, in the troop of Licinus, from Varcianus, 20 years old, with 20 years of service, is laid here. Maximus, son of Reginus, erected this.
EDCS-30200149.summary: This is a tombstone for Vercaius, a cavalryman of the Ala Nova Claudia, erected by Maximus, son of Reginus, detailing his origin, military unit, age, and years of service.

EDCS-30200150.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, preserving only parts of a name and an additional word, likely from a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-30200151.text_edition: M ARr Vitalis sibi ci coniugi suae
EDCS-30200151.translation: Marcus Ar(runtius) Vitalis for himself and his wife.
EDCS-30200151.summary: This inscription records a tombstone set up by Marcus Arruntius Vitalis for himself and his wife.

EDCS-30200152.text_edition: LIDEIO  suae A posuit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-30200152.translation: A. placed this monument for his Lideia. It will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30200152.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by A. for his Lideia, with a clause restricting inheritance.

EDCS-30200155.translation: Us. placed this for Juno, the Preserver, the Queen.
EDCS-30200155.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Conservatrix Regina by an individual whose name is partially preserved as 'Us.'.

EDCS-30200158.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius T votum solvit lI(ibens)#l(ibens)#I
EDCS-30200158.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Aurelius T. willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200158.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aurelius T., indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200159.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Panes Slator Ciassici votum solvit
EDCS-30200159.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Panes Slator, son of Ciassicus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200159.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Panes Slator, son of Ciassicus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200160.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo libens votum solvit Marcus P C
EDCS-30200160.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus P. C. willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200160.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus P. C., recording the willing fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200161.translation: Aure. Atar made a vow for his father.
EDCS-30200161.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aure. Atar, indicating a vow made on behalf of his father.

EDCS-30200162.text_edition: Silvano Augusto Titus Aurelius Quintus ex voto posuit
EDCS-30200162.translation: To Silvanus Augustus, Titus Aurelius Quintus placed this in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-30200162.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Titus Aurelius Quintus, placed in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200163.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Titus Candalio posuit
EDCS-30200163.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Titus Candalio placed this.
EDCS-30200163.summary: This is a sacred inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus, placed by Titus Candalio.

EDCS-30200164.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Valerius Firmus votum solvit
EDCS-30200164.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Valerius Firmus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200164.summary: This is a sacred votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus, where Valerius Firmus fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-30200165.text_edition: Silvano Silvestri Augusto posuit Aurelius T
EDCS-30200165.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris Augustus, Aurelius T. placed this.
EDCS-30200165.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to Silvanus Silvestris Augustus, placed by Aurelius T.

EDCS-30200167.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning the names Alexander and possibly Aeternalis, likely part of a larger, now mostly lost, monument or dedication.

EDCS-30200168.text_edition: oni DEEI Aurelius ovius SEP et  quia fil-- parent---
EDCS-30200168.translation: ---to the divine --- Aurelius Ovius SEP and because [his] son --- [his] parents ---
EDCS-30200168.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary text, that mentions Aurelius Ovius, a son, and parents, suggesting a dedication related to family members.

EDCS-30200169.text_edition: I(ovi) O(ptimo) M(aximo) Aurelius Sextus Ledri restituit libens
EDCS-30200169.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Sextus Ledri willingly restored this.
EDCS-30200169.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Sextus Ledri's willing restoration of a monument dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a religious dedication or repair work.

EDCS-30200170.text_edition: Tito Aurelio Terenti patri bene merito filii posuerunt
EDCS-30200170.translation: To Titus Aurelius Terentius, well-deserving father, his sons placed this.
EDCS-30200170.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by the sons for their well-deserving father, Titus Aurelius Terentius.

EDCS-30200171.translation: --- placed this for Platorius, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-30200171.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by a wife for her well-deserving husband, Platorius.

EDCS-30200172.text_edition: Titia Ursa puella infelicissima quae vixit nnos plus minus XXV parentes filiae bene merenti et sibi posuerunt
EDCS-30200172.translation: Titia Ursa, most unfortunate girl, who lived about 25 years. Her parents placed this for their well-deserving daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-30200172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titia Ursa, an unfortunate girl who lived approximately 25 years, erected by her parents for her and for themselves, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-30200180.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Verna Veturiani votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30200180.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Verna, slave of Veturianus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30200180.summary: This inscription records Verna, the slave of Veturianus, willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow made to Silvanus Augustus, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-30200377.text_edition: Quintus Murrius Quinti filius Sergia Augusta miles legionis VII annorum XXXV stipendiorum VI hic situs est
EDCS-30200377.translation: Quintus Murrius, son of Quintus, of the Sergian tribe Augusta, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 35 years old, with 6 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-30200377.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Murrius, a Roman soldier of the Seventh Legion, detailing his name, origin, military service, age, and place of burial.

EDCS-30200390.text_edition: Iovi Cohortali Titus Aurelius Proculus libens posuit
EDCS-30200390.translation: To Jupiter Cohortalis. Titus Aurelius Proculus willingly placed this.
EDCS-30200390.summary: This inscription records Titus Aurelius Proculus's willing dedication to Jupiter Cohortalis, likely a local or military cult of Jupiter.

EDCS-30200392.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aelius Maximus vixit annos XXX hic situs est Aeli Vigor Maximilla Cinthena procurante Aurelia Maximilla
EDCS-30200392.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Aelius Maximus lived 30 years. He is buried here. Aelia Vigor Maximilla Cinthena procuring this, with Aurelia Maximilla.
EDCS-30200392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aelius Maximus, who lived 30 years, erected by Aelia Vigor Maximilla Cinthena and Aurelia Maximilla, suggesting a familial or close relationship.

EDCS-30200393.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Parthino Aurelius Dasius IIvir votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30200393.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Parthinus, Aurelius Dasius, duumvir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30200393.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Parthinus by Aurelius Dasius, a duumvir, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200394.text_edition: Deo Soli Invicto  Methri sic  Aurelius Maximinus Flavius Marcellinus Flavius Marcellus  Rumanus sic Marcianus  IV  pinus II ficus X
EDCS-30200394.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras. Aurelius Maximinus, Flavius Marcellinus, Flavius Marcellus, Rumanus, Marcianus. Four pines, two figs, ten.
EDCS-30200394.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by several individuals, possibly indicating a sacred grove with specific trees planted.

EDCS-30200398.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Aelio Censorino optioni classis praetoriae Ravennatis natione nnnico vixit annos XLI militavit annos XXI
EDCS-30200398.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Aelius Censorinus, optiō of the Praetorian fleet of Ravenna, of [---] nation, who lived for 41 years and served for 21 years.
EDCS-30200398.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Aelius Censorinus, an optiō in the Praetorian fleet of Ravenna, detailing his age and length of service.

EDCS-30200400.text_edition: Dis Manibus Alogio qCui#qui#CUI et Saxxonio qui vixit annos XXVI posuit Urbica coniux cum quo vigxit sic concorditer annos sex bene merenti posuuit sic
EDCS-30200400.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Alogius and Saxxonius, who lived for 26 years. Urbica his wife placed this, with whom he lived harmoniously for six years, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-30200400.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Urbica to her husband Alogius and Saxxonius, with whom she lived harmoniously for six years, commemorating his 26 years of life.

EDCS-30200403.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Eutycheti Flavia Frontina marito incomparabili qui mecum vixit annis XVIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30200403.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Eutyches, Flavia Frontina placed this for her incomparable husband, who lived with me for 18 years, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-30200403.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Flavia Frontina for her beloved husband, Aurelius Eutyches, noting their 18 years of marriage.

EDCS-30200404.text_edition: Aulus Cardius Festus Iuliae MaxumNae sic#Maxumae sic#MAXUNE coniugi fecit suae
EDCS-30200404.translation: Aulus Cardius Festus made this for his wife, Iulia Maxuma.
EDCS-30200404.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Aulus Cardius Festus for his wife, Iulia Maxuma, likely for a tomb or monument.

EDCS-30200405.text_edition: Dis Manibus Felici Augusti liberto IONII
EDCS-30200405.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Felix, freedman of Augustus, of IONII.
EDCS-30200405.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felix, a freedman of the emperor Augustus, with an unclear place of origin or affiliation.

EDCS-30200406.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Trophimo Flavius Trophimus filius
EDCS-30200406.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Flavius Trophimus, Flavius Trophimus his son [made this].
EDCS-30200406.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavius Trophimus by his son, also named Flavius Trophimus.

EDCS-30200410.text_edition: et Sentia Primitiva heredes bene merenti
EDCS-30200410.translation: and Sentia Primitiva, heirs, for the well-deserving.
EDCS-30200410.summary: This inscription is likely a fragment of a funerary monument, indicating that Sentia Primitiva and other unnamed heirs dedicated it to a well-deserving individual.

EDCS-30200411.text_edition: Ulpiae Gaudentiae inconparabili sic filiae mensium II dierum VIIII Ulpius Parthenopeus et Iulia Sabina parentis sic posuerunt=M#posuerunt#POSUERUM
EDCS-30200411.translation: To Ulpia Gaudentia, an incomparable daughter, two months and nine days old. Ulpius Parthenopeus and Iulia Sabina, her parents, placed this.
EDCS-30200411.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Ulpia Gaudentia, a young child who lived for only two months and nine days, placed by her parents Ulpius Parthenopeus and Iulia Sabina.

EDCS-30200415.text_edition: Depositus Primus episcopus XII Kalendas Februarias nepos Domniones sic martores sic
EDCS-30200415.translation: Primus, bishop, was buried on the 12th Kalends of February. Grandson of Domnio, thus a martyr, thus.
EDCS-30200415.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Bishop Primus, described as a martyr and grandson of Domnio, buried on January 21st.

EDCS-30200418.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30200418.text_edition: Arca Iohanni satori et iae iugCali#iugali#IUCALI eius  a cum angelis
EDCS-30200418.translation: The sarcophagus for Iohannes, the sower, and his wife, with the angels.
EDCS-30200418.summary: This inscription marks a sarcophagus dedicated to Iohannes, a 'sower,' and his wife, indicating a Christian context with the mention of angels.

EDCS-30200431.text_edition: Caius Lartinius Cai filius Pollia domo Foro Corneli miles legionis VII nnorum XXXIV stipendiorum XVI hic situs est heredes posuerunt
EDCS-30200431.translation: Gaius Lartinius, son of Gaius, of the Pollian tribe, from Forum Cornelii, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, aged 34, with 16 years of service, lies here. His heirs placed this.
EDCS-30200431.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Lartinius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion from Forum Cornelii, erected by his heirs after his death at age 34 with 16 years of service.

EDCS-30200432.text_edition: Marcus Pytha Segni filius miles cohortis II Chyrrhesticorum domo Berea annorum LX stipendiorum XXXV testamento fieri iussit sibi Felici liberto
EDCS-30200432.translation: Marcus Pytha, son of Segnus, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Chyrrhestians, from Beroea, aged 60, with 35 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Felix, his freedman.
EDCS-30200432.summary: This inscription records a tomb ordered by will by Marcus Pytha, a 60-year-old soldier from Beroea with 35 years of service in the Second Cohort of Chyrrhestians, for himself and his freedman Felix.

EDCS-30200433.translation: Tytulius, son of Panentis, placed this for Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30200433.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Tytulius, son of Panentis, to Silvanus Domesticus, likely a deity or a deceased individual.

EDCS-30200434.text_edition: Marcus Domitius Marci filius Fabia Severus Brixsia sic miles legionis XI nnorum XXX stipendiorum VIII hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-30200434.translation: Marcus Domitius, son of Marcus, of the Fabian tribe, Severus, from Brixia, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, aged 30, with 8 years of service, lies here. His heir placed this.
EDCS-30200434.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Domitius Severus, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion from Brixia who died at age 30 with 8 years of service, erected by his heir.

EDCS-30200435.text_edition: Remmo Saeconis filius miles cohortis I Montanorum nnorum
EDCS-30200435.translation: Remmo, son of Saeco, a soldier of the First Cohort of Montani, aged [---] years.
EDCS-30200435.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Remmo, son of Saeco, a soldier of the First Cohort of Montani, with his age missing due to a lacuna.

EDCS-30200436.text_edition: L(uci)? Viri Luci fili Poblilia Novici Verona militis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Aquili Frontonis stipendiorum XXII heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-30200436.translation: For Lucius Virius, son of Lucius, of the Poblilian tribe, Novicius, from Verona, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, in the centuria of Aquilius Fronto, with 22 years of service, his heir had this made.
EDCS-30200436.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Virius Novicius, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis from Verona with 22 years of service, whose monument was erected by his heir.

EDCS-30200437.text_edition: Caius Oppius de sua pecunia Tiberius C   Caius Oppius --- trib(unus) militum legionis    Libra  S I  libra  sextarius   frumentum  hemina  sextarius
EDCS-30200437.translation: Gaius Oppius, from his own money. Tiberius C. Gaius Oppius [---], military tribune of the Legion [---]. A pound [---], a sextarius [---], grain, a hemina, a sextarius.
EDCS-30200437.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment, possibly a dedication or record of public works funded by Gaius Oppius, a military tribune, with mention of measurements related to commodities like grain.

EDCS-30200439.text_edition: Oppia Cai filia Aequina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30200439.translation: Oppia Aequina, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30200439.summary: This is a votive inscription where Oppia Aequina, daughter of Gaius, records the fulfillment of a vow she made.

EDCS-30200440.text_edition: Ex conventione finis inter Ortoplinos et Parentinos aditus ad aquam vivam Ortoplinis passus D latus I
EDCS-30200440.translation: By agreement, the boundary between the Ortoplini and the Parentini. Access to running water for the Ortoplini, 500 paces long, 1 pace wide.
EDCS-30200440.summary: This inscription records a boundary agreement between two communities, the Ortoplini and the Parentini, concerning access to water, likely dating from the 1st century BCE to 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30200441.text_edition: Sestus PLlaturius#Platurius#LLATURIUS TrIti filius vivus fecit sibi et coniugi annorum Dis Manibus
EDCS-30200441.translation: Sextus Plat[ur]ius, son of Titus, made this in his lifetime for himself and his wife, in her years. To the Spirits of the Departed.
EDCS-30200441.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Sextus Platurius in his lifetime for himself and his wife, dedicated to the Manes, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30200442.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Marcellus seplasiarius annorum LXX sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-30200442.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Marcellus, perfumer, 70 years old, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-30200442.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Titus Flavius Marcellus, a perfumer, for himself while still alive, dedicated to the Manes, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30200795.text_edition: Cassiae Cai filiae Annae Caius Cassius Longinus contubernali suae bene merenti fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-30200795.translation: Gaius Cassius Longinus made this for Cassia Anna, daughter of Gaius, his deserving partner, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-30200795.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Gaius Cassius Longinus for his deserving partner, Cassia Anna, and for himself and his family, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30200796.text_edition: Lucius Plaetorius Valens Luciae Plaetoriae filiae loricam fecit quae vixit annos X et sibi et suis fecit
EDCS-30200796.translation: Lucius Plaetorius Valens made this memorial for his daughter, Lucia Plaetoria, who lived 10 years, and made it for himself and his family.
EDCS-30200796.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Plaetorius Valens for his deceased daughter, Lucia Plaetoria, and for himself and his family, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30301342.text_edition: Flavos sic Bouti filius miles cohortis I Lucensium nnorum XXXI stipendiorum X domo Luco Augusti heres faciendum curavit hic situs est
EDCS-30301342.translation: Flavus, son of Boutus, soldier of the First Cohort of the Lucenses, 31 years old, 10 years of service, from Lucus Augusti. His heir took care of having this made. He is laid here.
EDCS-30301342.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavus, a soldier of the First Cohort of the Lucenses, erected by his heir, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30301343.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerio ValIenti#Valenti#VAIENTI protectori defuncto bello civile in Italia nnorum L
EDCS-30301343.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Valerius Valens, a protector, who died in civil war in Italy, 50 years old.
EDCS-30301343.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Valerius Valens, a protector who died in a civil war in Italy, likely dating to a period of internal Roman conflict, such as the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-30301344.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Stercoriae filiae defunctae
EDCS-30301344.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelia Stercoria, his deceased daughter.
EDCS-30301344.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Aurelia Stercoria, likely made by a parent for their deceased daughter, dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30301346.translation: To Paia Carpia, daughter of Plator, years [old].
EDCS-30301346.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Paia Carpia, daughter of Plator, with her age missing, likely dating from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30301347.text_edition: Semproniae Apuleiae quae vixit mecum annos XXII Aurelius Eutichius sic maritus bene merenti posuit  Monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-30301347.translation: To Sempronia Apuleia, who lived with me for 22 years. Aurelius Eutychius, her deserving husband, placed this. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30301347.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Eutychius for his deserving wife, Sempronia Apuleia, with a clause preventing inheritance of the monument, likely dating from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30301348.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vitalioni dulcissimo coniugi qui vixit annos XI et Crescentioni infelicissimo
EDCS-30301348.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Vitalio, my sweetest husband, who lived for eleven years, and to Crescentio, most unfortunate.
EDCS-30301348.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Vitalio, a husband who died at age eleven, and to Crescentio, by an unnamed dedicant, likely a spouse or family member.

EDCS-30301358.text_edition: Marcus Nasidius Secundus decurio municipii veteranus beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae vivus fecit sibi Nasidio Rufino filio annorum XX et
EDCS-30301358.translation: Marcus Nasidius Secundus, decurion of the municipality, veteran, beneficiary of the consular of the X Gemina Legion, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Nasidius Rufinus, his twenty-year-old son, and...
EDCS-30301358.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Marcus Nasidius Secundus, a decurion and veteran, in his lifetime for himself and his son, Nasidius Rufinus.

EDCS-30301359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Proclianus VERI-V NANI decurio municipii et Aeliae Honoratae coniugi carissimae viv sibi posuerunt
EDCS-30301359.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Proclianus VERI-V NANUS, decurion of the municipality, and Aelia Honorata, his dearest wife, placed this for themselves in their lifetime.
EDCS-30301359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Proclianus, a decurion, and his wife Aelia Honorata, which they set up for themselves during their lives.

EDCS-30301361.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Paiio nepotes vixit annos LXXX posuerunt
EDCS-30301361.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Paiio. Her grandchildren placed this. She lived eighty years.
EDCS-30301361.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aelia Paiio, who lived to be eighty years old, set up by her grandchildren.

EDCS-30301362.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Plator S socrui bene merenti
EDCS-30301362.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Plator S for his well-deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-30301362.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Plator S to his deserving mother-in-law.

EDCS-30301367.text_edition: Titus  filio suo Tito annorum novem
EDCS-30301367.translation: Titus to his son Titus, nine years old.
EDCS-30301367.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a father, Titus, to his son, also named Titus, who died at the age of nine.

EDCS-30301368.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating that Flavius Maximus was the maker or dedicator of the monument.

EDCS-30301369.translation: Plator, son of Udianus, placed this for his father.
EDCS-30301369.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Plator, son of Udianus, to his father.

EDCS-30301370.text_edition: iudex datus a Flavio Valerio Constantio viro clarissimo praeside provinciae Delmatiae sic fines inter Salviatas et Stridonenses detrminavit
EDCS-30301370.translation: The judge, appointed by Flavius Valerius Constantius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, thus determined the boundaries between the Salviatae and the Stridonenses.
EDCS-30301370.summary: This inscription records a legal decision concerning the demarcation of boundaries between the Salviatae and Stridonenses, established by a judge appointed by the governor of Dalmatia, Flavius Valerius Constantius, likely dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-30301371.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Iulius Rogatus beneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae et Gaius Iulius Rogatus Iunior voto libentes posuerunt
EDCS-30301371.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Julius Rogatus, beneficiarius consularis of the XI Claudia Legion, and Gaius Julius Rogatus Junior, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30301371.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Rogatus, a beneficiarius consularis, and his son Gaius Julius Rogatus Junior, in fulfillment of a vow, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30301372.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Victoris SINN  Victoris SCI ntissimi et Aefetur OOII
EDCS-30301372.translation: To the Divine Manes of Victor Sinn, of Victor Scintissimus, and of Aefetur OOII.
EDCS-30301372.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of three individuals: Victor Sinn, Victor Scintissimus, and Aefetur OOII.

EDCS-30301373.text_edition: Aelio Proculo basshn qo et Aelio  Aelius Iotus Quartus s pientissimis posuerunt
EDCS-30301373.translation: To Aelius Proculus Basshnqo and to Aelius. Aelius Iotus Quartus placed this for the most pious ones.
EDCS-30301373.summary: This is a funerary inscription, where Aelius Iotus Quartus dedicates a monument to Aelius Proculus Basshnqo and another Aelius.

EDCS-30301374.text_edition: Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus legatus pro praetore Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici iudicem dedit Manium Coelium |(centurionem) legionis VII inter Sapuates et Lamatinos ut fines regeret et terminoVs#terminos#TERMINVS poneret
EDCS-30301374.translation: Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus, legate pro praetore of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, appointed Manius Coelius, centurion of the Seventh Legion, as judge between the Sapuates and the Lamatinos, to regulate boundaries and set up boundary markers.
EDCS-30301374.summary: This inscription records a judicial decision by Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus, a legate of Emperor Gaius (Caligula), appointing a centurion to resolve a border dispute between two local groups, the Sapuates and Lamatinos, likely dating to the early Imperial period (c. 37-41 AD).

EDCS-30301375.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Plator
EDCS-30301375.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelius Plator.
EDCS-30301375.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Plator.

EDCS-30301378.text_edition: Aulus Larcius Clemens Triviis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301378.translation: Aulus Larcius Clemens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Triviae.
EDCS-30301378.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Aulus Larcius Clemens fulfilling a vow to the Triviae (a goddess often associated with crossroads, typically Hecate).

EDCS-30301379.text_edition: Dis Manibus V filius miles cohortisRE centuria Valentis parentes bene merenti fecerunt
EDCS-30301379.translation: To the Divine Manes. V, son of the cohort, soldier of the cohort, of the centuria of Valens. His parents made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-30301379.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents for their son, a soldier in a cohort, who was part of Valens' centuria.

EDCS-30301380.text_edition: Navina A Patavini filia annorum XXXX
EDCS-30301380.translation: Navina, daughter of Patavinus, 40 years old.
EDCS-30301380.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Navina, the daughter of Patavinus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-30301381.text_edition: Panto Ausulinis Cremetis verna nnorum X
EDCS-30301381.translation: Panto, slave born in the household, of Ausulinus Cremetis, 10 years old.
EDCS-30301381.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Panto, a ten-year-old house-born slave belonging to Ausulinus Cremetis.

EDCS-30301382.text_edition: $ Proc]culo Plahetera Triti filia annorum LXXX hic situs est
EDCS-30301382.translation: Proc]culus Plahetera, daughter of Tritus, 80 years old, lies here.
EDCS-30301382.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plahetera, daughter of Tritus, who died at the age of 80, with her first name only partially preserved.

EDCS-30301383.text_edition: Plaes Ctius Macri filius annorum V
EDCS-30301383.translation: Plaes Ctius, son of Macrus, 5 years old.
EDCS-30301383.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plaes Ctius, the son of Macrus, who died at the young age of 5.

EDCS-30301385.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextus Labricus, dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-30301386.text_edition: Trito Montani defuncto annorum XL et Maximae matri eius Maximus filius patri et aviae bene merenti fecit
EDCS-30301386.translation: To Tritus Montanus, deceased, 40 years old, and to Maxima, his mother. Maximus, his son, made this for his father and well-deserving grandmother.
EDCS-30301386.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maximus to his deceased father, Tritus Montanus, and his grandmother, Maxima.

EDCS-30301387.text_edition: Tudanio Maximo Panto mater anAI(norum)#anA(norum)#ANI XLV
EDCS-30301387.translation: To Tudanius Maximus. Panto, his mother, 45 years old.
EDCS-30301387.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tudanius Maximus, dedicated by his mother Panto, who was 45 years old at the time of dedication or death.

EDCS-30301388.text_edition: ae Augustae ia Apli filia unda pro se et suis voto suscepto porticum fecit
EDCS-30301388.translation: [To Juno?] Augusta. Apli's daughter, [---]unda, built the portico for herself and her family, having undertaken a vow.
EDCS-30301388.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico by [---]unda, daughter of Aplus, for herself and her family, as fulfillment of a vow to a deity, possibly Juno Augusta.

EDCS-30301389.text_edition: Lucius Aeronius Silvano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301389.translation: Lucius Aeronius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-30301389.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Lucius Aeronius fulfilled a vow to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-30301392.text_edition: Marcus Fraxsanius sic Sexti filius Pollia domo Regio Lepido veteranus legionis XI eques annorum XLIIII stipendiorum XXV donatus phaleris torquibus armillis hic situs est Primus libertus pro meritis
EDCS-30301392.translation: Marcus Fraxsanius, son of Sextus, of the Pollia tribe, from Regium Lepidum, a veteran cavalryman of the Eleventh Legion, 44 years old, with 25 years of service, decorated with phalerae, torques, and armillae, lies here. Primus, his freedman, made this for his merits.
EDCS-30301392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Fraxsanius, a veteran cavalryman of the Eleventh Legion, detailing his military career and honors, erected by his freedman Primus.

EDCS-30301395.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Eutycheti et Marco Iulio Iuliano Moschis coniugi et filio
EDCS-30301395.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Iulius Eutyches and Marcus Iulius Iulianus. Moschis made this for her husband and son.
EDCS-30301395.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Iulius Eutyches and Marcus Iulius Iulianus by Moschis, their wife and mother respectively.

EDCS-30301396.text_edition: Plaetoriae Titi filiae Maximillae annorum XXIIII Titus Plaetorius Victor pater infelix fecit
EDCS-30301396.translation: To Plaetoria Maximilla, daughter of Titus, 24 years old. Titus Plaetorius Victor, her unhappy father, made this.
EDCS-30301396.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plaetoria Maximilla, dedicated by her sorrowful father, Titus Plaetorius Victor.

EDCS-30301397.text_edition: Veronia Prochne mater infelicissima sibi et filio nnum XX Veronio Sementivo
EDCS-30301397.translation: Veronia Prochne, most unhappy mother, made this for herself and for her son, Veronio Sementivo, 20 years old.
EDCS-30301397.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by the grieving mother Veronia Prochne for herself and her 20-year-old son, Veronio Sementivus.

EDCS-30301398.translation: Coranus willingly fulfilled his vow to Iovilus.
EDCS-30301398.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Coranus fulfilled a vow to the deity Iovilus.

EDCS-30301399.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Fufisius signifer votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301399.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Fufisius, standard-bearer, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301399.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Fufisius, a standard-bearer, for fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30301400.translation: Gaius Voconius, son of Marcus, to Jupiter.
EDCS-30301400.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Jupiter by Gaius Voconius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-30301402.translation: Gaius Atilius Macedo fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301402.summary: This inscription records that Gaius Atilius Macedo fulfilled a vow, likely indicating a successful offering after a prayer.

EDCS-30301403.text_edition: Titus Lollius Titi filius Claudia Tarviso miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis hic situs est
EDCS-30301403.translation: Titus Lollius, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, from Tarviso, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, is buried here.
EDCS-30301403.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Titus Lollius, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, indicating his place of burial.

EDCS-30301404.text_edition: Caius Semius  Cai filius Tromentina Seus Aquis Statiellis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis signifer
EDCS-30301404.translation: Gaius Semius, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, Seus, from Aquae Statiellae, a standard-bearer of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-30301404.summary: This inscription commemorates Gaius Semius, a standard-bearer of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, detailing his origin and military role.

EDCS-30301405.text_edition: $ T]ertullius Novaria miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Cai Verecundi annorum XXIIX stipendiorum XXII testamento poni i(usst)
EDCS-30301405.translation: [A. T]ertullius from Novaria, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the century of Gaius Verecundus, aged 28, with 22 years of service, ordered this to be erected by his will.
EDCS-30301405.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for A. Tertullius, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected according to his will and detailing his age and years of service.

EDCS-30301408.summary: This inscription is a brief funerary record for Aelia, stating she lived for seven years, indicating the death of a child.

EDCS-30301410.translation: Licinius Macer, soldier, with ten years of service [---].
EDCS-30301410.summary: This fragmentary inscription pertains to Licinius Macer, a soldier with ten years of military service.

EDCS-30301412.text_edition: $ defunct-- nnorum ---] et Claudi--  defunct-- nnorum  Claudius Ursus et Aelia Marciana parentes
EDCS-30301412.translation: [---] deceased, aged [---] and Claudius deceased, aged [---]; Claudius Ursus and Aelia Marciana, parents.
EDCS-30301412.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the deaths of two individuals, likely children, erected by their parents Claudius Ursus and Aelia Marciana.

EDCS-30301413.text_edition: pidiae pientissimae quae vixit annos? ---XX  NE
EDCS-30301413.translation: [---] Pidia, most devoted, who lived [---] 20 years.
EDCS-30301413.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a funerary dedication to the most devoted Pidia, mentioning her age as approximately 20 years.

EDCS-30301414.text_edition: Orcleca Daphne Caio Orcleco Scaptia Felici
EDCS-30301414.translation: Orcleca Daphne (to) Gaius Orclecus Felicis, of the Scaptia tribe.
EDCS-30301414.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Orcleca Daphne dedicates a monument to Gaius Orclecus Felicis, likely a male relative, possibly a husband or son.

EDCS-30301415.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, recording the name Quintus or Quinta and their age at death, 60 years.

EDCS-30301416.translation: Valeria Trophime placed this for Luc[ius/a].
EDCS-30301416.summary: This is a funerary dedication where Valeria Trophime erected a monument for an individual named Luc[ius/a].

EDCS-30301418.text_edition: Publi-- Fl[avi-- &  $ vixit a]nnos hic situs est rus heres posuit
EDCS-30301418.translation: Publi[us] Fl[avius] [lived for] years; he is buried here. [His] heir placed this.
EDCS-30301418.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Flavius, noting his lifespan and burial location, erected by his heir.

EDCS-30301425.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to a patron, acknowledging their merit, likely from a client or freedperson.

EDCS-30301431.text_edition: Verus Vecae fI(ilius)#f(ilius)#I domo Bodiontius miles cohortis III Alpinorum a annorum  stip[endiorum &
EDCS-30301431.translation: Verus, son of Veca, from Bodiontium, a soldier of the III Alpine cohort, [of] years [and] [---] years of service.
EDCS-30301431.summary: This is a military epitaph for Verus, son of Veca, a soldier from Bodiontium serving in the III Alpine cohort, noting his origins and military service.

EDCS-30301432.text_edition: Publio Cloelio Publi liberto Hilaro et Cloeliae Publi libertae Fuscae optume! de se meritis patronus posuit
EDCS-30301432.translation: For Publius Cloelius Hilarus, freedman of Publius, and Cloelia Fusca, freedwoman of Publius, who deserved excellently from him, their patron placed this.
EDCS-30301432.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a patron to his two well-deserving freedpersons, Publius Cloelius Hilarus and Cloelia Fusca.

EDCS-30301436.text_edition: Dis Manibus   Helladio diem functo annorum Septimia Exuperia dulcissimo coniugi et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-30301436.translation: To the spirits of the dead. For Helladius, who completed his day, of [---] years. Septimia Exuperia placed this for her sweetest husband and for herself while living.
EDCS-30301436.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helladius, erected by his wife Septimia Exuperia for him and for herself in anticipation of her own death.

EDCS-30301437.text_edition: Caio Titio Maximo annorum XXX Sextus Titius Celsus pater
EDCS-30301437.translation: To Gaius Titius Maximus, of 30 years. Sextus Titius Celsus, father.
EDCS-30301437.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Titius Maximus, who died at 30 years old, dedicated by his father, Sextus Titius Celsus.

EDCS-30301439.text_edition: ANOI Lucius Publicius Tertius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301439.translation: [Unknown initial letters] Lucius Publicius Tertius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301439.summary: This is a votive inscription where Lucius Publicius Tertius fulfilled a vow, indicating an offering to a deity or for a specific purpose.

EDCS-30301440.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassia Paulina ex voto posuit
EDCS-30301440.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Cassia Paulina placed this ex voto.
EDCS-30301440.summary: This inscription records a votive offering dedicated by Cassia Paulina to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30301441.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Iulia Firmilla votum solvit libens merito
