EDCS-30301441.translation: Sacred to Father Liber, Julia Firmilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30301441.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication by Julia Firmilla to Father Liber, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30301442.translation: To the Great Mother, Gaius Petronius Philippus [dedicated this].
EDCS-30301442.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Petronius Philippus to the Great Mother, likely an altar or monument.

EDCS-30301443.translation: VADISA willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30301443.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by VADISA, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30301444.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucillae defunctae annorum XII Lucida mater filiae pientissimae fecit
EDCS-30301444.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucida, mother, made this for her most devoted daughter Lucilla, who died at 12 years of age.
EDCS-30301444.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Lucida for her deceased daughter Lucilla, who died at the age of twelve.

EDCS-30301446.translation: 12 days. The unhappy parents placed this.
EDCS-30301446.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, likely for an infant who lived for 12 days, placed by the grieving parents.

EDCS-30301449.text_edition: Vesia Cai filia Quincta votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301449.translation: Vesia Quincta, daughter of Gaius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30301449.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Vesia Quincta, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30301450.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Epetinus Caius Avillius Clemens Lucius Coelius Capella Publius Raecius Libo Publius Valerius Secundus iudices dati a Marco Pompeio Silvano legato Augusti pro praetore inter rem publicam Asseriatium et rem publicam Alveritarum in re praesenti per sententiam suam determinaverunt
EDCS-30301450.translation: Tiberius Claudius Epetinus, Gaius Avillius Clemens, Lucius Coelius Capella, Publius Raecius Libo, Publius Valerius Secundus, judges appointed by Marcus Pompeius Silvanus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power, determined by their judgment, concerning the present matter, between the community of Asseria and the community of Alveria.
EDCS-30301450.summary: This inscription records the legal decision made by a panel of five judges, appointed by a provincial governor, to resolve a dispute between two communities, Asseria and Alveria.

EDCS-30301451.text_edition: Quintus Magius Cai filius Publilia domo Verona veteranus legionis VII nnm L stipendiorum XXV testamento fieri iussit hic situs est
EDCS-30301451.translation: Quintus Magius, son of Gaius, of the Publilian tribe, from Verona, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, 50 years old, with 25 years of service, ordered this to be made by his testament. He is buried here.
EDCS-30301451.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Quintus Magius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion from Verona, erected according to his will, specifying his age and years of military service.

EDCS-30301453.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Tito Publicio SatIurnino#Saturnino#SAIURNINO fEratri#fratri#ER omniLbus#omnibus#OMNLBUS honoribus patriae suae functIo#functo#FUNCIO StIennia#Stennia#SIENNIA FaustIina#Faustina#FAUSIINA soror
EDCS-30301453.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Titus Publicius Saturninus, her brother, who held all honors in his native city, Stennia Faustina, his sister, [made this].
EDCS-30301453.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication made by Stennia Faustina to her brother, Titus Publicius Saturninus, who had held all public offices in his hometown.

EDCS-30301460.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hel<v=S>(iae#Helv(iae#HELS FIti quae vixit annos plus minus XXX  Felix maritus pientissimus posuit
EDCS-30301460.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Helvia, who lived for approximately 30 years. Felix, her most devoted husband, set this up.
EDCS-30301460.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by the devoted husband Felix to his deceased wife Helvia, who died at around 30 years of age.

EDCS-30301461.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary epitaph, with only parts of names legible.

EDCS-30301466.text_edition: Iovi Optimo sacrum ex voto Marcus Ac
EDCS-30301466.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus by vow, Marcus Ac[...].
EDCS-30301466.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus (Best and Greatest) made by Marcus Ac[...] as part of a vow.

EDCS-30301467.text_edition: Recus Sentius Iovi OptI(imo)#Opt(imo)#OPI Maxumo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301467.translation: Recus Sentius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-30301467.summary: This inscription records that Recus Sentius fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-30301468.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum votum solvit Vincentius votum posuit
EDCS-30301468.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Vincentius fulfilled his vow; he placed this vow.
EDCS-30301468.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, stating that Vincentius fulfilled and placed a vow.

EDCS-30301469.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely indicating a boundary or enclosure was made by someone named Certus, whose praenomen is lost.

EDCS-30301470.text_edition: Marcus Plusius V Ammianus nnrum XXIII Siscia natus Titius Genialis amico fecit
EDCS-30301470.translation: Marcus Plusius Ammianus, 23 years old, born in Siscia. Titius Genialis made this for his friend.
EDCS-30301470.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Titius Genialis for his friend Marcus Plusius Ammianus, who was 23 years old and born in Siscia.

EDCS-30301472.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Gratillae annorum XXVIII Claudia Eutychia mater posuit
EDCS-30301472.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Claudia Gratilla, 28 years old. Claudia Eutychia, her mother, set this up.
EDCS-30301472.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Eutychia to her deceased daughter, Claudia Gratilla, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-30301473.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, with only parts of the name Claudia Glycera tentatively legible.

EDCS-30301475.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius M posuit
EDCS-30301475.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius M[...].
EDCS-30301475.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Publius Aelius, whose cognomen is lost.

EDCS-30301477.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ter Luci libertus Severus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30301477.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Severus, freedman of Lucius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301477.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius' freedman Severus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30301481.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Procula Iuliae Veturiae matri suae pientissimae ob memoriam posuit
EDCS-30301481.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aelia Procula placed this in memory of her most devoted mother, Iulia Veturia.
EDCS-30301481.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Procula to her mother Iulia Veturia, a common practice in Roman burial.

EDCS-30301482.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Calpurnio Polioni  Caio Calpurnio Maximo avo Lucio Calpurnio Polioni Sextiliae Proculae parentibus Lucio Calpurnio Polioni fratri Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus
EDCS-30301482.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Gaius Calpurnius Polio, for Gaius Calpurnius Maximus, his grandfather, for Lucius Calpurnius Polio and Sextilia Procula, his parents, for Lucius Calpurnius Polio, his brother, Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus [set this up].
EDCS-30301482.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus for multiple deceased family members, including his grandfather, parents, and brother.

EDCS-30301483.translation: To Gaius Iulius Rufinus, Gaius Iulius [set this up].
EDCS-30301483.summary: This concise inscription is a dedication by Gaius Iulius to Gaius Iulius Rufinus, likely a tombstone or a monument of remembrance.

EDCS-30301484.text_edition: sius etI#et#EI  Maxima matri suae  faciendum curaverunt
EDCS-30301484.translation: Sius and Maxima had this made for their mother.
EDCS-30301484.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription erected by 'Sius' and Maxima for their mother.

EDCS-30301487.text_edition: Ex edictu sic Publi Corneli Dolabellae legati pro praetore determinavit Spurius Titius Geminus princeps posterior legionis VII inter Neditas et Corinienses restituti iussu Auli Duceni Gemini legati Augusti pro praetore per Aulum Resium Maximum |(centurionem) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis principem posteriorem et Quintum Aebutium Liberalem hastatum posteriorem legionis eiusdem
EDCS-30301487.translation: By edict, Publius Cornelius Dolabella, legate pro praetore, determined thus: Spurius Titius Geminus, junior princeps of the Seventh Legion, acting on the order of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, legate Augusti pro praetore, through Aulus Resius Maximus, centurion of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis and junior princeps, and Quintus Aebutius Liberalis, junior hastatus of the same legion, restored [the boundary] between the Neditas and the Corinienses.
EDCS-30301487.summary: This inscription records a boundary demarcation between two communities, Neditas and Corinienses, carried out by military personnel under the authority of Roman legates, likely dating to the early Imperial period given the official titles.

EDCS-30400554.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luci Lentidi Saturnini veterani legionis primae Minerviae piae fidelis vivus posuit sibi AVG et coniugi Ulpiae Crispinae annorum LX hic sita est
EDCS-30400554.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Lucius Lentidius Saturninus, a veteran of the First Legion Minervia Pia Fidelis, placed this for himself while living, and for his wife Ulpia Crispina, aged 60, who lies here.
EDCS-30400554.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Lentidius Saturninus, a veteran, for himself and his wife Ulpia Crispina while he was still alive, a common practice for securing a burial spot.

EDCS-30400555.text_edition: Silvano Caius O C pro salute sua
EDCS-30400555.translation: To Silvanus, Gaius O. C. for his own well-being.
EDCS-30400555.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Silvanus by Gaius O. C. for his own health or safety.

EDCS-30400556.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-30400556.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-30400556.summary: This inscription is a public dedication or monument honoring the Emperor Decius, likely dating to his reign (249-251 AD).

EDCS-30400557.text_edition: Ulpiae linae  carissimae defunctae annorum LII se vivo sibi liberisque
EDCS-30400557.translation: To Ulpia Lina, dearest, who died at 52 years of age. [He made this] for himself and his children while he was alive.
EDCS-30400557.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Lina, erected by her husband (implied) for her, himself, and their children while he was still living.

EDCS-30400559.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus S
EDCS-30400559.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus S[---]
EDCS-30400559.summary: This is a fragment of an imperial inscription, likely commemorating or dedicating something in the name of Emperor Florianus, who reigned briefly in 276 CE.

EDCS-30400580.text_edition: Lucius Cornelius Sabinus Silvano votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400580.translation: Lucius Cornelius Sabinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-30400580.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Lucius Cornelius Sabinus to the god Silvanus, likely on a small altar or shrine.

EDCS-30400581.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilio Silvestro filio pientissimo qui vixit annos XXXXV Aemilia Ursina mater et Silvinus pater bene merenti fecerunt  Dis Manibus sacrum Semele coniugi carissimae nnorum XXVI Victor consservus
EDCS-30400581.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Aemilius Silvester, a most devoted son, who lived 45 years. Aemilia Ursina, his mother, and Silvinus, his father, made this for their well-deserving son. Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Semele, a most beloved wife, aged 26 years. Victor, her fellow slave.
EDCS-30400581.summary: This monument is a tombstone, likely a double inscription, dedicated by parents to their son Aemilius Silvester, and by a fellow slave Victor to his beloved wife Semele, suggesting it might have originally been part of a larger funerary complex or a shared monument for different individuals.

EDCS-30400585.summary: This is a very brief and fragmentary inscription, possibly marking a grave or property of someone named Galerius.

EDCS-30400586.text_edition: RCVCVBIS Carpus annorum C vivos sic sibi posuit
EDCS-30400586.translation: RCVCVBIS Carpus, 100 years old, while living, thus placed this for himself.
EDCS-30400586.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Carpus, remarkably aged 100, preemptively set up his own tomb or monument while still alive.

EDCS-30400587.text_edition: Dis Manibus s(acrum) Nantia  IIvir infelicissim
EDCS-30400587.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nantia, most unfortunate duumvir.
EDCS-30400587.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Nantia, identified as a 'duumvir,' a Roman magistrate, but described as 'most unfortunate,' suggesting a tragic or untimely end.

EDCS-30400589.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Sabinus decurio TER alae Claudiae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400589.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Flavius Sabinus, decurion for the third time of the Claudian ala, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30400589.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication made by Titus Flavius Sabinus, a decurion of a cavalry unit (ala), to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, indicating gratitude or fulfillment of a promise.

EDCS-30400590.summary: This is a simple name inscription, likely a fragment from a larger monument, perhaps a gravestone or a property marker.

EDCS-30400591.text_edition: Silvano Silvestro sic sacrum Andes posuit
EDCS-30400591.translation: Thus Andes placed this sacred offering to Silvanus Silvester.
EDCS-30400591.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Andes to a deity named Silvanus Silvester, likely a local or specific aspect of the god Silvanus.

EDCS-30400592.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Rufus annorum XXV milIis sic#milis sic#MIIIS legionis sicundae sic Aurelius TIurranius#Turranius#IURRANIUS pater fI(ilio)#f(ilio)#I hic situs est
EDCS-30400592.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Rufus, 25 years old, soldier of the Second Legion. Aurelius Turranius, his father, lies here.
EDCS-30400592.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Aurelius Rufus, a 25-year-old soldier of the Second Legion, and also indicates that his father, Aurelius Turranius, is buried at the same location.

EDCS-30400594.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureli Ursi annorum LXXXV hic situs est
EDCS-30400594.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Ursus, 85 years old, lies here.
EDCS-30400594.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Aurelius Ursus, likely erected by his family or an associate.

EDCS-30400595.text_edition: Dis Manibus Di Anadri annorum LXII Gaius Iulius Certus amitae dignissimae et nutrici posuit hic sita est
EDCS-30400595.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Di Anader, 62 years old, lies here. Gaius Julius Certus placed this for his most deserving paternal aunt and nurse.
EDCS-30400595.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Julius Certus to his beloved aunt and nurse, Di Anader.

EDCS-30400596.text_edition: Caio Iulio Celeri Cai  liberto annorum LX et ClarIo#Claro#CLAIO annorum XVI Priscus patri et fratri
EDCS-30400596.translation: To Gaius Julius Celer, freedman of Gaius, 60 years old, and to Clar(ius)/Cla(rus)/Claius, 16 years old. Priscus (set this up) for his father and brother.
EDCS-30400596.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Priscus to his father, Gaius Julius Celer, a freedman, and his brother, Clar(ius)/Cla(rus)/Claius.

EDCS-30400597.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Nepoti nnorum L Ditueio marito incomparabili posuit hic situs (est)
EDCS-30400597.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Julius Nepos, 50 years old. Ditueius placed this for his incomparable husband. He lies here.
EDCS-30400597.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Ditueius for her incomparable husband, Julius Nepos.

EDCS-30400598.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae GTMA CF N
EDCS-30400598.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Julia [---].
EDCS-30400598.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Julia.

EDCS-30400599.text_edition: Leoni F P R O I R S
EDCS-30400599.translation: To Leo, father priest of the Invincible Omnipotent King. To Fontus, father priest of the Invincible Omnipotent King.
EDCS-30400599.summary: This inscription records a double dedication to Leo and Fontus, likely deities or revered figures, by individuals identified as 'father priests of the Invincible Omnipotent King,' possibly indicating a Mithraic context from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30400602.summary: This is a fragmentary religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a person named Lucius.

EDCS-30400606.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Sextilius beneficiarius consularis eLx#ex#LX legione XIIII gemina votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400606.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Julius Sextilius, a beneficiarius consularis from the Legio XIIII Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30400606.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Julius Sextilius, a beneficiarius consularis from the Legio XIIII Gemina, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30400607.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelia Procula Iulio Secundo coniugi pientissimo Laurenti Lavinatium sic qui vixsit annos XXXXII
EDCS-30400607.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Procula (set this up) for Julius Secundus, her most devoted husband, a Laurentian from Lavinium, who lived 42 years.
EDCS-30400607.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelia Procula to her most devoted husband, Julius Secundus, who was a Laurentian from Lavinium.

EDCS-30400614.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci municipii Metulensium Aurelius Maximus |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis votum posuit libens numini maiestatique eius Imperatore domino nostro Diocletiano
EDCS-30400614.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the municipality of Metulum. Aurelius Maximus, a centurion of Legio II Adiutrix, willingly placed this vow to his divinity and majesty, during the reign of our lord Emperor Diocletian.
EDCS-30400614.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of Metulum by Aurelius Maximus, a centurion of Legio II Adiutrix, during the reign of Emperor Diocletian (late 3rd century AD).

EDCS-30400616.translation: Cronius fulfilled his vow to Janus Pater.
EDCS-30400616.summary: This inscription records Cronius fulfilling a vow to the god Janus Pater.

EDCS-30400617.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, [...]onius F(ecit or Filius).
EDCS-30400617.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by an individual whose name is partially preserved as [...]onius.

EDCS-30400618.text_edition: Lucius Granius Voltimesis filius Rufus Iutossicae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400618.translation: Lucius Granius Rufus, son of Voltimesis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Iutossica.
EDCS-30400618.summary: This inscription records Lucius Granius Rufus fulfilling a vow to the deity Iutossica.

EDCS-30400619.text_edition: Geminus Boninus Hostiducis Sentonae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400619.translation: Geminus Boninus, son of Hostidux, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sentona.
EDCS-30400619.summary: This inscription records Geminus Boninus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Sentona.

EDCS-30400620.text_edition: Sentonae Felix Augusti nostri votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400620.translation: Felix, a slave of our Augustus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sentona.
EDCS-30400620.summary: This inscription records Felix, an imperial slave, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Sentona.

EDCS-30400621.summary: This is a dedication by Avillius to the Silvanae, a group of woodland goddesses.

EDCS-30400622.text_edition: Sextus Ceion(ius) Voltimesis filius  votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30400622.translation: Sextus Ceionius, son of Voltimesis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30400622.summary: This inscription records Sextus Ceionius fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30400623.translation: His mother placed this for Sextus Cliticus.
EDCS-30400623.summary: This inscription records a mother erecting a monument for her son, Sextus Cliticus.

EDCS-30600011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunatae quae vixit nnos XVIII Maximus Ianuari TITVSLAT suae delicatae et sibi posuit libens
EDCS-30600011.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Fortunata, who lived 18 years, Maximus Ianuarius TITVSLAT placed this for his beloved (wife or daughter) and for himself, willingly.
EDCS-30600011.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Maximus Ianuarius TITVSLAT to the memory of Fortunata, who died at age 18, and also for himself, likely anticipating his own burial.

EDCS-30600012.text_edition: Dis Manibus Maximinae filiae infelici Domitia mater bene merenti
EDCS-30600012.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for the unfortunate daughter Maximina, her mother Domitia [placed this] for her well-deserving child.
EDCS-30600012.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Domitia, a mother, for her deceased daughter Maximina.

EDCS-30600013.text_edition: Petronius et mater Maximilla Titivillae defunctae nnorum XXX filiae infelicissimae titulum posuerunt
EDCS-30600013.translation: Petronius and his mother Maximilla set up this tombstone for their most unfortunate daughter Titivilla, who died at the age of 30.
EDCS-30600013.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Petronius and Maximilla's dedication of a tombstone to their deceased daughter, Titivilla, who was 30 years old.

EDCS-30600017.translation: To Basilla, my dearest wife, Gaius Toberius [set this up].
EDCS-30600017.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Toberius to his beloved wife, Basilla.

EDCS-30600018.text_edition: pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate  Blaeso legato pro praetore
EDCS-30600018.translation: To the Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power, to Blaesus, legate pro praetore.
EDCS-30600018.summary: This inscription seems to be part of a larger dedication to an emperor (Pontifex Maximus with tribunician power) and to a governor, Blaesus, who served as a legate pro praetore.

EDCS-30600019.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius filius Aeliae Severae matri suae pientissimae libens posuit defunctae nnorum XXXVII
EDCS-30600019.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelius, son of Aelia Severa, willingly set this up for his most devoted mother, Aelia Severa, who died at the age of 37.
EDCS-30600019.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aelius, dedicating a monument to his most devoted mother, Aelia Severa, who died at 37 years old.

EDCS-30600020.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Veloci defuncto nnorum LXXV Iulius Saturninus filius patri posuit
EDCS-30600020.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Gaius Julius Veloc, who died at the age of 75, his son Julius Saturninus set this up for his father.
EDCS-30600020.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Julius Saturninus dedicates a monument to his father, Gaius Julius Veloc, who passed away at 75 years old.

EDCS-30600021.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Apludus MEVERTENS Pladomeni filius A magistro - publice
EDCS-30600021.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Apludus Mevertens, son of Pladomenus, from the master, publicly [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600021.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Apludus Mevertens, son of Pladomenus, likely on behalf of a master or collegium.

EDCS-30600022.text_edition: Tritus GermulIlius#Germullius#GERMUIL Germani filius decurio hic situs est
EDCS-30600022.translation: Tritus Germullius, son of Germanus, a decurion, is buried here.
EDCS-30600022.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Tritus Germullius, a decurion and son of Germanus.

EDCS-30600023.text_edition: Aplis Lunnicus Triti filius annorum XV
EDCS-30600023.translation: Aplis Lunnicus, son of Tritus, aged 15 years.
EDCS-30600023.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription or a record of a young individual named Aplis Lunnicus, son of Tritus, who was 15 years old.

EDCS-30600024.text_edition: Publius Cornelius Publi filius Eporediensis miles legionis XI nnorum XXX stipendiorum VIII
EDCS-30600024.translation: Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, from Eporedia, a soldier of the 11th legion, aged 30, with 8 years of service.
EDCS-30600024.summary: This is a funerary or military record for Publius Cornelius, a soldier of the XI Legion from Eporedia, who died at 30 after 8 years of service.

EDCS-30600025.inscription_type: carmina, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30600025.text_edition: Conditus infelix in ista sede perenni Principius habitat huic vitae finis in ipsis ter senis misero et quattuor paene peractis annis acerbVa#acerba#ACERVA fuit nam studiis iam Romae laetantem invida fortuna repenti funere mersit cuiusque reversum crudeli funere corpus exequitur genitor iniquo cum honore sepulcri iam securi suo gCaudete#gaudete#CAUDETE munere Manes Elysios per campos et dulcia prata vagantes
EDCS-30600025.translation: Unfortunate Principius, laid to rest in this eternal abode, dwells here. The end of his life was bitter, almost at eighteen years completed for the wretch. For as he was already delighting in his studies at Rome, envious fortune suddenly drowned him with a premature death. His body, having returned in cruel death, is honored by his father with an unworthy tomb. Now, secure in their gift, the Manes rejoice, wandering through the Elysian fields and sweet meadows.
EDCS-30600025.summary: This is a poetic funerary inscription for Principius, a young man who died tragically at the age of eighteen while studying in Rome, set up by his grieving father.

EDCS-30600026.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Tiberi fI(ilius)#f(ilius)#I Quadratus Claudio Aequo -- nnorum XXXV sibi et Pelsoniae Tertullae uxori testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-30600026.translation: Tiberius Claudius Quadratus, son of Tiberius, aged 35 years, ordered this to be made by his will for himself and his wife Pelsonia Tertulla.
EDCS-30600026.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Tiberius Claudius Quadratus for himself and his wife, as per his will.

EDCS-30600027.inscription_type: carmina, inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30600027.text_edition: Publius Fannius Aniensis Cremona eques legionis XI annorum XXIX stipendiorum XII hic situs est frater fratri posuit hospes resiste et tumulum contempla meum morare gressum litteras nostras lege mando et stiloilos alter [ut sciat &   II legem --- nam? fatus aliquid? ex? improviso facit titulum intuere scriptum quo fato occidi rumore sancto Illyrici iaceo in solo infunde lacrumas sic quisquis es mihi misericors aetate cuncta ceciderunt mecum brevi aequo animo vive vitam quoad fieri potest    OIAM CNORIS AL R quaedam  SADEMII  nomen in P  quorum     prima nocte  vivo fieri   satis
EDCS-30600027.translation: Publius Fannius of the Aniensis tribe, from Cremona, a cavalryman of the Eleventh Legion, 29 years old, with 12 years of service, is laid here. His brother placed this for his brother. Stranger, stop and contemplate my tomb; halt your step, read our letters. I command, and with stylus in hand, another [---] that he may know the law [---] For fate [---] does something unexpectedly. Look at the inscribed title, by what fate I died, by holy rumor. I lie on Illyrian soil. Pour tears, whoever you are, pitying me. Everything perished with me in a short time. Live with an even mind as long as possible. [Further untranslatable fragments].
EDCS-30600027.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription for Publius Fannius, a cavalryman of the Eleventh Legion, placed by his brother, including a poetic address to passersby lamenting his unexpected death in Illyria.

EDCS-30600028.text_edition: Lucius Icconius Luci filius Aniensis Surio Caesaraugusta miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Titi Silvani stipendiorum XXIII testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-30600028.translation: Lucius Icconius Surio, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, from Caesaraugusta, a soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, in the century of Titus Silvanus, with 23 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. His heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-30600028.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Icconius Surio, a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia, erected by his heir as per his will.

EDCS-30600029.text_edition: Aulus Sentius Auli filius Pomptina Arretio veteranus legionis XI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit hic est occisus finibus Varvarinorum in agello secus Titum flumen ad Petram Longam faciendum curavit heres Quintus Calventius Luci filius Vitalis
EDCS-30600029.translation: Aulus Sentius, son of Aulus, of the Pomptina tribe, from Arretium, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, is laid here. He ordered this to be made by his will. He was killed in the territory of the Varvarini, in a small field near the Titus river, at Long Stone. His heir, Quintus Calventius Vitalis, son of Lucius, saw to its construction.
EDCS-30600029.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Sentius, a veteran of the Legio XI, who was killed in the territory of the Varvarini, erected by his heir as per his will.

EDCS-30600031.text_edition: Sexto Saenio Sexti liberto Hermeroti nnorum LXX Saenia Campana marito suo posuit et sibi hic situs est
EDCS-30600031.translation: To Sextus Saenius Hermeros, freedman of Sextus, 70 years old. Saenia Campana placed this for her husband and for herself. He is laid here.
EDCS-30600031.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Saenia Campana to her husband, Sextus Saenius Hermeros, a freedman, and also for herself.

EDCS-30600034.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Augusto sacrum Caius Valius Festus conditor vineae huius loci qui nunc Valianus a Festo dicitur aeternumque tenet per saecula nomen voto succepto sic aram adampliavit et tauro immolando dedicavit
EDCS-30600034.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus. Gaius Valius Festus, founder of this vineyard, which is now called Valianus after Festus and holds the name forever through the ages, in fulfillment of a vow, thus enlarged the altar and dedicated it by sacrificing a bull.
EDCS-30600034.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus by Gaius Valius Festus, the founder of a vineyard, recording the enlargement and dedication of an altar with a bull sacrifice in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30600035.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescentia mater Crescentio filio infelicissimo qui vixit annos VII menses III
EDCS-30600035.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Crescentia, the mother, to her most unfortunate son Crescentius, who lived 7 years and 3 months.
EDCS-30600035.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a mother, Crescentia, dedicated to her young, deceased son, Crescentius.

EDCS-30600036.text_edition: Placido infelicissimo annorum XXX Dicatina mater
EDCS-30600036.translation: To the most unfortunate Placidus, 30 years old. Dicatina, his mother.
EDCS-30600036.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a mother, Dicatina, for her deceased son, Placidus.

EDCS-30600037.text_edition: Lucius T Lysonis filius Longinus testamento iussit fieri
EDCS-30600037.translation: Lucius T. Longinus, son of Lyson, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-30600037.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Lucius T. Longinus ordered its creation by his will.

EDCS-30600084.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Baetae defunctae annorum LXX Aureliae Aioia et Pinenta matri pro parte et Marcus Aurelii Memor et Baeta Aurelio Vindici filio huius Baetae defuncto annorum XXXV patri pro parte procurante inM#in#IM parte huius tituli et praebente sumptus Aurelia Supera matre sua
EDCS-30600084.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelia Baeta, deceased, 70 years old. Aurelia Aioia and Pinenta (placed this) for their mother, in part. And Marcus Aurelius Memor and Baeta (placed this) for Aurelius Vindex, son of this deceased Baeta, 35 years old, for their father, in part, with Aurelia Supera, his mother, managing this part of the inscription and providing the expenses.
EDCS-30600084.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Baeta, an elderly woman, and her son Aurelius Vindex, with various family members contributing to its erection and expenses.

EDCS-30600085.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpia Enena vixit annos XL hic sita est Publius Aurelius Quintinus nepti piissimae posuit
EDCS-30600085.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ulpia Enena lived for 40 years; she is laid here. Publius Aurelius Quintinus placed this for his most devoted granddaughter.
EDCS-30600085.summary: This is a funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, erected by Publius Aurelius Quintinus for his granddaughter, Ulpia Enena, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-30600086.text_edition: Libero Patri Titus Aurelius Proculus libens posuit
EDCS-30600086.translation: To Liber Pater, Titus Aurelius Proculus willingly placed this.
EDCS-30600086.summary: This is a votive dedication to the god Liber Pater, set up by Titus Aurelius Proculus.

EDCS-30600087.text_edition: Pro salute Augusti Iulius  Nepotianus ex voto pro pietate
EDCS-30600087.translation: For the health of Augustus, Julius Nepotianus, in accordance with a vow and out of piety.
EDCS-30600087.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Julius Nepotianus for the well-being of the emperor Augustus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30600088.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Menander
EDCS-30600088.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aurelius Menander [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600088.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Titus Aurelius Menander.

EDCS-30600089.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Titus Statius Crispinus decurio equitum cohortis II Delmatarum
EDCS-30600089.translation: To Diana Augusta, Titus Statius Crispinus, decurion of the cavalry of the Second Cohort of Dalmatians [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600089.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Diana Augusta, set up by Titus Statius Crispinus, a decurion in a Dalmatian cavalry unit.

EDCS-30600090.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci sacro sic dicavit Iulius Maternus |(centurio) cohortis VIII voluntariorum Laterano et Rufino consulibus
EDCS-30600090.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and to the sacred Genius of the place, Julius Maternus, centurion of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, thus dedicated this, in the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.
EDCS-30600090.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius Loci, made by Julius Maternus, a centurion, dated to the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus (262 AD).

EDCS-30600123.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo advento sic et pro salute et victorias sic Impperatorum nnostrorum Auggustorum populus  posuit
EDCS-30600123.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, on the arrival and for the safety and victories of our Augusti Emperors, the people placed this.
EDCS-30600123.summary: This is a public votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, set up by the populace to commemorate the arrival, safety, and victories of the emperors.

EDCS-30600124.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aemilius Titian=IIus#Titianus#TITIAIIUS IIvir quinquennalis libens posuit
EDCS-30600124.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Aemilius Titianus, quinquennial duumvir, willingly placed this.
EDCS-30600124.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Marcus Aemilius Titianus, a quinquennial duumvir, a high-ranking municipal magistrate.

EDCS-30600125.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori Marcus Aemilius Antonius IIvir libens posuit
EDCS-30600125.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus the Thunderer, Marcus Aemilius Antonius, duumvir, willingly placed this.
EDCS-30600125.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator (the Thunderer), set up by Marcus Aemilius Antonius, a duumvir.

EDCS-30600126.text_edition: quinquennali et sacerdoti sacerdotali et Aureli?  VM Publius Aelius Firminianus
EDCS-30600126.translation: [To the] quinquennial and priest, sacerdotal, and to Aurelius [---] Publius Aelius Firminianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600126.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or monument, set up by Publius Aelius Firminianus, mentioning someone who held quinquennial and priestly offices.

EDCS-30600128.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aelio Scaeviano qui vixit annos LXX Aelii Titianus et Aelianus filii posuerunt pro pietate
EDCS-30600128.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aelii Titianus and Aelianus, his sons, placed this for their piety, for Titus Aelius Scaevianus, who lived for 70 years.
EDCS-30600128.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelii Titianus and Aelianus in honor of their father, Titus Aelius Scaevianus, who lived to age 70.

EDCS-30600129.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelio Apro qui vixit annos L et Aureliae Candidae coniugigi
EDCS-30600129.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Aper, who lived for 50 years, and for Aurelia Candida, his wife.
EDCS-30600129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Aper, who died at age 50, and his wife Aurelia Candida, likely erected by a surviving family member or for both of them.

EDCS-30600130.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelio Apro qui vixit annos XXXX Lucida coniugi pientissimo posuit
EDCS-30600130.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucida placed this for her most devoted husband, Aurelius Aper, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-30600130.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucida for her very devoted husband, Aurelius Aper, who died at age 40.

EDCS-30600131.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Pantoni quae vixit annos XXXX Titus Aurelius Quintus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30600131.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Quintus placed this for his well-deserving wife, Aurelia Panto, who lived for 40 years.
EDCS-30600131.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Aurelius Quintus for his wife, Aurelia Panto, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-30600132.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Tittoni quae vixit annos XLV Aurelius Carus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30600132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelius Carus placed this for his well-deserving wife, Aurelia Titto, who lived for 45 years.
EDCS-30600132.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelius Carus to his wife, Aurelia Titto, who lived to be 45 years old.

EDCS-30600133.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Tritanoni quae vixit annos XXX Aurelius Maxsimus sic coniugi
EDCS-30600133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelius Maximus [placed] this for his wife, Aurelia Tritano, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-30600133.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Maximus to his wife, Aurelia Tritano, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-30600134.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Vendoni quae vixit annos XXIII Titus Aurelius Us
EDCS-30600134.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Aurelia Vendo, who lived for 23 years. Titus Aurelius Us[---].
EDCS-30600134.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Vendo, who died at age 23, likely erected by Titus Aurelius Us[...].

EDCS-30600135.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Sextus Statius Restitutus sibi et Aureliae Testoni coniugi posuit
EDCS-30600135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Sextus Statius Restitutus placed this for himself and for Aurelia Testo, his wife.
EDCS-30600135.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Sextus Statius Restitutus for himself and his wife, Aurelia Testo, during their lifetime.

EDCS-30600137.text_edition: Dis Manibus Catoni Statariae Tuionis quae vixit annos L Quintus Aelius Statianus coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-30600137.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus Aelius Statianus placed this for his well-deserving wife, Cato Stataria Tuionis, who lived for 50 years.
EDCS-30600137.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Quintus Aelius Statianus for his wife, Cato Stataria Tuionis, who died at age 50.

EDCS-30600138.text_edition: et Fusca M D P et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-30600138.translation: And Fusca M.D.P. placed this for herself while living.
EDCS-30600138.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a larger monument, set up by Fusca M.D.P. for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-30600139.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Statiae Frunitae Statius Vindemiator maritus bene merenti fecit quae vixit annos LVI
EDCS-30600139.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Statius Vindemiator, her husband, made this for Statia Frunita, who lived 56 years, as she deserved.
EDCS-30600139.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a husband to his deceased wife, Statia Frunita, dating from an indeterminate period.

EDCS-30600140.text_edition: Caius Statius Cai filius Sergia Celsus evocatus Augusti donis donatus bis corona aurea torquibus phaleris armillis ob triumphos belli Dacici ab Imperatore Caesare Nerva Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico Parthico Optimo |(centurio) legionis VII geminae in Hispania testamento poni iussit et epulo dedicavit
EDCS-30600140.translation: Gaius Statius Celsus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, an evocatus Augusti, honored with gifts twice, with a golden crown, torques, phalerae, and armillae for triumphs in the Dacian War by Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus Optimus, centurion of the Seventh Gemina Legion in Hispania, ordered this to be erected in his will and dedicated it with a public feast.
EDCS-30600140.summary: This inscription records the distinguished military career and honors of Gaius Statius Celsus, a centurion of the Legio VII Gemina, who ordered this monument and a public feast in his will, likely from the early 2nd century AD given the mention of Trajan and the Dacian Wars.

EDCS-30600564.text_edition: Caio Statio Cai filio Sergia Restituto annorum XV mensium VI horarum VI et semis horae Caius Statius Valens eFt#et#FT Caesia Secunda parentes filio posuerunt
EDCS-30600564.translation: Gaius Statius Valens and Caesia Secunda, parents, placed this for their son, Gaius Statius Restitutus, son of Gaius, of the Sergian tribe, who lived 15 years, 6 months, 6 and a half hours.
EDCS-30600564.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Statius Valens and Caesia Secunda for their son, Gaius Statius Restitutus, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-30600565.text_edition: Papia Luci filia Brocchina sacerdos divae Augustae testamento poni iussit
EDCS-30600565.translation: Papia Brocchina, daughter of Lucius, priestess of the Divine Augusta, ordered this to be erected in her will.
EDCS-30600565.summary: This inscription records that Papia Brocchina, a priestess of the deified Empress, ordered this monument to be set up through her will, indicating a funerary or commemorative context.

EDCS-30600566.text_edition: Lucius Herennius Luci filius Papiria Muliade sic veteranus legionis VII annorum LX stipendiorum XXX hic situs est
EDCS-30600566.translation: Lucius Herennius Muliade, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, 60 years old, with 30 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-30600566.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Herennius Muliade, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, detailing his age and years of service.

EDCS-30600567.text_edition: Marcus Plaetorius P miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum centuria Artani Marcelli Noni Valentis centuria easdem Nonio Valenti bene merenti titulum posuit annorum XXX stipendiorum VII
EDCS-30600567.translation: Marcus Plaetorius P., soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, from the centuria of Artanus Marcellus Noni Valens, placed this titulus for Nonius Valens, who deserved well, being 30 years old with 7 years of service.
EDCS-30600567.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Marcus Plaetorius for Nonius Valens, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, specifying his military unit, age, and years of service.

EDCS-30600568.text_edition: Primus Titti filius tubicen domo Caturix miles cohortis III Alpinorum annorum XLIIX stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Lucius optio et Tullius veteranus heredes posuerunt
EDCS-30600568.translation: Primus, son of Tittus, trumpeter, from Caturix, soldier of the Third Alpine Cohort, 48 years old, with 23 years of service, lies here. He ordered it to be made in his will. Lucius, an optio, and Tullius, a veteran, his heirs, placed it.
EDCS-30600568.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Primus, a trumpeter in the Third Alpine Cohort, set up by his heirs, Lucius and Tullius, according to his will, dating from an indeterminate period.

EDCS-30600569.text_edition: Lucio Livio Rufino filio pientissimo Aelia
EDCS-30600569.translation: Aelia for her most devoted son, Lucius Livius Rufinus.
EDCS-30600569.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription from a mother, Aelia, to her son, Lucius Livius Rufinus.

EDCS-30600570.text_edition: Mopsus Sexti Vari servus nnum XIIX hic situs
EDCS-30600570.translation: Mopsus, slave of Sextus Varius, 18 years old, lies here.
EDCS-30600570.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mopsus, a slave of Sextus Varius, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-30600574.text_edition: Publius Valerius Publi filius Rufus Lymphis sacrum
EDCS-30600574.translation: Publius Valerius Rufus, son of Publius, sacred to the Nymphs.
EDCS-30600574.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Publius Valerius Rufus to the Nymphs, likely part of a votive offering or a monument related to a water source.

EDCS-30600575.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIIII consule III patre patriae cohors II |(milliaria) Delmatarum pedes DCCC in his turris I sub cura Luci Annaei Serviliani tribuni vice tertia
EDCS-30600575.translation: In the time of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 24th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, the Second Cohort of Dalmatians, 800 foot soldiers, including one tower, under the charge of Lucius Annaeus Servilianus, tribune for the third time.
EDCS-30600575.summary: This inscription records a building activity, specifically the construction of a tower by a military cohort, during the reign of Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-30600578.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius sevir Salonitanorum? --- sibi se vivo posuit et Claudiae carissimae concubinae? ---
EDCS-30600578.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius, a sevir of the Salonitani, built this for himself while alive, and for Claudia, his dearest concubine.
EDCS-30600578.summary: This is a funerary inscription (likely from a tombstone or altar) dedicated by Publius Aelius to himself and his concubine.

EDCS-30600579.text_edition: Caio Titio Cai filio Velina Primo aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo Argyrus libertus fecit
EDCS-30600579.translation: Argyrus, a freedman, made this for Gaius Titius, son of Gaius, of the Velina tribe, Primus, aedile, and duovir with judicial power.
EDCS-30600579.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a freedman, Argyrus, to his former master, Gaius Titius Primus, who held various municipal magistracies.

EDCS-30600582.text_edition: Dis Manibus Paenio Fido collegae bene merenti
EDCS-30600582.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Paenius Fidus, a well-deserving colleague.
EDCS-30600582.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Paenius Fidus by his colleagues.

EDCS-30600585.text_edition: Quinto Aeronio Firmino defuncto nnorum XIIII mensium IIII Quintus Aeronius Crescens et Caetrania Firma parentes filio pientissimo libertis libertabusque suis invida Parcarum series livorque malignus bis septena meae ruperunt stamina lucis parcite iam lachrimis sic miseri solique parentes sat fletus vestros prima favilla bibit corpus habent cineres animam sacer abstulit aer
EDCS-30600585.translation: To Quintus Aeronius Firminus, who died at 14 years and 4 months. Quintus Aeronius Crescens and Caetrania Firma, his parents, placed this for their most devoted son, and for their freedmen and freedwomen. The envious series of the Fates and malignant envy broke the threads of my life twice seven times; spare your tears now, you miserable and lonely parents, enough weeping, the first ash has already drunk your tears; the ashes hold the body, sacred air took the soul.
EDCS-30600585.summary: This is a deeply emotional funerary inscription, with an appended poem, erected by parents for their deceased fourteen-year-old son, Quintus Aeronius Firminus.

EDCS-30600594.text_edition: Iulius Martyrius et Aurelia Procula parentes Gelliae filiae dulcissimae quae vixit annos XV menses VIIII dies V bene merenti posuerunt et sibi
EDCS-30600594.translation: Julius Martyrius and Aurelia Procula, her parents, placed this for their sweetest daughter, Gellia, who lived 15 years, 9 months, and 5 days, who well deserved it, and for themselves.
EDCS-30600594.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Julius Martyrius and Aurelia Procula for their beloved daughter, Gellia, and also for themselves.

EDCS-30700715.text_edition: Avitio Augurino militi legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Ulpius Calla miles legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-30700715.translation: For Avitius Augurinus, soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, Ulpius Calla, a soldier of the same legion, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-30700715.summary: This is a funerary monument for a soldier, Avitius Augurinus, from the Fourteenth Legion, set up by his heir, who was also a soldier of the same legion.

EDCS-30700721.text_edition: Dis Manibus Helviae Marroniae defunctae nnorum XXX Vitalis posuit
EDCS-30700721.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vitalis placed this for Helvia Marronia, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-30700721.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Vitalis to Helvia Marronia, who passed away at thirty years old.

EDCS-30700724.summary: This is a simple, concise funerary inscription indicating the name and age of the deceased, Rhodope.

EDCS-30700729.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Marci Aureli Marci fili Lucani decurionis municipii Magni IIviri iure dicundo?
EDCS-30700729.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Lucanus, son of Marcus, decurion of the municipium Magnum, duovir with judicial power.
EDCS-30700729.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Marcus Aurelius Lucanus, a local dignitary who held the positions of decurion and duovir in the municipium Magnum.

EDCS-30800055.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Statius Victor Brizidia voto libens posuit
EDCS-30800055.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Statius Victor Brizidia willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30800055.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Statius Victor Brizidia to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800057.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Marcus Aemilius Antonius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30800057.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Marcus Aemilius Antonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800057.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Marcus Aemilius Antonius to Silvanus Augustus, indicating a dedication to the deified emperor and the god of the woods.

EDCS-30800059.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication by OL Cambrianus, possibly a votive or a small monument, though its exact purpose is unclear due to its brevity.

EDCS-30800060.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Aelio Pladomeno Carvanio annorum  praefecto civitatium Pirustarum praefecto iure dicundo municipii Aureli Slo et Aeliae Pantoni coniugi eius vivae parentibus pientissimis Ateli=IL(ii)#Ateli(ii)#ATEIL Titus Lupus et Firminus heredes ponendum curaverunt hic situs est
EDCS-30800060.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Publius Aelius Pladomenus Carvanius, who lived for [---] years, prefect of the communities of the Pirustae, prefect for the administration of justice of the municipium Aureli Slo, and to Aelia Pantonia, his wife, while still living, their most devoted parents. Atelius Titus Lupus and Firminus, his heirs, arranged for this to be placed. He is buried here.
EDCS-30800060.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Pladomenus Carvanius, a notable local official, and his still-living wife Aelia Pantonia, erected by their heirs, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800061.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Martiali decurioni municipii IIviro defuncto Salona annorum XL Aurelia Varra coniugi pro pietate
EDCS-30800061.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Titus Aelius Martialis, decurion of the municipium, a deceased duumvir of Salona, who lived for 40 years. Aurelia Varra to her husband, out of devotion.
EDCS-30800061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Varra to her deceased husband, Titus Aelius Martialis, a decurion and duumvir of Salona, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800062.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Publius Aelius Maximus decurio municipii Malvesatium IIvir functus memoriae Publi Aeli Proculi defuncti annorum XXXVI et Publi Aeli Quintiliani defuncti annorum XXV filiorum piissimorum et Aureliae Annae neptis dulcissimae defunctae nnorum III et sibi et Aureliae Marcianae coniugi fideli et obsequentissimae vivis fecit
EDCS-30800062.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Maximus, decurion of the municipium Malvesatium, a former duumvir, made this for the memory of Publius Aelius Proculus, deceased, who lived 36 years, and Publius Aelius Quintilianus, deceased, who lived 25 years, his most devoted sons, and for Aurelia Anna, his sweetest granddaughter, deceased, who lived 3 years, and for himself and Aurelia Marciana, his faithful and most obedient wife, while they are still living.
EDCS-30800062.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Maximus, a decurion and former duumvir, for his deceased sons and granddaughter, and also for himself and his living wife, Aurelia Marciana, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Victori decurio municipii Malvesatium defuncto annorum XXXVII Aurelia Nindia filia et Aurelia Paulina marito pro pietate
EDCS-30800064.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelius Victor, decurion of the municipium Malvesatium, deceased, who lived 37 years. Aurelia Nindia, his daughter, and Aurelia Paulina, his wife, [made this] for their husband/father out of devotion.
EDCS-30800064.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Nindia, his daughter, and Aurelia Paulina, his wife, to Aelius Victor, a deceased decurion of the municipium Malvesatium, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800065.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Capitoni decurioni IIviro municipii Malvesatium defuncto annorum X FLIEIV et Severa sorores et Ulpiae Ursae annorum LX matri pro pietate
EDCS-30800065.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Capito, decurion, duumvir of the municipium Malvesatium, deceased, who lived X FLIEIV years. Severa and Ulpia Ursa, his sisters, [made this] for their mother, who lived 60 years, out of devotion.
EDCS-30800065.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Severa and Ulpia Ursa to their deceased brother, Capito, a decurion and duumvir of the municipium Malvesatium, and to their mother, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800066.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Victor vixit annos I Aurelius Memor A
EDCS-30800066.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Victor lived 1 year. Aurelius Memor A[...].
EDCS-30800066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child, Titus Aurelius Victor, who lived only one year, likely erected by Aurelius Memor, probably his father, during the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800067.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Tattae libertae et nutrici defunctae annorum L Flavia Prisca clarissima femina et Dazio fratri ipsius vilico vivo posuit
EDCS-30800067.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavia Tatta, freedwoman and nurse, deceased, who lived 50 years. Flavia Prisca, a most distinguished woman, and Dazius, her brother, a bailiff, placed this while still living.
EDCS-30800067.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavia Tatta, a freedwoman and nurse, by Flavia Prisca, a distinguished woman, and Dazius, Tatta's brother and a bailiff, erected during their lifetimes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800069.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Parthino Caius Iulius Rufus tribunus cohortis I milliariae Delmatarum pro salute [[]] []i libens posuit
EDCS-30800069.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Parthinus, Gaius Iulius Rufus, tribune of the First Cohort milliaria of the Delmatae, for the well-being of [--] willingly set this up.
EDCS-30800069.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a military tribune to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Parthinus for someone's well-being.

EDCS-30800070.text_edition: Libero Patri Cap Titus Aurelius Provincialis ob honorem IIviratus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30800070.translation: To Liber Pater, Titus Aurelius Provincialis, for the honor of his duumvirate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800070.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Aurelius Provincialis to Liber Pater, made in gratitude for his attainment of the duumvirate.

EDCS-30800072.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Flavius Albanus IIvir quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30800072.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Flavius Albanus, quinquennial duumvir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800072.summary: This inscription records a fulfilled vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavius Albanus, a quinquennial duumvir.

EDCS-30800073.text_edition: Libero Baccho Publius Aelius Clemens  veteranus E AETSOSE bene votum solvit  Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Clemens libens  posuit V sua pecunia TAE LV E votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30800073.translation: To Liber Bacchus, Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran, willingly fulfilled his vow well. To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Clemens willingly set this up at his own expense. He willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800073.summary: This inscription records two separate votive offerings by Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran, to Liber Bacchus and to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30800074.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Maximus veteranus cohortis I Belgarum vivus fecit sibi ed sic Avilliae Amabili coniugi suae
EDCS-30800074.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Iulius Maximus, a veteran of the First Cohort of Belgae, made this for himself while living, and likewise for Avillia Amabilis, his wife.
EDCS-30800074.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Maximus, a veteran, and his wife Avillia Amabilis, prepared by Maximus during his lifetime.

EDCS-30800075.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Gallus defunctus annorum LXV et Aurelia Madussa defuncta annorum LX Aurelius Capito parentibus pro pietate
EDCS-30800075.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Aurelius Gallus, deceased at 65 years old, and Aurelia Madussa, deceased at 60 years old. Aurelius Capito made this for his parents out of piety.
EDCS-30800075.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Capito to his deceased parents, Aurelius Gallus and Aurelia Madussa.

EDCS-30800077.text_edition: Sextus Caesernius Romanus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-30800077.translation: Sextus Caesernius Romanus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800077.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Sextus Caesernius Romanus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-30800078.text_edition: MVLD cohortis XI Gallorum domo Patavi nnorum XLVI stipendiorum XV
EDCS-30800078.translation: --- of the Eleventh Cohort of Gauls, from Patavium, 46 years old, 15 years of service.
EDCS-30800078.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription for an individual from Patavium who served in the Eleventh Cohort of Gauls, noting their age and years of service.

EDCS-30800079.text_edition: Publius Mescenius Pastor Publius Mescenius Pothinianus IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem
EDCS-30800079.translation: Publius Mescenius Pastor, Publius Mescenius Pothinianus, sexviri magistri Mercuriales, for the honor [--].
EDCS-30800079.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Publius Mescenius Pastor and Publius Mescenius Pothinianus, who were sexviri and masters of the Mercuriales, likely in honor of an event or position.

EDCS-30800080.text_edition: Publius Spedius Publi libertus Adiutor Publius Lastus  Caius Minidius Cai libertus Hieronomus Lucius Cer
EDCS-30800080.translation: Publius Spedius Adiutor, freedman of Publius; Publius Lastus; Gaius Minidius Hieronomus, freedman of Gaius; Lucius Cer[--].
EDCS-30800080.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a list, naming several individuals, including two freedmen, Publius Spedius Adiutor and Gaius Minidius Hieronomus.

EDCS-30800082.text_edition: Lucius Trebius Secundus praefectus castrorum inter Onastinos et Narestinos terminos posuit iussu Luci Volusi Saturnini legati pro praetore Cai C[ae]saris A[ugusti] Germanici ex sentententia quam iis athibRito sic#athibito sic#ATHIRITO consilio dixit
EDCS-30800082.translation: Lucius Trebius Secundus, prefect of the camp, placed boundaries between the Onastini and Narestini at the command of Lucius Volusius Saturninus, legate pro praetore of Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, according to the decision he rendered to them after holding a council.
EDCS-30800082.summary: This inscription records the establishment of boundaries between two tribes by a camp prefect, acting on the orders of a legate of Emperor Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, following a judicial decision.

EDCS-30800083.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aelii Pinnes et Temus parentes posuerunt filio pientissimo Pinnio militi legionis secundaes sic defuncto Bassianis annorum XXXII
EDCS-30800083.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Pinnes and Temus, parents, placed this for their most devoted son Pinnius, a soldier of the Second Legion, who died at Bassianae at 32 years of age.
EDCS-30800083.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by parents for their son, Pinnius, a soldier of the Second Legion, who died at the age of 32 at Bassianae.

EDCS-30800085.text_edition: Isante Suro coniugi inconparabili sic Dis Manibus Victorinae Annae bene merenti praecepto dominico factum miseram parentes perdederunt sic annis triginTta#triginta#TRIGITT
EDCS-30800085.translation: To Isas Syrus, incomparable spouse, to the Spirits of the Dead of Victorina Anna, well-deserving, made according to a master's precept. Parents lost their miserable child at thirty years.
EDCS-30800085.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Isas Syrus, set up by his parents, possibly also commemorating Victorina Anna, and mentions the loss of a child at thirty years of age.

EDCS-30900015.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely to Jupiter, by an individual named Sextus.

EDCS-30900101.text_edition: Publio Octavio  filio Sergia Certo decurioni Publius Octavius Placidus Luci filius frater vivus fecit
EDCS-30900101.translation: To Publius Octavius, son of Sergia Certus, decurion. Publius Octavius Placidus, son of Lucius, his brother, made this while alive.
EDCS-30900101.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Octavius Placidus for his brother, Publius Octavius, a decurion, erected while the dedicant was still living.

EDCS-30900102.text_edition: IIII Vibius Silvester filio piissimo vivus fecit
EDCS-30900102.translation: Vibius Silvester made this for his most devoted son while alive.
EDCS-30900102.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Vibius Silvester for his son during his own lifetime.

EDCS-30900104.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-30900104.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Valerius Valerianus miles cum insisterem ad capitella colIumnarum#columnarum#COIUMNARUM ad thermas Licinianas quas sic fiunt Sirmi votum libens solvit
EDCS-30900104.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Valerius Valerianus, a soldier, willingly fulfilled his vow as I stood at the capitals of the columns at the Licinian Baths which are being built in Sirmium.
EDCS-30900104.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Valerius Valerianus, a soldier, to Hercules Augustus, during the construction or renovation of the Licinian Baths in Sirmium.

EDCS-31200486.text_edition: Publius Gosselius Quinti filius Aniensis Cremona miles legionis XI signifer annorum XL stipendiorum XIIII hic situs est
EDCS-31200486.translation: Publius Gosselius, son of Quintus, from the Aniensis tribe, Cremona, standard-bearer of the XI Legion, 40 years old, 14 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-31200486.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Gosselius, a standard-bearer of the XI Legion from Cremona, noting his age, years of service, and place of burial.

EDCS-31200488.text_edition: Marcus Valerius Liberalis miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XIIX stipendii I domu Claudia Aequo mater memoriae viro et sibi et filio
EDCS-31200488.translation: Marcus Valerius Liberalis, soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, 18 years old, 1 year of service, from the Claudia tribe, Aequum. His mother made this for her husband and herself and for her son.
EDCS-31200488.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Liberalis, a young soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, erected by his mother, who also dedicates it to her husband and herself.

EDCS-31200489.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Maximus Valerio Proclo nnorum IXXX legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis DT Valeriae GIAE VET EN Valeriae Proclinae nepti nnorum V CV F M V p fecit
EDCS-31200489.translation: Lucius Valerius Maximus made this for Valerius Proclus, 80 years old, of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, and for Valeria [---], and for Valeria Proclina, his granddaughter, 5 years old. V. F. M. V. P.
EDCS-31200489.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Valerius Maximus for Valerius Proclus, a veteran of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, and for his granddaughter, Valeria Proclina, along with another Valeria.

EDCS-31200490.text_edition: L Menenia V Feltria miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI heres posuit
EDCS-31200490.translation: Lucius Menenia, daughter of V(ibius) Feltria, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, of 16 years of service, as heir, set this up.
EDCS-31200490.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Menenia, likely a female heir, for a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, recording his military service.

EDCS-31200498.text_edition: A LIIOI Ito Mesia matri suae fecit
EDCS-31200498.translation: A. LIIOI Ito made this for his mother Mesia.
EDCS-31200498.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by A. LIIOI Ito for his mother, Mesia.

EDCS-31200500.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Quinto, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument, with much of the text missing.

EDCS-31200501.translation: Quin Publi- Quinti, for her husband.
EDCS-31200501.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quin Publi-Quinti to her husband.

EDCS-31200503.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimae Marcellinae defunctae annorum XXVII Septimius Marcellinus pater filiae pientissimae posuit et sibi
EDCS-31200503.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Septima Marcellina, who died at 27 years old. Septimius Marcellinus, her father, set this up for his most devoted daughter and for himself.
EDCS-31200503.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits by Septimius Marcellinus for his deceased daughter, Septima Marcellina, and also for himself, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-31200504.translation: Sura Gemelli, for himself and his family.
EDCS-31200504.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sura Gemelli for himself and his relatives, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-31200514.text_edition: Lucius Vibius Maximus titulum posuit Proclii sic <f=C>ili sic#fili sic#CILI annorum II
EDCS-31200514.translation: Lucius Vibius Maximus set up this monument for Proclius, his son, who lived for two years.
EDCS-31200514.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Vibius Maximus for his son, Proclius, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-31200515.text_edition: Domitius Rufus Latrae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31200515.translation: Domitius Rufus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Latre.
EDCS-31200515.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording Domitius Rufus fulfilling a vow to the deity Latre.

EDCS-31200516.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription containing only the name Lucius Domitius Rufus, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-31200518.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII imperatori VI consuli VI patri patriae  Lucius Laelius Luci filius Claudia Proculus testamento fieri iussit epuloque dedicari
EDCS-31200518.translation: To Imperator Caesar, son of divine Nerva, Nerva Traianus Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Pontifex Maximus, in his 17th year of tribunician power, hailed Imperator 6 times, Consul 6 times, Father of the Fatherland. Lucius Laelius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, Proculus, ordered this to be made by his will and dedicated with a public feast.
EDCS-31200518.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription set up by Lucius Laelius Proculus, as per his will, for Emperor Trajan, detailing his titles and powers (likely around 113-114 CE), and stipulating its dedication with a public feast.

EDCS-31200519.translation: To our lord Emperor Licinius Augustus.
EDCS-31200519.summary: This is a dedication to the Emperor Licinius Augustus, likely from a statue base or an honorific monument dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-31200521.text_edition: Titus Iulius Titi filius Claudia Celer legionis II Augustae am ex HSXX--- milibus testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-31200521.translation: Titus Julius Celer, son of Titus, of the Claudian tribe, a veteran of the Second Augustan Legion, ordered this to be made from his will, from twenty [---] thousand sesterces.
EDCS-31200521.summary: This funerary inscription records that Titus Julius Celer, a veteran of the Legio II Augusta, posthumously arranged for the monument to be built through his will, allocating a sum of money for its construction.

EDCS-31200523.translation: [---] Pi[---] Pr() A[---] of the Fifteenth Legion [---]
EDCS-31200523.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a member of the Legio XV, likely part of a funerary or honorific monument, with many details about the individual and context lost.

EDCS-31200524.text_edition: decurio augur porticum testamento fieri iussit ex HS LXXXoctoginta milibus
EDCS-31200524.translation: A decurion and augur ordered a portico to be made from his will, from eighty thousand sesterces.
EDCS-31200524.summary: This inscription records that a decurion and augur posthumously arranged for the construction of a portico, funded by eighty thousand sesterces from his will, suggesting a public benefaction.

EDCS-31200527.translation: Lucius Antestius Secundus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-31200527.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Lucius Antestius Secundus, son of Lucius.

EDCS-31200528.text_edition: Gaio Arruntio Celeri Gaius Arrunttius Sedatus pater
EDCS-31200528.translation: To Gaius Arruntius Celer, his father Gaius Arruntius Sedatus [set this up].
EDCS-31200528.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Gaius Arruntius Sedatus, the father, erected a monument for his son, Gaius Arruntius Celer.

EDCS-31200529.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Arruntius Maro.

EDCS-31200531.translation: To Caesia Tertullina, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-31200531.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caesia Tertullina, identified as the daughter of Titus.

EDCS-31200534.text_edition: Tito Laelio Maximo annorum XIX Trosia Severa mater filio posuit
EDCS-31200534.translation: To Titus Laelius Maximus, aged 19 years. His mother, Trosia Severa, placed this for her son.
EDCS-31200534.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Laelius Maximus, who died at 19 years old, erected by his grieving mother, Trosia Severa.

EDCS-31200536.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Maximae feliae sic karissimae nnorum V mensium III Maxima et Secundinus parentis sic pientissimi
EDCS-31200536.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Maxima, a most beloved daughter, aged 5 years and 3 months. Maxima and Secundinus, her most dutiful parents, [placed this].
EDCS-31200536.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Maxima, who died at the age of five years and three months, erected by her devoted parents, Maxima and Secundinus.

EDCS-31200538.text_edition: Caio Oppio Cai filio Claudia Clementi Rusticello annorum XVI Baebia Titi filia Oppia mater filio posuit
EDCS-31200538.translation: To Gaius Oppius Clement Rusticus, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, aged 16 years. Baebia Oppia, daughter of Titus, his mother, placed this for her son.
EDCS-31200538.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Baebia Oppia, dedicating a monument to her deceased son, Gaius Oppius Clement Rusticus, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-31200666.text_edition: Quintus Gnorius Quinti libertus Faustus Octaviae Luci filiae Secundae uxori annorum XXII
EDCS-31200666.translation: Quintus Gnorius Faustus, freedman of Quintus, (set this up) for Octavia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, his wife, aged 22 years.
EDCS-31200666.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Quintus Gnorius Faustus, a freedman, dedicating a monument to his wife, Octavia Secunda, who died at 22.

EDCS-31200670.text_edition: Dis Manibus parens infelicissim- filio Severo carissimo qui vixit annos VIII menses X dies XVIII bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31200670.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The most unhappy parent placed this for his dearest son Severus, who lived 8 years, 10 months, 18 days, as one well-deserving.
EDCS-31200670.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an grieving parent to their beloved son, Severus, who died young at 8 years old.

EDCS-31200672.text_edition: torquibus armillis phaleris testamento fieri iussit Vibia Maxima soror faciendum curavit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-31200672.translation: By testament, she ordered it to be made with torques, armlets, and phalerae. Vibia Maxima, her sister, saw to the making of this monument. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-31200672.summary: This inscription records a testamentary instruction for the construction of a monument, specifying its adornments, carried out by the deceased's sister, Vibia Maxima, and explicitly stating it is not for an heir.

EDCS-31200686.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a simple name label or ownership mark for Titus Apuleius.

EDCS-31200688.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliis Fortunioni et Felicissimae qui vixserunt sic annus sic XC fecit iEen#ien#EEN EMmeImoriam#EMmemoriam#EMMIMORIAM Aurelia Quarta Aurelio Procino carissimo marito et sibi ipsaius qui vixserunt! in uno annos XIX Procinus vixit annos LXXX
EDCS-31200688.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Fortunio and Felicissima, who lived thus for 90 years. Aurelia Quarta made this memorial for Aurelius Procinus, her dearest husband, and for herself, who lived together for 19 years. Procinus lived 80 years.
EDCS-31200688.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription where Aurelia Quarta dedicates a monument to her dearest husband, Aurelius Procinus, who lived for 80 years and with whom she lived for 19 years, and for herself and the other deceased Aurelii Fortunio and Felicissima.

EDCS-31200872.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta. Flavia Bassilla (dedicated this).
EDCS-31200872.summary: This is a votive dedication to the goddess Venus Augusta, set up by Flavia Bassilla.

EDCS-31200873.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-31200873.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-31200873.summary: This is an honorific dedication to the Roman Emperor Marcus Iulius Philippus (Philip the Arab), likely from an imperial cult or a public body.

EDCS-31200875.text_edition: Quinto Cassio Aquilae decurioni Epidia Celerina uxor et Cassia Aquilina filia patri piissimo et sibi et suis vivae fecerunt
EDCS-31200875.translation: To Quintus Cassius Aquila, decurion. Epidia Celerina, his wife, and Cassia Aquilina, his daughter, made this while alive for their most dutiful father, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-31200875.summary: This is a funerary monument made while alive by Epidia Celerina and Cassia Aquilina for their deceased father, Quintus Cassius Aquila, a decurion, and for themselves and their family.

EDCS-31200876.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-31200876.text_edition: Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla filii  Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla parentes inauraverunt  Marco Flavio Titi filio Quirina Frontoni praefecto fabrum pontifici flamini divi Titi   Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla parentes inauraverunt
EDCS-31200876.translation: To Marcus Flavius Balbinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, deceased. The order of the Docleates decreed all honors and an equestrian statue to him.
