Flavius Fronto and Flavia Tertulla, his sons, gilded it. To Marcus Flavius Fronto, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of engineers, pontiff, flamen of the divine Titus. To Marcus Flavius Balbinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, deceased. The order of the Docleates decreed all honors and an equestrian statue to him. Flavius Fronto and Flavia Tertulla, his parents, gilded it.
EDCS-31200876.summary: This complex inscription records multiple honorific dedications: one to the deceased Marcus Flavius Balbinus, to whom the city council of Doclea decreed honors and an equestrian statue, which his sons gilded; another to Marcus Flavius Fronto, holding several priestly and engineering offices; and a repeated dedication to Marcus Flavius Balbinus by his parents who gilded the statue.

EDCS-31200880.text_edition: Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus IIvir iure dicundo praefectus fabrum testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-31200880.translation: Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus, duumvir with judicial power, prefect of engineers, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-31200880.summary: This inscription records that Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus, a local magistrate and engineer, ordered the construction of a monument through his will.

EDCS-31200881.text_edition: Serveniae Marcellae matri optimae Flavia Cai filia Prisca locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-31200881.translation: To Servenia Marcella, best mother, Flavia Prisca, daughter of Gaius, [made this]. The burial plot was granted by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31200881.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, records the dedication by Flavia Prisca to her mother Servenia Marcella, noting the plot was granted by municipal decree.

EDCS-31200883.text_edition: Caio Caninio Valenti Canini Iulianus et Proculus patri piissimo fecerunt
EDCS-31200883.translation: To Gaius Caninius Valens, Caninius Iulianus and Proculus made this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-31200883.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Caninius Iulianus and Proculus to their father, Gaius Caninius Valens.

EDCS-31200884.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Claudio Aniceto marito pientissimo Claudia Olympia uxor memoriam posuit qui vixit annos LVIIII menses VIIII dies V has pro meritis sedes carissimo coniugi memoriam posuit
EDCS-31200884.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Claudius Anicetus, a most devoted husband, Claudia Olympia, his wife, placed this memorial, who lived 59 years, 9 months, 5 days. For his merits, she placed these seats, a memorial to her dearest spouse.
EDCS-31200884.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Claudia Olympia, dedicating a memorial, possibly with a seating area, to her beloved husband Claudius Anicetus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-31200885.translation: Gemellinus, Augustalis sexvir, for the honor [of...]
EDCS-31200885.summary: This fragment likely records a public dedication by Gemellinus, a 'sexvir Augustalis,' an official of the imperial cult, probably made in honor of someone or something.

EDCS-31200886.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Iadestinus Baebiorum servus sibi et suis vivus fecit
EDCS-31200886.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Iadestinus, a slave of the Baebii, made this for himself and his family while living.
EDCS-31200886.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iadestinus, a slave, prepared his own burial monument for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-31200893.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus Aurelius Saturninus eques Romanus ex voto libens posuit dea virago Delia votum faventi numine quod exigor felix ago libens posui  OCcus
EDCS-31200893.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Aurelius Saturninus, a Roman knight, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow. O goddess, virgin huntress, Delian, I happily perform the vow with your favoring divinity, because I am compelled. I willingly placed this. Occus.
EDCS-31200893.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication by Titus Aurelius Saturninus, a Roman knight, to Diana Augusta, fulfilling a vow with a poetic flourish.

EDCS-31200894.text_edition: Iovi Cohortali sacrum Marcus Aemilius Antonius voto libens posuit
EDCS-31200894.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Cohortalis. Marcus Aemilius Antonius willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-31200894.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Aemilius Antonius, willingly dedicated to Jupiter Cohortalis in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31300270.text_edition: Marco Flavio Titi filio Quirina Frontoni sacerdoti in coloniis Narona et Epidauro IIviro iure dicundo IulIio#Iulio#IUIIO Risinio IIviro qCuinquennali#quinquennali#CUINQ pIontI(ifici)#pont(ifici)#IONI in colI(onia)#col(onia)#COI ScoCdra#Scodra#SCCDR IIviro iure dicundo quinMquennali#quinquennali#QUIM fl=IIamini#flamini#IIAM  praefecto fIabrum#fabrum#I pleps sic ex aere conlato=IC#conlato#CONLAIC
EDCS-31300270.translation: To Marcus Flavius Frontonus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, priest in the colonies of Narona and Epidauros, duumvir with judicial power, duumvir quinquennalis, pontifex in the colony of Scodra, duumvir with judicial power quinquennalis, flamen, prefect of engineers. The plebs [made this] from collected bronze.
EDCS-31300270.summary: This inscription is a public dedication by the plebs, financed by collected bronze, honoring Marcus Flavius Frontonus, who held numerous magistracies and priesthoods in several Roman colonies.

EDCS-31300271.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare Marco Opellio Severo Macrino Pio Felici Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate consule IIIII I[]MNIANS macellum vi ignis conflagratum curante ValeI(erio)#Vale(erio)#VALI Supero viro egregio procuratore argentariarum res publica Domaviana ad pristinam faciem restituit XXI Kalendas
EDCS-31300271.translation: In the reign of Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the fourth time, Valerius Superus, a distinguished man, procurator of the silver mines, oversaw the restoration of the market, which had been burned down by fire. The Republic of Domavia restored it to its pristine state 21 days before the Kalends [of...].
EDCS-31300271.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a market in Domavia, which had been destroyed by fire, under the emperorship of Macrinus, and overseen by Valerius Superus, likely dating to 217 CE.

EDCS-31300272.text_edition: Termini positi inter prata legionis et fines roboreti Flavi Marciani per Augustianum Bellicium procuratorem Augusti
EDCS-31300272.translation: Boundary markers placed between the meadows of the legion and the borders of the Flavian Marcian oak grove by Augustianus Bellicius, procurator of Augustus.
EDCS-31300272.summary: This inscription records the placement of boundary markers by an imperial procurator to delineate land between legionary meadows and an oak grove, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-31300277.text_edition: Apollini Tadeno Charmidis coloniae donum dedit
EDCS-31300277.translation: Charmidis of the colony gave this gift to Apollo Tadenus.
EDCS-31300277.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Charmidis of a colony to the god Apollo Tadenus.

EDCS-31300287.text_edition: Tito Flavio Simili IIviro quinquennali huic primo ex ordine statuam in basilica posuit et dedicavit eam victima percussa sportulis datis natale Augusti VI Kalendas Maias Quinto Sosio Prisco Publio Coelio Apollinare consulibus Titus Flavius Rufinus filius locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-31300287.translation: To Titus Flavius Similis, quinquennial duumvir, the first of his rank, his son Titus Flavius Rufinus placed and dedicated this statue in the basilica by striking a victim and distributing sportulae on the birthday of Augustus, the 6th Kalends of May (April 26), when Quintus Sosius Priscus and Publius Coelius Apollinaris were consuls. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31300287.summary: This inscription commemorates the dedication of a statue by Titus Flavius Rufinus to his father, Titus Flavius Similis, a quinquennial duumvir, placed in a basilica on April 26, 96 CE, with the site approved by local council decree.

EDCS-31300288.text_edition: Deo Magno Conservatori loci huius pro salute sua et suorum et numero convivii Aurelii Valerianus et Terentianus votum libentes laeti solverunt
EDCS-31300288.translation: To the Great Preserving God of this place, for their own safety and that of their kin, and for the number of guests at the feast, Aurelius Valerianus and Terentianus willingly and joyfully fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-31300288.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Valerianus and Terentianus to the Great Preserving God of the place for their safety and their families, likely made during a banquet.

EDCS-31300289.text_edition: Armato sacrum Sestia Onesime ex voto posuit libens
EDCS-31300289.translation: Sacred to Armatus. Sestia Onesime willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-31300289.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Sestia Onesime to the deity Armatus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-31300290.text_edition: Quintus Baebius  filius Zuprico aedilis tabernam pergulam maenianum rei publicae dedit
EDCS-31300290.translation: Quintus Baebius, son of Zuprico, aedile, gave a shop, a portico, and an overhanging balcony to the state.
EDCS-31300290.summary: This inscription records the public benefaction of Quintus Baebius, an aedile, who donated a shop, portico, and balcony to the community.

EDCS-31300291.text_edition: Bindo Neptuno sacrum Proculus Parmanicus praepositus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31300291.translation: Sacred to Bindo Neptunus. Proculus Parmanicus, a praepositus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31300291.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Proculus Parmanicus, a praepositus, to the deity Bindo Neptunus.

EDCS-31300292.text_edition: Bindo Neptuno sacrum Licinus Teuda praepositus et princeps Iapodum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31300292.translation: Sacred to Bindo Neptunus. Licinus Teuda, praepositus and chief of the Iapodes, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31300292.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Licinus Teuda, a praepositus and chief of the Iapodes, to the deity Bindo Neptunus.

EDCS-31300302.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum genti Latinianae et Epicadianae
EDCS-31300302.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, to the Latinian and Epicadian families.
EDCS-31300302.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to the Di Manes, honoring the Latinian and Epicadian families, likely placed on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-31300303.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus Carstimari filius Claudia Salinis eques cohortis III Alpinae nnorum LVII stipendiorum XXX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heredes posuerunt Tiberius Claudius Silenus et Tiberius Claudius Abascianus libertus
EDCS-31300303.translation: Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus, son of Carstimarus, of the tribe Claudia, from Salinae, an equestrian in the Third Alpine Cohort, 57 years old, with 30 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by will. His heirs, Tiberius Claudius Silenus and his freedman Tiberius Claudius Abascianus, erected it.
EDCS-31300303.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus, a veteran cavalryman of the Third Alpine Cohort, erected by his heirs according to his will.

EDCS-31300304.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Aurelio Iovino ex collegio saccariorum qui vixit annis XLV Aurelia Primilla virginio infelicissimo I fecit
EDCS-31300304.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Iovinus, from the college of porters, who lived 45 years. Aurelia Primilla, to her most unfortunate virgin son, made this.
EDCS-31300304.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Primilla to her deceased son, Aurelius Iovinus, a porter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31300305.text_edition: Amabili secutori natione Dacus pugnarum XIII fato deceptus non ab homine
EDCS-31300305.translation: For Amabilis, a secutor, of Dacian nation, who fought 13 battles, deceived by fate, not by man.
EDCS-31300305.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Amabilis, a Dacian gladiator of the secutor class, who died unexpectedly, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-31300306.text_edition: Lucio Anicio Luci filio Paetinati IIIIviro iure dicundo nni praefecto quinquennali Drusi Caesaris Germanici praefecto quinquennali Publi Dolabellae pontifici flamini Iuliae Augustae praefecto fabrum praefectura Phariaca Salonitana
EDCS-31300306.translation: For Lucius Anicius, son of Lucius, Paetinatus, quattuorvir for administering justice, quinquennial prefect for Drusus Caesar Germanicus, quinquennial prefect for Publius Dolabella, pontifex, flamen of Julia Augusta, prefect of engineers, and served in the prefectures of Pharos and Salona.
EDCS-31300306.summary: This inscription records the extensive public career of Lucius Anicius Paetinatus, detailing his numerous municipal and imperial offices, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-31300307.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Maximo a militiis negotiatori celeberrimo qui vixit nnos plus minus  Papirius Maximinus a Maxim
EDCS-31300307.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Maximus, a very famous merchant, formerly a soldier, who lived more or less [---] years. Papirius Maximinus [---]
EDCS-31300307.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Maximus, a former soldier who became a prominent merchant, dedicated by Papirius Maximinus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31300395.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name label or ownership mark for Munatius Trepus, with no further context.

EDCS-31400159.text_edition: Divo Tito Aug Lucius Flavius Quirina Epidianus IIIIvir iure dicundo quinquennalis municipii Docleatium ob honorem  IIIV
EDCS-31400159.translation: To the Divine Titus Augustus. Lucius Flavius Quirina Epidianus, quattuorvir for administering justice, quinquennial of the municipality of Doclea, for the honor of [---]
EDCS-31400159.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Emperor Titus Augustus by Lucius Flavius Quirina Epidianus, a municipal official in Doclea, made in honor of an unspecified event, likely dating to the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-31400161.translation: To the Divine Trajan, by decree of the decurions, [---].
EDCS-31400161.summary: This is a public dedication to the deified Emperor Trajan, established by a decree of the municipal council (decurions), likely dating after his death in 117 AD.

EDCS-31400168.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marci Marii Ambacti Corneli domo BriYxia#Brixia#BRYXIA Publius Scrasius Naeolus aequo sic publico scriba quaestorius amico inconparabi[li! &
EDCS-31400168.translation: To the Divine Manes of Marcus Marius Ambactus Cornelius, from Brixia. Publius Scrasius Naeolus, a public scribe of the quaestor, made this for his incomparable friend.
EDCS-31400168.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Marius Ambactus Cornelius from Brixia, dedicated by his friend Publius Scrasius Naeolus, a public scribe, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400169.text_edition: Marco Epidio Publi filio Quirina Latino decrecto decurionum
EDCS-31400169.translation: For Marcus Epidius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, Latinus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400169.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Marcus Epidius Latinus, made by a decree of the decurions, likely commemorating his public service or generosity, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400171.text_edition: Flavia Pinnia testamento fieri iussit Flavius Epidianus faciendum curavit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-31400171.translation: Flavia Pinnia ordered this to be made by her will. Flavius Epidianus took care of its construction. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400171.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument (likely funerary) for Flavia Pinnia, executed by Flavius Epidianus as per her will, with the land for it granted by the municipal council, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400172.text_edition: IIviro iure dicundo II Flavia Cai filia Rufina filio posuit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-31400172.translation: To the duumvir with judicial power, Flavia, daughter of Gaius, Rufina, placed this for her son. The plot was given by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the son of Flavia Rufina, a duumvir, with the burial plot granted by a decree of the town council.

EDCS-31400173.text_edition: Marco Licinio Probo decurioni qui vixit nnos L Marcus Licinius Severus patri optimo
EDCS-31400173.translation: To Marcus Licinius Probus, decurion, who lived 50 years. Marcus Licinius Severus [set this up] for his excellent father.
EDCS-31400173.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Licinius Severus to his father, Marcus Licinius Probus, a decurion, who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-31400174.text_edition: Marco Novio Quinti filio Iusto decurioni ex testamento eius Titus Novius Maximus frater ponendum curavit locus datus decreto decurionum
EDCS-31400174.translation: To Marcus Novius Iustus, son of Quintus, decurion. From his will, Titus Novius Maximus, his brother, had this set up. The plot was given by a decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Novius Iustus, a decurion, arranged by his brother Titus Novius Maximus according to his will, with the burial plot provided by a decree of the town council.

EDCS-31400175.text_edition: Aeliae Maximae coniugi carissimae quae vixit annos XXXV Lucius A[elius &
EDCS-31400175.translation: To Aelia Maxima, dearest wife, who lived 35 years. Lucius A[elius ---].
EDCS-31400175.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Aelius to his beloved wife, Aelia Maxima, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-31400176.translation: --- or C. f. A[---] / promised (?) to his brother (?).
EDCS-31400176.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary monument, involving a son of Gaius and a promise to a brother.

EDCS-31400177.translation: To Claudia Probilla, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-31400177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudia Probilla, identified as the daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-31400179.text_edition: Dis Manibus miserrimae infelicissimae FlE(aviae)#Fl(aviae)#FE Ursillae quae vixit annos VI menses II dies XXV Flavius Ursus et Flavia Baebia parentes filiae innocentissimae
EDCS-31400179.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for the most wretched and unfortunate Flavia Ursilla, who lived 6 years, 2 months, 25 days. Flavius Ursus and Flavia Baebia, her parents, [set this up] for their most innocent daughter.
EDCS-31400179.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Flavius Ursus and Flavia Baebia to their young and innocent daughter, Flavia Ursilla, who died at the age of six.

EDCS-31400180.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Laconi qui vixit annos XLV huic defE(uncto)#def(uncto)#DEE BaebiTa#Baebia#BAEBTA Moderata marito bene merenti fE(ecit)#f(ecit)#E
EDCS-31400180.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Marcus Iulius Laco, who lived 45 years. For this deceased man, Baebia Moderata made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-31400180.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Baebia Moderata to her deserving husband, Marcus Iulius Laco, who died at 45 years old.

EDCS-31400181.text_edition: ann(os) ---VIIII menses II dies VII et innocentissimo pusino Ienuario sic filio eius vixit annos --I menses III dies XVII Secundianus et Ianuaria parentes fEiliaeI#filiae#EILIAI et nepoti fEecerunt#fecerunt#EEC
EDCS-31400181.translation: --- years --- 9 months, 2 days, 7. And for the most innocent little boy Ianuarius, his son, he lived --- 1 year, 3 months, 17 days. Secundianus and Ianuaria, parents, made this for their daughter and grandson.
EDCS-31400181.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Secundianus and Ianuaria for their young daughter and infant grandson, Ianuarius, detailing their short lifespans.

EDCS-31400182.text_edition: Quartioni Gai Flavi Iusti servo Plaia contubernalis
EDCS-31400182.translation: To Quartio, slave of Gaius Flavius Iustus. Plaia, his common-law wife [set this up].
EDCS-31400182.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Plaia to Quartio, a slave of Gaius Flavius Iustus, identifying her as his contubernalis (common-law wife).

EDCS-31400186.text_edition: Libero Patri D - Aurelius Licinianus libens posuit
EDCS-31400186.translation: To Father Liber. D(ecimus) Aurelius Licinianus willingly placed this.
EDCS-31400186.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Liber Pater, willingly set up by Decimus Aurelius Licinianus.

EDCS-31400187.text_edition: Libero Patri Aurelia Zaca libens posuit
EDCS-31400187.translation: To Father Liber. Aurelia Zaca willingly placed this.
EDCS-31400187.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Liber Pater, willingly set up by Aurelia Zaca.

EDCS-31400188.text_edition: Dianae Augustae Aurelius Gaanus beneficiarius consularis provinciae Pannoniae inferioris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400188.translation: To Diana Augusta. Aurelius Gaanus, a beneficiarius consularis of the province of Lower Pannonia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400188.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Gaanus, a beneficiarius consularis from Lower Pannonia, dedicated to Diana Augusta, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400189.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci pro salute Imperatoris Marci Antonini Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti NMAtus vir egregius procurator eius V
EDCS-31400189.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, for the health of Imperator Marcus Antoninus Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, N. Matus, an excellent man, his procurator, willingly [set this up].
EDCS-31400189.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the local Genius for the well-being of Emperor Gordian III (238-244 AD), made by his procurator N. Matus.

EDCS-31400190.text_edition: pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimii Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi patris patriae tribuniciae potestatis
EDCS-31400190.translation: For the health of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, and Parthia, greatest father of his country, with tribunician power [---].
EDCS-31400190.summary: This inscription records a dedication made for the health of Emperor Septimius Severus (193-211 AD), likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400191.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Antonino Pio Augusto divi Severi filio Arabico Adiabencio Parthico Brittannico maximo patri patriae tribunicia potestate consuli IIII decreto decurionum
EDCS-31400191.translation: To Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Augustus, son of divine Severus, conqueror of Arabia, Adiabene, Parthia, and Britain, greatest father of his country, holding tribunician power, consul for the fourth time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400191.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla (211-217 AD), son of Septimius Severus, honoring him for his military victories and titles, set up by decree of the decurions, likely dating to around 213 AD when he held the consulate for the fourth time.

EDCS-31400192.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Traeboniano sic Gallo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto ordo decurionum coloniae metallae Domavianae
EDCS-31400192.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Vibius Traebonianus Gallus, Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, the order of decurions of the colony of Metalla Domaviana [dedicated this].
EDCS-31400192.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus (251-253 AD) by the council of decurions of the colony of Metalla Domaviana.

EDCS-31400193.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Gaio Vibio Traeboniano sic Veldumniano Volusiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto ordo decurionum coloniae metallae Domavianae
EDCS-31400193.translation: To Imperator Caesar Gaius Vibius Veldumnianus Volusianus, Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, the order of decurions of the colony of Metalla Domaviana [dedicated this].
EDCS-31400193.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Volusian (251-253 AD) by the council of decurions of the colony of Metalla Domaviana.

EDCS-31400201.text_edition: Dis Manibus Claudiae Hilarae vixit nnos L Lucius Barbius <V=I>atori#<V>atori#IATORI coniugi bene merenti et sibi fecit
EDCS-31400201.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Claudia Hilara, who lived 50 years. Lucius Barbius Viator made this for his well-deserving wife and for himself.
EDCS-31400201.summary: This is a funerary inscription (tombstone) dedicated to Claudia Hilara, who lived 50 years, set up by her husband Lucius Barbius Viator for her and himself.

EDCS-31400202.text_edition: Dis Manibus Salviae Here NIM Aurelius Fl DEONI coniugi A IIMI
EDCS-31400202.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Salvia Here. Aurelius Fl[---] made this for his wife [---].
EDCS-31400202.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Salvia Here, set up by Aurelius Fl[---] for his wife.

EDCS-31400207.text_edition: Marcus Ulpius Marci filius Maximus quondam SC
EDCS-31400207.translation: Marcus Ulpius Maximus, son of Marcus, formerly of the Senate (?).
EDCS-31400207.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Ulpius Maximus, possibly indicating his past senatorial status, although the abbreviation 'SC' is uncertain.

EDCS-31400215.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Iustus decurio municipii Bistuensis et Aelia Procula coniux vivi sibi posuerunt
EDCS-31400215.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Iustus, decurion of the municipality of Bistua, and Aelia Procula, his wife, erected this in their lifetime for themselves.
EDCS-31400215.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Iustus, a decurion of Bistua, and his wife Aelia Procula, in their own lifetime for their future burial.

EDCS-31400217.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulminatori Flavius Seneca vir egregius libens posuit
EDCS-31400217.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulminator. Flavius Seneca, an eminent man, willingly placed this.
EDCS-31400217.summary: This is a votive dedication by Flavius Seneca, a vir egregius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulminator.

EDCS-31400218.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Licinius Victorinus miles legionis II Aurelio Probo patri defuncto nnrum LV et Proculae matri defunctae piae bene merentibus posuit
EDCS-31400218.translation: To the Divine Manes. Licinius Victorinus, a soldier of the Second Legion, placed this for his deceased father Aurelius Probus, aged 55, and for his deceased mother Procula, both pious and well-deserving.
EDCS-31400218.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Licinius Victorinus, a soldier, in memory of his deceased parents, Aurelius Probus and Procula.

EDCS-31400219.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Titi filio Lucio decurioni municipii Bistuae et Aureliae Proculae Flavia Procilla viva fecit et sibi et suis
EDCS-31400219.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavia Procilla, while alive, made this for Titus Flavius Lucius, son of Titus, decurion of the municipality of Bistua, and for Aurelia Procula, and for herself and her family.
EDCS-31400219.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared by Flavia Procilla during her lifetime for Titus Flavius Lucius, a decurion of Bistua, Aurelia Procula, and for herself and her family members.

EDCS-31400221.text_edition: sacerdoti urbis Romae  ssimo qui vixit coniugi et sibi viva fecit
EDCS-31400221.translation: To the priestess of the city of Rome, the most holy one, who lived for [---] years, his wife made this in her lifetime for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-31400221.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a wife in her lifetime for herself and her husband, who was a priestess of the city of Rome.

EDCS-31400222.text_edition: Aurelius Crescentinus et Iuliana parentes FeIliciano#Feliciano#FILICIANO filio pientissimo defuncto annorum XXX  bene merenti et sibi vivis pro pietate etI#et#EI posteris
EDCS-31400222.translation: Aurelius Crescentinus and Juliana, parents, [made this] for their most devoted son Filicianus, deceased at 30 years of age, well-deserving, and for themselves while living, out of piety and for their descendants.
EDCS-31400222.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Crescentinus and Juliana for their deceased son Filicianus, and for themselves and their descendants, demonstrating filial piety.

EDCS-31400224.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Proculae vixit annos III Aurelii Nepos et Procilla filiae posuerunt
EDCS-31400224.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Nepos and Procilla placed this for their daughter Aurelia Procula, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-31400224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Procula, who died at the age of three, erected by her parents Aurelius Nepos and Procilla.

EDCS-31400226.text_edition: Dis Manibus Plator Batonis ann(orum) I?V?V et P
EDCS-31400226.translation: To the Divine Manes. Plator, son of Baton, aged approximately 4 or 5 years, and P[---].
EDCS-31400226.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Plator, son of Baton, who died as a young child, possibly mentioning another individual whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-31400227.text_edition: Dis Manibus Posaulionis Iaci filiae piissimae defunctae annorum XX parentes
EDCS-31400227.translation: To the Divine Manes. The parents [placed this] for Posaulion, daughter of Iacus, their most devoted daughter, deceased at 20 years of age.
EDCS-31400227.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents for their deceased, most devoted daughter, Posaulion, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-31400228.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo - Publius Aelius Severus libens posuit
EDCS-31400228.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Severus willingly placed this.
EDCS-31400228.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Severus, likely a civilian or a soldier offering thanks or fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-31400231.text_edition: vixit nnos XIImius Bato etva parentes eius infelicissimi fecerunt
EDCS-31400231.translation: He lived for 12 years. The most unhappy parents, Bato and [---], made this.
EDCS-31400231.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a 12-year-old child, set up by their grieving parents, Bato and another unnamed individual.

EDCS-31400235.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Terentio qui vixit annos LX et Vendoni coniugi aeius sic vivae Bessus parentibus pro pietate et sibi vivus posuit
EDCS-31400235.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Terentius, who lived 60 years, and for Vendon, his wife, while she is still living. Bessus, out of piety for his parents, and for himself while living, placed this.
EDCS-31400235.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Bessus to his deceased father Terentius and his still-living mother Vendon, also reserving a space for himself.

EDCS-31400237.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Lucius P S posuit
EDCS-31400237.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Lucius P. S. placed this.
EDCS-31400237.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus, associated with the emperor (Augustus), by Lucius P. S.

EDCS-31400239.text_edition: Dis Manibus Attio Victori filio pientissimo vixsit sic annos XXXI
EDCS-31400239.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Attius Victor, a most dutiful son, who lived for 31 years.
EDCS-31400239.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Attius Victor, a pious son who died at the age of 31.

EDCS-31400245.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Maximinus [placed this].
EDCS-31400245.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maximinus.

EDCS-31400246.text_edition: Aeliae Publi filiae Procillae sanctissimae feminae Caius Safinius Severus maritus ex voluntate ipsius Procillae
EDCS-31400246.translation: To Aelia Procilla, daughter of Publius, a most holy woman. Gaius Safinius Severus, her husband, [set this up] according to the will of Procilla herself.
EDCS-31400246.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Safinius Severus for his wife, Aelia Procilla, fulfilling her last will.

EDCS-31400247.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Tattaris Veneti filius et Temus Annaei filia Batoni filio annorum XX et Annaiae sic filiae annorum XXV filiis pientissimis vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-31400247.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Tattaris, son of Venetus, and Temus, daughter of Annaeus, while living, made this for their most dutiful children, Bato, their son, aged 20 years, and Annaia, their daughter, aged 25 years, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-31400247.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Tattaris and Temus for their deceased children, Bato and Annaia, and also for their own future burial and for their family.

EDCS-31400248.text_edition: Valerius Luci libertus Castor annorum XX Marcus Valerius Marci libertus Priscus annorum XII
EDCS-31400248.translation: Valerius Castor, freedman of Lucius, aged 20 years. Marcus Valerius Priscus, freedman of Marcus, aged 12 years.
EDCS-31400248.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the deaths of two freedmen, Valerius Castor and Marcus Valerius Priscus, at young ages.

EDCS-31400249.text_edition: O ICA beneficiarius consularis provinciae Pannoniae superioris posuit VII Idus Iunias Scapula Prisco et Tineio Clemente consulibus
EDCS-31400249.translation: O. Ica, beneficiarius consularis of the province of Upper Pannonia, placed this on the 7th day before the Ides of June, during the consulship of Scapula Priscus and Tineius Clemens.
EDCS-31400249.summary: This is a dated votive inscription set up by O. Ica, a beneficiarius consularis from Upper Pannonia, probably in honor of a deity or to commemorate an event, datable to June 7th of 195 CE.

EDCS-31400255.text_edition: Deo Invicto Mithrae ac Geniis sacrorum Augustorum Rus Pinnes miles cohortis primae Belgarum immunis libens merito posuit
EDCS-31400255.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras and to the Augustan Spirits of the Sacred Rites, Rus Pinnes, a soldier of the First Cohort of Belgae, exempt from duties, willingly and deservedly set this up.
EDCS-31400255.summary: This is a votive dedication by Rus Pinnes, an exempt soldier of the First Cohort of Belgae, to the god Mithras and the Genii of the sacred rites.

EDCS-31400256.translation: To [Vict]or, a deserving son, most devoted.
EDCS-31400256.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deserving and devoted son named Victor, likely erected by his parents.

EDCS-31400257.translation: To Iulius Teutmeitis, son of Plator.
EDCS-31400257.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Teutmeitis, identifying him as the son of Plator.

EDCS-31400258.text_edition: Caius Aponius Verus Silvano et Genio Epetinorun sic votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400258.translation: Gaius Aponius Verus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus and the Genius of the Epetini.
EDCS-31400258.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Aponius Verus, recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Silvanus and the Genius of the Epetini.

EDCS-31400259.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Vero decurioni municipii Rideri militi legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis militavit    nnos XXV Aurelii Varro et Candida parentes
EDCS-31400259.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Verus, decurion of the municipality of Rideri, soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, he served 25 years. Aurelii Varro and Candida, his parents.
EDCS-31400259.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Verus, a decurion and soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected by his parents Aurelii Varro and Candida, noting his 25 years of service.

EDCS-31400260.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemiliae Dracont defunctae annorum XX illae Vibia Lione delicio bene merenti
EDCS-31400260.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aemilia Dracont, who died at 20 years of age. Vibia Lione [set this up] for her beloved, who deserved well.
EDCS-31400260.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Vibia Lione for her beloved Aemilia Dracont, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-31400261.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulius Mercurialis delicato suo in infelicissimo annorum V bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400261.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Mercurialis set this up for his most unfortunate beloved, who died at 5 years of age and deserved well.
EDCS-31400261.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulius Mercurialis for his beloved, a child who died at the age of five.

EDCS-31400262.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caetenniae Callirhoe Iulia Secundina matri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400262.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Secundina set this up for Caetennia Callirhoe, her mother who deserved well.
EDCS-31400262.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Iulia Secundina for her mother, Caetennia Callirhoe.

EDCS-31400263.text_edition: Candido  ria S  coniugi T rian tri E
EDCS-31400263.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely dedicated to Candidus by his spouse.

EDCS-31400264.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Gaudentiae VictorinaeF#Victorinae#VICTORINAF coAniugCi#coniugi#CAIUCI incoAmparabRilIi#incomparabili#INCAMPARARIII qAuaeF#quae#AUAF vixit annis XXX <Cr=VDT>escens#Crescens#VDTESCES maritus bene merenti
EDCS-31400264.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaudentia Victorina, his incomparable wife, who lived 30 years. Crescens, her husband, for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-31400264.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Crescens for his incomparable wife, Gaudentia Victorina, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-31400265.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Supera filia matri pientissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400265.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Supera, her daughter, set this up for her most devoted and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-31400265.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Iulia Supera dedicating a monument to her deceased mother, who is praised for her piety and merit.

EDCS-31400266.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lollia Dracontilla.
EDCS-31400266.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Lollia Dracontilla.

EDCS-31400267.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lolliae Saecundinae Lollia Lupa filia matri piissimae
EDCS-31400267.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lollia Saecundina. Lollia Lupa, her daughter, [set this up] for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-31400267.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Lollia Lupa dedicating a monument to her deceased mother, Lollia Saecundina, praising her devotion.

EDCS-31400268.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely records the names Anicius Lupus and Maecius, possibly as owners or individuals associated with the object.

EDCS-31400269.translation: To Lutatius Firmo? Lutatia [set this up].
EDCS-31400269.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication by a woman named Lutatia to a man named Lutatius Firmus, likely for a funerary or honorific purpose.

EDCS-31400270.text_edition: Dis Manibus Severae Laelia AegCiale#Aegiale#AECIALE annae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400270.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Severa. Laelia Aegiale set this up for a well-deserving woman of many years.
EDCS-31400270.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Laelia Aegiale dedicating a monument to Severa, described as a well-deserving woman of advanced age.

EDCS-31400282.text_edition: IDIDur et sibi et=B#et#B suis vivus
EDCS-31400282.translation: During his lifetime, and for himself and his own.
EDCS-31400282.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that the monument was erected by the individual during their lifetime, for themselves and their family.

EDCS-31400286.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Publius Aurelius M C votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400286.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Publius Aurelius M. C. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400286.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aurelius M. C. dedicating an object to the goddess Diana Augusta in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31400289.text_edition: Silvano Viatori sacrum Quintus Anix votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400289.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Viator. Quintus Anix willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400289.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus Anix dedicating an object to the god Silvanus Viator in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31400290.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Viator. Nepotianus [set this up].
EDCS-31400290.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription by Nepotianus dedicated to the god Silvanus Viator.

EDCS-31400291.text_edition: Silvano Viatori sacrum Panes Pil votum solvit
EDCS-31400291.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Viator. Panes Pil fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400291.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Panes Pil to the god Silvanus Viator.

EDCS-31400292.translation: Panes Stul placed this for Silvanus.
EDCS-31400292.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Panes Stul to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-31400293.text_edition: Silvano Viatori sancto sacrum Augusto Quintus S Vo votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400293.translation: Sacred to the holy Silvanus Viator Augustus. Quintus S. Vo. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400293.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quintus S. Vo. to the holy god Silvanus Viator Augustus.

EDCS-31400299.text_edition: Aurelio Tullio Timammo marito obsequentissimo in[nocentissimo &
EDCS-31400299.translation: To Aurelius Tullius Timammus, a most compliant and most innocent husband [and...
EDCS-31400299.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by a spouse, commemorating Aurelius Tullius Timammus as a devoted and innocent husband.

EDCS-31400301.text_edition: fecit sibi et Sextoni coniugi carissimae quae vixit annos LV bene merenti et suis
EDCS-31400301.translation: He made this for himself and for Sextonia, his dearest wife, who lived 55 years, well-deserving, and for his own family.
EDCS-31400301.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a man for himself, his beloved wife Sextonia, and his family, commemorating her 55 years of life.

EDCS-31400302.translation: Mes. M. Mantur to his wife, while living.
EDCS-31400302.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Mes. M. Mantur, made for his wife during his lifetime, indicating a cenotaph or a shared tomb.

EDCS-31400303.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely by a husband, dedicated to his uniquely cherished wife, Messias.

EDCS-31400304.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely by Marcellus, for his wife.

EDCS-31400305.translation: Vav. Maxima to her husband and to herself.
EDCS-31400305.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely for a shared tomb, erected by Vav. Maxima for her husband and for herself.

EDCS-31400307.text_edition: ISI i U<r=P>si#U<r>si#UPSI OBvero et filio et sibi posuit
EDCS-31400307.translation: Isis Ursi placed this for [Ob]vero and for her son and for herself.
EDCS-31400307.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Isis Ursi for Obvero, her son, and herself, likely a family tomb.

EDCS-31400308.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Valerius Ursus.

EDCS-31400313.translation: For himself and his wife and his family.
EDCS-31400313.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a tomb or monument by an individual for himself, his wife, and his family, likely indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-31400315.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo ClI(audius)#Cl(audius)#CI MaNximAus#Maximus#MNXIAUS |(centurio) cohortis IE#I#E BelgC(arum)#Belg(arum)#BEC votum solvit
EDCS-31400315.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Claudius Maximus, centurion of the First Cohort of the Belgae, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400315.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Claudius Maximus, a centurion, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31400318.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Cassianus libens votum solvit
EDCS-31400318.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Cassianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400318.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Cassianus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-31400319.text_edition: FFllavis Apollinari et Honorio filiis carissimis et Frontino fratri et Maximae matri
EDCS-31400319.translation: To Flavius Apollinaris and Honorius, dearest sons, and to Frontinus, brother, and to Maxima, mother.
EDCS-31400319.summary: This inscription commemorates Flavius Apollinaris and Honorius, as well as their uncle Frontinus and their mother Maxima, likely a funerary monument dedicated by a surviving family member.

EDCS-31400320.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publiae Aeliae Tertullae filiae defunctae annorum III Aelius I et Cassia Maxima fecerunt
EDCS-31400320.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Publia Aelia Tertulla, daughter, who died at 3 years old. Aelius I and Cassia Maxima made this.
EDCS-31400320.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publia Aelia Tertulla, who died at a young age, made by her parents Aelius I and Cassia Maxima.

EDCS-31400322.text_edition: Militia insigni raptus trieteride sexta sedibus his situs est miserabilis Heliodorus Heliodorus et CIItianae parentes miseri fecerunt
EDCS-31400322.translation: Snatched away in his sixth year of military service, pitiable Heliodorus lies in this place; Heliodorus and Citiana, his sorrowful parents, made this.
EDCS-31400322.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heliodorus, who died during his military service, erected by his grieving parents Heliodorus and Citiana.

EDCS-31400324.text_edition: Venndo Laezarpa Plaenis filius cQum#cum#QUM infante suo hic situs
EDCS-31400324.translation: Venndo Laezarpa, son of Plaenis, lies here with his infant.
EDCS-31400324.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Venndo Laezarpa and his infant, placed by his father Plaenis.

EDCS-31400326.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Voltinia Verus domo Luco Augusti miles legionis XVI#XI#VI Claudiae piae fB(idelis)#f(idelis)#B nnorum I stipendiorum XVII hic situs est tI(estamento)#t(estamento)#I fieri iP(ussit)#i(ussit)#P Publius Annius Aquila faciendum curavit
EDCS-31400326.translation: Lucius Valerius Verus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Lucus Augusti, soldier of the Eleventh Claudian Legion, loyal and pious, 21 years old, with 17 years of service, lies here. He ordered it to be done by will. Publius Annius Aquila oversaw its construction.
EDCS-31400326.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Verus, a soldier, who died at 21 years old after 17 years of service, erected according to his will and overseen by Publius Annius Aquila.

EDCS-31400329.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valiae Maximae Veratius Maximus coniugi pientissimae et Valius Maximus frater sorori carissimae quae vixit annos XX bene merenti I posuerunt
EDCS-31400329.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Valia Maxima, Veratius Maximus to his most devoted wife, and Valius Maximus, brother, to his dearest sister, who lived 20 years, well deserving, they placed this.
EDCS-31400329.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Valia Maxima, who lived for 20 years, by her devoted husband Veratius Maximus and her loving brother Valius Maximus.

EDCS-31400331.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valenti Vatinio pientissimo puero annorum XVI dierum XVII
EDCS-31400331.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Valentius Vatinius, a most dutiful boy, sixteen years and seventeen days old.
EDCS-31400331.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to a young boy named Valentius Vatinius, erected by an unspecified dedicant.

EDCS-31400332.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely part of a funerary monument dedicated to beloved parents, but the dedicant is missing.

EDCS-31400334.text_edition: nis OCIO infelIicissim#infe]licissim#IICISSIM  QuintIio#Quintio#QUINIIO frater  ben=THEe#bene#THEE merit
EDCS-31400334.translation: [To ...]nis OCIO, most unhappy, and to Quintius, brother, well-deserving.
EDCS-31400334.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to two individuals, one described as 'most unhappy' and the other, Quintius, as a 'well-deserving brother', likely erected by family members.

EDCS-31400346.translation: To the Divine Manes of Andes Popius.
EDCS-31400346.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deceased Andes Popius, erected by an unspecified dedicant.

EDCS-31400347.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named M., who died at 21 years of age.

EDCS-31400348.translation: To Julia, wife of Julius Niger.
EDCS-31400348.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia, identified as the wife of Julius Niger, likely erected by her husband or family.

EDCS-31400349.translation: Slator, son of Oeplus, ten years old.
EDCS-31400349.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Slator, the ten-year-old son of Oeplus, likely erected by his father or family.

EDCS-31400350.text_edition: Vandano  Volturia Triti  filia annorum L
EDCS-31400350.translation: To Vandano, Volturia, daughter of Tritus, fifty years old.
EDCS-31400350.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volturia, daughter of Tritus, who was fifty years old, possibly erected by or mentioning a dedicant named Vandano.

EDCS-31400351.text_edition: Vendes Dennaia Andentis filia annorum XXX
EDCS-31400351.translation: Vendes Dennaia, daughter of Andens, thirty years old.
EDCS-31400351.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vendes Dennaia, the thirty-year-old daughter of Andens, likely erected by her father or family.

EDCS-31400352.text_edition: us Denannorum XC hic est situs
EDCS-31400352.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription marking the burial place of an individual named [---]us, who lived to the age of ninety.

EDCS-31400357.translation: Proculus, the sexton, and corrupted by age.
EDCS-31400357.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment, possibly a funerary epitaph mentioning Proculus, a sexton, and indicating deterioration due to age, likely from the monument itself.

EDCS-31400358.text_edition: Caius Reius Alcimo sibi et Publiciae Faustinae uxori pientissimae fecit
EDCS-31400358.translation: Gaius Reius Alcimus made this for himself and for Publicia Faustina, his most devoted wife.
EDCS-31400358.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Reius Alcimus establishing a monument for himself and his exceptionally devoted wife, Publicia Faustina.

EDCS-31400359.text_edition: Valentina mater et Crescentylia sic amita Preponti filio
EDCS-31400359.translation: Valentina, mother, and Crescentylia, thus his paternal aunt, for their son Prepontus.
EDCS-31400359.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother, Valentina, and a paternal aunt, Crescentylia, dedicating a monument to their son/nephew, Prepontus.

EDCS-31400361.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aelia Fausta PA EIOPAMIA POSI EOONMFAM AECINPNSOOITA quae VISENAS
EDCS-31400361.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Fausta [---] which [---] VISENAS.
EDCS-31400361.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, mentioning Aelia Fausta, with most of the text lost.

EDCS-31400362.text_edition: Proclo deefuncto annorum IIII C Procla infelicissimo  III
EDCS-31400362.translation: To Proclus, deceased, aged 4 years. C. Procla, most unhappy [made this for her son], [he lived] 3 [years].
EDCS-31400362.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Proclus, aged 4, dedicated by a grieving C. Procla, likely his mother, who lived for 3 [years or months?]. (Note: The age 'III' likely refers to a third year of life, not 3 full years as he is already stated to be 4 years old).

EDCS-31400363.translation: Saufeia Ide, mother, for Publius Saufeius.
EDCS-31400363.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Saufeia Ide dedicating a monument to her son, Publius Saufeius.

EDCS-31400365.translation: To the Divine Manes of Quintus Iulius.
EDCS-31400365.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes of Quintus Iulius.

EDCS-31400367.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription with missing sections, possibly including a number and the partial name Publius Fio[...].

EDCS-31400371.translation: To Galerius Valerius Maximinus, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-31400371.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Galerius Valerius Maximinus, likely dating to the early 4th century AD when he held the title of Caesar.

EDCS-31400373.text_edition: DDominis nnostris Impperatoribus Flavio Valerio Constantio Pio Felici Augusto et Flavio Valerio Severo nobilissimo Caesari  Domino nostro MN N baeatissimo sic Caesari
EDCS-31400373.translation: To our lords, the emperors Flavius Valerius Constantius, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and Flavius Valerius Severus, most noble Caesar; to our lord, the most blessed Caesar.
EDCS-31400373.summary: This is a public dedicatory inscription honoring multiple rulers: the Augustus Flavius Valerius Constantius (Constantius I or II), and two Caesars, Flavius Valerius Severus, and another unnamed but most blessed Caesar, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-31400375.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Volusianus Augustus.
EDCS-31400375.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Volusianus, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400377.text_edition: Imperatori Quinto Messio Deccio sic Traiano
EDCS-31400377.translation: To the Emperor Quintus Messius Decius Traianus.
EDCS-31400377.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Decius Trajan, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400378.text_edition: Imperatori QO(uinto)#Q(uinto)#O Messio Deccio sic Traiano
EDCS-31400378.translation: To the Emperor Quintus Messius Decius Traianus.
EDCS-31400378.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Decius Trajan, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400380.text_edition: Imperatori Caessari sic Herennio Aetrusco sic Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-31400380.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Herennius Etruscus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-31400380.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Herennius Etruscus, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400389.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II consul pater patriae a Salonis milia passuum XXXIIII  XXXIIII
EDCS-31400389.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, consul, father of the country, 34 miles from Salona.
EDCS-31400389.summary: This inscription is a milestone indicating the distance from Salona, dedicated to Emperor Gordian III and detailing his imperial titles and dating to around 240 AD.

EDCS-31400390.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus et Caius Herennius Etruscus Quintus Decius et Caius Valens Hostilianus Messius Quintus nobilissimi Caessares
EDCS-31400390.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, and Gaius Herennius Etruscus Quintus Decius, and Gaius Valens Hostilianus Messius Quintus, most noble Caesars.
EDCS-31400390.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the co-emperors Decius and Hostilian, and to the 'most noble Caesars' Herennius Etruscus and Hostilian (as potential successors), datable to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400391.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Claudius Pius Felix Augustus XXXV
EDCS-31400391.translation: The Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Pius Felix Augustus, 35.
EDCS-31400391.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Claudius Gothicus, likely from a milestone or statue base, datable to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400392.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Claudio Pio FE(elici)#F(elici)#E Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo
EDCS-31400392.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Claudius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus.
EDCS-31400392.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Claudius Gothicus, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31400395.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantius I, likely from a statue base or milestone, datable to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-31400398.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII consul IV pater patriae censor XXXVI
EDCS-31400398.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor for the thirty-sixth time.
EDCS-31400398.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Claudius, listing his extensive imperial titles and powers, datable to around 47-48 AD.

EDCS-31400399.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII consul IIII pater patriae censor XXXXIII
EDCS-31400399.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor for the 43rd time.
EDCS-31400399.summary: This inscription records the full titulature of Emperor Claudius, indicating his various offices and honors, likely dating to the later part of his reign (around 47 AD).

EDCS-31400400.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII consul IIII pater patriae censor XXXXVI
EDCS-31400400.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor for the 46th time.
EDCS-31400400.summary: This inscription records the full titulature of Emperor Claudius, indicating his various offices and honors, likely dating to the later part of his reign (around 47 AD).

EDCS-31400404.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XIIII consul IIII pater patriae censor LXIX
EDCS-31400404.translation: Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fourteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor for the 69th time.
EDCS-31400404.summary: This inscription records the full titulature of Emperor Claudius, indicating his various offices and honors, likely dating to the later part of his reign (around 47 AD).

EDCS-31400417.translation: From the estate of Sextus Mutellus Maximus.
EDCS-31400417.summary: This inscription likely indicates the ownership or origin of a monument or boundary stone, specifying it comes from the estate of Sextus Mutellus Maximus.

EDCS-31400418.translation: From the estate of Sextus Mutellus Maximus.
EDCS-31400418.summary: This inscription likely indicates the ownership or origin of a monument or boundary stone, specifying it comes from the estate of Sextus Mutellus Maximus.

EDCS-31400590.text_edition: Atilius Pudens Atiliae Sexti filiae Quartae coniugi annorum XXV a Sexti filia Clesta Atilio Maximo filio annorum XI vivi fecerunt
EDCS-31400590.translation: Atilius Pudens and Atilia Quinta, daughter of Sextus, his wife, 25 years old, as well as Sextia Cleta, his daughter, and Atilius Maximus, his son, 11 years old, made this while living.
EDCS-31400590.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a tomb or memorial by Atilius Pudens for his wife Atilia Quinta, and by their children Sextia Cleta and Atilius Maximus, for themselves while still alive, a common practice for securing a family burial plot.

EDCS-31400595.text_edition: Imperatori Caio Iulio Miaximino Pio Felici et Caio Iulio Vero Maximo nobilissimi sic Caesari Auggustis
EDCS-31400595.translation: To Emperor Gaius Julius Maximinus Pius Felix and to Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Augusti.
EDCS-31400595.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, likely dating to their joint reign between 236 and 238 AD.

EDCS-31400596.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-31400596.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-31400596.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to his reign between 244 and 249 AD.

EDCS-31400599.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul II proconsul pater patriae et Marcus Iulius Philippus nobilissimus Caesar consul curante Claudio Herenniano viro clarissimo legato Auggustorum pro praetore
EDCS-31400599.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, and Marcus Julius Philippus, most noble Caesar, consul; with Claudius Herennianus, a most distinguished man, legate of the Augusti with propraetorian power, in charge.
EDCS-31400599.summary: This inscription details the full titulature of Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II Caesar, who was also consul, with Claudius Herennianus overseeing the project as legate, dating to around 247 AD.

EDCS-31400600.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constantio nobilissimo Caesari filio domini nostri Constantini maximi victoris ac perpetui semper Augusti
EDCS-31400600.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, most noble Caesar, son of our lord Constantine, the greatest victor and perpetual Augustus.
EDCS-31400600.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Constantius II, addressed as 'nobilissimus Caesar' and son of Constantine the Great, indicating a date during the Tetrarchy or early Constantinian dynasty, likely after 324 AD.

EDCS-31400601.text_edition: Imperator Nerva Traianus divi Nervae filius Caesar
EDCS-31400601.translation: Emperor Nerva Trajan, son of divine Nerva, Caesar.
EDCS-31400601.summary: This inscription records the official title of Emperor Trajan, likely from a public monument or milestone, dating to his reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-31400604.text_edition: Tiberius Claudius Drusi filius Caesar Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator XV co(n)sul IIII pater patriae censor
EDCS-31400604.translation: Tiberius Claudius, son of Drusus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the seventh time, imperator for the fifteenth time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, censor.
EDCS-31400604.summary: This inscription lists the complete imperial titulature of Emperor Claudius, indicating a public monument from approximately 47-48 AD, during his seventh year of tribunician power.

EDCS-31400619.text_edition: Marco Opellio Antonino Diadumeniano nobilissimo Caesari principi iuventutis Caesaris Marci Opellii Severi Macrini Pii Felicis Augusti filio pecunia publica decreto decurionum
EDCS-31400619.translation: To Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diadumenianus, most noble Caesar, prince of youth, son of Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, with public money by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400619.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Diadumenianus, son of Emperor Macrinus, funded by public money and decreed by the city council, dating to 217-218 AD.

EDCS-31400620.text_edition: Aelius Bessus et Aelia Ursa filiae pientissimae et sibi vivi posuerunt
EDCS-31400620.translation: Aelius Bessus and Aelia Ursa placed this for their most devoted daughter and for themselves, while living.
EDCS-31400620.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by Aelius Bessus and Aelia Ursa for their deceased daughter and for their own future use, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-31400621.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Valeriae Te quae vixit Aelius Qumatri
EDCS-31400621.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Valeria Tea, who lived. Aelius Qumatri.
EDCS-31400621.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aelius Qumatri to Valeria Tea, commemorating her life.

EDCS-31400624.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. [Partial text of unknown meaning, including the name] Sixtus.
EDCS-31400624.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, likely for a person named Sixtus or by him, with most of the text lost.

EDCS-31400626.text_edition: Dis Manibus Totia Proculi filia annorum XVIII Proculus et Marcella filiae pro pietate
EDCS-31400626.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Totia, daughter of Proculus, aged 18 years. Proculus and Marcella, for their daughter, out of devotion.
EDCS-31400626.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Proculus and Marcella to their deceased daughter, Totia, who died at the age of 18, expressing their filial piety.

EDCS-31400628.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Ursinulae Aurelius Rufus sibi et suae coniugi vivus testamento faciendum curavit
EDCS-31400628.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Ursinula. Aurelius Rufus, while living, took care to have this made by testament for himself and his wife.
EDCS-31400628.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tomb for Ursinula, which Aurelius Rufus, while still alive, arranged to be built for himself and his wife through his will.

EDCS-31400631.text_edition: Lucio Domitio I Eroti viro ex equestribus turmis egregio procuratori metallorum Pannoniorum et Delmatorum mirae integritatis et bonitatis Marcus Aurelius Rusticus vir egregius ducenarius amico praestantissimo
EDCS-31400631.translation: To Lucius Domitius Eros, a man of outstanding character from the equestrian cavalry squadrons, an excellent procurator of the Pannonian and Dalmatian mines, of remarkable integrity and goodness. Marcus Aurelius Rusticus, an outstanding man of ducenary rank, to a most distinguished friend.
EDCS-31400631.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Domitius Eros, a procurator of mines, by his friend Marcus Aurelius Rusticus, highlighting Eros's distinguished career and virtues.

EDCS-31400632.text_edition: Dis Manibus Catilia Tryphena vixit nnos L Marcus Catilius Maximus filius et Iulius Atticus maritus bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-31400632.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Catilia Tryphena lived for 50 years. Marcus Catilius Maximus, her son, and Julius Atticus, her husband, placed this for a well-deserving woman.
EDCS-31400632.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Catilia Tryphena, who lived 50 years, dedicated by her son Marcus Catilius Maximus and her husband Julius Atticus, honoring her memory.

EDCS-31400633.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caminiae Eutychiae coniugi vixit annos L Caminius Hermes maritus et Hermetianus filius eius posuerunt et sibi
EDCS-31400633.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Caminia Eutychia, his wife, who lived for 50 years. Caminius Hermes, her husband, and Hermetianus, her son, placed this, and for themselves.
EDCS-31400633.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caminius Hermes and his son Hermetianus to Caminia Eutychia, his wife and her mother, also preparing space for themselves, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400634.text_edition: Genio Liberi Patris Papinius Primigenius sacr(avit)
EDCS-31400634.translation: To the Genius of Liber Pater. Papinius Primigenius dedicated this.
EDCS-31400634.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of Liber Pater by Papinius Primigenius, likely a votive offering from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400636.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali eandem aram Iovi Cohortali Saturninus consecravit
EDCS-31400636.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis. Saturninus consecrated this same altar to Jupiter Cohortalis.
EDCS-31400636.summary: This is a religious dedication by Saturninus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, likely an altar from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400640.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Super veteranus ex legione VIII Augusta memoriam sibi vivus et Verae coniugi Maximinae et Victorino filiis nostris filiam sic diffunctam sic vixit annos XXVIII
EDCS-31400640.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Aurelius Super, a veteran from the Eighth Augustan Legion, while living, made this memorial for himself and for Vera Maximina, his wife, and for our children Victorinus and our daughter who passed away, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-31400640.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelius Super, a veteran of Legio VIII Augusta, for himself, his wife Vera Maximina, and their deceased children Victorinus and a daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400641.text_edition: Z B P io Ulpia iusa marito ico bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400641.translation: [Possibly 'Z B P' - unclear abbreviation] Ulpia, by command of her husband, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-31400641.summary: This is a funerary dedication, possibly by Ulpia for a well-deserving individual, placed by her husband's command, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400643.text_edition: Caius Manlius Cai libertus Honesimus nnorum L hic situs est
EDCS-31400643.translation: Gaius Manlius Honesimus, freedman of Gaius, aged 50, is buried here.
EDCS-31400643.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Manlius Honesimus, a freedman, who died at age 50, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400647.text_edition: Ultima clauserunt Parcarum stamina filo Principii miserandi diem quem gloria nisi avus adque pater puerum dedere pRraeclara#praeclara#RRAECLARA militia patruoque suo iunxere fovendum cum primum pulchra lanugine sumeret annos spectantes magnum patriae columenque futurum heu miseri gloriari sibi laetamque senectam crudelem luctum domui Ravenna remisit hoc miseros titulo proprium signasse dolorem
EDCS-31400647.translation: The Fates' threads at last closed the day of a miserable beginning, which glory—had not his grandfather and father given the boy to a splendid military career and joined him to his uncle to be fostered—when first he took on years with beautiful down, watched him becoming a great pillar for the fatherland. Alas, his wretched family, who might have boasted of a joyous old age for him, Ravenna sent back cruel grief to their home. By this inscription, the miserable ones marked their own sorrow.
EDCS-31400647.summary: This is a poetic funerary epitaph commemorating a young man, emphasizing his early death despite a promising military career fostered by his family, and expressing the profound grief of his relatives, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400648.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Of Anicia [---].
EDCS-31400648.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Anicia, dedicated to the Spirits of the Dead, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400649.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Eudoxio et Eudoxiae Edoxius et Eudoxia posuerunt nnorum XXIII
EDCS-31400649.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Eudoxius and Eudoxia. Eudoxius and Eudoxia placed this. Aged 23.
EDCS-31400649.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Eudoxius and Eudoxia by two individuals, also named Eudoxius and Eudoxia, who are mentioned as being 23 years old, suggesting either the deceased were 23 or the dedicators, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400651.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caio S Lupo decurioni annorum L Claudia Maxima marito
EDCS-31400651.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius S. Lupus, decurion, aged 50. Claudia Maxima to her husband.
EDCS-31400651.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Maxima to her husband, Gaius S. Lupus, a decurion who lived for 50 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400652.text_edition: D I D OI Flavius Aponianus qui tulit annos X BLLARIO uxori  BNILV LVSI IVC XX marito et PRIO-BI MEF
EDCS-31400652.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavius Aponianus, who lived for ten years, to his wife BLLARIO, and BNI LVSI IVC XX to her husband and PRIO-BI MEF.
EDCS-31400652.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Aponianus, dedicated by his wife BLLARIO and another individual or group, likely dating to the early Imperial period due to the formula D.I.D.OI.

EDCS-31400653.text_edition: ONIIT Titus IulI(ius)#Iul(ius)#IUI Cai fI(ilius)#f(ilius)#I Asiaritanus nnrum XXVIIII
EDCS-31400653.translation: Titus Julius, son of Gaius, Asiaritanus, 29 years old.
EDCS-31400653.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Titus Julius Asiaritanus, likely a tombstone for a young man.

EDCS-31400654.text_edition: Caio Minidio Primigenio annorum XXXX hic situs est
EDCS-31400654.translation: Gaius Minidius Primigenius, 40 years old, lies here.
EDCS-31400654.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Gaius Minidius Primigenius, likely a tombstone for an individual who died at 40 years of age.

EDCS-31400655.text_edition: Plaetoria Marci filia Posilla annorum III hic sita est
EDCS-31400655.translation: Plaetoria Posilla, daughter of Marcus, 3 years old, lies here.
EDCS-31400655.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating a young girl, Plaetoria Posilla, who died at the age of three, indicating a child's tombstone.

EDCS-31400661.text_edition: Lucio Attio Restuto equiti ex singularibus cohortis III Alpinorum stipendiorum XXI hic situs est
EDCS-31400661.translation: To Lucius Attius Restutus, knight from the elite troops of the Third Cohort of Alpini, with 21 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-31400661.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Attius Restutus, a cavalryman of the elite guard, indicating a military tombstone from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400663.text_edition: Lucius Valerius Luci filius Lemonia domo Bononia miles le[gionis &
EDCS-31400663.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Lemonia tribe, from Bononia, soldier of the legion...
EDCS-31400663.summary: This is a fragmentary military inscription for Lucius Valerius, a soldier from Bononia, likely part of a longer text detailing his legion and service.

EDCS-31400665.text_edition: MAII vixerunt IIVI nnos XL Dassius Aeternalis miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum bene merentibus memoriam posuit
EDCS-31400665.translation: They lived for 40 years. Dassius Aeternalis, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, placed this memorial for the well-deserving.
EDCS-31400665.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Dassius Aeternalis, a soldier, likely commemorating his deceased comrades or family members who lived for 40 years.

EDCS-31400666.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Rastoriano equo publico decurioni IIviro et quinquennali municipii Bistuatium dispunctori civitatis Naronensium quaestori municippiorum Azinatium Splonistarum Arupinorum et Aeliae Procilianae defunctae nnorum  Albia Crispina coniugi incomparabili et filiae infelicissimae et sibi
EDCS-31400666.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Publius Aelius Rastorianus, who held public equestrian rank, decurion, duumvir and quinquennalis of the municipality of Bistua Nova, dispunctor of the civitas Naronensium, quaestor of the municipalities of Azinatium, Splonistae, Arupini, and to Aelia Prociliana, deceased, his incomparable wife and most unfortunate daughter, and for himself, Albia Crispina [set this up].
EDCS-31400666.summary: This is a comprehensive funerary monument dedicated by Albia Crispina for her husband Publius Aelius Rastorianus, a man of significant local political and administrative standing, and for their deceased daughter Aelia Prociliana, also preparing it for herself.

EDCS-31400670.text_edition: Lucius S Pudens Cereri votum solvit
EDCS-31400670.translation: Lucius S. Pudens fulfilled his vow to Ceres.
EDCS-31400670.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Lucius S. Pudens dedicated an offering to the goddess Ceres, likely as thanks for a prayer answered.

EDCS-31400671.text_edition: Maximinus sculpet sic Dianae Augustae sacrum  Titus Aelius Messor votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-31400671.translation: Maximinus shall sculpt thus: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Aelius Messor willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400671.summary: This is a votive inscription to Diana Augusta, indicating that Titus Aelius Messor made a dedication and Maximinus was the sculptor, probably for a statue or altar.

EDCS-31400672.text_edition: Triviabus votum solvit libens Lucius S Pudens
EDCS-31400672.translation: Lucius S. Pudens willingly fulfilled his vow to the Triviae.
EDCS-31400672.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Triviae by Lucius S. Pudens, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-31400673.text_edition: Imperatore Caesare divi Augusti filio Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XXVIII
EDCS-31400673.translation: To the Imperator Caesar, son of Divus Augustus, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 28th time.
EDCS-31400673.summary: This is a monumental inscription recording titles of the Emperor Tiberius, indicating a date of 26-27 CE based on his tribunician power count.

EDCS-31400674.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Anneus  EX  um votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31400674.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Anneus EXUM willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400674.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Anneus EXUM, indicating gratitude for a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-31400679.summary: This is a simple votive inscription recording that Nicala Avidia fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-31400680.text_edition: Ulpio Nepoti militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum bucinatori defuncto annorum XXX stipendiorum X Maximia Procula mater filio infelicissimo hic defunctus Est
EDCS-31400680.translation: To Ulpius Nepos, soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, a trumpeter, deceased at 30 years old, with 10 years of service. Maximia Procula, his mother, placed this for her most unhappy son, who died here.
EDCS-31400680.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpius Nepos, a soldier and trumpeter, set up by his mother Maximia Procula, likely dating to the Imperial period given the mention of a cohort of volunteers.

EDCS-31400681.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Ingenua mater et Aurelius pater urnam pientissimae filiae infelicissimae posuerunt quae vixit annos XVI
