EDCS-31400681.translation: To the Manes Gods. Aurelia Ingenua, his mother, and Aurelius, his father, placed this urn for their most dutiful and most unhappy daughter, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-31400681.summary: This is a funerary inscription in the form of an urn, dedicated to the Manes Gods for a 16-year-old daughter by her parents, Aurelia Ingenua and Aurelius.

EDCS-31400682.text_edition: Lupula Statili --? templum Matri Ma<gn=NG>[ae#Magnae?#MANG? Deorum de suo restituit cum suis f<i=E>liis#filiis#FEL
EDCS-31400682.translation: Lupula Statilii... restored the temple to the Great Mother of the Gods at her own expense, with her children.
EDCS-31400682.summary: This is a dedication recording the restoration of a temple to Magna Mater by Lupula Statilii and her children, indicating civic or religious patronage.

EDCS-31400683.translation: To the Manes Gods. Fulvia Primitiva.
EDCS-31400683.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes Gods for Fulvia Primitiva, likely marking her burial place.

EDCS-31400686.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Aemilius Hermosenax ex visu posuit
EDCS-31400686.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aemilius Hermosenax placed this after a vision.
EDCS-31400686.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aemilius Hermosenax, specifically placed in response to a vision.

EDCS-31400688.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quintiano filio annorum II dierum XXII Primula mater posuit
EDCS-31400688.translation: To the Manes Gods. Primula, his mother, placed this for her son Quintianus, who lived 2 years and 22 days.
EDCS-31400688.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes Gods for the young Quintianus by his mother Primula, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400691.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iulia Secundina, my wife.
EDCS-31400691.summary: This is a funerary inscription, dedicated to Iulia Secundina by her spouse, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400692.text_edition: Lucio Stanio Secundo Lucius Stanius Marcellinus patri
EDCS-31400692.translation: To Lucius Stanius Secundus, Lucius Stanius Marcellinus [set this up for his] father.
EDCS-31400692.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Stanius Marcellinus to his father, Lucius Stanius Secundus, likely for a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-31400702.text_edition: Nymphis sacrum Quintus Trebonius Eutactus fecit
EDCS-31400702.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, Quintus Trebonius Eutactus made this.
EDCS-31400702.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Quintus Trebonius Eutactus, indicating a religious offering or monument.

EDCS-31400703.translation: [---] Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400703.summary: This inscription fragment records an individual named Secundus fulfilling a vow, suggesting a votive offering or dedication.

EDCS-31400704.translation: Venator willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31400704.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Venator, indicating he fulfilled a promise to a deity or divine entity.

EDCS-31400708.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Atreccesianus miles legionis XIIII geminae stipendiorum XXVI posuit Aurelia Festa coniux bene merenti posuit qo(ui)#q(ui)#O vixit annos XXXXVIII et AtrKectia#Atrectia#ATKECTIA viva mamNmulIa#mammula#MANMUIA filio pientissimo
EDCS-31400708.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Festa, his wife, placed this for Atreccesianus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, with 26 years of service, who lived for 48 years, deserving well; and Atrectia, while living, [placed this for her] most devoted son.
EDCS-31400708.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atreccesianus, a veteran soldier of Legio XIIII Gemina, set up by his wife Aurelia Festa and his mother Atrectia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400709.text_edition: Aurelio Dizae equiti ex singularibus stipendiorum XVI Titia Valentina coniugi carissimo bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400709.translation: Titia Valentina placed this for Aurelius Diza, an equestrian from the singulares, with 16 years of service, her dearest husband, deserving well.
EDCS-31400709.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titia Valentina to her husband, Aurelius Diza, an equestrian of the imperial guard (singulares), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31400711.text_edition: IIAE militi legionis I Italicae Severianae stipendiorum XXIII vixit annos  Aurelius Marcianus miles legionis supra scriptae contubernalis bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400711.translation: [To ---]lius, a soldier of the First Italic Legion Severiana, with 23 years of service, who lived for [---] years. Aurelius Marcianus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion, his tent-mate, placed this for him, deserving well.
EDCS-31400711.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of Legio I Italica Severiana, set up by his contubernalis (tent-mate) Aurelius Marcianus, likely dating to the 3rd century AD given the legion's epithet.

EDCS-31400713.text_edition: ius Licinianus i centuria Serv     dulcissim  XIIII et
EDCS-31400713.translation: [---]ius Licinianus [---] in the centuria of Serv[---] most sweet [---] 14 and [---].
EDCS-31400713.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a soldier named Licinianus in a specific centuria, possibly dedicated to a loved one.

EDCS-31400714.text_edition: Iunius Rufus domo Heraclea veteranus legionis  curam egit veteranus le[gionis &
EDCS-31400714.translation: Iunius Rufus, from Heraclea, a veteran of the Legion [---] oversaw the care, a veteran of the Legion [---].
EDCS-31400714.summary: This fragmented inscription records Iunius Rufus, a veteran from Heraclea, overseeing a task, likely the creation of a monument or the arrangement of a burial, as a veteran of a legion.

EDCS-31400715.text_edition: Silvanus veteranus cohortis VIII voluntariorum vivus fecit sibi et  fratri defuncto cae coniugi meae vivae
EDCS-31400715.translation: Silvanus, a veteran of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, made this while living for himself and for his deceased brother and for my living wife.
EDCS-31400715.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb made by a veteran, Silvanus, for himself, his brother, and his wife, indicating his military service and familial dedications.

EDCS-31400727.text_edition: Qunito? Aeronio Tertio VIviro Augustali Quintus Aeronius Helius patri bene merenti
EDCS-31400727.translation: Quintus Aeronius Helius [set this up] for Quintus Aeronius Tertius, a Augustan Sevvir, his well-deserving father.
EDCS-31400727.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Quintus Aeronius Helius to his father, Quintus Aeronius Tertius, who held the esteemed public office of Augustan Sevvir.

EDCS-31400728.text_edition: Lucio Statio Publi filio Ma aedili IIviro flamini divi Ve[spasiani &
EDCS-31400728.translation: To Lucius Statius, son of Publius, from the Ma[cia tribe], aedile, duumvir, priest of the divine Vespasian.
EDCS-31400728.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Statius, a prominent citizen holding various municipal offices including aedile, duumvir, and flamen of the deified Vespasian, likely part of a public monument or statue base.

EDCS-31400742.translation: To Simplicia, the unfortunate C. [---] (remainder illegible).
EDCS-31400742.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Simplicia, who is described as "unfortunate," with additional fragmented and illegible text.

EDCS-31400744.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Arrius Saecularis Arriae Iucundae matri dulcissimae
EDCS-31400744.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Arrius Saecularis [set this up] for Arria Iucunda, his sweetest mother.
EDCS-31400744.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Arrius Saecularis to his mother, Arria Iucunda, expressing filial affection.

EDCS-31400745.text_edition: Dis Manibus Arsinoe Ursulo bene merenti coniugi posuit
EDCS-31400745.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Arsinoe placed this for Ursulus, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-31400745.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Arsinoe for her husband, Ursulus, honoring him as well-deserving.

EDCS-31400746.text_edition: PL II Arne Panniqui vixit annos  sodali bene merenti  dedicavit GI
EDCS-31400746.translation: P.L. II Arne Panniqui lived for years. [---] dedicated this to a well-deserving comrade G.I.
EDCS-31400746.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, possibly for Arne Panniqui, made by a comrade, indicating the deceased's age in years and a dedication to a "well-deserving comrade."

EDCS-31400749.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius, the most unfortunate [---].
EDCS-31400749.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius, described with the superlative "most unfortunate," likely expressing profound grief.

EDCS-31400750.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Messiano coniugi inconparabili annorum XXX qOui#qui#OUI vixit m[ecum &
EDCS-31400750.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Messianus, my incomparable husband, who lived for 30 years and lived with me [---].
EDCS-31400750.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Messianus by his wife, honoring him as an "incomparable husband" and noting his age and the duration of their life together.

EDCS-31400751.text_edition: Aurelius Sa Miccae coniugi quae vixit cum eo plus minus annos  et tio filio  domino nostro III
EDCS-31400751.translation: Aurelius [set this up] for Micca, his wife, who lived with him for more or less [an unspecified number of] years, and for his son, and for our lord III.
EDCS-31400751.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelius for his wife Micca, noting the duration of their marriage, and also for his son, with an additional, fragmented dedication to "our lord III," possibly indicating a larger monument or context.

EDCS-31400752.text_edition: VT SA ME tis et  Aurelia soror
EDCS-31400752.translation: Ut Sametis and Aurelia, his sister.
EDCS-31400752.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly for Sametis and his sister Aurelia.

EDCS-31400753.text_edition: Aureliae Eupateriae qui sic vixit annos XXIII Aurelia Vera filiae posuit Aurelio Iovino innocentissimo posuit
EDCS-31400753.translation: To Aurelia Eupateria, who lived for 23 years, Aurelia Vera placed this for her daughter. She placed this for the most innocent Aurelius Iovinus.
EDCS-31400753.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Aurelia Vera, dedicating a tombstone to her daughter, Aurelia Eupateria, and also to the very innocent Aurelius Iovinus.

EDCS-31400754.translation: Two and a half feet (wide), Aurelia, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-31400754.summary: This is likely a funerary inscription or boundary marker, specifying a dimension and identifying Aurelia, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-31400756.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Ursula Aurelia Secunda pro pietate posuisuerunt Iulio Labioni pientissimo parentibus amicibus sic fratri Iulio Tertio  bene merentibus
EDCS-31400756.translation: To the Divine Manes, Aurelia Ursula and Aurelia Secunda, out of piety, placed this for the most pious Julius Labio, for their deserving parents, friends, and thus for their brother Julius Tertius.
EDCS-31400756.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aurelia Ursula and Aurelia Secunda out of piety for Julius Labio, and also commemorating their parents, friends, and brother Julius Tertius.

EDCS-31400757.text_edition: DDis Manibus  Batullino infelicissimo? VELDVAM dies XVIII Batullus pater et mater Salonina posuerunt
EDCS-31400757.translation: To the Divine Manes. To the most unhappy Batullinus, who lived 18 days. Batullus, his father, and Salonina, his mother, placed this.
EDCS-31400757.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant, Batullinus, erected by his grieving parents, Batullus and Salonina.

EDCS-31400758.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Baebidio Eleuthero Ponponia sic Secunda marito
EDCS-31400758.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Baebidius Eleutherus, Pomponia Secunda placed this for her husband.
EDCS-31400758.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Pomponia Secunda to her husband, Lucius Baebidius Eleutherus.

EDCS-31400762.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caetenniae Decimiae vixit annos XXXV Iulia Secundina mater et Firmius Eutyches patronus
EDCS-31400762.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Caetennia Decimia, who lived 35 years. Iulia Secundina, her mother, and Firmius Eutyches, her patron, placed this.
EDCS-31400762.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caetennia Decimia, set up by her mother Iulia Secundina and her patron Firmius Eutyches.

EDCS-31400764.text_edition: Candida in fronte pedes XXXX in agro
EDCS-31400764.translation: Candida: 40 feet in front, [---] in the field.
EDCS-31400764.summary: This appears to be a boundary marker or property description, possibly for an estate named Candida, specifying dimensions.

EDCS-31400765.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antia Maximina Corneliae Secundinae materterae bene merenti
EDCS-31400765.translation: To the Divine Manes. Antia Maximina [placed this] for her well-deserving maternal aunt, Cornelia Secundina.
EDCS-31400765.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Antia Maximina to her deserving maternal aunt, Cornelia Secundina.

EDCS-31400772.text_edition: Dis Manibus Clodiae Marcae LI(ucius)#L(ucius)#I Clodius Germ coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-31400772.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Clodia Marca. Lucius Clodius Germanus [placed this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-31400772.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Clodius Germanus for his deserving wife, Clodia Marca.

EDCS-31400773.text_edition: Cluentio Fortunato  infelicissimo qui vix[it annos &
EDCS-31400773.translation: To Cluentius Fortunatus, most unhappy, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-31400773.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cluentius Fortunatus, recording his age at death, likely erected by his family or an associate.

EDCS-31400774.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Coelio Epithymeto marito optimo Coelia Secunda liberta bene merenti
EDCS-31400774.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Publius Coelius Epithymetus, a most excellent husband. Coelia Secunda, his freedwoman, to her well-deserving.
EDCS-31400774.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Coelia Secunda, a freedwoman, to her deceased husband Publius Coelius Epithymetus, expressing her appreciation for him.

EDCS-31400777.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with only a few partial words, making its purpose and context largely undecipherable.

EDCS-31400779.text_edition: SECa Egnatio Maritimo patri bene merenti
EDCS-31400779.translation: SECa Egnatius Maritimus, to his well-deserving father.
EDCS-31400779.summary: This is likely a funerary dedication made by Egnatius Maritimus to his deceased father, expressing respect and affection.

EDCS-31400782.text_edition: Dis Manibus Feliculae defunctae annorum XXVI  Maturus coniug(i) optimae
EDCS-31400782.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Felicula, who died at 26 years of age. Maturus to his most excellent wife.
EDCS-31400782.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maturus to his deceased wife Felicula, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-31400785.text_edition: nnos XV loco dato gratuito a Flavio Sabino patrono in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes X
EDCS-31400785.translation: [lived] years 15. A plot given freely by Flavius Sabinus, patron. In front, 15 feet; in the field, 10 feet.
EDCS-31400785.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot, generously granted by Flavius Sabinus to a deceased individual who lived for 15 years, likely a freedman or dependent.

EDCS-31400788.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to Lucius Fulvius Maior, but its full context and purpose are lost.

EDCS-31400790.text_edition: ad vitam volui Celerem te crescere mater ad mortem ingemui te Celerem misera Publius Gallius Celer Flavia Vi[r&
EDCS-31400790.translation: I wished, mother, for you, Celer, to grow to life; I groaned, wretched, for you, Celer, to death. Publius Gallius Celer, Flavia Vi[r---]
EDCS-31400790.summary: This is a poignant funerary epitaph from parents, Publius Gallius Celer and Flavia, lamenting the premature death of their child, also named Celer.

EDCS-31400791.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to a mother named Gelasia, likely by a daughter named Imana or by someone related to both.

EDCS-31400793.text_edition: I Tito Haio coniugi inconparabili qui vixit annis XXX Aurelia Tertulla coniux cum=ON#cum#CON quo concorditer vixit annis X bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400793.translation: To Titus Haius, an incomparable husband, who lived for 30 years. Aurelia Tertulla, his wife, with whom he lived harmoniously for 10 years, placed this for her well-deserving.
EDCS-31400793.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Tertulla for her incomparable husband, Titus Haius, celebrating their ten years of harmonious marriage before his death at the age of thirty.

EDCS-31400794.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Helvius Filin.
EDCS-31400794.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of Helvius Filin, likely placed on a tombstone by a family member or associate.

EDCS-31400795.text_edition: Dis Manibus Hilarrio Val annis  fratri
EDCS-31400795.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Hilarrio Val, a brother, [who lived] for years [---].
EDCS-31400795.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Hilarrio Val, dedicated by a sibling, likely appearing on a tombstone from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-31400797.text_edition: BI qui vixit annos XLVI merenti Imiaria conserva
EDCS-31400797.translation: Who lived for 46 years, to the deserving BI Imiaria, preserve.
EDCS-31400797.summary: This inscription appears to be part of a funerary monument for someone named BI Imiaria, noting their age and possibly including a plea for preservation.

EDCS-31400798.translation: To the most devoted mother and Inachus F[ilius].
EDCS-31400798.summary: This inscription is dedicated to a very devoted mother and Inachus Filius, likely part of a tombstone or memorial erected by a child for their parent and possibly another family member.

EDCS-31400800.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the nurse Ire[ne].
EDCS-31400800.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits of a nurse named Irene, indicating a bond of affection, likely placed on her tomb by those she nursed or their family.

EDCS-31400803.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Iuli Carpophori a BeleFnia#Belenia#BELFNIA filio
EDCS-31400803.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Iulius Carpophorus, son of Belenia.
EDCS-31400803.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Spirits of Iulius Carpophorus, explicitly identifying him as the son of Belenia, likely placed on his tomb by his mother or another family member.

EDCS-31400804.translation: To Decimus Iulius M. Hilarianus, most unfortunate.
EDCS-31400804.summary: This inscription is a memorial to Decimus Iulius M. Hilarianus, emphasizing his misfortune, likely a funerary dedication expressing grief.

EDCS-31400805.text_edition: Caius Iulius Cai filius Valens annorum XXVII
EDCS-31400805.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, Valens, 27 years old.
EDCS-31400805.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Gaius Iulius Valens, noting his paternity and age, likely part of his tombstone from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-31400806.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulia Sabina Tito Iulio Ingenuo filio infelicissimo defuncto nnorum XIIII titulum posuit mater pientissima
EDCS-31400806.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Iulia Sabina, a most devoted mother, placed this titulus for her most unfortunate son Titus Iulius Ingenuus, who died at 14 years old.
EDCS-31400806.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by the devoted mother Iulia Sabina for her unfortunate son, Titus Iulius Ingenuus, who died at the age of fourteen, serving as his tombstone.

EDCS-31400808.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary dedication by a person named Lucretia (or similar) to her son, with details lost due to damage.

EDCS-31400809.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Publius Lurius.
EDCS-31400809.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Lurius, likely placed on his tombstone.

EDCS-31400814.text_edition: Natali et Pieridi liberto us in fronte pedes XX
EDCS-31400814.translation: To Natalis and Pieris, freedman. Up to 20 feet in front.
EDCS-31400814.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot for Natalis and Pieris, a freedman, specifying a dimension for the frontage of the property.

EDCS-31400816.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nicini sic defunctae nnorum X mensium III dierum V Fortunatus nepotiae posuit
EDCS-31400816.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Nicini, who died thus at X years, III months, V days old. Fortunatus placed this for his granddaughter.
EDCS-31400816.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Fortunatus to his granddaughter Nicini, detailing her age at death.

EDCS-31400817.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Octavia  Aeliae filiae  vixit nnos  titulum libens posuit
EDCS-31400817.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Octavia placed this titulus willingly for Aelia, her daughter, who lived for years.
EDCS-31400817.summary: This funerary inscription records Octavia's dedication of a monument to her deceased daughter Aelia.

EDCS-31400822.text_edition: et Plaetoriae Primitivae Plaetoria Victorina parentibus optimis posuit
EDCS-31400822.translation: And to Plaetoria Primitiva. Plaetoria Victorina placed this for her most excellent parents.
EDCS-31400822.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Plaetoria Victorina to her excellent parents, including Plaetoria Primitiva.

EDCS-31400826.text_edition: Cai fili-- Rae et aviae karissimae Flaviae
EDCS-31400826.translation: Of Gaius, son... to Rae, and to his dearest grandmother Flavia.
EDCS-31400826.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, mentioning Gaius, Rae, and his grandmother Flavia.

EDCS-31400827.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely related to a person named Renatus, indicating a lifespan of 14 years or a similar temporal reference.

EDCS-31400828.text_edition: PRCV  Claudia Ressituta tatae suo bene merenti
EDCS-31400828.translation: PRCV. Claudia Restituta to her well-deserving father.
EDCS-31400828.summary: This is a dedication by Claudia Restituta to her father, who is described as well-deserving; the initial 'PRCV' is an abbreviation of uncertain meaning in this context.

EDCS-31400829.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely mentioning individuals named Quintus and Epaphroditus, with an indication of servile status for one or more.

EDCS-31400830.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Septimio Salutari G P C bene merenti
EDCS-31400830.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Septimius Salutaris, G. P. C., well-deserving.
EDCS-31400830.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Septimius Salutaris, described as well-deserving, with the abbreviation 'G. P. C.' potentially referring to his status or role.

EDCS-31400833.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Sempronius Marcellinus Cassiae Placidae uxori infelicissimae quae vixit annos mecum quindecim coniugi incomparabili
EDCS-31400833.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sempronius Marcellinus [set this up] for Cassia Placida, his most unfortunate wife, who lived with him for fifteen years, an incomparable spouse.
EDCS-31400833.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sempronius Marcellinus to his wife, Cassia Placida, commemorating their fifteen years of marriage.

EDCS-31400836.text_edition: Silio Hospiti patrono suo titulum posuit
EDCS-31400836.translation: [Someone] placed this memorial for his patron, Silius Hospes.
EDCS-31400836.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Silius Hospes by his client or former slave, honoring him as a patron.

EDCS-31400837.text_edition: Dis Manibus Soteri Leonati coniugi et patrono bene merenti
EDCS-31400837.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Soter Leonatus, a well-deserving husband and patron.
EDCS-31400837.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Soter Leonatus, described as a well-deserving husband and patron.

EDCS-31400840.text_edition: Dis Manibus Terentiae Quintinae matri pientissimae
EDCS-31400840.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Terentia Quintina, a most devoted mother.
EDCS-31400840.summary: This funerary inscription honors Terentia Quintina, commemorated as a most devoted mother.

EDCS-31400843.text_edition: et Tutidiae Agrippinae pientissimae suae coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-31400843.translation: And for Tutidia Agrippina, his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-31400843.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Tutidia Agrippina, described as a most devoted and well-deserving wife, likely part of a larger funerary monument.

EDCS-31400844.summary: This fragmentary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and likely pertains to a funerary context, with the name of the deceased partially preserved as 'Va...'.

EDCS-31400846.text_edition: Dis Manibus  duobus adulescentibus Val Dinenti viro egregio nnorum XXVII et Attia Valeriae coniugCi#coniugi#CONIUCI eius nnorum XVIII Octavia Quieta genero et filiae
EDCS-31400846.translation: To the Divine Manes. For two young people: Val. Dinenti, an excellent man of 27 years, and Attia Valeria, his wife, of 18 years. Octavia Quieta [set this up] for her son-in-law and daughter.
EDCS-31400846.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Octavia Quieta to her son-in-law, Val. Dinenti, and her daughter, Attia Valeria, both of whom died young.

EDCS-31400850.text_edition: ae alumnae defunctae annorum III Lucius Valerius Secundinus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31400850.translation: Lucius Valerius Secundinus placed this for his well-deserving foster-daughter, who died at the age of three.
EDCS-31400850.summary: Lucius Valerius Secundinus dedicated this inscription to his deceased foster-daughter, who passed away at the young age of three.

EDCS-31400851.text_edition: Valeria Aiela Valerio Secundiniano filio be[ne merenti &
EDCS-31400851.translation: Valeria Aiela [set this up] for her well-deserving son, Valerius Secundinianus.
EDCS-31400851.summary: Valeria Aiela dedicated this inscription to her son, Valerius Secundinianus, honoring him as well-deserving.

EDCS-31400854.text_edition: OFO  defuncto annorum XXVI Veiedius Quartinus FTP
EDCS-31400854.translation: [To the Divine Manes? For...] OFO, who died at the age of 26. Veiedius Quartinus [set this up].
EDCS-31400854.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the death of an individual named OFO at the age of 26, dedicated by Veiedius Quartinus.

EDCS-31400857.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To M. Verus. (Aged) 4 years.
EDCS-31400857.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to M. Verus, who died at the age of four, likely erected by his family.

EDCS-31400858.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Egnatiae Victorinae pientissimae infanti  annorum --- h(ic) sepulta
EDCS-31400858.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Egnatia Victorina, a most devoted infant, aged [---] years. Buried here.
EDCS-31400858.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Egnatia Victorina, a very devoted infant, marking her burial place.

EDCS-31400864.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning a son and a boy.

EDCS-31400865.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely mentioning a personal name, possibly Paternus Cimmerius or similar.

EDCS-31400866.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, preserving part of a name, likely Septimianus.

EDCS-31400871.text_edition: D(is) Manibus lo nce va coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-31400871.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lonceva, a well-deserving wife.
EDCS-31400871.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lonceva by her spouse, acknowledging her merits.

EDCS-31400876.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Ae, a most devoted daughter.
EDCS-31400876.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a most devoted daughter named Ae, likely erected by her parents.

EDCS-31400885.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly recording a dedication or commemoration, with a reference to a patron.

EDCS-31400888.translation: To Aie, a most unhappy sister.
EDCS-31400888.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aie, a sister remembered as very unhappy, likely set up by a sibling.

EDCS-31400895.translation: by will. To Dae, a freedwoman [---] &
EDCS-31400895.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, indicating a provision made by will for a freedwoman named Dae.

EDCS-31400903.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning a person named Septimius.

EDCS-31400908.text_edition: $ nnos] XXVI  menses VII dies VIII Marcus ma  posuit
EDCS-31400908.translation: Marcus placed this for [someone who lived] 26 years, 7 months, 8 days.
EDCS-31400908.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone, recording the age of the deceased and the person who erected the monument, Marcus.

EDCS-31400926.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, addressed to a woman named Aurelia.

EDCS-31400954.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly mentioning a person or entity associated with the name Nigrianus.

EDCS-31400955.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone, dedicated by a son to his mother, Primigenia.

EDCS-31400956.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary or dedicatory inscription, addressed to a wife named Quinta.

EDCS-31400958.text_edition: Nymphis et Silvano  Aurelius Annianus bucinator cohortis VIII voluntariorum Antoninianae votum libens posuit
EDCS-31400958.translation: To the Nymphs and Silvanus, Aurelius Annianus, trumpeter of the Eighth Cohort of Antoninian Volunteers, willingly and deservedly placed this vow.
EDCS-31400958.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs and Silvanus by Aurelius Annianus, a military trumpeter, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-31500386.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum lio Sa vixit annos XL ADERA
EDCS-31500386.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. He lived 40 years.
EDCS-31500386.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone, dedicated to the Divine Spirits of the deceased, stating their age at death.

EDCS-31500387.text_edition: Servena Marci filia Maxima annorum XXV
EDCS-31500387.translation: Servena Maxima, daughter of Marcus, 25 years old.
EDCS-31500387.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone, for Servena Maxima, indicating her filiation and age at death.

EDCS-31500392.text_edition: domo Arimino miles legionis XIII donatus torquibus armillis phaleris et |(centurio) cohortis I Campestris annorum LX testamento fieri iussit Posidonius et Prunicus liberti posuerunt et ali nemini hic situs est
EDCS-31500392.translation: From Ariminum, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion, honored with torques, armillae, and phalerae, and a centurion of the First Campestrian Cohort, 60 years old. He ordered this to be made by will. Posidonius and Prunicus, his freedmen, erected it, and no one else is buried here.
EDCS-31500392.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a distinguished veteran, a centurion from Ariminum, detailing his military honors, origin, age, and that his freedmen erected the monument according to his will, specifying that only he is buried there.

EDCS-31900002.translation: Velosunus placed this inscription at his own expense.
EDCS-31900002.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, recording that Velosunus himself funded its erection, dating probably to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900003.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Petronia Lucilla, described as a most devoted mother, likely from a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-31900008.translation: Verantius Iunior made this for Rinis (?).
EDCS-31900008.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or epitaph by Verantius Iunior, with the exact context unclear due to its incompleteness.

EDCS-31900017.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Verecund |(centurioni) cohortis VIII volunt(ariorum) --- Titia Spes coniux  Iulius Iulianus  parentes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-31900017.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Quintus Julius Verecundus, centurion of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, his wife Titia Spes and parents Julius Julianus and [---] placed this for their well-deserving loved one.
EDCS-31900017.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Julius Verecundus, a centurion in a volunteer cohort, erected by his wife Titia Spes and his parents, Julius Julianus and an unnamed individual.

EDCS-31900020.text_edition: Titus Iulius Dianae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31900020.translation: Titus Julius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Diana.
EDCS-31900020.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Titus Julius, commemorating the fulfillment of a vow to the goddess Diana, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900021.text_edition: Minucia Secunda votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-31900021.translation: Minucia Secunda willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-31900021.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Minucia Secunda, indicating she fulfilled a vow willingly and deservedly, likely to an unnamed deity.

EDCS-31900022.summary: This inscription records a gift or offering made by Victorina, indicating a dedication or a votive act.

EDCS-31900026.text_edition: Matri Magnae Marcus Calpurnius Primus et Trebonia Chreste restituerunt
EDCS-31900026.translation: Marcus Calpurnius Primus and Trebonia Chreste restored this to the Great Mother.
EDCS-31900026.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a monument or offering to the goddess Magna Mater (the Great Mother) by Marcus Calpurnius Primus and Trebonia Chreste, indicating a religious dedication.

EDCS-31900028.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary or military record, mentioning a veteran of a cohort, with the full context lost.

EDCS-31900029.text_edition: Sextus Clodius C(ai) filius Publilia Verona miles legionis VII Claudia pia fidelis
EDCS-31900029.translation: Sextus Clodius, son of Gaius, of the Publilian voting tribe, from Verona, a soldier of the Seventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-31900029.summary: This inscription identifies Sextus Clodius as a soldier of the Legio VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, detailing his father's name, voting tribe, and origin, likely from a funerary monument or military diploma dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900030.text_edition: Sextus Cornelius Sexti filius Camilia nonanorum veteranus I
EDCS-31900030.translation: Sextus Cornelius, son of Sextus, of the Camilia tribe, a veteran of the Ninth Legion, I.
EDCS-31900030.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran, likely from the early Imperial period, recording his name, filiation, tribe, and military service.

EDCS-31900031.text_edition: Caius Parp-- Fabia ARECX annorum XLV stipendiorum XVI N
EDCS-31900031.translation: Gaius Parp[---] of the Fabia tribe, ARECX, 45 years old, 16 years of service, N.
EDCS-31900031.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a man who served 16 years, likely a soldier or veteran, indicating his age, name, and tribe.

EDCS-31900033.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marcus Novellius Ursus pater filiae Flaviae Tertullinae dulcissimae defunctae annorum VIII mensum sic IIII
EDCS-31900033.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Novellius Ursus, father, to his sweetest daughter, Flavia Tertullina, who died at 8 years and 4 months.
EDCS-31900033.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a father, Marcus Novellius Ursus, dedicated to his deceased daughter, Flavia Tertullina, who died at a young age.

EDCS-31900034.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Aeneas Proclianus aedem restituit  libens
EDCS-31900034.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor. Aeneas Proclianus willingly restored the temple.
EDCS-31900034.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription recording the restoration of a temple to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Depulsor by Aeneas Proclianus.

EDCS-31900036.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Surus Pirami libens posuit
EDCS-31900036.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Surus, slave of Piramus, willingly set this up.
EDCS-31900036.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the god Silvanus, set up by Surus, who was a slave of Piramus.

EDCS-31900037.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to a son named Sperantius, likely by a parent.

EDCS-31900039.text_edition: ius Ambrosianusosianuslius vBotum#votum#BOTUM   Iovi Optimo Maximo
EDCS-31900039.translation: Ius Ambrosianusosianuslius v. A vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-31900039.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, seemingly set up by someone named Ius Ambrosianusosianuslius.

EDCS-31900042.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Mi veteranus legionis V Macedonicae qui vixit annos
EDCS-31900042.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius M[---], a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, who lived [---] years.
EDCS-31900042.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aurelius M[---], a veteran of Legio V Macedonica, likely indicating his age at death.

EDCS-31900045.text_edition: AGI  et Agathiali   Veneria mater   bene merenti
EDCS-31900045.translation: To Agi and Agathialia. Veneria, mother, to her well-deserving children.
EDCS-31900045.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a mother, Veneria, to her two deceased children, Agi and Agathialia.

EDCS-31900050.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Castinus Aeliae Iobinae sic uxori infelicissimae quae vixxit sic annos XXVI si quis autem voluerit super haec trea sic corpora ponere voluerit inferret decreto folles |(mille) curiae nostrae
EDCS-31900050.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Castinus to his most unfortunate wife, Aelia Iobina, who lived 26 years. If anyone, however, should wish to place more bodies upon these three, he shall pay by decree 1000 sesterces to our curia.
EDCS-31900050.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelius Castinus for his wife Aelia Iobina, including a grave-protection clause fining anyone who dares to inter additional bodies in the family tomb.

EDCS-31900052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Gemella by an Aurelius, likely a family member, dating probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900055.summary: This fragment provides only an abbreviated name, possibly indicating a dedicant or an individual honored, without further context.

EDCS-31900056.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, identifying an individual named Celer.

EDCS-31900058.text_edition: II Elpistus  Corneliae Severae amicae bene merenti
EDCS-31900058.translation: Elpistus to Cornelia Severa, his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-31900058.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from Elpistus to Cornelia Severa, his friend who deserved well of him, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-31900061.translation: To the Divine Manes of Fannia Maritima, Capriusmus [set this up].
EDCS-31900061.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Fannia Maritima by Capriusmus, likely a family member or freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31900062.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning a person named Felix or Felicis.

EDCS-31900063.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Quinto Flavio Felici patri et Flaviae
EDCS-31900063.translation: To the Divine Manes of Quintus Flavius Felix, father, and Flavia.
EDCS-31900063.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Flavius Felix, a father, and Flavia, likely a family member, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31900065.text_edition: Caio Iulio Cai filio Tromentina Admeto nnum XXII
EDCS-31900065.translation: To Gaius Iulius Admetus, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, 22 years old.
EDCS-31900065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Iulius Admetus, recording his filiation, tribe, and age at death, probably dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31900066.summary: This is a fragmented inscription mentioning Lucianus, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-31900067.text_edition: MAM filio dulcissimo et piissimo mihi posuit
EDCS-31900067.translation: [---] to my sweetest and most devoted son, [I/he] placed this for me.
EDCS-31900067.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely erected by a parent to a beloved and devoted son, though the full context of who placed it for whom is somewhat ambiguous due to the fragment.

EDCS-31900071.translation: To the Divine Shades of Pomponia, freedwoman of Hi[...].
EDCS-31900071.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a freedwoman named Pomponia.

EDCS-31900073.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Publicio Proculo defuncto nnorum XV Titus Publicius Ursus filio dulcissimo pater
EDCS-31900073.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Publicius Ursus, father, to his sweetest son Titus Publicius Proculus, who died at 15 years old.
EDCS-31900073.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a father, Titus Publicius Ursus, for his deceased son, Titus Publicius Proculus, who passed away at the age of 15.

EDCS-31900076.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating a very pious individual named Secundus or Secunda.

EDCS-31900077.summary: This inscription records a testamentary act by Publius Statius I, indicating something was done according to his will.

EDCS-31900078.text_edition: matri pientissimae et merenti Sullius Inventus filius bene merenti
EDCS-31900078.translation: To his most devoted and deserving mother. Sullius Inventus, son, to the well-deserving.
EDCS-31900078.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Sullius Inventus, a son, honoring his most devoted and deserving mother.

EDCS-31900096.text_edition: Sexto Fundanio Quinti f(ilio) --- Ponponiae sic Sexti filiae Tertiae Caius Fundanius Clemens parentibus fecit
EDCS-31900096.translation: To Sextus Fundanius, son of Quintus [...]. To Pomponia Tertia, daughter of Sextus. Gaius Fundanius Clemens made this for his parents.
EDCS-31900096.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Fundanius Clemens to his parents, Sextus Fundanius and Pomponia Tertia.

EDCS-31900100.translation: [A]emilia Marcellina, a citizen of Novensium. MII.
EDCS-31900100.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aemilia Marcellina, who was associated with the municipality of Novensium.

EDCS-31900211.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Urso domo AcQruvino#Acruvino#AQR qui vixit annos plus minus XXXVIII Valeria Marcellina
EDCS-31900211.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Flavius Ursus from Acruvinum, who lived about 38 years. Valeria Marcellina [set this up].
EDCS-31900211.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Valeria Marcellina to Flavius Ursus, who was from Acruvinum and lived for approximately 38 years.

EDCS-31900216.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Flaviae Ianuariae quae vixit annos XLVIII Gratus coniugi incomparabili posuit
EDCS-31900216.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Flavia Ianuaria, who lived 48 years. Gratus placed this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-31900216.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Gratus for his incomparable wife, Flavia Ianuaria, who lived to be 48 years old.

EDCS-31900217.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Pinniae quae vixit annos XXX Marcus Valerius Quintianus uxori et Flaviae Quintinae filiae quae vixit annos X
EDCS-31900217.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Flavia Pinnia, who lived 30 years. Marcus Valerius Quintianus [set this up] for his wife and for Flavia Quintina, his daughter, who lived 10 years.
EDCS-31900217.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Marcus Valerius Quintianus, honoring his wife, Flavia Pinnia, who lived 30 years, and his daughter, Flavia Quintina, who lived 10 years.

EDCS-31900219.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Tullius Claudianus [set this up].
EDCS-31900219.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Lucius Tullius Claudianus.

EDCS-31900221.text_edition: Dis Manibus miserrimo S qui vixit annos X plus minus  MIIMO fi[li-- &
EDCS-31900221.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To a most wretched individual, who lived 10 years, more or less. [---] son [---].
EDCS-31900221.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a child who lived approximately ten years, set up by an unspecified relative.

EDCS-31900225.text_edition: Ausonia diaconissa pro voto suo et filiora sic suora sic fecit
EDCS-31900225.translation: Ausonia, a deaconess, made this according to her vow, for herself and her daughters, thus.
EDCS-31900225.summary: This inscription records that Ausonia, a deaconess, fulfilled a vow for herself and her daughters.

EDCS-31900226.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Publius Aelius Fuscinus miles legionis I adiutricis beneficiarius consularis Imperatore Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinace Augusto et Albino Caesare consulibus votum libens merito posuit
EDCS-31900226.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Fuscinus, a soldier of the First Adiutrix legion, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly placed this vow when Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus and Albinus Caesar were consuls.
EDCS-31900226.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Fuscinus, a soldier, placed during the joint consulate of Septimius Severus and Albinus (circa 193 AD).

EDCS-31900227.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Aentio filio piissimo qui vixit annos XIII Herma et Martia parentes ipsi sibi in memoriam posuerunt
EDCS-31900227.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Aentius, a most dutiful son, who lived for 13 years. Herma and Martia, his parents, placed this for themselves in memory.
EDCS-31900227.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Herma and Martia for their most dutiful son, Lucius Aentius, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-31900229.text_edition: Dis Manibus Germano qui vixit annos XV NISV filio pater posuit
EDCS-31900229.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Germanus, who lived for 15 years. His father placed this for his son, NISV.
EDCS-31900229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Germanus, who died at 15, set up by his father for his son.

EDCS-31900234.text_edition: Lucius Antonius Menander Aphrodisieys sic ILnvicto#Invicto#LNVICTO AugC(usto)#Aug(usto)#AUC votum fecit
EDCS-31900234.translation: Lucius Antonius Menander, an Aphrodisian, made this vow to the invincible Augustus.
EDCS-31900234.summary: This inscription records a vow made by Lucius Antonius Menander from Aphrodisias to an invincible Augustus.

EDCS-31900235.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Booni vixit annos LVI hic situs est Titus Aurelius Laiscus patri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-31900235.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Titus Aurelius Booni, who lived 56 years. He lies here. Titus Aurelius Laiscus placed this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-31900235.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Booni, who lived 56 years, set up by his son Titus Aurelius Laiscus.

EDCS-31900236.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelio Numeriano fratri pientissimo defuncto annorum XXVI et Aurelio Valerio avunculIo#avunculo#AVNCUIO defE(uncto)#def(uncto)#DEE annorum XL Aurelius Dazas pro pietate
EDCS-31900236.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelius Numerianus, a most devoted brother, who died at 26 years old, and to Aurelius Valerius, an uncle, who died at 40 years old. Aurelius Dazas [made this] out of piety.
EDCS-31900236.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Dazas, out of piety, to his most devoted brother, Aurelius Numerianus (who died at 26), and his uncle, Aurelius Valerius (who died at 40).

EDCS-31900237.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aureli-- Tit Bricussa- TiSIAtae matri infelicissimae defunctae annorum LXXV
EDCS-31900237.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelia [---] Tit Bricussa [---] to a most unhappy mother, who died at 75 years old.
EDCS-31900237.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a mother, Aurelia [---] Tit Bricussa [---], who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-31900238.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Titus Aurelius Saturninus Aureliae Amurcianae coniugi pientissimae bene merenti et sibi vivus memoriam posuit quae vixit annos XXVIIII menses IIII
EDCS-31900238.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Aurelius Saturninus placed this memorial for himself, while living, and for his most devoted and well-deserving wife Aurelia Amurciana, who lived for 29 years and 4 months.
EDCS-31900238.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Titus Aurelius Saturninus to his wife Aurelia Amurciana and to himself, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31900239.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Crescens.
EDCS-31900239.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to an individual named Crescens, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900240.text_edition: I Flavio Aprioni Aurelia Apronia m[emoriam &
EDCS-31900240.translation: To Flavius Aprioni, Aurelia Apronia [placed this] memorial.
EDCS-31900240.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely part of a tombstone or monument, dedicated by Aurelia Apronia to Flavius Aprioni, probably a relative, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900241.text_edition: piissimae ac devotissimae coniugi vivens hoc posuit
EDCS-31900241.translation: He placed this, while living, for his most devoted and affectionate wife.
EDCS-31900241.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely from a tombstone, dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900260.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunatae annorum XVIII et Rufo annorum XII Restutianus Valentini et Crescentilla filiis infelicibus
EDCS-31900260.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Fortunata, 18 years old, and Rufus, 12 years old. Restutianus, son of Valentinus, and Crescentilla [placed this] for their unfortunate children.
EDCS-31900260.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Restutianus and Crescentilla to their tragically deceased children, Fortunata and Rufus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900267.text_edition: Libero Patri sacrum Marcus Sextius Epaphroditus
EDCS-31900267.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Marcus Sextius Epaphroditus [dedicated this].
EDCS-31900267.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Marcus Sextius Epaphroditus to the god Liber Pater, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900268.text_edition: Marti Augusto Titus Aelius Firminus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit merito libens
EDCS-31900268.translation: To Mars Augustus. Titus Aelius Firminus, a beneficiarius consularis of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-31900268.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Titus Aelius Firminus, a soldier from the Legio XIIII Gemina, to Mars Augustus, indicating a fulfilled vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-31900270.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely funerary or a list, identifying an individual named Lucius Aemilius from Cremona, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900272.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victori Scenobarbi miles cohortis I Belgarum stipendiorum X tubicen testamento fieri iussit Munnius et Gentius heredes posuerunt
EDCS-31900272.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Victor Scenobarbus, a trumpeter in the First Cohort of Belgae, with ten years of service, ordered this to be made by will. Munnius and Gentius, his heirs, erected it.
EDCS-31900272.summary: This is a funerary monument for Victor Scenobarbus, a military trumpeter, erected by his heirs Munnius and Gentius according to his will, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-31900273.text_edition: Caio Publicio Romano decurioni nnorum XXX Caio Licinio Expectato Publiciae Iluricae nnorum V infelicissima mater filiis et nepotiae viva fecit et pater infelix senex
EDCS-31900273.translation: To Gaius Publicius Romanus, decurion, 30 years old; to Gaius Licinius Expectatus; to Publicia Ilurica, 5 years old. The most unfortunate mother, while living, made this for her children and granddaughter, and the unfortunate old father.
EDCS-31900273.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by an unfortunate mother and an old father, while still living, to their children, Gaius Publicius Romanus (a decurion) and Gaius Licinius Expectatus, and their granddaughter Publicia Ilurica, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900274.text_edition: Sexto Publicio Sexti liberto Secundo IIIIIIviro Sextus Publicius Hesper VIvir libertus
EDCS-31900274.translation: To Sextus Publicius Secundus, freedman of Sextus, sevir Augustalis. Sextus Publicius Hesper, freedman, sevir Augustalis, [set this up].
EDCS-31900274.summary: This is a funerary or dedicatory inscription set up by a freedman, Sextus Publicius Hesper, a sevir Augustalis, for another freedman, Sextus Publicius Secundus, who also held the office of sevir Augustalis, likely in the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-31900275.text_edition: Quinto Turelio Quinti liberto Acuto IIIIIIviro Tureliae Quinti libertae Hilarae Lucio Turelio Quinti filio Feroci nnorum XXII Sexto Turelio Quinti filio Veloci DI
EDCS-31900275.translation: To Quintus Turelius Acutus, freedman of Quintus, sevir Augustalis; to Turelia Hilara, freedwoman of Quintus; to Lucius Turelius Ferox, son of Quintus, 22 years old; to Sextus Turelius Velox, son of Quintus, [aged ---].
EDCS-31900275.summary: This is a family funerary monument dedicated to several members of the Turelia gens, including a freedman who was a sevir Augustalis, his freedwoman relative, and two sons, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-31900276.text_edition: Quinto Veturio Secundino decurioni coloniae Naronae qui vixit nnos XLVIII Livinia Fortunata marito inconparabili sic posuit
EDCS-31900276.translation: To Quintus Veturius Secundinus, decurion of the colonia Narona, who lived 48 years. Livinia Fortunata placed this for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-31900276.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Livinia Fortunata for her husband, Quintus Veturius Secundinus, a decurion of the colonia Narona, who died at age 48, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900277.text_edition: Saturnino actori Saturnianus et Valeria patri rarissimo
EDCS-31900277.translation: To Saturninus, the agent. Saturnianus and Valeria [set this up] for their most rare father.
EDCS-31900277.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Saturnianus and Valeria to their father, Saturninus, who held the title of 'actor', likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900278.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Annaeo Quinto filio Annaeus Pudens pater miser qui filium terrae dedi annorum XXXX
EDCS-31900278.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Annaeus, my son, Annaeus Pudens, his wretched father, who gave his son to the earth, aged 40 years.
EDCS-31900278.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from a grieving father, Annaeus Pudens, for his son, Annaeus, who died at the age of 40, typical of Roman tomb markers of the Imperial era.

EDCS-31900279.text_edition: Aquilliae Luci libertae Myrsinae Exoratae Luci filiae Luculla Luci filia Tigris matri et patri et sorori et fratri fecit
EDCS-31900279.translation: To Aquillia Myrsina, freedwoman of Lucius; to Exorata, daughter of Lucius; Luculla Tigris, daughter of Lucius, made this for her mother and father and sister and brother.
EDCS-31900279.summary: This is a family funerary monument set up by Luculla Tigris for multiple relatives, including her mother, Aquillia Myrsina, a freedwoman, her father, and her siblings, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900280.text_edition: Atilia Tranquilla et Atilius Theseus vivi fecerunt sibi et suis
EDCS-31900280.translation: Atilia Tranquilla and Atilius Theseus, living, made this for themselves and their family.
EDCS-31900280.summary: This is a funerary monument made while Atilia Tranquilla and Atilius Theseus were still alive (a 'vivi fecerunt' inscription), intended for themselves and their family, a common practice in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31900282.text_edition: Cornelia Luci liberta  Baebia Ha here--- eius
EDCS-31900282.translation: Cornelia Baebia, freedwoman of Lucius, her heir.
EDCS-31900282.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary or dedicatory inscription involving Cornelia Baebia, a freedwoman, and mention of an heir, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900284.text_edition: annorum  Iulia Primitiva uxor marito bene merito
EDCS-31900284.translation: [Aged ---] years. Iulia Primitiva, wife, for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-31900284.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Iulia Primitiva for her 'well-deserving' husband, with his age partially lost, consistent with Roman tomb markers of the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900285.summary: This is a fragmented dedicatory or funerary inscription, likely honoring Marcus Iunius Ingenuus, characteristic of Roman epigraphy from the Imperial period.

EDCS-31900286.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Maximus Lupulae coniugi incomparabilIi#incomparabili#INCOPARABIII benae sic merenti fecit et con sic qOua#qua#OUA vixit annos plus minus XX et filiae Maximae qOuae#quae#OUAE vixit annos plus minus IIIII
EDCS-31900286.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Maximus made this for Lupula, his incomparable and well-deserving wife, who lived more or less 20 years, and for their daughter Maxima, who lived more or less 5 years.
EDCS-31900286.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Maximus for his incomparable wife Lupula and their daughter Maxima, likely from the Imperial period based on common formulae.

EDCS-31900287.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Mucio Filumeno sic marito rarissimo et Muciae Proculinae filiae Porcia Procula bene merenti viva fecit sibi et suis
EDCS-31900287.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Porcia Procula, while living, made this for herself and her family, for Mucius Philumenus, her most rare husband, and for Mucia Proculina, her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-31900287.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Porcia Procula while alive for herself, her husband Mucius Philumenus, her daughter Mucia Proculina, and her family, common for Roman burial practices in the Imperial era.

EDCS-31900288.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Philete bene meritae Publius Mescenius Severianus
EDCS-31900288.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Mescenius Severianus [set this up] for Philete, who deserved well.
EDCS-31900288.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Mescenius Severianus for Philete, acknowledging her good character.

EDCS-31900289.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a dedication to or for Publius Publicius Antho, likely from a tomb or public monument.

EDCS-31900290.text_edition: Titus Pullicus Rufi filius Romilia Caius Pullicus Titi filius Romilia annorum XXIIII Quintus Pullicus Titi filius Salvius
EDCS-31900290.translation: Titus Pullicus, son of Rufus, of the Romilian tribe. Gaius Pullicus, son of Titus, of the Romilian tribe, 24 years old. Quintus Pullicus, son of Titus, Salvius.
EDCS-31900290.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, Titus Pullicus, Gaius Pullicus, and Quintus Pullicus, potentially from a family funerary monument or a public record of citizens, indicating their filiation, tribe, and age for Gaius.

EDCS-31900291.text_edition: nia Secunda i avonculo sic consubrino sua pecunia fecit
EDCS-31900291.translation: Nia Secunda, for her uncle and cousin, made this with her own money.
EDCS-31900291.summary: This inscription records Nia Secunda dedicating a monument, possibly a tomb, to her uncle and cousin using her own funds, highlighting familial piety and personal expenditure.

EDCS-31900292.text_edition: Valeriae Piae annorum XII Spedia Procula mater filiae pientissimae et Valerio Tauro coniugi et sibi et posteris suis viva fecit
EDCS-31900292.translation: For Valeria Pia, 12 years old. Spedia Procula, mother, made this while living for her most devoted daughter, and for Valerius Taurus, her husband, and for herself and her descendants.
EDCS-31900292.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Spedia Procula while alive for her deceased daughter Valeria Pia, her husband Valerius Taurus, herself, and their future descendants, typical of Roman family tombs.

EDCS-31900293.text_edition: Dis Manibus Victori filio pientissimo vixit nnis XX diebus XVI Eutyches et Ursula parentes et sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-31900293.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Eutyches and Ursula, his parents, made this while living for their most devoted son Victor, who lived 20 years and 16 days, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-31900293.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Eutyches and Ursula while alive for their deceased son Victor, and also for themselves and their family, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-31900294.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ger Lucullae sorori pientissimae titulum posuit defunctae annorum XXVI
EDCS-31900294.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. [Someone, likely 'Ger'] placed this titulus for his most devoted sister Luculla, who died at 26 years of age.
EDCS-31900294.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a tombstone (titulus) for the deceased Luculla, a devoted sister who died at age 26, by an unstated relative, likely a brother given the partial name 'Ger'.

EDCS-31900298.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aureliae Lucretiae Aurelius Surus et Aurelia Licinia matri pientissimae posuerunt titulum
EDCS-31900298.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aurelius Surus and Aurelia Licinia placed this titulus for their most devoted mother Aurelia Lucretia.
EDCS-31900298.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Lucretia, erected by her children Aurelius Surus and Aurelia Licinia, honoring their most devoted mother with a tombstone (titulus).

EDCS-31900299.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelia Ursina Artoriae Privatae matri pientissimae posuit quae vixit annos LX menses sex
EDCS-31900299.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelia Ursina set this up for Artoria Privata, her most devoted mother, who lived for 60 years and six months.
EDCS-31900299.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelia Ursina to her mother, Artoria Privata, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31900301.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedication to the Emperor Augustus, probably from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-31900304.text_edition: Andamionius Andami filius eques cohortis I Lucensium nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XV hic situs est Gavillius frater eius posuit
EDCS-31900304.translation: Andamionius, son of Andamus, a cavalryman of the First Cohort of Lucenses, 35 years old, with 15 years of service, is laid here. Gavillius, his brother, set this up.
EDCS-31900304.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a Roman auxiliary cavalryman, Andamionius, erected by his brother, Gavillius, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31900305.text_edition: Lucius Domitius Luci filius nniensi Aquila veteranus legionis VII domo Myliada sic stipendiorum XXXIII vivus fecit sibi et Caio Domitio  Aniensi Aquilino militi legionis VII stipendiorum XX honesta missione    A V
EDCS-31900305.translation: Lucius Domitius Aquila, son of Lucius, of the Aniensis tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, from Myliada, made this alive for himself and for Gaius Domitius Aquilinus, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, with 33 years of service for himself and 20 years of service for Gaius Domitius, honorably discharged.
EDCS-31900305.summary: This is a funerary monument prepared during his lifetime by Lucius Domitius Aquila, a veteran of Legio VII, for himself and for Gaius Domitius Aquilinus, likely a relative, also of Legio VII, from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31900306.text_edition: Rufus Angeti filius miles cohortis I Lucensium annorum XXX stipendiorum XI hic situs est heres posuit
EDCS-31900306.translation: Rufus, son of Angetus, a soldier of the First Cohort of Lucenses, 30 years old, with 11 years of service, is laid here. His heir set this up.
EDCS-31900306.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufus, an auxiliary soldier, erected by his heir, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31900307.text_edition: Marcus Sosius Marci filius Fabia Sebastopoli veteranus? legionis VII
EDCS-31900307.translation: Marcus Sosius, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, from Sebastopolis, a veteran (?) of the Seventh Legion.
EDCS-31900307.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Marcus Sosius, a veteran of Legio VII, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31900309.text_edition: Vanaius Venionti filius domo Bodionticus? eques cohortis III Alpinorum annorum LIIII stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Valeria et Marcella posuerunt
EDCS-31900309.translation: Vanaius, son of Veniontus, from Bodionticus, a cavalryman of the Third Alpine Cohort, 54 years old, with 25 years of service, is laid here. Valeria and Marcella set this up.
EDCS-31900309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Vanaius, an auxiliary cavalryman, erected by Valeria and Marcella, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-32100153.text_edition: I et Iacus CE frater et -A Festus coniugi pientissimae
EDCS-32100153.translation: I and Iacus, his brother, and Festus, for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-32100153.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely dedicated to a woman by her husband, Festus, and other family members, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-32100154.text_edition: e Ni<g=C>idius#Ni<g>idius#NICI  filius  us VE us libertus eius maritus
EDCS-32100154.translation: Ni<g>idius, son of Ni<g>idius, his freedman, her husband.
EDCS-32100154.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that mentions a freedman and a husband, likely part of a funerary monument, from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-32100155.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Marcus Ulpius Vitalis beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32100155.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars, Marcus Ulpius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis of the Tenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32100155.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Marcus Ulpius Vitalis, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio X Gemina, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-32200004.text_edition: Trosia Prima Buccionis liberta votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32200004.translation: Trosia Prima, freedwoman of Buccio, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-32200004.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Trosia Prima's fulfillment of a vow to a deity, likely during the early Imperial period.

EDCS-32200011.translation: Of Gaius Oppius Cleme[ns or similar].
EDCS-32200011.summary: This fragment records the name of a man, Gaius Oppius, likely from a funerary monument or dedication from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-32200014.text_edition: Dis sacrum Manibus Flavius Rufantius Deidmu vivus sibi posuit et Aureliae Melliti coniugi pientissimae quae vicxit sic nnos XLVII
EDCS-32200014.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Flavius Rufantius, while living, set this up for himself and for Aurelia Melliti, his most devoted wife, who lived for 47 years.
EDCS-32200014.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Flavius Rufantius for himself and his beloved wife, Aurelia Melliti, indicating a date likely in the High Imperial period.

EDCS-32200016.text_edition: Dis Manibus s(acrum) PPFOS PII sibi et coniugi -
EDCS-32200016.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. PPFOS PII for himself and his wife.
EDCS-32200016.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual and his wife, dedicated to the Divine Manes, probably from the High Imperial period.

EDCS-32200026.translation: Gaius Flavius Valens, soldier of the legion.
EDCS-32200026.summary: This is an epitaph or a dedication for a Roman soldier named Gaius Flavius Valens, serving in an unnamed legion, likely from the High Imperial period.

EDCS-32200027.text_edition: Bindo Neptuno sacrum ob honorem IV-sti PROG amici posuerunt
EDCS-32200027.translation: Sacred to Bindo Neptune, in honor of their friend Iustus Prog[---], they set this up.
EDCS-32200027.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Bindo Neptune, set up by a group of people in honor of their friend Iustus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32201011.text_edition: Iovi Iunoni Minervae Marcus Ulpius Canius beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201011.translation: To Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, Marcus Ulpius Canius, beneficiarius consularis of the First Adiutrix Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201011.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to the Capitoline Triad (Jupiter, Juno, Minerva) by Marcus Ulpius Canius, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Adiutrix, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-32201014.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Naevius Maximus beneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201014.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Naevius Maximus, beneficiarius consularis of the Eleventh Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201014.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Naevius Maximus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-32201015.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Marti Augusto sacrum  Lucius Aemilius miles legionis  I ad(iutricis) beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201015.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Augustus, Lucius Aemilius, soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, beneficiarius consularis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201015.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Mars Augustus by Lucius Aemilius, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Adiutrix, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-32201016.text_edition: Lucius Aurelius Lupus cum suis donum dedit
EDCS-32201016.translation: Lucius Aurelius Lupus with his people gave this gift.
EDCS-32201016.summary: This inscription records a gift or dedication made by Lucius Aurelius Lupus and his family or associates, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32201017.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae divi Hadriani filio divi Traiani Parthici nepoti divi Nervae pronepoti pontifici maximo tribunicia postestate XXI consuli IIII decreto decurionum
EDCS-32201017.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the homeland, son of divine Hadrian, grandson of divine Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 21st time, consul for the 4th time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-32201017.summary: This inscription is a public dedication, likely for a statue or monument, honoring Emperor Antoninus Pius, commissioned by the local town council (decurions), and can be dated precisely to 158 AD based on his tribunician power and consulships.

EDCS-32201019.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary stone, likely part of a funerary or dedicatory monument, mentioning a veteran, but its original purpose and full content are largely lost due to its damaged state.

EDCS-32201021.text_edition: ANI  Flavii Silvanus et Severus patri filii D
EDCS-32201021.translation: ]ANI. Flavii Silvanus and Severus, sons to their father D.
EDCS-32201021.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Flavius Silvanus and Severus, likely brothers, dedicate a monument to their father, whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-32201022.text_edition: Dis Manibus Seiae Flavi Silvani servae LXXV Scorpus maritus pro pietate
EDCS-32201022.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Seia, slave of Flavius Silvanus, 75 years old. Scorpus, her husband, for his devotion.
EDCS-32201022.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Scorpus to his deceased wife Seia, a slave of Flavius Silvanus, honoring her memory with piety.

EDCS-32201024.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Flavius M AV--- b(ene)f(iciarius)? co(n)s(ularis) legionis X  |(centurio) legionis XI votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201024.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno, Minerva. Flavius M. AV---, beneficiarius consularis of the X Legion, centurion of the XI Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201024.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by Flavius M. AV---, a high-ranking soldier who served as a beneficiarius consularis and a centurion in two different legions, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32201027.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ulpiae Catiae annorum L iugius coniugi pientissimae et sibi vivo posuit
EDCS-32201027.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Ulpia Catia, 50 years old. Iugius placed this for his most devoted wife and for himself while living.
EDCS-32201027.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iugius to his deceased wife, Ulpia Catia, and also prepared for himself while still alive, expressing his deep affection for her.

EDCS-32201029.text_edition: Dis Manibus Septimia Matisa vixit annos LX Septimius Victor sibi et coniugi vivus bene merenti pro pietate
EDCS-32201029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Septimia Matisa lived 60 years. Septimius Victor, for himself and his well-deserving wife, while living, out of devotion.
EDCS-32201029.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Septimius Victor for himself and for his deceased wife, Septimia Matisa, celebrating her life and their bond with piety.

EDCS-32201030.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Lucius Sicinius Macrinus beneficiarius consularis Pannoniae superioris votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201030.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place. Lucius Sicinius Macrinus, beneficiarius consularis of Upper Pannonia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201030.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and the local guardian spirit by Lucius Sicinius Macrinus, a high-ranking military official in Upper Pannonia, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32201031.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septimio A veteFrano#veterano#VETFRANO vixit annos LII Titia  marito pientissimo et sibi C B
EDCS-32201031.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Septimius, a veteran, who lived 52 years. Titia, for her most devoted husband and for herself C.B.
EDCS-32201031.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Titia for her deceased husband, Septimius, a veteran, expressing her devotion, and also for herself.

EDCS-32201032.text_edition: Soli Invicto Mithrae VeFturius#Veturius#VFTUR Lucius votum solvit
EDCS-32201032.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras. Veturius Lucius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201032.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the syncretic deity Sol Invictus Mithras by Veturius Lucius, indicating his participation in the Mithraic cult and the fulfillment of a religious promise.

EDCS-32201033.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication to a woman named Saturnina, with much of the text missing.

EDCS-32201034.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, preserving only the beginning of a name, Lucius Fronto.

EDCS-32201037.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius C[---]
EDCS-32201037.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Publius Aelius C[---].

EDCS-32201040.text_edition: LIO Iuniori decurioni coloniae Salonitanorum quaestori aedili IIviro iure dicundo designato
EDCS-32201040.translation: to LIO the Younger, decurion of the colony of Salonitana, quaestor, aedile, duumvir with judicial power designate.
EDCS-32201040.summary: This inscription records the public offices held by LIO the Younger, including decurion, quaestor, aedile, and duumvir with judicial power designate, in the colony of Salonitana.

EDCS-32201043.text_edition: -? Aelio Epidio Statorius Florus amico bene merenti
EDCS-32201043.translation: [---] Aelio Epidio Statorius Florus [erected this] to a well-deserving friend.
EDCS-32201043.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Statorius Florus to his well-deserving friend, Aelio Epidio.

EDCS-32201046.text_edition: Flavio ? Crescenti filia pientissima patri bono bene merenti testamento iussa
EDCS-32201046.translation: To Flavius Crescentius, his most devoted daughter, by command of his will, to a good, well-deserving father.
EDCS-32201046.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a devoted daughter to her good and well-deserving father, Flavius Crescentius, as instructed by his will.

EDCS-32201047.text_edition: Dis Manibus Fortunata Fortunio fEilio#filio#EILIO defuncto nnorum III mensium VII
EDCS-32201047.translation: To the Divine Manes. Fortunata [erected this] to Fortunius, her deceased son, who lived 3 years and 7 months.
EDCS-32201047.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, by Fortunata for her son, Fortunius, who died at the age of three years and seven months.

EDCS-32201048.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Murcidio Maximo defuncto annorum X mensium VI Murcidia Maxima mater
EDCS-32201048.translation: To the Divine Manes. Murcidia Maxima, his mother, [erected this] to Marcus Murcidius Maximus, deceased, who lived 10 years and 6 months.
EDCS-32201048.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Murcidia Maxima for her son, Marcus Murcidius Maximus, who died at the age of ten years and six months.

EDCS-32201049.text_edition: Dis Manibus Murcidiae Maximae heredes bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-32201049.translation: To the Divine Manes. The heirs placed this for Murcidia Maxima, who deserved well.
EDCS-32201049.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, erected by the heirs for Murcidia Maxima, who was well-deserving.

EDCS-32201052.text_edition: Viscia Paula soror et coniunx Viscia Firma uno anno obiere simul duo mensibus inter nonaginta annos vixerunt adque duobus sic quadraginta et quinque soror reliquos fuit uxor quarum pro meritis tumulis duo corpora iunxi Quintus Viscius Valens duplici pietate propinquus
EDCS-32201052.translation: Viscia Paula, sister and wife. Viscia Firma. The two of them died together in one year, with ninety months between them. They lived for [---] years, and the sister remained a wife for forty-five [years]. Quintus Viscius Valens, their kinsman, with double piety, joined their two bodies in these tombs according to their merits.
