EDCS-32201052.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Quintus Viscius Valens for Viscia Paula, his sister and wife, and Viscia Firma, commemorating their lives and shared death.

EDCS-32201056.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary epitaph, mentioning the name Petrus and possibly a surname Pinosat.

EDCS-32201058.text_edition: Deo Magno Conservatori loci huius et omnium suorum Aurelius Terentianus votum libens solvit
EDCS-32201058.translation: To the Great God, preserver of this place and all his own, Aurelius Terentianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32201058.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Aurelius Terentianus to a Great God, likely a local deity, for preserving the place and his possessions, indicating a thanksgiving or fulfillment of a pledge.

EDCS-32201059.text_edition: Iunia Rhodine cum coniuge et fili- Deum Matri Magnae aedem refecit et ampliavit votum susceptum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32201059.translation: Iunia Rhodine, with her husband and children, rebuilt and enlarged a temple to the Great Mother Goddess, willingly and deservedly fulfilling a vow undertaken.
EDCS-32201059.summary: This inscription records that Iunia Rhodine, along with her family, restored and expanded a temple dedicated to the Magna Mater, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-32201064.translation: Marcus Cornelius Priscus, of the cohort...
EDCS-32201064.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Marcus Cornelius Priscus and indicating his affiliation with a military cohort.

EDCS-32201065.text_edition: Flavius Serenus ex primicerio  vixit nnos X
EDCS-32201065.translation: Flavius Serenus, former primicerius, lived for 10 years.
EDCS-32201065.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius Serenus, a former primicerius, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-32201068.text_edition: cohortis   ex peditibus   defuncto nnorum  stipendiorum XIIII  Amandus fili--
EDCS-32201068.translation: Of the cohort, among the foot soldiers, to the deceased who lived for [---] years and served 14 years. Amandus, his son, [---]
EDCS-32201068.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a soldier who served 14 years, placed by his son Amandus.

EDCS-32201080.text_edition: Africano defunto mensium VIII Aurelia Fortunata et Aurelius Saturninus filio infelicissimo
EDCS-32201080.translation: To Africanus, who died at 8 months, Aurelia Fortunata and Aurelius Saturninus [placed this] for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-32201080.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Africanus, who died as an infant at eight months old, erected by his parents Aurelia Fortunata and Aurelius Saturninus.

EDCS-32201085.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedication or epitaph, mentioning Aurelius Epicadius or a similar name.

EDCS-32201086.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelio Asterio defuncto nnorum XL Aurelia Octavia coniux inconparabili et Aurelius Dionysius patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-32201086.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Aurelius Asterius, who died at 40 years of age, Aurelia Octavia, his incomparable wife, and Aurelius Dionysius placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-32201086.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Asterius, who died at 40, erected by his wife Aurelia Octavia and his son Aurelius Dionysius.

EDCS-32201089.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Caerellia Successa.
EDCS-32201089.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caerellia Successa, invoking the Dis Manibus.

EDCS-32201092.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Cania Quadratia Canioio Lucifero filio qui vicxit sic annos XXX mater inMfelicissima#infelicissima#IMFELICISSMA posuit
EDCS-32201092.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Cania Quadratia, a most unhappy mother, placed this for her son Canioius Luciferus, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-32201092.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Cania Quadratia, dedicating a tombstone to her son, Canioius Luciferus, who died at the age of 30, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32201094.text_edition: vixit annis LXVI Clara marito inco[mparabili &
EDCS-32201094.translation: She lived for 66 years. Clara [set this up] for her incomparable husband...
EDCS-32201094.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by Clara for her deceased husband, who lived to be 66 years old.

EDCS-32201095.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a deceased person named Claudia.

EDCS-32201098.text_edition: Dis Manibus Corneliae Maximinae quae vixit annos XX Cornelius ius pater filiae infelicissimae fecit
EDCS-32201098.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Cornelius Ius, her father, made this for his most unhappy daughter Cornelia Maximina, who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-32201098.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Cornelius Ius for his daughter, Cornelia Maximina, who died at the age of 20, indicating a father's grief.

EDCS-32201099.text_edition: Dis Manibus ia Crescentilla Septimiae Secundinae matri pientissimae defunctae nnorum XL
EDCS-32201099.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. [---]ia Crescentilla [made this] for Septimia Secundina, her most devoted mother, who died at 40 years of age.
EDCS-32201099.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Crescentilla to her mother, Septimia Secundina, who passed away at 40 years old.

EDCS-32201101.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Euryalini Onesimus maritus et filii Ursulianus et Ursina matri pientissimae posuerunt
EDCS-32201101.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. Onesimus, her husband, and their children, Ursulianus and Ursina, placed this for their most devoted mother Euryaline.
EDCS-32201101.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Onesimus and their two children, Ursulianus and Ursina, to Euryaline, their wife and mother.

EDCS-32201102.text_edition: Dis Manibus Felicissimae bene merenti quae vixit aannos
EDCS-32201102.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. For Felicissima, who deserved well and lived for [---] years.
EDCS-32201102.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Felicissima, praised for her good deeds, with her age at death partially lost.

EDCS-32201104.text_edition: defunct-- annorum III mensium  Titus Flavius Syneros  alumn
EDCS-32201104.translation: [---] died at 3 years and [---] months. Titus Flavius Syneros, his foster-child.
EDCS-32201104.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, for a child who died at three years old, dedicated or referenced by Titus Flavius Syneros, possibly as a foster parent or guardian.

EDCS-32201105.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flaviae Rufinae annorum plus minus XXVI Donatus coniugCi#coniugi#OIUCI inAconparabili sic#inconparabili sic#IACONPARLI
EDCS-32201105.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the Dead. For Flavia Rufina, about 26 years old. Donatus [made this] for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-32201105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Donatus to his incomparable wife, Flavia Rufina, who died at approximately 26 years of age.

EDCS-32201107.text_edition: Fundaniae Cai libertae Ias hic sita eFst#est#FS Caius Quintius Proculus heres fecit
EDCS-32201107.translation: For Fundania Ias, freedwoman of Gaius, she lies here. Gaius Quintius Proculus, her heir, made this.
EDCS-32201107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fundania Ias, a freedwoman of Gaius, erected by her heir, Gaius Quintius Proculus, indicating her freed status and the role of an heir in her burial.

EDCS-32201108.translation: Istus Galaticus, her husband, [set this up] for his sweetest wife.
EDCS-32201108.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a husband's dedication to his beloved wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32201109.summary: This fragment is too incomplete to determine the type of monument or its full purpose, but it mentions a name, Hilar(us/a), and likely refers to some action or state.

EDCS-32201110.text_edition: M Illyrica Iulio Diadumeno marito pientissimo defuncto annorum
EDCS-32201110.translation: M. Illyrica [set this up] for Iulius Diadumenus, her most devoted husband, who died at the age of [---] years.
EDCS-32201110.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a wife, M. Illyrica, dedicating a memorial to her deeply devoted deceased husband, Iulius Diadumenus.

EDCS-32201111.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gaius Iulius Fil(---).
EDCS-32201111.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, mentioning Gaius Iulius and an abbreviated cognomen or filiation.

EDCS-32201113.text_edition: Dis Manibus  IuliaeF#Iuliae#IULIAF Faustinae defEunctae#defunctae#DEEUNCTE annorum XXV posuerunt parentes pientissimi? ---
EDCS-32201113.translation: To the Divine Manes. The most devoted parents placed this for Iulia Faustina, who died at the age of 25 years [---].
EDCS-32201113.summary: This is a funerary inscription by grieving parents for their deceased daughter, Iulia Faustina, who passed away at 25 years old.

EDCS-32201114.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Iulia Satrieni, [daughter/wife] of Chresimus.
EDCS-32201114.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the dead, commemorating Iulia, whose filiation or marital relation is linked to Satrienus Chresimus.

EDCS-32201119.text_edition: defunct-- nnorum X occoni Fortunat coniugi
EDCS-32201119.translation: [---] deceased [at the age of ---] years, [---] to Fortunata, his/her spouse.
EDCS-32201119.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for an unnamed individual who died at an unknown age, dedicated by their spouse Fortunata.

EDCS-32201120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Papiriae Eutunae Arrius Primus coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-32201120.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arrius Primus [set this up] for Papiria Eutuna, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-32201120.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Arrius Primus dedicating a memorial to his deserving wife, Papiria Eutuna.

EDCS-32201122.translation: Of Primus, most devoted and delicate [child/servant].
EDCS-32201122.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a beloved and devout individual named Primus, possibly a child or a servant, but its exact context (e.g., funerary) is incomplete.

EDCS-32201125.text_edition: s Sex tus et  ia Chres[te &
EDCS-32201125.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning two individuals, Sextus and Iulia Chreste, whose relationship or the purpose of the inscription is unclear due to its incompleteness.

EDCS-32201133.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Ulpia Celerina dulces sic habe sic Gorgonio tuo castissima senper sic cuius simplicitas et obsequentia laudatur et amatur ubique et cuius cOonsiliis#consiliis#OONSILIS nulla decepta amore aeternamque domum ut fecit superstes maritus sic maeret virginia votissima mihi uno marito et vos benAe#bene#BEAE vivendo haec saltem sperare debPetis#debetis#DEPETIS Ulpius Gorgonius piissimus Celerinae conpari pientissimae cum qua concorditer vicxit sic nnos XXIIII menses V dies XXV vivBi#vivi#VIBI sibi posuerunt posterisque suis

EDCS-32300021.text_edition: Dasas annorum IX Pantus Tit Panes filio
EDCS-32300021.translation: Dasas, nine years old. Pantus (and) Tit Panes (his) son.
EDCS-32300021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a nine-year-old named Dasas, dedicated by Pantus and his son, Tit Panes.

EDCS-32300022.summary: This is a brief inscription identifying Trittus Druanus as the son of Plahenini.

EDCS-32300023.text_edition: Dis Manibus Suttis IOM posuit sibi annorum LXI
EDCS-32300023.translation: To the Divine Manes. Suttis IOM placed this for herself, 61 years old.
EDCS-32300023.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Suttis IOM for herself, indicating she was 61 years old.

EDCS-32300025.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a person named Ulpius and their age, likely from a funerary monument.

EDCS-32300026.text_edition: Dis Manibus AEQIOM annorum  et Dasio
EDCS-32300026.translation: To the Divine Manes. AEQIOM, [---] years old, and Dasio.
EDCS-32300026.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for AEQIOM and Dasio.

EDCS-32300027.text_edition: Armato Augusto sacrum Mattonia Tertia libens posuit
EDCS-32300027.translation: Sacred to Armatus Augustus. Mattonia Tertia willingly placed this.
EDCS-32300027.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Armatus Augustus by Mattonia Tertia, who willingly placed it.

EDCS-32300028.translation: Sacred to Diana. Aelius Maximinus (placed this).
EDCS-32300028.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by Aelius Maximinus.

EDCS-32300029.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus Flavius Secundus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32300029.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Flavius Secundus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32300029.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta by Titus Flavius Secundus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32300031.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or dedicatory, mentioning a name ending in -ius and possibly starting with Januarius.

EDCS-32300033.text_edition: Titus Flavius Ursio Lavoni coniugi bene merenti defunctae annorum XXX posuit
EDCS-32300033.translation: Titus Flavius Ursio placed this for Lavoni, his most deserving wife, who died at 30 years old.
EDCS-32300033.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Titus Flavius Ursio for his deceased wife, Lavoni, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-32300034.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named Victor, noting his age.

EDCS-32300036.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning a person named Victorinus.

EDCS-32300038.text_edition: Marti Augusto sacrum Caius Terentius Celsus evocatus Augusti votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32300038.translation: Sacred to Mars Augustus. Gaius Terentius Celsus, an evocatus Augusti, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32300038.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Mars Augustus by Gaius Terentius Celsus, a veteran recalled to service, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-32300039.text_edition: Quintus Gavius Quinti filius Arnensis Optatus domo Brixello miles legionis XI annorum XXXIII stipendiorum XIII hic situs est
EDCS-32300039.translation: Quintus Gavius Optatus, son of Quintus, of the Arnensis tribe, from Brixellum, a soldier of the 11th legion, 33 years old, with 13 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-32300039.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Gavius Optatus, a soldier of the Legio XI, recording his origin, age, and years of service.

EDCS-32300040.text_edition: Iano p(atri) Publius AelI(ius)#Ael(ius)#AEI Castus votum solvit libens merito somnio admonitus
EDCS-32300040.translation: To Father Janus, Publius Aelius Castus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, admonished by a dream.
EDCS-32300040.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Father Janus by Publius Aelius Castus, who fulfilled a vow after being prompted by a dream.

EDCS-32300043.text_edition: D(is)? M(anibus) Valeri Valeriani militis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XXXVII
EDCS-32300043.translation: To the Divine Manes. Of Valerius Valerianus, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Legion, loyal and faithful, 37 years old.
EDCS-32300043.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes for Valerius Valerianus, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, recording his age.

EDCS-32300050.text_edition: Situs est crudelem funus puaero Gaio Paetronio Valenti annorum XIIII parentes desolati
EDCS-32300050.translation: Here lies the cruel death of the boy Gaius Petronius Valens, 14 years old. His parents are desolated.
EDCS-32300050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the young boy Gaius Petronius Valens, erected by his grieving parents, noting his premature death at age 14.

EDCS-32300055.text_edition: Quintus Publicius et Flavia Primula Augustioni delicato suo Dis Manibus
EDCS-32300055.translation: Quintus Publicius and Flavia Primula [dedicated this] to the Divine Manes for their beloved Augustio.
EDCS-32300055.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Manes for Augustio, dedicated by Quintus Publicius and Flavia Primula, likely his parents or guardians.

EDCS-32300060.summary: This is a brief funerary or dedicatory inscription identifying Aeta as the daughter of Roscius Turus.

EDCS-32300063.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning an individual named Cornelius.

EDCS-32300065.text_edition: Tito Iulio Titi filio Nicephoro Tito Iulio  Hilaro Tito Iulio T(iti) filio Sergia Caro Petillia Volsoni filia Maxima viva fecit
EDCS-32300065.translation: Petillia Maxima, daughter of Volso, made this while alive for Titus Iulius Nicephorus, son of Titus, Titus Iulius Hilarus, and Titus Iulius Carus, son of Titus, of the Sergian tribe.
EDCS-32300065.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Petillia Maxima to three individuals, likely family members, while she was still alive, indicating it is a tomb or cenotaph set up in anticipation.

EDCS-32300066.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a dedication or epitaph, honoring Gaius Iulius and an incomplete name starting with 'Vo'.

EDCS-32300068.text_edition: Dis Manibus Rutilius Narcissus Rutilio Narcisso pRatri#patri#RATRI beGneG#bene#BGNG meGreGnti#merenti#MGRGTI posui=IVt#posuit#POSIVT plus minus qui bixxit sic annos LXV
EDCS-32300068.translation: To the Divine Manes. Rutilius Narcissus placed this for Rutilius Narcissus, his well-deserving father, who lived about 65 years.
EDCS-32300068.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Rutilius Narcissus dedicates a tombstone to his father, also named Rutilius Narcissus, recording his father's age at death.

EDCS-32300069.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Rutiliae Victoriae annorum XXVII mensium V coniugi carissimaesius Rus maritus cum qua vixi
EDCS-32300069.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Rutilia Victoria, 27 years and 5 months old, his dearest wife. Her husband [---]ius Rus, with whom he lived, [set this up].
EDCS-32300069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a husband named [---]ius Rus to his beloved wife, Rutilia Victoria, noting her age at death.

EDCS-32300070.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Rutili- F Rutili- T Rutilia F infelicissimis filiis bene merentibus qui vixerunt Rutili- F annos XXIII Rutili- T annos XXV mater infelicissima posuit filiis suis tituluam
EDCS-32300070.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Rutilia F[---] and Rutilia T[---], most unhappy sons, well-deserving, who lived Rutilia F[---] 23 years, Rutilia T[---] 25 years. The most unhappy mother placed this title for her sons.
EDCS-32300070.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from a grieving mother dedicated to her two sons, Rutilia F[---] and Rutilia T[---], who died at the ages of 23 and 25 respectively.

EDCS-32300074.text_edition: Titus Loantius Rufus praepositus Iapodum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32300074.translation: Titus Loantius Rufus, commander of the Iapodes, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32300074.summary: This is a votive inscription where Titus Loantius Rufus, identified as a commander of the Iapodes, dedicates an offering to a deity in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32300076.text_edition: Martinus qui Felicissimus s vixit menses VIII memoriam Lochu(s) fecit
EDCS-32300076.translation: Lochus made this memorial for Martinus, who lived as Felicissimus for 8 months.
EDCS-32300076.summary: This is a memorial inscription set up by Lochus for Martinus, who was also known as Felicissimus and died at a very young age of 8 months.

EDCS-32300080.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name label or a fragment of a larger text, identifying Gaius Iulius Mustela.

EDCS-32300082.translation: To Jupiter. Quintus Papinius Proculus [dedicates this].
EDCS-32300082.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Jupiter by Quintus Papinius Proculus.

EDCS-32300083.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely part of a dedication or epitaph, mentioning a name associated with 'Iulia' and an unclear abbreviation 'FR()'.

EDCS-32300084.text_edition: Ut decet parenti filius fecit suo Lucius Statius Luci filius Venetus hic situs est
EDCS-32300084.translation: As is fitting, a son made this for his father. Lucius Statius Venetus, son of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-32300084.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by a son for his father, Lucius Statius Venetus.

EDCS-32300085.text_edition: Impperatoribus Caessaribus Publio Licinio Valeriano pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate
EDCS-32300085.translation: To the Emperors Caesars Publius Licinius Valerianus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power...
EDCS-32300085.summary: This is a fragmented dedicatory inscription, likely honoring the emperor Valerian, mentioning his titles of pontifex maximus and tribunician power (253-260 AD).

EDCS-32300087.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-32300087.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-32300087.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring the emperor Diocletian (284-305 AD), listing his imperial titles.

EDCS-32400035.translation: To Volusia Tertia, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-32400035.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volusia Tertia, identified as the daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-32400038.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, containing only a few letters, including part of the cognomen 'Pius'.

EDCS-32400312.text_edition: Lucius Artorius Castus primus pilus legionis V Macedonicae praefectus legionis VI victricis
EDCS-32400312.translation: Lucius Artorius Castus, primus pilus of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, prefect of the Sixth Victorious Legion.
EDCS-32400312.summary: This inscription records the military career of Lucius Artorius Castus, detailing his service as primus pilus and legionary prefect, likely dating to the late 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32500101.text_edition: V T et V O et A P filius sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-32500101.translation: V. T. and V. O. and A. P., sons, made this for themselves and their family while living.
EDCS-32500101.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that multiple sons (represented by initials) built a tomb for themselves and their family during their lifetime.

EDCS-32500102.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Sextus Aurelius Arg sibi et Ae
EDCS-32500102.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Sextus Aurelius Arg... for himself and Ae...
EDCS-32500102.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, recording Sextus Aurelius Arg... building a tomb for himself and another individual.

EDCS-32500105.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcellina vixit annos XLV pia mater et Bonus et Urbanus Marcellinae infelicissimae et sibi ipsorum superviventibus titulum posuerunt
EDCS-32500105.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Marcellina lived 45 years, a devoted mother. Bonus and Urbanus placed this monument for the most unfortunate Marcellina and for themselves, surviving.
EDCS-32500105.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Marcellina, a devoted mother who lived 45 years, erected by Bonus and Urbanus for her and for themselves while still living.

EDCS-32500107.text_edition: Iunoni Reginae Marcus Ulpius Marcianus cum suis libens posuit
EDCS-32500107.translation: To Queen Juno, Marcus Ulpius Marcianus willingly placed this with his family.
EDCS-32500107.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by Marcus Ulpius Marcianus and his family, placed willingly.

EDCS-32500108.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo LI(ucii)#L(ucii)#I Petronii Maximinus et Severus
EDCS-32500108.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius Petronius Maximinus and Severus (dedicated this).
EDCS-32500108.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Lucius Petronius Maximinus and Severus.

EDCS-32500109.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Probo defuncto nnorum IV MAAMAo filio fecit
EDCS-32500109.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Publius Aelius Probus, who died at four years old, his mother made this for her son.
EDCS-32500109.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a mother for her son, Publius Aelius Probus, who died at the age of four.

EDCS-32500110.text_edition: Dis Manibus AelioS#Aelio#AELIS Rufo et Tattuiae defunctae nnorum LX Publius Aelius Verus parentibus posuit
EDCS-32500110.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aelius Rufus and Tattuia, who died at sixty years old, Publius Aelius Verus placed this for his parents.
EDCS-32500110.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Aelius Verus for his deceased parents, Aelius Rufus and Tattuia.

EDCS-32500111.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Aurelius Maximus et Mandeta vivi fecerunt sibi et Aureliae Macae defunctae annorum  et Maximino defuncto annorum II carissimis
EDCS-32500111.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Maximus and Mandeta, while living, made this for themselves and for Aurelia Maca, who died at [---] years old, and for Maximinus, who died at two years old, their dearest ones.
EDCS-32500111.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Aurelius Maximus and Mandeta for themselves and for their dearest deceased relatives, Aurelia Maca and Maximinus.

EDCS-32500114.text_edition: Aelia Zorada hic sita annorum LX Zanatis et Tatta matri bene merent(i)
EDCS-32500114.translation: Aelia Zorada, buried here, sixty years old. Zanatis and Tatta (made this) for their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-32500114.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Zorada, placed by Zanatis and Tatta for their mother.

EDCS-32500117.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Maximus IV- votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32500117.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32500117.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Fortuna Augusta by Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-32500120.text_edition: Caius Safius Severus aedilis IIIIvir quinquennalis patronus coloniae simulacrum ex sua pecunia posuit
EDCS-32500120.translation: Gaius Safius Severus, aedile, quattuorvir quinquennalis, patron of the colony, placed this statue from his own money.
EDCS-32500120.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a statue by Gaius Safius Severus, a prominent civic official and patron of the colony, using his own funds.

EDCS-32500121.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for a mother named Bolana.

EDCS-32500123.text_edition: Dis Manibus Epictesi coniugi bene merenti Haermes sic datus locus a Caio Cli Censorino
EDCS-32500123.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Epictesis, his well-deserving wife. Haermes (made this). This place was thus given by Gaius Cli(us) Censorinus.
EDCS-32500123.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Haermes to his well-deserving wife Epictesis, with the burial plot provided by Gaius Clius Censorinus.

EDCS-32500128.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Herculanus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae
EDCS-32500128.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Iulius Herculanus, beneficiarius consularis of the First Italic Legion.
EDCS-32500128.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Iulius Herculanus, a beneficiarius consularis of Legio I Italica.

EDCS-32500129.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo iuxtaX#iuxta#IUXTX quod diivina - ] Ava Batoniana votum solvit
EDCS-32500129.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, by divine [will?], Ava Batoniana fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-32500129.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ava Batoniana to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-32500130.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio municipii N(ovensium) Publius Satrius
EDCS-32500130.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the municipality of Novensium, Publius Satrius [dedicates this].
EDCS-32500130.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Publius Satrius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the protective spirit of the municipality of Novensium.

EDCS-32500132.text_edition: miles legionis    beneficiarius consularis  votum libens merito solvit d(ominis)? n(ostris)? Deciis Messiis consulibus
EDCS-32500132.translation: A soldier of the legion, a consular beneficiarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to our lords Decius and Messius, consuls.
EDCS-32500132.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a beneficiarius of an unspecified legion, dedicated in the consulship of Decius and Messius, likely referring to the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-32500133.text_edition: us beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae
EDCS-32500133.translation: A consular beneficiarius of the Tenth Legion Gemina.
EDCS-32500133.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription identifying an individual as a consular beneficiarius of the Legio X Gemina.

EDCS-32500134.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo L NIENA  beneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis
EDCS-32500134.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, L. Niena, a consular beneficiarius of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-32500134.summary: This inscription is a dedication by L. Niena, a consular beneficiarius of Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-32500137.text_edition: anus ex collegio saccariorum qui vixit annus sic XL menses IIII dies novem infelicissimus iubenis sic
EDCS-32500137.translation: He was from the college of porters, who lived for 40 years, 4 months, 9 days, a most unfortunate young man.
EDCS-32500137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man, a member of the college of porters, noting his age at death and expressing sorrow.

EDCS-32500138.text_edition: Anchario defuncto o suo Ancharius Abellio et Iulius Augustalis filio et Iulia Macrina coniugCi#coniugi#COIUCI posuerunt
EDCS-32500138.translation: To Ancharius, deceased. Ancharius Abellio and Iulius Augustalis placed this for their son, and Iulia Macrina for her husband.
EDCS-32500138.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased Ancharius by Ancharius Abellio and Iulius Augustalis, identified as his sons, and Iulia Macrina, identified as his wife.

EDCS-32500139.text_edition: Dis Manibus t Apolloni vixit annos XXVII infelicissimo
EDCS-32500139.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Apolloni, who lived 27 years, a most unfortunate [man].
EDCS-32500139.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed for Apolloni, noting his age at death.

EDCS-32500140.text_edition: Aurelius Titus filius patri defuncto nnorum XXV
EDCS-32500140.translation: Aurelius Titus, son, to his deceased father, 25 years old.
EDCS-32500140.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aurelius Titus for his deceased father, who was 25 years old.

EDCS-32500142.text_edition: annorum  mensiu=VIm#mensium#MENSVIM I Ianuaria mater posuit
EDCS-32500142.translation: A mother, Ianuaria, placed this for her son, who lived for [---] years and 6 months.
EDCS-32500142.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by the mother Ianuaria for her son, specifying his age at death as having lived for a certain number of years and six months.

EDCS-32500143.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Hermadio Iuliae coniugi pientissimae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32500143.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Hermadio placed this for Iulia, his most devoted and well-deserving wife.
EDCS-32500143.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Gaius Iulius Hermadio dedicating a monument to his wife, Iulia, who is praised for her devotion and merit, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32500145.text_edition: Laetae Laetillae Iulius Zoticus coniugi bene merenti defunctae annorum XV
EDCS-32500145.translation: To Laeta Laetilla. Iulius Zoticus placed this for his well-deserving wife, who died at fifteen years of age.
EDCS-32500145.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iulius Zoticus commemorates his young wife, Laeta Laetilla, who passed away at the age of fifteen, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32500146.text_edition: ]] []E [[]] [[]] merenti posu---  Larcia UrsillIa#Ursilla#URSLIA Aurelio Amantio liberto posuit qui vixit annos plus minus LX
EDCS-32500146.translation: [---] well-deserving placed [---]. Larcia Ursilla placed this for Aurelius Amantius, her freedman, who lived for approximately sixty years.
EDCS-32500146.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Larcia Ursilla to her freedman, Aurelius Amantius, who lived to about sixty years old, with some initial parts of the text missing.

EDCS-32500147.translation: To Maxima, five years old. He placed this.
EDCS-32500147.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Maxima, who lived to be five years old, with the dedicant unnamed but implied by 'he placed this'.

EDCS-32500149.text_edition: IV  coniugi infelicissimo qui vixit annos plus minus scripsit Naevius Cassius fratri misero XXXVIII
EDCS-32500149.translation: To his most unfortunate spouse, who lived for approximately [---] years. Naevius Cassius wrote this for his wretched brother, thirty-eight.
EDCS-32500149.summary: This fragmented inscription is a funerary dedication by Naevius Cassius to his 'wretched brother', who was 38 years old, and also mentions an 'unfortunate spouse' whose age is unclear, suggesting a complex family dedication.

EDCS-32500150.text_edition: IVL LXX SECV Aelia Crescentilla matri M
EDCS-32500150.translation: IUL[IUS] LXX SECV[NDUS]. Aelia Crescentilla [to her] mother M[ ---].
EDCS-32500150.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary monument, where Aelia Crescentilla dedicates something to her mother, and also mentions 'Iulius LXX Secundus', whose relationship is not fully clear.

EDCS-32500151.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titio Quintiliano infanti infelicissimo qui vixit annos V pater infelicissimus posuit
EDCS-32500151.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Titius Quintilianus, a most unfortunate infant, who lived for five years. His most unfortunate father placed this.
EDCS-32500151.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a deeply sorrowful father dedicates a monument to his young son, Titius Quintilianus, who tragically died at the age of five.

EDCS-32500152.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vendimiae sic defunctae annorum VIII eques Romanus filiae posuit
EDCS-32500152.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Vendimia, who thus died at eight years of age. A Roman knight placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-32500152.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a Roman knight dedicates a monument to his young daughter, Vendimia, who passed away at the age of eight.

EDCS-32500153.text_edition: Dis Manibus Viscio Victori  Dis Manibus C
EDCS-32500153.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Viscius Victor. To the Divine Spirits. C[---].
EDCS-32500153.summary: This appears to be a double or possibly reused funerary inscription, with one dedication to Viscius Victor and another fragmented one to an individual whose name starts with 'C', both addressed to the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-32500154.text_edition: vixit annos l Ursu coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-32500154.translation: He lived for fifty years. Ursu[s] placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-32500154.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Ursus dedicates a monument to his well-deserving wife, who lived for fifty years, with her name unfortunately missing from the text.

EDCS-32500156.text_edition: mus tribunus cohortis eiusdem praefectus cohortis I Flaviae Hispanorum
EDCS-32500156.translation: [---]mus, tribune of the same cohort, prefect of the First Flavian Cohort of Spaniards.
EDCS-32500156.summary: This is an excerpt from a military inscription, likely a career epitaph, detailing the commands held by an individual, including a tribunate and a prefecture of an auxiliary cohort, probably dating from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32700227.text_edition: ius Publi libertus Phileros sevir  libertus Himeros sevir ---? aedem deae Barbaricae sua pecunia faciendum coeraverunt idemque dedicaverunt probaveruntque
EDCS-32700227.translation: Publius' freedman Phileros, a sevir, [and ---]'s freedman Himeros, a sevir, supervised the construction of a temple to the goddess Barbarica with their own money, and they also dedicated and approved it.
EDCS-32700227.summary: This inscription records the dedication and financing of a temple to the goddess Barbarica by two freedmen, Phileros and Himeros, both of whom held the municipal office of sevir, indicating a probable date in the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700229.translation: Sempronius Marcellinus, in accordance with a vow, to Diana.
EDCS-32700229.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by Sempronius Marcellinus, fulfilling a vow, likely on a small altar or monument from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700230.text_edition: Fortunae Augustae sacrum Papinius Maximianus Fortunae ex viso posuit
EDCS-32700230.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta. Papinius Maximianus placed this to Fortuna, in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-32700230.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna Augusta by Papinius Maximianus, made in response to a divine vision, probably on an altar or stele from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700237.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Septumius sic Secundus
EDCS-32700237.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Septumius Secundus.
EDCS-32700237.summary: This is a simple votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Septumius Secundus, likely on an altar, probably dating to the Imperial era.

EDCS-32700238.text_edition: Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Iulia Auli liberta Maxima ex viso posuit
EDCS-32700238.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus. Julia Maxima, freedwoman of Aulus, placed this in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-32700238.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater Augustus by Julia Maxima, a freedwoman of Aulus, in response to a divine vision, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700242.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Quintus Cloelius Helius votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32700242.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Quintus Cloelius Helius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32700242.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Quintus Cloelius Helius, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow, likely on an altar or shrine from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700243.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Lupus Marciani ex visu sic posuit
EDCS-32700243.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Lupus Marciani placed this in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-32700243.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Lupus Marciani, placed in response to a divine vision, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700244.summary: This is a fragment of a votive inscription where an individual whose name ends in '-dion' willingly fulfilled a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700248.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning an Augustus and a consul, likely indicating a date by consular year or a dedication to an emperor, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700253.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luci Acci Zosimi nnorum XIII mensium et Aeli Hermaisci annorum XXXV mensium  Aelius Augusti libertus I provincia Dalmatia Accia Amabilis  si
EDCS-32700253.translation: To the Divine Manes of Lucius Accius Zosimus, 13 years and [X] months old, and Aelius Hermaiscus, 35 years and [X] months old. Aelius, freedman of Augustus, of the first province of Dalmatia, and Accia Amabilis [made this].
EDCS-32700253.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a shared tombstone or memorial, dedicated by Aelius, an imperial freedman, and Accia Amabilis to Lucius Accius Zosimus and Aelius Hermaiscus.

EDCS-32700255.text_edition: Aurelius s duplicarius legionis VIII piae fidelis
EDCS-32700255.translation: Aurelius, 'duplicarius' of the Eighth Legion, 'Pia Fidelis'.
EDCS-32700255.summary: This is a military inscription, recording Aurelius's name and rank as a 'duplicarius' (a soldier receiving double pay) in the 8th Legion, 'Pia Fidelis' (Pious and Faithful).

EDCS-32700262.text_edition: Pompeius Titullus eques ? domo Dertona testamento fieri iussit ae Secundae uxoris Cordiae Cae uxori Secun[d&
EDCS-32700262.translation: Pompeius Titullus, a knight from Dertona, ordered this to be made by his will for Aea Secunda, his wife, and Cordia Cae[lia?], his wife, Secun[da?].
EDCS-32700262.summary: This is a testamentary funerary inscription, where Pompeius Titullus, a knight, specified in his will that this monument be erected for his wives, Aea Secunda and Cordia Cae[lia?].

EDCS-32700264.text_edition: Dis Manibus Surus Victoris miles cohortis I miliariae Aureliae Terentiae hospitae nnorum XXXVII bene merenti posuit titulum
EDCS-32700264.translation: To the Divine Manes. Surus Victor, a soldier of the First Milliarial Aurelian Cohort, placed this tablet for Aurelia Terentia, his hostess, a well-deserving woman, 37 years old.
EDCS-32700264.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Surus Victor, a soldier, for Aurelia Terentia, his hostess, indicating a personal relationship and her commendable character.

EDCS-32700266.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Vettio Probo beneficiario consularis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis Lucius Vettius Gratus et Vettia Ianuaria patri bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-32700266.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Vettius Gratus and Vettia Ianuaria placed this for their well-deserving father, Lucius Vettius Probus, 'beneficiarius consularis' of the Eleventh Legion, Claudia Pia Fidelis.
EDCS-32700266.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Vettius Gratus and Vettia Ianuaria to their father, Lucius Vettius Probus, who served as a 'beneficiarius consularis' in the 11th Legion, 'Claudia Pia Fidelis'.

EDCS-32700268.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment with non-Latin or very obscure Latin, possibly recording names or a dedication in a local language or dialect.

EDCS-32700275.text_edition: Lucio Anicio Cai filio Paetinati IIIIviro iure dicundo praefecto fabrum IIIIviro iure dicundo quinquennali decurionum decreto publice bene merito
EDCS-32700275.translation: To Lucius Anicius, son of Gaius, Paetinatus, 'quattuorvir iure dicundo', 'praefectus fabrum', 'quattuorvir iure dicundo quinquennalis', publicly well-deserving by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-32700275.summary: This is an honorary inscription, likely a statue base, dedicated to Lucius Anicius Paetinatus, detailing his distinguished civic career and public service as recognized by the decurions.

EDCS-32700277.text_edition: Fabiano viro egregio decurioni coloniae Salonitanorum sacerdoti qui vixit annis
EDCS-32700277.translation: [To] Fabianus, eminent man, decurion of the colony of Salona, priest, who lived for [X] years.
EDCS-32700277.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabianus, an esteemed citizen who held the offices of decurion in the colony of Salona and priest.

EDCS-32700278.text_edition: Quinto Maesio Ursulo VIviro Augustali defuncto nnorum XXX die natalis Aurelia Quinta coniugi inconparabili sic posuit
EDCS-32700278.translation: To Quintus Maesius Ursulus, 'sevir Augustalis', deceased at 30 years old on his birthday. Aurelia Quinta thus placed this for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-32700278.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Quinta to her husband, Quintus Maesius Ursulus, a 'sevir Augustalis', who died on his 30th birthday.

EDCS-32700280.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary or honorary, mentioning a 'sevir' (a member of a college of six) and a relative, possibly a nephew.

EDCS-32700284.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aurelius [---].
EDCS-32700284.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius, likely by a relative or associate.

EDCS-32700288.text_edition: Dis Manibus Silvestro homini probissimo peregrino huius loci Sextinus conservuOs#conservus#CONSERVOS posuit
EDCS-32700288.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sextinus, a fellow slave, set this up for Silvester, a most upright man and a stranger in this place.
EDCS-32700288.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Sextinus, a fellow slave, for Silvester, a deceased peregrinus (foreigner or non-citizen) recognized for his integrity.

EDCS-32700289.text_edition: Dis Manibus  magister Threptioni discenti suo bene merenti
EDCS-32700289.translation: To the Divine Shades. A master for his deserving student, Threption.
EDCS-32700289.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a master in honor of his student, Threption, who had served him well.

EDCS-32700291.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Fortunatus Flaviae Tertiae fecit bene merenti
EDCS-32700291.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Fortunatus made this for Flavia Tertia, who well deserved it.
EDCS-32700291.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelius Fortunatus to Flavia Tertia, indicating a close relationship such as spouse or parent, and acknowledging her merit.

EDCS-32700292.text_edition: Gaio A T Caius P S filius patri suo merenti
EDCS-32700292.translation: To Gaius A. T. Gaius P. S., his son, to his deserving father.
EDCS-32700292.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a son, Gaius P. S., for his deserving father, Gaius A. T.

EDCS-32700295.text_edition: Dis Manibus AeFliae#Aeliae#AFLIAE Florae alumnae Titus AelI(ius)#Ael(ius)#AEI Florus bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700295.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Aelius Florus set this up for Aelia Flora, his alumna, who well deserved it.
EDCS-32700295.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Aelius Florus to Aelia Flora, his alumna (a former foster-child or pupil), acknowledging her merit.

EDCS-32700296.translation: To her husband, by Aelia Maxima.
EDCS-32700296.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aelia Maxima for her deceased husband.

EDCS-32700298.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely erected by Aphrodisia for her husband.

EDCS-32700299.text_edition: $ Flor]iniae Aphrodite Florinia Florentina patronae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700299.translation: Florinia Florentina set this up for her patroness, Florinia Aphrodite, who well deserved it.
EDCS-32700299.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Florinia Florentina to her patroness, Florinia Aphrodite, acknowledging her worth.

EDCS-32700300.summary: This is an extremely brief inscription, likely a dedication to or identification of someone named Apronius.

EDCS-32700301.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucius Armenius CresceIns#Crescens#CRESCIS Aureliae Marcianini conugi suae bene merenti posuit defuncta est annorum XVI mensium VIII
EDCS-32700301.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Armenius Crescens placed this for Aurelia Marcianini, his well-deserving wife. She died at 16 years and 8 months of age.
EDCS-32700301.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Armenius Crescens to his deceased wife, Aurelia Marcianini, who died young.

EDCS-32700303.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Attia Pardalis. Herma.
EDCS-32700303.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Attia Pardalis, likely erected by Herma, possibly a relative or freedman.

EDCS-32700306.text_edition: Aureli Apolloni--- Cai libert-- OP SC
EDCS-32700306.translation: Aurelius Apolloni[us]... freedman of Gaius... OP SC.
EDCS-32700306.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aurelius Apollonius, a freedman of Gaius, with an unclear purpose due to its incomplete nature.

EDCS-32700307.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Communi defuncto annorum LX Cassia GAMIO et coniugi
EDCS-32700307.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Communis, who died at 60 years of age. Cassia Gamio and to her husband.
EDCS-32700307.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Communis, who died at 60, by Cassia Gamio and her husband, possibly the deceased himself or her living husband dedicating it to him.

EDCS-32700308.text_edition: Aurelius Lucius delicato suo defuncto dier(um) XXV infanti? --- posuit
EDCS-32700308.translation: Aurelius Lucius placed this for his darling, who died at 25 days old, an infant...
EDCS-32700308.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelius Lucius for his beloved infant, who died very young.

EDCS-32700310.text_edition: Aurelia Athenodora Clementiano marito suo et sibi posuit
EDCS-32700310.translation: Aurelia Athenodora placed this for Clementianus, her husband, and for herself.
EDCS-32700310.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Athenodora for her husband, Clementianus, and prepared for her own future use, likely a joint tombstone.

EDCS-32700314.text_edition: Dis Manibus Birriae Primae quae et
EDCS-32700314.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Birria Prima, who also...
EDCS-32700314.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Birria Prima, whose full story is lost.

EDCS-32700316.text_edition: annos XXI Baebius Caecus coniugi inconparabili sic
EDCS-32700316.translation: ...years old. Baebius Caecus to his incomparable wife, thus...
EDCS-32700316.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription from Baebius Caecus to his wife, praising her as incomparable.

EDCS-32700317.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription dedicated to Gaius Caesius, with further details lost.

EDCS-32700320.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio nnio Maximo Caetennia Aphrodisia mater
EDCS-32700320.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Publius Nnius Maximus. Caetennia Aphrodisia, his mother.
EDCS-32700320.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caetennia Aphrodisia to her son, Publius Nnius Maximus.

EDCS-32700321.text_edition: defuncto annorum  Publius Caetennius Onesimus patronus alumno posuit
EDCS-32700321.translation: To the deceased, of [---] years. Publius Caetennius Onesimus, his patron, erected this for his foster-child.
EDCS-32700321.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Caetennius Onesimus, a patron, for his deceased foster-child (alumnus).

EDCS-32700322.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Caltilia Hilarilla, my wife.
EDCS-32700322.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Caltilia Hilarilla, erected by her spouse.

EDCS-32700323.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Cassiano  nnos XVII Cassia Gemina
EDCS-32700323.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Cassianus, [---] years old. Cassia Gemina [erected this].
EDCS-32700323.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits for Cassianus, aged 17, set up by Cassia Gemina.

EDCS-32700324.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cassius Aelius Marciae Aureliae def(unctae) coniugi annorum XXVI bene merenti
EDCS-32700324.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Cassius Aelius [erected this] for Marcia Aurelia, his deceased wife of 26 years, who deserved well.
EDCS-32700324.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Spirits by Cassius Aelius for his deceased wife, Marcia Aurelia, who lived 26 years.

EDCS-32700326.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Claudio o defuncto Lucius Taro[nius &
EDCS-32700326.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For the deceased Claudius [---]. Lucius Taro[nius ---].
EDCS-32700326.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Divine Spirits for the deceased Claudius, dedicated by Lucius Taronius.

EDCS-32700330.text_edition: RIS Chresimus defunctus nnorum   super et  N
EDCS-32700330.translation: [To the Divine Spiri]ts. Chresimus, deceased, of [---] years, [---] above and [---].
EDCS-32700330.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely dedicated to the Divine Spirits, for the deceased Chresimus.

EDCS-32700331.text_edition: Crispinus? --- in fronte pedes XII in [agro pedes &
EDCS-32700331.translation: Crispinus? [---] in front, 12 feet; in the field, [---] feet.
EDCS-32700331.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or property description, possibly involving Crispinus, specifying dimensions in feet.

EDCS-32700332.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Curtius Nepos.
EDCS-32700332.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits for Curtius Nepos.

EDCS-32700333.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cusiae Sperantiae infanti defunctae annorum  mensium II Rufinus
EDCS-32700333.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Cusia Sperantia, a deceased infant of [---] years, 2 months. Rufinus [erected this].
EDCS-32700333.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits by Rufinus for Cusia Sperantia, a deceased infant who lived 2 months.

EDCS-32700334.text_edition: Dasantillae quae vixit nnos plus minus XXX quae bVene#bene#VENE laboravit posui
EDCS-32700334.translation: I erected this for Dasantilla, who lived more or less 30 years, and worked very well.
EDCS-32700334.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by an unnamed individual for Dasantilla, who lived approximately 30 years and was praised for her diligent work.

EDCS-32700336.text_edition: Dis Manibus Democrito Fa VMETVM IN
EDCS-32700336.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Democritus [---].
EDCS-32700336.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Democritus, likely placed on a tomb or funerary monument.

EDCS-32700337.text_edition: Dionysiae Avidi Spendontis Lucius Atatienus Secundus
EDCS-32700337.translation: To Dionysia, daughter of Avidius Spendo, Lucius Atatienus Secundus [dedicates this].
EDCS-32700337.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Lucius Atatienus Secundus to Dionysia, daughter of Avidius Spendo, likely on a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-32700338.text_edition: IT Marco Domitio Saturnino in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XX
EDCS-32700338.translation: [---] to Marcus Domitius Saturninus, with a frontage of 15 feet and a depth of 20 feet.
EDCS-32700338.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot or monument for Marcus Domitius Saturninus, indicating the legal boundaries of the property.

EDCS-32700339.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Domitia Fortunata matri piissimae inconparabili sic quae vixit annos plus minus
EDCS-32700339.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Domitia Fortunata [dedicates this] to her most devoted and incomparable mother, who lived approximately [---] years.
EDCS-32700339.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Domitia Fortunata to her exceptionally devoted mother, detailing her age at death, likely for a tombstone.

EDCS-32700340.text_edition: filius Lo  Tertullam Lucius Dr SI ICD
EDCS-32700340.translation: Her son Lucius Dr[---] [dedicates this] to Tertulla.
EDCS-32700340.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a son, Lucius Dr[---], to Tertulla, likely a mother or other female relative, suggesting a funerary context.

EDCS-32700341.text_edition: Lucius Egnatius Luci libertus Maximus vivus fecit sibi et Egnatis Amyco patri eidem patrono Myrinae matri Liberali sorori Lucensi fratri et Iuliae Proculae uxori suis in fronte pedes XXXX in agro pedes XXX hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-32700341.translation: Lucius Egnatius Maximus, freedman of Lucius, made this for himself while alive, and for the Egnatii Amycus his father and also patron, Myrina his mother, Liberalis his sister, Lucens his brother, and his wife Iulia Procula. The frontage is 40 feet, the depth is 30 feet. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-32700341.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Egnatius Maximus during his lifetime for himself and his family members, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot and declaring that the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-32700343.text_edition: Eutychia Victorina uxor coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-32700343.translation: Eutychia Victorina, wife, to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-32700343.summary: This is a dedication by Eutychia Victorina to her deceased husband, indicating a funerary monument erected in his honor.

EDCS-32700344.translation: [To her] husband and to Faberia [---] her sister.
EDCS-32700344.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a husband and a sister named Faberia, likely placed on a shared funerary monument by a woman.

EDCS-32700345.text_edition: Lucio Fabio Surai sic Fausta liberta
EDCS-32700345.translation: To Lucius Fabius Sura, Fausta his freedwoman [dedicates this].
EDCS-32700345.summary: This is a dedication by Fausta, a freedwoman, to Lucius Fabius Sura, likely commemorating her former master on a tomb or monument.

EDCS-32700346.text_edition: Dis Manibus  ae defunctae mensium dierum XII A Nymfae sic Felici filiae bene merenti
EDCS-32700346.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, to [---], deceased, [who lived] 12 days of [---] months. To Nymphae Felix, her well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-32700346.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased individual who lived only 12 days, and also mentions Nymphae Felix as her well-deserving daughter, suggesting a tombstone for an infant with a dedication from the mother.

EDCS-32700347.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavio Agathoni filio piissimo Sextus pater
EDCS-32700347.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Agatho, a most dutiful son. Sextus, his father [set this up].
EDCS-32700347.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a father, Sextus, to his deceased son, Flavius Agatho.

EDCS-32700348.text_edition: Flavia   Flavio Alexandro bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700348.translation: Flavia [---] set this up for Flavius Alexander, who deserved well.
EDCS-32700348.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Flavia for Flavius Alexander, likely her husband or a close relative, honoring him for his good deeds.

EDCS-32700349.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning parts of names like Gem[---] and Prima.

EDCS-32700353.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, with mention of the name 'Iulius' or 'Iulia' and a 'son'.

EDCS-32700357.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iulio Crescenti qui et Asifa Servilia Prima coniugi bene merenti bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700357.translation: To the Divine Manes. Servilia Prima set this up for Iulius Crescens, who was also called Asifa, her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-32700357.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Servilia Prima to her husband, Iulius Crescens, also known as Asifa, who deserved well.

EDCS-32700358.text_edition: Caius Iulius  Epicadi filius vivi fecerunt coniugi et Saturnino  Caio Iulio Plotino      hoc monumentum in fronte pedes
EDCS-32700358.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Epicadi, while alive, made this for his wife and for Saturninus [and] for Gaius Iulius Plotinus [---]. This monument extends for [---] feet in front.
EDCS-32700358.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Gaius Iulius, son of Epicadi, while he was alive, for his wife, Saturninus, and Gaius Iulius Plotinus, indicating a family tomb with specific dimensions for the frontage.

EDCS-32700359.text_edition: Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Felici Quintius Sabianus fratri bene merenti
EDCS-32700359.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintius Sabianus [set this up] for his well-deserving brother, Gaius Iulius Felix.
EDCS-32700359.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintius Sabianus to his brother, Gaius Iulius Felix.

EDCS-32700360.text_edition: Iulio Ursino infanti infelicissimo qui vixit
EDCS-32700360.translation: To Iulius Ursinus, a most unfortunate infant, who lived [---].
EDCS-32700360.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Ursinus, a young child who died prematurely.

EDCS-32700361.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning parts of names like Iulia and Iu[---].

EDCS-32700364.text_edition: Dis Manibus Iuliae Felicitati defunctae nnorum plus minus XX Vitalio uxori infeFlicissimae#infelicissimae#INFFLICISSIMAE quae vixit mecum annis IIII quodT#quod#QOT vivae melius destinavi mortuae tamen  bene merenti posui
EDCS-32700364.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Iulia Felicitas, deceased, aged approximately 20 years. I, Vitalio, set this up for my most unfortunate wife, who lived with me for four years, because what I had intended better for her while she was alive, I have now, nevertheless, placed for her, though she is dead, because she deserved well.
EDCS-32700364.summary: This is a poignant funerary inscription from Vitalio to his wife, Iulia Felicitas, who died at about 20 years old after four years of marriage, expressing his sorrow and her deserving nature.

EDCS-32700365.text_edition: Iuliae Palaestrice nnum V Eleutheris mater
EDCS-32700365.translation: To Iulia Palaestrice, five years old. Eleutheris, her mother.
EDCS-32700365.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Eleutheris, dedicating a tombstone to her five-year-old daughter, Iulia Palaestrice.

EDCS-32700367.text_edition: ilae Iulia Zosime  patronae bene merenti
EDCS-32700367.translation: Iulia Zosime to her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-32700367.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Iulia Zosime to her patron, commemorating their benefactor-client relationship.

EDCS-32700368.text_edition: Iulia Ursa viva fecit sibi et Lucio Iulio EFuaristo#Euaristo#FUARISTO patrono bene merenti Ceriali filio annorum XXIII et suis in fronte pedes XV in agCro#agro#ACR pedes XX hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur
EDCS-32700368.translation: Iulia Ursa made this while living for herself and for Lucius Iulius Euaristus, her well-deserving patron, and for her son Cerialis, 23 years old, and for her family. In front, 15 feet; in depth, 20 feet. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-32700368.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Iulia Ursa during her lifetime for herself, her patron Lucius Iulius Euaristus, her son Cerialis, and her family, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot and declaring it inalienable by inheritance.

EDCS-32700370.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely part of a larger monument, with insufficient information to determine its full purpose or date.

EDCS-32700371.translation: BR and C. Licinius and other friends.
EDCS-32700371.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication, set up by C. Licinius and other friends.

EDCS-32700375.text_edition: Quinto Lucio Democrito defuncto annorum LXXIIII Quintus Lucius Democritus patri optimo et Ravonia Marronia coniugi incomparabili
EDCS-32700375.translation: To Quintus Lucius Democritus, deceased, 74 years old. Quintus Lucius Democritus to his excellent father and Ravonia Marronia to her incomparable husband.
EDCS-32700375.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a son, Quintus Lucius Democritus, dedicates a memorial to his father, also Quintus Lucius Democritus, and Ravonia Marronia dedicates one to her husband, the same individual, who died at 74.

EDCS-32700376.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luria Bictoria sic quae nata XXVI item filius Marinus qui natus IIII posibit sic coniug(i) Aurelius Dioclis sic
EDCS-32700376.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Luria Bictoria, who was born on the 26th, and her son Marinus, who was born on the 4th. Aurelius Dioclis placed this for his wife.
EDCS-32700376.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, commemorating Luria Bictoria and her son Marinus, placed by Aurelius Dioclis for his wife.

EDCS-32700378.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary monument, mentioning a name, Marcellinus, and an abbreviation, 'CAP', of uncertain meaning.

EDCS-32700380.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Naevius Tertullinus Larcia Ursilla filio suo Publio Domitio
EDCS-32700380.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Naevius Tertullinus and Larcia Ursilla to their son Publius Domitius.
EDCS-32700380.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, erected by Naevius Tertullinus and Larcia Ursilla for their son, Publius Domitius.

EDCS-32700387.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Papirio Suro defuncto nnorum LXV Primitiva uxor et Prima filia bene merenti
EDCS-32700387.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Publius Papirius Surus, deceased, 65 years old. Primitiva, his wife, and Prima, his daughter, to their well-deserving loved one.
EDCS-32700387.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, erected by Primitiva, his wife, and Prima, his daughter, for Publius Papirius Surus, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-32700389.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pardalae nnorum XIII mensium V Flavius verna et Domitia Primitiva parentes posuerunt
EDCS-32700389.translation: To the Divine Shades of Pardala, 13 years and 5 months old. Flavius Verna and Domitia Primitiva, her parents, set this up.
EDCS-32700389.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by parents, Flavius Verna and Domitia Primitiva, for their deceased daughter, Pardala, who died young.

EDCS-32700390.text_edition: innocus Peregrinus Quintus Vedius Restitutus Dasius ria iuvenisL#iuvenis#IUVENIL ANA R FrPont#Front#FPONT Severa a Blaste Primigenia CV  Aurelio AgathorPo#Agathoro#AGATHOPO
EDCS-32700390.translation: Innocent Peregrinus, Quintus Vedius Restitutus Dasius. [---] youth. Iuvenil Ana R FrPont Severa, a Blaste, Primigenia CV. Aurelius Agathor.
EDCS-32700390.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly for multiple individuals, including a youth and Aurelius Agathor, dating is uncertain but likely Imperial Roman.

EDCS-32700392.text_edition: Petronio Polycarpo Petronia Cervia bene m(erenti) p(osuit)
EDCS-32700392.translation: To Petronius Polycarpus, Petronia Cervia set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-32700392.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Petronia Cervia for her husband, Petronius Polycarpus, whom she describes as well-deserving.

EDCS-32700393.translation: Polytimus, a friend, for his well-deserving [friend].
EDCS-32700393.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Polytimus for a well-deserving friend, likely commemorating their bond.

EDCS-32700394.text_edition: Pomponia Vera testamento fieri iussit sibi et libertis libertabusque suis ex HS XXviginti milibus curantibus heredibus in quo opere adiecerunt heredes HS IIIIquattuor milia
EDCS-32700394.translation: Pomponia Vera ordered this to be made by her will for herself and for her freedmen and freedwomen, from twenty thousand sesterces, with her heirs supervising the work, to which the heirs added four thousand sesterces.
EDCS-32700394.summary: This inscription records the testamentary arrangements of Pomponia Vera for a monument for herself and her freedpeople, funded by her legacy and an additional contribution from her heirs.

EDCS-32700395.text_edition: Vi Prima Montano fratri defuncto annorum XVI bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700395.translation: Vi Prima set this up for her well-deserving brother, Montanus, who died at 16 years of age.
EDCS-32700395.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Vi Prima for her deceased brother, Montanus, who passed away at a young age.

EDCS-32700397.translation: Primitivus set this up for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-32700397.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Primitivus for his well-deserving spouse, expressing his devotion.

EDCS-32700399.text_edition: Dis Manibus Priva defunct- annorum VI HE T F
EDCS-32700399.translation: To the Divine Shades of Priva, deceased at 6 years old. H. E. T. F.
EDCS-32700399.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Priva, a child who died at the age of six, with standard funerary abbreviations at the end.

EDCS-32700400.text_edition: RIIO Publio Publ Aemilia be[ne merenti &
EDCS-32700400.translation: RIIO Publius. Aemilia, for her well-deserving [relative].
EDCS-32700400.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely set up by Aemilia for a well-deserving Publius or other relative.

EDCS-32700401.translation: To the Divine Shades of Publicius.
EDCS-32700401.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Publicius, invoking the Divine Shades.

EDCS-32700403.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man named Quartfrater, set up by someone whose name begins with AF.

EDCS-32700405.text_edition: Quinto iuvBeni#iuveni#IUBENI inMfelicissimo#infelicissimo#IMFELICISSIMO defuncto annorum XXI Titus Aelius Philus
EDCS-32700405.translation: To Quintus, a most unfortunate young man, who died at the age of 21. Titus Aelius Philus [set this up].
EDCS-32700405.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young man named Quintus, erected by Titus Aelius Philus.

EDCS-32700407.text_edition: Rufio Saturnini vernae hic sito VO
EDCS-32700407.translation: To Rufius, a home-born slave of Saturninus, buried here. VO.
EDCS-32700407.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufius, a slave of Saturninus, who was born in his master's household.

EDCS-32700408.text_edition: $ Satur]nino annorum mensium VI dierum XV Titus Valerius Iulianus ITSAI
EDCS-32700408.translation: To Saturninus, 6 months and 15 days old. Titus Valerius Iulianus [set this up]. ITSAI.
EDCS-32700408.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Saturninus, set up by Titus Valerius Iulianus.

EDCS-32700410.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Saufeius, likely in the context of a family dedication, possibly a father.

EDCS-32700413.text_edition: EP IOS  Secundi to marito um
EDCS-32700413.translation: EP IOS, to Secundus, her husband.
EDCS-32700413.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a husband named Secundus.

EDCS-32700416.text_edition: Quinto Septimio Antonio defuncto nnorum XVIII mensium V dierum II et Quinto Septimio Antonino defuncto nnorum XXII mensium XI dierum XXIII ego Antoninus umbra tenus have tibi dico meator quamvis festinanti gradu carpas iter resiste paulum et lege casum sic ego VAMMAFIretinere cupisco spiritum MCMreliquit malignusque casus MA corpori toto post cuius exitiumque funeris terrae datas decubui prope POI noctesque diesque iam trahor in tenebras ducorque amplectere fratrem hoc fatali casu datum est pergam nunc Tartara divo reliquiasque meas curentC orbati parentes iam casum cognosti meum feliciter perge viator decepti senes testemur funera natuum et pietas titulo deplorat fata parentum
EDCS-32700416.translation: To Quintus Septimius Antonius, deceased at 18 years, 5 months, 2 days, and to Quintus Septimius Antoninus, deceased at 22 years, 11 months, 23 days. I, Antoninus, say 'farewell' to you, wanderer, just a shadow. However quickly you travel, stop a moment and read my fate. Thus I longed to retain my spirit, but cruel fate abandoned my entire body. After his death and burial, I lay near. Night and day I am now dragged into darkness and led away. Embrace your brother, this was given by a fateful chance. I will now go to Tartarus, and let my bereaved parents care for my remains. Now you have learned my fate, traveler, go forth happily. We, deceived old parents, attest to the funerals of our sons, and parental piety mourns the fates in this title.
EDCS-32700416.summary: This is a lengthy, highly emotional funerary inscription by grieving parents, Antoninus and his wife, mourning the deaths of their two sons, Quintus Septimius Antonius and Quintus Septimius Antoninus, expressing profound sorrow and addressing the passing traveler.

EDCS-32700418.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Septimiae Pusinnae defunctae nnorum XXX et Septimiae Hilarae filiae nnorum X Aurelia Marcia soror bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700418.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Septimia Pusinna, deceased at 30 years, and to Septimia Hilara, her daughter, deceased at 10 years. Aurelia Marcia, her sister, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-32700418.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Septimia Pusinna and her daughter Septimia Hilara, erected by Septimia Pusinna's sister, Aurelia Marcia.

EDCS-32700420.text_edition: Severus et A Severina  ae Severae matri bene merenti
EDCS-32700420.translation: Severus and Severina, to Severa, their well-deserving mother.
EDCS-32700420.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Severus and Severina to their mother, Severa.

EDCS-32700421.text_edition: Statia Donata enio oto filio defuncto anorum XVI
EDCS-32700421.translation: Statia Donata to Enius Oto, her son, deceased at 16 years.
EDCS-32700421.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Statia Donata for her son, Enius Oto, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-32700422.text_edition: Dis Manibus Stefano infelicissimo nnorum XIII
EDCS-32700422.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Stefano, most unfortunate, 13 years old.
EDCS-32700422.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Stefano, a child who died at the age of 13, likely placed by his family or guardians.

EDCS-32700426.text_edition: Dis Manibus Terentiae Syntrophe defunctae nnorum XIII Apuleia Primitiva mater filiae carissimae
EDCS-32700426.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Terentia Syntrophe, who died at 13 years old. Apuleia Primitiva, mother, to her dearest daughter.
EDCS-32700426.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Apuleia Primitiva to her beloved daughter, Terentia Syntrophe, who passed away at the age of 13.

EDCS-32700428.text_edition: Titiae Firminae coniugi Statius Faustinus maritus
EDCS-32700428.translation: To Titia Firmina, his wife. Statius Faustinus, her husband.
EDCS-32700428.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from Statius Faustinus to his wife, Titia Firmina.

EDCS-32700430.text_edition: Dis Manibus Tulliae Veneriae coniugi incomparabili quae vicxit sic nnos mecum XXIII menses VIII dies XXI Titus Aurelius UrsinusY#Ursinus#URSINUY  et sibi posuit
EDCS-32700430.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Tullia Veneria, an incomparable wife, who lived with me for 23 years, 8 months, 21 days. Titus Aurelius Ursinus placed this for her and for himself.
EDCS-32700430.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Aurelius Ursinus for his incomparable wife, Tullia Veneria, noting their time together, and also for himself.

EDCS-32700431.text_edition: Dis Manibus Luci Tulli Veneriani vixit annos V menses VIIII dies XXV So
EDCS-32700431.translation: To the Divine Shades. Of Lucius Tullius Venerianus, who lived 5 years, 9 months, 25 days.
EDCS-32700431.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Tullius Venerianus, a child who lived for a little over five years.

EDCS-32700434.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Calus Valerius, possibly indicating a date or a specific context of offering with 'lacio XX' being unclear in its full meaning without further context.

EDCS-32700435.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valerio Mariniano Valeria Firmina fratri
EDCS-32700435.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Valerius Marinianus. Valeria Firmina to her brother.
EDCS-32700435.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Valeria Firmina to her brother, Valerius Marinianus.

EDCS-32700436.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Valerio Marci filio Phocati Aurelia Fortunata coniugi bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700436.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Valerius Phocatus, son of Marcus. Aurelia Fortunata placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-32700436.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aurelia Fortunata for her deserving husband, Marcus Valerius Phocatus.

EDCS-32700438.text_edition: $ One]sima Valeria Onesimini filiae defunctae annorum V
EDCS-32700438.translation: Onesima Valeria, daughter of Onesimus, who died at 5 years old.
EDCS-32700438.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Onesima Valeria, a young girl who died at the age of five.

EDCS-32700439.text_edition: Valeriae Quintianae defEunctae#defunctae#DEEUNCTAE nnorum III meFnsium#mensium#MFNS X dierum XX nepoti Rutilius Marinianus MA Valeria Felicula avia infanti infelicissimaeF#infelicissimae#INFELICISSIMAF
EDCS-32700439.translation: To Valeria Quintiana, who died at 3 years, 10 months, 20 days. To their granddaughter. Rutilius Marinianus and Valeria Felicula, her grandmother, to their most unfortunate infant.
EDCS-32700439.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Rutilius Marinianus and Valeria Felicula (the grandmother) to their most unfortunate infant granddaughter, Valeria Quintiana, who died at just over three years old.

EDCS-32700440.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae SeveriEnae#Severinae#SEVERENAE defunctaVe#defunctae#DEFUNCTVE annorum XXIIII filiae infelicissimae Calpurnia Marcellina mater cum suis
EDCS-32700440.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Valeria Severina, who died at 24 years of age, her most unfortunate daughter, Calpurnia Marcellina, her mother, with her own family, [set this up].
EDCS-32700440.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Calpurnia Marcellina, a mother, to her deceased daughter Valeria Severina, who died at the age of 24.

EDCS-32700441.text_edition: Dis Manibus Valeriae Victorinae Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus coniugi de se bene merenti
EDCS-32700441.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Valeria Victorina, Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus [set this up] for his wife, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-32700441.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus to his wife, Valeria Victorina, indicating her merit towards him.

EDCS-32700443.text_edition: ano defuncto annorum XI mensium II dierum X Vetidia Secunda mater
EDCS-32700443.translation: [To the Divine Manes. To a child] who died at 11 years, 2 months, 10 days, Vetidia Secunda, the mother, [set this up].
EDCS-32700443.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Vetidia Secunda, a mother, for her child who died at a very young age.

EDCS-32700444.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription that includes a dedication to Vibia Syntyche.

EDCS-32700445.text_edition: Vibiano Tertio Auli filio Gelasio VLV
EDCS-32700445.translation: To Vibianus Tertius, son of Aulus, Gelasius [set this up]. VLV.
EDCS-32700445.summary: This is a dedication made by Gelasius to Vibianus Tertius, son of Aulus.

EDCS-32700449.text_edition: Dis Manibus Quinto Volusio Respecto Vo
EDCS-32700449.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Quintus Volusius Respectus...
EDCS-32700449.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Quintus Volusius Respectus.

EDCS-32700451.translation: Ursa set this up for herself.
EDCS-32700451.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Ursa for her own burial.

EDCS-32700453.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a maker's mark or dedication, indicating someone named ending in '-enicus' created it.

EDCS-32700455.text_edition: AN lia cundi a Nomedito bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700455.translation: ...Nlia Cundi A. To Nomedius, who deserved well, [someone] placed this.
EDCS-32700455.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, dedicated to Nomedius, acknowledging his merit.

EDCS-32700457.text_edition: $ I]usta  TILEO vixit annos  L P piis CV
EDCS-32700457.summary: This is a very fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning a woman named Iusta who lived for 50 years.

EDCS-32700459.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment, possibly recording personal names or a dedication, but its exact purpose is unclear due to its fragmentary nature.

EDCS-32700463.text_edition: tumulum est inan I rogitantis con m laus est non fac tres EIIV  Aninia coniugi FVIGINI IIII
EDCS-32700463.translation: The tomb is empty. The praise of the inquirer is not to make three. Aninia to her husband FVIGINI, IV.
