EDCS-32700463.summary: This fragmentary inscription is part of a tombstone, likely dedicated by Aninia to her husband, FVIGINI.

EDCS-32700481.text_edition: Zosimi  ? emeriti per Deum omnipotentemN#omnipotentem#TEN eos=IIS#eos#IIS adiuro
EDCS-32700481.translation: Zosimus, retired veteran, through almighty God, I adjure them.
EDCS-32700481.summary: This inscription is a Christian adjuration by Zosimus, possibly a retired soldier, invoking God and swearing by Him.

EDCS-32700488.text_edition: Dis Manibus Flavius Plares miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum beneficiarius praefecti defunctus nnorum XXXX stipendiorum XX Lucius Valerius Surus heres eius genero bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700488.translation: To the Divine Manes. Flavius Plares, soldier of the VIII Cohort of Volunteers, beneficiary of the prefect, deceased at 40 years of age with 20 years of service. Lucius Valerius Surus, his heir, set this up for his well-deserving son-in-law.
EDCS-32700488.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Lucius Valerius Surus to his son-in-law, Flavius Plares, a soldier of the VIII Cohort of Volunteers.

EDCS-32700494.text_edition: Claudiae Quintinae defunctae annorum II mensium III Iulia Maxima avia et Tiberius Claudius Tiberinus pater fecerunt
EDCS-32700494.translation: For Claudia Quintina, deceased at 2 years and 3 months old. Julia Maxima, her grandmother, and Tiberius Claudius Tiberinus, her father, made this.
EDCS-32700494.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl, Claudia Quintina, erected by her grandmother, Julia Maxima, and her father, Tiberius Claudius Tiberinus.

EDCS-32700497.text_edition: Dis Manibus Sacronius Ursinus Anniae Quintae defunctae annorum LX bene merenti posuit
EDCS-32700497.translation: To the Divine Manes. Sacronius Ursinus placed this for Annia Quinta, deceased at 60 years of age, who deserved well.
EDCS-32700497.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Sacronius Ursinus for Annia Quinta, honoring her after her death.

EDCS-32700500.text_edition: Caius Octavius Cai filius Fabia Vitalis domo Brixiae sic veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Octaviae Secundaes sic libertae suae
EDCS-32700500.translation: Gaius Octavius, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, Vitalis, from Brixia, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will, at the discretion of Octavia Secunda, his freedwoman.
EDCS-32700500.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Octavius Vitalis, a veteran of the VII Claudia Legion, whose tomb was erected according to his will by his freedwoman, Octavia Secunda.

EDCS-32700501.text_edition: Alfia Luci filia Valentina viva fecit sibi et Lucio Alfio Valenti patri et Raiae Marcellae matri optimae et Lucio Alfio Valentino fratri posterisque suis
EDCS-32700501.translation: Alfia Valentina, daughter of Lucius, made this while living for herself and for Lucius Alfius Valens, her father, and Raia Marcella, her excellent mother, and Lucius Alfius Valentinus, her brother, and for their descendants.
EDCS-32700501.summary: This is a funerary monument (a 'viva fecit' inscription) prepared by Alfia Valentina for herself, her parents, her brother, and their future descendants.

EDCS-32700502.translation: Caritas to her well-deserving fellow freedman.
EDCS-32700502.summary: This is a dedication from Caritas to a fellow freedman, honoring him for his good merits.

EDCS-32700503.text_edition: D--- Paulina filiae dulcissimae anni unius mensium XI
EDCS-32700503.translation: To Paulina, her sweetest daughter, one year and eleven months old.
EDCS-32700503.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to a very young daughter named Paulina.

EDCS-32700504.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassius Dasantis cohortis III Alpinorum tesserarius centuria Nunnii Isiani votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32700504.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cassius Dasantis, tesserarius of the Third Cohort of Alpinorum, in the century of Nunnius Isianus, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32700504.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Cassius Dasantis, a tesserarius in the Roman army, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32700509.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Nigrinianus
EDCS-32700509.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Nigrinianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-32700509.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Titus Flavius Nigrinianus, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-32700511.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio municipii  Valerius Severus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis votum solvit
EDCS-32700511.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the municipality, Valerius Severus, a consular beneficiarius of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32700511.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of a municipality by Valerius Severus, a beneficiarius of Legion XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32700522.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Maximinus quinquennalis suo votum libens solvit
EDCS-32700522.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Aurelius Maximinus, quinquennalis, willingly fulfilled his own vow.
EDCS-32700522.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Titus Aurelius Maximinus, who held the office of quinquennalis, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32700524.text_edition: Septimius Panes et Septimius Titianus Apuri patri suo pientissimo et matri posuerunt
EDCS-32700524.translation: Septimius Panes and Septimius Titianus set this up for their most devoted father, Apur.
EDCS-32700524.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Septimius Panes and Septimius Titianus in memory of their father, Apur, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-32700530.text_edition: ni suorumque omnium Philocyrius tibicen ex voto posuit et tempulum sic restituit Idibus Iuliis Imperatore domino nostro Caesare Gordiano Augusto II et Pompeiano consulibus
EDCS-32700530.translation: [...] and for all of his own. Philocyrius, the flutist, placed this according to his vow and thus restored the small temple on the Ides of July, when our lord Emperor Caesar Gordian Augustus for the second time and Pompeianus were consuls.
EDCS-32700530.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a small temple by Philocyrius, a flutist, according to a vow, dated to July 15, 241 AD, during the second consulship of Emperor Gordian and Pompeianus.

EDCS-32700531.text_edition: defuncto annorum LX et filio Marcus Ulpius Rusticus SIT REL annorum X plus minus titulum pecunia sua posuit  VEM
EDCS-32700531.translation: For the deceased, 60 years old, and for his son, Marcus Ulpius Rusticus, approximately 10 years old. Marcus Ulpius Rusticus placed this titulus with his own money.
EDCS-32700531.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument (titulus) erected by Marcus Ulpius Rusticus with his own money for a deceased 60-year-old and his son, about 10 years old, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-32700546.translation: [...] for the most unhappy mother, Aurelia.
EDCS-32700546.summary: This inscription is a fragment of a funerary dedication, possibly by a child, to their 'most unhappy mother,' Aurelia, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-32700547.text_edition: Dis Manibus Dastoni matri et Paiioni sorori Publius Aelius Varro pro pietate
EDCS-32700547.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Dasto their mother and Paiio their sister, Publius Aelius Varro, out of piety.
EDCS-32700547.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to the spirits of the departed, erected by Publius Aelius Varro out of piety for his mother Dasto and sister Paiio, likely from the Imperial Roman period.

EDCS-32700550.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Geminus ex voto suscepto
EDCS-32700550.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Julius Geminus, from a vow undertaken.
EDCS-32700550.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Julius Geminus, fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32700552.text_edition: Dis Manibus cTae Severinae filiae dulcissimae  posuit
EDCS-32700552.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Severina, his sweetest daughter, he placed this.
EDCS-32700552.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by an unnamed father to his beloved daughter, Severina.

EDCS-32700556.text_edition: Herculi sacrum Claudius Peregrinus decurio cohortis I Belgarum votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-32700556.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Claudius Peregrinus, decurion of the First Cohort of Belgae, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32700556.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Claudius Peregrinus, a decurion, to Hercules, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-32700558.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titulenius Respectus TIM
EDCS-32700558.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titulenius Respectus.
EDCS-32700558.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titulenius Respectus, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-32700560.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Gaius Valerius Euhodus to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-32700561.translation: Sicil[ius] Secundus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-32700561.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Sicil[ius] Secundus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-32700563.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely from an imperial title, indicating military victories over Germans and Dacians, suggesting a date from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32700565.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelio Messiano veterano defuncto nnorum LXX Tesonia Iulianae sic pientissimo marito et sibi se viva fecit
EDCS-32700565.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Aelius Messianus, a veteran who died at 70 years of age. Tesonia Iuliana made this for her most devoted husband and for herself while still living.
EDCS-32700565.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Messianus, a veteran, erected by his wife Tesonia Iuliana for him and for herself in anticipation of her death.

EDCS-32700566.text_edition: Sextus Anternius Quinti filius Scaptia Severus domo Florentia
EDCS-32700566.translation: Sextus Anternius Severus, son of Quintus, from the Scaptia tribe, from the town of Florentia.
EDCS-32700566.summary: This is a personal inscription identifying Sextus Anternius Severus by his name, filiation, tribe, and place of origin, likely part of a funerary monument or public record.

EDCS-32700568.text_edition: Caius Av Cai filius Fa miles legionis  XXI hic situs est
EDCS-32700568.translation: Gaius Av[entius], son of Gaius, of the Fa[bien] tribe, a soldier of the Twenty-First Legion, is buried here.
EDCS-32700568.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Av[entius], a soldier of the XXI Legion, buried at this location.

EDCS-32700570.text_edition: Publio Carsidio Publi filio Galeria Calvo Lugduno aquilifero legionis IIII Flaviae felicis annn(orum) XLI stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est
EDCS-32700570.translation: To Publius Carsidius Calvus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, from Lugdunum, aquilifer of the Fourth Flavian Felix Legion, who lived 41 years and served for 18 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-32700570.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Carsidius Calvus, an aquilifer from Lugdunum who served in the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, detailing his age and years of service.

EDCS-32700572.text_edition: Caius Coccius Cai filius Veturia Tertius Placentia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XII centuria Caecili Sabini beneficiarius tribuni heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-32700572.translation: Gaius Coccius Tertius, son of Gaius, from Veturia, Placentia, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, with twelve years of service, from the century of Caecilius Sabinus, a beneficiarius (aide) to the tribune, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-32700572.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Coccius Tertius, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, erected by his heir, likely from the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-32700574.text_edition: Caius Fabricius Cai filius Oufentia Primus Como miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Gemini Flavi stipendiorum XX nnorum XXXVIII testamento fieri iussit heres faciundum curavit
EDCS-32700574.translation: Gaius Fabricius Primus, son of Gaius, from Oufentina, Como, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the century of Geminus Flavus, with twenty years of service, thirty-eight years old, ordered this to be made by his will; his heir arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-32700574.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Fabricius Primus, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, erected by his heir following his will, likely from the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-32700575.text_edition: Lucius Flavius Luci filius Fabia Valens Heraclea miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Iuli Prisci annorum XLII stipendiorum XXII centuria Iuli Prisci heres faciendum curavit
EDCS-32700575.translation: Lucius Flavius Valens, son of Lucius, from Fabia, Heraclea, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion Claudia Pia Fidelis, from the century of Iulius Priscus, forty-two years old, with twenty-two years of service, from the century of Iulius Priscus, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-32700575.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Flavius Valens, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion, erected by his heir, likely from the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-32700946.text_edition: Seneca Sestenius Rustici filius annorum XXXXV hic situs est
EDCS-32700946.translation: Seneca Sestenius, son of Rusticus, forty-five years old, lies here.
EDCS-32700946.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Seneca Sestenius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700947.translation: Sacred to the Augustan goddesses. IIO
EDCS-32700947.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Augustan goddesses, likely part of a larger monument or altar, dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700949.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named ]nia, who lived to be 95 years old, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700951.text_edition: Floriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate  consuli  l(ocus)? d(atus) decreto decurionum
EDCS-32700951.translation: To Florianus Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul, a place was given by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-32700951.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to the Emperor Florianus, likely dating to 276 AD, acknowledging a public grant of land.

EDCS-32700953.text_edition: Dis Manibus VitelliFae#Vitelliae#VITELLFA Ammidae nnorum L et Aelio Publi filio Prisco nnorum IIII Quintus OctavO(ius)#Octav(ius)#OCTAO Quintilianus commentariensis matri pientissimae et fratri
EDCS-32700953.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, to Vitellia Ammida, fifty years old, and to Aelius Priscus, son of Publius, four years old; Quintus Octavius Quintilianus, a commentariensis (clerk), dedicated this to his most devoted mother and brother.
EDCS-32700953.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Quintus Octavius Quintilianus to his mother Vitellia Ammida and his young brother Aelius Priscus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700954.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription with unclear meaning, possibly part of a name or a dedicatory phrase, making its purpose difficult to determine.

EDCS-32700969.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Pio Felici Augusto
EDCS-32700969.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-32700969.summary: This is a dedicatory or honorific inscription to the Emperor Marcus Aurelius (Caracalla), likely dating from the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-32805509.text_edition: Silvinae carissimae quod ego sperabam paulam mihi Silvinam facturam ego feci Silvinae delicatae nnorum XV quae est sepulta hic te rogo qui legis hunc titulum ut dicas Silvina puella sit tibi terra levis
EDCS-32805509.translation: To dearest Silvina. That which I hoped Silvina would make a little bit for me, I have done for Silvina, my darling, 15 years old, who is buried here. I ask you who read this inscription, that you say: 'Silvina, girl, may the earth be light for you.'
EDCS-32805509.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a grieving parent for their beloved daughter, Silvina, aged 15, requesting passersby to offer a traditional funerary wish.

EDCS-33600282.text_edition: Primigenivola Mevi serva  votum libens merito solvit
EDCS-33600282.translation: Primigenivola, slave of Mevius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-33600282.summary: This inscription records that Primigenivola, a female slave belonging to Mevius, fulfilled a vow she had made, likely to a deity, indicating a religious dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-34100064.text_edition: Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Dasio et Paiioni coniugi posuit Aelius Baebius filius
EDCS-34100064.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Baebius, son, set this up for Publius Aelius Dasius and Paiiona, his wife.
EDCS-34100064.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Aelius Baebius for his parents, Publius Aelius Dasius and Paiiona.

EDCS-34100065.text_edition: Dasas posuit sibi et Aeliae Datoni coniugi suae ---
EDCS-34100065.translation: Dasas set this up for himself and for Aelia Dato, his wife [---].
EDCS-34100065.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Dasas dedicates a monument for himself and his wife, Aelia Dato, likely while still living, a common practice in the Roman world.

EDCS-34100066.text_edition: Arrius Rufinus veteranus legionis I adiutricis
EDCS-34100066.translation: Arrius Rufinus, veteran of the First Adjutrix Legion.
EDCS-34100066.summary: This inscription identifies Arrius Rufinus as a veteran of the First Adjutrix Legion, likely a funerary monument or a simple identification marker from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-34100068.text_edition: Dis Manibus nIano filio nnorum  Publius Aelius Maximinus pater posuit
EDCS-34100068.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Maximinus, father, set this up for his son Nianus, [aged ---] years.
EDCS-34100068.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Aelius Maximinus for his son Nianus, commemorating the deceased.

EDCS-34100069.text_edition: Publio Aelio Epicati Edano defuncto annorum LXV Publia Aelia Mo coniux pro pietate
EDCS-34100069.translation: Publia Aelia Mo, wife, set this up out of piety for Publius Aelius Epicatus Edanus, deceased at 65 years of age.
EDCS-34100069.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Publia Aelia Mo, a wife, for her deceased husband, Publius Aelius Epicatus Edanus, who lived to be 65 years old.

EDCS-34100070.summary: This is a brief inscription simply stating the name Iulia Secundilla, which could be part of a funerary monument or a personal possession.

EDCS-34100071.text_edition: Rubria Quinti filia Rufina infelicissima sibi posuit
EDCS-34100071.translation: Rubria Rufina, daughter of Quintus, most unhappy, set this up for herself.
EDCS-34100071.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Rubria Rufina, the daughter of Quintus, for herself, expressing her profound sorrow, likely in anticipation of her own death.

EDCS-34100072.text_edition: Dis Manibus Rubria Restuta sibi viva fecit
EDCS-34100072.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Rubria Restuta made this for herself while living.
EDCS-34100072.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Rubria Restuta for herself during her lifetime, a common practice to prepare one's tomb in advance.

EDCS-34100132.text_edition: Deanae et Asclepio aedem publice Nerastini vetustate corruptam a solo restituerunt
EDCS-34100132.translation: To Diana and Aesculapius, the Nerastini publicly restored from the ground up the temple, which had been corrupted by age.
EDCS-34100132.summary: This inscription records the public restoration of a temple to Diana and Aesculapius by the community of Nerastini, indicating a communal religious dedication and architectural renovation.

EDCS-34100135.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aletius Barbius infelicissimae coniugi suae Marcellae quae vixxitX#vixxit#VIXXIX bene mecum annos plus minus LV filios reliquit quattuor quae nullum dolum de se vidit
EDCS-34100135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Aletius Barbius [made this] for his most unfortunate wife Marcella, who lived with me happily for about 55 years, leaving four sons, and who saw no deceit from herself.
EDCS-34100135.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aletius Barbius dedicated to his deceased wife Marcella, commemorating her life, marital happiness, and motherhood, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-37900107.text_edition: Dis Manibus Panianti qui vixit nnos V
EDCS-37900107.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Panias, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-37900107.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Panias, who died at the young age of five, indicating a child's burial monument.

EDCS-41300186.text_edition: Lucius Tertius Luci filius Tertianus Heraclea nnorum  HIR ACLRA miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII Lucius Pomponius Pollio? ---
EDCS-41300186.translation: Lucius Tertius, son of Lucius, Tertianus, from Heraclea, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, of 40 years, with 17 years of service. Lucius Pomponius Pollio? [---]
EDCS-41300186.summary: This inscription commemorates Lucius Tertius Tertianus, a veteran soldier from Heraclea who served in the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, likely a funerary monument partially mentioning a benefactor or heir.

EDCS-41300187.text_edition: Lucius Tertius Luci filius Sergia Tertianus Heraclea mil=NV(es)#mil(es)#NV legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII testamento fieri iussit Lucius Ponponius sic Pollio heres ponendum curavit
EDCS-41300187.translation: Lucius Tertius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, Tertianus, from Heraclea, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, of 40 years, with 17 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Lucius Pomponius Pollio, his heir, took care of placing it.
EDCS-41300187.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Tertius Tertianus, a veteran of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, erected according to his will by his heir Lucius Pomponius Pollio, detailing his military service and origin.

EDCS-41600067.summary: This brief inscription records Publius Rutilacius Rufus as the maker or dedicator of an unspecified object or monument, typical of a signature on a crafted item.

EDCS-41600068.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription naming Alfisius Men[...], likely part of a larger dedication or funerary text with missing information.

EDCS-41600117.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Ulpius Iustus vixit annos XI hic situs est Ulpius Priscus pater faciendum curavit
EDCS-41600117.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Iustus lived for 11 years; he is buried here. Ulpius Priscus, his father, took care of having this made.
EDCS-41600117.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Marcus Ulpius Iustus, who died at age 11, and was set up by his father, Ulpius Priscus.

EDCS-41600119.text_edition: Dis Manibus Vibius NMVS V Vibius Priscus CVYVS nepos  BINCV H
EDCS-41600119.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibius [---] Vibius Priscus, his nephew [---]
EDCS-41600119.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes, mentioning Vibius and his nephew Vibius Priscus, indicating a family relationship.

EDCS-41600120.text_edition: Dis Manibus Pyrami vixit annos XVI Aemilius Placidus maritus et Euplus et Ieroitice mater posuerunt
EDCS-41600120.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Pyrami, who lived 16 years. Aemilius Placidus, her husband, and Euplus and Ieroitice, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-41600120.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes for Pyrami, who died at age 16, erected by her husband Aemilius Placidus, and her mother Euplus and Ieroitice, indicating a family memorial.

EDCS-41600122.text_edition: Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto tribunicia potestate II imperatori III consuli II proconsuli patri patriae decreto decurionum
EDCS-41600122.translation: To the Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, with tribunician power for the second time, Imperator for the third time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of his country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-41600122.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument, likely a statue or honorary arch, to the Emperor Septimius Severus by the local council (decurions) during his reign (late 2nd/early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-41600124.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum in memoriam aeternam coniugi infelicissimo Aurelio Lupo aequiti sic Romano qui vixit annos XXXVIII Aurelia Notalie marito pientissimo
EDCS-41600124.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. In eternal memory of my most unfortunate husband, Aurelius Lupus, a Roman knight, who lived 38 years. Aurelia Notalie to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-41600124.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Lupus, a Roman knight, dedicated by his wife, Aurelia Notalie, expressing her devotion and sorrow.

EDCS-41600125.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Lupinus et Rufilla parentes filiae Fuscinae pientissimae quae vixit an=AInos#annos#AAIN XXVII fecerunt
EDCS-41600125.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lupinus and Rufilla, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter, Fuscina, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-41600125.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Fuscina, dedicated by her parents, Lupinus and Rufilla.

EDCS-41600126.summary: This short inscription, likely a dedication or marker, appears to name a person, Ias Risinis, possibly a praeses (governor or president), with an abbreviated ending.

EDCS-41600127.text_edition: VT et VO et AP filius sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-41600127.translation: Ut and Vo and Ap, his son, made this for themselves and their family while alive.
EDCS-41600127.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument, likely a family tomb, by Ut, Vo, and Ap, the son, for themselves and their descendants during their lifetimes.

EDCS-41600128.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Falia Murci filia viva memoriam fecit sibe sic et filiis suis
EDCS-41600128.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Falia Murci's daughter, while alive, made this memorial for herself and her sons.
EDCS-41600128.summary: This is a funerary inscription and memorial prepared by Falia, daughter of Murcus, during her lifetime, for herself and her sons.

EDCS-41600129.text_edition: Gentio Sai filio patri et Platurae matri parentibus Clevata Eutychum servum facere iussit
EDCS-41600129.translation: To Gentius, son of Sai, father, and to Platura, mother, Clevata ordered Eutychus, his slave, to make this for his parents.
EDCS-41600129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Clevata to his parents, Gentius and Platura, which he ordered his slave, Eutychus, to execute.

EDCS-44200188.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori K Patricius et Paula pro incolumitate sua suorumque pridie Nonas Septembres
EDCS-44200188.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator. K. Patricius and Paula for their safety and that of their kin, the day before the Nones of September.
EDCS-44200188.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Fulgurator (Thunderer) made by K. Patricius and Paula for the well-being of themselves and their family, dated to September 4th.

EDCS-44200205.text_edition: Dis Manibus hic positus est Titus Aurelius Aurelianus filius pientissimus vixit annis VIIII
EDCS-44200205.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Here lies Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, a most devoted son, who lived for 9 years.
EDCS-44200205.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy named Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, who died at the age of nine.

EDCS-44200207.text_edition: Tito Flavio Titi filio Pollia castrensi Lucilio vexillario equitum singularium Augusti sesquiplicario eiusdem numeri duplicario et summo curatori equitum singularium Augusti |(centurioni) cohortis VIII voluntariorum honesta missione commodis acceptis omnibus ab Imperatore Hadriano Augusto Titus Flavius Eulogus libertus et heres vivus fecit ex voluntate sua et sibi et libertis libertabusque suis posterisque eorum
EDCS-44200207.translation: To Titus Flavius Lucilius, son of Titus, of the Pollian tribe, a castrensis, vexillarius of the Augustan mounted singulares, sesquiplicarius of the same unit, duplicarius, and chief curator of the Augustan mounted singulares, centurion of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, having received an honorable discharge with all emoluments from Emperor Hadrian Augustus. Titus Flavius Eulogus, his freedman and heir, made this during his lifetime, by his own will, for himself and his freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants.
EDCS-44200207.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Lucilius, detailing his extensive military career including promotions within the Augustan mounted singulares and as a centurion, and notes that it was erected by his freedman and heir, Titus Flavius Eulogus, for their shared burial.

EDCS-44200208.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo  Lucilla quod ante hos annos tibi hoce sic in loco voveram restitui votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-44200208.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow she had made to you on this very spot years ago.
EDCS-44200208.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucilla, fulfilling a vow she made previously.

EDCS-44200209.text_edition: Dis Manibus IulI(ius)#Iul(ius)#IUI Trofimus Trosiae Thycini sic bene merenti  Dis Manibus Leoni
EDCS-44200209.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Trofimus to Trosia Thycini, who deserved so well. To the Spirits of the Departed. To Leo.
EDCS-44200209.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Iulius Trofimus to Trosia Thycini and also to Leo, honoring the deceased.

EDCS-44200213.text_edition: Victorino decurioni municipii Aquae filioque infelicissimo vixit annos X sic menses LI sic dies XIIII et Ulpiae Galliae coniugi incomparabili Publius Aelius Victorinus  quaestor IIIvir quinquennalis ed sic sibi vivus fecit
EDCS-44200213.translation: To Victorinus, decurion of the municipality of Aquae, and to his most unfortunate son, who lived 10 years, 51 months, and 14 days; and to Ulpia Gallia, his incomparable wife. Publius Aelius Victorinus, quaestor, triumvir quinquennalis, made this during his lifetime for himself.
EDCS-44200213.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Publius Aelius Victorinus for himself and his family members, including his son who died young, and his wife.

EDCS-44200214.text_edition: na Iacu ipantis fili- nnorum LV
EDCS-44200214.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating an individual named Iacu[---]pantis who lived to be 55 years old.

EDCS-46400015.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the health of Emperor Aemilianus Augustus and his entire divine house, the Second Millenary Cohort, new, Dalmatian, equestrian, Aemiliana, with Quintus Sextilius Marinus, tribune, and Asinius Maximus, consular, [set this up].
EDCS-46400015.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the well-being of Emperor Aemilianus and his family, made by a military unit, the Second Millenary Cohort, led by a tribune and a consular official, likely dating to 253 CE.

EDCS-46400961.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Luccius Valens |(centurio) cohortis I milliariae cum Marco Luccio Frontone filio libens merito posuit
EDCS-46400961.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Marcus Luccius Valens, centurion of the First Millenary Cohort, with his son Marcus Luccius Fronton, willingly and deservedly placed this.
EDCS-46400961.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by a centurion, Marcus Luccius Valens, together with his son, Marcus Luccius Fronton.

EDCS-46600036.text_edition: Caius Iulius Ampliatus Iuliae Mollatinae nnum V idem sic Servando annuculo mensium VII hic siti sunt filiis suis posuit
EDCS-46600036.translation: Gaius Iulius Ampliatus placed this for his children, Iulia Mollatina, aged 5, and Servandus, an infant of 7 months, who are buried here.
EDCS-46600036.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius Iulius Ampliatus commemorating his two deceased children, Iulia Mollatina and Servandus, who died young.

EDCS-46600037.text_edition: Nimphis sic Et  Lucius Baebidius Cassius |(centurio) et Marcus Flavius
EDCS-46600037.translation: To the Nymphs, also. Lucius Baebidius Cassius, centurion, and Marcus Flavius [set this up].
EDCS-46600037.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs, set up by Lucius Baebidius Cassius, a centurion, and Marcus Flavius, likely as a votive offering.

EDCS-46600038.text_edition: Herculi Augusto sacrum Donatus Cnaei Corneli Certi votum solvit libens
EDCS-46600038.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Donatus, slave of Gnaeus Cornelius Certus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-46600038.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Donatus, a slave of Gnaeus Cornelius Certus, in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-46600039.text_edition: Salaciae Augustae sacrum Salvius Cai libertus Panus
EDCS-46600039.translation: Sacred to Salacia Augusta. Salvius Panus, freedman of Gaius, [set this up].
EDCS-46600039.summary: This is a dedication to Salacia Augusta, set up by Salvius Panus, a freedman of Gaius.

EDCS-46600040.text_edition: Titus Buzetius Diti filius Tura Ti filia Tito Buzetia Titi? filia mtena Gelli filia testamento fieri iussit tus Buzetius Titi libertus patrono arbitratu Titonis Buzetiae sororis et Sextonis Buzetiae sororis
EDCS-46600040.translation: Titus Buzetius, son of Ditus, Tura, daughter of Titus, and Titus Buzetia, daughter of Titus, and Mtena, daughter of Gellius, ordered this to be made by will. Tus Buzetius, freedman of Titus, [made this] for his patron, by the judgment of Titonis Buzetia, his sister, and Sextonis Buzetia, his sister.
EDCS-46600040.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a monument, probably a tomb, ordered by will by several individuals, and executed by a freedman for his patron with the consent of the patron's sisters, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-46600041.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Adiutori coniugi bene merenti con sic quo vixi annos XXXVIII menses III Sextilia Infgenua fecit
EDCS-46600041.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Adiutor, a well-deserving husband, with whom I lived 38 years, 3 months. Sextilia Infgenua made this.
EDCS-46600041.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Sextilia Infgenua to her well-deserving husband, Adiutor, who lived for 38 years and 3 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-46600042.text_edition: Quinto Rubrio Apli filio Rufo Rubria Rufina patri posuit
EDCS-46600042.translation: To Quintus Rubrius Rufus, son of Aplus. Rubria Rufina placed this for her father.
EDCS-46600042.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Rubria Rufina for her father, Quintus Rubrius Rufus, son of Aplus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46600043.text_edition: Lucio Veratio Martiali Lucius Veratius Lupus patri posuit
EDCS-46600043.translation: To Lucius Veratius Martialis. Lucius Veratius Lupus placed this for his father.
EDCS-46600043.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Lucius Veratius Lupus for his father, Lucius Veratius Martialis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46600046.text_edition: Dis Manibus sibi et coniugi Maximae annorum XL posuitS#posuit#POSUIS Maximus III
EDCS-46600046.translation: To the Divine Manes. Maximus placed this for himself and for his wife Maxima, 40 years old. Maximus III.
EDCS-46600046.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Maximus for himself and his wife Maxima, who was 40 years old, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46600047.text_edition: - Procline vixit annos L Maximus et Melio filii annos IX
EDCS-46600047.translation: Procline lived for 50 years. Maximus and Melio, her sons, lived for 9 years.
EDCS-46600047.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lives of Procline, who lived 50 years, and her sons Maximus and Melio, who both lived 9 years, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46700052.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Seneca, who was 25 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-46700053.text_edition: Aurelius Ursus annorum XXXX coniugi posuit
EDCS-46700053.translation: Aurelius Ursus, 40 years old, placed this for his wife.
EDCS-46700053.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aurelius Ursus, who was 40 years old, for his wife, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46700054.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for an individual named Aureli[us/a], 20 years old, with Saria also mentioned, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46700055.text_edition: enoni a filia matri posuit bene merenti
EDCS-46700055.translation: To Enoni, her daughter placed this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-46700055.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by a daughter for her well-deserving mother, Enoni, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-46700056.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Aurelius, who lived to be 60 years old.

EDCS-46700057.summary: This inscription records that Martinus, described as pious, erected an unnamed monument.

EDCS-46700058.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ansilla, who lived to be 30 years old.

EDCS-46700061.text_edition: Festae annorum VIIII Crescens pater posuit filiae hic sita est
EDCS-46700061.translation: To Festa, nine years old. Her father Crescens placed this for his daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-46700061.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Festa, erected by her father Crescens, recording that she died at age nine and is buried at this spot.

EDCS-47800005.text_edition: nus M sibi et Clemen ssimo F
EDCS-47800005.translation: ...nus M... for himself and the most Clement... F...
EDCS-47800005.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely erected by a man named ...nus M... for himself and a person named Clemen... F..., probably a relative.

EDCS-47800131.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only part of the name Flavius.

EDCS-47800189.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales

EDCS-47800263.text_edition: ae quae vixit annos XX et Gel
EDCS-47800263.translation: ...to her who lived 20 years, and Gel...
EDCS-47800263.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a woman who lived 20 years, possibly also mentioning Gel..., likely another individual associated with the monument.

EDCS-47800299.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, referring to a location and a duration of 5 days, perhaps the age of a deceased.

EDCS-47800328.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, preserving only the word 'parents', suggesting a dedication or monument erected by or for parents.

EDCS-47800495.text_edition: vianae defunctae annorum plus minus VII pater
EDCS-47800495.translation: ...to Viana, deceased, approximately 7 years old. Her father...
EDCS-47800495.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a young girl named Viana, who died at approximately seven years old, erected by her father.

EDCS-47800512.summary: This fragment records a dedication to someone named Ianuarius, likely on a tombstone or an altar.

EDCS-47800533.translation: To the Divine Manes, Constant BV.
EDCS-47800533.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely the beginning of a tombstone dedication to the underworld gods for an individual named Constant.

EDCS-47800534.text_edition: cus ex  nomine Mes cae qua[e vixit &
EDCS-47800534.translation: [Someone] from the name of Mesca, who lived [life duration].
EDCS-47800534.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely detailing the name of the deceased (Mesca) and their lifespan.

EDCS-47800536.text_edition: rina  quae vixit nnos XX menses IIII  A
EDCS-47800536.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for a woman named Rina, stating she lived for 20 years and 4 months.

EDCS-47800538.text_edition: IRECA  et Aureli-- Res VI ergo B ATVRBB
EDCS-47800538.translation: IRECA and Aurelius [---] six, therefore B. ATVRBB.
EDCS-47800538.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly mentioning individuals named IRECA and Aurelius, but its purpose is unclear due to extensive damage.

EDCS-47800539.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely indicating a name ending in -sius and a possible origin from Salona.

EDCS-47800566.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a dedication or monument for a son.

EDCS-47800725.text_edition: Domitio Vincentio qui vixit annis quinque diebus numero XX- Domitius Eufrasus filio karissimo in pace fecit
EDCS-47800725.translation: To Domitius Vincentius, who lived five years and twenty days. Domitius Eufrasus made this for his dearest son in peace.
EDCS-47800725.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Domitius Eufrasus for his beloved son, Domitius Vincentius, who died at the age of five years and twenty days.

EDCS-47800728.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-47800728.text_edition: Ius et Aua qui et Fy sic sibi arcam
EDCS-47800728.translation: Ius and Aua, also known as Fy, thus [made] this chest for themselves.
EDCS-47800728.summary: This inscription records that Ius and Aua, also called Fy, commissioned a sarcophagus or burial chest for their own future use, likely a joint funerary monument.

EDCS-47800735.text_edition: Sit tibi notum recordationi QV Patricius hic iacet qui DOCI in officio praefecturae et BINEC vitam suam QVAD CIRM
EDCS-47800735.translation: Let it be known to you for remembrance that Patricius lies here, who [died] in the office of prefect and [lived] his life QVAD CIRM.
EDCS-47800735.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Patricius, who held the office of prefect, and serves as a record for remembrance.

EDCS-47800739.text_edition: NA Ingenuposuit filiae III IERENTRONIIE illae quae I vixit annis
EDCS-47800739.translation: Ingenuus placed this for his daughter, III Ierentrone, she who lived for I year.
EDCS-47800739.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Ingenuus for his daughter, Ierentrone, who died at the age of one year.

EDCS-48000063.text_edition: a Publicia  virginia  vixit annos posuit
EDCS-48000063.translation: Publicia Virginia lived for years. She placed this.
EDCS-48000063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publicia Virginia, likely dedicated by herself or on her behalf.

EDCS-48000066.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely on a tombstone, dedicated by Marcus, a son, to his mother.

EDCS-48000079.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli honorarii, tituli sacri
EDCS-48000079.text_edition: Domino nostro Flavio Constanti victori ac triumfatori Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus vir clarissimus praeses provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eius
EDCS-48000079.translation: To our Lord Flavius Constantius, the victorious and triumphant, Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus, a most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divinity and majesty, [dedicated this].
EDCS-48000079.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus, the governor of Dalmatia, to the Emperor Flavius Constantius, likely dating to the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-48100001.translation: The Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his 5th tribunician power, 13th acclamation as Imperator, father of the country, consul for the 5th time, designated consul for the 6th time, censor, restored this under Gnaeus Pedius Cascus, legate of Augustus with pro-praetorian power.
EDCS-48100001.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a structure by Emperor Vespasian, likely around 74 CE, with Gnaeus Pedius Cascus serving as his provincial governor.

EDCS-48100007.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-48100007.text_edition: Arca Andreae  VMERVNOBVCN NICATE coniugi eius
EDCS-48100007.translation: The tomb of Andreas [and] UMERUNOBUCN NICATE, his wife.
EDCS-48100007.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the tomb of Andreas and his wife, Umerunobucn Nicate.

EDCS-48100010.summary: This is a religious or dedicatory inscription to a lord named Menna.

EDCS-48100017.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a person named Sabinian, described as "most sweet."

EDCS-48100053.text_edition: Aeli-- Victor mensium V dierum XX
EDCS-48100053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young child named Victor, son of Aeli---, who died at the age of five months and twenty days.

EDCS-51000060.text_edition: Invicto Mithrae sacrum Caius O C pro salute sua votum libens solvit
EDCS-51000060.translation: Sacred to the unconquered Mithras. Gaius O. C. willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-51000060.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Gaius O. C., fulfilling a vow for his own health, likely from a Mithraic sanctuary.

EDCS-51200024.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caesidio Quadratiano patri pientissimo qui vixit annos LXXX Tertianus et Quadratianus filii fecerunt
EDCS-51200024.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Caesidius Quadratianus, a most devoted father, who lived 80 years. Tertianus and Quadratianus, his sons, made this.
EDCS-51200024.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by Tertianus and Quadratianus for their father, Caesidius Quadratianus, who died at 80 years old.

EDCS-51200026.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aelius, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-51400004.text_edition: Lucius Vettius Marci filius Maecia Secundus Libarna veteranus legionis VII vivus fecit sibi et Vettiae Luci filiae Verae et Vettiae Luci libertae S
EDCS-51400004.translation: Lucius Vettius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Maecia tribe, a veteran of the VII Legion from Libarna, made this for himself while alive, and for Vettia Vera, daughter of Lucius, and for Vettia S, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-51400004.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Lucius Vettius Secundus, a veteran, for himself and two women, Vettia Vera and Vettia S, while he was still alive.

EDCS-51400005.text_edition: Dis Manibus Arria Ner sibi et Arrrio Platori coniugi vivi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-51400005.translation: To the Divine Manes. Arria Ner and Arrius Plator, her husband, made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-51400005.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Arria Ner and Arrius Plator for themselves and for each other while they were still alive.

EDCS-51400006.text_edition: aurus defunctus Aelia Maxima posuit marito suo et sibi
EDCS-51400006.translation: ...aurus, deceased. Aelia Maxima placed this for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-51400006.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aelia Maxima for her deceased husband, whose name ended in -aurus, and for herself.

EDCS-51400007.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Messius  CIRBN vivi sibi et Aureliae Surae carissimae coniugi posuerunt
EDCS-51400007.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Messius CIRBN and Aurelia Sura, his dearest wife, placed this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-51400007.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aurelius Messius CIRBN and his wife, Aurelia Sura, for themselves while they were still alive.

EDCS-51400008.text_edition: defunctae nnorum XL posuit Aelia Ursa filia matri pientissimae
EDCS-51400008.translation: For the deceased, aged 40. Aelia Ursa, her daughter, placed this for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-51400008.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by Aelia Ursa for her deceased mother, who died at 40 years old.

EDCS-51400010.text_edition: suis Buo contubernali et bonis NI
EDCS-51400010.translation: For his own, Buo, his tent-mate, and for good NI.
EDCS-51400010.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Buo, a 'contubernalis' (tent-mate or partner), and possibly other individuals.

EDCS-51400011.translation: Publius Aelius, son of Titus Aelius.
EDCS-51400011.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name of Publius Aelius, likely indicating his filiation to Titus Aelius.

EDCS-51400012.text_edition: Dis Manibus patri  Iulio ---  pienitissimo posuit monumentum
EDCS-51400012.translation: To the Divine Manes. For his most devoted father, Iulius [---], he placed this monument.
EDCS-51400012.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a most devoted father, Iulius, whose name is partially lost, set up by his son.

EDCS-51400013.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, making it impossible to determine its original purpose, dedicant, or recipient.

EDCS-51400046.text_edition: Calvidiae Publi filiae Secundae uxori piissimae Annaeus Publi filius Tromentina Aper aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo et Annaeo Publi filio Tironi aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo et Annaeo Publi filio Apro et sibi et suis
EDCS-51400046.translation: To Calvidia Secunda, daughter of Publius, most devoted wife; to Annaeus Aper, son of Publius, of the Tribe Tromentina, aedile, duumvir with judicial power; and to Annaeus Tiro, son of Publius, aedile, duumvir with judicial power; and to Annaeus Aper, son of Publius; and for himself and his family.
EDCS-51400046.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Annaeus Aper for his devoted wife, himself, his family, and possibly other relatives who held municipal offices as aediles and duumvirs.

EDCS-51400049.text_edition: I(n)? h(onorem)? d(omus) divinae Deo  Plares  Vale solv---
EDCS-51400049.translation: In honor of the divine house, to the god Plares. Vale. Solved...
EDCS-51400049.summary: This fragmentary inscription is likely a religious dedication to the god Plares, possibly made 'in honor of the divine house', with the end suggesting the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-51400050.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aemilius Felici et Melitine MP
EDCS-51400050.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilius Felici and Melitine MP.
EDCS-51400050.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Aemilius Felici and Melitine, likely erected by themselves or their loved ones.

EDCS-51400052.text_edition: Dis Manibus  ius Celer MACVM a sodali
EDCS-51400052.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. ---ius Celer MACVM from a comrade.
EDCS-51400052.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Celer, erected by a comrade.

EDCS-51800031.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Maxitto vixit annos XXX Vipius coniunx coniugi carissimae vivus pro pietate
EDCS-51800031.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Maxitto lived 30 years. Vipius, the husband, for his dearest wife, while living, out of devotion.
EDCS-51800031.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Maxitto, who lived 30 years, erected by her devoted husband Vipius while he was still alive.

EDCS-51800032.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Aplis Aurelius Argurianus
EDCS-51800032.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aplis Aurelius Argurianus.
EDCS-51800032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Aplis Aurelius Argurianus.

EDCS-51800037.text_edition: Herculenti sic Aurelius Dassto sic Triti posuit
EDCS-51800037.translation: To Herculens, thus Aurelius Dassto, thus Triti placed this.
EDCS-51800037.summary: This is a dedication to Herculens, set up by Aurelius Dassto and Triti.

EDCS-51800048.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicated to Publius Aelius Ursus.

EDCS-51800137.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Conservator Augustus. Aeli--
EDCS-51800137.summary: This is a religious dedication to Silvanus Conservator Augustus, likely made by someone with the name Aelius or a similar name.

EDCS-51800138.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelia Sergia Marci filia  Maxima Gemellina Luci liberta votum solverunt libentes merito
EDCS-51800138.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelia Sergia Maxima, daughter of Marcus, and Gemellina, freedwoman of Lucius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-51800138.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two women, Aurelia Sergia Maxima and Gemellina, indicating the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-51800141.text_edition: Dis  Manibus  Aureliae annorum XXX Secundae  coniugi piae posuit
EDCS-51800141.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. He placed this for his pious wife, Aurelia Secunda, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-51800141.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelia Secunda by her husband, commemorating her pious character and age at death.

EDCS-51800150.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius AriRES IIE
EDCS-51800150.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius ARIRES IIE.
EDCS-51800150.summary: This is a fragmented votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-51800151.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Albanus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-51800151.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Albanus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51800151.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Flavius Albanus, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-51800206.summary: This is a very short votive inscription indicating that a woman named Silvia made a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-52400214.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Papinio Firmo heredes Papinius Laetinus et Papinia Paula et Papinius Laetio filii bene merenti posuerunt
EDCS-52400214.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lucius Papinius Firmus, his heirs—Papinius Laetinus and Papinia Paula and Papinius Laetio, his children—placed this for their well-deserving father.
EDCS-52400214.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Papinius Firmus by his children and heirs, Papinius Laetinus, Papinia Paula, and Papinius Laetio, honoring their deserving father.

EDCS-54900279.text_edition: Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari
EDCS-54900279.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-54900279.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Galerius Valerius Maximinus, recognizing him as a 'nobilissimus Caesar', likely dating from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900345.text_edition: Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobili[ssimus Caesar &
EDCS-54900345.translation: Pius Felix, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, imperator for the third time, consul, father of the country, and Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar...
EDCS-54900345.summary: This is a fragmentary imperial inscription, likely part of a dedication or honorific monument, listing the titles of an emperor (Pius Felix) and a co-emperor or heir, Gaius Iulius Verus Maximus, likely from the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-54900348.text_edition: Imperatoribus dominis nostris Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Augusto et Marco Aurelio Maximiano Augusto
EDCS-54900348.translation: To the Emperors, our lords, Gaius Valerius Diocletianus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Maximianus Augustus.
EDCS-54900348.summary: This inscription is an honorific dedication to the co-emperors Diocletian and Maximian, recognizing them as 'Augusti' and 'our lords', likely dating from the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900353.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari  Imperatori domino nostro Diocletiano Augusto
EDCS-54900353.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar. To our lord Emperor Diocletian Augustus.
EDCS-54900353.summary: This is an imperial dedication, honoring Marcus Aurelius Carinus as 'nobilissimus Caesar' and Emperor Diocletian as 'Dominus Noster Augustus', likely indicating a period of co-rule or transition in the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-55100445.text_edition: Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator XVIII pater patriae
EDCS-55100445.translation: The Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 8th time, hailed Imperator for the 18th time, Father of the Country.
EDCS-55100445.summary: This inscription records the titles and honors of the Emperor Vespasian, likely from a public monument or dedication during his reign (ca. 77-78 CE).

EDCS-55500822.text_edition: Aurelio Titiano decurioni municipii IIviro avo et proavo defuncto nnorum
EDCS-55500822.translation: To Aurelius Titianus, decurion of the municipium, duumvir, grandfather and great-grandfather, deceased at the age of [---] years.
EDCS-55500822.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication honoring Aurelius Titianus, a prominent local official who served as a decurion and duumvir.

EDCS-55701978.text_edition: Neptuno sacrum periculorum absolutori Petronius Asper votum solvit libens animo
EDCS-55701978.translation: Sacred to Neptune, deliverer from dangers. Petronius Asper willingly and sincerely fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701978.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Neptune by Petronius Asper, thanking the god for deliverance from dangers.

EDCS-55701979.text_edition: Mandetae Victorinae nepotiae quae vixit annos XII posuit
EDCS-55701979.translation: [Someone] placed this for Mandeta Victorina, his granddaughter, who lived 12 years.
EDCS-55701979.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mandeta Victorina, a young girl who died at the age of twelve, set up by a relative.

EDCS-55701980.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aelius Acutianus vivus posuit filio suo Gentio qui vixit annos III
EDCS-55701980.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Acutianus, in his lifetime, placed this for his son Gentius, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-55701980.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, set up by Aelius Acutianus for his young son Gentius during his own lifetime.

EDCS-55701981.text_edition: Dis Manibus Cipius Statianus vixit annos L et Aurelia Panto vixit annos LX
EDCS-55701981.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cipius Statianus lived 50 years, and Aurelia Panto lived 60 years.
EDCS-55701981.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Cipius Statianus and Aurelia Panto, noting their lifespans.

EDCS-55701982.text_edition: Silvano Augusto sacrum Aurelius Plares libens posuit
EDCS-55701982.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Aurelius Plares willingly placed this.
EDCS-55701982.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus, set up by Aurelius Plares.

EDCS-55701983.text_edition: us Nov  Maximus  pientissimi nepotes  MOV vivi et  Samiano et Rufinus vivi ipsi sibi fecerunt
EDCS-55701983.translation: [---]us Nov[---] Maximus, most devoted grandchildren [---], living, and [---] MOV, living, and Samianus and Rufinus, living, made this for themselves.
EDCS-55701983.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument, possibly a family tomb, made by and for several individuals including devoted grandchildren, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-55701984.text_edition: Caius Iulius Mara veteranus cohortis II Cyrrhestarum nnorum XC domo Beroea testamento fieri iussit Magnus libertus idemque heres posuit
EDCS-55701984.translation: Gaius Julius Mara, a veteran of the Second Cohort of Cyrrhestae, 90 years old, from Beroea, ordered this to be made by his will. Magnus, his freedman and also his heir, placed it.
EDCS-55701984.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Mara, a 90-year-old veteran, erected posthumously by his freedman and heir, Magnus, according to Mara's will.

EDCS-55701987.text_edition: Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus R S votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-55701987.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus R. S. willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701987.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta by Titus R. S., fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-55701989.text_edition: Imperatori Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto
EDCS-55701989.translation: To Emperor Marcus Aurelius Probus, Pious, Fortunate, Undefeated, Augustus.
EDCS-55701989.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Probus, likely part of a statue base or monumental inscription honoring his reign around 276-282 AD.

EDCS-55701990.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Maximinus Caesar, possibly referring to Maximinus Thrax during his co-rule or a later Maximinus, recognizing his imperial title.

EDCS-55701992.summary: This short inscription likely indicates ownership or responsibility by the military century commanded by Pompeius, perhaps marking a property boundary, a work unit, or a grave belonging to that century.

EDCS-55701993.text_edition: Lucius NARION filius miles cohortis Cyrrhestarum domo Beroea nnorum L stipendiorum XV hic situs est testamento fieri iussit
EDCS-55701993.translation: Lucius Narion, son, a soldier of the Cohort of Cyrrhestes, from Beroea, 50 years old, with 15 years of service, is laid here; he ordered this to be made by will.
EDCS-55701993.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Narion, a soldier from Beroea who served in a Roman cohort, recording his age, service, origin, and his wish to have this monument erected through his will.

EDCS-55900002.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication by Naius to the goddess Harmonia, possibly on an altar or votive offering.

EDCS-56900018.text_edition: Dis Manibus Statiae Provinciali Statia Messaliane matri bene merenti posuit
EDCS-56900018.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Statia Messaliana placed this for Statia Provincialis, her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-56900018.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Statia Messaliana to her mother, Statia Provincialis, honoring her as a well-deserving parent.

EDCS-56900048.text_edition: patri bono defunctus in sepultus in n annorum CX vFixit#vixit#FIXIT
EDCS-56900048.translation: To a good father, deceased, interred at 110 years old. He lived.
EDCS-56900048.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a father, noting his long life of 110 years, and confirming his burial.

EDCS-56900079.summary: This fragmentary inscription preserves the name Julius and an abbreviated cognomen, likely marking a personal possession, a tombstone, or a simple signature.

EDCS-56900081.translation: Slator, son of Oeplus, 10 years old.
EDCS-56900081.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription or a record of a young individual named Slator, identifying him by his father Oeplus and his age at death as 10 years.

EDCS-56900082.text_edition: nnos XXI Sinus? --- Andes Sini fi[lius &
EDCS-56900082.translation: 21 years. Sinus? --- Andes, son of Sinus.
EDCS-56900082.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, recording the age of 21 years and mentioning two individuals, possibly a father and son, Sinus and Andes.

EDCS-56900090.text_edition: Dis Manibus Titus defunctus annorum L Baezo uxor et sibi viva posuit
EDCS-56900090.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Baezo, his wife, placed this for Titus, who died at the age of 50, and for herself, while living.
EDCS-56900090.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a wife dedicating a tombstone for her deceased husband and for herself while still alive, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-56900091.text_edition: Dis Manibus Celtemio vixit annos LXX Titus Plati vivus titulum vivo ipsi posuit sibi et contubernali
EDCS-56900091.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Celtemio lived for 70 years. Titus Plati, while living, placed this titulus for himself, while living, and for his partner.
EDCS-56900091.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating a tombstone set up by Titus Plati for himself and his partner, with a specific mention of Celtemio's lifespan, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-56900119.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Publia Aelia Everina, mother.
EDCS-56900119.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publia Aelia Everina by her children or another family member, acknowledging her role as mother.

EDCS-56900123.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be part of a dedication to the goddess Fortuna.

EDCS-56900126.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly mentioning a person with the nomen Aurelius.

EDCS-56900134.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly referring to a person named Lucius Felicius Caius or a similar combination, likely a personal dedication.

EDCS-56900136.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, making its original purpose and content impossible to determine.

EDCS-56900138.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a *seviri Augustales*, an important college of local magistrates often composed of freedmen, indicating a public or honorary dedication.

EDCS-56900139.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Valeria nata V -ertu fili
EDCS-56900139.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Valeria, daughter of V. Vertus...
EDCS-56900139.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Valeria, identified by her filiation, likely by her family.

EDCS-56900141.translation: To Octavius Pothinus, freedman of Gnaeus.
EDCS-56900141.summary: This is an inscription dedicated to Octavius Pothinus, who is identified as a freedman of Gnaeus, likely a funerary or honorary monument.

EDCS-56900149.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication by Coelius Gemellus, but the recipient and monument type are unclear.

EDCS-56900151.text_edition: Colchide sic uxori merenti qui sic vixit=LI#vixit#VIXLI annos XXX Dibus Manibus
EDCS-56900151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Colchis to her deserving husband, who lived for 30 years. Colchis to her deserving husband, who lived for 30 years. To the Divine Manes.
EDCS-56900151.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Colchis to her deserving husband, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-56900164.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication, but the names and purpose are mostly lost.

EDCS-56900169.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly mentioning names like Soso and Cassia, but the context and type of monument are entirely unclear.

EDCS-56900170.summary: This is a severely damaged inscription, potentially including the name Marcus, but its purpose and monument type are undeterminable.

EDCS-56900173.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely mentioning C. Stertinia Licini-, possibly as a dedicant or recipient, but the context remains unclear.

EDCS-56900180.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased son by his parents or another family member, expressing sorrow.

EDCS-56900183.text_edition: pientissimo dierum X VII  Iulia Philia
EDCS-56900183.translation: To the most devoted, for ten days, to Julia Philia.
EDCS-56900183.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Julia Philia, possibly for a most devoted individual who lived for only ten days.

EDCS-56900188.text_edition: D(is) M(anibus)  Flavio Aphrodisio Aphrodisia coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-56900188.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Flavius Aphrodisius. Aphrodisia to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-56900188.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Flavius Aphrodisius by his wife Aphrodisia, honoring him as a well-deserving husband.

EDCS-56900193.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Onesimus, but its overall context is lost.

EDCS-56900199.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely part of a larger dedication or record, with insufficient text to determine its specific purpose or context.

EDCS-56900202.translation: On the 26th of January, he/she set this up for his/her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-56900202.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication, recording a monument set up for a deceased daughter by a parent on a specific date.

EDCS-57000047.text_edition: Silvianus nnorum X pugnarum VII de suo sibi cui dolet posuit
EDCS-57000047.translation: Silvianus, 10 years old, with 7 victories, set this up for himself at his own expense, for whoever grieves.
EDCS-57000047.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Silvianus, a young gladiatorial contestant, erected by himself, acknowledging potential mourners.

EDCS-57000049.text_edition: Dis Manibus Lucio Caesidio Primo defuncto nnorum V parentes infelicissimi
EDCS-57000049.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lucius Caesidius Primus, deceased at 5 years old, his most unhappy parents [set this up].
EDCS-57000049.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from grieving parents to their young son, Lucius Caesidius Primus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-57000054.text_edition: Dis Manibus Marco Attio Verecundiano vetera qui vixit nnos LV Cania Ursina coniugi incomparabili posuit
EDCS-57000054.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Marcus Attius Verecundianus, an old soldier, who lived 55 years. Cania Ursina set this up for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-57000054.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument erected by Cania Ursina for her husband, Marcus Attius Verecundianus, a veteran who lived for 55 years.

EDCS-57100061.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication, mentioning Verzo and possibly his relationship as a grandson, but much of the text is lost.

EDCS-57200001.text_edition: Turus Livius Hospotis filius et Volses Oplinocus Noventi filius praetores aedem Veneris ex pecunia publica reficiendum curaverunt idemque probaverunt
EDCS-57200001.translation: Turus Livius, son of Hospes, and Volses Oplinocus, son of Noventus, as praetors, took care to have the temple of Venus restored with public money, and they also approved the work.
EDCS-57200001.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple of Venus, funded by public money and overseen by two praetors, Turus Livius and Volses Oplinocus.

EDCS-57200003.text_edition: Marco Cassio Quinti filio Sergia Rufo equo publico pontifici nnum XVI decreto decurionum Sestia Marci filia Ravonilla filio piissimo inpensa remissa posuit
EDCS-57200003.translation: To Marcus Cassius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, a public horseman, pontiff for 16 years, by decree of the decurions, Sestia Ravonilla, daughter of Marcus, set this up for her most devoted son, with the expense remitted.
EDCS-57200003.summary: This inscription is a monument erected by Sestia Ravonilla for her son, Marcus Cassius Rufus, a public horseman and pontiff, honored by decree of the decurions and at no cost to the mother.

EDCS-57200006.text_edition: Silvano sacrum Claudia Saturnina solvit libens merito
EDCS-57200006.translation: Sacred to Silvanus, Claudia Saturnina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-57200006.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Claudia Saturnina to the god Silvanus, fulfilling a vow she made.

EDCS-57200010.text_edition: Petiliae Petili filiae Valentinae defunctae annorum VI mensium VIII dierum XVIII Primus et Seccia Silvia parentes filiae dulcissimae posuerunt
EDCS-57200010.translation: To Petilia Valentina, daughter of Petilius, deceased at 6 years, 8 months, 18 days, Primus and Seccia Silvia, her parents, set this up for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-57200010.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Primus and Seccia Silvia to their beloved daughter, Petilia Valentina, who died at a young age.

EDCS-57200011.text_edition: Dis Manibus Ancharia Paulina Anchariae Primitivae libertae bene merenti posuit
EDCS-57200011.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ancharia Paulina set this up for Ancharia Primitiva, her well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-57200011.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Ancharia Paulina to her freedwoman, Ancharia Primitiva.

EDCS-57200022.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely commemorating a woman named Attia, possibly mentioning her as a daughter.

EDCS-57200025.text_edition: Priapo Augusto sacrum Lucius Cornelius Lupio aedem ampliavit votum solvit
EDCS-57200025.translation: Sacred to Priapus Augustus. Lucius Cornelius Lupio enlarged the temple and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-57200025.summary: This inscription records that Lucius Cornelius Lupio enlarged a temple dedicated to Priapus Augustus, thus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-57200031.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Ancharius.
EDCS-57200031.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription dedicated to Ancharius.

EDCS-57200032.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelius Titus Statiae Ursulae coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-57200032.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Titus [set this up for] Statia Ursula, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-57200032.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Titus for his wife, Statia Ursula.

EDCS-57200033.text_edition: uci Narcissus et Felicitas parentes loco concesso EI et Si
EDCS-57200033.translation: [Lu]ci Narcissus and Felicitas, parents, in the granted place...
EDCS-57200033.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a funerary dedication by parents, Narcissus and Felicitas, indicating a burial plot.

EDCS-57200035.text_edition: Dis Manibus Crescentio coniugi suo pientissimo posuit bene merenti
EDCS-57200035.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [She] set this up for Crescentius, her most devoted and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-57200035.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her most devoted husband, Crescentius.

EDCS-57200036.text_edition: Dis Manibus Antonia Secunda Antoniae Secundae matri infelicissimae defunctae nnorum L bene merenti posuit
EDCS-57200036.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Antonia Secunda set this up for Antonia Secunda, her most unfortunate and well-deserving mother, who died at 50 years of age.
EDCS-57200036.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Antonia Secunda for her mother, also named Antonia Secunda, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-57200037.text_edition: Magnae Matri Mescenia Publi filia porticum fecit de sua pecunia
EDCS-57200037.translation: To the Great Mother. Mescenia, daughter of Publius, built a portico with her own money.
EDCS-57200037.summary: This inscription records that Mescenia, daughter of Publius, built a portico for the Great Mother using her own funds, likely a votive or dedicatory monument.

EDCS-57200038.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary dedication mentioning Iuliana and Donata.

EDCS-57200040.text_edition: Caius Baebius Proculus de sua pecunia
EDCS-57200040.translation: Gaius Baebius Proculus [did this] with his own money.
EDCS-57200040.summary: This inscription records a private benefaction by Gaius Baebius Proculus, who funded something from his own resources.

EDCS-57200041.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Magiae Laetinae annorum XXVIIII Caius Magius Valens et Silia Laeta parentes filiae piissimae sibi et suis vivi fecerunt
EDCS-57200041.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Magia Laetina, 29 years old. Gaius Magius Valens and Silia Laeta, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter, and for themselves and their family, while alive.
EDCS-57200041.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Magia Laetina, set up by her parents, Gaius Magius Valens and Silia Laeta, for her and for themselves, indicating a family tomb.

EDCS-57200048.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Titus Statius Asprenas.
EDCS-57200048.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Titus Statius Asprenas, invoking the protection of the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-57200049.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Aurelius Quintus.
EDCS-57200049.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Aurelius Quintus, invoking the protection of the Divine Spirits.

EDCS-57200136.text_edition: Latrae Aug(ustae) libentes Gellii sub Clodio Gemino aedile [fecerunt]
EDCS-57200136.translation: To Latra Augusta, the Gellii willingly made this under Clodius Geminus, aedile.
EDCS-57200136.summary: This inscription records a dedication to a deity or empress, Latra Augusta, made by members of the Gellius family under the oversight of Clodius Geminus, who held the magistracy of aedile.

EDCS-57200167.text_edition: Species martialis orum VI V |L IIII P
EDCS-57200167.summary: This inscription likely details a quantity or measurement of 'martial species' in units of 'ora' and pounds, possibly related to military supplies or a commercial transaction.

EDCS-57200180.text_edition: Helviae Luci filiae Maximillae Lucius Cassius posuit
EDCS-57200180.translation: Lucius Cassius placed this for Helvia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-57200180.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication set up by Lucius Cassius in honor of Helvia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-57200181.translation: Ursio, brother, placed this for Lupa of the Kaninii.
EDCS-57200181.summary: This is a funerary monument or dedication set up by Ursio for his sister, Lupa, who belonged to the Kaninius family.

EDCS-57200183.text_edition: Baebiae Saturninae Iulia Iadestina amicae et sodali bene meritae viva posuit
EDCS-57200183.translation: Iulia Iadestina, while alive, placed this for Baebia Saturnina, her well-deserving friend and companion.
EDCS-57200183.summary: This is a funerary monument or memorial set up by Iulia Iadestina for her friend and companion, Baebia Saturnina, while Iulia herself was still alive.

EDCS-57200184.text_edition: Lucio Papirio Rufo S Lucius Papirius Didimus sic pater filio se vivo posuit
EDCS-57200184.translation: To Lucius Papirius Rufus, son of Lucius. Lucius Papirius Didimus, father, placed this for his son while he himself was alive.
EDCS-57200184.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Lucius Papirius Didimus for his son, Lucius Papirius Rufus, while the father was still living, indicating a pre-prepared family burial plot.

EDCS-57200186.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name label on an object or a fragment of a larger monument, recording the name of Lucius Paconius Fronto.

EDCS-57200190.text_edition: Dis Manibus  Lucio Bennio Honorato decurioni coloniae Aequensium qui vixit nnos X menses III Lucius Bennius Maximus et Flavia Tertulla parentes filio pientissimo et infelicissimo posuerunt
