EDCS-57200190.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Bennius Maximus and Flavia Tertulla, the parents, placed this for their most devoted and most unfortunate son, Lucius Bennius Honoratus, decurion of the colony of Aequum, who lived for 10 years and 3 months.
EDCS-57200190.summary: This is a funerary inscription from grieving parents, Lucius Bennius Maximus and Flavia Tertulla, dedicated to their son Lucius Bennius Honoratus, a decurion of the colony of Aequum, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-57200191.translation: Lucius Magius Celer, son of Sextus.
EDCS-57200191.summary: This is a simple inscription providing the name and patronymic of Lucius Magius Celer, likely a label or part of a larger, lost monument.

EDCS-57300131.text_edition: Gaio Arruntio Felicissimo Sempronia Maximilla marito defuncto nnorum LXX bene merenti posuit
EDCS-57300131.translation: Sempronia Maximilla placed this for Gaius Arruntius Felicissimus, her well-deserving husband, who died at 70 years of age.
EDCS-57300131.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Sempronia Maximilla's dedication to her deceased husband, Gaius Arruntius Felicissimus, who lived to be 70 years old.

EDCS-59500029.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Gallus tribunus cohortis II Aureliae Delmatarum
EDCS-59500029.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Tiberius Claudius Gallus, tribune of the Second Aurelian Cohort of Dalmatians.
EDCS-59500029.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tiberius Claudius Gallus, a military tribune, likely from a public or military context.

EDCS-59500031.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelia Tirentia sic pro se et pro s(uis) libens merito
EDCS-59500031.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aurelia Tirentia, for herself and her family, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-59500031.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelia Tirentia, made on behalf of herself and her family, indicating a personal vow fulfilled.

EDCS-59500034.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Varro FVCT.
EDCS-59500034.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by an individual named Varro, with the final letters likely being an abbreviation for a standard votive formula.

EDCS-59500035.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Viator. Q. P.
EDCS-59500035.summary: This is a brief sacred dedication to the deity Silvanus Viator, likely a local or specialized aspect of Silvanus, with the dedicant's identity abbreviated as Q. P.

EDCS-59500037.text_edition: Matri Deum Magnae Publius Domitius Publicola
EDCS-59500037.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods. Publius Domitius Publicola.
EDCS-59500037.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Magna Mater (Great Mother of the Gods) by Publius Domitius Publicola.

EDCS-59500038.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus dedicavit
EDCS-59500038.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus dedicated this.
EDCS-59500038.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus.

EDCS-59500040.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Tanarus, likely made by an individual named S. Apeius, whose full identity is partially lost.

EDCS-59500042.summary: This is a brief religious dedication to Liber, referring to him as 'Father'.

EDCS-59500043.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo MGP EG beneficiarius consularis votum libens solvit
EDCS-59500043.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. MGP EG, a beneficiarius consularis, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-59500043.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by MGP EG, a beneficiarius consularis (a soldier detached for special duty to a consular governor), who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-64100445.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum dulcissimae filiae Epidiae quae vixit annos XXXVIII menses IIII infelicissima mater Eutichia sic memoriam fecit
EDCS-64100445.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To my sweetest daughter Epidia, who lived 38 years, 4 months. Her most unhappy mother Eutychia thus made this memorial.
EDCS-64100445.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a mother's sorrowful dedication of a memorial to her daughter Epidia, who died at age 38.

EDCS-64100446.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Statius Lupus hic situs est Titus Statius Threptus
EDCS-64100446.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Statius Lupus is buried here. Titus Statius Threptus [is also buried here].
EDCS-64100446.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating the burial of Titus Statius Lupus and likely Titus Statius Threptus in the same location.

EDCS-64100447.text_edition: Iuliae Crispinae ex testamento Stati Cilonis Egnatius Kapito gCener#gener#CENER posuit
EDCS-64100447.translation: To Julia Crispina, according to the will of Statius Cilo. Egnatius Capito, her son-in-law, placed this.
EDCS-64100447.summary: This is a funerary or memorial inscription dedicated to Julia Crispina, placed by her son-in-law Egnatius Capito, fulfilling the will of Statius Cilo.

EDCS-64100449.text_edition: Dis Manibus Nonius Valens hic situs
EDCS-64100449.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Nonius Valens is buried here.
EDCS-64100449.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription marking the burial place of Nonius Valens.

EDCS-64100450.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Statius Paulinus vixit annos XXXIIII
EDCS-64100450.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Statius Paulinus lived 34 years.
EDCS-64100450.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Gaius Statius Paulinus, who lived for 34 years.

EDCS-64100451.translation: To Octavia Quintina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-64100451.summary: This is a brief inscription likely for a monument or dedication to Octavia Quintina, identifying her as the daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-64100452.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Iuliae Maximae vixit annos LXII Statius Lupus coniugi sanctissimae
EDCS-64100452.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Julia Maxima, who lived 62 years. Statius Lupus [made this] for his most sacred wife.
EDCS-64100452.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Maxima, who lived 62 years, erected by Statius Lupus for his revered wife.

EDCS-64100453.text_edition: Dis Manibus sacrum  Sexto Caesonio Pulchro an=NAnorum#annorum#NAN XXXVII
EDCS-64100453.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. To Sextus Caesionius Pulcher, 37 years old.
EDCS-64100453.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Sextus Caesionius Pulcher, who died at the age of 37, erected by an unspecified commemorator.

EDCS-64100459.summary: This is likely a funerary or dedicatory inscription for Sextus Manlius, identifying his origin as Sirmio.

EDCS-64100460.text_edition: Ipse implevi omni et lucernas accendi Ditus
EDCS-64100460.translation: I myself filled everything and lit the lamps. Ditus.
EDCS-64100460.summary: This inscription records Ditus as the person who performed specific actions, likely related to filling or lighting within a monument or sacred space.

EDCS-64100461.text_edition: Cereri Augustae sacrum Victoriae et Veneri Augustis Cnaeus
EDCS-64100461.translation: Sacred to Ceres Augusta, and to Victoria Augusta and Venus Augusta. Gnaeus...
EDCS-64100461.summary: This is a religious dedication made by Gnaeus to the goddesses Ceres Augusta, Victoria Augusta, and Venus Augusta, indicating a veneration of imperial cult deities.

EDCS-64100462.text_edition: Silvano Viatori Augusto Libero Augusto Iuli---
EDCS-64100462.translation: To Silvanus Viator Augustus, to Liber Augustus. Iuli[---]...
EDCS-64100462.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deities Silvanus Viator Augustus and Liber Augustus, with the dedicant's name, Iulius, partially preserved.

EDCS-64100467.text_edition: Larciae Amarullini sic nnorum LXXX et filiae Gentillae nnorum XXXX filii fecerunt matri sui sic carissimae et serori sic
EDCS-64100467.translation: To Larcia Amaryllinus, 80 years old, and to her daughter Gentilla, 40 years old. The sons made this for their dearest mother and for their sister.
EDCS-64100467.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built by sons for their beloved mother, Larcia Amaryllinus, and their sister, Gentilla.

EDCS-64100468.text_edition: Publius Coelius Pamphilus vivus fecit sibi et Coeliae Muntillae filiae defunctae annorum XXI et Publio Coelio Niciphoro sic filio defuncto annorum XXVII et Coeliae Arescusae uxori vivae
EDCS-64100468.translation: Publius Coelius Pamphilus made this while living for himself, and for Coelia Muntilla, his deceased daughter, 21 years old, and for Publius Coelius Nicephorus, his deceased son, 27 years old, and for Coelia Arescusas, his living wife.
EDCS-64100468.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Publius Coelius Pamphilus during his lifetime for himself, his two deceased children, and his living wife, Coelia Arescusas.

EDCS-64100469.text_edition: Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari  Imperatori domino nostro Diocletiano Augusto
EDCS-64100469.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar. To Emperor and our Lord Diocletianus Augustus.
EDCS-64100469.summary: This inscription is a dedication to two Roman emperors, Marcus Aurelius Carinus, referred to as 'noblest Caesar,' and Diocletianus Augustus, our Lord and Emperor, likely dating to the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-64100470.text_edition: l Constan defeNsori rio MC piissimo Caesari bono rei publicae nato  X
EDCS-64100470.translation: To Constans, the defender, the most pious Caesar, born for the good of the Republic.
EDCS-64100470.summary: This is a laudatory inscription dedicated to Constans, identifying him as a 'defender' and 'most pious Caesar,' suggesting a date in the 4th century AD.

EDCS-64100473.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Valerius Sotericus votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-64100473.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Marcus Valerius Sotericus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-64100473.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, made by Marcus Valerius Sotericus as a fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-64100474.translation: Sextus Atilius, son of Sextus, Rufus.
EDCS-64100474.summary: This is a fragment of an epitaph, likely for Sextus Atilius Rufus, indicating his name and filiation.

EDCS-64100475.text_edition: annis XL Ursus coniugi carissimaeP SIT
EDCS-64100475.translation: Ursus dedicated this to his dearest wife, who lived for 40 years. May the earth cover her lightly.
EDCS-64100475.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Ursus for his beloved wife, recording her age at death and a common funerary wish.

EDCS-65500082.translation: Lucius Valerius, for his wife, in the year [---].
EDCS-65500082.summary: This is a fragmented dedication, likely an epitaph, from Lucius Valerius for his wife.

EDCS-65500084.translation: Cisilla for the Lares (household gods), as an offering.
EDCS-65500084.summary: This is a religious dedication from Cisilla to the Lares, likely a votive offering for protection or thanks.

EDCS-65500087.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, preserving only a patronymic, likely from an epitaph or dedication.

EDCS-66100163.summary: This is a fragmented dedication, likely a votive offering, made by Lucretia (and possibly Bellona, or dedicated to Bellona) to an unknown recipient.

EDCS-66100164.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Messianus, an infant foster child.
EDCS-66100164.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes for Messianus, an infant foster child.

EDCS-66100165.text_edition: vixit annos XXX Munatia Secunda coniugi bene merenti
EDCS-66100165.translation: She lived for 30 years. Munatia Secunda [dedicated this] to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-66100165.summary: This is an epitaph from Munatia Secunda for her husband, recording his age at death and her esteem for him.

EDCS-66200102.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Ursus iunior votum solvit
EDCS-66200102.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Claudius Ursus the younger fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-66200102.summary: This is a votive inscription from Claudius Ursus iunior, dedicating an offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-66200106.text_edition: Dis Manibus is HAER Q VeIrinae#Verinae#VIRINE C coniugi
EDCS-66200106.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---] to his wife Verina.
EDCS-66200106.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes by a husband for his wife, Verina.

EDCS-66200163.text_edition: Lucius Oppius Luci filius Sergia Signinus domo Constantia veteranus legionis VII nnum LXXI hic situs est
EDCS-66200163.translation: Lucius Oppius Signinus, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, from Constantia, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 71, is buried here.
EDCS-66200163.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Oppius Signinus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-66400016.translation: To Crispin, my most devoted husband, who has passed away.
EDCS-66400016.summary: This is a short funerary dedication from a wife to her deceased husband, Crispin, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-66600371.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning Claudius D. Fabius, with the rest of the text missing, making its exact purpose unclear.

EDCS-67300045.text_edition: AV  et Catoni coniugi et nepoti annorum XXX et Dassio annorum XXV  et
EDCS-67300045.translation: ... and to Cato, husband, and grandson, 30 years old, and to Dassius, 25 years old, and...
EDCS-67300045.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Cato, a husband and grandson, and Dassius, indicating a family burial or memorial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-67300048.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, mentioning a 'son of Ionio' and providing little further context.

EDCS-67300051.summary: This is a brief inscription naming Aelius Ferox and Aelia Platino, likely part of a funerary monument or a dedication by a married couple, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-67300056.text_edition: Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali posuit Titius Surus votum posuit libens
EDCS-67300056.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, Titius Surus willingly placed this vow.
EDCS-67300056.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Titius Surus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-67300057.text_edition: Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Secundus dec(urio) et Varro Dasantis  posuerunt pro salute sua et collegio aerariorum BMILIAN
EDCS-67300057.translation: Sacred to Augustus. Publius Aelius Secundus, decurion, and Varro Dasantis, placed this for their own well-being and that of the college of treasury officials.
EDCS-67300057.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to Augustus, set up by Publius Aelius Secundus and Varro Dasantis for their own health and that of a collegium of treasury officials, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-67400476.text_edition: Dis Manibus Aurelia Turoni VIO VEIT heres posuit
EDCS-67400476.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Turoni, heiress, placed this.
EDCS-67400476.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Manes, placed by Aurelia Turoni, the heiress, for an unnamed deceased individual, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-67500106.text_edition: conservatrici  potenti  Calpurnia Luci Pisonis auguris filia Cnaei Pisonis neptis donum dedit
EDCS-67500106.translation: To the mighty protectress, Calpurnia, daughter of Lucius Piso the augur, granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, gave this gift.
EDCS-67500106.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription from Calpurnia, a woman of notable senatorial lineage, offering a gift to a 'mighty protectress,' likely a goddess, dating probably to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-67800034.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. By vision, for his own salvation, for that of his sons, and all his freedmen, Publius Cornelius Donatus, a veteran, set this up.
EDCS-67800034.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the veteran Publius Cornelius Donatus for the well-being of himself, his sons, and all his freedmen, prompted by a vision.

EDCS-68800075.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Paternal. Aurelia Maxima set this up.
EDCS-68800075.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, referred to as the Paternal, set up by Aurelia Maxima.

EDCS-69200413.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Lucius Adennius Philetianus, soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers, with three years of service. [---]
EDCS-69200413.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, commemorating Lucius Adennius Philetianus, a soldier of the Eighth Cohort of Volunteers who served for three years.

EDCS-71300207.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. [---] Varius R. [---] of the First Cohort, one thousand men strong. By command, willingly.
EDCS-71300207.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by someone from the First Cohort, likely a large unit, made willingly and by command.

EDCS-71300208.translation: The most fortunate Scarabantia, willingly, I have placed this vow.
EDCS-71300208.summary: This inscription records a vow made and willingly placed by someone named Felicisssimus Scarabantia.

EDCS-71300214.translation: To Jupiter Capitolinus. Marcus Ulpius Marcianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-71300214.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Capitolinus by Marcus Ulpius Marcianus, who willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-71400008.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Aureli Victor and Memor and Paulinus willingly set this up for Maximus Scenocali, their relative.
EDCS-71400008.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana Augusta by Aurelius Victor, Memor, and Paulinus on behalf of their relative, Maximus Scenocali.

EDCS-71400009.translation: [---] Aurelius F[---] [---] L. P. Mal[---] [---] and Al[---] [---] on the Ides [---]
EDCS-71400009.summary: This highly fragmented inscription, likely a dedication or official record, mentions an Aurelius, other names, and a date referring to the Ides.

EDCS-71400015.summary: This extremely fragmented inscription contains only parts of names, including 'Aemili', making its original purpose largely indecipherable.

EDCS-71400190.translation: To Marcus Servilius, son of Gaius, consul, septemvir epulonum, propraetorian legate of Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-71400190.summary: This inscription is a public honorific dedication to Marcus Servilius, detailing his distinguished career as consul, septemvir epulonum, and propraetorian legate under Caesar Augustus, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-71400191.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Cattius Marcianus, who died at the age of 40. Cattia Valentina made this for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-71400191.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Cattia Valentina to her brother, Cattius Marcianus, who died at age 40, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-71400192.translation: To Valerius Licinius [Fe]lix Maximus, Father of the Country [---].
EDCS-71400192.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a public dedication, possibly to an emperor or high-ranking official, Valerius Licinius Felix Maximus, who held the title 'Father of the Country', likely from the 3rd or 4th century AD.

EDCS-71400193.translation: [---] to Bellia Laetina, for herself and her family, he made this while alive [---] possession [---] L[---].
EDCS-71400193.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to be a funerary monument prepared by an unnamed individual for Bellia Laetina and her family during his lifetime, possibly indicating a tomb or mausoleum.

EDCS-71400194.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely refers to a veteran named Martialis, possibly part of a funerary monument or a public record of his service.

EDCS-71400582.translation: To Aurelia Prisca, a most noble woman.
EDCS-71400582.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aurelia Prisca, described as a 'most noble woman,' indicating a high social status, likely part of a funerary monument or an honorific statue.

EDCS-72200021.text_edition: Finis inter Neditas et Corinienses derectus mensuris actis iussu Auli Duceni Gemini legati per Aulum Resium Maximum |(centurionem) legionis XI principem posteriorem cohortis I et per Quintum Aebutium Liberalem |(centurionem) eiusdem legionis hastatum posteriorem cohortis I
EDCS-72200021.translation: The boundary between the Nediti and the Corinienses was established and surveyed by order of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, legate, through Aulus Resius Maximus, centurion of the 11th legion, junior princeps of the 1st cohort, and through Quintus Aebutius Liberalis, centurion of the same legion, junior hastatus of the 1st cohort.
EDCS-72200021.summary: This inscription records the official demarcation of a boundary between the Neditas and Corinienses communities, executed under the authority of legate Aulus Ducenius Geminus by two centurions of the 11th Legion, providing evidence of Roman land administration.

EDCS-72700156.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Petilius Saturus. Petilia Saturnina, his freedwoman, placed this for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-72700156.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Petilia Saturnina, a freedwoman, to her well-deserving patron, Petilius Saturus, highlighting the social relationship between a freedperson and their former owner.

EDCS-72700157.summary: This fragment indicates that an individual named Firmus erected an altar, suggesting a religious dedication, though the deity and context are lost.

EDCS-72900249.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Valerius placed this.
EDCS-72900249.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication to Silvanus Augustus, a syncretic deity blending the Roman god Silvanus with the imperial cult, placed by Valerius.

EDCS-72900250.text_edition: Silvano Domestico sacrum Titus Aurelius Dasas libens votum solvit
EDCS-72900250.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Titus Aurelius Dasas willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-72900250.summary: This inscription is a religious dedication to Silvanus Domesticus, a god associated with the household, erected by Titus Aurelius Dasas in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-72900253.text_edition: Dis Manibus Atecto infanti qui vixit nnos VII menses III dies XII Sterceia mater filio infelicissimo posuit
EDCS-72900253.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Atectus, an infant who lived 7 years, 3 months, 12 days. Sterceia, his mother, placed this for her most unhappy son.
EDCS-72900253.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a mother, Sterceia, dedicated to her infant son Atectus, recording his brief lifespan.

EDCS-72900254.text_edition: Triviam sic Marcus T M pro Dio votum solvit libens merito
EDCS-72900254.translation: Thus, Marcus, son of Tiberius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Trivia on behalf of Dio.
EDCS-72900254.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Marcus, son of Tiberius, fulfilling a vow to the goddess Trivia on behalf of Dio.

EDCS-73700315.translation: Sacred to Mars. Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, with his family, willingly placed this.
EDCS-73700315.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mars by Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, and his family.

EDCS-73700316.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Aelius Silvanus lived 2 years; he is buried here. Aelius Quintilianus placed this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-73700316.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aelius Quintilianus for his son, Aelius Silvanus, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-73700317.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. To Gaius Statius Bession and his wife Aeronia Severina. Statius Velocianus and Severina, their children, placed this for their parents.
EDCS-73700317.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Statius Velocianus and Severina dedicated to their parents, Gaius Statius Bession and Aeronia Severina.

EDCS-74800001.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. To Publius Aelius, son of Gaius, Novanianus, decurion of the municipality of the Baloenses...
EDCS-74800001.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Publius Aelius Novanianus, a decurion of the municipality of the Baloenses.

EDCS-75300231.translation: Gaius Aulius, son of Gaius, of the Aniensis tribe, Secundus, from Cremona, a veteran of the Legion VII Claudia Pia Fidelis, is buried here. Arria Agathemeris placed this for her dearest and most deserving husband, and for Quintus Arrius Priscus, and for herself while alive.
EDCS-75300231.summary: This is a funerary inscription from Arria Agathemeris for her husband, Gaius Aulius Secundus, a veteran of Legion VII Claudia, also including Quintus Arrius Priscus and herself.

EDCS-75300234.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription mentioning a Caesar who held the consulship, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-75300236.translation: ...Gaius Alasinius Valentinus, centurion of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, placed this for his most dutiful and deserving parents.
EDCS-75300236.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Gaius Alasinius Valentinus, a centurion, to his parents.

EDCS-75700071.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. For everlasting security. Saufeius Valens placed this memorial for his beloved Ursinus, who lived about 25 years. Saufeius Valentinus likewise placed this memorial while alive.
EDCS-75700071.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Saufeius Valens for his beloved Ursinus, and also by Saufeius Valentinus for himself while still living, implying a family monument.

EDCS-77200008.translation: The Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, pontifex maximus, [holding tribunician power for the second time(?), acclaimed imperator] for the third time.
EDCS-77200008.summary: This is a monumental inscription honoring Emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from a public monument or building, dating to around 236 CE, given his imperial titulature.

EDCS-78300155.translation: Pinnius Dalmatius, while alive, set up this chest for himself and his family. If anyone should wish to move it from its place or place bodies [other than those intended], he shall pay a fine of 100,000 denarii to the treasury.
EDCS-78300155.summary: This inscription records the erection of a tomb or burial chest by Pinnius Dalmatius for himself and his family, including a strong clause to prevent disturbance or misuse, and likely dates from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300157.translation: To the Divine Manes. C. Maxima placed this for Gaius Tatusius Sabinus, her most deserving husband.
EDCS-78300157.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by C. Maxima to her husband, Gaius Tatusius Sabinus, a common practice in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300160.translation: ... six months ... M. Mar... Titus Tarc[... Agath[... ADH...
EDCS-78300160.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary due to the mention of 'months', possibly indicating age at death, and contains several personal names.

EDCS-78300161.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucretius Ferox placed this for Lucretia Verecunda, his mother.
EDCS-78300161.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucretius Ferox in memory of his mother, Lucretia Verecunda, a typical filial dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78300162.translation: ... deceased at [three] years old. Titus Flavius Hospes [made this] for the well-deserving.
EDCS-78300162.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a child who died at a young age, dedicated by Titus Flavius Hospes.

EDCS-78300164.translation: ... for Valeria, freedwoman of Gaius, Earin(a)e, from Mediolanum, who lived for 30 years. Gaius Valerius Telesphorus, procurator of the 20th [inheritance tax], made this for his dearest freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-78300164.summary: This funerary inscription was erected by Gaius Valerius Telesphorus, a procurator of the 20th inheritance tax, for his beloved wife, Valeria Earin(a), a freedwoman originally from Mediolanum, who lived to be 30 years old.

EDCS-78300181.translation: To Liber and Libera. Tiberius Claudius Antonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-78300181.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the deities Liber and Libera, made by Tiberius Claudius Antonius in fulfillment of a vow, typical of Roman religious practice.

EDCS-78300182.summary: This is a fragmentary dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the chief Roman deity, made by an individual whose name, partly preserved as Aurelius, is incomplete.

EDCS-78300749.translation: ... incomparable... freedman Pollux... of the 20th freedom tax...
EDCS-78300749.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or honorific, mentioning a freedman named Pollux and the 'vicesima libertatis', referring to the 5% tax on manumissions.

EDCS-78300754.translation: Overseers of the gate to be built, Gaius Catellius, freedman of Marcus, Gaius Vettius, freedman of Lucius, Marcus Annaeus, freedman of Quintus, Med[---], saw to it from money from outside the gate.
EDCS-78300754.summary: This inscription records that a group of freedmen, acting as magistrates, oversaw the construction of a gate using funds collected from outside the gate.

EDCS-78300755.translation: freedman of Gaius Vi[---], son of [---] Libo, [---] son of Quintus, quaestors, saw to the building of the tower.
EDCS-78300755.summary: This inscription records that a group of quaestors, including a freedman and a man named Libo, oversaw the construction of a tower.

EDCS-78400005.translation: Gaius Feresius, freedman of Gaius, Urbanus(?), Augustalis sexvir.
EDCS-78400005.summary: This inscription is a funerary or dedicatory record for Gaius Feresius Urbanus, a freedman who held the position of Augustalis sexvir, likely a priest of the Imperial cult.

EDCS-78400006.translation: ---rtia, daughter of Marcus, and to Gaius Petronius, sexvir.
EDCS-78400006.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or funerary monument involving a woman named ---rtia, daughter of Marcus, and Gaius Petronius, who was a sexvir (a member of a six-man college, possibly an Augustalis sexvir).

EDCS-78400008.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment listing several names, possibly part of a dedicatory or funerary monument.

EDCS-78400009.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, only preserving a partial name, Gaius Ter[---].

EDCS-78400014.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary monument for Aninia Saturnina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-78400015.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument mentioning Gaius.

EDCS-78400017.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory text, mentioning a person whose name ends in -nius and whose father was Marcus.

EDCS-78400018.summary: This inscription is likely a funerary monument for Annaea Ermiona, a freedwoman of Quintus.

EDCS-78400024.translation: To Gaius Curtius Super, the college [erected this].
EDCS-78400024.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a college to Gaius Curtius Super, likely for a public office or benefaction.

EDCS-78400026.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record names, possibly indicating a dedication or part of a monument erected by or for individuals named Dommi and Domm[cinius].

EDCS-78400027.translation: To his wife [---]illa, Gaius Iunius Cissus made this while alive.
EDCS-78400027.summary: This inscription records a tomb or monument built by Gaius Iunius Cissus for his wife, [---]illa, during his lifetime.

EDCS-78400028.translation: [To Gaius?] Papirius, son of Lucius, Ru[fus?], [and ---] Valeria, daughter of Valerius, Vale[ria?].
EDCS-78400028.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication or monument for members of the Papiria and Valeria families, mentioning their filiation.

EDCS-78400029.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria Posilla placed this for herself, Ba[---].
EDCS-78400029.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Valeria Posilla, who erected a monument for herself.

EDCS-78400032.translation: [---]aeus [---]bius made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-78400032.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a monument built by an individual, whose name ends in -aeus [---]bius, for himself and his family during his lifetime.
